name: Viktor
description: Viktor (ビクトール, Bikutōru) is a major character in Suikoden and Suikoden II who also makes a cameo in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.1.
quote: Get going, Flik. If you have to... that's the way it is.

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
A large, muscular, tanned man, Viktor had black, unkempt hair and dark brown eyes. He wore a breastplate over a mustard tunic with the arms torn off. Due to his size and appearance, he was sometimes called "Bear", a nickname he usually laughed off. In his younger days, he had worn a similar tunic with the arms attached, was slenderer, and had neater hair.

He also wore black pants, as well as brown boots, gloves, and a belt. He also carried a pouch with him, secured to a strap that we wore across his chest. The costume was designed with an emphasis on practicality as a warrior. His sword name, Taia Sword, is the same as a powerful sword in the original Water Margin, though it was not wielded by his literary equivalent, Lu

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
Viktor had a strong sense of justice and always wielded his sword for what he believed in. Viktor often held his despair deep inside so as not to bother anyone. It was said he had recurring nightmares of what had happened at North Window.

Although outwardly brash and intimidating, he also had a childlike curiosity and a keen eye for detail which made him an invaluable comrade. He was a good judge of character, quickly recognizing the leadership qualities of several youths.

As a man who lived his life searching for vengeance, Viktor viewed himself as someone who could only live in battle. During the escape from Gregminster Palace at the end of the Gate Rune War, he described himself as someone not needed once the war was over. He felt that his blood-stained hands were not suited to creating a new era of peace.

Over the years, he never forgot his true mission, to find and defeat Neclord. When the opportunity arose during the Gate Rune War, Viktor dropped all pretense of any other motivation. Even following Neclord's defeat, Viktor's immediate reaction was to leave for North Window. When Neclord revealed himself during the Dunan Unification War, Viktor was crushed but quickly refocused himself on striking down the vampire once and for all.

His nickname, "Vagabond Viktor", gives the impression that he travels wherever the wind blows, but Viktor seems to have the idea that it comes from him making a name for himself as a warrior in various places across the

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Survivor of North Window
Viktor grew up in the town of North Window in the City-States of Jowston. He began his mercenary career at a young age, becoming acquainted with Anabelle of Muse City at some point in time. His father had been the commander of the 4th North Window unit but fell in combat against the Scarlet Moon Empire in SY 447. His sword skills were self-taught.

He avoided the destruction of the town by Neclord as he had been out of town buying medicine for his mother, and other supplies, at the time. He would rush back to the village in an attempt to rescue Daisy, but was easily defeated by Neclord and nearly killed, his life only saved by a Sacrificial Jizo he had bought at her request.

After recovering in Kuskus Town, he would be part of the South Window City investigation team, helping to clear out the remaining zombies in North Window. Once this was complete, he would see to the burial of every villager personally, refusing to be helped.

Following this, Viktor fell into a stupor, having been refused a task force to hunt down Neclord with. In the Lion's Fang Inn, he would be rescued from a drunken bar brawl by Zamji. Completely despondent, Viktor would spend the next six months with Zamji's family, slowly recovering emotionally from his ordeal.

Learning of the increasing tensions between South Window and Two River City, Viktor would work with Anabelle and Zamji to uncover the plot and defeat the arms merchants, including an acquaintance, Kou Long, who had set the two city-states against one another, using the incident at North Window as a flashpoint.

Uncovering the conspiracy and capturing Kou Long, peace would be preserved and sometime later, Viktor would decide to leave Jowston in order to find and destroy Neclord.
Vagabond Days
Ever since then, Viktor wandered the lands as a mercenary, hoping to gain his revenge against Neclord someday. He…

recruitment: Joins automatically following plot events in Gregminster.

name: Pixie
description: The Pixie (ピクシー, Pikushī) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 163 · DEX 107 · DEF 65 · SPD 113 · MAG 85 · LCK 182

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Pixie is a wicked fairy small enough to ride in the palm of a hand. During the Dunan Unification War, many of these were spotted on Rakutei Mountain.

Its attacks have a chance of inflicting the Shrink status and this, combined with its high magic resistance and evasion, make it a very nasty enemy. It is best to try and beat it with physical attacks.

trivia: * The later version of the Pixie has boosted stats but has most of its resistances removed.

name: Pixie Rune
description: The Pixie Rune (ピクシーの紋章, Pikushī no Monshō) is a rune that appears in Suikoden II and Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Pixie Rune was a rune utilized by wielders of magic rods. The name of the rune, "Pixie," derived from a subtle and elusive little spirit.

This rune granted the bearer the ability to conjure a magical blast from their rod, extending its reach to any distance. The Pixie Rune significantly expanded the attack options available to mages, even when they were not actively casting rune spells.

name: Shining Wind Rune
description: The Shining Wind Rune (光る風の紋章, Hikarukaze no Monshō) is a rune that appears in Suikoden II and Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: A unique rune borne by Feather, it was one of the lower order variants of the Wind Rune. It grants the Shining Wind wind-element attack which upgrades (single, horizontal row, whole) as the magic level of the bearer grows. It was also more effective against flying enemies.

name: Hikari Attack
description: A combination attack performed by Borgan, Gantetsu and Lonchanchan. A sudden burst of light over the enemies' heads damages every foe at once. The catch: it also damages every ally except the three who launched it.
quote: So blinding that it harms your own side — a fearsome technique indeed.

name: Flint
description: The Flint Stone (火打ち石; Hiuchi-ishi) is a key item in Suikoden II.

name: Hiiragi Papa
description: A monster that lives mainly in forests. It closely resembles an ordinary Hiiragi but is far larger; it is presumed to be what a Hiiragi grows into.

name: Hiiragi Master
description: A forest monster. It looks like an evolved form of the Hiiragi line, with its abilities — attack power, magic and the rest — all raised across the board.

name: Hikusaku
description: The hero who founded the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia two years before the start of the solar era, and the inheritor of the Circle Rune. Granted longevity by its power, he almost never appears before others — a figure shrouded in mystery.

name: Mado Seal Orb
description: An orb that controls the power of the Window Rune — an art rare even among Window mages. It lay hidden in a cave in the grounds of Kuron Temple until the hero discovered it and handed it to Windo, who lived in the warriors' village, helping to persuade her to join the Liberation Army.

name: Magic Users
description: A unit of women skilled in the magical arts, assembled by Jeane, Hellion and Vicky. With their combined sorcery they routed great numbers of Imperial soldiers.

name: Lost Forest
description: A vast woodland spreading out from the edge of the great forest village. It was once a true maze: barrier magic turned intruders around in circles. After the giant tree that sustained the elves' village faded, the barrier vanished as well, and it is now an ordinary forest like any other.

name: Marie
description: Marie (マリー, Marī) is a minor character who appears in Suikoden who also makes a cameo in Suikoden II. Marie is an inn keeper and proprietress who sheltered McDohl for a time during the Gate Rune War.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Marie ran the Scarlet Moon Inn in the city of Gregminster and was acquainted with McDohl from an early age. She had a sweet disposition and was admired by many for her maternal warmth and big heart.

After McDohl, Cleo, and Gremio became fugitives, Marie sheltered them in her inn until they escaped the city. Following this, Marie's actions were uncovered, and she was forced to flee the city as well, eventually ending up in Seika.

There she was reacquainted with McDohl, whom she did not blame for what happened, and asked to join the Toran Liberation Army. Marie opened an inn at Toran Castle in order to provide a place for the army's warriors to relax and rest. She eventually needed the aid of a chef, leading to the recruitment of Antonio, and later Lester.

Following the end of the Gate Rune War, Marie returned to Gregminster where she reopened her inn. By the time of the Dunan Unification War, she was still running the inn, which was as popular as

recruitment: Talk to Marie at the inn in Seika at any time following the story events at the Ancient Castle of Toran.

name: Marco
description: Marco (マルコ, Maruko) is a character in Suikoden. He is a gambler who challenges passers-by to a cup game with potch being wagered on the outcome.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Marco was a young and resourceful orphan who relied on his exceptional skills in a shell game to earn his living, having no family to rely on.

His travels took him far and wide, showcasing his remarkable speed and dexterity in this game of chance. During his journey, Marco crossed paths with McDohl in the town of Rockland, long before the young hero assumed leadership of the Toran Liberation Army.

After this initial encounter, Marco's path intersected with McDohl once more, this time in Warriors' Village, situated in the Lorimar region. Inspired by McDohl's impressive victory in the shell game, Marco decided to join forces with the young leader. He saw the Liberation Army as his best bet for surviving the intensifying conflict.

Over the course of the Gate Rune War, Marco established a gambling den where he continued to showcase his cup game, providing amusement and diversion for the soldiers. Additionally, he lent his expertise as an archer in significant field battles.

Following the conclusion of the Gate Rune War, Marco's adventurous spirit persisted, leading him to lead a vibrant life in various cities throughout the

recruitment: When the castle is at Level 3 or more, talk to Marco in the Warriors' Village and win 5,000 bits from him.
trivia:
• In the Sega Saturn release of Suikoden, Marco's shell game is replaced by a "heads or tails" game in which Marco tosses three coins and you guess what side each coin will land on. Potch is awarded if you get two or more coins correct.
• Playing with Marco in Rockland is known to be an easy way to get a lot of money early in the game: Save the game, play with him and remember the pattern. After loading the game, his pattern doesn't change, allowing the player to easily win a string of rounds.

name: Maria (alias)
description: When boarding Kuwabara's castle ship on the way from Lenankampu to Seika, Clive gave his name as a woman's — "Maria." The gag earned a sharp retort from Viktor, which seems to have left Clive rather annoyed.

name: Tir McDohl
description: The hero (主人公, Shujinkō), known by several different names, is the protagonist of Suikoden. As well as being the protagonist of the original game, he also appears as a secret recruit in Suikoden II and makes a cameo in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2.

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
The hero is a slender youth, with dark brown hair and brown eyes. He wears a red tunic over a white, short-sleeved shirt and beige pants. He also wears black shoes with wraps around both feet.

The hero also wears a fashionable green bandana that switches to purple in the middle. Early in Suikoden, he wears a glove on his left hand, likely to prevent his staff from exerting too much force on his non-dominant arm. After inheriting the Soul Eater from Ted, he also wears a glove on his right hand to hide the rune marking.

His staff was made of black oak with gold decorations and decorative orbs on both ends. Slightly taller than the hero as a young adult, the staff was originally a gift from his master,

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
Somewhat passive, the hero was known to be a nice, genial youth with a mischievous side that came to the surface around close friends and confidants. He was also said to have a certain irresistible charm.

The hero had no problem fighting but was squeamish when it came to killing. Once, when hunting a rabbit he was willing to knock it out but couldn't bring himself to kill it with a hunting knife.

He also sometimes chaffed at Gremio's protective nature but found it difficult to hold a grudge considering the lengths Gremio went to to protect him as a child.

His ability to keep moving forward despite the heavy burden placed on him as a bearer of the Soul Eater is what inspires a great many people to follow and trust in his abilities as a leader despite his youth. That burden also forced him to mature rapidly, dealing with the loss of so many comrades.

His driving force was to fight for justice. Although some may have questioned the wisdom of prioritizing justice during war, for the hero, justice was his motivation for fighting and kept him going.

The hero also held a forgiving and pragmatic nature. His ability to hold back his anger for losses caused by the Imperial generals for the greater good was another reminder of his capacity as a leader. Seeing this in action only strengthened the bonds of loyalty and respect between the members of the Toran Liberation Army and the

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Childhood
The hero was born to Great General Teo McDohl of the Scarlet Moon Empire. His mother came from a common background and was considered to be a wise and caring woman. However, she passed away from illness soon after his birth and so the hero was mostly raised by Gremio, Teo's attendant.

As a child, he was extremely clingy with his father as his only surviving parent, even trying to sneak into his carriage so he could travel with him on military expeditions.

During the War of Succession, McDohl was kidnapped by Assam as leverage to use against his father. Taken to Sarady, he would be rescued by the efforts of Gremio, Eiji, and Ronettes. This experience, and Gremio's tenacity in finding him, endeared the young master to Gremio.

With the support of Gremio and Teo's other subordinates, such as Cleo and Pahn, he grew into a vigorous young man with a strong sense of justice. Master Kai, meanwhile, trained him in the use of the staff as a youth. He was quick learner and picked up many techniques and once Kai left, his abilities became entirely self-taught.

Later, he would become best friends with Ted, who was brought to the McDohl household by Teo and adopted as a war orphan.
Gate Rune War
He was sent to serve Emperor Barbarossa when Teo was assigned on a mission to the north and was supposed to be assured of a future in the Imperial Army. However, that changed completely when he inherited the Rune of Life and Death, one of the 27 True Runes, from his best friend, Ted. Mortally wounded, Ted would beg the hero, his only true friend, to take the rune, and the hero, trusting Ted in turn, agreed.

This brought down the attention of Court Magician Windy, who desired the rune herself, and he was soon driven from the Empire. Escaping Gregminster allowed him to see the truth of the Empire during his flight. Realizing the increasing oppression and disarray, he found…

recruitment: Automatically joins at the beginning of the game
trivia: • The hero won every annual [https://twitter.com/gensosenkyo Gensosenkyo fan poll] he has been eligible for (2016, 2017, 2019, 2020, 2022, and 2023). In 2018 and 2021, he was awarded a "hall of fame" rank separate from the poll itself.

name: Soul Eater
description: The Rune of Life and Death (生と死の紋章, Nama to Shi no Monshō), more commonly known as the Soul Eater (ソウルイーター Souru Ītā) is one of the 27 True Runes. It is the rune of the protagonist of Suikoden and is also usable in Suikoden II and Suikoden IV.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Soul Eater, also referred to as the Cursed Rune, was inherited by Ted, survivor of an attack on his village, the Village of the Hidden Rune, some 300 years prior. The rune's power would be used by Ted on Mt. Seifu, which alerted Windy to his presence. Attacked and wounded, Ted managed to pass the rune onto McDohl. The rune is said to have power over life and death.

Windy wanted the rune so she could use it in conjunction with her own Gate Rune to take her revenge on the world. Said to be cursed, the rune is sometimes credited with causing wars and stealing the souls of those close to its bearer in order to increase its power. In fact, Ted declared its very intentions to be malevolent.

The grief it causes in its bearers is said to be the source of its power and it has been speculated that this in turn gives 'life' to its bearer, hence its name. There are also theories that the rune will eventually consume the souls of its bearer, but the truth behind this is unclear. These fears led to its epitheth as the Soul Eater.

The Soul Eater would continue to maintain some sort of connection with surviving past bearers. Ted was able to command the rune to take his soul even when the rune was borne by McDohl. It also appeared to weaken the effects of other runes in some fashion.

Leknaat cautioned against the use of the Soul Eater even at the last battle when she was unable to remove all of Windy's 80,000 strong monster army by herself. Following the defeat of Barbarossa Rugner Windy made a final attempt to seize the Cursed Rune but was rebuffed by the Soul Eater itself.

McDohl continued to bear the rune when he left the Toran Republic following the end of the Gate Rune War. Part of his motivation for leaving was his concern that the rune would bring further misfortune to those around him.

After leaving, he would try not to use the rune, fearing its power, but would be p

name: Flik
description: Flik (フリック, Furikku) is a character in Suikoden and Suikoden II who also makes a cameo in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.1.

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
Unsurprisingly, given his sobriquet, Flik wears a blue cape and headband. In Suikoden, he wears these over a breastplate, a mustard shirt, blue pants, and high, brown boots. In Suikoden II, he instead wears a pale blue jacket along with black pants and boots, with blue leg guards. He wears brown gloves.

Physically, Flik was said to be a handsome man, a trait that got him unwanted attention from women at times, including with Kimberly, Rina, and Nina. Kawano Junko drew Flik's final design to her own personal tastes after her first design was rejected for not being bishonen enough. His nickname, Blue Thunder Flik, was decided before his design, influencing his blue

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
Though Flik grew up in Warriors' Village and followed some of their traditions, such as the naming of his sword, he did not frequently return to the village and was not considered to have completed his trial of manhood.

Before meeting Odessa, Flik considered himself willfully ignorant of the Empire's wrong-doings, finding comfort in Warriors' Village autonomy and history of producing high-quality soldiers of the Imperial Army.

Flik was deeply in love with Odessa Silverberg. When Flik learned of Odessa's death, he went into a state of shock. He felt a great deal of guilt that he had not become the leader that he felt Odessa had wanted him to be and decided that he would have to become a better man before he met Odessa in the afterlife.

Odessa remained on Flik's mind throughout much of the Toran Liberation War. Despite her death, he considered himself unavailable, and even late into the night prior to the Battle at Floating Fortress Shasarazade, he was still up late into the night thinking of Odessa. 

Flik had a somewhat rocky relationship with McDohl; unwilling to accept him into the Toran Liberation Army at first, Flik could not understand why Odessa trusted McDohl on important assignments. After joining forces, Flik began to notice the traits Odessa had seen in McDohl. Flik became quite protective of McDohl, joining him on missions and even taking an arrow for him in Gregminster Palace.

During much of the Toran Liberation War, Flik was immature and hotheaded. Sanchez claimed that that immaturity was something that both he and Odessa had loved about Flik, as he was also a very honest person.

Due to that immaturity and rashness, Flik made some careless decisions, such as his ordering all troops to advance to Scarleticia Castle following the Toran Liberation Army's victory at the Fortress of Garan without doing any reconnaissance.

Flik would become more mature…

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Rebel Soldier
thumb|frameless|180px|right|Flik c.453 as depicted in Suikoden STAR LEAP
Flik is a wandering swordsman. Born and raised in Warriors' Village, Flik left the village on his Manhood Ceremony and eventually became a mercenary. Around SY 453, after a number of years working as a mercenary, Flik saved Odessa Silverberg from some Scarlet Moon Empire soldiers while on the road to Gregminster. Enraptured, he quickly joined her cause and eventually became Vice-Commander of the Toran Liberation Army.

As a swordsman, he gained the title "Blue Thunder Flik" due to his blue clothing and sharp swordsmanship. Over time, he and Odessa would fall in love with one another and become a couple all while Flik continued to serve as a soldier in the fledgling Liberation Army.

When the hideout at Lenankamp was attacked, Flik fled to Kunan. Flik tried to round up the scattered former soldiers of the Toran Liberation Army, but when there was a sudden crackdown on rebel factions within the area, many of these former soldiers were captured. After hearing of a revived Toran Liberation Army at Toran Castle, Flik mustered his remaining troops and headed there. 

Once he arrived, Flik heard of Odessa's death and was overcome with grief. Flik was unable to accept McDohl as the leader of the Toran Liberation Army but after calming down and realizing how his reaction would have disappointed Odessa, agreed to join forces to help rescue their comrades who had been captured. Flik's hotheadedness remained an issue as he led the Toran Liberation Army to a dismal defeat at Scarleticia Castle. 

However, Flik gradually came to mature and accept McDohl as a leader. When McDohl made the unpopular decision to pardon Milich Oppenheimer, Flik supported him and accepted him as the true leader of the Liberation Army. From that moment on, Flik would fight as a productive, invaluable member of the…

recruitment: Recruited automatically in Kaku after defeating Kwanda Rosman.
trivia:
• In the English version of Suikoden, Flik is forced to spend an evening drinking tea with Kimberly, but in the Japanese version, the two spend the evening consuming sake.
• Similarly, Sanchez offers Flik tea in the English version, but in the Japanese version, Sanchez offers Flik good wine.
• He is known as Blue Thunder Flik due to a combination of his outfit, skill and speed with a sword and his use of a Lightning Rune.

name: Gremio
description: Gremio (グレミオ, Guremio) is a supporting character in Suikoden who makes a cameo in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Appears in
facts.value: Suikoden, Suikoden II, Suikoden STAR LEAP

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
Gremio is a slight man with long, blond hair and green eyes. His hair is tied into a ponytail at the back. Despite his small stature, Gremio is quite strong, able to wield his battle axe deftly. He has two scars on his face, meeting in a cross on his left cheek, from when he was captured by thugs who had captured the young McDohl.

He wore a large, stylish, green cloak, matching the color of his eyes, over a simple blue outfit. His axe has a long shaft, gifting Gremio a variety of ways to attack, such as cutting, poking, hooking and striking. Given Gremio's reluctance to fight, it's possible that the size of the axe head is for intimidation

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
Gremio was entirely loyal and dutiful towards the hero, whom he always called Young Master. He saw something of himself in his charge, as both had lost their mothers at early ages. At first, Gremio looked after McDohl as part of his gratitude to Teo but came to develop his own familial love for the hero.

Due to the incident involving McDohl's kidnapping as a child, Gremio was highly protective of him, willing to throw his body in the way to shield him if necessary. As the hero grew older, Gremio realized that he was not needed as much in that regard, but still wished to be near him. In truth, he himself was uncomfortable if not near the Young Master.

Gremio was an excellent cook, whose stew recipes came from his mother, though his own special stew was an original creation. Although a capable fighter, he was never happier than when working in the kitchen, preparing good food for the household. Other duties in the household included taking care of the finances, something that would lead him into hot water with the debt-collector

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Steward of a Scion
Gremio originally lived with his parents near the Scarlet Moon border with the City-States of Jowston. In SY 439, when he was nine, his parents were killed during a border dispute. His mother protected him from the Jowston soldiers' blades, dying as a result. Alone, Gremio spent the next seven years of his life wandering. Teo McDohl found him lost in the Karakas Desert and, seeing Gremio's good character, brought him to Gregminster.

There, he would work as a servant as well as helping to raise Teo's son. Initially, he worked merely to repay his debt to Teo but quickly came to truly care for his young charge. The axe Gremio wielded was originally his father's, who had used it in battle himself.

The large gash on his cheek came from an incident three months into Gremio's tenure, during the War of Succession. Teo's son was kidnapped by Imperial Army soldiers under the employ of Geil Rugner in order to prevent Teo McDohl from attacking. Gremio attempted to prevent the abduction but was easily felled and tied up by Assam's men.

Gremio was kept alive to deliver Assam's ultimatum. Either Teo's famed Armored Cavalry would meet Geil's force in a decisive battle at the Fortress of Kwaba without their famed armor, or the young McDohl would be killed. His face was scarred as a mark of this ultimatum.

After being brought to Teo and relaying the ultimatum, Gremio would sneak back to Gregminster in order to rescue his young charge. Caught, he would almost be killed only to be rescued by Eiji. Eiji would help Gremio escape to Sarady, where the two would uncover McDohl's whereabouts. Gremio rescued the young child from the Sarady inn, slaying the bandit kidnappers with the help of Eiji and Ronettes, receiving his second facial scar in the fight.

After the rescue, Gremio engraved the words "I will always protect young master" onto his axe, giving it the name…

recruitment: Recruited automatically before your journey to Magician's Island.
trivia: • Unlike Cleo and Pahn, Gremio does not have a diary that can be read in Suikoden.

name: Odessa Silverberg
originalName: オデッサ・シルバーバーグ
description: Odessa Silverberg (オデッサ・シルバーバーグ, Odessa Shirubābāgu) is a major character in Suikoden. Odessa is the leader of the Toran Liberation Army whose strength and ideals inspired the rebel force throughout the war.

facts.label: Family
facts.value: George Silverberg (cousin), Leon Silverberg (uncle), Mathiu Silverberg (brother), One youn

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
Odessa is a young woman with long chestnut-brown hair and green eyes (though they have also been depicted as blue and hazel). In her youth as an Imperial noble, she seems to have mainly worn long dresses.

As the leader of the Toran Liberation Army, she wears a grey tunic fitted to her waist by a brown corset. Under this she wears white leggings, and her outfit is accented with brown gloves and boots. To complete her outfit, she wears a red cape as well as a small circlet around his head.

In battle, Odessa wields a common bow, holding it in her left hand, marking her as

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
Odessa was noted for her tenderness and strength, as well as her determination never to turn her back on those who needed aid. These attributes drove her to fall for Achilles and, later, Flik. These attributes bolstered her reputation with those sympathetic to her cause. 

As leader of the Toran Liberation Army, she garnered support and sympathy from scores of people suffering under the Imperial regime. She rarely confided in people as she felt she needed to maintain a certain aura as the leader of the liberation movement. That said, she had an open-heart and was very trusting of people. Flik would even claim she could be overly trusting.

She was also capable of holding a grudge. Her split with her brother, Mathiu, was acrimonious and even when she entrusted McDohl to him for help, her words indicated that she never fully forgave what she condemned as his cowardice.

Odessa had a habit of grinding her teeth while sleeping. When McDohl's group accompanied Odessa to Sarady, he had to listen to Odessa's teeth grinding at night when they stayed in the inn

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Odessa Silverberg is the founder and original leader of the Toran Liberation Army. Despite her birth into an aristocratic family, Odessa developed a sincere concern for how people were suffering under the corruption of the Scarlet Moon Empire after meeting Achilles, who was involved in an anti-imperial movement. 

She was once close with her brother, Mathiu, even being tutored in military strategy by him. However, her entry into sedition led to a falling out between the two, Odessa believing him to be a coward for turning his back on suffering and refusing to use his considerable talents, with the two never reconciling. Odessa eventually became Achilles' fiancée and joined the movement, helping him raise a war orphan named Michel. 

Achilles was arrested on trumped-up charges of fugitive-harboring due to his role in the anti-imperial movement. Odessa attempted to save her fiancée; demanding Achilles evoke his "last rights" to marry Odessa before his execution. Granted the final request, she would stage a daring escape attempt on the wedding day, fighting through Imperial soldiers clad in her wedding dress, wielding a sword.

Tragically, Achilles was felled by Imperial archers in the attempt. Odessa managed to escape the capital and, finding refuge in Lenankamp, founded the Toran Liberation Army with the express goal of opposing misrule and overthrowing the Imperial regime, beginning the Gate Rune War.

Over the next two years, the Liberation Army grew in size. Odessa laid the foundation for future strategies years in advance, including the building of the Secret Factory as well as the purchase of the plans for the Fire Spear. 

As the leader of the Liberation Army, Odessa was a symbol of its ideals, being idolized as its "star of hope". Although one of many rebel groups operating in the Empire, she would cooperate with other rebel leaders, such as Zella and Alec,…

recruitment: Joins automatically following plot events in Lenankamp.
trivia:
thumb|right|220px|Ronnie Bell has competition.
• In the English-language localization of Suikoden, the rumormonger in Sarady claims Odessa is a 9-foot-tall giant. In the original Japanese, he claims she is three meters tall, which is closer to 10 feet tall.
• Ted shares the same weapon name as Odessa in Suikoden. They are stated to be common, mass-produced bows and are difficult for blacksmiths to work with.
• In true Warriors' Village tradition, Flik named his sword after Odessa, that which is most dear to him.

name: Mathiu Silverberg
originalName: マッシュ・シルバーバーグ
description: Mathiu Silverberg (マッシュ・シルバーバーグ, Masshu Shirubābāgu) is a main character in Suikoden. Mathiu Silverberg was the military strategist for the Toran Liberation Army during the Gate Rune War.

facts.label: Family
facts.value: George (cousin), Leon (uncle), Odessa (sister), One younger brother

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
Mathiu has a strong jawline and high cheekbones. His medium-length brown hair is slicked back, revealing a high forehead, and narrow eyes, often shown as closed in artwork.

His attire consists of a beige coat with gold and brown accents, a high collar, and a front closure. He wears a red scarf around his neck. Underneath, he dons dark pants and knee-high brown

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Early Career and Seclusion
Mathiu Silverberg was a renowned military tactician of the illustrious Silverberg family, celebrated for his brilliance and unyielding dedication to strategy.

A quiet and contemplative man, Mathiu served as a strategist under Kasim Hazil during the War of Succession, earning renown for his intellect and loyalty to the Scarlet Moon Empire. However, the Kalekka Incident, a massacre of civilians, left him deeply haunted, prompting him to abandon Imperial service and retreat to Seika.

Mathiu would establish a school in Seika, taking in local children and orphans and giving them a general education. Mathiu also travelled to the Grasslands for reasons unknown, before returning to Seika in the year 453. Among his students were Apple and Shu, both of whom would later become distinguished strategists in their own right.

Mathiu's refusal to join the Toran Liberation Army, led by his sister Odessa Silverberg, caused a rift between the siblings, one that Odessa never forgave. This schism also led Shu to leave Mathiu's tutelage, further isolating the strategist.

After Odessa's death, Mathiu initially refused to discuss her legacy with McDohl, the young leader of the Liberation Army. However, when Imperial soldiers, acting on Kasim Hazil's orders, attempted to take one of his students hostage to force his return to service, Mathiu realized he could no longer remain detached from the world.

Following the confrontation, he joined the Liberation Army as its chief strategist, vowing to fulfill Odessa's vision through McDohl.
Master Strategist
As strategist, Mathiu transformed the Liberation Army from a disjointed collection of rebels into a formidable fighting force. His ability to unite disparate factions, assign roles to officers, and devise innovative strategies proved instrumental in the war effort.

He was unafraid to act decisively, as seen in his…

recruitment: Recruited automatically following story events in Seika.
trivia:
• Mathiu also had a named student who had not become a famous strategist, by the name of Finn. Finn was the first student to befriend Apple when she started attending Mathiu's school.
• In the 1996 English language localization of Suikoden, Mathiu is sometimes referred to as a doctor, and at other times as a strategist. This is a translation error; Mathiu does not have any medical background and he is only a military strategist. This is not his only translation woe; Mathiu's line "Oh my god. All this killing... in front of a children." is one of the series most famous quotes.

name: Jeane
description: Jeane is a mysterious runemistress who has appeared in each of the five main games of the Suikoden series as the Chiketsu Star. She also appears in Genso Suikogaiden Vol. 1 and Suikoden Tactics.

name: Neclord
description: Neclord (ネクロード, Nekurōdo) is an antagonist in Suikoden and Suikoden II. Neclord is a vampire with a penchant for taking victims as his brides.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Neclord was a vampire who betrayed his brethren and cursed them to a painful death or a life of feeding on humans when he stole the Moon Rune from its shrine in the Blue Moon Village. Since then, he has been hunted by the original Moon Rune bearer, Sierra Mikain.

Neclord traveled the land, striking villages with his powers, including the creation of zombies, and kidnapping women to be his "bride" before feasting on them. He also held other eccentricities, such as his playing of the pipe organ. His greatest joy was seeing his victim's despair and the invicibility he felt as bearer of the Moon Rune.

North Window, the hometown of Viktor was one such victim of his massacres, inspiring a life-long pursuit of revenge. In SY 449, he would choose Daisy as his 65th bride, taking her and destroying the village in the process. Viktor would attempt to rescue Daisy but would be swiftly defeated and left for dead. Daisy's subsequent taking of her own life merely bemused him.

Other victims also became dedicated to his demise, such as the Marley family of vampire hunters. During the Gate Rune War, Neclord was appointed as the magistrate of Lorimar as he was an old comrade of Windy. There, he would kill the Imperial officers under his charge, turning them all into loyal zombie minions.

Neclord traveled to Warriors' Village and attempted to take Tengaar as one of his brides. The Toran Liberation Army attempted to stop him but were defeated without landing a single blow. However, the army would learn of the Celestial Sword, and obtaining it, were able to defeat the vampire.

However, despite appearances, Neclord had not, in fact, been killed. Neclord had used his "Doppelganger Secret", a technique that meant that the Neclord felled during the Gate Rune War was not the real Neclord. He would reappear at North Window during the Dunan Unification War before disappearing when…

trivia: • Neclord has had at least 69 "brides" prior to his abductions of Tengaar and Lilly Pendragon. Nice.

name: Riou
description: The hero (主人公, Shujinkō), known by several different names, is the protagonist of Suikoden II and Genso Suikoden Card Stories. He also makes cameo appearances in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.1 and Vol.2.

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
220px|thumb|left|Facial expression art from Genso Suikogaiden Vol.1
The hero is a young man with short brown hair and hazel eyes. He wears a red tunic with white piping, tied together by a white waistband. He accompanies this outfit with a yellow cloth around his shoulders and brown gloves. This cloth was worn by Genkaku in his youth and was passed down to the hero.

Under his tunic, the hero wears calve-length black spats and high brown boots. He wears a golden circlet around his forehead, received as a child when Genkaku bought it for him at the first fair he had ever seen. The hero wields twin wooden tonfa as his weapon of choice. Fashioned by Genkaku, they were an uncomplicated make, tipped with metal at both ends.

The hero's build was regarded as slim. When meeting Gustav Pendragon, Gustav remarked that many youths his age had arms twice as big as his. As someone raised in Kyaro Town, the hero had a noticeable Highland accent.

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
The hero was noted for his strength, but also his lack of belligerence. He fought solely to protect others, as the Bright Shield Rune he inherited symbolizes. Living up to the expectations of those who relied on his strength was a major motivation for him to continue fighting even through the despair of the Dunan Unification War.

As a citizen of the Highland Kingdom and a member of its Unicorn Brigade, he had no little loyalty and feeling towards the land of his birth. However, the destruction of Toto Village truly cemented the need to fight against Highland's aggression in his mind. Still, he hoped to someday return to his home in peace.

The idea of "family" was central to the hero and his motivations also. As an orphan, the family he had found with Genkaku and Nanami, as well as Jowy, was extremely important to him. Later, during the Dunan Unification War, he would see his comrades as part of another family, spurring him to continue fighting for them.

Shu saw in him the hope of the people of Jowston, not merely as the son of Genkaku, but as his own person. Because of this, Shu would push the hero to become the leader of the New Alliance Army despite his youth. It was said his true strength was his uncanny ability to touch the hearts and earn the trust of his comrades. His kind-heartedness inspired others to help him in turn.

Even were he to abandon his duties and flee with Nanami from Tinto Principality, it is likely that this too would be an attempt to fulfil her wish, as he felt great anguish and guilt about all the worry and danger his path placed Nanami in. In the end, his lack of hostility is what would allow both he and Jowy to survive their duel at Tenzan Pass.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Early Life
The hero was a war orphan who was adopted and raised by Genkaku at his dojo in Kyaro Town. Nanami was also picked up by Genkaku in similar circumstances and in short order, a close brother-sister relationship was formed. When the two were old enough, Genkaku trained both in martial arts. The two continued to grow healthy and progress smoothly in their martial arts training.

The hero and Jowy Atreides first met one summer day when they were both young. Both boys had never been part of the normal children's circle, being shunned for different reasons. The two found a sense of belonging with one another and they soon became best friends. The two ended up studying martial arts together, along with Nanami.

Following Genkaku's death, he enlisted in the Unicorn Brigade alongside Jowy. While Jowy had enlisted for honor, the hero had signed up to earn his keep. The two were equally matched, tying in the final of a tournament of Unicorn Brigade officers after a long bout. They excelled in the brigade, and both believed that their futures lay in the Highland Army.
Victim of War
On the eve of their return home, the Unicorn Brigade came under a night attack at Tenzan Pass. Attempting to flee, the hero and Jowy discovered that the raid was not conducted by the City-States of Jowston, but by Luca Blight under the guise of an attack, a betrayal. The two were eventually corned on a cliff by Captain Rowd.

With no other choice, they made a mark on a nearby rock and promise to meet again here if they ever get separated before jumping desperately into the rapids below. He was discovered by Viktor and Flik and taken to their Mercenary Fortress. He would escape with Jowy's aid and return to Kyaro Town.

There, he was reunited with his sister, Nanami. Realizing that Rowd had…

trivia: • The hero has finished at a highest of fourth in the annual [https://twitter.com/gensosenkyo Gensosenkyo fan poll] in 2018. His lowest rank is sixth, which he claimed in 2019 and 2020.
recruitment: Automatically joins.

name: Jowy Atreides
originalName: ジョウイ ・アトレイド
description: Jowy Atreides (ジョウイ ・アトレイド, Joui Atoreido) is a major character in Suikoden II and Genso Suikoden Card Stories who also appears in both Genso Suikogaiden Vol.1 and Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2.

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
Jowy is a young man of slender build, with grey eyes and long, blond hair usually tied into a long ponytail. He usually wore a blue armless top, white pants with a mustard sash belt, and similarly colored boots. He also wore brown bracers on his arms.

After his return to the Highland military, Jowy would wear a mostly white outfit, with a long white coat and white boots over black pants and a blue undershirt. His normal weapon was a long, blue staff with silver dragon heads holding jewels tipping both ends, giving it a luxurious feel. It may have been bought by his father or mother.

As a Highland officer, he carried a sword, perhaps because it served him better as a commanding officer or maybe because he was hesitant to use his childhood weapon in his new position. He would take up his staff again at the end of the Dunan Unification War when he made for Tenzan Pass.

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
Jowy was regarded as a peace-loving idealist by many but was nonetheless prepared to betray his own morals to make his dreams a reality.

Although he did truly hate Luca Blight for his cruelty and evil deeds, Jowy was also fascinated by his strength and hoped to procure a similar strength that he could wield to protect others, not destroy them. In the end, Jowy's convictions led him to the conclusion that only power could establish a peaceful land.

Living in a loveless household, where even his mother could not openly show him much affection, Jowy always harbored an enviousness towards the home life of the hero, Nanami, and Genkaku. They were not related by blood yet cherished each other regardless.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
The Early Days
Jowy was not an Atreides by birth, but rather the result of a previous relation his mother, Rosa, had before marrying Marcel Atreides. As such, Jowy was never fully accepted as a family member by his stepfather or stepbrother. Despite this, Jowy had a relatively happy childhood thanks to his friends; the son of Genkaku and Nanami. Young Jowy also held a crush for the young Princess Jillia Blight. 

Jowy entered the Highland Army's youth division, the Unicorn Brigade, along with his friend and was eventually assigned to Tenzan Pass following the truce between Highland and Jowston. Following the massacre of the Unicorn Brigade by Luca Blight, Jowy washed up in Jowston and was rescued in Toto Village by Joanna, Marx and Pilika. He then went to rescue his friend who was being held by Viktor and Flik but ended up being captured as well. 

Eventually, the duo escaped and returned to Kyaro Town only for Jowy to be rejected by his family. Led away by Highland soldiers, Jowy would cry out to see his mother, only to be rebuffed by Marcel and Marco. This would be the last time Jowy would see his family.

He and the hero would then be marched through Kyaro as captured spies. Passing through, the carriage of Princess Jillia stopped the procession and interrogated the two as to their motives. Jowy would protest their innocence during this first meeting between the two.

Jowy would be sentenced alongside his friend to be executed as spies. Luckily, they, along with Nanami and Mukumuku were rescued by Viktor and Flik. Jowy ended up working alongside Viktor's mercenary force and aided Jowston in their struggle against Highland. Following the fall of Viktor's Mercenary Fortress, Jowy received the Black Sword Rune, half of the Rune of Beginning, in the ruins of Toto, while…

trivia:
• In Greek mythology, the "Atreides" are the descendants of the mythological Greek king Atreus. The name Atreides referred to his sons, Menelas and Agamemnon and their descendants in turn.
• Jowy wields a Star Staff during his tenure in your group. The second and third levels of his weapon, the Heaven Staff and Heavenly Star Staff, can only be obtained through some sort of game-hack. Jowy departs long before his weapon can be modified that high, and when he makes one more return appearance later in the game, he is using an unnamed sword. 
• Jowy's name is misspelled as "Joei" at some point in conversations by both Pilika and Nanami later in the game.
• No other character in Suikoden II…

name: Nanami
description: Nanami (ナナミ, Nanami) is a major character in Suikoden II and Genso Suikoden Card Stories who also appears in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.1 and Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2.

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
Nanami has short brown hair, pulled back with a pink headband, and brown eyes. Her torso clothing is pink, with cream-colored shoulders. She wears pale blue pants and brown shoes, as well as white gloves. She also has a white sash around her waist, accented with pale blue.

A soldier at Muse City once described her, rather unkindly, as a pug-nosed, whiny, frog-faced tomboy. Still, Sheena held something of an interest in her, indicating that perhaps the soldier was not critiquing her appearance in good faith.

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
A bubbly young tomboy, Nanami believed herself capable of acting ladylike, but was more frequently considered a child. She expressed her emotions with her whole body, making her very easy to read. Nanami was also a terrible liar and actress, completely unable to charm or fool the guards of North Swallow Pass and Muse City when attempting to pass through their checkpoints.

Trained by Genkaku, Nanami was a talented martial artist with a strong work ethic, training during the Dunan Unification War through the night. Nanami boasted such skill that even the hero himself had never won a practice round with his adopted sister (Though it seems likely that Nanami dismisses 'unlucky' losses).

Nanami used her skills to try to protect her brother, escorting him on missions to keep him safe even with professional soldiers and mercenaries such as Viktor and Flik by his side. 

Nanami was a great fan of cooking, despite her own mediocre talent. She attempted to make a meal for Nash Latkje while in Muse City, to thank him for finding a lost Pilika. The ensuing meal knocked Nash out instantly, forcing him to retire to bed. To the amazement of many, the hero was a fan of Nanami's meals, having grown up eating them.

As schooling and formal education were normally reserved for the wealthy and privileged in both Highland and Jowston, she admired schooling to an extent and was very excited to attend the New Leaf Academy during the infiltration of Greenhill City.

Nanami got along well with Apple, both due to her educated nature and their shared experience of being outsiders within the City-States of Jowston. She was also very protective of Pilika, treating her as family, partly due to Pilika's bond with Jowy. She held a certain apathy towards Shu and other leaders, for what she saw as…

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Nanami is a boisterous war orphan who was adopted and trained in martial arts by Genkaku, along with the hero. Although they are not related by blood, the bond between the orphans was said to be even stronger than that of family. She always considered herself the older sister, despite neither sibling knowing their true age.

Despite the distance of the townsfolk, Nanami and the hero enjoyed a happy childhood, becoming close friends to Jowy Atreides. At the same fair where the hero got his signature circlet, Genkaku had bought Nanami a straw hat. However, she promptly ate it due to hunger. Her chosen weapon, a three-piece staff with each segment connected by metal rings, was also a gift from Genkaku.

When Jowy and the hero signed up for the Unicorn Brigade, Nanami remained in Kyaro Town, taking over the upkeep of the family dojo. She had hopes of one day reopening the dojo with herself as the instructor, taking after Genkaku.

She was worried when she heard that the Unicorn Brigade had been wiped out, but never doubted that her brother would be okay. When the hero returned, now accused of having been a spy in the massacre of the unit, Nanami immediately fled without hesitation with him to the City-States of Jowston.

Nanami joined Viktor's mercenaries along with the hero. When Jowy was captured during a reconnaissance mission in the Highland camp on behalf of Muse City, Nanami decided to be the first to greet him when he returned. As the sun set on Muse, Nanami and the hero reminisced with each other about Jowy and growing up together. She would be overjoyed by Jowy's return.

After having witnessed the atrocities of Luca Blight in Toto Village and the death of Anabelle, Nanami began to long to run away from war and live a quiet life, rebuilding the dojo in Kyaro Town.…

trivia:
• The attack Nanami threatens the guards in Kyaro Town with is Genkaku's Golden Bird Holy Flower Dragon Tooth Glory Punch.
• Nanami has two recipes named after her: Nanami Ice Cream and Nanami Cake, which mix Salt with Ice Cream and Cake, respectively. These foods heal HP, but also have a chance of causing the Panic status effect.
• If you try and pass through the North Swallow Pass after Nanami joins, she will try to replicate Rina's infamous convincing of the guards, but with more violence and less successful results.
recruitment: Talk with her in Kyaro, and she will join. Alternatively, if you refuse her assistance, she will join later when you go to Muse.

name: Luca Blight
description: Luca Blight (ルカ・ブライト, Ruka Buraito) is the major antagonist of Suikoden II and Genso Suikoden Card Stories who also appears in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.1. The Prince, and later King, of Highland, Luca Blight orchestrates the Dunan Unification War out of hatred for the world.

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
Luca is a tall, powerfully built man, with dark black hair and small, dark eyes. He has strong black eyebrows.

He is always seen in his royal armor, which was white, the color of Highland, with gold trim, with a royal blue cape. His armor consisted of a breastplate, pauldrons, bracers, greaves, and boots. Underneath he wore all black underclothes. He carried a longsword in a scabbard attached to the waist on his left side, marking him as right-handed.

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
Luca's cold, violent, and brutal character earned him the nickname the "Mad Prince", and he was feared by all, ally and enemy alike. His only joy came from destruction and death, and his greatest joy was reserved for unleashing the power of the Beast Rune. His hatred for the weak was speculated to stem from despising his own weakness as a child.

That said, he was also considered a general of the highest caliber and not capriciously cruel to his soldiers. He never killed a subordinate just because he didn't like them. However, he was an extreme meritocrat and had no mercy for those he viewed as "incompetent".

Luca was considered an incredibly skilled swordsman as well as a supremely strong and brave fighter. However, he never opened his heart to anyone and always fought alone. He despised his father, Agares, above anyone else but in truth held contempt in his heart for everyone. Even his sister, Jillia, was only spared due to her resemblance to his mother.

As a child, he could be temperamental and easily embarrassed but was otherwise an ordinary boy. He loved and respected both his parents and was sad that he could not spend much time with them due to their official duties.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Path of Darkness
Luca Blight was a man of unparalleled ferocity and cunning, feared and revered by both allies and enemies alike. His brutal nature and martial prowess earned him the title "Mad Prince," a name that encapsulated his demonic reputation. As a leader and warrior, he commanded the unwavering loyalty of the Highland Army, though his methods were as ruthless as they were effective.

Luca's hatred for the City-States of Jowston and the world at large was born from the traumas of his youth. Though his early childhood was unremarkable, Luca was known for his volatile temperament and prodigious skill with a blade. Trained in swordsmanship by Han Cunningham, he was regarded as one of Highland's finest swordsmen by the age of eleven.

In the year SY 443, when Luca was ten years old, the royal caravan of the Blight family was ambushed by a group of brigands. Though disguised as bandits, their equipment and discipline betrayed their origins as soldiers trained by Muse.

During the attack, Luca's father, King Agares Blight, fled the scene, leaving Luca and his mother, Sara, to be captured. Luca was forced to hide as his mother was violated by the mob, an experience that scarred him deeply. Though they were eventually rescued, the incident ignited Luca's hatred for his father, the City-States, and the world itself.

Over time, Luca's misanthropy grew, and he began to revel in violence, seeing it as a means to assert the strength his father lacked. He exacted vengeance by slaughtering the brigands responsible for his mother's suffering, but her descent into catatonia shattered his heart. From that moment, Luca cast aside his emotions, embracing his strength and cruelty as his defining traits.

Though Luca initially displayed open contempt for his father, he learned to…

trivia:
• Luca Blight's sword is stated to be coated with some sort of flammable material. This material is what allows his sword to ignite in flames when using special attacks on the party. 
• Luca Blight's strength and prowess was modeled after [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Xiang_Yu Xiang Yu], who is considered by many to be the strongest general in Chinese history.

name: Bright Shield Rune
description: The Bright Shield Rune (輝く盾の紋章, Kagayaku Tate no Monshō) is one of the 27 True Runes. It is the rune of the protagonist of Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Bright Shield Rune was a rune inherited by the leader of the New Alliance Army as the Dunan Unification War began to intensify. It originally comprised half of the Rune of Beginning, along with the Black Sword Rune, which was inherited by Jowy Atreides.

Although considered a True Rune, it does not confer eternal youth while split in two. The rune is destined to come into conflict with the person who bears the Black Sword Rune. In comparison to the aggressive Black Sword Rune, the Bright Shield Rune holds many powerful curative spells and abilities. Like its counterpart, use of the rune chips away at the life of its bearer.

Rune scholars interested in the nature of True Runes debate whether the two runes choose their bearers based on their personalities or if the runes instead manipulated the bearers to take paths reflecting the runes they bore.

Genkaku held the rune in the past but sealed it in Toto Village, along with Han Cunningham's Black Sword Rune. Once Toto was destroyed during the Dunan Unification War, the runes were inherited by Jowy and Genkaku's adopted son. The rune would become something of a symbol for the New Alliance Army during the war.

name: Black Sword Rune
description: The Black Sword Rune (黒き刃の紋章, Kuroki Yaiba no Monshō, Black Blade Rune) is one of the 27 True Runes. It is the rune of Jowy Atreides.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Black Sword Rune was a rune inherited by Jowy Atreides as the Dunan Unification War began to intensify. It originally comprised half of the Rune of Beginning, along with the Bright Shield Rune, which was inherited by the leader of the New Alliance Army.

Although considered a True Rune, it does not confer eternal youth while split in two. The rune is destined to come into conflict with the person who bears the Bright Shield Rune. The rune possesses powerful attack magic, able to decimate large groups or focus on single foes.

Han Cunningham held the rune in the past but sealed it in Toto Village, along with Genkaku's Bright Shield Rune. Once Toto was destroyed during the Dunan Unification War, the runes were inherited by Jowy and Genkaku's adopted son.

name: Gate Rune War
description: The Gate Rune War (門の紋章戦争, Mon no Monshō Sensō), also referred to less grandiosely as the Toran Liberation War (トラン解放戦争 Toran Kaihō Sensō), was a significant civil war that occured in the Toran region between the Scarlet Moon Empire and the Toran Liberation Army. The events of the war form the setting of Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Following the War of Succession, Emperor Barbarossa Rugner shifted his focus away from politics, leading to an increase in corrupt bureaucrats within the Scarlet Moon Empire, causing public order to deteriorate, and allowing piracy and banditry to flourish.

Growing dissatisfaction with the empire culminated in SY 453 with the establishment of the Toran Liberation Army, founded by Odessa Silverberg. Although other resistance and anti-imperial groups existed before and after, this growing Liberation Movement marked a turning point, sparking open dissent and turmoil, eventually becoming the Gate Rune War.

After several years of rebel activity, the Scarlet Moon Empire would respond. The unexpected raid on the Liberation Army's hideout in Lenankamp in 455 resulted in the death of Odessa, and the leadership was taken up by Teo McDohl's son, the hero.

With the strategic guidance of Mathiu Silverberg, the initially small resistance group evolved into a formidable military force. The rebellion gained momentum, forming powerful alliances and liberating numerous regions from the control of the Scarlet Moon Empire.

The war lasted for a total of four years - two years under Odessa's leadership and two years after the founding of the New Liberation Army. It culminated in SY 457 with the defeat of Barbarossa and the fall of Gregminster in the final battle.

Following the Scarlet Moon Empire's defeat, the Toran Republic emerged. Many war veterans later joined the New Alliance Army during the Dunan Unification War.

name: Dunan Unification War
description: The Dunan Unification War (デュナン統一戦争 Deyunan Touitsu Sensou) is a major war between the Highland Kingdom and the City-States of Jowston in the Dunan region. The events of the war occupy much of Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Dunan Unification War broke out in 460 in the wake of a plot by Highland Prince Luca Blight to incite war by slaughtering Unicorn Brigade troops and blaming it on the City-States.

This war gave birth to the New Alliance Army, following the collapse of the Jowston Alliance following the invasion of Muse City. The war also proved historically important as it saw the death of King Agares, the ascension of his son, Luca, to the throne and his swift usurpation by Jowy Atreides. Despite early advances by the Highland Army, including the capture of Greenhill City, the momentum shifted shortly after the establishment of the New Alliance Army and the war concluded the same year with the fall of the Highland capital, L'Renouille.

The war would be ended with the annexation of Highland and the proclamation of a new Dunan Republic.

name: Toran Republic
description: The Toran Republic (トラン共和国 Toran Republic) is a country located in the southeast region of the Northern Continent. Its capital city is the city of Gregminster, located northeast of Lake Toran in the center of the nation. Toran borders the Dunan Republic to the north, a number of unknown territories to the west, Kanakan via an ocean border to the southwest an

name: Gregminster
description: Gregminster (グレッグミンスター, Guregguminsutā) is a city that appears in both Suikoden and Suikoden II as well as the upcoming Suikoden STAR LEAP. It is the capital of the Toran Republic and its predecessor, the Scarlet Moon Empire.

facts.label: Nation
facts.value: Holy Kingdom of Harmonia (until 230), Scarlet Moon Empire (230-457), Toran Republic (457 onwards)

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The city of Gregminster stands on the former site of the Harmonian Holy City of Rupanda. In Solar Year 230, when the Emperor Kranach Rugner founded the Scarlet Moon Empire, the city was renamed Gregminster.

For over 200 years, Gregminster would serve as the empire's political and cultural center. 

Gregminster was virtually destroyed during the War of Succession of 446 as Geil Rugner's own bandit forces ransacked and looted the city while imposing martial law and harsh curfews. But the rise of Barbarossa Rugner to the throne saw the city rebuilt, more beautiful than before.

Commerce drives the local economy, with artworks and pottery flourishing as key specialties under protectionist policies once established by Emperor Barbarossa. The architecture of the city reflects this, and all houses are built with ivory-colored walls and contrasting red doors.

As the city prospered, Gregminster housed many high-ranking officials of the Empire, giving rise to a sense of superiority from inhabitants of the city. A monthly market was held in the center of the city.

Gregminster suffered greatly during the Gate Rune War, the city eventually becoming isolated from the rest of the nation. Eventually, after a final climatic battle, Gregminster fell to the forces of the Toran Liberation Army, ending the war. Following the war, Gregminster remained the capital, now of the Toran Republic. Since then, President Lepant has continued to develop the city as a cultural and commercial center.

name: Pico
description: Pico (ピコ, Piko) is a character in Suikoden II. He is a musician in a band with Annallee and Alberto.
quote: You're a strong woman. I couldn't live without you.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Pico was a musician in a band with Annallee and Alberto. He was an attractive playboy who had broken the hearts of countless women in the past, though he says that it was never his intention and that he was always serious about any woman he was involved with. Due to this, Pico was almost always dealing with one crisis or another involving the ladies in his life.

He was previously a solo performer but partnered up with Alberto when the latter came to Muse City. They would later meet Annallee, completing the group. The trio were performing in Muse when the Dunan Unification War broke out, and in the confusion the three were separated. Pico found his way to Greenhill City, where he briefly dated a young girl named Mairie.

The hero happened to overhear Annallee's name during a heated argument between the two and mentioned to Pico that Annallee was now a member of the New Alliance Army. Pico, overjoyed at hearing this, regarding Annallee as like a sister to him, quickly rushed off to join the New Alliance Army himself, further devastating Mairie.

The reunited band would often perform at New Alliance Army Headquarters, reinvigorating the troops who would come to watch them perform. Following the war, Pico continued to travel and perform with his bandmates, still getting into his romantic troubles wherever they went, much to Alberto's consternation.

trivia:
• Artwork from Genso Suikoden Card Stories erroneously shows Pico playing a cello and Alberto playing a guitar. In Suikoden II, Pico is the guitarist and Pico is the cellist.
• Pico is a Spanish word meaning 'beak'; however, it can also be used as a word for mouth.
recruitment: After recruiting Annallee, speak to him at the Greenhill inn.

name: Tsai
description: Tsai (ツァイ, Tsuai) is a character in Suikoden II. Tsai is a craftsman who is so well-known for his skills with spears that he has acquired the nickname of 'Tsai of the Divine Spear.'

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Tsai was a talented spearman and spear craftsman. He once pierced an iron helmet with a spear, earning him the nickname 'Divine Spear.'

Unpretentious and strong-willed, he was considered first-rate both as a soldier and as a craftsman but considered himself a craftsman first and foremost. Though capable of many forms of crafting, he preferred to work mainly on spears.

He originally lived in Ryube Village but moved into the mountains surrounding the town seeking privacy. His reputation also led to his recruitment by Viktor as Viktor recognized his skill and believed that only Tsai could repair the damaged Fire Spears.

Tsai would agree to help the group, worried about Luca Blight's advance. Too late to prevent the razing of Ryube Village, Tsai recognized Luca's strength and prevented the hero and Jowy from confronting him, knowing they would die in the attempt. Tsai would coninue to aid the mercenary force, eventually joining the New Alliance Army after it was founded.

Tsai served as an infantry leader during the Dunan Unification War, utilizing his outstanding handling of the spear. Although he downplayed his nickname as the Divine Spear, he was still proud of his spearmanship, which he brought to bare against the Highland Army.

During the war, he would return to him home to collect some tools only to be confronted by his daughter, Tomo. It was revealed that Tsai was estranged from his family as he was absorbed in his work. He would refuse to return home with her, saying he had a responsibility to the New Alliance Army. This instead led to Tomo joining the army over Tsai's protests.

Prior to the Liberation of Greenhill, Tomo would once again try to persuade Tsai to come home. Tsai would again refuse, stating that all he had done was made his wife and child sad. He…

trivia: • Tsai's ending will change depending on whether or not you've recruited his daughter, Tomo.
recruitment: Joins automatically.

name: Vicky
description: Viki is the Chitatsu Star in Suikoden, Suikoden II, Suikoden III, Suikoden IV, and Suikoden V. She also appears in Genso Suikogaiden Vol. 2 and Suikoden STAR LEAP.

name: Hilda
description: Hilda (ヒルダ, Hiruda) is a character in Suikoden II. Hilda is an innkeeper who is married to Alex.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Hilda is a young woman from Muse City who married Alex. She was working in Muse as a battlefield nurse when she met Alex during Muse's harvest festival; the two quickly fell in love and were married within six months of meeting. 

Alex and Hilda opened the White Deer Inn, in the outskirts of Muse City after marrying and had a son, Pete. Hilda often worked alone at the inn due to her husband's growing obsession with treasure hunting. Her own hobby was preparing herbs.

During the Dunan Unification War, Hilda loaned her Entry Permit to Nanami and the hero after their group stayed at the inn and helped Hilda recover from a sudden illness caused by overwork by discovering medicinal herbs in the ruins behind the inn. This incident opened Alex's eyes to his neglect, and he began to take a more active role in helping his wife.

Due to the war, they were later forced to desert their inn and headed to South Window City. Once there, they were reunited with the hero. Hilda, hearing that the young man had become the leader of the New Alliance Army, wanted to help and volunteered her and Alex's services. The couple moved to the New Alliance Army Headquarters where Hilda opened an inn. 

Though she mostly worked in a non-combative role, Hilda and Alex did help the New Alliance Army by acting as (fake) reinforcements during a battle with the Matilda Knightdom.

Following the war, Hilda returned to the White Deer Inn, happily living with her husband and son.

trivia: • Hilda's design was modeled after a high school classmate of one of the development team who resembled a certain movie actress.
recruitment: Find Hilda and Alex on the upper floor of the South-Window inn. They will ask to join you, which you should accept.

name: Fitcher
description: Fitcher (フィッチャー, Ficchā) is a character in Suikoden II. Fitcher is a clever politician who worked for several of the Jowston City-States.
quote: Let me be blunt — that's just my honest opinion.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Fitcher was a politician who worked in Muse City and was third in line for the role of mayor at the time of the Dunan Unification War. 
Despite being quite intelligent and an expert at negotiation and conversing, Fitcher used his scruffy appearance and a dummy act to try to make people underestimate him. He was a man of little pride and always looked to the long term. As a result, he had no problem surrendering or debasing himself temporarily to secure an advantage later.

Following the death of Anabelle and the fall of Muse, Fitcher escaped to Two River Principality, working for Makai. After hearing of the foundation of the New Alliance Army, he would strike out on his own initiative to request an alliance with Two River. His style of diplomacy was to achieve results no matter the cost, hence his reaching out without official sanction.

Fitcher's employment under Two River did not last long, however, as Shu requested his services after seeing Fitcher anticipate and help foil the Highland plot to annex Two River. As part of the New Alliance Army, he played a key role on the army's diplomatic front.

Following the war, Fitcher returned to Muse City, where he would be later elected as mayor. He was still serving as mayor of Muse by the War of the Champions.

trivia: • Fitcher is able to sleep with his eyes open.
recruitment: Joins automatically after allying with Two-River.

name: Futch
description: Futch (フッチ, Fucchi) is a supporting character in Suikoden, Suikoden II, and Suikoden III.

facts.label: Appears in
facts.value: Suikoden, Suikoden II, Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2, Suikoden III

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
A handsome lad with brown hair and eyes, in his adolescence Futch was a medium sized boy who wielded a spear. In Suikoden, he wore a small breastplate over a tunic along with brown boots and gloves. He also wore a distinctive green scarf and winged headband. His spear was originally made to be the same height as himself when he was 11 years old.

In Suikoden II, Futch wore a purple tunic over black pants. He retained his other clothing, including his scarf and headband, and also wore an armband around his upper right arm.

A fully matured adult in Suikoden III, Futch stood 177cm tall and was well-toned and tanned. He wore a dark, sleeveless tank-top over a purple tunic and a single pauldron on his left shoulder, along with black pants and boots as well as brown gloves. He wore a purple armband on his upper right arm and maintained his distinctive green scarf and winged

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
As a child, Futch was quick to retort to any perceived slight against him and Black. This was the cause of his animosity towards Ted when he called the young Dragon Knight a brat. His youthful brash confidence is what propelled him to fly to Gregminster Palace despite the dangers involved.

When it came to dragons, Futch was very kind-hearted and caring towards them. The grief of losing Black, his partner since childhood, however, cast a deep shadow on Futch's heart. He chose to carry the blame for Black's death but also took comfort in knowing Black loved him enough to die protecting them.

Humphrey Mintz would help Futch understand that though it was noble to grieve for Black, it should not keep him from his future, nor would it be a betrayal of Black's memory to one day partner with a new dragon. After finding Bright and rescuing Kent on Rakutei Mountain, Futch was able to move on from his grief and look to the future.

As Sharon's guardian, he preferred to apologize for her rather than scold her directly. He saw something of himself in her youthful impetuousness and desire to see the

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Futch was a 9th Rank Dragon Knight who was partnered with the dragon Black. During the Gate Rune War, Futch and Black were hired to escort the Imperial Guard to Magician's Island. Feeling Ted was disrespectful of him and Black, the two would argue during the flight.

Later, Futch was reunited with the leader of the Toran Liberation Army at the Dragon's Den while the dragons were filled from a sleeping sickness. Impatient and wanting to help prepare a cure, Futch rushed off to gain a needed ingredient, the Black Dragon Orchid, from Gregminster Palace's Floating Garden.

There, he was caught by Emperor Barbarossa, who let the child go, as he had no part in the dragon's poisoning. As Futch was leaving, however, Windy attacked him using her magic, shooting Futch and Black out of the sky. Black was killed in the fall, taking the brunt of the impact so that Futch survived with the Black Dragon Orchid intact.

After Futch woke, Joshua Levenheit told Futch that, as he had lost his dragon, he could no longer stay with the Dragon Knights. Joshua Levenheit asked Humphrey Mintz to look over Futch and following the end of the Gate Rune War, Futch departed with Humphrey on a journey.

Futch and Humphrey headed toward Crystal Valley to research dragons but were slowed by the Dunan Unification War and were forced to stay in Highway Village. Futch befriended Kent and would share stores of dragons and the Dragon Knights with him.

When Kent failed to return home, Futch took part in a search for him on Rakutei Mountain. After rescuing Kent from an attacking Harpy, they found what appeared to be a dragon's egg. Futch was hesitant, as he still missed Black terribly, but once the egg hatched, he eventually christened the new pup Bright.

Futch and Humphrey joined the New Alliance Army to repay its leader for his help in searching for Kent. After the war, Humphrey and Futch continued on…

recruitment: Recruited automatically following the story events at the Dragon Knight's Domain.
trivia: • Futch's weapons in Suikoden and Suikoden II, Verserk, Sigmund, and Sigurd all stem from Norse mythology, although Verserk is a poor translation of Berserker. In Norse mythology, Sigmund was the father of Sigurd, the slayer of Fafnir.

name: Stallion
description: Stallion (スタリオン, Sutarion) is a supporting character in Suikoden, Suikoden II, and also makes a cameo in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.1.
quote: My name is Stallion. Fastest in the world. Catch me if you can.

facts.label: Origin
facts.value: Village of the Elves, Toran Republic, |S1= yes, |S2= yes, |G1= yes

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
Stallion has long blue hair, tied into a ponytail, and blue eyes. He has a protruding nose and long pointed elf ears, the left of which was pierced.

In Suikoden, he wore a white tabard with blue piping over a white undershirt, the collar of which was erroneously colored blue in his full body artwork.

In Suikoden II, he wore a purple scarf and a blue tabard with white piping over a white undershirt. He also wore black pants and pale green boots, as well as dark, fingerless gloves and a purple arm brace on his left

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
Incredibly fleet-footed, Stallion could run 100 meters in around 7 seconds. He gave himself the nickname "Great Runner Stallion" and "Itaden" for his skill. For him, the only thing that mattered was running and improving his speed and he held little interest in mundane matters such as war or politics. In that sense, he was very different to most other elves.

Stallion excelled at sensing danger. This, combined with his remarkable speed, allowed him to escape perilous situations but also caused some to scoff at him as a coward, saying Stallion only showed his true speed when fleeing. He himself held great pride in both talents.

He was popular among Suikoden development staff for allowing play testers to navigate the game at an accelerated

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Great Runner
Stallion is an elf who is extremely fast, thanks to his High-Speed Rune. Stallion was extremely proud of his speed and considered himself the fastest pair of feet in the Village of the Elves. His speed was such that he held the nickname "Great Runner Stallion" though some considered him more of a "great coward".

At some point in the past he would meet Hou. The two would hit it off, with Stallion gifting the man a Speed Rune.

Stallion was locked in the village's prison after running away during the battle against Kwanda Rosman. After being freed by Sylvina, Stallion used his incredible speed to save Sylvina and himself from the Burning Mirror. Reuniting Sylvina with Kirkis at the Kobold Village helped reaffirm Kirkis' beliefs and inner strength, which had been rocked by the destruction of the Village of the Elves.
