After the Battle at Pannu Yakuta, Stallion asked to join the Toran Liberation Army. Stallion served the Liberation Army throughout the war, acting as a messenger between Sanchez and McDohl when Teo McDohl attacked Toran Castle. However, he was of limited use as a scout due to his habit of running from danger without taking in his surroundings.

Following the end of the Gate Rune War, Stallion departed the newly formed Toran Republic, going on a journey to become the fastest man in the world.
Training in Dunan
Stallion's training eventually took him to the City-States of Jowston. While he was in Greenhill City, the Highland Army attacked. Using his speedy feet, he was able to rescue Teresa Wisemail from a pinch. Following this, Stallion fled to Radat Town, though few there took his story seriously.

After meeting the hero and telling him his story, Stallion challenged him to a race. After somehow being beaten by the young leader, Stallion agreed to join the New Alliance Army. During the Dunan Unification War, Stallion continued to train,…

recruitment: Joins automatically following plot events at Pannu Yakuta Castle.
trivia: • Idaten, one of Stallion's titles, is a Japanese god who protects Buddhist monastic sects. He is known for his great speed.

name: Chaco
description: Chaco (チャコ, Chako) is a character in Suikoden II. Chaco is a mischievous young Winged Horde whose love for his town led him to fight for the New Alliance Army.
quote: I love this town! I really love this town!

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Chaco was a mischievous and ill-disciplined child belonging to the Winged Horde in Two River City. He was popular with his fellow children in the community and often played games with them. As a child he was watched over and picked on by Sid in equal measure.

He would participate in the SY 459 Spirit Festival, which would mark the first time he was able to complete the festival's trail of courage for children. He would encounter the spirit of his recently deceased uncle, Phobo, during this festival.

Known for his tricks and pick-pocketing tendencies towards both humans and kobolds, he often stole wallets and coin-purses from unsuspecting individuals in the city in a bid to both "toughen them up" and make a little money on the side. Despite his questionable actions, Chaco had a deep sense of pride for his hometown.

During the Dunan Unification War, Chaco's encounter with the young leader of the New Alliance Army led to a series of events that changed his perspective. Initially stealing the young man's wallet, Chaco repeatedly taunted him before eventually being ordered by his grandmother, Susu, to lead the young man to her.

Chaco's attitude towards the hero gradually shifted as he witnessed his efforts to thwart the Highland Army's plot exploiting racial tensions in Two River. Inspired to protect his hometown, Chaco played a crucial role in uniting all races of Two River, leading to the successful repelling of the Highland invasion.

Impressed with the camaraderie and mission of the New Alliance Army, Chaco decided to join their ranks, relocating to New Alliance Army Headquarters. He still held resentment and fear towards Sid, due to past mistreatment, such as being locked in a well for three days. So, when Chaco inadvertently introduced Sid to the New Alliance…

trivia:
• During the Dunan Unification War, Chaco is dumped by an unseen and, other than in one investigation by Richmond, unmentioned girlfriend, but it doesn't get him down.
• Chaco is the only character in Suikoden II who has the war battle ability Flight which increases the movement radius of his unit to a 3x3 grid area.
recruitment: Joins automatically after allying with Two-River.

name: Mondo
description: Mondo (モンド, Mondo) is a character in Suikoden II. He is an experienced Rokkaku Hamlet ninja.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Mondo was a ninja originating from Rokkaku Hamlet. Regarded as a senior ninja in comparison to Sasuke and Kasumi, Mondo held the rank of middle-ranked chūnin. However, his potential for advancement to high-ranked jōnin was constrained by his bloodline. Despite this limitation, Mondo earned the trust of Hanzo, who esteemed his capabilities.

He bore the Mayfly Rune, which had been passed down to ninja since ancient times. The rune allowed Mondo to teleport short distances by projecting his thoughts. It also allowed him to make ephemeral clones, a useful skill for any ninja and Mondo's strongest skill.

This trust earned Mondo the role as the head instructor of the Swallow House, which included Sasuke. Although he was a strict teacher, he was also a big-hearted man who laughed a lot, earning him the respect of his young charges.

Mondo occasionally displayed a lighthearted aspect of his personality, as evidenced by his teasing of Sasuke concerning his affections for Kasumi. This playful demeanor contrasted his typically composed and serious disposition.

He was one of the ninja who survived the destruction of Rokkaku during the Gate Rune War, having been responsible for evacuating the children of the village at the time. He would later participate in the last battle of the war under Hanzo's command.

During the Dunan Unification War, Mondo and Sasuke received orders from Hanzo to provide assistance to the leader of the New Alliance Army, leading to his integration into the force. Sasuke looked up to him as a father figure, having no memories of his parents.

Mondo, while experienced and skillful, consistently dedicated substantial daily effort to honing his speed and jumping prowess. Frequent training sessions with Kasumi also underscored his commitment. Mondo's combat…

recruitment: Joins with Sasuke.

name: Pilika
description: Pilika (ピリカ, Pirika) is a supporting character in Suikoden II. Pilika is a young girl from Toto Village who gets swept up in the events of the Dunan Unification War.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Pilika was the young girl who found Jowy Atreides after he had become separated from his best friend following their jump into the Tenzan Pass ravine. It would be Pilika and her parents who would care for Jowy as he recuperated in Toto Village. She was a honest and cheerful child.

Even after the death of her parents in Luca Blight's razing of Toto, Pilika was able to maintain her spirits thanks to the efforts of Jowy, whom she had come to seen as a parental figure in her life, calling him "Uncle Jowy". Through her selfless affection, she gave Jowy the loving family he never experienced growing up.

However, having Luca move to kill her amidst the blazing Mercenary Fortress was too traumatic and Pilika would be stricken with a form of aphasia for some time afterwards. Upon her arrival in Muse City, she would be seperated from Nanami and Jowy briefly but would be found and reunited with her carers by Nash Latkje. She would continue to be cared for by Nanami and the hero following the fall of Muse City and Jowy's subsequent defection to the Highland Army.

Late in the Dunan Unification War, Pilika would begin to speak again after reuniting with Jowy in Muse City but it would take more time than had passed for her mind to fully recover from the emotional trauma. Though Jowy was by this point the ruler of the Highland Kingdom, to Pilika he was still the kindly "Uncle Jowy".

Once the fall of the Highland Kingdom was evident, Pilika did not want to be seperated from Jowy again but nonetheless did not fight against his decision to send her away. She did, however, have one last request - to be lifted up and held as her father used to do. Following this emotional farewell, she was sent with Jillia to live a secluded life in a rural area of the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia. There,…

name: Lily Pendragon
originalName: リリィ・ペンドラゴン
description: Lilly Pendragon (リリィ・ペンドラゴン, Riryi Pendoragon) is a minor character in Suikoden II and a major character in Suikoden III. Lilly is the spoiled daughter of Gustav Pendragon who, as a young lady, investigates rumors of Fire Bringer raids on Tinto caravans.
quote: Yeah! I love him! My daddy is really, really strong!

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Encounter with Neclord
Lilly was the daughter of Gustav Pendragon, who served as mayor of Tinto City during the Dunan Unification War.

She was the apple of his eye and was a bright and cheerful child who lived a happy, comfortable life. Though in later years, it was said that her father's duties meant she didn't spend much time with her parents.

Her pigtails were her defining feature as a child. She loved her father dearly, though she took after her mother in looks. Her cheerfulness reminded Nanami of Pilika to a certain extent.

When Neclord attempted to take over Tinto in the middle of the Dunan Unification War, Lilly was kidnapped with the intent of making her his bride. Thanks to the efforts of Viktor, Marlowe, and Sierra, among other members of the New Alliance Army, Lilly was safely rescued and returned to her father.
First Daughter of the Republic
As a young woman, Lilly grew up to be both beautiful and clever but also selfish and stubborn due to her spoiled upbringing in Tinto. She was also very hard on her attendants, Samus and Reed, putting too much pressure on them and generally displacing her own failings onto them.

Still, even with all that, she was courageous and not easily scared and willing to fight for her beliefs. She was also very dependable in a crisis. As an adult, Lilly deftly wielded an elegant one-handed sword with a slender blade and guard shaped like a cross.

She first met Chris Lightfellow at the Zexen Federation Day Ball in 470. There, she insisted that Chris dance with her and when Chris, who was not good at dancing at the time, accidentally spilled a glass of wine on Lilly's dress, a brawl soon broke out. Lilly ended up throwing a cake at Chris, who retaliated with a slap.

The fight would finally end after the two finished hurling…

name: Lucia
description: Lucia (ルシア, Rushia) is a character who appears in Suikoden II and Suikoden III. She is the leader of the Karaya Clan.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Lucia is the virtuous and trustworthy chieftain of the Karaya Clan who dwell in the Grasslands. Her father, Kinua, was poisoned in a plot by Alec Wisemail, mayor of Greenhill, some years before. In order to fulfil her revenge, she and her elite soldiers fought with the Highland Army during the Dunan Unification War.

Fighting alongside Highland, Lucia would confront the New Alliance Army several times, wielding her whip. Eventually, she would be defeated by the New Alliance Army in a battle at L'Renouille Palace and would finally give way when Teresa Wisemail's sincerity convinced her that the wrongs committed against her clan could be righted.

Although she had hated Teresa due to her father's doings and had tried to assassinate the leader of the New Alliance Army, Lucia was open to explanation and fair dealings. Once Teresa became mayor of Greenhill, the Karaya Clan quickly received an offer of reconciliation.

Following the Dunan Unification War, Lucia continued to lead the Karaya, becoming a strong leader for the clan itself and also within the Six Clans. She would battle against the Zexen Federation many times over the years.

Shortly after the war, she would give birth to her son, Hugo, whose father's name would go unrevealed. Lucia would raise him with pride, becoming as fierce as a mother as she was as a warrior. She would also become known for her diplomatic prowess as well as her martial talents.

When the War of the Champions broke out between the Six Clans and Zexen, the Karaya Village was razed by the Zexen Knights as part of their ploy to escape an ambush. Following this loss, the Karaya survivors would regroup under Lucia at the Great Hollow where she clashed with the Silver Maiden Chris Lightfellow.

From there, she would strike with the Lizard Clan at…

trivia:
• Lucia's weapon upgrades as Uranne Galo, Uranne Galor. The first, Uranne, is not available without a cheating device. Perhaps it is the same whip she used against the hero 15 years previously.
• Lucia learns the unique skills Continual Attack and Magic Repel. She is able to do well in either Melee or Magic, although she cannot get S level in any particular skill, most end in A or B+.
• Lucia participates in the truly awesome Clan Leader attack with Dupa, and the Lovely Woman attack with Estella and Yuiri in which the ladies blow kisses for damage somehow.
• Lucia was apparently highly rated in Budehuc Castle's "Secret Beauty Contest".
• If Hugo is Flame Champion, she will fill the…
recruitment: Joins Automatically in Chapter 4.

name: Ain Gide
description: Ain Gide (アイン・ジード, Ain Jīdo) is a character in Suikoden. A general of the Scarlet Moon Empire, Ain Gide stayed true to Barbarossa Rugner throughout the Gate Rune War.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Initially starting his military career as a low-ranking soldier in the Scarlet Moon Empire's Imperial Army, Ain Gide earned a place in Teo McDohl's unit, drawing attention from Teo himself, which led to a promotion.

During the War of Succession, Ain Gide fought alongside his close friend Teo in the crucial battle at the Fortress of Kwaba, providing valuable support to his comrade amidst challenging circumstances.

Years later, amidst the unfolding Gate Rune War, Ain Gide held authority over the Fortress of Kwaba, fulfilling the role of Imperial security commander. Notably, he was recognized for his skills and merits; however, he modestly refrained from seeking titles or nicknames.

Due to his deep friendship with Teo, when Teo's son sought passage through the fortress while fleeing charges of treason, Ain chose to overlook the young man's identity, prioritizing loyalty to his friend over his official duties.

Nevertheless, Ain Gide remained a dedicated warrior, unswervingly committed to the Scarlet Moon Empire. As the Gate Rune War approached its culmination, Ain's forces were withdrawn from Kwaba and repositioned to defend Gregminster. Following the final battle, with the awareness that the war had concluded, Ain ordered his men to surrender.

In the closing moments of the conflict, as the Toran Liberation Army approached Gregminster Palace, Ain Gide stood alone as the last defender of the Empire. Despite being encouraged to stand down, and despite recognizing the Empire's defeat, Ain staunchly refused to yield.

In an unyielding display of fidelity to both his friendship with Teo and his loyalty to Emperor Barbarossa, Ain Gide valiantly battled the forces of the Liberation Army until he met his end. His actions exemplified the essence of a true warrior, proving that the decisions and conduct of an individual hold paramount

trivia:
• Despite having General rank, he is not counted among the Five Great Generals of the Scarlet Moon Empire.
• The meteor storm attack that Ain Gide uses is not available through any Rune in the game.
• Cleo is the only character in the entire game to refer to Ain Gide by only his first name.

name: Alen
description: Alen (アレン, Aren) is a supporting character in Suikoden who makes a cameo in Suikoden II. During the Gate Rune War, Alen is a member of General Teo McDohl's Armored Cavalry, along with his colleague Glenshiel.

facts.label: Appears in
facts.value: Suikoden, Suikoden II, Suikoden STAR LEAP

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Alen served as one of the commanders of the Scarlet Moon Empire's Armored Cavalry alongside Glenshiel, under the command of General Teo McDohl. His personal loyalty was to Teo, rather than the Empire as a whole. Bearing a Rage Rune, he held the nickname of the "Flame General" due to his intense, heated personality and his sword was named in kind.
Childhood and Training
Born to a fishing family, Alen was first inspired to join the Imperial Army at the age of 12 when he saw Teo McDohl's victory parade in Gregminster following his victory over the City-States of Jowston. His parents were reluctant to give their blessing, partly because of their lack of noble or military ties, but eventually agreed and at 15, Alen would enroll in the Imperial Academy.

Within six months, Alen was recognized as one of the more promising students. Skilled in swordplay from the beginning, he had steadily improved in rune handling but suffered from an impatient temperament. He would be classmates with Clet and would quickly develop a rivalry with Glenshiel, whose more reserved, studious natured irked the passionate Alen.

An argument between the two would provoke Glenshiel into improving his swordsmanship as well as his mind, and in the 4th round of a training tournament, Alen would be soundly defeated by Glenshiel's more methodical fighting style. This greatly unnerved Alen as he felt that his sword skills were what gave him the edge in competing for a rare, coveted appointment in the Central Army.

Alen would attempt to rematch Glenshiel later that night, only for the two to come to the understanding that they had both been driving the other forward the entire time. More appreciative of each other, the two vowed to do all they could to graduate to the Central Army, a goal they eventually reached.
Gate Rune War
During the Gate Rune War, Alen aided General Teo in the First Battle with Teo.…

recruitment: Recruited automatically following the Second Battle with Teo.
trivia:
• Alen and Glenshiel have the exact same endings in the English-language versions of Suikoden, not a word between them has been changed, but in the Japanese version, the two have similar but unique endings.
• Alen is the only character in the game to come equipped with a Rage Rune.

name: Anji
description: Anji (アンジー, Anjī) is a supporting character who appears in Suikoden. Anji is the leader of a gang of pirates, a crew which operated on Lake Toran.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Anji, the charismatic leader of a daring crew of lake pirates, held sway over the tumultuous waters of Lake Toran during the Gate Rune War. Despite his piratical nature, Anji was far from malevolent; he upheld his word and adhered to his own personal set of principles. Prior to embracing his pirate persona, Anji's life was steeped in commerce, owning his own vessel as a respected merchant.

However, the call of rebellion against the oppressive Scarlet Moon Empire beckoned, and around SY 450, he, alongside loyal comrades Kanak and Leonardo, took to the waters of Lake Toran as pirates to defy tyranny and fight for a just cause. This choice inevitably led to conflict with the Imperial Navy, particularly with the formidable Sonya Shulen, as clashes between the pirate crew and the Empire became commonplace.

As naval commander and pirate leader, both engaged in multiple battles and raids against each other. Their rivalry escalated when Anji taunted Sonya by pinning a wedding dress to the Floating Fortress of Shasarazade, insinuating her unwed status. Anji's declaration that she should "put this on and be a woman" in particular was said to have rendered Sonya apoplectic with rage.

In retaliation, she arranged for the pirates to capture a supply of high-quality liquor only to lead a raid when the bandits were all drunk, dealing a large blow to their operations. Anji was enraged by what he saw as an underhanded retaliation and so the rivalry between the two continued unabated over the years.

A visit from Tai Ho, Yam Koo, and the hero would see Anji join the Toran Liberation Army. Tai Ho would taunt Anji, insinuating that he and his band were "wasting away". Anji's pride could not tolerate the insult, even amidst his chosen life of constant struggle against the Imperial Navy. After being defeated, he would agree to align his small band with the Liberation Army.

At Toran…

recruitment: Talk to Anji at Pirate's Fortress with Yam Koo and Tai Ho in the party and defeat him in battle.
trivia:
• Anji was originally supposed to have an important role in the Battle at Floating Fortress Shasarazade, but this had to be abandoned as a water-based battle could not be represented.
• His name comes from a band Murayama Yoshitaka knew from long ago.
• His clothing, according to several staff members, is exceptionally warm.

name: Antonio
description: Antonio (アントニオ, Antonio) is a character in Suikoden who makes a cameo appearance in Suikoden II. Antonio is a skilled chef who worked for Marie during the Gate Rune War.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Antonio was a skilled chef known for his dedication to the culinary arts. He wandered from place to place, seeking opportunities to showcase his talents. His talent meant he could be selective about where he plied his trade.

When Marie joined the Toran Liberation Army and expressed the need for a cook, Antonio eagerly offered his services to the army. He held a fondness for Marie, and rumors circulated that he had a rivalry with Lester over her in the past.

Grateful for the chance to contribute to the cause of peace, Antonio actively participated in the Gate Rune War, wielding a surprisingly heavy ceramic pan as his weapon. However, his true prowess lay in using his culinary skills to uplift the spirits of the troops.

After the conclusion of the Gate Rune War, Antonio continued to serve as the cook at Marie's inn, which was once more located in Gregminster.

During the Dunan Unification War, Antonio visited the New Alliance Army Headquarters and playfully challenged Hai Yo, a celebrated cook in his own right. Their friendly Cook-off rekindled Hai Yo's passion for cooking. In the end, Hai Yo emerged victorious, and Antonio graciously shared his specialty recipe, Ramen, with

recruitment: Recruit Marie, then speak to her at headquarters unless she mentions a need for a cook, then talk to Antonio in the Seika inn.
trivia: • The initial reason for there being two cooks in Suikoden was that it was envisioned there would be a lot more highly similar, recolored characters to make up the numbers. As development continued and more unique characters kept being added, this became less of a factor.

name: Apple
description: Apple (アップル, Appuru) is a recurring character who appears in Suikoden, Suikoden II and Suikoden III as well as Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2 and Genso Suikoden Card Stories. Apple is a military strategist who studied under Mathiu Silverberg.

facts.label: Appears in
facts.value: Suikoden, Suikoden II, Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2, Genso Suikoden Card Stories, Suikoden III

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
Apple has chin-length light brown hair and soft green eyes. She wore large, round glasses.

In Suikoden II, Apple wore a long, cream-colored coat with brown trim, belted at the waist, along with a red scarf tied around her neck. She also wore dark leggings and knee-high cream boots with brown cuffs. Her Suikoden outfit is similar but less refined.

By the time of Suikoden III, Apple wore a beige tunic with structured shoulders and darker brown geometric trim along the hem and cuffs. The sleeves were long, gray, and slightly loose. She also wore a high blue scarf wraps around her neck.

This was paired with a long, flowing blue skirt with subtle beige stripes as well as sturdy brown lace-up

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
Apple is a deeply empathetic and principled individual, shaped by a life of profound loss and the teachings of her mentor, Mathiu Silverberg. Her childhood as a war orphan left her with a strong disdain for conflict, viewing all battles as tragedies regardless of their outcome.

However, under Mathiu's guidance, she came to understand that strategy could be a means of ending wars swiftly and minimizing suffering, a perspective that softened her initial aversion to military tactics as the cause of battles.

Her personality is marked by a strong sense of justice and an unyielding commitment to helping the vulnerable, which sometimes made her decisions more idealistic than pragmatic. Despite her occasional naïveté as a strategist, Apple is a woman of action, willing to sacrifice her pride and face any hardship to uphold her beliefs, inspiring respect in those around her, including her senior, Shu.

Her life's work, including completing Mathiu’s biography and opening a school to pass on her knowledge, reflects her enduring commitment to peace and

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Mathiu's Student
Apple was raised in Dana but had lost her parents in the Scarlet Moon-Jowston War at a young age. As such, she believed all battles were tragedies, regardless of the results.

Mathiu Silverberg would take her in from her distant relatives, staying at the Fortress of Garan, when he noticed her passion for reading. Taught in Mathiu's classroom in Seika, she would become something of a foster child.

From Mathiu, she learned his maxim of ending battles as quickly as possible, as opposed to seeking victories at all costs. This led her to soften her stance on military strategy, something which she previously thought of as starting battles, like the one that cost her her home, not ending them.

Boasting a clear mind, she was eventually able to study at Crystal Valley in the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia on Mathiu's recommendation before returning home once she heard news of the Gate Rune War. When she heard that Mathiu had become the strategist of the Toran Liberation Army, she had doubts over his decision as it seemed so opposed to the non-violent and friendly teacher she knew and admired.

She would journey to Toran Castle and, once convinced that Mathiu was working "to end the war" and bring a peaceful future, joined the Liberation Army in order to support Mathiu. She would continue her studies under Mathiu there, in his spare time. Apple's esteem for her teacher would only grow as she would see his stratagems put into action during the war, but she continued to resent McDohl for supposedly dragging Mathiu back to the battlefield.

Apple would lose her mentor at the war's end as he passed away from the wounds he suffered at Shasarazade.
New Alliance Strategist
Having lost Mathiu to war, Apple endeavored to write the biography of her late master. In order to gather materials for this task, she tried to follow in the footsteps of his own journey.

Along the…

recruitment: Talk to Apple in Mathiu's school in Seika after Kwanda Rosman's defeat.

name: Barbarossa Rugner
description: Barbarossa Rugner (バルバロッサ・ルーグナー, Barubarossa Rūgunā) is a main antagonist in Suikoden. Barbarossa Rugner was the 17th Emperor of the Scarlet Moon Empire, renowned as the "Golden Emperor" for his golden armor and early benevolent rule.
originalName: バルバロッサ・ルーグナー

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Barbarossa Rugner, the 17th Emperor of the Scarlet Moon Empire, was a figure of both triumph and tragedy, whose reign was marked by great victories and profound sorrow. Born the sixth son of Emperor Michelan, Barbarossa was initially overlooked in the line of succession, allowing him a childhood of relative freedom among commoners.

However, the untimely deaths of his elder brothers and the corruption of the remaining heirs led Michelan to name Barbarossa as Crown Prince. Upon his father's death, Barbarossa ascended to the throne, inheriting the Sovereign Rune and the responsibility of ruling the empire.

In year 446 of the solar calendar, Barbarossa's reign was challenged when his uncle, Geil Rugner, seized the throne during his absence, sparking the War of Succession. Despite early setbacks, including the death of his beloved wife, Claudia, Barbarossa rallied his forces from Pannu Yakuta Castle and emerged victorious, reclaiming the throne and earning the title of the "Golden Emperor" for his golden armor and benevolent early rule.

However, his reign took a dark turn in the year 449 when he became enamored with Windy, the Court Magician, who bore a striking resemblance to Claudia. Manipulated by Windy's magic and his own loneliness, Barbarossa allowed corruption to fester within the empire, leading to widespread discontent.

This would eventually manifest as open rebellion by 453, with the rise of the Toran Liberation Army sparking what would become known as the Gate Rune War. During the war, he largely withdrew from governance, leaving the defense of the empire to the Five Great Generals while he indulged in his grief and misplaced love.

In the final days of the war, as the Liberation Army stormed Gregminster, Barbarossa made a desperate last stand, using the Sovereign Rune to transform into a Golden Hydra. Defeated, he confessed that his actions were not the…

trivia:
• Barbarossa was, along with Windy, confirmed as dead following the events of Suikoden. Since then, however, Konami has decided to downgrade their status to 'Missing in Action', hence the lack of a date of death.
• Presumably named after Emperor Frederic Barbarossa, Operation Barbarossa (in German, Unternehmen Barbarossa) was also the code name for the invasion of USSR by the 3rd Reich, during WW2.
• "Barbarossa" also translates into Italian as "red beard", although the character himself doesn't even have a beard.
• Barbarossa commissioned the Golden Goddess statue that is seen in Gregminster in the likeness of his late wife, Claudia.

name: Blackman
description: Blackman (ブラックマン, Burakkuman) is a character in Suikoden. Blackman is a farmer residing in the razed town of Kalekka.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Blackman was a farmer residing in Kalekka. He had chosen to remain in the area even after its destruction, tending to his plants in quiet contemplation. Driven by a belief that amidst the ravages of war, the world needed the healing touch of greenery, he saw his role as a cultivator of life.

The leader of the Toran Liberation Army displayed consideration by avoiding stepping on Blackman's flowers while passing through. Touched by this gesture, Blackman felt a strong connection with the Liberation Army's mission.

Recognizing their shared aspiration to overthrow the Scarlet Moon Empire and establish a new society, he decided to join their ranks, believing they shared his vision of rejuvenating the land.

In battles, Blackman wielded a hoe, his beloved farming tool, which had previously been used to plow fields before he took up arms with the Liberation Army. He was a surprisingly competent warrior, leading the Fellows unit in several key battles of the Gate Rune War.

Following the conclusion of the Gate Rune War, Blackman returned to his fields in Kalekka, actively participating in the town's ongoing restoration

recruitment: Talk to Blackman in Kalekka without stepping on the saplings in front of him.

name: Camille
description: Camille (カミーユ, Kamīyu) is a supporting character in Suikoden. A debt collector, Camille joined the Toran Liberation Army in order to keep an eye on her debtor, Gremio.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Camille, famously known as "Camille the She-Devil," gained prominence as a debt collector due to her relentless pursuit of debtors and exceptional spear-wielding skills.

Her spear featured a slightly thicker blade, setting it apart from ordinary spears. Often boasting the debt collector's motto, "I'll collect from your grave!", she struck fear into those who owed her.

During the Gate Rune War, Camille's assignment involved collecting debts owed by Gremio, who, due to the exile of the McDohl household from Gregminster, had been unable to repay them.

Viktor convinced her to join the group in their quest to find treasure at Toran Lake Castle, hoping it would enable Gremio to settle his debts. This led her to become a member of the Toran Liberation Army, staying close to Gremio until she could collect what was owed.

Upon Gremio's unfortunate demise, Camille remained devoted to the Liberation Army, her commitment to her role as a debt collector aligning with her dedication to the army's cause. Throughout the war, she served in the Soldier Beauties unit.

Following the establishment of the Toran Republic, Camille's exceptional skills with the spear were acknowledged, leading to her appointment as the lancing instructor of the Republican Army. Ultimately, Cleo settled Gremio's debt, but it is believed that if she hadn't, Camille would have relentlessly pursued him until the debt was paid, even if it meant chasing him to the farthest reaches of the

recruitment: Recruited automatically in Kaku before the first trip to the castle on Lake Toran.
trivia:
• If you choose to execute Milich Oppenheimer, Camille will undertake the role of executioner, out of vengeance for Gremio's death. Ironically, her vengeance will prevent Gremio from being resurrected... by making a complete game impossible.
• Camille's weapons (Garm, Fenrir, Loki) get their names from Norse mythology. Garm was the giant wolf that guarded Hel, Fenrir is another giant wolf who was actually the son of Loki, the Norse God of mischief. They are all interrelated because of the side they fight on during Ragnarök, the final battle that would end the world, according to Norse mythology.
• Camille is one of the very few characters whose castle dialogue will change following Gremio's…

name: Chandler
description: Chandler (チャンドラー, Chandorā) is a character in Suikoden. An idle peddler, he was recruited to join the Toran Liberation Army and sold his wares to supply the soldiers in their effort.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Chandler was a wandering merchant known for his successful travels, seeking profits from various ventures. His reliable distribution network enabled him to trade a diverse range of goods. Despite his success as a traveling peddler, his true aspiration was to establish his own store.

Upon meeting McDohl at the Fortress of Kwaba, Chandler eagerly joined the Toran Liberation Army. He was motivated both by the prospect of opening his own store and having seen the tyranny of the Scarlet Moon Empire with his own eyes during his travels.

Drawing on his business acumen, he played a crucial role in supplying the nascent army. Utilizing the knowledge and experience gained from his years as a wandering merchant, Chandler efficiently provided the Liberation Army with nearly any item they required, albeit for a price.

Following the conclusion of the Gate Rune War, Chandler chose not to remain at Toran Castle. Instead, he further realized his long-held dream by opening a full-time civilian item shop in an undisclosed location, embracing a new chapter in his business

recruitment: Talk to Chandler outside of the Fortress of Kwaba any time after establishing Toran Castle as your base.

name: Chapman
description: Chapman (チャップマン, Chappuman) is a character in Suikoden. Before and during the Gate Rune War, he was an armor merchant, but later quit his job to enter the entertainment industry.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Chapman was a merchant who managed an armor shop in the town of Antei. His interactions with customers were often contentious, marked by his sharp tongue, leading to frequent arguments and a damaged reputation for his store.

Behind his bitterness lay a deep frustration with his perceived lack of usefulness to the growing rebel cause throughout the Scarlet Moon Empire. When the Toran Liberation Army arrived in Antei, Chapman seized the opportunity and joined them, driven by righteous anger.

Upon becoming a member of the Liberation Army, Chapman established an armor shop within Toran Castle, offering the finest armor available at all times. His straightforward nature was evident as he never hesitated to demand payment for his products and services, even from his comrades in the fight for freedom.

Following the conclusion of the Gate Rune War, Chapman decided to transition away from the armor business entirely. He ventured into the field of entertainment and opened a theatre, which proved to be a resounding

recruitment: Talk to Chapman in his armor store in Antei and agree to let him join.

name: Cleo
description: Cleo (クレオ, Kureo) is a supporting character in Suikoden who makes a cameo in Suikoden II. Cleo is a servant of the McDohl household who followed her charge into the Toran Liberation Army during the Gate Rune War.

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
Cleo was conceived very early in the development of Suikoden, along with Pahn, as a prototypical "big sister" type character for the hero.

Cleo has brown hair and blue eyes. She normally wore a silver circlet with purple fabric hanging from the sides. She also wore a brown tunic over red underclothes, protected by a breastplate and pauldrons on her shoulders. She also wore purple forearm guards. Purple leggings and boots, protected by a pair of silver greaves, completed her equipment.

In battle, Cleo uses throwing knives, flinging them with great force at the

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
Cleo was known to hold the manner of both a gentle woman and powerful warrior in the role of the hero's protector and companion. She was generally good natured and rare to anger. She showed no irritation to Hix's questioning of her being a warrior, simply explaining that being a woman did not mean she needed a "special" reason to fight, and remained calm in the face of Sonya Shulen's anger.

One of the few incidents that earned her direct ire was the treatment of Varkas and Sydonia by Grady after their capture. She could also be irritable shortly after waking up. In the past, she was more prone to outbursts, lambasting Gremio during the War of Succession for not being able to prevent McDohl's abduction to the point that Teo had to reign her in.

She often worried about the young McDohl and his relationship with his father but mostly Cleo seemed to keep her feelings hidden. She has a diary in her room in Gregminster but it is always locked. Cleo carries the key on her

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Protecting the Young Master
220px||thumb|Leknaat gifts Cleo the [[Fire Rune. (PS1)|right]]Cleo served as a direct subordinate of General Teo McDohl, living in his Gregminster home along with Gremio and Pahn. She and the hero held a close bond having watched him grow from infancy, with Cleo regarding him with real love as something akin to a younger brother.

Cleo was also a knife throwing expert and there are heroic tales of her felling dozens of mounted cavalry single-handedly in one battle with her knives.

When on a mission to Magician's Island, Cleo would be met by Leknaat who would tell her than her duty was to protect the hero before gifting her with a Fire Rune. Although puzzled as to why she was addressed specifically, as events would begin to spiral out of control, Cleo would remember these words and continue her efforts in playing out her role.

Cleo aided Ted when he escaped from Windy, injured; Cleo was one of the first to notice that his wounds were magically inflicted and also was the first to recognize Ted's rune as one of the 27 True Runes. Later, when Kraze Miles came to collect Ted, Cleo stood her ground defending the McDohl household, arguing that no one could enter Teo McDohl's household without just cause.

Later fleeing Gregminster with the hero, Cleo would help her young charge in undoing some of the injustices inflicted by the Empire even after she worried about whether they could ever return to their past lives. Despite this, she would remain loyal, continuing to serve the hero as he rose to become leader of the Toran Liberation Army.

During the war, Cleo would station herself in the room closest to the hero, in order to continue protecting him. As the war progressed, she would worry about the possibility of father and son having to fight each other. Still, Cleo would continue to defend the hero, never leaving him to endure his fate…

recruitment: Recruited automatically before the journey to Magician's Island.
trivia: • Cleo is the only character in the game that can accompany the hero on every mission from the beginning of the game to the end.

name: Clive
description: Clive (クライブ, Kuraibu) is a supporting character in Suikoden and Suikoden II who also appears in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.1. Clive is a Howling Voice Guild agent who pursued Elza throughout several lands.

facts.label: Appears in
facts.value: Suikoden, Suikoden II, Genso Suikogaiden Vol.1

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
Clive was a stern looking man with blond hair and black eyebrows. Elza once noted that his hair and complexion had tanned during his journey,

He wore a large black, hooded cloak which usually concealed most of his body, and his gun, Storm. In Suikoden II, he wore a green short-sleeved shirt and khaki pants beneath his cloak, as well as green boots and a single black, finger-less glove on his left hand. He almost always wore his hood up.

Kawano Junko intended her design of Clive to look wild but felt that with his concealing cloak it was perhaps difficult to tell.

The "Clive Quest" of Suikoden II was originally planned to be included in Suikoden. However, Murayama Yoshitaka would delay the implementation of the plotline due to the Howling Voice Guild's scale and importance as a guild of gun wielders in a world mostly without

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
Clive's means of support were Kelley, Elza, and his life in the Howling Voice Guild. To be denied that life by what had happened between Kelley and Elza filled Clive with a rage that fueled his obsession with tracking down and punishing Elza.

Ruthless, he had little hesitation in threatening or shooting anyone who would obstruct his pursuit. Elza described him as always honest and someone who neither desired nor shunned power and responsibility.

He knew Nash Latkje from his time in the Howling Voice Guide and disliked him, even though his death sentence for leaving the guild was postponed indefinitely.

He did not open up to anyone else. During his time in the Toran Liberation Army, he distanced himself from his colleagues and had little interest in their goal. Even though he had some gratitude towards the hero's help during the Dunan Unification War, he still kept his thoughts to himself for the most part. When Elza fell, however, Clive unleashed his grief and sadness at everything that had

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Clive is a knight-class gunner of the Howling Voice Guild, skilled in the secret techniques of gunpowder. Picked up by the organization at a young age, Clive underwent rigorous training and was raised to be a loyal combatant and officer of the guild.

Highly reticent, he traveled to the Scarlet Moon Empire in pursuit of a mysterious woman, who had killed the leader of the Howling Voice Guild, Kelley, their best friend and comrade, before fleeing. Encountering the Toran Liberation Army in a chance encounter at the inn in Rikon, Clive would agree to join in hopes of getting clues that would lead him to her. 

This search proved mostly fruitless, however; the only clue he found was a grave in Rockland, allegedly of a woman who died on a street. He was not fooled by this attempt to dissuade him in his hunt, however, and instead renewed his vow to find her.

His ability to provide accurate, devastating attacks from a distance in battle greatly served the Liberation Army. Some records point to Clive playing a key in the Battle at Floating Fortress Shasarazade, with this skill allowing the Liberation Army to target the empire's naval officers, throwing Sonya Shulen's ships into disarray.

Ever on the hunt, by the time the Gate Rune War was over, Clive had left the Liberation Army and simply resumed his quest.

Later, he participated in the Dunan Unification War, continuing to gain information about Elza. He caught up to her in Muse City but the fugitive managed to escape, using the hero as a shield to cover her fire. He would also encounter Nash Latkje, whom he disliked, in the city, warning him to watch his back.

From there, Clive would pursue Elza to South Window City only for him to lose her trail. He knew she had gone to Lakewest, but without a ship had no means to pursue her. He would once again meet the hero and, upon learning that he had access to a vessel, agreed…

recruitment: Clive has a 1 in 2 chance of appearing on the ground floor of the Rikon inn (1 in 8 in the original or PSP remaster). Talk to him to recruit him.

name: Crowley
description: Crowley (クロウリー, Kurourī) is a character in Suikoden. He is a mysterious and supremely powerful magician.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Crowley was a figure shrouded in mystery, known for his age and formidable power. Referred to as "great and ancient" in ancient texts, he was also credited as the discoverer of rune unites.

Throughout his life, Crowley pursued an ambitious goal—to attain power equal to or surpassing that of the 27 True Runes. To achieve this, he dedicated his days to extensive research and rigorous training in ordinary rune magic, honing his skills in pursuit of his aspirations. He claimed to have a staggering 100 runes embedded in his body, a feat that set him apart from ordinary rune users.

At a certain point in his life, Crowley took Mazus as his disciple, a noteworthy event given Mazus's own prowess in magic. The two would eventually have a falling out and engaged in an epic battle, with their magical prowess being so immense that it reportedly leveled three mountains and brought forth a week-long rain. Additionally, there are claims that Piccolo served as the third disciple under Crowley's tutelage.

During the Gate Rune War, Crowley was discovered conducting his research and training within the Cave of the Past at Qlon Temple. Despite expressing surprise at the discovery, he willingly joined the ranks of the Toran Liberation Army when asked, stating a desire to enjoy the sunshine.

While he undoubtedly contributed to the Liberation Army's cause, it is said that he only utilized a fraction of his immense power on the battlefield, leaving observers awestruck by the glimpse of his true capabilities. He would spend his time in Toran Castle's basement, preferring the darkness and perhaps musing on his magic research.

After the conclusion of the Gate Rune War, Crowley returned to the seclusion of the caverns, showing a disinterest in world affairs. Mazus, who participated in the Dunan Unification War three years later, sought a duel with his former master, but whether the…

recruitment: Talk to Crowley in a hidden passage within the Cave of the Past located at the back of Qlon Temple.
trivia:
• All three of Crowley's weapons have a celestial quality in their naming: Comet Rod, Meteor Rod, Cosmo Rod. The same is true for his disciple Mazus.
• Crowley is named after the real-life British mystic, Aleister Crowley.
• In Suikoden V, the player can find an Old Book which is about rune unites. Its author claims he discovered them during his research. If Crowley is indeed the discoverer of rune unites, he most likely is the author of the book. If that was true, then he did not know about the unite of Dawn Rune and Twilight Rune. Alternatively, the book could have been authored by someone else who discovered rune unites independently from Crowley.
• If what Piccolo says is true and he is…

name: Eikei
description: Eikei (エイケイ, Eikei) is a supporting character in Suikoden. Eikei is a powerful martial artist who respects only those he perceives as strong.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Eikei was a powerful wandering martial artist who only respected strength and was always wandering and looking to increase his power from fighting with warriors more powerful than himself, rather than to defeat strong opponents. His claw weapons are made of steel and so he was able to use them to deflect sword and arrow strikes.

He joined the Toran Liberation Army after being suitably impressed with the leader of the Liberation Army's level of strength in Teien. He had no interest in the ideological war between the Liberation Army and the Scarlet Moon Empire. Eikei was also not overly concerned with the hero's status as leader of an army. McDohl's personal strength was the only thing that impressed him.

According to some more obscure sources, during his time with the army, he opened a colosseum in Toran Castle, using his Lion's Blade Rune to summon monsters to attack opponents and aid in training purposes.

He seemed to get along with Kai, both being dedicated to their martial arts. He also used his physical strength to aid the Liberation Army in combat as its shield until the war's end, training himself and helping the army at the same time.

Following the end of the Gate Rune War, Eikei left to continue his journey in search of worthy opponents with seemingly no end in sight for his

recruitment: Talk to Eikei in Teien once the main character is at least Level 40
trivia: • In the Sega Saturn version of Suikoden, Eikei will open a battle arena at Toran Castle. The player faces several one-on-one battles versus monsters and receives rewards. This is the only way to obtain the Darkness Rune and the Silver Dragon Armor which were not available in the original PlayStation version of the game.

name: Eileen
description: Eileen (アイリーン, Airīn) is a supporting character in Suikoden who makes a cameo in Suikoden II. A dedicated family woman, Eileen is the wife of Lepant and the mother of Sheena.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Eileen, a woman of remarkable strength and grace, was the wife of Lepant, who served as the President of the newly established Toran Republic, and the caring mother of Sheena.

Her relationship with Lepant began at a social gathering hosted by a regional lord. Their connection blossomed rapidly, leading to love and eventually marriage. Eileen's strong-willed nature complemented her lady-like demeanor, making her a notable and respected figure.

During the Gate Rune War, Eileen fell victim to a kidnapping orchestrated by the lecherous Kraze Miles. However, her husband, along with the Toran Liberation Army, acted swiftly and successfully rescued her from captivity.

This incident led Lepant to consider divorcing Eileen in an attempt to join the Liberation Army without concern for her safety. Nevertheless, Eileen firmly rejected the idea of divorce, demonstrating her unwavering love for her husband and her preparedness for any hardships that may arise in their journey together.

Beyond her strength of character, Eileen was also skilled in the magical arts and had an aptitude for throwing arrow-like darts. Interestingly, her magical abilities and aversion to physical conflict seemed to be inherited by her son, Sheena. Throughout their time at Toran Castle, the Lepant family resided together, maintaining a sense of stability amidst the war.

As the Gate Rune War concluded, Lepant ascended to the presidency of the Toran Republic, and Eileen remained steadfastly by his side, deepening the bond between them. Eileen would also be the one to gift Kirinji to Sheena, at Lepant's behest, before he left on his travels.

Some have speculated that Eileen may hold considerable influence within the nation, as Lepant was unable to talk back to

recruitment:
Recruited automatically along with Lepant after story events in Kouan.
|armorIntRobe
|other1Int=Star Earrings
trivia:
• Eileen is an Irish name meaning "pleasing."
• Nothing different will happen in Suikoden if you bring Eileen along with Lepant to recruit Sheena.
• Although Eileen will protect both Lepant and Sheena in battle, only Lepant will reciprocate the favor.

name: Esmeralda
description: Esmeralda (エスメラルダ, Esumeraruda) is a character in Suikoden. She is a naïve and idle woman of aristocratic distinction.

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
Esmeralda's outfit consists of a pure white one-piece dress with an inverted V-shaped opening in the front and a pink skirt underneath, emblazoned with a rose motif. The large polka-dot ribbon at the waist was said to give the outfit a maidenly look.

She wore her blonde hair in long vertical rolls with a huge hair ornament, creating a romantic and dainty image. In her Genso Suikoden Card Stories TCG artwork, she is also depicted wearing a bonnet. Her eyes are depicted as green in Suikoden and blue in her Card Stories artwork.

She was said to look young for her age. Overall, her appearance was very elegant and befitting her aura of a naïve

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
Esmeralda is described as both naïve and elegant in her mannerisms, as well as somewhat dreamy in terms of her grasp on events. She not only interpreted the Toran Liberation Army approaching her as an attempted kidnapping, she also would demand to be brought a gift, the Opal before acquiescing to the supposed abduction.

She is also proud of herself and her beauty. Part of the reason for demanding an Opal from the group was because she steadfastly refused to undervalue herself or her worth.

Although she spent most of her time in the company of fellow narcissists Milich Oppenheimer and Vincent De Boule, overall Esmeralda found Toran Castle to be a wearisome and boorish place for

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Esmeralda was a slightly ambiguous and enigmatic but mostly elegant and naïve woman fond of dressing fashionably and luxuriously. Like Vincent De Boule, she appeared to be an aristocrat of the Scarlet Moon Empire, but any details on her noble name or her identity besides her gender seems to be shrouded in mystery.

This included why she was staying at an inn in Antei during the Gate Rune War. It is not clear whether she was fleeing the war, hiding from someone, or simply residing at the inn for an extended stay.

During said conflict, she was invited to join the Toran Liberation Army at the inn. Esmeralda misunderstood and believed she was being kidnapped, believing her beauty to be a curse which drew the interests of the Liberation Army.

She demanded the Opal both as a token of good faith and because she refused to undervalue her worth or beauty. The Liberation Army at the time was only comprised of a few and so recruited the unreasonable companion.

As a member of the Liberation Army, she was not a particularly active recruit, contributing little besides her presence. She had only a handful of friends such as Vincent and Milich Oppenheimer (and later Simone Verdricci) and they commonly took tea together.

After the Gate Rune War, it appears Esmeralda lived an elegant life in a house with a garden full of roses in Antei as a guest of Milich. Given that she was possibly a former Scarlet Moon Empire noble but lost her nobility following the war, she must have inherited her fortune.

Three years following the Gate Rune War, Nash Latkje would be inadvertently teleported by Viki to Esmeralda's location while she was attending a tea party for herself, Milich, Vincent and Simone in a flamboyant

recruitment: Bring the Opal (found by defeating Nightmares in Soniere Prison) to Esmeralda on the top floor of Antei inn once the castle is at least Level 2.
trivia: • Esmeralda was intentionally designed to be an idle or "useless" character who brought nothing to the Toran Liberation Army, though she does contribute soldiers and ATK value to her war unit, The Narcissists.

name: Fu Su Lu
description: Fu Su Lu (フー・スー・ルー, Fū Sū Rū) is a character in Suikoden. A powerful man wearing a tiger skin with a voracious appetite, Fu Su Lu is one of the physically strongest characters in the game.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Fu Su Lu was a huge man as well as a powerful warrior, being the only one able to swing his two heavy battle-axes with ease. He wore a tiger skin, with the head of the tiger being used as a mask. This appears to be not to conceal his identity. Rather, it seems to be tied into the purpose of his journey.

Fu Su Lu held a voracious appetite, a personality trait that showed itself in Kobold Village when he ate so much food, he received a 10,000 potch bill. Fu Su Lu would join the Toran Liberation Army when its leader agreed to pay off the debt.

An easy-going warrior, he was not particularly invest in the ideological conflict of the Gate Rune War but was happy to fight for his food. He fit into the kobold culture very well and even personally led the Kobolds unit during the conflict. Following the war, Fu Su Lu returned to Kobold Village for a short while before continuing on his journey. His whereabouts after this are

recruitment: Talk to Fu Su Lu in the Kobold Village after defeating Kwanda Rosman and agree to pay 10,000 potch.

name: Fukien
description: Fukien (フッケン, Fukken) is a supporting character in Suikoden. Fukien is the Head Monk of Qlon Temple.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Fukien served as the Head Monk of Qlon Temple. His long training as a monk appearently gave him some measure of insight into the flow of the world and the essence of things.

At some point, the virtuous Fukien became aware of the 108 Stars and predicted the gathering of the 108 Stars under the Starmaster. When visited by the leader of the Toran Liberation Army, he realized this was the person he was awaiting and that the wheel of history were slowly starting to turn.

Fukien also knew that the army had visited him in order to find a means to defeat Neclord and pointed them to the Cave of the Past located behind Qlon Temple, noting that what would be revealed there would be distressing. Once McDohl and his party retrieved the Celestial Sword, Fukien counselled McDohl to accept what he had seen in the past.

He would then travel with McDohl in order to participate in the gathering of the 108 Stars. He wielded two rods in battle, holding one in each hand and using them to dispel the evil spirits of the enemy. Following the Gate Rune War, Fukien resumed his duties at Qlon.

Fukien opened Qlon to all who wished to study there, including Sanchez, but was forced to eject the warrior priest Gantetsu for his increasingly violent behavior. Following the Dunan Unification War, however, Fukien welcomed a presumably changed Gantetsu back into the

recruitment: Recruited automatically upon meeting him in Qlon Temple for the first time.
trivia: • Fukien is interesting in that he appears to be one of few characters in the series who is fully aware of the 108 Stars.

name: Fuma
description: Fuma (フウマ, Fūma) is a character in Suikoden. He is a ninja not formally attached to any espionage organization, a rarity for the occupation.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Fuma was an enigmatic and skilled ninja known for his training at the secretive community of Rokkaku Hamlet. For reasons unknown, he eventually departed from the community and embarked on a solitary journey to expand his skills and knowledge.

During the Gate Rune War, Fuma was spotted in the Dragon Knight's Domain by the leader of the Toran Liberation Army. The leader's notable ability to detect Fuma's presence even when he was in stealth impressed the ninja. Eager to further develop his abilities, Fuma agreed to join the Liberation Army.

Throughout the Gate Rune War, Fuma played a vital role in the Liberation Army's espionage efforts, working alongside fellow ninjas, Kasumi, and Kage. He could be found atop Toran Castle practicing his technique. In battle, Fuma wielded a large manji-shaped shuriken, a weapon that demanded considerable skill to be effectively utilized.

Following the fall of the Scarlet Moon Empire, Fuma received an invitation from Hanzo to return to the ninja community at Rokkaku. Embracing this opportunity, he contributed to training the next generation of ninja, ensuring the legacy of Rokkaku's stealth and prowess would

recruitment: Talk to Fuma who is hiding behind the northeast tip of the walls of the Dragon Knights' Fortress exterior.

name: Gaspar
description: Gaspar (ガスパー, Gasupā) is a character in Suikoden and Genso Suikogaiden Vol.1. Gaspar is a gambler who specializes in the game of Chinchirorin.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Gaspar was an underground gambler and avid Chinchirorin player who ran his operations in Kaku. Frustrated at losing access to Tai Ho, his best customer, he would challenge the hero to a game of Chinchirorin.

Losing, Gaspar was suitably impressed by McDohl's bravery at gambling. Finding this attitude becoming of the leader of the Liberation Army, Gaspar decided to take a gamble on him, joining the army.

Gaspar thought of life and gambling as the same thing and the Gate Rune War was the gamble of a lifetime. As a man, he entrusted his life to luck, choosing to always live dangerously.

He opened up a Chichinorin stall at Toran Castle during the war. There Gaspar would sometimes win and sometimes lose great amounts of potch but always without complaint, believing it to be the way of the world and the nature of gambling. 

The fall of the Scarlet Moon Empire saw Gaspar win again and he moved his business back to Kaku. Shilo, who would later join the New Alliance Army was his long-time rival and the two would confront each other in the Greenhill Forest during the Dunan Unification War, engaging each other in a fierce gambling

recruitment: Talk to Gaspar in the basement of the Kaku bar and win at least 5,000 potch from him in Chinchirorin.
trivia:
• Losing to Gaspar when trying to recruit him will cause him to refer to the "God of Dice." This is one of very few references to any form of deity in the series.
• Gaspar's easily exploitable "double or nothing" feature when gambling with him makes him the perfect source for party funds.

name: Gen
description: Gen (ゲン, Gen) is a supporting character in Suikoden. A shipwright by trade, Gen invented a motorboat with the scientist Kamandol.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Gen was a highly skilled shipwright residing in the town of Teien. Known for his exceptional craftsmanship, he took immense pride in his work. With his strong-willed and inquisitive nature, he maintained a deep sense of pride in his creations. He was always looking for challenging new work in order to improve his skills.

Gen was also a neighbor of Kamandol, and while they often engaged in heated arguments and appeared to dislike each other, they shared a bond of mutual understanding and camaraderie.

During the Gate Rune War, the Toran Liberation Army sought Gen's expertise to construct a boat capable of navigating the treacherous Rapids of Dunan. He was curt with the group until he learned what they needed. Offered the chance to push his skills yet further, he eagerly agreed to the commission. Collaborating with Kamandol, he successfully built the required vessel, known as the Speedboat.

Subsequently, Gen decided to join the Liberation Army and served as a commander in the Carpenters unit during the war. As the conflict progressed, he further improved his ship, enhancing its speed and performance. He would often be seen at Toran Castle's docks, continually working.

Following the conclusion of the Gate Rune War, Gen returned to Teien, resuming his vocation as a shipwright, and continued to contribute his remarkable talents to his

recruitment: Recruited automatically in Teien during events in the Gouran region.
trivia:
• Gen wields his favorite work saw in combat and is pretty dangerous as he has ensured the sawteeth are sharpened for maximum efficiency both in his work and in battle.
• The names Gen bring up when struggling to remember Premier L'Amour include Plummy Lamar, Prommy Lamb, and Pramyam Lobby.
• In the English version of Suikoden, when Gen visited Kamandol, Kamandol offers him his finest tea but in the Japanese version, Kamandol offers him sake.

name: Georges
description: Georges (ジョルジョ, Jorujo) is a minor character in Suikoden. He is a wandering idler with an interest in card games.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Georges was a proficient gambler renowned for his expertise in card games. Embracing a nomadic lifestyle, he lived without any fixed abode or responsibilities, journeying freely across the world without concern. He described himself as a "free man" but also abashedly admitted he could be considered an "idler" also.

It was during his travels that he encountered the Toran Liberation Army and, interestingly, joined their ranks after losing a game of Matching Cards. He quite possibly viewed the Gate Rune War was merely yet another game for him to play.

Within the Liberation Army, Georges served as an archer and proved to be a capable and satisfactory combatant. Even amidst the turmoil of war, he remained true to his passion for card games and continued playing them during his time at the Liberation Army's base at Toran Castle. He got along well with fellow game players such as Marco.

After the conclusion of the Gate Rune War, Georges set his sights on the northern regions, enticed by a promising money-making opportunity. His natural affinity for gambling and risk-taking likely influenced his decision to explore new horizons in search of financial

recruitment: Talk to Georges in the Kirov inn and set a new record at any level of his card game.
trivia:
• In the English-language version of Suikoden, Georges describes himself as a 'free man.' A 'free man' is a person in the socioeconomic construct of medieval serfdom who is not tied to land. While this gave the free man a large amount of personal freedom it also meant the free man had no land with which to grow food or make money. As such, many free men turned to gambling or crime to survive.
• His design was based on a character from an original manga Kawano Junko used to draw as a hobby in middle school and high school.

name: Giovanni
description: Giovanni (ジョバンニ, Jyobanni) is a supporting character in Suikoden. He is the loyal servant of Lepant.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Giovanni was a dedicated clerk who served the influential Lepant family in Kouan, showing loyalty to Lepant and his wife, Eileen. Though sometimes timid and prone to complaining, Giovanni consistently delivered excellent work.

During the tumultuous events of the Gate Rune War, a critical moment occurred when Eileen was abducted by the town's newly appointed military commander, Kraze Miles. Giovanni attempted to intervene but was unsuccessful. However, he acted swiftly to inform Lepant, resulting in Eileen's timely rescue.

After Eileen's safe return, Lepant joined the Toran Liberation Army, and Giovanni chose to stand by their side, faithfully continuing his service. To the surprise of many, Giovanni displayed a remarkable talent for espionage, becoming a valuable member of the special thieves unit, aiding the Liberation Army with his stealth abilities.

Following the war, Giovanni's dedication earned him high praise from Lepant, inspiring him to become an entrepreneur with Lepant's support, establishing his own

recruitment: Recruited automatically following story events in Kouan.

name: Gon
description: Gon (ゴン, Gon) is a supporting character in Suikoden. Gon is a young kobold who is a close friend of Kuromimi.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Gon was a devoted friend and admirer of Kuromimi, whom he affectionately referred to as "big brother." Inspired by Kuromimi's prowess as a warrior, Gon aspired to become a skilled and admirable fighter, just like his idol.

His dreams were realized when he received am invitation from Kuromimi and the leader of the Toran Liberation Army to join their ranks. Elated by this opportunity, Gon eagerly accepted and became a valued member of the Kobolds & Pal regiment within the Liberation Army, determined to reach the same heights of greatness as Kuromimi.

In battle, Gon wielded a fine-quality sword, and although he could have given it a special name, he chose to keep his weapon names simple, much like his idol. He spent his time at Toran Castle at Kuromimi's side, thrilled to be with his idol having missed him during his absence from the Kobold Village.

Following the conclusion of the Gate Rune War, Gon remained dedicated to his training, striving to become the foremost kobold warrior. Deep in his heart, he held the belief that he would forever remain in second place, humbly accepting his role as an ardent admirer and follower of his beloved idol,

recruitment: Talk to Gon in Kobold Village with Kuromimi in the party once the castle is at Level 2.
trivia: • Gon was named after the lead character of Japanese children's story Gongitsune (ごん狐), a little fox named Gon.

name: Griffith
description: Griffith (グリフィス, Gurifisu) is a supporting character in Suikoden. A man with a great deal of respect for his men's lives, Griffith was a soldier in the Scarlet Moon Empire Imperial Army as well as the Toran Liberation Army.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Griffith was appointed the commanding officer of the Northern Checkpoint during the Gate Rune War, to protect Moravia Castle from the south. As an officer of the Scarlet Moon Empire, Griffith always posed as a slippery person but in actuality, he cared for the lives of his troops more than his own pride.

His airy demeanor covered his more serious and responsible attitude towards his men. This trait was exploited by the Toran Liberation Army in their attack on the Northern Checkpoint as Griffith quickly agreed to surrender in exchange for the lives of his men.

Following his surrender, Griffith joined the Liberation Army and aided the rescue of Warren from Moravia Castle. For the remainder of the war, he fought bravely in the Former Imperial Soldiers unit was often seen in the company of his fellow former imperials at Toran Castle. His swords were painted black, which added an extra layer of intimidation to the man in battle.

He would state he had no problem joining the Liberation Army as they were more likely to win the war, saying he preferred to side with the strong. However, it seems he may have said this out of embarrassment of his swift defeat at the Northern Checkpoint.

After the war, he refused to join the nascent Toran Republican Army and instead started a business, the talents that allowed him to lead his men so well being applicable to more than just

recruitment: Recruited automatically following the Battle at the Northern Checkpoint.
trivia: • Despite changing sides in the war, Griffith still remains a subordinate of Kasim Hazil in the Former Imperial Soldiers unit following Kasim's recruitment.

name: Hanzo
description: Hanzo (ハンゾウ, Hanzō) is a minor character in Suikoden and Suikoden II. Hanzo is the leader of the ninjas of Rokkaku Hamlet.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Hanzo was the leader of the skilled ninjas of Rokkaku Hamlet and was well regarded for his ability and insight. As a ninja he was skilled not only in espionage but at uncovering deception and seeing the hidden truth in people, making him talented in judging others. He had become chief in the traditional manner, being selected as Vice-Chief by the previous incumbent due to his ability and succeeding them after their passing.

During the Gate Rune War, Rokkaku resisted imperial rule but was attacked by and fell to Teo McDohl's Armored Cavalry, bringing it under the control of the Scarlet Moon Empire. Hanzo helped Kasumi escape the carnage, and ordered her to tell the Toran Liberation Army of what had happened.

Following the last battle, Hanzo appeared before McDohl, introducing himself and admitting he and the other surviving ninjas had hid after the fall of Rokkaku, biding their time. Kasumi was surprised at Hanzo's survival, and Hanzo apologized to her for having to keep it a secret. He would assist the Liberation Army during the invasion and securing of Gregminster Palace.

Following the Toran Liberation War, Hanzo welcomed Kasumi back to Rokkaku and eventually appointed her as Vice-Chief of Rokkaku, recognizing her potential as a ninja and leader. He would also receive Fuma, who had left the village previously. Over the next three years, he would devote himself to the restoration of the village.

During the Dunan Unification War, meeting with the hero, he recognized the same look in his eyes as McDohl, who had led the Liberation Army to victory three years prior and judged him a worthy ally. He bequeathed his subordinates Sasuke and Mondo to the New Alliance Army as part of their alliance with the Toran

trivia: • Hanzo is named after the famed Sengoku era ninja, Hattori Hanzō. He was the head of a clan from Iga, and served Tokugawa Iyeasu loyally. Hattori Hanzo is also commonly depicted in manga and other forms of fiction.

name: Hellion
description: Hellion (ヘリオン, Herion) is a character in Suikoden. Hellion is a magician known for her 'Power of Recall'.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Hellion, an esteemed and elderly magician, was known for her exceptional mastery of the Power of Recall. Possessing a sharp intellect and keen perception, she was widely regarded as a true sage, deserving of the title bestowed upon her.

Captivated by the potential future that both the leader and his powerful Rune of Life and Death held, Hellion joined the ranks of the Toran Liberation Army. In a display of her wisdom and generosity, she bestowed upon the leader the invaluable Blinking Mirror, a magical artifact that allowed its wielder to swiftly return to its larger counterpart when gazed upon.

Thanks to Hellion's remarkable contribution, the travel time of the Liberation Army's officers was significantly reduced, greatly enhancing their efficiency and effectiveness. In addition to her strategic role, Hellion showcased her incredible prowess as a magician, unleashing rare and potent spells that decimated the forces of the Imperial Army.

After the conclusion of the Gate Rune War, Hellion's exceptional talents were acknowledged, leading to her appointment as the esteemed Seer of the Toran Republic. In this revered position, she assumed the role previously held by Leknaat on Magician's Island, where her wisdom and foresight continued to shape the destiny of the

recruitment: Talk to Hellion in Teien's inn and item shop.

name: Hix
description: Hix (ヒックス, Hikkusu) is a supporting character in Suikoden and Suikoden II who makes a cameo in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.1. Hix is a young warrior who is undergoing a trial of manhood.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Hix was a timid and somewhat indecisive young man from Warriors' Village. He and Tengaar grew up together and the two eventually became a couple.

When the Toran Liberation Army arrived in Warriors' Village, Hix would ask Cleo why she fought as women generally did not become warriors in the village. Cleo's response, that regardless of her gender she had people and ideals she wanted to protect, resonant with Hix who had been unable to find any true motivation for fighting.

When Neclord took Tengaar, Hix's indecisiveness saw him do nothing to stop the vampire. It was only after her abduction that his inner courage awoke as a descendant of Clift the Crusader. Swearing to rescue Tengaar, Hix joined Zorak, McDohl and the villagers in storming Neclord's Castle.

Hix begged Zorak and McDohl to allow him to go battle Neclord alongside them. Recognizing his newly discovered motivation, Zorak allowed Hix to go after designating Hix as a warrior and granted him permission to name his sword. Hix aided McDohl's party in combat and they proved victorious over the vampire.

After leaving Neclord's castle, Tengaar and Hix volunteered to join the Liberation Army. He would wield his sword to prove himself as a full-fledged warrior on the battlefield. Following the end of the Gate Rune War, Hix became further accepted as a true warrior and left on a journey with Tengaar at his side.

Hix continued traveling, pushed by the tenacious Tengaar into continuing his Manhood Ceremony. Eventually, they traveled to the City-States of Jowston in order to undergo the Test of the Unicorn in Kobold Village. However, when they arrived, they discovered the ritual was no longer practiced. 

After spending the night in the Chief of Kobold Village's home, Hix was unable to wake Tengaar. The Kobold Chief claimed Tengaar was suffering from the curse of the unicorn and that the only way to reawaken her was…

recruitment: Recruited automatically before entering Neclord's Castle.

name: Hugo (Suikoden)
description: Hugo (ユーゴ, Yūgo) is a character in Suikoden. He serves as a librarian working at Qlon Temple.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Hugo was a librarian and scholar who worked and studied at Qlon Temple in Lorimar. He was also responsible for the upkeep and organization of ancient scrolls and texts held by the temple archives. He was very knowledgeable and dressed and carried himself as someone much older, though he was a young man during the Gate Rune War.

His meticulous personality led him to despair if his materials and books were not organized correctly. When he noticed a gap in the bookshelf caused by the missing Illustrated War Scroll, he was anxious to restore it to its proper place. He decided to join the Toran Liberation Army after members of the organization helped him retrieve the scroll he had misplaced from deep within the Cave of the Past.

At the Liberation Army headquarters at Toran Castle, he was in charge of the library there, organizing and maintaining the bookshelves with his impeccable filing skills. Following the conclusion of the Gate Rune War, Hugo became a member of the Toran Republic government, being appointed the fledgling nation's first Chief

recruitment: Bring Hugo the Illustrated War Scroll found inside the Cave of the Past.

name: Humphrey Mintz
description: Humphrey Mintz (ハンフリー・ミンツ, Hanfurī Mintsu) is a supporting character in Suikoden and Suikoden II who also appears in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2. Humphrey is a skilled but taciturn swordsman.

facts.label: Appears in
facts.value: Suikoden, Suikoden II, Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2, Suikoden STAR LEAP

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
Humphrey has a strong, rugged appearance with short, spiky blonde hair and sharp facial features, including thick eyebrows, and a defined jawline. His skin is fair, and his eyes are a hazel shade of brown.

In Suikoden, Humphrey wears a full suit of metallic armor with a segmented design worn over black underclothes. The armor is accented with gold details, including rounded studs. He also wore violet wristbands and a short, mustard cape.

His Suikoden II outfit is more geared towards traveling. He wears a green tunic with long sleeves, secured at the waist with a brown belt. Over this, he wears a large yellow cloak that drapes over his shoulders and extends down his back. He also wears black pants and brown boots. His broadsword is strapped to his back and is wrapped in cloth, rather than kept in a

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
A taciturn man, Humphrey's silence causes confusion in others, and some consider him a loner or even unfriendly. But for the most part, Humphrey's silence is not perceived as cold, but rather as shyness.

As such, he is not a strong socializer. Though it is also suggested that Humphrey was more open before the Kalekka Incident, he was never a paticularly gregarious man.

Despite his silent nature, Humphrey would occasionally open up to others. Before the Battle at Floating Fortress Shasarazade, Humphrey told Mathiu Silverberg how he had attacked his commanding officer during the Kalekka Incident; it had been the first time, and, he hoped, the last time, he had slain someone out of sheer hatred. Humphrey also enjoyed a long (albeit one sided) talk with the hero and paid for the young man's room in gratitude.

Humphrey was also a practical man. Despite his shock at hearing of the death of Odessa Silverberg, he immediately recognized that McDohl would be able to succeed Odessa as a leader. When Humphrey and Futch discovered Bright, Humphrey was prepared to destroy Bright should Futch fail to accept him, despite the rarity of the creature.
