Befitting his logical nature, Humphrey was also unafraid to ask former adversaries for aid; during the Gate Rune War, Humphrey asked Kreutz to join the Toran Liberation Army, immediately noticing that Kreutz would be of great help to the force. Despite his practicality, Humphrey could also be empathetic, and comforted Futch over Black's

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Imperial Service
Humphrey Mintz was a renowned centurion of the Scarlet Moon Empire, celebrated for his mastery of the greatsword, a weapon measuring over one meter in length. His skill and unwavering dedication earned him the nickname "Broadsword Humphrey," a title that reflected both his martial prowess and his imposing presence on the battlefield.

As a centurion, Humphrey held the highest rank attainable by ordinary soldiers within the Empire. Despite his achievements, he repeatedly declined offers of promotion to the officer class, extended to him by Kasim Hazil. Humphrey preferred to remain among the ranks of common soldiers, a decision that underscored his humility and loyalty to his comrades. In his earlier years, he served under the corrupt general Gillan, though his integrity remained unblemished.

Humphrey's reputation for honor and strength was further solidified during a mission to investigate a series of dragon attacks. It was during this mission that he met Joshua Levenheit, Commander of the Dragon Knights, and the two formed a lasting friendship.

Humphrey earned Joshua's respect by refusing to follow orders to deliver a slain dragon's corpse to Gillan, who sought to exploit the remains for personal gain. This act of defiance demonstrated Humphrey's commitment to justice over blind obedience.

During the War of Succession, Humphrey fought under Emperor Barbarossa Rugner, where he developed a rivalry with Kreutz, a general serving under Geil Rugner. At the time, Humphrey was a staunch believer in Barbarossa's cause. However, his faith in the Empire was shattered two years later when he was ordered to participate in the Kalekka Incident, a brutal massacre of civilians.

Overcome with rage, Humphrey attacked his commander and became a fugitive, marking the first time he had ever acted out of anger.
Liberation Army
Following the incident, Humphrey…

recruitment: Recruited automatically in Kaku after defeating Kwanda Rosman.
trivia: • Humphrey's weapon, despite being a broadsword, upgrades as Muramasa, Murasame, and Masamune, three historically significant types of katana.

name: Ivanov
description: Ivanov (イワノフ, Iwanofu) is a supporting character in Suikoden. Ivanov is a skilled artist whose fame reaches from Toran to Falena.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Ivanov is a genius painter, known for his keen senses and excellent sense of color. "Freedom" was always the theme of his works, but he found himself imprisoned in Scarleticia Castle by Milich Oppenheimer, who coveted his abilities. However, while there, Ivanov found himself unable to paint, his lack of freedom destroying his creative energies no matter the environment.

During the Toran Liberation Army's siege on Scarleticia Castle, he beseeched the army to give him his freedom. Following the defeat of Milich and encountering the freedom fighter leader, a reinvigorated Ivanov remarked that he had never seen true freedom and would join the Liberation Army so he could see the real freedom represented by the Liberation Army.

He would use a room at Toran Castle as his studio and with the aid of paints discovered over the course of the Gate Rune War, Ivanov completed a mural of prominent Liberation Army warriors as part of his meditation on freedom. Delighted with the progress he had made on capturing "Freedom", he would gift his binoculars.

Following the end of the war, Ivanov set out on a journey, to try and discover the more colors to express freedom, which had unlimited possibilities in his

recruitment: Talk to Ivanov inside Scarleticia Castle following plot events at the same location.
trivia: • Ivanov's ending will change depending on whether or not he completes the mural he is painting in Toran Castle.

name: Jabba
description: Jabba (ジャバ, Jaba) is a supporting character in Suikoden. Jabba is a master appraiser who has only failed to appraise a single item in his long career.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Jabba, an esteemed expert appraiser, operated an appraisal store located in the quaint town of Rikon. Known for his unwavering confidence in his own discerning eye, Jabba boasted the ability to appraise virtually any item with ease, showcasing his profound expertise in dating and placing antiques.

Throughout his illustrious career, Jabba encountered countless artifacts, leaving no doubt about his vast experience in the field of appraisal. He even claimed he would do whatever was asked of him should he prove stumped by any object. However, there remained one elusive object from his past that had managed to defy his classification: the Nameless Urn.

It was during the Gate Rune War that Jabba's expertise was truly put to the test. When McDohl presented him with the enigmatic Nameless Urn, even the seasoned appraiser had to acknowledge defeat. True to his word, Jabba agreed to join the ranks of the Toran Liberation Army.

Post-war, Jabba's remarkable talents did not go unnoticed. His unique eye for appraisal earned him a position of great responsibility within the newly established Toran Republic. He was entrusted with the crucial task of financial management as treasurer, ensuring the prosperity and stability of the budding nation.

By the year 475, after years of dedicated service to the Toran Republic, Jabba chose to retire from his post, leaving behind a legacy of excellence and wisdom in the world of antiquities and

recruitment: Bring a Nameless Urn (found by defeating Holly Fairies on the world map outside Rikon) to Jabba in the appraisers' in Rikon and choose to recruit him.
trivia: • If you sell Jabba the Nameless Urn instead of recruiting him, he'll give you a measly 100 potch for the antique. You can find another one and try and recruit him again.

name: Joshua Levenheit
description: Joshua Levenheit (ヨシュア・レーベンハイト, Yoshua Rēbenhaito) is a supporting character in Suikoden. Joshua was the captain of the Knights of the Dragon's Den during the Gate Rune War.
originalName: ヨシュア・レーベンハイト

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Dragon Knight Commander
The circumstances surrounding Joshua’s inheritance of the Dragon Rune remain shrouded in mystery. What is known is that he rose to prominence in an age of turmoil, the Dragon Rune binding his spirit to that of the dragons and granting him both their command and their continued existence in the mortal realm.

Burdened with immortality, Joshua guided the Dragon Knights for over two centuries, never claiming a single dragon as his mount. To him, the loss of any dragon was as grievous as the death of his own kin.
Friendship with Humphrey
Joshua held Humphrey Mintz in high esteem, considering him a trusted friend. Their first meeting came when Humphrey, then a Centurion of the Scarlet Moon Empire, was dispatched to investigate rumors of a dragon’s rampage. Joshua accompanied him, and though his slight frame belied his station, his skill in battle was beyond question.

Together, they confronted the undead dragon Sterling, who had risen as a revenant to guard its fallen master. Humphrey defied his orders in order to preserve Sterling’s honor, an act that earned Joshua’s admiration. From that day, Humphrey was regarded as a sworn ally of the Dragon Knights.
Gate Rune War
Though Joshua had pledged fealty to Emperor Barbarossa during the War of Succession, the growing corruption of the Scarlet Moon Empire and its increasing disdain for the Dragon Knights caused a rift.

During the Gate Rune War, when the dragons were struck down by Windy's poison, Joshua was persuaded by Humphrey and the sage Liukan to seek the aid of the Toran Liberation Army.

An antidote was devised, but it required the liver of Black, the dragon of the young knight Futch. Joshua bore the terrible responsibility of informing Futch of the sacrifice, knowing it would result in his expulsion from the order. Seeking to ease his grief, Joshua entrusted Futch to Humphrey’s care, perhaps…

recruitment: Recruited automatically following events at the Dragon Knight's Domain and Seek Valley.
trivia: • He was originally going to be named Geist (ガイスト, Geisto) but Joshua ended up being chosen as a more "dignified" name.

name: Juppo
description: Juppo (ジュッポ, Juppo) is a supporting character in Suikoden. Juppo is a trickster, as well as an inventor.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Juppo was an inventor of various machines, bearer of the Gadget Rune and a self-proclaimed trickster, always dressed in bizarre clothing. His unique fashion, as well as his manner of speech, were perhaps a symptom of his eccentric genius.

Already able to create some mechanical devices, he travelled to increase his knowledge. During the Gate Rune War, he had met Lepant while in the middle of a journey to learn about mechanical devices and became his guest after Lepant recognized his talents, constructing several devices for the security of Lepant's mansion.

He would join the Toran Liberation Army along with Lepant. At Toran Castle, he spent his time working around Kamandol's laboratory, with the two sometimes trading knowledge with one another. During the war, he would use his creations to fight the Imperial Army as part of the Tricksters unit.

Following the war, he lived in a workshop in Rikon for about two years, honing his skills and occasionally receiving orders for curious gadgetries such as mechanical birds and more practical tools such as herb grinders. While there, he tried to counsel his niece, Meg, whose bad habit of ignoring blueprints and hastily disassembling and reassembling creations in different (usually non-working) configurations, often frustrated him.

He had plans to leave on a journey to gain further mechanical knowledge. So, in order to shake off his niece, who had been pestering Juppo to teach her, he left her his creation, the barrel robot Gadget, and ordered him to bring her home to Lenankamp before

recruitment: Joins automatically after story events in Kouan.

name: Kage
description: Kage is the Teni Star in Suikoden. He also makes an appearance in Suikoden II.

name: Kai
description: Kai (カイ, Kai) is a supporting character in Suikoden. Kai is a master stickfighter who trained the younf McDohl in the use of his staff.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Kai was the hero's former bo staff instructor, living in the household of Teo McDohl. One day he disappeared, only saying that he was going for a walk and never returning.

Years later, during the Gate Rune War, Kai encountered the hero again at the Fortress of Garan, where Kai wanted to journey west, but was prevented from doing so due to the Imperial Army crackdown and so offered his services and support to the Toran Liberation Army. 

Even after becoming leader of the Liberation Army, the hero always referred to Kai as "Master Kai" with deference and respect. Kai's presence helped to bolster the hero's heart as he suffered from having to duel his own father over the course of the war. From the master's standpoint, the hero still had a lot to learn.

In battle, Kai used his Haziness Rune as well as numerous other stunning tricks to gain the upper hand in battle. His staff was quite long, slightly taller than Kai himself and was of a simple design, save for the gold tips on both ends.

Following the formation of the Toran Republic, Kai became one of the country's Six Great Generals and instructed troops in the use of bo staffs. As the past instructorof the Gate Rune War's hero, he held a great deal of respect among his charges. By the year 475, Kai vacated his position and assumed the title of Honorary

recruitment: Talk to Kai at the Fortress of Garan any time after the plot events in Seika.
trivia: • Kai's outfit in the Genso Suikoden Card Stories TCG has the emblem of the Liberation Army emblazoned on it. His Suikoden artwork displays the emblem of the Scarlet Moon Empire.

name: Kamandol
description: Kamandol (カマンドール, Kamandōru) is a supporting character in Suikoden. An alchemist by trade, Kamandol invented an engine that ran on oil.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Kamandol was a distinguished alchemist hailing from the town of Teien, who devoted his life to extensive research, particularly following the theories presented by the alchemist Dennis Toller in his renowned work "Dialogues of Wind and Earth." In addition to his alchemical pursuits, Kamandol showcased remarkable skills as a mechanic, successfully crafting an engine that operated on burning oil.

Noted for his idiosyncrasies, those in his vicinity often perceived him as a grumpy and eccentric old man who strongly disliked interruptions. To discourage uninvited visitors, he even had a sign on his door imploring people to stay away.

Although socializing was not his forte, he did engage in good-natured arguments and shared drinks on occasion with his neighbor, Gen. It was Gen who recognized the potential value of Kamandol's work and convinced him to join the Toran Liberation Army, as he believed Kamandol's expertise could aid the party in navigating the treacherous Rapids of Dunan.

During the events of the Gate Rune War, Gen and Kamandol collaborated and further improved their design, leading to the creation of a more powerful Speedboat. While at Toran Castle, Kamandol tirelessly toiled away in the castle's laboratory, devoting himself day and night to his work.

He despised being called old. Although his youth was indeed spent, it was all for the purposes of research and his youuthful enthusiasm never wavered. His mind was as youthful and curious as ever when it came to discovering new applications for his alchemy.

After the conclusion of the Gate Rune War, Kamandol simply resumed his unwavering focus on alchemical studies, continuing his profound dedication to the

recruitment: Recruited automatically in Teien during events in the Gouran region.
trivia:
• Kamandol's final weapon, the Rod of Hermes is a reference to the "Staff of Hermes," which is the symbol of medicine.
• Kamandol is described in Old Book Volume 5 as a "Popular alchemist of Teien."
• In the English-language version of Suikoden, when Kamandol is visited by Gen, he offers Gen his finest tea, but in the Japanese version, Kamandol offers Gen sake.

name: Kanaan
description: Kanaan (カナン, Kanan) is a minor antagonist in Suikoden. He is the Assistant Commander of the Imperial Guard and something of a negative symbol for the bloated Scarlet Moon Empire of the time.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Kanaan was Vice Commander of the Imperial Guard under Kraze Miles and a typical example of a corrupt Imperial official of the time.

As an officer, his only goal was to fill his own pockets, making him extremely susceptible to bribes. He was also prone to taking credit for his subordinate's efforts. His advancement came entirely from his irritating brand of flattery. 

Serving under Kraze as his adjutant, he was ordered to accompany McDohl to Rockland and investigate the tax situation there. He would report Ted's use of the Soul Eater to Windy. After Kraze was demoted for being unable to capture McDohl, Kanaan served under Yuber for the remainder of the Gate Rune War.

Kanaan fled after the establishment of the Toran Republic, turning to a life of petty crime and fraud but was later arrested and

name: Kanak
description: Kanak (カナック, Kanakku) is a supporting character in Suikoden. Kanak is one of Anji's subordinates in their pirate gang.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Kanak was a member of the lake pirate crew that operated on Lake Toran. He served as part of the crew under the leadership of Anji and alongside fellow crew member Leonardo. Unlike the stereotypical boisterous and roughneck persona often attributed to lake pirates, Kanak exhibited a friendly and even-tempered nature.

During combat situations, Kanak displayed a surprising level of ferocity and showed his exceptional physical strength, particularly when defending his friends and allies. This quality was evident when confronted by the Imperial Army, who witnessed his formidable prowess in battle.

When visited by the Toran Liberation Army, Kanak was the only member of the pirate crew not to act disparagingly towards the group. Kanak would follow Anji and Leonardo into the force following their defeat by McDohl, Tai Ho, and Yam Koo. 

Throughout the course of the Gate Rune War, Kanak remained a steadfast member of the Liberation Army. He possessed a quiet demeanor that set him apart, even when compared to the likewise reserved Humphrey Mintz. Despite his silence, Kanak was known to wear a consistent smile and exhibited a friendly disposition, welcoming interactions with those he encountered.

After the culmination of the Gate Rune War and the subsequent formation of the Toran Republic, Kanak continued to stand by his friends, faithfully embracing their new business

recruitment: Talk to Anji at Pirate's Fortress with Yam Koo and Tai Ho in the party and defeat him in battle.

name: Kasim Hazil
description: Kasim Hazil (カシム・ハジル, Kashimu Hajiru) is a supporting character in Suikoden. Kasim Hazil is a Great General of the Scarlet Moon Empire at the time of the Gate Rune War.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Kasim Hazil is one of the Great Generals of the Scarlet Moon Empire and the Toran Republic. He is said to be the greatest swordsman of the group. During the War of Succession, Kasim rose to the rank of general, fighting for Barbarossa Rugner, with Mathiu Silverberg serving as his army strategist.

Normally a good-natured man, as a commander he was full of confidence and dignity. He would also become a very close friend of fellow general Teo McDohl and was considered the second most experienced of the Great Generals behind Teo.

He is known as "Kasim of the Blue Moon" due to his Blue Moonlight sword. This curved blade scimitar was forged by Senan nomads using a long-lost technique of the Sindar. These people has since disappeared and the secrets of Kasim's sword have been lost to time. Originally from the northern wastes himself, Kasim was entrusted with overseeing the Senan region from Moravia Castle as he was used to desert warfare.

During the early stages of the Gate Rune War, Kasim Hazil attempted to recall Mathiu Silverberg into the service of the Scarlet Moon Empire, but this attempt failed. Following the death of his good friend Teo McDohl, Kasim Hazil was assigned to guard the northern defenses and decided to build up his forces. Kasim also began to apply extreme pressure on the rebel factions within Senan.

A man of strong patriotism and loyalty, it was thought that Kasim Hazil would be impossible to convince to join the Toran Liberation Army. When the Liberation Army infiltrated Moravia Castle and Mathiu manipulated the Jowston army into invading, Kasim was infuriated as a man who had spent his life protecting the north from Jowston.

Even though he knew Mathiu oppossed the empire, Kasim never thought their view of Jowston would differ so. However, he was ultimately convinced to stand down by Milich Oppenheimer, fighting for the Liberation Army for the…

recruitment: Recruited automatically after plot events in Moravia Castle.
trivia:
• Kasim is a Persian name meaning "the one who cuts."
• Kasim is the only Great General that is not an optional recruit. If Milich Oppenheimer was not recruited, Mathiu will lambaste Kasim until he joins instead.
• At the beginning of the American version of Suikoden, Kasim Hazil is mistakenly referred to as "Lord Kasim Hazin." 
• Kasim's Ending in the original Japanese version of Suikoden was changed in translation to the English language version; in the English version, he accepts the position of Commander-in-Chief conflicting with Kwanda Rosman who also accepts the position in his ending, but in the Japanese version, he turns this position down in order to return to Moravia Castle.
• An…

name: Kasios
description: Kasios (カシオス, Kashiosu) is a supporting character in Suikoden. Kasios is a passionate musician who had an undying respect for his muse, Milich Oppenheimer.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Kasios is a troubadour who swore loyalty for Milich Oppenheimer. Unlike Ivanov, who was forced to use his artistic talents for Milich's pleasure, Kasios was completely loyal to Milich due to the general's understanding and appreciation of music.

As Milich's personal bard, Kasios sang daily in his home at Scarleticia Castle. He had little interest in moral conflicts such as those between the Imperial Army and the Liberation Army, preferring to focus on his craft and those who appreciated it.

Still, Kasios joined the Toran Liberation Army, following his master and patron with the utmost loyalty. At Toran Castle, Kasios would perform a wide variety of songs upon request. His music and ballads would inspire and lift the spirits of a great many people within the army throughout the war.

Following the Gate Rune War, Kasios departed on a personal journey to spread the songs of the Liberation

recruitment: Talk to Kasios in Scarleticia Castle with Milich Oppenheimer in the party following plot events there once the castle is at Level 3 or higher.
trivia:
• In Old Book Volume 5, Kasios' entry states him to be a woman. This is an error; Kasios is indeed a man. It also states that Kasios is in love with Milich, which is another mistranslation.
• That said, several staff members also apparently confused Kasios for a woman.

name: Kasumi
description: Kasumi (カスミ, Kasumi) is a supporting character in Suikoden and Suikoden II. Kasumi is a ninja from Rokkaku Hamlet who rose to the position of Vice-Chancellor of Rokkaku.

facts.label: Appears in
facts.value: Suikoden, Suikoden II, Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Kasumi was a ninja from Rokkaku Hamlet. Like all Rokkaku ninja, she had been trained in the swallow manor from a young age.

Her time in the manor overlapped with Sasuke for her final two years there, where she became an admirable sister-figure for the young ninja. Kasumi would help take care of Sasuke during those two years.

Bearing the Shrike Rune which gave her further abilities, Kasumi was said to have a natural affinity for the ninja arts. As such, she was highly skilled and even higher-ranked ninja in Rokkaku looked up to her.

Kasumi was forced to flee her home when Teo McDohl's Armored Cavalry attacked Rokkaku. Following the Toran Liberation Army's victory in the Second Battle at Scarleticia Castle, Kasumi successfully reached Toran Castle and warned the Liberation Army of Teo's advance, fighting with the group for the remainder of the war.

Her talents at espionage were utilized several times through the war, including in the infiltration of Moravia Castle. Kasumi's talents usually meant that enemy movements were laid out for all to see. In battle, her long reach and ninjitsu abilities made her well able to defeat her opponents.

After the last battle, Kasumi discovered that a number of ninja, including Hanzo, survived Teo McDohl's attack on Rokkaku. She had fallen in love with the leader of the Liberation Army during the war but was unable to confess her feelings.

Her feelings were not always as hidden as she would have liked. Krin, in particular, mocked her for her crush and change in demeanor whenever the hero was in her vicinity. She decided to follow her duty, rather than her heart, and following the end of the war, Kasumi returned to Rokkaku.

Three years later, during the Dunan Unification War, Kasumi had become Vice-Chief of Rokkaku Hamlet. She had initially turned down the position due to feeling inexperienced, but eventually relented as she felt…

recruitment: Recruited at Toran Castle automatically following story events at Scarleticia Castle.
trivia:
• Kasumi means "mist" in Japanese.
• Her claw weapon is designed for assassination purposes.
• She was originally designed to use a shuriken, which is why her weapon range in the original Suikoden is long-range. The change to a claw weapon came in mid-development and her range was not updated to match.

name: Kessler
description: Kessler (ケスラー, Kesurā) is a supporting character in Suikoden. Kessler was a bandit who supported the ideals of the Toran Liberation Army.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Kessler was the chief of a gang of bandits who used to haunt Mt. Tigerwolf. Kessler held such esteem for Odessa Silverberg and the Toran Liberation Army that he became enraged after he discovered his subordinate, Ledon, had drugged and robbed Odessa with Robber's Tea without realizing who she was.

He held strong resentment towards the oppressive nature of the Scarlet Moon Empire but felt powerless to do anything as a mere bandit. As such, he entrusted all his hopes to Odessa. That said, in his heart, he wished to abandon his banditry in order to fight for the people.

Later on, during the height of the Gate Rune War, Kessler and Ledon travelled to the Liberation Army's Secret Factory after an Imperial crackdown at Mt. Tigerwolf. There, they were discovered by Ronnie Bell, who captured them after accusing them of snooping. Luckily, the Liberation Army members he had met at Mt. Tigerwolf arrived.

Kessler joined the Liberation Army, fighting with them for the remainder of the war. He wielded a slightly undersized double-edged axe, a hatchet, in battle. While he was happy to be able to fight for a worthy cause, he possibly felt too uncomfortable as a bandit to associate with the core members of the Liberation Army.

After the formation of the Toran Republic, Kessler's caring and distinguished personality as well as his familiarity with the region led to him being appointed the captain of the Toran Republic Patrol Corps. By the year 475, he had retired from the

recruitment: Talk to Kessler at the Secret Factory after witnessing his conversation with Ronnie Bell.

name: Kimberly
description: Kimberly (キンバリー, Kinbarī) is a supporting character in Suikoden. A master counterfeiter, Kimberly joined the Toran Liberation Army in order to put her skills to use.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Kimberly was a skilled artisan crafter residing in the town of Antei. Her true role, however, was as a renowned master counterfeiter. Her exceptional talent allowed her to create near-perfect forgeries, and she primarily earned her income by forging cargo slips for smuggling purposes.

Known for her fondness for alcohol, her avoidance of hangovers, and her avid pursuit of romantic interests, Kimberly had been commissioned by Mathiu Silverberg for cargo smuggling purposes in the past. Mathiu reached out to her through a written letter, seeking her assistance in forging a document for the Toran Liberation Army.

Upon having Mathiu's Letter read to her, as she was unable to read it herself, Kimberly stipulated that she would only join the Toran Liberation Army if Flik accompanied her to dinner. Despite Flik's reservations, Viktor permitted Kimberly to proceed as she desired with Flik.

After spending an evening with Flik, Kimberly made her way to Toran Castle. Before departing, she informed McDohl and his party about the whereabouts of Tesla. Together with Tesla, Kimberly skillfully forged the Fake Orders required by the Liberation Army, attributing the exceptional quality of their work to her pursuit of perfection.

Throughout the course of the Gate Rune War, Kimberly maintained a close working relationship with the Liberation Army. In combat, she wielded a kitchen knife that had been sharpened to possess the lethality of a sword.

During her service with the Liberation Army, Kimberly encountered Tai Ho and his sworn brother, Yam Koo. She attempted to pursue Tai Ho romantically, prompting Yam Koo to intervene in an effort to protect his friend from her advances. Subsequently, Kimberly established herself in Tai Ho's household after their marriage.

However, their marriage was far from harmonious, and Tai Ho and Yam Koo eventually traveled to Dunan with the assistance…

recruitment: Recruited automatically during events in Antei.
trivia:
• Kimberly's name, in Suikoden II, is spelled as Kimberley, but in Suikoden, it is spelled as Kimberly.
• Kimberly and Flik drink tea together in the English version of Suikoden, but in the Japanese version, she and Flik consume sake; "".

name: Kirke
description: Kirke (キルケ, Kiruke) is a supporting character in Suikoden. Kirke is an executioner who describes his sole talent as "cutting off heads."

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Kirke, who served as an executioner stationed at the Fortress of Lorimar, found himself facing a peculiar predicament during the Gate Rune War. Neclord's sinister acts of reanimating the deceased in the region significantly affected his line of work, causing business to slow down considerably.

The morbid nature of his duty, involving the execution of criminals through decapitation, had a profound impact on Kirke's heart, darkening it unbeknownst even to himself. However, fate would soon offer him an unexpected opportunity for redemption when he received an invitation to join the Toran Liberation Army. This invitation heralded a new chance at life, allowing Kirke to escape the confines of his disliked occupation and embrace a brighter path.

Embracing his new role as a valued member of the Wild Geese unit within the Liberation Army, Kirke's once ominous scythe, which had once served as an instrument of finality for the condemned, now symbolized hope and resilience in the pursuit of a better world. With the hatchet-like blade of his scythe now drawn out to champion the cause of the Liberation Army, Kirke showcased his mettle on the battlefield.

Following the triumph of the Liberation Army and the fall of the Scarlet Moon Empire, Kirke made a remarkable career shift, relinquishing his scythe to the capable hands of Lorelai and embracing a new vocation as a farmer, where he would wield a plough instead, symbolizing the fertile prospects of a peaceful

recruitment: Talk to Kirke at Lorimar.
trivia:
• Kirke's three weapon names are derived from Tarot cards. He shares this distinction with Suikoden II's Rina.
• Kirke has become somewhat of a memorable optional character because he wants to make sure you are completely okay with the fact that all he knows how to do is cut off heads.

name: Kirkis
description: Kirkis Shana Ques Laviankaana (キルキス・シャナ・クエス・ラビアンカーナ, Kirukisu Shana Kuesu Rabiankāna), also known simply as Kirkis, is a supporting character in Suikoden. Kirkis is an elf who attempted to prevent the destruction of the Village of the Elves.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Kirkis was an idealistic elf known for his strong aversion to the prejudice prevailing among the races inhabiting the Great Forest. His fervent desire was to foster peaceful cooperation between elves, dwarves, and humans, despite the traditional disdain elves had historically shown toward other races. Due to his radical views, Kirkis faced suspicion and mockery from his fellow elves.

Upon learning of the Burning Mirror and having his fears dismissed by his fellow elves, Kirkis embarked on a journey to seek assistance from the Toran Liberation Army in countering Kwanda Rosman's scheme to annihilate the Village of the Elves. He fled his village, swimming across Lake Toran to reach the Liberation Army, a perilous journey that nearly resulted in his drowning.

Despite Kirkis's efforts and the support of the Liberation Army, Kirkis would be imprisoned for his efforts. His fiancée, Sylvina, would ask him why he cared so much about humans. He would eloquently express his desire for peace between all peoples to her. Loving Sylvina more than anyone else, he wanted her to understand his feelings most of all.

Freed by Slyvina, Kirkis would fight on valiantly but ultimately Kwanda's Burning Mirror ultimately led to the destruction of the Village of the Elves, crushing his spirits for a time, as he felt that distrust between races had ultimately cost the lives of his people. Realizing the importance of hope and learning of the survival of Sylvina, Kirkis would resolve to fight on.

Following the Battle at Pannu Yakuta, Kirkis and Sylvina joined the Liberation Army, continuing their fight for peace. He, like his elven comrades, preferred to spend their time in the outdoor areas of Toran Castle, in order to feel closer to nature. In combat, Kirkis utilized a light bow, complementing Sylvina's chosen weapon.

After the conclusion of the Gate Rune War, Kirkis dedicated himself to…

recruitment: Joins automatically at Toran Castle following plot events in Kouan.

name: Kraze Miles
description: Kraze Miles (クレイズ・マイルス, Kureizu Mairusu) is a minor antagonist in Suikoden. Cruel and disdainful, Kraze Miles was the Commander of the Imperial Guard during the Gate Rune War.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Kraze Miles served as Commander of the Imperial Guard during the Gate Rune War. Known for his snide and scornful demeanor, he embodied the corrupt spirit prevailing within the Scarlet Moon Empire.

His hair was thick with oil, and he had a thin beard and mustache. His high-pitched voice was supposedly very harsh on the ears.

Despite being considered a coward, Kraze managed to advance his career primarily through influential connections, a factor that contributed to his unpopularity among his subordinates.

One notable individual who served under Kraze was the son of Teo McDohl, who frequently faced belittlement from him through sarcastic remarks. Working with Windy, he attempted to procure the Soul Eater for her by luring Ted into a trap. However, Ted's escape and the hero's subsequent escape from Gregminster with the rune led to his fall from Windy's favor and subsequent demotion.

As a consequence of his failures, Kraze was reassigned to the town of Kouan as its new military commander, a position he deeply resented. Being "exiled to the countryside" frustrated him, and this led to frequent clashes with Lepant, another significant figure in Kouan. Eventually, Kraze committed the heinous act of abducting Lepant's wife, Eileen.

However, the tables turned when Lepant, along with the emerging Toran Liberation Army, successfully rescued Eileen, thereby overthrowing Kraze's rule over Kouan. The fate of Kraze Miles ultimately rested in the hands of the very hero he had once scorned and

trivia:
• Kraze is the only character in Suikoden that can be executed without imposing any long-term penalties on game completion. If you do not execute him, he will run off, never to be seen again, and Gremio will chide you for it, although Cleo will praise you for having a big heart.
• For some reason, Konami is quite unambiguous about Kraze's fate considering it's optional; practically all reference material and novelisations state that he was executed, although the Kiwami Encyclopedia reverts back to an ambiguous ending.

name: Kreutz
description: Kreutz (クロイツ, Kuroitsu) is a character in Suikoden. Kreutz was a loyal follower of Geil Rugner during the War of Succession who stayed in the Dragon Knight's Domain following the war.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Kreutz was a warrior who served under Geil Rugner during the War of Succession, having served Geil since his youth. As such, he sometimes referred to Geil by his old name, Georg. Loyal to Geil, Kreutz was considered the only truly great leader in his oppressive government. He was a rival of Humphrey Mintz and was gravely wounded by him during the war.

Following the death of Geil and the ascension of Barbarossa Rugner to the throne, Kreutz made his way to Dragon Knight's Domain and secluded himself. As an autonomous region recognized by the Scarlet Moon Empire, he was safe from extradition and reprisal there. Even years later, he still considered Geil to be his only master.

There, during the height of the Gate Rune War, his former rival from the War of Succession, Humphrey, invited him to join the Toran Liberation Army, considering him Geil Rugner's greatest general. Though initially taken aback, Kreutz accepted this offer only for the chance to kill Barbarossa and avenge his master. He held no interest in the struggles of the Toran Liberation Army itself.

Still, Kreutz was a powerful ally in battle, wielding an oversized, two-handed sword in battle. Kreutz's strength with the weapon was such that he could crush bones with the power behind his swing, instead of merely killing foes quickly. It is said that fighting with the Liberation Army allowed him to regain his stature as a warrior after having lived in the Dragon Knight's Domain for the past seven years.

Following the defeat of Barbarossa and the fall of the Scarlet Moon Empire, Kreutz simply left the country, his vengeance secured, refusing attempts to bring him into the Republican

recruitment: Talk to Kreutz in the Dragon Knight's Domain with Humphrey in the party.

name: Krin
description: Krin (クリン, Kurin) is a supporting character in Suikoden. Krin is a thief who attempts to use situations for his own profit.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Krin was a petty thief who cannot really be considered anything of a real threat. While skilled at escaping and disappearing, he lacked any real cunning as a thief and was usually dreaming of bigger heists, making him prone to blunders in the present. He was seen operating in Lenankamp, breaking into one house and stealing the savings of another person from their room at the Keyaki Inn.

During the Gate Rune War, he attempted to steal the sword Kirinji from Lepant's mansion in Kouan, both directly and by trying to manipulate McDohl, only to fail both times. Still, he did not discard his dreams of wealth, instead deciding to join the Toran Liberation Army. He did not really understand the conflict between the Liberation Army and the Imperial Army, only joining having smelled the possibility of a profit from following the group.

He would prove useful during the war in espionage actions, helping to sneak the Liberation Army into Moravia Castle to rescue Viktor and Warren. Even then, his cowardice was clear as he maintained he would abandon the party at the first sign of trouble, much to Kasumi's ire. The two had a volatile relationship, with Krin mocking Kasumi for her crush on the hero.

Despite seeming like something of a dolt, Krin was in fact quite shrewd. At the end of the war, Krin managed to loot Gregminster Palace before disappearing with his ill-gotten

recruitment: Recruited automatically following plot events in Kouan.
trivia: • Before his compulsory recruitment, Krin can be found in Lenankamp. If you bring him back there towards the end of the game, you can find a small scene of him being confronted by one of his victims on the second floor of the inn.

name: Kun To
description: Kun To (クン・トー, Kun Tō) is a supporting character in Suikoden. A sea-faring merchant and trader, Kun To transported the Fire Spear from Kirov to Toran Castle for the Toran Liberation Army.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Kun To was a highly regarded merchant residing in Kirov, known for his thriving trade ventures. He earned the affectionate moniker "Boss" in the town due to his benevolent nature. While engaged in legitimate business through trade, Kun To also dabbled in smuggling and other illicit activities.

However, during the Gate Rune War, the efforts of the Scarlet Moon Empire to crack down on such illegal activities limited Kun To's involvement in such ventures. Despite this setback, his combined experience in both legitimate and underground dealings garnered him considerable wealth over the years.

When his old friend, Tai Ho, a member of the Toran Liberation Army, arrived in Kirov seeking assistance to smuggle Fire Spears from the Secret Factory to Toran Castle, Kun To readily decided to join the cause. He saw this as an opportunity to support the Liberation Army and play a pivotal role in their endeavors. He would spend his time at Toran Castle with fellow wealthy merchants, Warren and Lepant.

Following the conclusion of the war, Kun To assumed the leadership of the Merchant's Guild within the newly established Toran Republic. In this position, he wielded significant influence, and some speculate that his true motivation for supporting the Liberation Army was to gain power within the new republic.

Moreover, Kun To continued to assist his friend Tai Ho, this time by offering him employment and aiding his escape from his wife, Kimberly. He facilitated Tai Ho's journey to the City-States of Jowston, ensuring the continuation of their enduring friendship and mutual

recruitment: Recruited automatically in Kirov following plot events at the Secret Factory.
trivia: • In the English-language version of Suikoden II, Kun To is referred to as Kun Tze, but this is a mistranslation, not a relative as was once theorized.

name: Kuromimi
description: Kuromimi (クロミミ, Kuromimi, Black Ears) is a supporting character in Suikoden. Kuromimi is a kobold warrior who set out to rescue Kobold Village's townspeople from the effects of Kwanda Rosman's Conqueror Rune and later arose to the position of Chief of Kobold Village.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Kuromimi is a valiant young kobold warrior, driven by a quest to find a cure for the afflicted kobolds of Kobold Village, who were driven into a frenzy by Kwanda Rosman's Conqueror Rune. He was a kind-hearted kobold who cared deeply for his friends and family.

In his pursuit of a cure, Kuromimi journeyed to the Village of the Dwarves, seeking their assistance. However, his efforts proved fruitless as the dwarves refused to provide any remedy.

Undeterred, he later aligned himself with the Toran Liberation Army, joining forces to combat the Imperial Army and thwart Kwanda's malevolent schemes, particularly in protecting the Village of the Elves.

Even when surrounded by the Imperial Army in the Kobold Village, Kuromimi never gave up, admonishing the beleaguered Liberation Army group. After the successful defeat of Kwanda Rosman in the Battle at Pannu Yakuta, Kuromimi experienced a heartwarming reunion with his now-cured mother and brother, whose joyous excitement led them to repeatedly exclaim his name.

Even after accomplishing his initial goal, Kuromimi expressed his desire to continue serving the greater cause. Filled with determination, he opted to join the Liberation Army, acknowledging that even if his people were now better, the nation as a whole still faced challenges.

Throughout his time with the army, he demonstrated his compassionate nature while training and fighting alongside Gon, a younger kobold who greatly admired the seasoned warrior. Kuromimi wielded a sword of excellent craftsmanship, though he opted not to give it a special name, similar to his friend Gon, preferring simplicity. He and Gon both preferred the outdoors at Toran Castle, enjoying the sun on their fur.

Upon the conclusion of the Gate Rune War, Kuromimi returned to Kobold Village, where his bravery and compassion were recognized. He was appointed as the Village Chief, taking on a…

recruitment: Recruited automatically prior to the Battle at Pannu Yakuta.
trivia:
• Kuromimi is listed as "Black Ears" in the United States Suikoden manual. This is likely because the words kuro and mimi mean black and ears, respectively, in Japanese.
• Despite giving a speech where he is portrayed as the leader of the kobolds unit, Kuromimi is actually only a subordinate of the kobolds unit; the Commander is the non-kobold Fu Su Lu.

name: Kwanda Rosman
description: Kwanda Rosman (クワンダ・ロスマン, Kuwanda Rosuman) is a supporting character in Suikoden. One of the Five Great Generals of the Scarlet Moon Empire, Kwanda Rosman loyally served Barbarossa for 10 years.

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
Kwanda Rosman was a large, powerful man. He had blue eyes. He is never shown without his helmet and full body armor, so his hair color is unknown. His silver armor consists of large pauldrons, a breast plate, graves, and gauntlets. His chest plate alone weighs over 30kg (66 lbs.). Underneath he wears navy blue clothing as well as a red tabard.

His halberd has an axe head attached to one side of the spearhead and a thorn on the opposite

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
Some assume that Kwanda's strength lies in his armor, but they would be mistaken. He is able to fight for long periods of time without any loss of stamina due to his armor. He also has the mental strength to continue defending himself against any attack without moving at all, and to aim for an opening in the enemy.

He has inexhaustible stamina and unbreakable mental strength. He was stern looking and known to have a short temper but was also respected as a honorable man.

His loyalty to Emperor Barbarossa was absolute but he also held firm to his convictions as a warrior, declaring he could not serve the current emperor, whoever they were, in the crumbling Scarlet Moon Empire. It is said that if he were not under the corrupting influence of Windy's Conqueror Rune he would have been even more difficult for the hero to overcome in

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Kwanda Rosman was a powerful soldier and Great General who favors the use of the halberd in battle. Kwanda was born into a military family and his impressive armor had been handed down through his family for generations.

When the War of Succession broke out, Kwanda was an officer stationed at Pannu Yakuta Castle. He would become part of Barbarossa's forces when Barbarossa began to reorganize his forces from the region. Kwanda's force, which protected Barbarossa, became known as the Guard. Following the war, they would be reorganized into the Imperial Guard.

During the War of Succession, he once used his own body as a shield when Barbarossa was taken by surprise by a volley of archers and had a visible scar since. He wore his armor ever since. He became known as "Iron Wall Rosman" and "Kwanda the Insurmountable" for his invulnerable defense, heavy armor and dedication towards protecting Barbarossa's life during the war.

Five years prior to the Gate Rune War, Kwanda Rosman was stationed at Pannu Yakuta Castle and, later, under the influence of Windy's Conqueror Rune, violated the Great Contract, culminating in his use of the Burning Mirror to destroy the Village of the Elves. In response to this, the Toran Liberation Army defeated Kwanda at the Battle at Pannu Yakuta and its leader defeated Kwanda in a duel.

Freed from the effects of the Black Rune and realizing that the Empire was quickly becoming corrupt beyond repair, Kwanda initially requested to be executed as befitting a warrior. However, the hero would convince him to join the Liberation Army as his work was not finished. He wished to remonstrate with the emperor and be the wall that shielded him from the corrupt influences destroying the Empire.

As a former Great General, he was more comfortable spending time with his fellow former imperials at Toran Castle. Following the fall of the Scarlet Moon Empire,…

recruitment: Choose not to execute Kwanda when given the opportunity following the duel with him at Pannu Yakuta Castle.
trivia:
• The names for Kwanda’s hatchets in the original: Alkaid, Mizar, and Alioth, are stars in the constellation Ursa Major, forming the tail of the bear; these stars are also present in the constellation of the Big Dipper. 
• The remaster updated the name of Kwanda's hatchets to: Merak, Dubhe, and Alkaid, all of which are still stars in the constellation Ursa Major.
• Kwanda is the first Star of Destiny the player will encounter who has the option of being executed. Should one choose to execute Kwanda when given the first opportunity to do so, Windy will show up to taunt the player, but she will not appear if the player chooses to kill Kwanda later on in the scene after he requests his…

name: Ledon
description: Ledon (ルドン, Rudon) is a supporting character in Suikoden. Ledon was a bandit who would rob travellers at his inn on Mt. Tigerwolf.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Ostensibly an inn keeper based on Mt. Tigerwolf, in actuality Ledon would serve Robber's Tea in order to rob unconscious patrons of their valuables.

He did relish his work and he saw his announcement of the tea's name after his victims consumed it as something of a victory blow against a foolish foe. However, he had a code never to kill people, and some would describe him as having an innocent side despite his activities. 

During the Gate Rune War, he poisoned the son of Teo McDohl in this way but Kessler prevented any theft when he noticed that Odessa Silverberg was part of the group.

Later, Ledon's inn was destroyed by angry Scarlet Moon Empire troops after he had attempted the trick once again and fleeing with Kessler to the Secret Factory he was able to join the Toran Liberation Army.

At Toran Castle, he would serve normal tea to comrades there, apparently giving him an idea. Following the war, Ledon went on to find some success, this time opening a prosperous honest

recruitment: Talk to Ledon at the Secret Factory after witnessing his conversation with Ronnie Bell.
trivia: • The antidote to Ledon's Robber's Tea includes a rabbit's tail, rose leaves, and a handful of sugar.

name: Leknaat
description: Leknaat (レックナート, Rekkunāto) is a character in Suikoden, Suikoden II, Suikoden III, Suikoden IV, and Suikoden V. Leknaat is a mysterious, blind seer with an interest in the 108 Stars and holds possession of the back half of the Gate Rune.

facts.label: Appears in
facts.value: Suikoden, Suikoden II, Suikoden III, Suikoden IV, Suikoden V, Suikoden STAR LEAP

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
Leknaat is a tall, slender woman with dark hair that reaches all the way down her back, to her feet. As her eyes are always shown closed, her eye color is unknown. She has some sort of jewel prominently placed in her forhead.

After fleeing from the massacre of the Clan of the Gate, she would wear a mask while traveling. The Masked Man would wear this very same mask during Suikoden III.

Leknaat is always shown in a hooded, white robe with yellow trim. The robe is worn over a similar colored dress. The exact details of her outfits change, but she is normally shown with pink detailing on her robes and a pink tabard. The tabard is not worn during Suikoden IV. Her feet are mostly hidden by her robes, but she appears to wear navy blue shoes.

She is usually shown with at least one bracelet worn on her left wrist. In the first three Suikoden games, she wears a gold choker on her neck. Suikoden IV shows her without any neck adornment. In Suikoden V she wears a more ornate

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Leknaat is a blind seer who survived the massacre of the Clan of the Gate by forces from the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia. She and her clan sister, Windy, used the power of the Gate Rune in order to escape. Leknaat and Windy each took one half of the Gate Rune, Leknaat receiving the "back" or "exit" half of the rune in her chest.

She would become known as the "Keeper of Balance" and would dedicate herself to this maintaining of balance in the world, saying that the will of the runes was nothing less than the truth of the world. Where exactly she received her knowledge of the 27 True Runes is uncertain but she would appear infrequently to offer advice and guidance to those who bore them.

During the Island Liberation War, she would appear to the bearer of the Rune of Punishment on several occasions and even confronted the Fog Ship Guide, urging the being to return to the Eternal City.

Intending to keep the balance of the world, Leknaat isolated herself on Magician's Island. Leknaat also became the Seer of the Scarlet Moon Empire, preparing Astral Predications for them and took on an apprentice, Luc, after rescuing him from his imprisonment in the One Temple.

In the events of the Sun Rune War, Leknaat appeared several times before the Prince to impart her wisdom on the nature of the Dawn Rune, as well as its counterpart, the Twilight Rune, and the Sun Rune itself.

She first appeared at Ceras Lake following the occupation of Doraat Fortress by the Imperial City Recapture Army. Noting that Zerase was present, Leknaat asked her to help the Prince on his quest before vanishing after giving a cryptic message about the three runes of Falena.

She reappeared once again in the Deep Twilight Forest ruins, where the Prince had headed in the hope of increasing the power of his Dawn Rune in order to save Lyon. She let the party know that the visions seen in the Sindar ruins were…

name: Leon Silverberg
description: Leon Silverberg (レオン・シルバーバーグ, Reon Shirubābāgu) is a supporting character in Suikoden and an antagonist in Suikoden II. Leon Silverberg is a skilled military strategist with experience in many wars including the War of Succession, the Gate Rune War and the Dunan Unification War.
originalName: レオン・シルバーバーグ

facts.label: Family
facts.value: Albert (grandson), Caesar (grandson), George (son), Mathiu (nephew), Odessa (niece), One o

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Imperial Service
Leon Silverberg is a famed strategist of the Silverberg family and served as Barbarossa Rugner's chief strategist during the War of Succession, leading Barbarossa's forces to a stunning victory. Possessed with a rational and realistic eye for strategy, his goal was to achieve victory swiftly at any cost, with the aim of minimizing protracted suffering even if that resulted in immediate loss.

Prior to his Imperial service, he lived his life traveling around the country. He would occasionally visit his nephew, Mathiu in Gregminster and would become his guardian, tutoring his nephew in the ways of military strategy. Once the War of Succession broke out, Mathiu would introduce Leon to Emperor Barbarossa, leading him to become the lead strategist for his forces.

Following the war, Leon would both propose and participate in the Kalekka Incident and he is historically credited as the man who rallied the Scarlet Moon Empire forces after witnessing the devastation supposedly wreaked upon the village by the army of the City-States of Jowston.

In reality, Leon had planned and led the razing of Kalekka in order to shore up support for the Empire's defensive war against the Jowston invaders. Once he saw Windy begin to frequent the halls of power within the empire, he would retire from service.

Following his retirement from Imperial service, Leon moved to Kalekka, not out of any sense of regret but simply because he liked the silence of the now-abandoned town.
Active Retirement
During the Gate Rune War, Leon remarked how impressive the Toran Liberation Army had become and to let Mathiu know this, in an encounter with McDohl. In turn, Mathiu asked the leader to deliver a letter to Leon but nevertheless journeyed to Kalekka himself to beg for Leon's aid, noting that while he could not trust Leon, he could trust his abilities. 

Leon himself was surprised but…

recruitment:
Once the castle is at level 4 (90+ Stars recruited), talk to Leon in Kalekka and deliver his message to Mathiu when he is not in the meeting hall. Deliver Mathiu's letter to Leon and he will join. There are essentially four opportunities to do this if you've been recruiting efficiently.
• After returning from Neclord's Castle and agreeing to go to the Dragon Knight's Domain.
• After returning…
trivia: • Leon's oddly proportioned Suikoden artwork was due to Kawano Junko working without a draft due to a time crunch.

name: Leonardo
description: Leonardo (レオナルド, Reonarudo) is a supporting character in Suikoden. Leonardo is one of Anji's subordinates in his pirate crew.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Leonardo was a vandal who wrought havoc in the Lake Toran region, having pledged allegiance to Anji as a member of his band of pirates. With fervor for combat coursing through his veins, he wielded a prized hatchet, becoming a formidable force on the battlefield. The band patrolled the lake, boldly looting Imperial ships to amass wealth and goods.

Bound by an unwavering loyalty to Anji, whom he esteemed as a leader worth risking his life for, Leonardo willingly followed him into the ranks of the Toran Liberation Army, alongside his comrade Kanak, following the trio's defeat at the hands of the Liberation Army's leader, Tai Ho, and Yam Koo.

Despite the perils of confronting the mighty Scarlet Moon Empire head-on, Leonardo fearlessly embraced the cause. In most circumstances, he exclusively heeded the commands of Anji, the only leader he deemed worthy of his unwavering trust and respect.

As the Gate Rune War reached its conclusion, Leonardo, along with Anji and Kanak, transitioned into a new venture, embarking on a shipping business, building their future

recruitment: Talk to Anji at Pirate's Fortress with Yam Koo and Tai Ho in the party and defeat him in battle.

name: Lepant
description: Lepant (レパント, Repanto) is a character in Suikoden who makes an appearance in Suikoden II. He is a rich merchant who became the President of the Toran Republic.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Rise to Prominence
Lepant was from Kouan, a prosperous town in the Gouran region. From a young age, he displayed a remarkable aptitude for both the literary and martial arts, earning him widespread respect among his peers.

However, it is also said that during his youth Lepant acted in a similar manner to his future son, Sheena, flirting with multiple woman and generally wandering carefree, looking for good times. This side of Lepant would be tempered later in life by duty and, of course, marriage.

As he grew older, Lepant became Kouan's wealthiest trader, and his business acumen and philanthropic efforts were credited with bolstering the town's welfare. His mansion became a hub for intellectuals, alchemists, and scholars, who frequented his estate to seek his patronage and engage in intellectual discourse.

Lepant's reputation as a fair and capable leader grew, and he became known for his charisma, sense of responsibility, and unwavering commitment to his family. Despite his mild-mannered demeanor, Lepant was fiercely protective of those he loved, a trait that would define many of his later actions.
Family and Friendships
During a party hosted by a local lord, Lepant met Eileen, the daughter of a regional lord. The two quickly developed a deep romantic connection and married shortly thereafter.

Their union produced a son, Sheena, born in SY 442. Lepant's devotion to his family became a cornerstone of his character, influencing many of his decisions in both his personal and political life.

Lepant's intellectual curiosity also led him to seek out Mathiu Silverberg, a renowned military strategist who had retired to Seika to establish a school. The two men struck up a friendship, often sharing drinks and discussing philosophy, strategy, and governance.

Lepant provided material support for Mathiu's school, further solidifying their bond. This friendship would later…

recruitment: Recruited automatically during plot events in Kouan.
trivia:
• Lepant is perhaps named after the town of Lepanto or the battle which took place there. On 7 October 1571, there was a large naval battle in the Gulf of Patras (Greece), near the town of Lepanto. 
• Lepant passes his sword onto his son, Sheena, after taking on a more prominent government role.
• Lepant is the only returning character in Suikoden II who receives a portrait, but never joins the player's party or convoy.
• Like McDohl, Lepant's ending in Suikoden was inexplicably changed in the English versions of Suikoden; in his Japanese ending, he becomes elected the first President of the Toran Republic, but the English version says that Lepant returns to Kouan to live in peace with his…

name: Lester
description: Lester (レスター, Resutā) is a supporting character in Suikoden who makes a cameo in Suikoden II. Lester is a chef who specialises in making stews.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Lester was a master of making stew in the city of Kirov. He was passionate about researching stews day and night in search of the ultimate taste. It is said that he and Antonio, another cook, once fought each other over Marie, the innkeeper.

During the Gate Rune War, Lester joined the Toran Liberation Army, thrilled after McDohl sampled his stew in a private residence. Seeing that McDohl truly understood stew, Lester was only too happy to join him, proudly working at the inn in Toran Castle.

Like Antonio, Lester would fight in battles during the Gate Rune War. He would wield his cooking pan in battle, where it would receive surprisingly few dents due to its strength.

After the war, Lester would open a restaurant specializing in stews. Later, during the Dunan Unification War, he would challenge Hai Yo to a cook-off on Antonio's suggestion. This duel would help Hai Yo realize the secret to Retso's stunning victory

recruitment: Examine the stew cooking in both small houses in Kirov before returning to the large house in town and speaking to Lester there.

name: Liukan
description: Liukan (リュウカン, Ryuukan) is a supporting character in Suikoden who makes a cameo in Suikoden II. He is considered to be the finest physician in all of the Toran Republic.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Liukan was renowned as the "Holy Doctor," and his true age remained a mystery, even to himself, having lived so long he could no longer recall it. His medical skill was peerless; any ailment treated by his prescriptions was swiftly cured, earning him widespread reverence as a healer.

Many traveled to Rikon to seek his help over the years. In the past he even treated Sheena, Lepant's son. Yet, though he could mend the body, he lamented that he could not heal the turmoil in men's hearts that drove them to war and violence. Disillusioned by the chaos of the age, he withdrew from the world, retreating deep into the mountains in seclusion. 

During the Gate Rune War, General Milich Oppenheimer, fearing Liukan might devise an antidote to the deadly poison of his flower Antoinette, had him forcibly taken and imprisoned in Soniere Prison. Though Liukan had refused to involve himself with medicine, his captivity persisted until the Toran Liberation Army stormed the fortress to free him.

When Gremio sacrificed himself to shield his comrades, Liukan was moved, declaring that though he sought to withdraw from the world, he could no longer not involve himself. From that moment, he resolved to lend his skills to the liberation cause. 

Liukan proved invaluable, neutralizing Milich’s poisonous fog and later rousing the slumbering dragons with his remedies. Despite his advanced years, he even took to the battlefield, wielding his walking staff with surprising force. During the war, he resided in Toran Castle, often in discourse with the monk Fukien, the two reflecting somberly on the toll of conflict. 

When peace came, Liukan established a clinic, saving countless lives—yet he remained a recluse, seldom descending from his mountain refuge. Years later, during the Dunan Unification War, he emerged once more to treat the youth Ko, who had been poisoned by a monstrous attack in…

recruitment: Recruited automatically at Toran Castle following plot events at Soniere Prison.
trivia:
• Liukan is sometimes referred to as the "Holy Doctor."
• Liukan's weapon upgrades as Stick, Oak Stick, and Ebony Stick. Both of these types of wood have medicinal uses - Oak bark has been used by herbalists for years as an astringent, and the leaves and other parts of the trees of the ebony family were traditionally used to treat intestinal parasites, wounds, dysentery, and fever.

name: Lorelai
description: Lorelai (ローレライ, Rōrerai) is a recurring character who appears in Suikoden, Suikoden II, Suikoden V, Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2, and Genso Suikoden Card Stories. Lorelai is a Sindar hunter, hoping to uncover the mysteries of the ancient race.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Adventures in Falena
Lorelai was a warrior enduring an adventurous journey. The purpose of her journey was never entirely clear, but it seemed tied into the Sindar, the ancient people who had mysteriously disappeared from history. She has gained, and thwarted, many rivals in this quest including Killey, whom she managed to get thrown into a dungeon.

She and her rival Killey seem to have crossed paths several times; at one point, the two even became trapped in the Deep Twilight Forest ruins together and would nearly have died, if not for a time-traveling Viki (along with Nash Latkje, Millie, and Meg) crashing through the ceiling of the Twilight Ruins, creating an opening for them to escape from.

Two years later in 449, Lorelai tracked fellow "Sindar hunter" Zweig to the ruins at Ceras Lake, demanding to explore the interior with Zweig and the Prince, even challenging the Prince to a duel to prove he was worthy. Upon the success of unlocking the sluice gate at the ruins and the discovery of the Ceras Lake Castle, Lorelai joined his army along with Zweig, wanting to study the newly found ruins.

There, she once again met Viki. Lorelai teased Viki about letting her out of the Twilight Forest ruin; however, Viki had no recollection of this and was frightened by Lorelai's insistence that she admit that she had done it, complaining that she didn't know why Lorelai kept picking on her. Lorelai also once again crossed paths with Killey, who also joined the Prince's army, over her objection.

After the war, Lorelai helped herself to Zweig's research materials and took off to journey to where it pointed her - towards the Scarlet Moon Empire.
Exploring the Northern Continent
During the Gate Rune War, she would join in the Toran Liberation Army in Kouan, seemingly just to improve her own strength. She had no interest in the motivations of the Liberation Army or their…

recruitment: Talk to Lorelai at the inn in Kouan when the Hero is at Level 25 or above.
trivia: • Lorelai's in-game design was initially based on Odessa's but quickly developed beyond that.

name: Lotte
description: Lotte (ロッテ, Rotte) is a supporting character in Suikoden. Lotte is a magician who happens to be fond of cats.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Lotte was a skilled self-proclaimed magician from Kanakan, always accompanied by her cat, Mina. Her proficiency with magic was evident, wielding a rod nearly as tall as herself with ease. She did not have a naturally battle-minded temperment.

During the Gate Rune War, Lotte found herself lodging at an inn in Rikon when her beloved Mina went missing. Grateful for the help she received in locating Mina, who had somehow ended up in Kaku, from the hero, she willingly offered her aid to the Toran Liberation Army.

Lotte's magical abilities proved invaluable in supporting the Liberation Army during various battles throughout the Gate Rune War. Her dedication to their cause was evident as she used her full power to assist them as a member of the Children of the Runes unit.

Following the conclusion of the war, Lotte settled into a small house in Gregminster, continuing to live with her cherished companion, Mina.

During the Dunan Unification War, Lotte found herself with another companion named Momo, adding to her collection of feline

recruitment: Talk to Lotte on the first floor of Rikon's inn, then go to Kaku and catch the cat roaming the village before returning it to Lotte in Rikon.

name: Luc
description: Luc (ルック, Rukku) is a character in Suikoden, Suikoden II, and Suikoden III. He is also confirmed to appear in the upcoming Suikoden STAR LEAP.

facts.label: Appears in
facts.value: Suikoden, Suikoden II, Suikoden III, Suikoden STAR LEAP

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
Luc was said to be a very handsome boy when silent, but his bitter, prideful attitude and poor character tended to negate that. He has green eyes and brown hair that reaches his shoulders.

In Suikoden and Suikoden II, he wears a green tabard over white robes with gold fringe as well as brown boots. He also wears a small circlet around his forhead. For Suikoden, Kawano Junko designed Luc with the idea of making him coy. Meanwhile, Murayama Yoshitaka felt that he had a strange presence and said a lot with his eyes, even when silent.

In Suikoden III, he wears a long, dark green coat over a brown shirt, along with black pants, brown boots and black gloves, to hide his True Wind Rune. His clothing is lighter and more practical for the traveling he was undertaking as part of the Destroyers.

He no longer wears his circlet, and his hair is cut slightly shorter than before. This is one of the few things that differentiates him physically from Sasarai, who he looks otherwise identical to. His ring, the Guardian's Eye, serves as a medium in place of a staff, and is both expensive and precious.

The mask he wore as the Masked Man was previously Leknaat's, which she wore after fleeing the Clan of the Gate massacre. Luc would play with the mask as a child, finding it in Leknaat's

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
Luc has a great deal of magical power and is skilled in the arts of summoning and teleportation. He was also terribly unsociable and said to have a rotten personality. As a youth, he was considered cocky, mocking, and generally unpleasant to be around. This discrepancy being his looks and personality was said to make him more charming to some. Still, he was always unfalteringly polite around Leknaat.

Even so, at the time of the Gate Rune and Dunan Unification Wars, he had hope in his heart that he was fighting for a better future. As he grew older, this gave way to a sense of despair and anger about his destiny and the nature of his existence, entwined with the True Wind Rune.

Though filled with an uncompromising sense of duty and seemingly prepared to do anything for the greater good, his heart also contained real human frailty. The most obvious example being his letting the little girl he encountered in the Karaya Village flee. Although it could have proved detrimental to his entire plan, he could not bring himself to personally kill the child.

This conflict, of dooming the Grasslands to save the future, and the weight of responsibility Luc placed upon himself would continue to haunt him over the course of the War of the Champions. Although he considered himself something other than human due to the nature of his creation, Luc himself was unaware that he had lived for decades as a human and was fighting for and with that same humanity he forsook.

In Suikoden, Luc used the power of the True Wind Rune to summon a single Clay Doll, but in Suikoden III he was able to conjure up and control numerous monsters on the battlefield. Over the years, he mastered the techniques of his master, Leknaat, as well as his True Rune. His teleportation ability is also based on his True Wind Rune, using it to activate a teleportation space around himself to another location in an…

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Apprentice of Leknaat
Luc was created as a clone of Hikusaak, leader of the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia, in order to bear the True Wind Rune. However, he was considered a failure as the True Rune became intertwined with his soul. As such, he had been confined to the One Temple, used merely as a storage device for the True Rune.

When he was five years old, Leknaat would take pity on his circumstances and rescue him in what would later be described as an "incident" at the temple. He would be taken to Magician's Island and raised as Leknaat's apprentice. Luc would study under Leknaat, perform tasks for her, and assist in the cleaning of her home.

During the Gate Rune War, Luc surprised McDohl and his party, summoning a Clay Doll for the party to fight under the pretext of establishing their identities. After the battle, he would take the group to Leknaat and teleport them back to the shore of Magician's Island after their audience was finished.

When the Toran Liberation Army acquired Toran Castle, Luc joined the Toran Liberation Army on the request of Leknaat. Luc stood guard over the Stone Tablet of Promise for the duration of the war and brought his powerful magic to battle.

Following the end of the Gate Rune War, Luc disappeared with Leknaat.

When Leknaat gifted the New Alliance Army the Stone Tablet of Promise during the Dunan Unification War, she also gifted them Luc, despite his own consternation. However, Luc was focused when he noticed the presence of the Harmonian officer Sasarai in a skirmish at Radat Town.

Following the battle, he revealed that he knew Sasarai well and told the New Alliance Army to let him deal with him. Using his True Wind Rune, he decimated Sasarai's forces instantly, forcing them to withdraw their support from the war. His similarity to Sasarai was noted but no one knew of their true connection. This confrontation also showed how truly…

recruitment: Recruited automatically following plot events at the Ancient Castle of Toran.
trivia: • In the 1996 English-language localization of Suikoden, when he summons the Clay Doll, Luc merely calls it forth with a wind rune, but in the original Japanese and 2025 Suikoden I&II HD Remaster English localization, he uses the True Wind Rune to do so.

name: Maas
description: Maas (マース, Māsu) is a supporting character in Suikoden. Maas is one of several apprentices of the master blacksmith, Mace.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Maas was the apprentice of the legendary blacksmith, Mace but still considered himself a beginner. He was the youngest of Mace's pupils and relatively inexperienced. Still, but had a great work ethic with a high ambition to improve himself.

McDohl found Maas working in the Great Forest Village and allowed an eager Maas to join the fledgling Toran Liberation Army. In order to hammer out the distortions of the world, he single-mindedly continued to strike with his hammer at Toran Castle. There he would continually hone his skills in the workshop.

When Meese heard Maas had joined the Liberation Army, he also agreed to join, citing Maas' opinion. Later, along with Mose, the three blacksmiths were able to convince Moose - the best of Mace's students - to join the war effort.

Reunited, the four apprentices would later find their old master hiding away at Seek Valley. Together, they were able to convince Mace to come out of retirement and serve the Toran Liberation Army.

Following the Gate Rune War, dissatisfied with his work and his inexperience, Maas redoubled his efforts to become a better

recruitment: Talk to Maas at the Great Forest Village blacksmith any time following the plot events at the Ancient Castle of Toran.

name: Mace
description: Mace (メース, Mēsu) is a supporting character in Suikoden. Mace is a master blacksmith, with several apprentices across the Toran region.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Mace was known as the greatest blacksmith in the land, holding the title of master blacksmith above all others. He was also a great teacher with several disciples of his own before he disappeared without warning.

Mace moved to the isolated Seek Valley, working in solitude and perfecting his technique by studying new ways of forging iron. Although he wished to be left alone, Mace's disciples, Maas, Meese, Mose and Moose were able to rekindle Mace's passion and convince him to come out of retirement and serve the Toran Liberation Army.

His passion rekindled, he was able to forge weapons to their highest level, truly embodying his title of master blacksmith. Seeing his students grow and improve so much also sparked his joy as a master. He often spoke philosophically about the nature of forging weapons, again showing his mastery in the field.

Following the Gate Rune War, Mace let his disciple, Moose succeed him as the master blacksmith before retiring for

recruitment: Talk to Mace in his home at Seek Valley with all four other blacksmiths, Maas, Meese, Moose and Mose, in the party.
trivia: • Mace's weapon level when he joins you is 15. As Mace cannot sharpen his own weapon, he is the only permanent character in the game who cannot have a level 16 weapon without cheating. This was changed in Suikoden I&II HD Remaster, where his weapon level is now 16 when you recruit him.

name: Maximillian
description: Maximillian (マクシミリアン, Makushimirian) is a character who appears in both Suikoden and Suikoden II as well as Genso Suikogaiden Vol.1. Maximillian is the leader of the once proud Maximillian Knights and is ably assisted by his squire, Sancho.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Maximillian was a knight commander who had led the Maximillian Knights in the former Scarlet Moon Empire. Once respected and prosperous, many years ago, Maximillian and the order decided to fight for the City-States of Jowston in their border dispute with Highland without permission and the order was disbanded. Still, Maximillian would continue in the ways of the knight, his pride and chivalry never diminishing.

During the Gate Rune War, Maximillian would grow steadily angrier, hearing the activities of the corrupt Imperial regime. When he received word that the Toran Liberation Army was fighting at Moravia Castle, he would attempt to aid in the attack with his squire, Sancho. Although he was too late to join the attack, Max was able to join the Liberation Army when they crossed paths at the Northern Checkpoint.

Being around so many young fighters would seem to rejuvenate the ageing knight and he was eager to take to battle at any time, determined to relieve the people from the empire's oppression. His fame and renown, and that of his order, was such that many former and new comrades rode to fight under the banner of the Maximillian Knights and the Liberation Army.

Following the war, he seemed to retire from the knighthood but three years later, he would fight in the Dunan Unification War. Hearing rumors of war in the City-States of Jowston, Maximillian determined that the New Alliance Army was on the side of justice and travelled to fight alongside him.

He fought without Sancho by his side, his squire having returned home to visit family at the time. At the age of 65, by this point Max's skills were a little rusty and he was unaided by the many knights who rose to fight under his banner during the Gate Rune War, being in a different region from the one where he made his fame.

Still, he was determined not to be shown up by any young soldier, impressing all with…

recruitment: Following story events at Moravia Castle, encounter Maximillian and Sancho at the Northern Checkpoint and agree to recruit him.
trivia: • Maximillian's name is written with a single L in Suikoden, but with a double L in Suikoden II.

name: Meese
description: Meese (ミース, Mīsu) is a supporting character in Suikoden. Meese is one of several apprentices of the master blacksmith, Mace.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Meese was a blacksmith who studied under Mace, the famous master blacksmith. He was generally regarded as the third best of Mace's apprentices. He was considered a typical smith in many ways, gruff and a little foul-mouthed. Meese had traveled all the way to the Village of the Dwarves to continue his studies so his skills as a blacksmith were without question.

Although Meese initially showed little interest in the Toran Liberation Army, he agreed to join when he heard that fellow apprentice Maas had joined.
