He worked hard alongside Maas, whom he was generally considered superior to as a smith, although he could be found complaining that the facilities at Toran Castle were too cramped.

Later, along with Mose the three blacksmiths were able to convince Moose - the best of Mace's students - to join the war effort. Reunited, the four apprentices would later find their old master hiding away at Seek Valley. Together, they were able to convince Mace to come out of retirement and serve the Toran Liberation Army.

Following the Gate Rune War, Meese returned to his apprenticeship under the

recruitment: Talk to Meese inside the blacksmiths at the Village of the Dwarves any time after you have recruited Maas.

name: Meg
description: Meg (メグ, Megu) is a character who appears in Suikoden, Suikoden II and Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2. Meg is an adventurous trickster eager to explore and learn the tricks of her trade.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Meg was a young woman from Lenankamp, who, while pure and positive, was also reckless and did not think about the consequences of her actions. She ran away from home, shouting her maxim "Adventure calls!", much to the frustration of her parents, in the hopes of becoming a trickster. That said, her parents did encourage her thirst for knowledge.

She would eventually end up in Kaku, looking for her uncle Juppo, regarded as one of the world's finest tricksters. It was said her costuming was a deliberate attempt to mimic her uncle. She eventually encountered him and joined the Toran Liberation Army against his wishes in order to learn the trickster trade from him. Juppo refused to teach her during this time, which frustrated Meg to a large extent.

Following the end of the Gate Rune War, Meg apparently returned home for a short time before leaving again, once more stating "Adventure calls!" Meg would eventually catch up with Juppo, spending the next two years at his Rikon workshop and generally disrupting his actual work rather than tinkering with the box of spare and broken parts he would frequently try and direct her to. Her bad habit of ignoring blueprints and hastily disassembling and reassembling creations in different (usually non-working, smoke spewing, exploding) configurations, often frustrated her uncle.

He would build her Gadget. After that, he would leave, feeling both that Meg needed to see more of the world to become a true trickster and that she needed to return to Lenankamp under Gadget's escort. Meg attempted to follow him, reasoning he would go to the City-States of Jowston, before losing track of him.

She was noted for not really thinking ahead, even her pursuit of Juppo had no real reasoning behind it, save for her craving of adventure. It was noted that her fearlessness made her a fine candidate as a trickster, but her lack of finesse when working…

recruitment: Talk to Meg in Kaku with Juppo in the party at any time.
trivia:
• If you bring Meg to her parents' house in Lenankamp near the end of the first game, they will assume that McDohl is her new husband and awkwardness ensues.
• Apparently the color of Meg's stockings in Suikoden was a topic of hot debate among the development team.

name: Melodye
description: Melodye (メロディ, Merodi) is a character in Suikoden. She is a young woman looking for a rare rune to unlock her own special ability.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Melodye was a young acoustician who was told had the special talent to draw out the power of the rare Sound Rune. However, she was worrying in the city of Kirov during the Gate Rune War as, even with the knowledge of her potential, she had no way to obtain such a rare rune.

Melodye's motivation in searching for the Sound Rune was not to enrich herself but to gain the ability to help those suffering in troubled times, however little her comfort may be.

Luckily, the the leader of the Toran Liberation Army found a Sound Rune and gave it to her. In gratitude, Melodye joined the Liberation Army's cause.

At Toran Castle, Melodye would spend her time under the blue sky at the castle garden, enjoy the use of her Sound Rune and smiling happily when speaking. During the war, she used her phonological skills and awakened power to aid the force to the best of her ability, giving the Liberation Army a variety of sounds.

Following the end of the war, Melodye took off with her Sound Rune to collect all the sounds of the

recruitment: Give the Sound Orb found in Kalekka to Melodye in Kirov.

name: Milia
description: Milia (ミリア, Miria) is a supporting character in Suikoden. Milia is the partner of Thrash and the Captain of the Knights of the Dragon's Den.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Milia served as the Vice-Commander of the Dragon Knights under Joshua Levenheit before and during the Gate Rune War. As a 9th class Dragon Knight apprentice, she had once met Humphrey Mintz when he was visiting Joshua.

Her ability to never lose her cool, no matter how difficult the mission, made her very well trusted by Joshua. She has bonded with her red dragon, Thrash, as a child and she rode him into battle.

Her valiant and majestic bearing made her a much-feared opponent on any battlefield. She wielded a spear with a straight spearhead and unadorned handle.

During the Gate Rune War, Milia accompanied the Toran Liberation Army to Seek Valley in order to find the Moonlight Herb needed to cure the sleeping disease that had affected the order's dragons. Immediately after Ted died following his unexpected confrontation and reunion with McDohl, Milia was the one who stepped forward to take the Moonlight Herb, demonstrating her collected nature that served her well.

Once the dragons had been cured, Milia joined the Toran Liberation Army. She would spend some time with Futch, attempting to keep his spirits up and keep himself from blaming himself for Black's death.

The freezing breath of her dragon, Thrash, would be used prior to the Battle at Floating Fortress Shasarazade to create a fleet of ice ships for the attack. Combined with Joshua and Futch, their Dragon Knight regiment reigned down destruction on Imperial forces.

Following the Gate Rune War, she would return to the Dragon's Den, continuing her duties.

Around the time of the Dunan Unification War, Milia would have a daughter, Sharon. At some point before the War of the Champions, she would also ascend to the position of Commander of the order sometime after the Gate Rune War, succeeding Joshua and becoming the bearer of the Dragon Rune, one of the 27 True

recruitment: Recruited automatically following the story events at the Dragon Knight's Domain.
trivia: • Milia's weapon upgrades as Valkyrie, Valhalla, and Brunhildt, which are all characters or terms from Norse mythology. The Valkyries were the choosers of the slain who escorted those who died in battle to Valhalla. Valhalla was the majestic hall where those slain in battle were allowed to spend the afterlife. Brunhildt is a mangling of Brunhild, who was a valkyrie doomed to live a mortal life and married Gunnar/Gunther after being rescued by Sigurðr/Sigfreid.

name: Milich Oppenheimer
description: Milich Oppenheimer (ミルイヒ・オッペンハイマー, Miruihi Oppenhaimā) is a supporting character in Suikoden who makes a cameo appearance in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2. Milich is one of the Five Great Generals of the Scarlet Moon Empire, who is also known for being the series' original narcissist.
originalName: ミルイヒ・オッペンハイマー

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
Although Milich Oppenheimer owned a great deal of ostentatious outfits, the costume most associated with him consisted of a blue and white striped shirt with large puffy sleeves and a white vest. The garishness of the outfit could have easily been in bad taste, but his white jacket was said to keep it just within the realm of tastefulness.

His riding pants, which are puffed out at the bottom, have the same blue and white stripes as the shirts. The collar is open to the third button, and inside is a yellow scarf. His red hat, adorned with flowers, and yellow cloak complete the ensemble.

Always meticulous, he always had his outfits for the week planned ahead. His wardrobes in Gregminster were brimming with collections of outfits. He also owned a peppermint blue hat with feather trim.

He wielded a slender one-handed sword, thought to be a rapier, but no accurate records have survived to corroborate

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
thumb|right|220px|Milich (first on the left) enjoys tea with Simone Verdricci|Simone, Vincent, and [[Esmeralda.]]Whether he was in battle or in ceremony, there was never a time when Milich was seen without an elaborate outfit.

His title of "Flower General" originally stemmed from his ostentatious fashion and love of art and gardening. He felt it was his duty as the "Flower General" to live up to that ideal, much as Emperor Barbarossa projected the ideal of the Golden Emperor with his golden armor.

Although given to fastidiousness, he was not overly formal. When Mille Feuille gave birth on his new hat, Milich's reaction was one of delight for Mille Feuille and Ruth Demongeot, not despair over his hat. However, when he unknowingly attended an audience at Gregminster Palace covered in the hairs and fluids involved, he was so horrified when he realized that he fell ill for two days.

His two cats, Mille Feuille and Ruth Demongeot, are both rescue animals, taken into his home. Milich was also a passionate botanist. Besides the monstrous Antoinette, Milich also bred the beautiful White Amour rose and the striking Madame Borgia flower.

Milich's preferred breakfast consisted of a freshly baked croissant, half-boiled egg with truffle, salad with hot vegetables, Rikon-style, and royal milk tea. He wore a cone nightcap to bed and a clip over his beard. He could be slothful on occasion, especially when presented with inconveniences such as poor weather but was nevertheless a dutiful Great General of the Scarlet Moon Empire, often beginning his day before dawn.

During the Gate Rune War, he would spend much of his time with Vincent De Boule and Esmeralda, the atmosphere for their time together completely removed from the stresses of

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Milich is a talented swordsman who was one of the Five Great Generals of the Scarlet Moon Empire. Milich became well known for his creative tastes, redesigning Scarleticia Castle.

In the past, he had a faint love for Claudia, and even bred the White Amour for her grave on her passing. His true loyalty, however, was to Emperor Barbarossa who he had served since the War of Succession. Barbarossa once questioned Milich's attire but when Milich explained it was important for him to project himself as the Flower General much as Barbarossa did as the Golden Emperor, Barbarossa relented.

On one occasion, Milich rushed to a summons at Gregminster Palace just after his cat, Mille Feuille had given birth on his hat, leaving him in a quite an undignified state. Upon realizing his appearance upon his return, Milich fell ill for two days.

During the Sun Rune War, Milich would serve as the Scarlet Moon envoy for the marriage of Lymsleia Falenas and Gizel Godwin, traveling to Sol-Falena for the ceremony.

In the early stages of the Gate Rune War, Milich Oppenheimer was given command of the Kunan region. Windy also gave him a Conqueror Rune. Under the influence of the Conqueror Rune, Milich began to make many changes: changing the names of the towns in the area to ones he found more suitable, cracking down on remnants of the Toran Liberation Army, and creating biological weapons such as the highly poisonous rose, Antoinette.

Milich remained one step ahead of the Liberation Army as they attempted to gain control of Kunan, using Antoinette to rout the rebel forces during the First Battle at Scarleticia Castle, then kidnapping Liukan, the only man capable of creating an antidote.

When the leader of the Liberation Army sneaked into Soniere Prison to free Liukan, Milich unleashed a rare vial of the Spores of Agony, a man-eating spore, on his party; despite suffering casualties, the…

recruitment: Refuse to execute Milich and then accept his request to join you during the story events at Scarleticia Castle.
trivia:
• When Milich changes the names of towns under his command, he renames Antei as Bier Blanche, Rikon as Premier L'Amour and Teien as Lac Viriginite. Milich's love of renaming thing to be more elegant extends to people, including his servants Pierre and Andre.
• If Milich is brought to the bath with a party of all men (no women), he will bathe in the women's section instead of the men's.
• In true tribute to his nickname, "the Flower General", all three of Milich's weapon names - Rose, Marguerite, and Orchid are that of flowers.
• If you pay close attention to Milich's battle stance, you will see that he is left-handed. He stands with his right hand slightly behind his back, with his left…

name: Mina
description: Mina (ミーナ, Mīna) is a supporting character in Suikoden. Mina is a popular dancer in Antei.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Mina is a professional dancer whose skills was renowned enough that she had been invited to the Imperial court in the past. However, she has always turned those invitations down, perfering her freedom. With the type of personality that stops her from being tied down to anything, she drifts through life with a carefree air.

During the Gate Rune War, she was working in a tavern in Antei and was beloved by her frequent patrons. She recognized the Toe Shoes-clad McDohl when he came to Antei and joined the Toran Liberation Army on the condition that he danced with her.

As Mina explained, dancing allows a person to open up, showing her what they are truly like. By dancing with him, Mina was able to ascertain his true character and being drawn to his heart, joined the Liberation Army.

Even though she did not have a stage to perform on at Toran Castle, with her naturally cheerful personality, she was well loved by all members of the Liberation Army.

Following the end of the Gate Rune War, Mina because the most famous dancer in the new Toran Republic, living a life free of inconvenience. The dancer Karen would become her disciple and participate in the Dunan Unification

recruitment: Talk to Mina at the inn in Antei while anyone in the party is equipped with Toe Shoes and agree to dance with her.

name: Mina (cat)
description: Mina (ミーナ, Mīna) is a supporting character in Suikoden. Mina is a popular dancer in Antei.

name: Moose
description: Moose (ムース, Mūsu) is a supporting character in Suikoden. Moose is one of several apprentices of the master blacksmith, Mace.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Moose was the number one apprentice of the master blacksmith Mace and trained fiercely in order to earn the title for himself someday.

Although possessed with a thoughtful personality, he also had a stubborn streak which occasionally caused conflict with other people. This also made him slow to trust others. He worked in the Warriors' Village during the Gate Rune War and was convinced to join the Toran Liberation Army by Mace's other students, who had all joined the war effort.

Reunited, the four apprentices would work their comrades weapons to the very limit, knowing they needed to relied upon in life-or-death situations. They would later find their old master hiding away at Seek Valley. Together, they were able to convince Mace to come out of retirement and serve the Toran Liberation Army. Following the Gate Rune War, Moose became the master blacksmith following Mace's final

recruitment: Talk to Moose inside the blacksmiths at the Warriors' Village with the three previously recruitable blacksmiths, Maas, Meese and Mose, in the party.

name: Morgan
description: Morgan (モーガン, Mōgan) is a supporting character in Suikoden. Morgan is a blind former gladiator who currently studies at Qlon Temple.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Morgan was once a gladiator slave in the far southern Queendom of Falena. Due to his abilities as a hand-to-hand fighter, he was eventually blinded to even the odds against his opponents. Even after this brutal act, Morgan was a peerless combatant and was eventually sold on.

His slave ship would be wrecked at sea and Morgan would eventually be washed ashore. He made his way to the Scarlet Moon Empire and eventually entered Qlon Temple. Although unable to see, Morgan was said to be exceptionally skilled at seeing people's hearts.

Morgan was encountered by the Toran Liberation Army studying at Qlon Temple due to, as he described it, special circumstances. There, Morgan told McDohl that he had been born blind and when asked, agreed to join the Liberation Army, saying he could see a big sky behind McDohl's back with his mind's eye.

He did not experience his blindness as a hinderance, explaining that the true essence of things went far beyond what could be seen. He spent his time at Toran Castle in the outdoors, enjoying the feel of the sun on his skin. The claws he used to fight for the Liberation Army are the same claws he used when he was a slave fighting as a gladiator.

Following the end of the Gate Rune War, Morgan returned to Qlon Temple in order to resume his

recruitment: Talk to Morgan inside Qlon Temple.
trivia: • In Suikoden V, Shoon makes reference to a gladiator he had heard was blinded due to his skill and was sold on once he still proved unbeatable, referring to Morgan. Given the dates involved, this would mean Morgan was blinded and sold when he was aged 13 at the most.

name: Mose
description: Mose (モース, Mōsu) is a supporting character in Suikoden. Mose worked in the Toran Liberation Army's Secret Factory, as well as working as a blacksmith at Toran Castle.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Mose is a blacksmith and the second apprentice of the master blacksmith Mace. He was one of the early members of the Toran Liberation Army and put his skills to work running the Liberation Army's Secret Factory. From a young age he was called "Pops" by colleagues due to his appearance, much to his consternation.

Using the ninja Kage as a messenger, Mose received plans from Odessa for a powerful new weapon: the Fire Spear. Though he and Ronnie Bell became isolated following the raid on the Toran Liberation Army's headquarters in Lenankamp, they continued to develop the Fire Spears.

The hero eventually came to collect the weapons, informing Mose of the Liberation Army's exploits following the attack on Lenankamp. Mose expressed sorrow upon hearing of Odessa's death and considered the Fire Spear blueprint she had given him a keepsake.

Mose followed the hero to Kirov, where he and Ronnie Bell both decided to follow the leader to the new headquarters of the Liberation Army, Toran Castle. There, Mose served as a soldier and used his skills as a blacksmith to service the weapons of the officers there. When he and his fellow apprentices came across Mace, Mose would explain that it was Mace's teachings that led him to join the Liberation Army.

Following the Gate Rune War, Mose devoted himself to the reconstruction of Gregminster as Minister of Construction. Mose also married Ronnie Bell and the two had a daughter,

recruitment: Recruited automatically following story events at the Secret Factory.
trivia:
• Mose can sharpen weapons up to the 12th level, making him the third-best blacksmith in the game.
• Odessa Silverberg tests Kage's integrity by telling a fake story about Mose taking care of a sick pigeon.

name: Onil
description: Onil (オニール, Onīru) is a supporting character in Suikoden. Onil is a serial gossiper whose web of information often helped out the Toran Liberation Army.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Onil, despite appearing to be a normal gossip, had access to much classified information thanks to a wide network of sources. As such, her knowledge ranged from local, prosaic gossip to far-reaching matters of empire and state. Exactly where she gets these snippets of information from was a mystery, but her sources were usually highly reliable.

She was aware of the disappearance of Odessa Silverberg, long before information about her death was released. She was also aware of the hero's ascendency to leader of the Toran Liberation Army almost immediately after it happened.

Knowing the Liberation Army to be short on people, she joined, naturally knowing exactly where Toran Castle was located. She would often be seen in the center of Toran Castle, talking with anyone who stopped by.

Sometimes asking for information and sometimes dispensing it, Onil continued to serve as a center of rumors and information for the Liberation Army. Anyone willing to share things with her would be able to learn much in return.

Following the end of the Gate Rune War, Onil merely continued her gossiping ways, her personality

recruitment: Talk to Onil in her home in Seika at any time following plot events at the Ancient Castle of Toran.

name: Pahn
description: Pahn (パーン, Pān) is a supporting character in Suikoden who makes a cameo in Suikoden II. Pahn is a member of the McDohl household who protected McDohl during the Gate Rune War.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Pahn is a skilled martial artist who swore allegiance to Geil Rugner during the War of Succession. Pahn faced General Teo McDohl in a duel during the war and lost; after this, Pahn defected to Teo McDohl's forces. Pahn came to live in the McDohl household and even helped in the upbringing of Teo's young son. Pahn continued to protect and help McDohl during his missions for the Imperial Guard.

He was a big eater, chronicling his meals in a diary at the McDohl manor, and had a good-natured side but was often serious-minded. He was frequently concerned with doing what was just and following the right path. As such, when Ted arrived at the McDohl household badly injured, Pahn deduced that the Imperial Guard who had wanted Ted and left to notify the Imperial Guard of his whereabouts, not wanting to bring any harm to the McDohl household or Master Teo.

During the Gate Rune War, Pahn initally served the Scarlet Moon Empire, but he eventually reunited with McDohl in Kouan. Though commanded by Kraze Miles to capture McDohl, Pahn declined. Struggling against his notions of loyalty versus what his own heart felt was right, he turned on Kraze, choosing to follow his heart from that point on.

Although he wished to join the Toran Liberation Army, Pahn also understood that he may not be easily forgiven for his past choices. Still, he would be accepted into the army. Pahn protected McDohl loyally and was one of the first to arrive after an assassin attacked the young master, afterwards standing guard outside of his door.

Pahn proved his allegiance further when Teo McDohl attacked Toran Castle. To buy the Toran Liberation Army time, Pahn dueled Teo. This threw the choice Pahn made into stark relief. He was now fighting for Teo's son against Teo himself, whereas before he had betrayed his confidence for what he thought was Teo's best interests.

Pahn proved the victor of the duel,…

recruitment: Recruited automatically before your journey to Magician's Island.
trivia:
• It is quite possible to lose Pahn forever, as he must win his duel with Teo. The fight is so one-sided that even the game makes it appear that Pahn is supposed to lose. It takes the best available equipment and as much of a weapon upgrade as he can get just to slip by sometimes. 
• Pahn shares the same battle sprite as Morgan, it's a simple palette swap with minor differences.
• If one examines Pahn's diary in Gregminster after returning from the audience with Emperor Barbarossa, the player can see Pahn's food diary, which will be updated the next day. One can also see an updated version in Suikoden II.

name: Pesmerga
description: Pesmerga (ペシュメルガ, Peshumeruga) is a supporting character in Suikoden and Suikoden II. Pesmerga is known almost entirely for his single-minded pursuit of Yuber.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Gate Rune War
Pesmerga was a mysterious warrior, clad entirely in black, whose origins are unknown. His detached personality and first-class fighting abilities has only enhanced his mystery and it was not known if Pesmerga was even human. His only known goals or motives were to settle his business with Yuber.

Pesmerga was encountered by the Toran Liberation Army on the top floor of Neclord's Castle. Pesmerga explained he was hunting Neclord in the hope that he would lead him to Yuber. Pesmerga agreed to join the Liberation Army, reasoning that Yuber was now working for the Imperial Army and that alliance would be a shortcut to his goal.

Wielding a large two-handed sword in battle, Pesmerga reaped an innumerable number of victims on the battlefield. Off the battlefield, he kept to himself on the roof of Toran Castle and was generally considered unapproachable and certainly did not attempt to reach out to anyone himself.

Some sources place Pesmerga in a key role in the last battle, cutting through Yuber's formation and attacking him directly, causing the imperial ranks to crumble. Following the end of the Gate Rune War, Pesmerga left the country, in pursuit of his prey.
Dunan Unification War
Pesmerga would continue to pursue Yuber, following his trail to the City-States of Jowston. Three years later, during the Dunan Unification War, Pesmerga would be encountered in the Cave of the Wind. Still searching, he agreed to join the New Alliance Army and fought against the Highland Army of which Yuber was a part.

He remained a mystery, even to Richmond who could not find out any valuable information on him. Some rumored that he was a shadow of another existence, and he has never been seen eating or sleeping. During the war, Pesmerga implied that he and Yuber's fate were tied together in some manner.

In both the Gate Rune and Dunan Unification Wars, Pesmerga was one of…

recruitment: Ascend to the organ room at the top of Neclord's Castle and talk to Pesmerga.
trivia: • Even though Richmond claims that no one has ever seen Pesmerga eating, he is a judge during Hai Yo's Cook-off contests. He is known as a harsh judge who generally gives low scores, so perhaps he truly is not used to eating.

name: Qlon
description: Qlon (クロン, Kuron) is a character in Suikoden. He is a gatekeeper living in Antei, who later joins the Toran Liberation Army.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Qlon, a young gatekeeper from Antei, was responsible for warmly announcing the name of the town to travelers passing through. His friendly smile and cheerful greetings provided comfort to weary travelers find a spot of respite after traversing the wilderness and its myriad dangers.

During the oppressive administration of Kunan by Milich Oppenheimer, the general forced through a change to rename Antei to 'Bier Blanche,' a name that Qlon did not find memorable nor agreeable. He opposed this change, voicing his disapproval. Though young, he valued the history and traditions of the town.

After the defeat of Milich and the restoration of Antei's original name, Qlon expressed his gratitude by joining the Toran Liberation Army. Even within the ranks of the Liberation Army, Qlon continued his role as a gatekeeper, now stationed at Toran Castle's docks, where he warmly greeted visitors by proudly announcing the castle's name. His welcoming demeanor and infectious smile uplifted the spirits of many who arrived at the castle.

It was evident that Qlon's passion in life lay in his gatekeeper duties. Following the conclusion of the Gate Rune War, Qlon was officially appointed as the gatekeeper of the post-war Toran Castle, where he continued his dedicated service, proudly announcing the castle's name to all who came to

recruitment: Talk to Qlon at the entrance to Antei following plot events at Scarleticia Castle.

name: Quincy
subtype: Chiyu Star |tab1= S1 |tab2= CS2-331 — Star of Destiny
description: Quincy (クインシー, Kuinshī) is a character in Suikoden. Quincy is a forest-dwelling hunter with a tendency to avoid risking commitment to a cause until he's sure of the results.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Quincy, a skilled and remarkably handsome hunter, resided in the forested regions of the Scarlet Moon Empire with the aspiration of achieving recognition for his abilities.

During the course of the Gate Rune War, Quincy experienced discontent due to the detrimental impact of the Imperial Army's actions on his hunting grounds. Despite his grievances, he refrained from joining the Toran Liberation Army, primarily attributing his decision to the perceived small size of the force and his unwillingness to partake in what he deemed a futile struggle.

The concept of timing held great significance for Quincy, both in his hunting endeavors and in life. He viewed the two domains as akin, thus meticulously calculating his actions, just as he would when pursuing prey.

Subsequently, when the tide of the war shifted in favor of the Liberation Army, Quincy seized the opportunity and willingly enlisted in their ranks. Within the force, he became an integral part of the Adventurers unit, leading a group of skilled archers against the Imperial Army.

Although he demonstrated expertise as an archer, Quincy chose to reserve his bow exclusively for hunting purposes. In combat, he skillfully wielded kunai, which served as his preferred weapons. While stationed at Toran Castle, Quincy found some enjoyment in his role but admitted it did not compare to the satisfaction of residing in his familiar forest home.

Following the conclusion of the war, Quincy opted to return to his peaceful hunting lifestyle, embracing a solitary existence in the wilderness, seemingly content with his freedom. At this juncture, the war had not yet etched his name into the annals of

recruitment: After recruiting 81 or more characters, talk to Quincy at the Fortress of Garan.
trivia: • According to Murayama Yoshitaka, Quincy was originally conceived of as a roster-filling pallet swap of Kirkis with the idea solicited from within the development team. He was eventually fleshed out a little beyond that and ended up more of a swap for Sydonia.

name: Ronnie Bell
description: Ronnie Bell (ロニー・ベル, Ronī Beru) is a supporting character in Suikoden. A strong woman, Ronnie Bell worked in the Toran Liberation Army's Secret Factory.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Ronnie Bell was a robust and physically capable female martial artist who sought to reconcile her strength with her desire to embrace her feminine side. Though she was proficient at protecting herself, she wished to be protected in turn.

Her involvement in the Toran Liberation Army was characterized by her significant physical prowess, attributed to her training as a martial artist. Her work with Mose within the army's Secret Factory demonstrated her aptitude for both combat and technical work.

However, Ronnie's self-perception was marred by insecurities related to her size, strength, and appearance. Despite her internal doubts, her inherent kindness, resilience, and charm resonated with those around her. An encounter with Ledon and Kessler at the Secret Factory amplified her sensitivity regarding her height, resulting in a display of anger.

The arrival of the leader of the Liberation Army saw Ronnie play a vital role in transporting a consignment of Fire Spears, showcasing her dedication to the cause. Her subsequent move to Toran Castle alongside Mose saw her contemplate learning how to cook to enhance her traditional feminine traits.

Ronnie Bell's combat skills were centered around her adeptness with claw weapons, which she sharpened for additional damage on the battlefield. Her use of the Blaze Rune also allowed her to cover her own weaknesses, making her a formidable force on the battlefield. Despite her prowess, she acknowledged the incongruity between her martial prowess and her aspiration for a more demure image.

Following the Gate Rune War, Ronnie Bell's journey took a new course. Her determination led her to pursue atraining to become a bride. This aspiration was fulfilled when she eventually married her comrade Mose. The union bore fruit, as Ronnie and Mose welcomed a daughter named Emily into their

recruitment: Recruited automatically in Kirov following story events at the Secret Factory.
trivia: • Ronnie Bell is the only character known to use the Blaze Rune.

name: Rubi
description: Rubi (ルビィ, Rubyi) is a character in Suikoden. Rubi is an argumentative elf who left the Village of the Elves.

facts.label: Origin
facts.value: Village of the Elves, Toran Republic, |S1= yes

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Rubi was an elf who had grown weary of the Village of the Elves' adherence to antiquated traditions. He made the bold decision to leave the village when Kirkis was still a youth.

Being someone who believed in firsthand experiences, Rubi sought a new path unburdened by the weight of old ideals. At some point he lost his left eye, with some facial scarring in the area visible under his hair.

It was during the Gate Rune War that Rubi's journey brought him to the Kobold Village, where fate reunited him with Kirkis. At first, Rubi dismissed Kirkis' plea to join the Toran Liberation Army, but eventually, he had a change of heart.

Witnessing the remarkable growth of Kirkis into a formidable warrior, transcending the boundaries of race and fighting for his convictions, Rubi realized he was still mired in old ways of thinking and was compelled to join forces.

Embracing the role of an archer, Rubi fought valiantly throughout the war. He would spend his free time atop the roof of Toran Castle, used to being alone, though he did grow more comfortable with having comrades he cared about in time.

After the war's conclusion, Rubi's dedication and bravery earned him the continued respect of Kirkis, who welcomed him back to the Village of the Elves. Together, they worked to rebuild and restore the village to its former

recruitment: When Kirkis is at Level 35 (41 or higher in the original or PSP remaster) or higher and the castle is at Level 2 or higher, talk to Rubi in the Kobold Village inn with Kirkis in the party.

name: Sanchez
description: Sanchez (サンチェス, Sanchesu) is a supporting character in Suikoden. A member of the Toran Liberation Army, Sanchez served as the treasurer of the fledgling organization during the Gate Rune War.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Sanchez was a man of keen intellect and meticulous calculation, renowned for his mastery of finance. He first served under Barbarossa Rugner during the War of Succession, where his administrative prowess earned him distinction.

In the early days of the Toran Liberation Army, Sanchez joined their ranks, taking charge of their treasury. His prodigious memory and encyclopedic knowledge of logistics earned him the sobriquet "Sanchez the Living Archive." Yet beneath this façade lay his true allegiance as a covert agent of the Scarlet Moon Empire.

During the Gate Rune War, Sanchez's betrayal led to the Imperial assault on the Liberation Army's hideout in Lenankamp, resulting in the death of Odessa Silverberg. Though remorse gnawed at him, he maintained his deception, rejoining the surviving rebels and even managing their officers' schedules. Yet as the conflict deepened, so too did his inner turmoil, torn between newfound camaraderie and sworn duty to the Empire.

When Mathiu Silverberg deduced a spy lurked among them and restricted communications, Sanchez's effectiveness waned. But at the Battle at Floating Fortress Shasarazade, he struck openly, stabbing Mathiu and setting the floating fortress ablaze, trapping his former comrades in the inferno.

Confronted by Flik, who had fought beside Sanchez since the rebellion's dawn, Sanchez's entire history of betrayal was laid bare. Enraged, Flik prepared to execute him and Sanchez, resigned to fate, did not resist.

Yet Mathiu, ever the strategist, intervened, delaying judgment to preserve morale. Thus Sanchez remained, forced to serve as if untainted by treachery until war's end.

With the Toran Republic established, President Lepant granted Sanchez a private pardon—not for mercy, but necessity, lest exposure of his betrayal shatter the fragile new order. In exchange for his life, Sanchez was sworn to exile, swearing never…

recruitment: Recruited automatically following story events at Pannu Yakuta Castle.

name: Sancho
description: Sancho (サンチョ, Sancho) is a supporting character who appears in Suikoden. Sancho is the faithful squire of Maximillian, leader of the Maximillian Knights.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Sancho was a former knight who found his calling as the devoted squire to the distinguished Maximillian. Tasked with tending to Maximillian's everyday needs, Sancho served his master with unwavering loyalty. His family had apparently served Maximillian's for generations.

When Maximillian embarked on a mission to aid the Toran Liberation Army in their attack on Moravia Castle, Sancho faithfully accompanied him, leading them to join the army upon encountering its esteemed leader at the Northern Checkpoint.

Always anxious for his master's well-being, Sancho shadowed Maximillian, a constant guardian of his safety. Despite his unease, he never allowed his anxiety to falter his unyielding faith in his beloved master. Even after the culmination of the Gate Rune War, Sancho remained dutifully by Maximillian's side, serving him diligently.

During the Dunan Unification War, however, an unfortunate circumstance arose when Sancho was away visiting his home, rendering him unable to attend to Maximillian during this crucial period. Nevertheless, his dedication and loyalty remained unwavering, and his bond with Maximillian remained steadfast despite the

recruitment: Following story events at Moravia Castle, encounter Maximillian and Sancho at the Northern Checkpoint and agree to recruit him.
trivia: • Sancho has one of the few potential alternative endings in Suikoden. His ending will change if Maximillian dies in a war battle before completing the game. His new ending states "Puts flowers on his master's grave daily."

name: Sansuke
description: Sansuke (サンスケ, Sansuke) is a character in Suikoden. He is a passionate bath maker and designer who joined the Toran Liberation Army.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Sansuke was a skilled bath maker known for creating baths that provided relaxation and relief from fatigue, a result that brought him great satisfaction. He resided near the Great Forest due to the exceptional quality of the wood found there, which he used in crafting his baths.

Equipped with a high-quality saw, Sansuke's expertise allowed him to cut through nearly any material with ease. This saw served not only for building baths but also as a weapon in his battles against various monsters. He held little interest in anything beyond his work, being completely dedicated to his vocation above all else.

During the Gate Rune War, Sansuke joined the Toran Liberation Army with the purpose of providing a bath for use at their headquarters. Diligently, he worked to maintain the baths' constant temperature, ensuring the soldiers' access to soothing hot baths. His exacting nature meant he managed every aspect of his creations himself.

Following the conclusion of the Gate Rune War, Sansuke returned to his passion, continuously designing new and innovative baths to pursue his

recruitment: Talk to Sansuke in the Great Forest Village any time after plot events at the Ancient Castle of Toran.

name: Sarah (Suikoden)
description: Sarah (セイラ, Seira) is a supporting character in Suikoden who makes a cameo in Suikoden II. Sarah is a maid and washerwoman who seems to enjoy dealing with large amounts of laundry.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Sarah was a dedicated washerwoman working in the town of Kirov during the Gate Rune War. When encountered by the hero, she expressed genuine love for her work and willingly offered joined the Toran Liberation Army upon the condition of receiving soap to take on more laundry.

A series of trades ensued as Sarah eagerly sought the soap, only to realize later that she had an ample supply all along. Despite this, she would join the force as she originally offered. Her enthusiasm to join the Liberation Army might have led her to leave some clothes behind to dry in Kirov, much to her dismay.

In battle, Sarah showcased her proficiency with throwing knives, making her a valuable asset to the Liberation Army. Her personality could be characterized as somewhat abrupt, and she possessed a sharp tongue that could occasionally catch others off guard. This trait was likely due to her dislike of being disturbed while engrossed in her work.

After the conclusion of the Gate Rune War, Sarah found employment as a maid at the Scarlet Moon Inn in Gregminster, thanks to Marie's offer. During her time at the inn, her sharp tongue sometimes led her to berate customers whom she did not favor. She was still working in the inn as of the Dunan Unification War and gifted the hero a Cyclone

recruitment: Talk to Sarah in Kirov and agree to find her some Soap. Buy Sugar in the Kirov item shop and trade it in Kirov for a Yardstick, that for Salt, that for Soy Sauce and that for the soap, then bring the soap to Sarah.

name: Sergei
description: Sergei (セルゲイ, Serugei) is a character in Suikoden. He is an inventor whose greatest triumph is probably his elevator.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Sergei was a diligent and innovative inventor known for his advanced designs that were often underappreciated and scorned by the authorities of his time. Despite his intelligence and hard work, he faced challenges in selling his inventions, and by the time of the Gate Rune War, none of his creations had been successfully marketed.

His inventive work was driven by a desire to expand freedom and benefit users of his creations. As such, he dismayed at the imperial way of thinking which suppressed such notions.

Drowning his sorrows in a tavern in Kaku, he found the hero receptive to his ideas and so joined the Toran Liberation Army as an early member.

A notable achievement by Sergei was the installation of his invention, the elevator, at Toran Castle. This creation proved to be a valuable time-saving device for officials within the Liberation Army.

He would spend his time by the elevator, maintain its works and assisting people in using it when required. In combat, Sergei utilized his trusty spanner, an essential tool in the development and maintenance of his elevator.

Following the conclusion of the Gate Rune War, Sergei's fortunes changed dramatically. His inventions gained widespread recognition and adoption, leading to significant wealth earned through patents and

recruitment: Talk to Sergei in Kaku at any time after plot events at the Ancient Castle of Toran.

name: Sheena
description: Sheena (シーナ, Shīna) is a supporting character in Suikoden and Suikoden II who also appears in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2.

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
Taking after his mother in appearance, Sheena is a slight, handsome youth with blond hair and amber eyes and a prominent forehead.

His blond hair is light like his mother's. He wears a loose, green shirt along with black pants and brown boots with green trim.

In Suikoden II, the green shirt is a buttoned affair with beige trim, though Sheena always leaves it mostly unbuttoned and hanging from him. Under his shirt, he wears an armless maroon form-fitting body piece with a high neckline.

In Suikoden, he wears a low-hanging necklace and an earring on his left ear, while in Suikoden II he instead wears a loose choker around his neck and no earring. He also has a decorative tassel hanging from his tunic

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
Immature and prone to skirt-chasing, Sheena is frequently admonished by his father for his continuous antics. However, it is said that Lepant himself acted no different during his own youth.

Lighthearted and brimming with confidence, he flits from one situation to the next with ease. Although he seems entirely without a care, it appears he has some semblance of justice in his heart as he is generally willing to fight for a cause and even clashed with his father ideologically.

Tesla noted he was talented at drawing out people's true opinions. He is said to have the sword skills of his father and the magical ability of his mother.

Despite his flirting, he has not had a girlfriend. This is almost entirely due to his inability to focus on any one woman for

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Immature Playboy
Sheena is the son of Lepant and Eileen who has somewhat of a wandering eye. Sheena was spoiled by his mother during his youth and has grown up to be a rather immature man, by his father's standards. He had prodigious potential, combining the martial skill of his father with the magical arts of his mother but, rather than training, Sheena spent of his time idling and chasing women.

When he was a child, Sheena had fallen ill. The illness was serious enough that his father sought out the great healer Liukan to administer treatment.

During the Gate Rune War, annoyed by his son's indolence, Lepant cast Sheena out into the world on a learning excursion. The youth did not take the intended project at face value. Later, Sheena was discovered flirting in Seika, boasting about his family's wealth, when he was overheard by Lepant. Unhappy at his son's playboy attitude, Lepant forced his son to join the Toran Liberation Army with the aim of making Sheena into a mature adult.

Throughout the war, Sheena served in his father's unit, shoving off his own tactical skill. Although a reluctant fighter, his natural talents and potential were evident. He wielded a rapier, a straight and slender sword with a sharp point.

Following the fall of the Scarlet Moon Empire and the end of the Gate Rune War, Sheena served briefly as a clerk alongside Tesla and Lepant even attempted to appoint him as a military governor. However, Sheena disagreed vehemently with the retention of an old imperial system of governance and made up his mind to leave.

Sheena left his parents once more, but not before Eileen gifted him the family sword, Kirinji, at Lepant's behest. He switched to wielding Kirinji after he broke his rapier protecting a servant boy from an angry noble who had water spilled on him. After this, he began wandering around and having a good time as usual. 
Toran…

recruitment: When the castle is at Level 2 or higher, talk to Sheena at the inn in Seika with Lepant in the party.
trivia:
• Though he uses a rapier in Suikoden, Sheena takes on his father's old sword for Suikoden II: Kirinji, Kirinji 2, Kirinji 3. 
• Fans speculate that Sheena may be Apple's un-named former husband that she mentions in Suikoden III.
• Sheena uses a rather unusual technique with the Kirinji. Though the Kirinji is a flamberge, a type of sword normally held with two hands, Sheena attacks holding it with one hand.

name: Sonya Shulen
description: Sonya Shulen (ソニア・シューレン, Sonia Shūren) is a character in Suikoden. One of the Five Great Generals of the Scarlet Moon Empire, Sonya serves as admiral of the Imperial Navy and commander of the Floating Fortress of Shasarazade.

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
Sonya has long, straight blonde hair and striking blue eyes. She is particularly known for her beauty, with several characters remarking on it over the course of the game.

Her equipment consists of a metal breastplate and greaves, worn over blue underclothes. She also wears a red skirt and red pauldrons, as well as a blue cape. On her forehead, she wears a red and gold

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
Sonya is known for her bravery and striking presence, and carries herself with a blend of grace and authority that commands respect. Beneath her composed exterior, however, lies a deeply emotional and driven soul, shaped by the weight of her mother's legacy and her own aspirations.

Sonya's fierce loyalty to her duties and loved ones was matched only by her fiery temper, particularly when provoked, as seen in her intense rivalry with Anji. Meanwhile, her relationship with Teo McDohl revealed a softer, more vulnerable side.

Her ability to reconcile her emotions and adapt to changing circumstances highlights her complexity as a leader who balanced strength with compassion, pride with humility, and vengeance with

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Sonya Shulen was a distinguished Great General of the Scarlet Moon Empire, renowned for her bravery, magic acumen, and striking beauty. With her golden hair and piercing blue eyes, Sonya commanded both admiration and respect, earning her place as one of the Empire's most prominent military leaders.

She inherited her position as Great General and naval commander following the death of her mother, Kilauea Shulen, a revered veteran of the War of Succession. However, the weight of her mother's legacy burdened Sonya, driving her to prove herself worthy of the Shulen name.

Sonya's career was marked by her fierce rivalry with Anji, the infamous lake pirate. As commander of the Imperial Navy, she led her forces in numerous engagements against Anji's bandits, achieving notable success due to her unit's discipline and lack of corruption—a rarity within the Empire. 

Their rivalry reached its peak when Anji taunted Sonya by pinning a wedding dress to the Floating Fortress of Shasarazade, mocking her unmarried status. Enraged but composed, Sonya retaliated by orchestrating a raid on the pirates while they were intoxicated, dealing a significant blow to their operations.

During her tenure, Sonya formed a close bond with Teo McDohl, another of the Empire's Great Generals. Their relationship, initially rooted in mutual respect and mentorship, blossomed into a deep romantic connection. Teo's guidance helped Sonya navigate the pressures of her role, though his eventual death during the Gate Rune War plunged her into grief and turmoil. Learning that Teo had fallen in battle against his own son, the leader of the Toran Liberation Army, Sonya's sorrow turned to a desire for vengeance.

In the climactic Battle at Floating Fortress Shasarazade, Sonya fought valiantly, refusing to retreat even as the fortress burned around her. Defeated but unbowed, she attempted to take her own life to…

recruitment: Talk to Sonya in the cell in the basement of the castle following the Battle at Floating Fortress Shasarazade. Ask her to join you, then tell her you don't care that she hates you.
trivia:
• If the player is playing Suikoden efficiently for completion purposes, Sonya will be the last character they get to join.
• The blue magic-burst attack that Sonya uses when you fight her is not available through any rune in the game.
• There is a black cat who hangs out at the Mercenary Fortress in Suikoden II that, when inspected, will say "Sonya...". This is not a reference to Sonya, however, but rather a mistranslation of the cat meowing.
• Despite being the leader of the Soldier Beauties unit, Sonya can only be used as such in the last battle.
• Sonya's artwork depicts her wielding a sword, but her in-game weapon resembles a spear, but it levels up as a one-handed sword.
• Parts of…

name: Sydonia
description: Sydonia (シドニア, Shidonia) is a supporting character in Suikoden. Sydonia is a bandit of few words with a unique teleportation ability.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Sydonia was raised as a spectacle in a traveling show, lured away from his parents as a child, due to his unique teleport powers. He could teleport short distances by concentrating on his left hand.

One day, that group would be raided by Guin's bandits. Initially distrustful, he began to open up to Varkas who ensured his safety in captivity, even having his collar removed by Lee. He disliked his name being used as it was also the word used to activate the magical shock collar that kept him in check, when the person speaking it held a ring magically connected to the collar.

When he learned that the acrimony between Varkas and Guin had spilled over into physical confrontations, he offered to teleport to Guin at night and stab him to death. Varkas would dismiss the idea, not wanting Guin to die, and also not wanting to use Sydonia's power as a tool.

When Guin poisoned Varkas and then tried to murder him, Sydonia would use his teleportation to stop Guin from reaching his blade. Varkas would strike down Guin, and Sydonia and Lee would have Hodai cure Varkas. Since then, he has worked under Varkas in the group, as his second-in-command.

Sydonia's teleporting has earned him the title of "Sydonia of the Toate." The bandits would attack Scarlet Moon Empire officials around the area of Rockland. He fought using a projectile weapon, a rope sling which fired a metal weight. He was extremely taciturn, with Varkas being the only person he was willing to communicate at any length with. He had a habit of beginning his few sentences with "Heh...."

Sydonia and Varkas were captured by the son of Teo McDohl on the orders of Kanaan. When the Imperial Guard left Rockland, Sydonia and Varkas were strung up and left to starve to death. Although eventually rescued by the same group who had captured them, Sydonia apparently could have teleported away at any time but chose not to escape…

recruitment: Joins automatically at Toran Castle following plot events in Kouan.
trivia:
• In the English version of Suikoden, Sydonia's ending simply says "Disappears.", but his ending in the Japanese version lists that he was asked to join the Toran Republic Border Guards by Varkas ().
• Sydonia may be an in-name only reference to the demon Asmodeus, as his name is very close to "Sydonai", a Romanian word name for the demon.
• Sydonia is the only character in Suikoden that teleports when attacking.
• Sydonia shares the same battle sprite as Quincy; their sprite is a simple pallet swap with minor differences.

name: Sylvina
description: Sylvina (シルビナ, Shirubina) is a supporting character in Suikoden. Sylvina is a naive, but kind hearted, elf and the romantic interest of Kirkis.

facts.label: Origin
facts.value: Village of the Elves, Toran Republic

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Sylvina was the granddaughter of the Chief of the Elves and was Kirkis' lover. Her appearance may have suggested someone young and naive but her love for Kirkis was deep and genuine.

Like most elves at the time, Sylvina held a low opinion of other races, including humans and dwarves. However, Sylvina still protested Kirkis' arrest for consorting with humans and later helped him and his companions escape from their captivity.

Despite her misgivings, because she loved Kirkis, she decided to believe in his ideals, even if they appeared incomprehensible to her. Still, Sylvina declined to accompany Kirkis on his quest to stop Kwanda Rosman's Burning Mirror, promising to wait for him instead.

After the complete destruction of Lukie Ende Towayo, Kirkis believed Sylvina to be dead. Fortunately, the swift-footed Stallion was able to rescue Sylvina from the blazing inferno, making those two part of the few survivors of the catastrophe. After their emotional reunion, Kirkis appeared to reveal his intention to marry Sylvina and the couple joined the Toran Liberation Army.

As the war continued, she began to overcome her prejudices, coming closer to Kirkis' point of view. In battle she used a small bow befitting her slight build. The bow was designed to be a companion piece to the bow wielded by her lover, Kirkis. The two would spend most of their time together in the outdoors of Toran Castle.

Following the Gate Rune War, Sylvina and Kirkis were married, keeping the promise the two had made when they were

recruitment: Joins automatically following plot events at Pannu Yakuta Castle.
trivia: • Sylvina's design was based on a character Kawano Junko once drew as a university student.

name: Taggart
description: Taggart (ダカート, Dakāto) is a supporting character in Suikoden. Taggart is the servant of Warren who notified the Toran Liberation Army of his master's capture.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Taggart faithfully served as a dedicated servant in the household of his master, Warren. His appearance was very slender, almost gaunt, and he held a deep loyalty to Warren. However, some considered him somewhat unreliable in his duties. Despite this perception, Taggart made every effort to follow any orders given to him, earning Warren's trust.

During the tumultuous events of the Gate Rune War, Warren was captured by the nefarious Kasim Hazil. Before being taken into custody, Warren entrusted Taggart with the task of relaying what had happened to the leader of the Toran Liberation Army.

When Taggart was discovered at Toran Castle, he remained tight-lipped, following his master's orders to the letter and refusing to speak even as other officers debated what to do with the intruder, until McDohl himself arrived. 

Thanks to Taggart, the Liberation Army learned of Warren's plight and swiftly organized a rescue mission. Warren and Viktor were successfully freed from captivity in Moravia Castle. Impressed by the Liberation Army, Warren decided to join their ranks, and Taggart followed his master, continuing to serve him as part of the Liberation Army for the duration of the war.

After the conclusion of the Gate Rune War, Taggart remained by Warren's side as a trusted assistant, demonstrating unwavering loyalty and commitment to his master's

recruitment: Recruited automatically following plot events at Moravia Castle.
trivia: • As full body character artwork was created as needed as production material for Suikoden (to inform color choices and other design elements for in-game depiction) rather than as promotional material for magazine and artbook reproduction, Taggart's artwork was not actually created until after the game was developed.

name: Tai Ho
description: Tai Ho (タイ・ホー, Tai Hō) is a supporting character in Suikoden and Suikoden II. Tai Ho is a fisherman with a penchant for Chinchirorin.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Tai Ho was a fisherman and mariner hailing from the fishing village of Kaku. He formed a sworn brotherhood with Yam Koo after the two encountered each other during a gambling brawl, fighting together.

During the Gate Rune War, Tai Ho's reputation as the only fearless fisherman willing to navigate to the Toran Lake Castle reached the attention of the McDohl and his party. Upon being found by Camille, they sought Tai Ho's assistance to travel to Toran Castle.

Initially, Tai Ho dismissed the idea as preposterous but agreed to accompany them if the hero would wager all of his money in a game of Chinchirorin, a game Tai Ho favored. When the hero managed to win the game, Tai Ho kept his promise and even joined the party at Toran Castle, eager to face any monsters that crossed their path. He was the one proposed naming the castle "Dragon Castle" after the defeat of the Zombie Dragon.

Although Tai Ho continued with the Toran Liberation Army following this, he claimed to do so purely for enjoyment, and his loyalty mainly lay with the hero due to their shared good fortune. He would stay in a hut near the docks of Toran Castle, not wanting to be too tied down to the central administration of the Liberation Army.

Tai Ho's skills as a sailor proved valuable to the Liberation Army, particularly when they needed to travel to the Secret Factory to obtain the Fire Spears. Admiring the Speedboat recently upgraded by Kamandol and Gen, Tai Ho brought the hero to Kirov and travelled with him onwards to the Secret Factory, enlisting the help of his friend Kun To to secure transport for the Fire Spears. 

During the Gate Rune War, Tai Ho encountered Kimberly, an artisan from Antei, who took an interest in him, leading to Yam Koo's attempts to prevent them from being alone together due to Tai Ho's weakness for women. After the conclusion of the Gate Rune War, Tai Ho returned to his…

recruitment: Recruited automatically during plot events in Kaku.
trivia:
• Tai Ho's trident, Satsuki, Akemi, and Mizuki are all feminine Japanese names.
• For some unknown reason, Tai Ho is the only character in Suikoden II who will not get any bonus from loading a Suikoden save game.

name: Ted
description: Ted (テッド, Teddo) is a supporting character in Suikoden and Suikoden IV and also makes a cameo in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2.

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
Ted has reddish-brown hair and eyes. In Suikoden, Ted wears a blue tabard over a mustard tunic and brown pants. He also wears black boots. In Suikoden IV he wears a heavier blue coat over similar pants and boots. He is always seen wearing brown gloves. The Rune of Life and Death is emblazoned on his right hand and his gloves are worn to cover this.

His clothes were designed by Kawano Junko to be blue in order to contrast with the hero's red-themed outfit. He carries a bow as his weapon as he finds it easy to use as well as advantageous to be able to attack from a distance or from cover as a

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
Ted was certainly considered a strong-willed youth, able to shoulder the burden of carrying the Rune of Life and Death for so many years. Still, this burden also gave him a crushing sense of loneliness and duty which, at least for a time, rendered him sullen and standoffish with others. In his heart, however, he was a loyal and kind-hearted fellow.

In later years, he was able to form a bond with others at the McDohl household, able to immerse himself in a relatively normal life. He was heavily devoted to his friends, especially the hero. He used the Soul Eater at Mt. Seifu to protect him and the others, despite being all too aware of the dangers involved. At Seek Valley he would give up his very soul to protect his friend, showing the depths of his devotion.

This devotion was entangled with his duty of carrying the Soul Eater and keeping it out of the clutches of Windy. Ted was willing to pass the rune to the hero, even knowing the pain it would cause. He even claimed he didn't care if the hero hated him for it. In that sense, it could be argued that the rune itself always remained paramount to Ted.

He had a habit since childhood of asking for "big favors". Despite his advanced age, he still sometimes reacted like a youth. When he was called a kid by Futch, the remark provoked him into almost spilling his

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Victim of Fate
Ted was the owner of the Rune of Life and Death and a survivor of the destruction of the Village of the Hidden Rune. Raised in the village, which had spent generations in hiding hoping to prevent the use of the cursed rune, Ted acquired his rune at a young age, when Windy came to the village seeking the rune.

Ted's grandfather, the Village Chief passed it on to Ted and bade him to escape while Windy attacked the village. Ted was able to escape but would be forced to wander for over 300 years, endlessly pursued by Windy and her minions.

Halfway through his wanderings, Ted attempted to escape the curse of the Soul Eater, boarding the Fog Ship and entrusting the rune to the Fog Ship Guide. He would sail on the Fog Ship for many years, physically growing while being removed from the rune's influence, before encountering the Stronghold Ship during the Island Liberation War.

Serving as a herald of the Fog Ship Guide, he would gradually regain his spark, seeing the hero forge ahead despite also being the bearer of a cursed True Rune, the Rune of Punishment. Finally, Ted would confront the Fog Ship Guide and reclaim his True Rune, agreeing to join the crew of the Stronghold Ship in order to repay his debt.

Because the curse of the Rune of Life and Death cast a shadow over his life, Ted tried to keep his distance around others. Deep inside however, he was a friendly boy who cherished his friends. Still, this cloud made him act stand-offish and rude to those who tried to get close to him, such as Aldo, although towards the end of the war he began to open up slightly more.

Following the end of the conflict, he would resume his wanderings for another 150 years or so.

At some point, pursued by Windy's minions, he would be found hurt outside a forest by Mu Village. After convalescing there, he would fall in love with the young woman who nursed him back to…

recruitment: Joins automatically during plot events in Gregminster.
trivia:
• Ted shares the same weapon name as Odessa in Suikoden. They are stated to be common, mass-produced bows and are difficult for blacksmiths to work with.
• In Suikoden IV, he still uses a bow, but its name upon upgrading is different from his Suikoden appearance.

name: Templeton
description: Templeton (テンプルトン, Tenpuruton), also styled Templton, is a supporting character in Suikoden and Suikoden II who also appears in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.1. Templeton is a young, attention detailed cartographer whose dream is to map the entire world.

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
Templeton had golden-brown hair, neatly cut just above his shoulders. He had light blue eyes.

His attire was designed for comfort while traveling. In Suikoden, he wore a blue waistcoat with gold trim over his white shirt, with a red tie. He also wore white socks and simple beige shoes.

By Suikoden II, his outfit consisted of a navy blue hat with a yellow band, a white shirt with red tie, a cream-colored vest, and knee-length gray trousers. He also wore tall, dark blue socks and black shoes.

His shorts especially allowed him greater freedom of movement while traveling. When traveling, he carried a large backpack filled with cartography equipment and supplies, ensuring he was always prepared for his

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
Templeton possessed an insatiable curiosity about the world, which fueled his passion for cartography. His dedication to his craft was unwavering, and he took great pride in his work, sometimes coming off as a little cocky due to his recognized talent and maturity at such a young age.

However, his confidence was well-earned, as he was a master cartographer despite his youth. He was highly observant, able to pick up on small details such as Nash Latkje's Harmonian accent after a short conversation.

Though he often traveled alone, he believed that people were generally kind to children, making his journey easier. He also held the belief that only adults who lacked self-confidence would talk down to others, such as children.

While initially dismissive of those not knowledgeable in the intricacies of his work, he eventually grew to appreciate those like Colin, who valued more than just the final result of his maps.

Templeton was not just skilled at cartography but also highly adaptable in war. His knowledge of terrain gave him an advantage, allowing him to lead troops through shortcuts and escape danger when necessary. These shortcuts are also how Templeton kept himself safe despite traveling alone.

His advanced knowledge of secret paths, routes, and shortcuts allowed him to easily escape and evade most danger, be it from bandits or monsters. These skills in navigation and understanding local customs were invaluable to Templeton in both military and civilian

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Templeton was born in Kanakan and became fascinated with maps at a young age. His passion was ignited when he first saw a map at the age of five or six, brought to his village by a traveling merchant.

This fascination soon turned into an obsession as he collected maps and began noticing the discrepancies between different renderings of the same areas. Determined to create the most accurate map of the world, he set out on a lifelong journey to map every corner of the land with perfect detail.

His travels took him through many lands, constantly updating his maps as new discoveries were made or as territories changed due to warfare. He eventually joined the Toran Liberation Army during the Gate Rune War after witnessing the destruction of the Village of the Elves, lamenting the need to update his maps due to war.

He provided the army with a world map and worked at Toran Castle, meticulously mapping its interior. After the war, he set out to find the edge of the world.

Three years later, Templeton traveled through the Dunan region, starting in South Window City. He was mistaken for a suspicious individual and brought to the Mercenary Fortress, where he reunited with Viktor and Flik.

Though he refused Viktor's request to map the Highland Kingdom on behalf of Muse Principality, he agreed to map the fortress in exchange for lodging. He later left his map with Leona before departing.

On his journey, he met Colin, an aspiring mercenary, and eventually grew to appreciate his enthusiasm for his work. After parting ways, he encountered Nash Latkje in Muse City and shared a room with him at an inn.

He later escaped Muse when the Highland Army attacked, only to discover the destruction of Toto Village and the death of Colin. This would drive Templeton to join the New Alliance Army, not only to explore new places but also to oppose forces that either restricted his…

recruitment: Talk to Templeton in the Village of the Elves following story events at the Dwarves' Vault and the Village of the Dwarves.
trivia: • In the 1999 English language release of Suikoden II, his name is given as "Templton" (no second 'e'). This is because the name boxes in Suikoden II can only accommodate up to 8 characters.

name: Tengaar
description: Tengaar (テンガアール, Tengaāru) is a supporting character in Suikoden and Suikoden II. Tengaar is a headstrong woman from Warriors' Village who was nearly forced to marry the vampire Neclord.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Interview with a Vampire
Tengaar was the daughter of Zorak, who was the Chief of Warriors' Village. Growing up alongside Hix, their childhood bond eventually blossomed into a romantic relationship. Hix had actually made a childhood promise to marry Tengaar, which she took to heart though Hix had long since forgotten that particular vow.

She was characterized by a bright and cheerful personality, albeit occasionally tempered by her frustration with Hix's indecisiveness, in contrast to her own forthright nature. Deeply devoted to Hix, Tengaar's love for him was unwavering, prompting her to fiercely defend him when necessary.

However, her life took an unexpected turn when she fell victim to Neclord's kidnapping scheme, intending to make her his bride. In response, Hix courageously embarked on a mission to free her and, with the aid of the Toran Liberation Army, succeeded in rescuing Tengaar.

Remarkably, during her captivity under Neclord, Tengaar remained steadfast in her belief that Hix would eventually come to her rescue. Following their victorious battle against Neclord, both Tengaar and Hix decided to join the ranks of the Liberation Army. At Toran Castle, she could often be seen lamenting Hix's progress with his swordsmanship.

Upon the conclusion of the Gate Rune War, Tengaar completed her service with the Liberation Army and accompanied Hix on a journey. Their objective was to assist him in undertaking his Manhood Ceremony, a pivotal rite of passage.
The Test of the Unicorn
Embarking on their journey together, Tengaar's headstrong nature occasionally clashed with Hix's more timid personality. Nevertheless, they shared tender moments that revealed the depth of her affection for him. One main motivation for her pushing Hix to complete his Manhood Ceremony was so they could get married.

Their journey eventually led them to the City-States of Jowston, where they…

recruitment: Recruited automatically following plot events at Neclord's Castle.
trivia:
• Though Tengaar's weapon in Suikoden is animated as and is named after a knife, it's actually classified as a rod in terms of attack power.
• In Marugoto! Genso Suikoden Book, she placed at Number 16 in the "Favorite female character of the series" column. In the same book, she ranked Number 9 in the "female character wanted as a girlfriend" list.
• Tengaar made Number 1 in Lisa Foiles' Top 5 Cutest Redheads on The Escapist on March 29, 2011.

name: Teo McDohl
description: Teo McDohl (テオ・マクドール, Teo Makudōru) is a character in Suikoden. He is the father of the game's primary protagonist and he serves as one of the generals of the Scarlet Moon Empire.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Teo McDohl, one of the esteemed Five Great Generals of the Scarlet Moon Empire, played a crucial role in the War of Succession under the command of Barbarossa Rugner. He served as the general of the 2nd Imperial Central Army, a distinguished force without specific territorial jurisdiction.

Teo earned the moniker "Invincible General" due to his remarkable record of never losing a battle. He was also known as "The victor of 100 out of 100 battles", a straightforward title for a straightforward man. He gained recognition for his visionary understanding of the potential of the previously under-utilized Armored Cavalry, molding them into a legendary unit.

The general displayed unwavering loyalty to Emperor Barbarossa and demonstrated numerous acts of valor during the War of Succession, which garnered him the trust of the emperor. Teo's noble demeanor and character earned him immense respect from many and unwavering loyalty from his subordinates. Physically, he stood tall and imposing, especially when adorned in his silver armor and distinctive yellow cape.

Before the war, Teo embarked on a mission to subdue lake bandits in Gouran, who were funded by Assam as part of Geil Rugner's divisive plot to isolate Barbarossa's allies before claiming the throne. Teo blamed himself for not recognizing Geil and Assam's schemes, and he absolved Gremio of any fault in his son's abduction, another part of Assam's plot.

When faced with Assam's ultimatum for a decisive battle without the Armored Cavalry's armor, Teo rejected it outright, refusing to compromise his loyalty and principles for the ransom of his son. Although the incident impacted the morale of his unit, with his good friend, Ain Gide, even volunteered to have his cavalry take the lead in the ensuing battle, news of his son's rescue revitalized Teo's forces, leading to a decisive victory.

Teo never remarried after the…

name: Tesla
description: Tesla (テスラ, Tesura) is a supporting character in Suikoden who makes a cameo appearance in Suikoden II. A scrivener who did not wish to be called for the war effort; Tesla nevertheless found himself working for the Toran Liberation Army.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Tesla was a skilled scrivener known for his preference for a quiet and peaceful life. Acquainted with Mathiu Silverberg, he had previously worked as a document forger under Mathiu's supervision to facilitate smuggling activities. Recognizing Tesla's talent, Mathiu would later hand-pick him for recruitment into the Toran Liberation Army.

After settling down in Antei under the alias of Albert Onyx following Mathiu's departure from imperial service, Tesla sought to distance himself from his past and lead a tranquil existence as a scrivener. However, when members of the Liberation Army, including Viktor, sought his services, Tesla's pseudonym was quickly uncovered. Reluctantly, he agreed to travel to Toran Castle alone to fulfill the task he was given.

Despite his initial hesitancy to join the Liberation Army, Tesla devoted himself to his assigned responsibilities. Collaborating with Kimberly, he crafted perfectly forged Fake Orders that enabled the Liberation Army to successfully rescue Liukan from Soniere Prison.

Throughout the Gate Rune War, Tesla expressed a strong aversion to fighting and often yearned to return to his home in Antei, emphasizing the need to tidy his dwelling, though he seemed resigned to his situation in truth.

Following the conclusion of the Gate Rune War, Tesla's exceptional clerical abilities earned him the appointment as the Secretary of State in the newly formed Toran Republic, a position bestowed upon him by President Lepant. During the Dunan Unification War, Tesla served as one of Lepant's advisers when the hero visited

recruitment: Recruited automatically in Antei during plot events in the Gouran region.
trivia:
• Nikola Tesla (1856 - 1943) was a Yugoslavian inventor and engineer in electricity. He worked a lot on the conversions of energy and is the author of over 900 patents. 
• During his interrogation with Viktor, Tesla creates a whole string of made-up relatives, including his mother, Marianne Onyx, his father Allen Onyx and his grandmother, Leah Onyx.
• Tesla's post in the Toran Republic is given in the English localization as "Secretary-General", however a more literal translation would be "Secretary of the Toran Republic". The Genso Suikoden Encyclopaedia also gives his title as "Secretary of the Republic", another way of saying "Secretary of State".

name: Valeria
description: Valeria (バレリア, Bareria) is a supporting character in Suikoden and Suikoden II who also appears in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2. Valeria attempted to stop Kwanda Rosman's scheme to annihilate the Great Forest.

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
Valeria has a strong and confident appearance, with long, wavy, light brown hair and piercing green eyes. Her facial expression is calm but determined, giving her an air of discipline and skill.

She wears a striking red outfit with gold and black armor elements. Her attire consists of a red dress or tunic with high slits on the sides, allowing for ease of movement in battle.

Over this, she wears gold and black plate armor, including shoulder guards, a breastplate, and protective elements on her waist and hips. She also wears red boots with black and gold accents, completing her uniform.

She wears a long white glove on her sword hand and a shorter glove on her opposite hand. She wields a rapier, the Star Sword, which once belonged to her

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
Known as "Blazing Valeria" for her fierce combat style and red attire, Valeria was disciplined, straightforward, and deeply loyal to those she cared for. Her sense of duty and moral integrity often drove her to make difficult choices, such as defecting from the Imperial Army to protect her homeland and joining the Toran Liberation Army.

Despite her stern exterior, Valeria was deeply compassionate, as seen in her willingness to sacrifice herself to save others and her efforts to mediate with the elves after the war. Although disciplined, she was a very emotional and passionate person.

Her rivalry with Anita, though filled with competitive banter, is rooted in mutual respect and a shared drive to improve. Valeria's pragmatic nature and experience as a veteran also makes her a voice of wisdom during times of crisis, as she understands the importance of leadership and composure in the face of

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Born in a humble village near the Great Forest, Valeria grew up in poverty, her father having retired from the Imperial Army to become a farmer. When her mother was killed by a one-tusked Gold Boar, Valeria, at the age of 13, vowed revenge and took on the responsibility of her family.

By 14, she had taught herself swordsmanship and, with the aid of the swordsman Kong Long, avenged her mother's death. Her feat of slaying the boar earned her local renown, and she later discovered her father's Star Sword, which became her weapon of choice.
