Trained by Kong Long and inspired by tales of the Tiger General, Valeria joined the Imperial Army under General Kwanda Rosman during the War of Succession, earning the nickname "Blazing Valeria" for her fiery combat style and crimson attire. She rose through the ranks due to her skill and intelligence, eventually training under the legendary Roundia Haia in Kanakan, where she formed a fierce rivalry with Anita.

Though their personalities clashed—Valeria being disciplined and Anita carefree—they shared a deep mutual respect. After graduating, Valeria was granted the Falcon Rune, further enhancing her prowess.

During the Gate Rune War, Valeria's loyalty to the Empire wavered when Kwanda Rosman planned to burn the Great Forest using the Burning Mirror. Horrified at the potential destruction of her homeland, she defected, warning the elves of Lukie Ende Towayo.

Though initially imprisoned, she joined the Toran Liberation Army after meeting its members. Valeria's selflessness was evident when she surrendered herself to the Imperial Army to ensure the destruction of the Burning Mirror, a sacrifice that nearly cost her life.

Rescued by Liberation forces, she continued to fight for their cause for the remainder of the war. Following the conflict, she would become a Great General of the Toran Republic and a mediator with the elves.

In…

recruitment: Joins automatically following plot events in the Village of the Elves.
trivia: • Valeria was Murayama Yoshitaka's favorite character and he credits his favoritism for the power of Valeria's Falcon Rune, as well as its unique critical hit animation, in Suikoden II.

name: Varkas
description: Varkas (バルカス, Barukasu) is a supporting character in Suikoden who makes a cameo appearance in Suikoden II. Varkas is the leader of the Mt. Seifu bandits and was named the Captain of the Border Guard.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Varkas was the leader of the Mt. Seifu bandits, known as the "Whirlwind Axe", due to his battlefield prowess and the force of the small hand-axes he wielded in combat. He was described as a well-built man with a square, tanned face and thick eyebrows, protruding from a ridge. He was the third son of a farming family and had learned some letters and words from a priest as a youth, marking him as not completely illiterate.

He originally served as second-in-command of the bandits, under his childhood friend, Guin. However, Guin's increasingly autocratic, self-serving, and paranoid rule would lead to arguments between the two, culminating in Guin poisoning Varkas and attempting to assassinate him. Sydonia, who Varkas had been taking care of after finding him in a carriage the group had raided, would prevent the attack and with his last ounce of strength, Varkas would strike Guin down.

Varkas would draw the ire of Grady, the military commander of Rockland, who accused Varkas and the Mt. Seifu bandits of pillaging local villages and rendering Rockland unable to collect taxes. This, however, was untrue, as Varkas' Mt. Seifu bandits only stole from Scarlet Moon Empire officials and never from common citizens such as farmers or merchants. By this point, banditry was also a way of avenging the resentment of those who had suffered under the oppressive rule of the Scarlet Moon Empire. 

Eventually, the son of Teo McDohl would be ordered to apprehend him and Varkas and his second-in-command, Sydonia, were defeated and captured. Varkas and Sydonia were strung up on stakes to starve outside of Grady's mansion. One of the Mt. Seifu bandits travelled to Lenankamp on behalf of Varkas, asking for the Toran Liberation Army to rescue them. His previous captors, outraged by Grady's behavior, would rescue the bandits. 

After the Toran Liberation Army acquired Toran Castle, Varkas and…

recruitment: Joins automatically at Toran Castle following plot events in Kouan.

name: Viki
description: Viki (ビッキー, Bikkī) is a recurring character who appears in seven games in the Suikoden series. Viki is a ditzy teleporter from another dimension who remains much of a mystery.

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
Viki is a pretty young woman with long brown (sometimes depicted as black) hair and pale green eyes. Her age is apparently locked to 16 years old, due to her teleportation being temporal as well as physical.

Her outfit across her many game appearances is broadly consistent in depicting a long, white dress with blue and yellow design tails along with a blue tabard. She accompanies this with a pair of white shoes. She usually wears a small necklace. Her Suikoden III outfit is perhaps the most

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
Despite Viki's unassuming appearance, she is a very absent-minded young lady and often makes mistakes. However, her easy-going personality and good-natured tone make her easily forgiven. Carefree, she speaks in a familiar manner to almost everyone she meets.

She is a skilled magician and a bearer of the Blinking Rune but her eccentric nature marks her as somewhat unreliable. Her sneezes often caused unpredictable results, teleporting people and items either to or away from her. One example of Viki's unpredictable nature is the existence of her younger self, who sometimes appears when Vicky sneezes.

This younger Viki has a much more somber personality and is the complete opposite of Viki. The relationship between the two is

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Viki's home is a mysterious place of unknown age and region. It seems that the doors of each room in this place are connected to other points in time and space, but the details remain a mystery. It may have something to do with the word "waffu..." which she occasionally muttered in her sleep. Her teleporting apparently had a destination in mind.

Viki first appeared in the Toran region when she teleported into the Forest of Moran while McDohl and his entourage were passing through. Confused and disoriented, Viki was surprised to find out that she was in the Scarlet Moon Empire, remarking that it was "almost on the other side".

Lost and obviously out of place, Viki was taken in by the Toran Liberation Army where she helped teleport the hero's entourage where they needed to go in the Toran region. Befitting her nature, she would sometimes forget spells.

Following the end of the Gate Rune War, Viki disappeared without a trace following a failed teleportation attempt. She would end up in the Matilda Knightdom, three years later, during the Dunan Unification War. Appearing in front of the leader of the New Alliance Army, she would agree to join the group, once again lending her teleport magic to the group.

Following the end of the Dunan Unification War, Viki would sneeze during a celebratory banquet, once again vanishing in the process. According to one story, she would appear in front of Nash Latkje as he traveled to Crystal Valley.

Noticing that her friends from the New Alliance Army, Millie and Meg were there and overhearing that they were lost, she attempted to teleport them to their destination. This would end up taking the group through a variety of times and locations. During this adventure, they would also meet a younger version of Viki, deepening her mystery further.

Viki would next appear at the Mountain Path near the border between the Grasslands and the…

recruitment: Talk to Viki when she appears in the Great Forest and agree to take her in.
trivia: • Viki is the second most prolific character in the Suikoden series, behind Jeane, with nine appearances across 14 games. She also has eight different Genso Suikoden Card Stories TCG character cards, tying her with the hero of Suikoden II.

name: Vincent de Boule
description: Vincent De Boule (ヴァンサン・ド・ブール, Vansan Do Būru) is a character in Suikoden and Suikoden II who also makes a cameo in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2. Vincent is an aristocrat prone to fanciful claims although his noble standing is called into question by some.
originalName: ヴァンサン・ド・ブール

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
Vincent De Boule is a pale, slender man clad in an elaborate outfit befitting his nobility. In Suikoden, he wears a green vest, embroidered with gold, with white underclothes. He also wears a red cape, white ruffle and knee-high green, gold embroidered boots.

In Suikoden II, his outfit is even more extravagant. His green outfit now consisting of puff shoulders, green and silver striped pantaloons, a large red ribbon around the waist and golden ribbons and bows tied around his white leggings and green shoes. He also wore a half-cape, emblazoned with a rose, over his right arm. 

The white elements of his clothing were said to offset the more ostentatious offerings of his outfit while the coordination of green across all elements of his outfit were said to speak to his eye for

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
thumb|left|220px|Vincent (second from the right) enjoys tea with Milich Oppenheimer|Milich, Simone and [[Esmeralda.]]Vincent always carried himself with poise and was rarely seen without a rose in his hand. His eccentric tone gave him a generally elusive air.

Some of his actions during the Gate Rune War, such as his running out on bills and imprisonment led some to believe he was a fraudulent or self-proclaimed noble but both his inheritance and the investigations of Richmond would confirm that prior to the collapse of the Scarlet Moon Empire, he was indeed a nobleman of the state.

Trust was something valued very highly by Vincent, as he felt that true friendship could not blossom from mistrust. He was deeply hurt by accusations that his sentiments and actions were not motivated by honesty. In his mind, the reason why he asked the hero to pay for his lodging in Antei was to make the young master reflect on the foolishness of not trusting others.

As a nobleman of the Scarlet Moon Empire, he was very much a soul mate of other aristocratic-inclined individuals such as Simone Verdricci, Milich Oppenheimer and Esmeralda and often took tea and chatted with them. However, it was not only people similar in manners to him who Vincent could consider a soul mate.

As well as declaring McDohl a dear friend for rescuing him during the Gate Rune War, he would later consider the leader of the New Alliance Army his bosom friend, for aiding Simone during the Dunan Unification War. He would also remind Simone that their friendship was a beautiful bond that could not be weakened by the losing of trinkets.

Although it may seem like Vincent dispensed this title freely, he did not, in fact, devote himself lightly. He merely had several close friends whom he held a sincere respect for. His faith in such true friends, therefore, is not shaken easily and he is sure not to betray such…

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
thumb|left|220px|Vincent was first encountered in Antei, trying to charm his way out of paying for his meal.Vincent De Boule was a member of the Scarlet Moon Empire aristocracy. He was very much a natural noble, enjoying poetic conversations, hosting elegant tea parties and always making sure to be at the cutting edge of aristocratic fashion. 

As a child, he used to sneak through a hidden entrance to visit the Dragon Knight's Domain. During these times, Vincent would often see Joshua Levenheit riding his dragon. Although the two did not seem to interact, for Vincent, a nobleman of the Empire, Joshua, the leader of the Knights of the Dragon's Den, honored by the Empire, was a natural "dear friend".

During the Gate Rune War, he first encountered the Toran Liberation Army in Antei, where he skipped out on a meal due to a claimed lack of money. He would encounter the group again at the entrance to the Dragon's Den, where he showed them his secret entrance to the guarded cave.

He would later be imprisoned in Moravia Castle and when rescued he would immediately join the Toran Liberation Army, discarding his noble status and joyfully embracing its leader as a true friend.

Although a noble, he lost his title after the Gate Rune War, when the new Toran Republic abolished the Scarlet Moon nobility. Vincent was still entitled to return home and receive his inheritance, however. After that, he would use his funds to study abroad, traveling around the world. A month before arriving in the City-States of Jowston, he would meet and become swift friends with Simone Verdricci.

thumb|right|220px|In Radat Town, Vincent searched for his dear friend, Simone.As the Dunan Unification War raged, he would join the New Alliance Army after hearing how its leader had helped Simone recover the Rose Brooch Vincent had given him, although he would gently admonish Simone for thinking their…

recruitment: Following the rescue of Viktor and Warren return to the jail-cell on the top floor of Moravia Castle and talk to Vincent inside the cell.
trivia: • Vincent's best performance in the annual [https://twitter.com/gensosenkyo Gensosenkyo fan poll] is a tie for 51st in 2018. He did not place at all in 2017 or 2019.

name: Warren
description: Warren (ウォーレン, Uōren) is a supporting character in Suikoden. Warren is a noble sympathetic to the liberation movement within the Scarlet Moon Empire.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Warren was a wealthy man and landowner from the northern reaches of the Scarlet Moon Empire. The wealthiest man in Dana, he was known affectionately as "Master Warren" due to his excellent reputation as a kind-hearted and generous man. He was respected both by the empire and its enemies.

Many passers-by had become grateful house guests at Warren's mansion. During the Gate Rune War, however, some of those guests included fugitives fighting against the Scarlet Moon Empire and so he was arrested by Kasim Hazil and taken to Moravia Castle, following the imperial crackdown of rebel groups in the region.

He was swiftly rescued by the Toran Liberation Army, along with Viktor who had been arrested at the mansion as well. It should be noted that Warren did not know Viktor was a member of the Liberation Army when he invited him to stay. He had merely heard rumor of a powerful wandering swordsman passing through and had sought Viktor out on that basis.

Following his rescue from Moravia Castle and having heard tales from Viktor of the Liberation Army and its leader, Warren returned the kindness by joining the army. Although he thanked the hero lightly, he took his debt to him seriously and was a fierce and zealous supporter of the liberation movement's fight against oppression.

He fought in Viktor's Team during the war, showing pride in his skill with the spear while clad in colorful clothing. His spear head was elongated, marking it as something akin to a naginata. Many were amazed that his prowess was not dulled by his years. In addition, Warren's financial contributions to the Liberation Army's cause was a giant step forward to their eventual victory.

Following the end of the war and the foundation of the Toran Republic, Warren's virtue led him to be appointed the first Chairman of the Republican Congress, working to support President

recruitment: Recruited automatically following plot events at Moravia Castle.
trivia:
• Warren was originally envisioned as Camille's employer but all events and dialogue alluding to this were removed.
• He was likely named after American actor Warren Beatty.

name: Window
description: Window (ウインドウ, Uindou) is a character in Suikoden. Window is a young man of the Warriors' Village with little interest for the village's traditions.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Window was a member of the Warriors' Village, but he did not pursue the path of a warrior. Instead, his true passion lay in the art of decorative window making. This rare vocation was held in high-esteem so he had no issues living in the village regardless of his lack of martial prowess.

As the designated heir to the position of window craftsman in the village, Window harbored a lifelong desire to obtain the Window Rune. When the Toran Liberation Army presented him with the rune he had sought since childhood, having found it in the Cave of the Past, he agreed to join their cause.

During his time at Toran Castle, Window showcased his exceptional skills by installing numerous windows that blended both aesthetic beauty and practical functionality. He also fought alongside other artisans in the Musicians unit on the battlefield alongside Melodye and Kasios.

His craftsmanship earned him recognition, and after the conclusion of the Gate Rune War, he was honored with a commission to work on the grand windows on Magician's

recruitment: Bring Window in Warriors' Village the Window Orb found in a chest inside the Cave of the Past.
trivia: • Initial designs had a window in Window's top hat, from which pigeons could emerge.

name: Windy
description: Windy (ウィンディ, Uindei) is a main antagonist in Suikoden. A Court Magician within the Scarlet Moon Empire, Windy utilizes her influential position and formidable magical abilities in relentless pursuit of the Soul Eater.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Windy was a formidable magician and member of the Clan of the Gate. She was also the bearer of the front half of the Gate Rune, acquired during an assault by the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia on the clan.

During this encounter, Windy and her clan sister, Leknaat, divided the rune, with each taking a portion and subsequently employing the Gate Rune's powers to escape. Windy assumed ownership of the "front" Gate Rune, granting her the capability to summon monsters into the world.

In the aftermath of the aforementioned attack, Windy's heart sank into despair, fueling her fervent desire for vengeance against Harmonia, High Priest Hikusaak, and the entire world. This thirst for retribution compelled her to pursue another True Rune relentlessly. Initially targeting Leknaat's half of the Gate Rune, her endeavors proved fruitless.

Subsequently, Windy fixated on a new objective, the Rune of Life and Death, developing an unnatural obsession with it. Her relentless quest for the True Rune resulted in the repeated recurrences of the tragic event that had been inflicted upon her by Harmonia in the past.

220px||thumb|By 455, Windy was serving as Emperor Barbarossa Rugner|Barbarossa's Court Magician (PS1)|left
Windy's pursuit of the Soul Eater spanned over three centuries, reaching the year SY 449. At this point, she had ascended to the esteemed position of Court Magician within the Scarlet Moon Empire. Leveraging her physical resemblance to the late Empress Claudia, Windy garnered ever-increasing power and influence within the Empire, particularly over Emperor Barbarossa, who was drawn to her anguish.

Manipulating the Empire through her magical prowess, she granted Conqueror Runes to the Great Generals and to Barbarossa, effectively maintaining control over them. During this period, she succeeded in locating Ted and sought to claim the Soul Eater from him. However, Ted managed to…

trivia: • Though the game frequently refers to Leknaat as Windy's sister, the two are clan sisters only and are officially unrelated.

name: Yam Koo
description: Yam Koo (ヤム・クー, Yamu Kū) is a supporting character in Suikoden and Suikoden II.

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
Yam Koo's face was obscured by his messy blond hair, but he was at least attractive to Kimberly, who held a torch for him, despite her marriage to his sworn brother, Tai Ho. His eyes are not visible in any of his artwork, but his Suikoden unique sprites indicate that they are possible grey.

He wore a loose-fitting blue kimono, tied with a dark blue obi. In Suikoden, he wore black boots but by Suikoden II was wearing simple, straw sandals. He also wore a green headband, mostly obscured by his hair. He also wore a necklace.

Yam Koo's weapon is a trident, a type of three-pronged spear. His trident is very similar to that of Tai Ho's with only the hilt being a different

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
Unlike Tai Ho, Yam Koo tended to be more logical and didn't wish to live his life purely by chance, calling Tai Ho's whims his "Big Brother's madness". Despite this, he would often smile and go along with Tai Ho's whims, despite his complaints.

He had a sense of responsibility as regards Tai Ho, working to ensure that Kimberly did not take advantage of his sworn brother, not realizing her eyes were in fact on him.

During the Dunan Unification War, he became immersed in his fishing, spending much of his time at the docks of the New Alliance Army Headquarters

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Yam Koo was a fisherman from Seika and the sworn brother of Tai Ho. The two met during a gambling brawl and have been friends ever since. Yam Koo was a boat pilot operating out of Kaku at the time of the Gate Rune War.

Yam Koo was swept away in one of Tai Ho's whims when Tai Ho agreed to have he and Yam Koo escort McDohl to Toran Castle after losing a game of Chinchirorin. When Tai Ho joined the Toran Liberation Army, Yam Koo joined along with him, despite his grumblings. 

During the war, Tai Ho met Kimberly. Yam Koo, knowing Tai Ho's weakness for women, tried to protect Tai Ho from Kimberly. Following the end of the Gate Rune War, Yam Koo lived near the shores of Lake Toran, where he continued to live a carefree life and enjoyed fishing, oblivious to the fact that Kimberly had her sights set on him, not Tai Ho.

Later, Yam Koo followed Tai Ho to the City-States of Jowston, helping Tai Ho escape his now-wife Kimberly. Tai Ho and Yam Koo met the hero in Coronet Town and helped transport his party to Kuskus Town once Tai Ho lost a game of Chinchirorin. 

Later, Tai Ho agreed to join the New Alliance Army, and Yam Koo agreed to go along, with some reluctance. At the New Alliance Army Headquarters, Yam Koo showed his passion for fishing, even encouraging the hero to get into the sport.

He decided not to join Tai Ho in battle during the Dunan Unification War, reasoning that his presence might drive his sworn brother to act more carelessly. His passion for fishing may also have discouraged him from leaving the docks.

Following the war, Yam Koo returned to the Toran Republic, where he enjoyed a life of fishing on Lake

recruitment: Recruited automatically during plot events at the Ancient Castle of Toran.

name: Yuber
description: Yuber (ユーバー, Yūbā) is a recurring antagonist who appears in Suikoden, Suikoden II and Suikoden III. Known as the Black Knight, Yuber has appeared in many conflicts throughout history.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
A demon who violently hates True Runes and desires nothing but to create utter chaos and destruction in the world. His thirst for suffering is quenched only by killing others and hearing their dying screams. Yuber bears one of the 27 True Runes, the Eightfold Rune, and is a master of both magic and sword technique.

His cruelty, aura of darkness and awesome power mark him as something other than a human and he has been described as a "soldier of death". Known to appear on bloody battlefields as an eerie black knight, Yuber has been a scourge in many wars over the years. 

Yuber has been a subordinate of Windy, complicit in her attack on the Village of the Hidden Rune as well as during the Gate Rune War. Yuber led the Imperial Army during the last battle. Following his loss, however, he was dismissive of the result, holding no true loyalty to Windy. He would then disappear from the battlefield, pursued by Pesmerga, who had been hunting him for some time. 

During the Dunan Unification War, Yuber was invited by the genius strategist Leon Silverberg to fight for the Highland Army. He showed more of his great ability under Jowy Atreides. Stationed at Greenhill City, he would encounter the leader of the New Alliance Army there, seemingly taking an interest in his True Rune, regarding it with anger and hatred. He would summon a Bone Dragon to attack his enemies before abandoning the city.

He would participate in several more battles during the war but would abandon the Highland Kingdom during the last battle, seeing little value in fighting for a doomed country. He was once again pursued by Pesmerga, who called him "a devil, a shadow, my arch-nemesis......" although the meaning of this has yet to be revealed and their true connection is unknown.

Some 15 years later, prior to the War of the Champions, he was contacted by Luc, through Albert Silverberg, who knew how to…

recruitment: Joins automatically during Chapter 6 provided 104 Stars are recruited before the end of Chapter 5.

name: Zen
description: Zen (ゼン, Zen) is a supporting character in Suikoden. Zen is a gardener who worked at Qlon Temple before joining the Toran Liberation Army.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Zen was a skilled gardener known for his expertise in landscaping and his ability to cultivate vibrant and colorful flowers. He served as the gardener at Qlon Temple, where his talent for nurturing flowers shone through in the beautiful displays he created.

When approached by the leader of the Toran Liberation Army, Zen willingly agreed to join their cause. However, before joining, he requested specific seeds—Red Flower Seeds, Blue Flower Seeds, and Yellow Flower Seeds—to finish his work at the temple. These flowers were chosen as they didn't require constant maintenance, allowing their natural beauty to flourish over time.

After planting the requested seeds, Zen became a member of the Liberation Army. During his time at Toran Castle, he worked on the flower garden where Milich Oppenheimer and other aristocrats spent their time but often mentioned that it was not an ideal season for flowers due to a lack of wind.

With a gentle and good-natured demeanor, Zen was a reserved individual who preferred working quietly in the background, tending to his flowers with utmost care and tenderness.

Even after the conclusion of the Gate Rune War, Zen remained at Toran Castle, continuing to lend his expertise and passion to create flourishing gardens adorned with blossoming flowers. His presence and efforts contributed to the beauty and ambiance of the castle's

recruitment: Talk to Zen at the rear of Qlon Temple and bring him the Red, Yellow and Blue Flower Seeds. They are found at the item shops in the Warriors' Village, Teien and Rikon respectively.

name: Zorak
description: Zorak (ゾラック, Zorakku) is a supporting character in Suikoden. Zorak is the loquacious chief of Warriors' Village.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Zorak held the esteemed position of being the leader of the renowned Warriors' Village and was also the father of Tengaar. At some point in the past he lost his eye, suffering a grievous wound in the process, suggesting he was once a brave veteran of many battle. He took great pride in his heritage and possessed a vast wealth of historical knowledge concerning both Warriors' Village and the wider Lorimar region.

As an older man, Zorak had a penchant for going off on long tangents. When the hero arrived at the village, Zorak welcomed them into his home to discuss the village's situation with Neclord, which then diverted into a speech on the history and customs of Warriors' Village. The discussion continued until the sun had set.

The following morning, Neclord appeared at the village, seeking Tengaar. Despite Zorak's vow to prevent Neclord from taking his daughter, he proved powerless to stop the villain. Determined to rescue Tengaar, Zorak, accompanied by Hix, marched to Neclord's Castle. However, Neclord barred their entry, allowing only McDohl and his party inside.

In a plea to the hero, Zorak sought assistance in rescuing Tengaar and granted Hix full warrior status to aid in the mission. Secretly, Zorak knew of Hix and Tengaar's relationship and saw this opportunity as a way to help Hix realize his potential as a warrior. As the future husband of Tengaar, Hix's success would make him the rightful leader of Warriors' Village, and Zorak held high hopes for him.

Following the triumphant defeat of Neclord, Zorak made a binding agreement with the Toran Liberation Army, pledging that the Warriors' Village would serve the army. During the final battle, Zorak valiantly led his men to Gregminster to provide aid to the Liberation Army.

Reflecting on the Gate Rune War, Zorak expressed his admiration for the conflict and began to recount the tale of his ancestor, Clift…

name: Abizboah
description: Abizboah (アビズボア, Abizuboa) is a character in Suikoden II who also appears in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2. He is a kraken who aided the New Alliance Army during the Dunan Unification War.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Abizboah is a kraken, a type of sea monster, who lived in the waters in the Tinto Mines. Information on Abizboah's exact biology is unknown, but it is known he was very intelligent, both speaking his own language as well as being able to understand the language of humans. He could not speak a human language due to the sheer difference in their vocal cords.

During the Dunan Unification War, Abizboah encountered the hero. Normally, he would never even approach a human, but the Listening Orb the hero was carrying made communication possible. Abizboah decided to join the New Alliance Army, along with his wife, Rulodia, apparently moved by the hero's aspirations for peace.

His exploits as a naval unit during the war proved his commitment to the hero's cause. Abizboah had a playful nature, teasing Viki while she was walking on the docks. However, she teleported him into the middle of the lake for doing so. 

During the Dunan Unification War, Rulodia and Abizboah had a child at the New Alliance Army Headquarters, Chuchara. Following the war, Abizboah remained at New Alliance Army Headquarters and was occasionally spotted from time to time by the residents there.

recruitment: Feather: After recruiting Badeaux, go to Forest Village and defeat the Griffons, then agree to spare Feather. Sigfried: After recruiting Badeaux, as well as completing the sidequest for Hix/Tengaar, go to the same place you found him in Unicorn Forest with a young female in the party (Mikumiku and Sierra count) Abizboah: Go to the Tinto mines and find the little pool of water close to the entrance, and use the Listening Crystal there.

name: Adlai
description: Adlai (アダリー, Adarī) is a character in Suikoden II who also appears in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.1. Adlai is an eccentric inventor who came up with a form of elevator.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Adlai is a self-proclaimed genius inventor, although there is little evidence that any more than a handful of his designs that have been completed and worked. Where he gets his research funds from is a mystery although many rumors abound that he creates them himself.

His eccentricities and almost entirely useless inventions (such as his rabbit noise shoes) have caused a lot of damage, making him something of a nuisance to those who live near his home in South Window City, which doubles as his laboratory. The salvation of the hapless residents of South Window City would be the leader of the New Alliance Army.

Adlai rudely demanded several errands from him and after one order too far, a Wind Orb soared towards his head by some unexpected coincidence. The shock apparently inspired Adlai to continue his work on the elevator mechanism. Joining the New Alliance Army, he immediately headed for the New Alliance Army Headquarters and installed an elevator there without permission. The speed in which he installed it may indicate another talent of his.

It is later shown that Adlai's elevator was in fact powered by humans, called "Elevator barbarians", located in a hidden room above the elevator. They would operate the elevator using a system of pulleys and could sometimes be heard complaining about the heat. On his journey through Greenhill Forest, Nash Latkje came across Adlai training his elevator barbarians.

Returning to South Window following the war, the possibility that the elevator is Adlai's first (and last) successful invention is undeniable, but with his elevator running and its merits plain to see, it appears his talent as an engineer is, in fact, genuine.

Shizu was an apprentice of Adlai and built and maintained elevators using Adlai's methods.

recruitment: In a house in South-Window he will ask for three items. Bring him a Wooden shield, Sacrificial Jizo and finally a wind rune. Throw the Wind Rune at him and he will join!

name: Agares Blight
description: Agares Blight (アガレス・ブライト, Agaresu Buraito) is a supporting character from Suikoden II. Agares is introduced as the ruler of the Highland Kingdom, although he is quickly marginalised by his son Luca Blight as part of the latter's maniacal plans.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
thumb|left|220px|After his ascension, Agares (seated upper left) immediately pushed for peace with City-States of Jowston|Jowston.Born in 408, Agares Blight became the 22nd king of Highland at the age of 27, following the death of his father, Robert, during the Jowston-Highland war. Although not a talented ruler, he was a hopeful pacifist who disliked combat and so sued for peace, ending the war soon after his reign began. He continued to maintain an uneasy peace with the City-States of Jowston over the years.

He was married to the noble Sara by the time he took the throne in 434 and his son, Luca was born the same year. The marriage was an arranged one, although Agares still felt love for his wife. Indeed, some of Agares' desire for peace may have been entrusted to him by his wife. 

In 443, nine years after Agares took the throne, the Blight royal caravan was attacked by what was ostensibly a group of bandits, although the equipment and training of the brigands indicated they were trained by Muse. There, Agares abandoned his wife and son Luca and made for the capital to raise the alarm.

Although Agares had escaped because he thought the bandits would target him alone as King of Highland, and also because of the need to raise the Highland Army to rescue his family, he also felt guilt and shame for how things turned out. He suspected Muse of orchestrating the attack but knew that without proof any reprisal would be seen as aggression by the world.

Luca would always hold his father responsible for the events and their repercussions, including the breakdown and eventual death of Sara. Realizing Luca's hatred, Agares would begin to gradually distance himself from his son despite still holding affection for him. Meanwhile, Agares was always distant and unaffectionate…

name: Alberto
description: Alberto (アルバート, Arubāto) is a character in Suikoden II. He is a musician in a band with Pico and Annallee.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Alberto is the cellist and leader of his traveling band. He was raised in a craftsman household and was once apprenticed to a barrel maker, but he quit his job and left his family in order to pursue a career in music. He was obsessed with music from an early age and unable to live the life of a craftsman, chose to distance himself entirely by becoming a wandering musician.

Traveling to Muse City, he would first meet Pico and the two started performing music together. Later the two would encounter Annallee, who had come to Muse following the loss of her parents. The trio would eventually become a traveling band, with Alberto acting as the leader as he was the oldest and most mature.

Alberto's dexterity made him a skilled cellist, and he was also a gifted chef and tailor. He was the one who sewed Annallee's outfits, handled Pico's romantic problems, and managed the finances of the group.

During the Dunan Unification War, Alberto, Pico, and Annallee traveled to Muse Principality. When Muse was attacked, the three were scattered and Alberto eventually wound up in Crom Village, where he raised money as an armorer with the intent of searching for Pico and Annallee.

When Alberto learned from the hero that his friends had joined the New Alliance Army, he eagerly joined, reuniting the group. Alberto and his bandmates performed music for the New Alliance Army throughout the duration of the war, helping soothe the heart of the weary soldiers resting at New Alliance Army Headquarters.

Following the end of the war, Alberto continued to travel with his group while improving his smithing skills.

trivia: • Artwork from Genso Suikoden Card Stories erroneously shows Alberto playing a guitar and Pico playing a cello; in Suikoden II, Alberto is the cellist and Pico is the guitarist.
recruitment: After recruiting both Annallee and Pico, speak to him at Tinto Armor Shop.

name: Alex
description: Alex (アレックス, Arekkusu) is a character in Suikoden II. Alex is a treasure hunter who looks to find treasures to provide for his wife, Hilda, and son, Pete.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Alex is a treasure hunter who lives near the Sindar Ruins at the White Deer Inn with his wife, Hilda. Alex met his wife during Muse's Harvest Festival and the two married six months later. The two eventually had a son, Pete, and opened an inn on the outskirts of Muse. 

Alex became obsessed with the White Deer Inn ruins, believing them to be full of treasure. Though he styled himself a treasure hunter, he was a family man at heart and aimed to provide Hilda and Pete with a better life through his treasure hunting.

He was unable to secure much support for his plans in Muse. This obsession led him to neglect his work at the inn. When the hero, Jowy, and Nanami's party arrived at the White Deer Inn, Alex made a deal that he would loan the party their Entry Permit's if they would help him look for treasure.

The ruins were a disappointment as Alex only found some herbs, much to his initial disappointment. However, when he discovered Hilda had fallen ill from overwork, the herbs proved priceless as they quickly cured her and helped Alex realize his true priorities.

During the Dunan Unification War, Alex and Hilda were forced to abandon the White Deer Inn and flee to South Window City. There, they ran into the hero again and became part of the New Alliance Army. Alex set up an item store for the New Alliance Army and was briefly involved in battle when he acted as a faux reinforcement during a battle in the Matilda Knightdom.

Killey would later aggressively question Alex about his experience at the Sindar Ruins. Demanding to know what Alex discovered there, Alex would respond by pointing out his wife, his most dear treasure, and telling Killey that was what he discovered, the importance of his loved ones. Killey was seemingly satisfied with the answer.

After the war,…

trivia: • Alex is one of the few characters who is known to be able to read Sindarin text.
recruitment: Joins with Hilda.

name: Amada
description: Amada (アマダ, Amada) is a character in Suikoden II. He is a sailor from the Island Nations who spent some time working in Radat Town during the Dunan Unification War.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Originally hailing from the Island Nations, Amada found employment in Radat Town, a place known for its frequent trade with the islands. There, he worked as a sailor on trading vessels as well alongside the crew responsible for operating the sluice gate and managing water levels in the area. His sailing work meant that he had somewhat of a professional relationship with Shu.

When the hero, accompanied by Nanami and Apple, needed the water level lowered, Amada initially refused, following Gin's initial rebuff. However, upon witnessing the hero's determination, he eventually agreed to lower the water level after being defeated by the hero in a duel.

Following the establishment of the New Alliance Army, Amada became one of the two individuals considered to guide the hero and the party to Lakewest. Before accepting the task, he challenged the hero to a second duel. Defeated once more, he willingly joined the ranks of the army, showcasing his prowess as a sailor and a naval soldier during the ensuing battle against the Highland Kingdom.

After the conclusion of the Dunan Unification War, Amada assumed command of a vessel and returned to his native Island Nations, continuing his life tied to the sea.

recruitment: After meeting Fitcher and being told to find a captain, go to Radat town where you met him before, and defeat him at a duel and he'll join.

name: Anabelle
description: Anabelle (アナベル, Anaberu) is a supporting character in Suikoden II and Genso Suikoden Card Stories. Anabelle is the popular mayor of Muse City at the beginning of the Dunan Unification War.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Standing as tall as most men, Anabelle was distinguished by her reddish-brown curly hair, which flowed below her shoulders, and her characteristic attire, often consisting of a blue shirt, white trousers, and a vest, complemented by a blue bandana.

Anabelle assumed the mayoral position in Muse City following her father Darell's tenure as mayor. This elevated her to a prominent leadership role within the City-States of Jowston, where she demonstrated exceptional talent in fostering unity among its diverse factions despite the frequent political clashes.

Anabelle shared a longstanding friendship with Viktor, forged during his early days as a mercenary in Muse City. In SY 449, after the vampire Neclord wreaked havoc on North Window, Anabelle joined forces with Viktor to form the South Window City investigation team.

This mission marked her first time away from her father, and the gruesome nature of the task, coupled with her personal connection to Viktor, weighed heavily on her. Six months later, she enlisted Viktor's help for another mission—investigating a plot that exploited the North Window incident to stoke tensions between South Window and Two River City, threatening war.

During the investigation, Anabelle, armed with a slender sword, displayed limited combat experience, rendering her most vulnerable among her companions. Nevertheless, with the timely intervention of Hauser, she emerged from the ordeal unscathed. Subsequently, she bid Viktor farewell as he embarked on his quest to vanquish Neclord, dedicating herself to strengthening the Jowston Alliance.

Anabelle's achievements stemmed from her political acumen, impartiality, and personal charisma. Additionally, the support and assistance provided by individuals such as Hauser, Jess, and Fitcher significantly…

name: Anita
description: Anita (アニタ, Anita) is a character in Suikoden II who also has a cameo in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2. She is a Falcon Rune user referred to as a master of the sword, love, and alcohol.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Anita, a formidable swordswoman hailing from the Nameless Lands, received her training in Kanakan under the skilled tutelage of Roundia Haia, a renowned master of the falcon-style of swordplay. As a testament to her skill, Anita was granted the Falcon Rune, symbolizing her proficiency and dedication to the art of swordplay. 

However, her journey was far from solitary, as she found herself entwined in a compelling rivalry with another student named Valeria—both entering Roundia's gates on the very same day. Embracing the fervent desire to surpass her rival, Anita embarked on a journey of further training, seeking worthy opponents to test her skills. However, the road to excellence proved to be arduous, leaving her with few chances to demonstrate her prowess.

It was during a moment of respite in vibrant Muse City that fate intervened. Observing Nash Latkje succumbing to the effects of Nanami's Special Beef Stew, Anita's journey became intertwined with the unfolding events of the Dunan Unification War.

Already known for her penchant for relaxation and her reputation for beauty, Anita's affection for liquor often brought forth a more amiable and friendly side. While her rivalry with Valeria endured in the realm of combat, with Valeria having a record of 10-1 against her, Anita's charm and charisma granted her an undeniable advantage in matters of the heart (such as with Gary or Edward). Although rivals, the two respected one another underneath their barbs and bluster.

Impressed by the helpful spirit of the New Alliance Army's leader, Anita made the pivotal decision to join the ranks of the army. While her quirks may have been well-known, her commitment to the cause was unquestionable. Stepping onto the battlefield, she fought with an unmatched ferocity, contributing…

trivia:
• Anita's ending changes depending on whether or not the player recruits Valeria.
• Anita can be recruited in Muse City as well as Banner Village; if Valeria is in the party, the two will have special dialogue.
recruitment: Speak with her several times in the Tavern at Muse City or the Inn at Banner Village. Meet her requests and flatter her. Leave the town and return after every request she makes. Eventually you will be able to recruit her.

name: Annallee
description: Annallee (アンネリー, Annerī) is a character in Suikoden II and Genso Suikogaiden Vol.1. Annallee is a shy but talented singer in a band with Pico and Alberto.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Annallee was originally from the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia. Her parents were killed after being falsely accused in her childhood, leaving her distant and unable to open her heart to others.

At some point, she would move to Muse City with the help of an acquaintance. Meeting Pico and Alberto there, she would gradually regain her vitality and energy thanks to the music the duo would perform together. Naturally gifted with a beautiful voice, she had no formal training. She began to sing along with their performances before deciding to follow a career as a singer.

When Muse City fell early in the Dunan Unification War, Annallee and her friends became scattered. Ending up in South Window City, she would meet the leader of the New Alliance Army. Drawn by his kindness, Annallee would agree to join the force. As the war continued, she would be reunited with her friends.

As part of the New Alliance Army, Annallee would perform to improve the morale of the soldiers. Even of the field of battle, her singing could provide great encouragement, a particularly talented ability.

Following the war, Annallee would continue to travel with Alberto and Pico, sharing her beautiful singing voice wherever she went.

trivia: • Her name is an homage to Anneli Drecker, frontwoman of the Norwegian duo "Bel Canto"..
recruitment: With a Level 3 castle, after defeating Luca, go to the South-Window inn and view a scene. Then find her out to the right of Town hall and listen to her sing (in some versions of the game you will not hear any music). You can recruit her before defeating Luca, but the castle needs to be at least level 3.

name: Ayda
description: Ayda (エイダ, Eida) is a character in Suikoden II who also appears in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.1. She is an archer and calls herself a "Guardian of the Forest".

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Ayda was born into a family of Guardians who had protected the forests in Greenhill Principality from poachers for generations. Ayda grew up to be such a Guardian herself, in her father's image, making full use of her knowledge of the forests and her skills as an archer.

The scar on her left cheek comes from a fight with some of these poachers and she considered it a badge of honor. At some point, Ayda's parents were killed by griffins although she bore the creatures no ill-will over the incident.

Perhaps because she was raised around animals and nature since birth, Ayda had a special ability to connect with animals and other creatures through their hearts. As she was unused to socializing with people, she could sometimes come off as unfriendly but in truth, Ayda was a very kind-hearted woman who truly loved the natural world.

Early in the Dunan Unification War, she would have an encounter with Nash Latkje when he inadvertently bought a horse belonging to Ayda from Hoi, who had stolen it. The misunderstanding would be soon cleared up and Ayda would take her ire out on Hoi, driving him from the forest.

Later during the war, an injured griffin arrived in the Forest Village and Ayda tried to stop the villagers from killing it. She stated that if the griffin were to recover and attack, she would fight it, but to do so when he was injured would be murder and would hold no meaning. When a group of griffins swooped in to attack Feather, Ayda tried to defend it but was defeated. The hero and his companions then arrived to help her.

After the incident, Ayda decided to join the New Alliance Army, feeling the root of evil would be more swiftly severed at its source and that the Highland Army present an existential threat to the forests.

Ayda liked the New Alliance Army…

recruitment: After recruiting Badeaux, go to forest Village and agree not to kill Feather. She and the Griffon will join. Alternatively, you can loose the fight against the Griffons then speak with her behind the building.

name: Badeaux
description: Badeaux (バド, Bado) is a character in Suikoden II who also appears in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.1. He is a taciturn "Beastmaster".

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Badeaux, a skilled Beastmaster hailing from the Grasslands, possessed a unique gift bestowed upon him by the Rune of Mindfulness, which allowed him to communicate with animals. During Nash Latkje's journey to Greenhill City, he stumbled upon Badeaux tenderly embracing a bear in the depths of the forest.

Being able to communication with animals led him to trust the hearts of animals over those of his fellow man. His enigmatic nature was first encountered by the New Alliance Army at the Greenhill-Matilda border, where he remained reticent until a distinctive "voice" reached his ears – a voice belonging to an animal.

Instinctively trusting the guidance of the animals and recognizing their participation in the ongoing conflicts, Badeaux decided to ally himself with the New Alliance Army, claiming to have heard the collective voices of many within its leader. In an act of generosity, he bestowed two Listening Orbs, powerful artifacts enabling the holder to communicate with and recruit animals, to the leader.

However, Badeaux's proficiency in communication with animals starkly contrasted his interactions with fellow humans, often displaying a lack of finesse in human relations. Consequently, he primarily engaged with animals and other creatures dwelling at the New Alliance Army Headquarters. The whip he wielded was part of his training methods and his scars were also a consequence of said methods not always been received positively.

His moments of leisure were spent atop the castle, nurturing a bond with pigeons. Among the vast array of creatures, his personal favorites were the charming Furballs but he was slightly unnerved by insects.

Following the culmination of the Dunan Unification War, Badeaux dedicated himself to his calling as a Beastmaster, continuing to serve…

recruitment: After Castle is Level 3, speak to him at the entrance to the Forest Path, with either Shiro or a Squirrel in your party.

name: Barbara
description: Barbara (バーバラ, Bābara) is a character in Suikoden II. Barbara is the storage keeper of the New Alliance Army.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Barbara is a storage keeper from North Window and a long time friend of Viktor. Barbara avoided Neclord's attack on the town as she had left three years previously for Ryube Village in order to be with her husband. When Viktor was recovered from the ruins, she would help treat his wounds in Kuskus Town, Barbara having stopped in the town on her way back to her family in North Window following her break-up.

With her good-hearted personality and strength, Barbara was invited by Viktor became the storage keeper for the Mercenary Fortress prior to the beginning of the Dunan Unification War. Her forthright personality lent itself well to keep the roughneck mercenaries in line and she was something of a maternal figure for the group.

As Viktor's mercenaries became part of the New Alliance Army, Barbara continued to work at the New Alliance Army Headquarters, maintaining its warehouse. She was poisoned with the Sleeping Maiden by Ryuki in order to goad Hai Yo into a cook-off but was given an antidote after Hai Yo defeated him.

Once the war ended, Barbara became the keeper of the Dunan Republic's national vault, presiding over the treasury.

recruitment: Joins automatically after establishing the headquarters.

name: Bashok
description: Bashok (リュウキ, Bashoku) is a minor character who appears in Suikoden II. He is a skilled chef and the father of Mamie

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Bashok was the fifth challenger who competed against Hai Yo in a Cook-off at New Alliance Army Headquarters. His specialty was Fried Rice.

He and Hai Yo were old friends, but he was forced to become an assassin for the Black Dragon Cooking Clan to raise money for the treatment of his seriously ill daughter, Mamie.

Following his loss, Bashok would renounce ties with the group, realizing he was foolish to trust the group, and swearing to save Mamie himself. She would eventually be saved by the treatments of Huan and Liukan. Her recovery following their treatments came from medicinal dishes prepared by Bashok and Hai Yo both.

name: Beecham
description: Beecham (ビッチャム, Bitchamu), localized as Beechum in Suikoden II, is a supporting character in Suikoden III who also appears in Suikoden II. Beecham is a veteran warrior of the Karaya Clan and Lucia's loyal aide.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Beecham was a subordinate of Lucia, chief of the Karaya Clan. He served Lucia during her attempts at reviving the clan and vengeance against the Wisemail family.

During the last battle of the Dunan Unification War, when the Karaya Clan were ordered to protect L'Renouille at all costs, Beecham risked his life to ensure that Lucia could escape, entrusting the future of the Karaya to her. Beecham charged the lines of the New Alliance Army to buy her time. It was during this battle when Beecham received the scar on his face.

His whereabouts were unknown immediately after the battle, but evidentially he survived and reunited with his people. Since the Dunan Unification War, Beecham took up bonsai as a hobby, but he was still a veteran warrior. His skills had not diminished by age, and he continued to assist Lucia both in and out of battle during the War of the Champions.

His reserved nature led a lot of the Karaya to have faith in him, but he could be hot-blooded. He had an especially hard time dealing with his hatred of the Zexen Federation although he managed to fight alongside them without incident as part of the Fire Bringer.

Following the war, Beecham continued to serve Chief Hugo at Lucia's behest.

name: Bernand
description: Bernand (ベルナンド, Berunando) is a character who appears in supplemental materials concerning Suikoden II. He was the founder of the Dunan Monarchy.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Bernand was the man who established the Dunan Monarchy, which once ruled the region, around the current city of South Window. Under his reign, the monarchy stretched as far as Two River Principality in the west to the eastern lands of Muse Principality in the east.

He imposed heavy taxation on his people and his reign is characterized as one of misrule and maladministration, although it is said that he laid a firm foundation for the new country.

trivia: • Localized name revealed on the second Suikoden Live event on 15 May, 2025.

name: Bianca
description: Bianca (ビアンカ, Bianka) is a minor character in Suikoden II. She is the pet cat of the private detective, Richmond.

facts.label: Artist
facts.value: Genso Suikoden Genso Shinsho Vol.9Genso Shinsho Vol.9

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Bianca was a female cat who was found and adopted by Richmond. She was a long-haired cat, which was something of a rarity. She was considered ugly, so Richmond was the only person willing to take her in. She has the talent to be a detective, perhaps because she is often watching her master's work.

It is said that she was asked to help investigate animals and monsters by Richmond in his work for the New Alliance Army. Since Richmond did not speak cat, it seemed that he relied on Badeaux to interpret Bianca's words for him.

name: Bob
description: Bob (ボブ, Bobu) is a character in Suikoden II who also makes a cameo in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.1. Bob is a lycanthrope whose village was destroyed by Windy.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Bob is a lycanthrope who holds a Rabid Fang Rune. This rune helps him maintain his human form, although when his body is weakened, he transforms into his werewolf form more easily. With his enhanced werewolf strength, he could perform outstanding physical attacks. He used a hard staff as a weapon in his human form. Maintaining his hairstyle was one of his hobbies.

Bob was born into the Lycanthrope Village in the Grasslands; however, his homeland was destroyed by Windy. After its destruction, Bob became a wanderer, often being discriminated against and deceived by humans due to his half-beast nature. Due to this, he was very cautious and embittered but also a lonely figure.

In the Greenhill Forest, he was seen peeking out at others passing through the forest and interacting, wary but lonely. Eventually, as the Dunan Unification War flared up, Bob ended up in the kobold district of Two River City.

As the war continued, Bob wanted to join the New Alliance Army but was hesitant to trust the fledgling force due to the discrimination he had suffered. As the New Alliance Army grew bigger, and Bob saw the diversity of races who comprised the force, he eventually consented to the hero's request that he join.

After the Dunan Unification War, Bob returned to the kobold district of Two River where he was said to frequently complain, but nevertheless live a happy life.

recruitment: He's at Two-River's Kobold district. Ask him to join when you have at least 80 Stars of Destiny recruited.

name: Bolgan
description: Bolgan (ボルガン, Borugan) is a character in Suikoden II who also appears in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2. Bolgan is a circus performer who journeys with Rina and Eilie.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Bolgan was a young man with a unique circus act as a fire-breather. He was adopted by a cirus in Zexen when the circus master awoke and found a basket outside with Bolgan, some bread, and the Fire Breath Rune, apparently to keep the child warm.

He used the rune in his act as he grew up but was treated as a servant rather than a fellow performer. Due to this constant bullying and mistreatment, Bolgan decided to run away from the circus. With the help of Eilie and Rina who were working with the circus at the time, he was able to escape and set off on a journey alongside the duo, who treated him as a brother.

Their travels led them to Ryube Village in the City-States of Jowston, where they encountered the hero. To navigate the treacherous North Swallow Pass, Bolgan and his sisters joined forces with the hero temporarily. Afterward, they reunited with the hero in Coronet Town and chose to become members of the New Alliance Army.

Bolgan was granted a position in the hero's Royal Guard. He also developed a keen interest in learning and spent much time studying at the castle library. He was a kind and empathetic soul, friendly to all. He also noticed Eilie's crush on the hero. Knowing they would leave their friends behind at the end of the war, Bolgan tried his hardest to come up with a solution for Eilie's worry, suggesting that the hero could join their troupe.

Following the conclusion of the Dunan Unification War, Bolgan returned to traveling with the circus troupe while continuing his studies. The troupe's journey eventually brought them to Milit Village, where Bolgan played a crucial role in helping Nash Latkje and others fend off a raid orchestrated by the fraudulent Fire Bringer.

trivia: • Bolgan's education can be seen in his weapon upgrades: gluv, Gluv, and Glove.
recruitment: Joins automatically in Coronet after the events in Muse.

name: Bonaparte
description: Bonaparte (ボナパルト, Bonaparuto) is a character in Suikoden II who also appears in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2. Bonaparte is a mysterious creature that Millie keeps as a pet.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Bonaparte is a mysterious creature that Millie kept as a pet during the Dunan Unification War. Millie had caught the creature herself when he was still too young to fight back. According to Millie, Bonaparte was a groundhog. But his appearance, ability to grow enormously in size, and ferocity suggest that he was not an ordinary groundhog, to say the least.

Millie always doted on Bonaparte, oblivious to how the strange creature might be viewed by others. At some point, Bonaparte would run away from Millie, only to be cornered by her and the New Alliance Army. After being subdued in a fight that showed Bonaparte's unusual ferocity, he would return to Millie's care.

At the castle, she delighted in showing Bonaparte the expansive views of Lake Dunan and would write letters for the Suggestion Box about him also. Millie found Bonaparte's every action to be "cute", going so far as to sleep inside his expansive mouth. She said he enjoyed her poking him in the eye, though his reaction suggested anything but.

Following the Dunan Unification War, Millie decided to travel to the Grasslands in order to find a bride for Bonaparte.

trivia: • Before you defeat Bonaparte, Millie cannot use the Groundhog Rune she has permanently equipped to her person.

name: Boris Wizen
description: Boris Wizen (ボリス・ワイゼン, Borisu Waizen) is a character in Suikoden II. He is the son of Ridley Wizen and will join the New Alliance Army should his father perish during the Dunan Unification War.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Boris was the son of Ridley Wizen, Commander of Two River City's kobold armies. His dream was to one day follow in his father's footsteps and succeed him in leading the kobold forces.

While perhaps not quite as gifted as his father and lacking in practical experience, Boris had all the makings of an outstanding soldier. His looks come from his mother's side of the family though his ability to look good in a uniform was surely his father's influence.

Boris was studying in the Nameless Lands when the Dunan Unification War broke out. If the leader of the New Alliance Army attempted to abandon the army in Tinto City, then Ridley would perish searching for his commander as the city was besieged by Neclord's zombie horde. Hearing of his father's death, Boris would travel to join the New Alliance Army in his place in order to fulfil his father's wishes.

Following the war, regardless of his father's fate, Boris would continue to try and follow his father's path by eventually succeeding him as commander of Two River's kobold forces.

name: Bright
description: Bright (ブライト, Buraito) is a supporting character in Suikoden II, Suikoden III and Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2. Bright is the partner of Futch.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Bright was a baby dragon who hatched on Rakutei Mountain during the Dunan Unification War. Futch, who had lost his dragon, Black, in the past agreed to raise him so he would not be destroyed. Still, due to Bright's unique coloring and his extremely small size (dragons were usually several meters long at birth), it was unsure whether Bright was a real dragon.

Following the Dunan Unification War, Futch, Bright and Humphrey traveled towards Crystal Valley in an attempt to ascertain the truth about Bright. When a rogue black dragon attacked Marid Village, Bright tried to confront him. Some sort of resonance occurred between the two, opening a passage to the World of Wings and Scales. This incident seemed to confirm Bright's nature as a dragon.

By the time of the War of the Champions, Bright was Futch's matured and powerful dragon steed. Although large and fearsome in appearance, Bright remained fond of people and a pretty friendly personality. His size and speed made him a much-feared opponent on the battlefield during the war.

He continued to be Futch's mount after the war, loving to soar in the skies. Still, sometimes Futch is put out by his pestering.

recruitment: Joins with Sharon and Futch.

name: Camus
description: Camus (カミュー, Kamyū) is a character in Suikoden II and Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2. He is the cool-headed captain of the Red Knights of the Matilda Knightdom.

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
Handsome and charming, Camus had short brown hair and matching eyes. He held special pride in his sword, Uriah.

As a knight of Matilda, he wore a red coat along with a purple half-cape on his left shoulder. Beneath this, he wore white pants and knee-high black boots, trimmed with red. He also wore white gloves. On the left side of his chest, he wore the emblem of the Matilda Knights. During the Dunan Unification War, he removed this for a time due to abandoning his post to join the New Alliance Army.

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
Camus' fighting skills came to be considered first-rate as leader of the Red Knights, but he was also lauded as a charming, polite young man who knew how to treat women. Camus' behavior was also said to be less naïve than that of other knights and because of his charm, was less stiffly formal than Miklotov. 

Like Miklotov, he was held in high esteem in Rockaxe Castle, in contrast to the less popular Gordeau. In contrast to the more hot-headed Miklotov, Camus kept calm and believed that a cool head was needed in battle. Even when upset, he was unlikely to reveal his emotions.

He has a 1-3 record against Miklotov in training duels, though the two have never fought to their fullest extent and Camus was known to be quick to concede. Though seemingly the opposite of Miklotov in many ways, their shared chivalrous nature and passion for justice forged a firm friendship between them.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Camus is a knight of the Matilda Knightdom. Originally from the Free Knights of Camaro in Grasslands, Camus was born the second son of Hugo, as part of a family that possessed a domain. Growing up, he would become childhood friends with Maki, the daughter of a trader who frequented his family's lands. In Camaro, only eldest sons were eligible to become knights and so Camus moved to Matilda in his youth and took the test to become a knight.

He and Miklotov took the test in the same year and they set a new record for the longest mock fight during their exam. Both were accepted on the same day as knights and Camus rose through the ranks of the knighthood, eventually becoming the leader of the Red Knights of Matilda, and was sometimes referred to as the Red Knight as a result, whereas Miklotov became the leader of the Blue Knights.

Camus and Miklotov became captains of their respective companies at a relatively early age due to a combination of factors. The Matilda Knights meritocracy was a great benefit to them both, as was their being positioned at a key generational change within the order caused in part by Gordeau causing the downfall of more senior leading knights that could rival him.

Camus accompanied Gordeau to the meeting on Jowston Hill prior to the outbreak of the Dunan Unification War, where he charmed Nanami after Gordeau brusquely bumped into her. His Red Knights were not ordered to Muse City's aid during the Highland Army advance but Camus was assigned to bring Miklotov Gordeau's withdrawal command.

When the hero came to Rockaxe Castle in an attempt to convince Gordeau to ally with the New Alliance Army, Camus broke his oath as a knight to join the New Alliance Army in order to fight against the Highland Kingdom, disgusted as he was with Gordeau's…

recruitment: Joins automatically after the Rockaxe events.

name: Chuchara
description: Chuchara (チュカチャラ, Chukachara) is a character in Suikoden II who also appears in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2. It is the kraken spawn of Rulodia and Abizboah.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Chuchara was the spawn of Abizboah and Rulodia, born sometime during the Dunan Unification War. Apparently, it was brought to the New Alliance Army Headquarters by its parents after they both joined the New Alliance Army. It was spotted one day at the castle's docks.

As a newborn kraken, it was considered pretty cute and had a soft "Kororo..." type cry. As a baby, its body was still delicate and so it took to wearing a pot on its head. It was said that baby krakens grew into adults after 3 to 5 years.

It would remain in its castle home with its loving family, living in harmony following the war.

name: Coates
description: Coates (コーツ, Kōtsu) is a minor character in Suikoden II. He is the son of Gordon.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Coates is a trader, like his father, and worked under Gordon at Gordon's Mercentile in Gregminster. Under his father's guidance, he had all the makings of a fine trader with a promising future ahead of him.

When Gordon joined the New Alliance Army, he gave Coates control of the Gregminster trading post. Managing the enterprise with great success, Coates continued to run the store as Gordon did not return to retake control after the Dunan Unification War.

name: Connell
description: Connell (コーネル, Kōneru) is a supporting character in Suikoden II who also appears in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.1. Connell is a acoustician aiming to follow in his father's footsteps.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Connell was raised in the intricacies of phonology from an early age by his father and mother, both revered practitioners in the field. He was a very kind-hearted young man, which was reflected in his innocent and charming appearance.

Driven by the ambition to follow in his father's footsteps, he embarked on a journey to the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia. There, in Crystal Valley, Connell immersed himself in the study of phonology. It was within the domain of phonology that Connell would discover his unique ability to manipulate emotions through the artful use of sound. Though still a student, he was highly skilled in the use of the Sound Rune as he had also studied runes in Harmonia.

When the Dunan Unification War erupted, Connell felt the need to return to the Forest Village, where his younger sister resided. Fate would place him in the heart of Greenhill City during its siege by the Highland Army. In an act of great cunning, Connell utilized his Sound Rune to stun the rebelling soldiers of Muse City, enabling the escape of the captive Teresa Wisemail through a cacophony of sound.

Later, after finally returning to the Forest Village, Connell's path intertwined with the New Alliance Army. Moved by the resounding call for peace he could hear emanating from the clear heart of its leader he joined the ranks of the New Alliance Army.

Connell was eager for an opportunity to listen to the heartfelt echoes of people and also to help end the war as son as possible for his sister's sake. Throughout the course of the conflict, his phonological talents proved instrumental in inspiring and calming the hearts of soldiers.

Following the conclusion of the Dunan Unification War, Connell briefly reunited with his sister in the Forest Village before embarking on a pilgrimage back to…

recruitment: After Castle is Level 3, speak to him in Forest Village while having one or more sound sets in your inventory.

name: Culgan
description: Culgan (クルガン, Kurugan) is a supporting antagonist in Suikoden II who also appears in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.1. He is a general of the Highland Army who shifted his loyalties from Luca Blight to Jowy Atreides during the Dunan Unification War.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Culgan was a skilled officer within the Highland Army, known for his solid judgment and intelligence. While he exhibited a certain amount of ruthlessness on the field of battle, deep inside, he held a strong sense of justice and unwavering dedication to causes he believed in.

In contrast to his more outgoing colleague Seed, Culgan was reserved in demeanor but demonstrated remarkable proficiency in swordsmanship though it was said Seed had an edge on him. Like Seed, he was not from a prestigious military family and had worked his way up the ranks from a common soldier and this shared experience helped them bond. Notably, he possessed a Thunder Rune, granting him the ability to wield powerful lightning magic.

During the Dunan Unification War, Culgan received orders from Luca Blight regarding a request from Zaj Quiroz of the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia to track down Nash Latkje in Greenhill Forest. Together with Seed, Culgan successfully apprehended Nash, but during the encounter, Seed was poisoned by Nash's Twin Snake Sword Großer Fluss. Culgan, seeking the antidote for Seed, made a deal with Nash, allowing him to escape in exchange for the life-saving remedy, despite Luca's orders.

As the Dunan Unification War progressed, Culgan grew increasingly convinced that Luca Blight's continued presence as ruler of the Highland Kingdom would bring about catastrophe. As someone who benefited from the Highland Army's extreme meritocratic ethos himself, he held no jealousy or envy over Jowy Atreides's rapid rise to prominence. As such, he secretly supported Jowy in his plot against Luca. After Jowy's ascension to the throne, Culgan collaborated with Jowy in the establishment of a new Highland.

Culgan's cool-headed ruthlessness on the battlefield was shown during the Battle of Muse…

name: Daisy
description: Daisy (ディジー, Deijī) is a character from Suikoden II. She was a young woman from the village of North Window, targeted by Neclord.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Daisy was a childhood friend of Viktor who dreamed of being with him, rejecting two other suitors in the past in favor of him. Her parents had passed away when she was young, so she had lived with Viktor's family for a long time.

Often seen wearing her blue apron dress, she was described as a nice, unassuming girl, but not necessarily beautiful. She was very popular among the villagers of North Window, young and old.

In 449, she would be targeted by Neclord as his 65th bride. This was the reason the town of North Window was attacked. Viktor would rush to rescue her but was easily defeated. Thinking Viktor dead, Daisy threw herself on his sword in despair, piercing her throat, and taking her own life.

Once Viktor recovered, thanks to a Sacrificial Jizo he had been carrying at her request, he would make graves for Daisy and all the other villagers of North Window. He would carefully bury Daisy under a large tree, unburned in the destruction of the village, her tombstone adorned by a pendant Viktor had bought for her.

Years later, during the Dunan Unification War, Viktor encountered Neclord again in the ruins of the village. Neclord would conjure up a fake Daisy in an attempt to rattle Viktor, but he lopped its head off with less hesitation, revealing it to be an empty copy of the girl he knew.

name: Darell
description: Darell (ダレル, Dareru) is a minor character who is mentioned in Suikoden II. He is the father of Anabelle and former mayor of Muse City.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Darell was the father of Anabelle and the predecessor of his daughter as mayor of Muse City and was raised in Muse, the center of the City-States of Jowston. A young Luca Blight would describe him as a well-dressed, rotund middle-aged man with a mean look in his narrow eyes, similar to Gordeau.

Despite his fine upbringing, as mayor he was not able to prevent the invasion of the country by the Highland Kingdom in 428. Muse would fall and Darell would be forced into exile in South Window City.

Although this disastrous beginning to the war was reversed by Genkaku's Great Offensive, Darell began to be overtaken by envy. He also disagreed with Genkaku repatriating the Winged Horde to Two River City, feeling it damaged the war effort, and soon began plotting against him.

He coated Genkaku's sword with poison before his duel with Han Cunningham, forcing him to forfeit the bout, and Kyaro Town, to Highland. Darell subsequently had Genkaku exiled.

There also theories that sometime after the ceasefire with Highland, Darell had the caravan of Agares Blight attacked after he visited Muse City. This was likely to prevent a formal peace agreement between the two powers. Whatever the truth was, it is clear that Darell was skilled at underhanded plotting and concocting schemes behind one's back.

Afterwards, Darell's reign as mayor continued until his passing. He would be remembered for his excellent political skills in addition to his cunning.

name: Eilie
description: Eilie (アイリ, Airi) is a character in Suikoden II who also appears in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2. She is part of a circus group along with Rina and Bolgan.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Eilie is a part of a traveling group of circus performers along with Rina and Bolgan. When she was a child, her parents were killed by thieves, leaving her sister, Rina as her only family. The two had traveled ever since, leaving the Grasslands behind.

Eilie performed as a knife thrower, having perfected the basics she had learned from her parents, and was said to possess considerable skill with them, both as a performer and a fighter. The group would perform as part of a circus for a time, which was where they first met Bolgan.

Eilie and her traveling group first encountered Genkaku's grandson while performing in Ryube Village when Eilie selected the hero as part of her act. After the performance, Eilie, Rina, and Bolgan travelled with him and Jowy to the Highland Kingdom.

However, business proved poor in Highland and the trio quickly moved back to the City-States of Jowston. They would end up stranded in Coronet Town following the fall of Muse City. The troupe would meet the hero's group again while waiting for an opportunity to travel to South Window Principality.

Here Rina would first reveal Eilie's crush on the hero, pointedly asking if Nanami was his girlfriend, then exclaiming her delight for Eilie when their sibling relationship was revealed, much to her flustered irritation.

After being reunited, Eilie followed him to South Window City, becoming part of the New Alliance Army. Eilie expressed great concern during the Dunan Unification War and did her best to cheer him on. Her feelings for the New Alliance Army leader continued to deepen but she was unable to express her feelings, partly due to her sister complex.

Having known the hero since his travels with Jowy and Nanami, Eilie was all too aware of the impact the war and the loss of his loved ones was…

recruitment: Joins automatically in Coronet after the events in Muse.

name: Ellie
description: Ellie (エリ, Eri) is a minor character who appears in Suikoden II. She is the older sister of Ko.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Ellie is the daughter of the innkeepers in Banner Village. She was a cute young woman with freckles and red hair styled in pigtails. Even as a young girl, she worked hard to help the success of the inn despite its low business, welcoming weary travelers with a cheerful smile.

During the latter stages of the Dunan Unification War, her brother Ko was kidnapped by bandits. He was quickly rescued by the leader of the New Alliance Army, who had happened to be in the area, with the aid of Gremio and the hero of the Gate Rune War, who was resting in the village at the time.

trivia: • If you load save data into Suikoden II from Suikoden where Gremio was not revived, then Ellie will take his place. This is the reason why she has portrait art, so she can be shown in the player's convoy if needed.

name: Elza
