description: Elza (エルザ, Eruza) is a character in Suikoden II and Genso Suikogaiden Vol.1. She is a skilled gunner evading pursuit from the Howling Voice Guild.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Training in the Howling Voice Guild
Born in Sajah Village, Elza's parents sold her to the Howling Voice Guild, an organization based in the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia. She held no real memories of her parents or her time in Sajah, but held onto what little remained in her memory as a comfort.

Despite being an outsider to the Tower-born children, she forged strong bonds with her fellow apprentices, Kelley and Clive. Their collective ambition was to attain the esteemed Knight-class gunner status, a feat that had eluded many for years.

As the years passed, Elza would grow into a tall, slender woman. Her blonde hair and blue eyes often attracted attention, especially outside of Harmonia. She felt that her being an outsider meant she was not as obsessed with the Guild as Kelley and Clive, having known an life outside its walls.

In addition to her friendship with Clive and Kelley, Elza developed a particular camaraderie with Nash Latkje. Although societal class distinctions separated them, their shared age and experiences in the guild fostered a healthy rivalry. Nash acknowledged his inability to surpass Elza's skills, yet she held him in high regard.

However, suspicions arose among the Guild's elders concerning the trio's close relationship, especially due to their apprenticeship under Sauro, one of the senior members. Apprehensive about a potential power imbalance, the elders appointed Kelley, the most easily influenced of the three, as the new Guildmaster, thereby isolating him from his companions.

In a fateful turn of events, Elza faced a duel against Kelley, likely orchestrated by certain elders. The duel demanded mastery over Storm, the guild master's weapon known to have a will of its own, choosing its worthy wielder. Unlike Kelley, Elza demonstrated effortless…

name: Emilia
description: Emilia (エミリア, Emiria) is a character in Suikoden II and Genso Suikogaiden Vol.1. She is the librarian for the New Alliance Army.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Emilia is a bright and beautiful young woman who worked as a clerk and librarian for the New Leaf Academy. She was mostly a librarian, with her clerical work being an additional duty due to a lack of trained clerks at the school. Her serious and conscientious nature lent itself well to paperwork, so her job performance was always excellent.

Her beauty also made her popular amongst the people at the academy. Although she projected a serious side as part of her work, she also liked to relax by reading romance books. She apparently had little practical experience in the subject matter.

During the Dunan Unification War, she cooperated with Teresa Wisemail and Nash Latkje in resisting the Highland Army attack. She also played an active role in helping Teresa escape capture by the rebelling Muse City soldiers by disgusing herself as the mayor.

Some time after the occupation of Greenhill, Emilia encountered the leader of the New Alliance Army while he was attempting to gain information about Teresa Wisemail. After confiding his mission to her, Emilia helped the New Alliance Army, becoming a member herself after Teresa escaped from Greenhill City.

Emilia opened a library at the New Alliance Army Headquarters. She was surprised by the soldiers' initial lack of interest in books but became happy when more people began reading. After the war ended, Emilia returned to New Leaf Academy, where she became the headmaster and taught at the school.

trivia:
• Emilia reads tons of romance novels.
• What would become Emilia's design was initially submitted for Anabelle before being repurposed.
recruitment: Speak to her after the tour she gives and tell her what you're really up to. She will join after the events in Greenhill if you do this.

name: Feather
description: Feather (フェザー, Fezā) is a character in Suikoden II. Feather is a Griffin prince who joined the New Alliance Army.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Feather is a Griffin who came to Forest Village. He was originally from the Windswept Valley where he reigned as a prince but was apparently exiled in a coup. His round collar was said to be proof of his royal lineage.

Feather found himself under attack in Forest Village, first from villagers who believed he was a threat. Ayda and the hero defended Feather against both humans and other griffins, protecting Feather from harm.

Feather was able to communicate his gratitude through Badeaux's Listening Orb and joined the hero and Ayda in the New Alliance Army to repay his debt. He was a very proud griffin who set himself apart from his more ferocious brethren. He was also highly intelligent, being able to understand the human language to at least some extent.

Following the war and unable to return to his home, Feather remained in the New Alliance Army Headquarters, guarding the castle from harm.

trivia:
• If the player loses the battle against the wild griffons during Feather's recruitment, the game will continue normally, but Feather has been killed and cannot be recruited anymore. Ayda, however, can still be recruited. This allows the player to recruit all 108 Stars with Abizboah and Siegfried.
• In the Italian HDR translation, his name got translated as "Piuma".
recruitment: Feather: After recruiting Badeaux, go to Forest Village and defeat the Griffons, then agree to spare Feather. Sigfried: After recruiting Badeaux, as well as completing the sidequest for Hix/Tengaar, go to the same place you found him in Unicorn Forest with a young female in the party (Mikumiku counts). If you bring Sierra, there will be a cutscene, but you won't be able to recruit him.

name: Freed Yamamoto
description: Freed Yamamoto (フリード・ヤマモト, Furīdo Yamamoto), usually referred to as Freed Y (フリード・Ｙ, Furīdo Y) in-game, is a character in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Freed Yamamoto is a South Window soldier with strong loyalties to his home principality. Freed served as an aide to the mayor of South Window City, Granmeyer, who he held in high esteem. Freed was introduced to his wife, Yoshino, through Granmeyer. 

When South Window and Tinto forces occupied the Senan region of the then Scarlet Moon Empire, Freed participated in the attack. He would hold a bias against the Empire, which transferred itself to the Toran Republic following the Gate Rune War.

During the Dunan Unification War, Freed Y accompanied the hero and Viktor on a mission at North Window to investigate the disappearance of young women in the area. As Freed attempted to return to South Window to report on their findings, he discovered that South Window had been taken by the Highland Kingdom.

When Freed became a member of the New Alliance Army, his ties to South Window proved useful as Shu ordered him to infiltrate the South Window soldiers, recently conscripted by Highland, in order to spread rumors of their imminent execution and pro-New Alliance Army propaganda. Freed succeeded in his mission, turning the former State forces to the New Alliance side and increasing the then-small army's recruits.

When the New Alliance Army decided to ask the Toran Republic for military aid during the war, Freed was an outspoken critic of the decision. Still, Freed also agreed to lead the New Alliance party to the Toran Republic and even personally pleaded the case for an alliance to President Lepant, being convinced to prioritize the safety of his homeland over his own biases. 

After the Dunan Unification War, Freed was offered a position in the Dunan Republic, to become the mayor of South Window City, but refused, choosing instead to live happily in Radat Town, as he had…

recruitment: Joins automatically when going to investigate disappearances of woman in North-Window.

name: Fu Tan Chen
description: Fu Tan Chen (フー・タンチェン, Fū Tanchen) is a character from Suikoden II. He is the vibrant host of the cook-off contests.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Fu Tan Chen was the host of the cook-offs which took place between Hai Yo and his various challengers at the New Alliance Army Headquarters. Speaking through a megaphone, he would liven up the crowd with his energetic introductions but judges would sometimes take umbrage to some of his comments.

He apparently had the uncanny ability to instantly appear, complete with music, wherever a cook-off was taking place. When Hai Yo and Yen Qing dueled on Black Dragon Island, Fu Tan Chen suddenly appeared to announce the contest and vanished just as quickly when the cook-off was decided, much to both chef's confusion.

name: Gabocha
description: Gabocha (ガボチャ, Gabocha) is a character in Suikoden II. Gabocha is a fan of Gengen who fought for the New Alliance Army.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Bright, energetic and with a friendly personality, Gabocha was very popular in Two River City. His bright and friendly personality made him well liked by everyone. During the Dunan Unification War, he was a huge fan of Gengen and considered him his hero.

He secretly joined the New Alliance Army behind Gengen's back such was his desire to emulate his hero and see him in action with his own eyes. Though Gengen tried to refuse Gabocha, as he did want to put a child in such danger, Gabocha joined regardless. He also enjoyed telling others about Gengen's feats, including his ability to stay underwater for 15 minutes, and various monsters he had defeated.

Due to his youth, Gabocha functioned as something of a mascot to those who lived at the New Alliance Army Headquarters, although he was quite capable of fighting in battle as a soldier and fought as an infantry soldier during the war. Although inexperienced, his bravery and will to be like Gengen kept him fighting.

Following the war, his efforts were recognized and Gabocha became Gengen's first lieutenant, where he was said to perform admirably.

recruitment: He's located in a house in Two-River (Kobold side). Enter this house while Gengen is in the party. Even though Gengen says no, he will follow you and join.

name: Gadget
description: Gadget (からくり丸, Karakurimaru) is a character in Suikoden II and Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2. He is a machine built by Juppo to assist Meg.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Gadget, a clockwork doll crafted by Juppo, served as a companion to Meg on her ventures and was entrusted to her as his master. An exceptional creation, Gadget was a product of advanced trickster techniques, possessing the ability to interact with people, act independently, and even learn. When initially constructed, Juppo likened him to a newborn, requiring fundamental concepts like handshakes to be explained.

Although incapable of feeling pain, Gadget demonstrated emotions. For instance, when Meg affectionately knocked on his head during their first encounter, Gadget did not experience pain but felt apprehension at being "struck" until the meaning of the gesture was clarified.

Among his diverse abilities were a transformation protocol that reached its true potential during the Dunan Unification War and a thermal detection capability. However, it should be noted that Gadget was not waterproof.

The intricacies of Gadget's internal mechanisms remain largely obscure, except for his utilization of a mainspring (or wind-up) power source. Bamboo was chosen for his joints due to its flexibility, and his sturdy construction allowed him to support Meg's weight, occasionally being used as her mount.

Originally assigned by Juppo to accompany Meg back to Lenankamp, Gadget eventually acknowledged his responsibility as not merely bringing her home, but also safeguarding her wherever she ventured. Consequently, he agreed to accompany her in her quest to locate her uncle who had headed towards Crystal Valley.

During their journey from Greenhill to Muse, Meg and Gadget were stopped at the border checkpoint. Meg would try and order Gadget to fight his way through, but Gadget refused, his ego preventing him from being used as a tool for fighting.

Stuck at the checkpoint, the two…

trivia: • In the Italian HDR translation, his name got translated as "Aggeggio".
recruitment: Joins with Meg.

name: Gantetsu
description: Gantetsu (ガンテツ, Gantetsu) is a character in Suikoden II. He is a priest with violent tendencies.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Gantetsu, once a neophyte of Lorimar's Qlon Temple, was known for his frequent boasts of physical strength, earning a reputation as something of a roughneck. However, his violent tendencies and penchant for indulging in sake ultimately led to his excommunication from the temple by Fukien.

It was evident that his faith lacked depth, as he prioritized training his body over nurturing his spiritual growth. Some speculated that he had only become a monk due to having no other options. The prominent knot on his forehead came from the repeated headbutting he did during his physical training.

After his expulsion, Gantetsu found himself aimlessly brooding in South Window City with no clear purpose. However, fate took an unexpected turn when he encountered and suffered defeat at the hands of the leader of the New Alliance Army and his comrades in a test of strength. This humbling experience shocked Gantetsu, leading him to acknowledge the New Alliance Army's prowess and ultimately convincing him to join their ranks, eager to have a reason to wield his strength.

Following the conclusion of the Dunan Unification War, Gantetsu returned to the sacred grounds of Qlon Temple, where, through his service during the war, he had earned the reinstatement of his status within the temple. Though unlikely to ever become an outstanding monk, Gantetsu began his training anew, embracing his newfound opportunity, perhaps having learned something from his wartime experiences.

trivia: • When entering or exiting the party, Gantetsu will say, "Namyo Ho Renge Kyo...", a Buddhist chant.
recruitment: Speak to him after defeating Neclord, with a party that has an overall strength of 400 in order to beat him at a pushing contest and he will join.

name: Gengen
description: Gengen (ゲンゲン, Gengen) is a character in Suikoden II. Gengen is a kobold warrior who fought in the Dunan Unification War.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Gengen is a soldier from Kobold Village. His stated goal in life was to become a "great warrior". He began as a mercenary in a group based near his village before joining Viktor's mercenaries

Although he still had quite a way to go as a warrior, his inherent brightness was found to be calming and he became something of a familiar mascot for the Mercenary Fortress. He was especially popular with children, such as Gabocha and Tuta. He seemed to like the well at the fortress and could often be found peering into it.

Following the defeat of Viktor's mercenaries, Gengen, along with other survivors of the attack, became a member of the New Alliance Army, where he served as an Infantry Captain during the Dunan Unification War. As a captain, he was not a very stern commander, but his passion to encourage and protect everyone under his charged shined through.

Although he spoke simply, many kobolds found his work ethic inspirational and worked hard to follow in his footsteps, not just Gabocha. He wielded an ordinary sword that he was nonetheless proud of, as reflected in the names he gave it.

Following the war, Gengen remained a soldier, becoming a captain of the Dunan Republican Army, coming closer to his goal of being a truly great warrior.

trivia:
• The position of Gengen's tail and ears give away his emotions. When he is happy, his tail is standing up. When Gengen is sad, his tail is down. When Gengen spots something delicious, his ears stand straight up.
• Gengen is a hero in his native village; not only does the Chief of Kobold Village thank you for taking good care of their hero Gengen, but he also has a rival.
• Gengen can stay underwater for up to 15 minutes.
recruitment: Automatically joins after returning from Kyaro.

name: Genkaku
description: Genkaku (ゲンカク, Genkaku) is a minor character in Suikoden II. Genkaku is a war hero of the City-States of Jowston who was exiled after refusing to duel his friend, Han Cunningham.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Genkaku was the adoptive father of the hero and Nanami and was once the bearer of the Bright Shield Rune.

Genkaku was a renowned war hero during the conflict between the Highland Army and the City-States of Jowston in the year 428. He gained prominence within the State Army after leading a successful raid on a Highland supply tent, providing crucial time for the City-State to recover, beginning the Great Offensive.

Instrumental in aiding the Winged Horde's migration from Tinto Principality to Two River City after their lands were seized for strip-mining, Genkaku earned the appreciation of the Winged Horde. However, his growing fame and the mayor of Muse City, Darell's jealousy led to strained relations between them.

During the war, Genkaku found himself facing his hometown friend, Han Cunningham, who served as an enemy commander. Despite the conflict, their friendship remained intact, and they even shared drinks together between their respective camps. Both men realized the senselessness of the war and hatched a plan for a peace treaty that garnered wide approval, except from Darell.

To settle the conflict, a duel was proposed between champions from each side. Genkaku and Han were chosen as champions, but during the duel, Genkaku refused to fight his friend. This decision was initially met with anger, but it was later revealed that Darell had poisoned Genkaku's sword, intending to frame him for Han's murder if he had won. Genkaku was exiled, bearing the false label of a traitor.

After leaving the City-States of Jowston, Genkaku settled in the Highland Kingdom, where he established a dojo. There, he adopted two orphans, the hero and Nanami, and they formed a close-knit family. He trained both of them and was also a mentor to Jowy Atreides. He passed away peacefully…

name: Genshu
description: Genshu (ゲンシュウ, Genshuu) is a character in Suikoden II. Genshu is a wandering swordsman who lent his strength to the New Alliance Army.

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
Genshu is slender, almost delicate in build, his frame draped in a purple kosode with diamond patterns along the edges. The sleeves of the kosode are wide and flowing. He wears this over a white robe and loose-fitting hakama, which are loose-fitting.

His outfit is completed with a black sash tied around his waist, securing his katana as well as a wakizashi. On his feet, he wears simple straw sandals.

His face is pale and almost gaunt, but his sharp, disciplined features leave no doubt as to his intensity and skill. He has long, silver hair tied back into a high ponytail.

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
Genshu is a swordsman and master of Iaido, a martial art centered on the swift and precise drawing of the sword. Known for his unparalleled skill and stoic demeanor, Genshu’s adult life was marked by a profound internal struggle between his identity as a killer and his search for redemption. His journey took him from the depths of guilt and self-loathing to a renewed commitment to the sword as a path of honor and protection.

He considered himself someone who ceased to be human when fighting, a dark desire for obtaining strength and mastery compelling him to fight to the death any swordsman he found worthy. This feeling took on nearly addictive properties. When facing off against Liu Shao, he did not even remember the ultimate duel, waking up in a haze of remorse.

Genshu’s philosophy of the sword evolved significantly over his lifetime. Initially driven by a desire for strength and weighed down by his own self-loathing, he eventually embraced the philosophies of Liu Shao and Tesshu, seeing the sword as a tool for life rather than death. In Ling Hua Town, his knowledge, aura, and skill led him to being called Sensei by the town's citizens, who both respected and feared the foreigner.

By the time of the Dunan Unification War, Genshu had become a quite, retrospective man who who meditated often.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Foolish Sibling
Genshu was born in a foreign land, where he trained rigorously in the art of Iaido under a harsh and demanding master. His early years were defined by an obsessive pursuit of strength, driven by a desire to surpass his younger brother, Tesshu, a natural prodigy.

Though Tesshu looked up to and adored his older brother, Genshu's self-loathing would eventually drive him to leave. He would continue to develop his skills by fighting in life-and-death duels against many warriors he came across.

With rumors of Genshu now spreading, Tesshu would pick up his sword in the hopes to stopping his brother. Genshu would defeat a masked Tesshu in a duel and would only discover what he had done when his brother revealed himself.

Listening to his brother's story, his death would haunt Genshu for the rest of his life. Teshuu’s final words—“For what purpose do you wield your sword?”—became a recurring question that Genshu struggled to answer.
Killer
After Teshuu's death, a despondent and self-loathing Genshu left his homeland and became a killer-for-hire, taking on contracts to kill for money. During this period, he was hired through an intermediary to kill the legendary swordsman Liu Shao, though he was unaware of the client’s identity at the time.

The duel with Liu Shao left a lasting impression on Genshu, as Liu Shao’s noble philosophy of the sword as a tool for protection and life deeply contrasted with Genshu’s own path of destruction. Some time after this, he would reside in Ling Hua Town where he presented himself as a sword expert, though his true calling was still in assassinations.

Two years after his duel with Liu Shao, Genshu crossed paths with Liu Mei, Liu Shao’s daughter, who sought revenge for her father’s death. Initially reluctant to engage with her,…

trivia: • Genshu comes from the same "certain country" as Hazuki.
recruitment: Ride the boat from Kuskus to Coronet, and speak to him. At least one person in your active party must have a weapon with a Level 14 rating.

name: Georg Prime
description: Georg Prime (ゲオルグ・プライム, Georugu Puraimu) is a central character of Suikoden V who first appeared in Suikoden II and Genso Suikogaiden Vol.1.

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
In his younger days, Georg cut a dashing figure with his dark hair and amber eyes. During his time in the Scarlet Moon Empire, his hair was unkempt but Suikoden V onward, his hair is cut shorter and kept much more neatly.

As a Queen's Knight, George wears a custom circlet with cloth covering his left eye. Prior to that, as one of the Scarlet Moon Empire's Great Generals, he wore a more standard eyepatch. Despite this, he had use of both his eyes, as shown plainly in Suikoden II.

In Suikoden V, his sword has Falenan designs on the scabbard and handle, making it appear like a normal, albeit large, sword, but it is a broadsword. He wore a variant of the Queen's Knights uniform; black clothing with gold trim worn over white underclothing. He also wore red eyeliner as part of his Queen's Knights regalia.

Following his escape from the Sun Palace, Georg wears a more familiar outfit that he would continue wearing into Suikoden II; a billowing brown cloak over dark underclothes.

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
George Prime was a fierce swordsman who made a name for himself all over the world. He was known as "Deathblow Georg", or "George Who Needs No Second Strike", due to his battlefield prowess. He was also known for his sincerity and alacrity, as well as his trustworthiness. He was a close friend of Ferid for many years.

Though he wore an eyepatch for a time to remind him to not become complacent on the battlefield, he never truly moved as if he were compensating for a lack of eyesight, something that would catch Oboro's attention during the Sun Rune War.

Despite his personal power and skill, he had no desire to seize any kind of political power or leverage over others. He had little interest in titles or accolades. He much preferred to live his life as a free spirit who traveled as the wind blows, often abandoning important roles on a whim.

That said, he was willing to take on personal responsibility when a situation called for it. He agreed to become a member of the Queen's Knights in Falena at the behest of his friend, Ferid. Georg would claim this was due to his debt of gratitude but in all likelihood, he would have helped Ferid as a friend, debt or no debt. His involvement in the Queen's Assassination was also a pivotal moment of him putting duty and loyalty over his own whims.

Over the course of the Sun Rune War, he would become protective of and close to the Prince of Falena, even inviting him to travel together after the war. He would ask the Prince if he harbored a grudge over Georg's killing of his mother but said it without guilt or remorse. He had a fiery friendship with Sialeeds and her betrayal and subsequent demise hit him hard.

George had something of a sweet tooth, especially for cheesecake.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Early life
Born in the year 420, his birthplace unknown, the earliest records of Georg Prime place him fighting alongside respected friend, Ferid, as a mercenary prior to the year 446.

thumb|left|220px|Sadly, Georg Prime was not granted a fancy hat to cover his mess of hair during the War of SuccessionDuring a particularly fierce battle, Georg made the error of lowering his guard on his left side, allowing an enemy soldier to inflict damage to his eye. Although Georg's life, as well as his eyesight, was saved by Ferid's quick intervention, following the incident, Georg took to wearing an eye patch over his left eye, to remind himself of the price of overconfidence in battle.

Records of Georg Prime's movements following this battle are non-existent up until the year 446, where the warrior, already gathering fame for his abilities, aligned himself with Barbarossa Rugner in the Scarlet Moon Empire's War of Succession. Georg's ability in battle led him to be titled as one of the nation's Great Generals. Although Prime left his position, and Scarlet Moon, following the war, his promise to Barbarossa fulfilled, his fame still lingered in the area.

From here, records are patchy, although it is known that between the years 446 and 449, Georg traveled to the Grasslands, where his skill with a blade earned him another title, Knight of the Ebony Moon.
Queen's Knight
By the year 449, Georg Prime resurfaced again - this time in the Queendom of Falena. He became one of the nation's elite Queen's Knights, under the invitation of his old friend and Queen's Knight Commander, Ferid. By this time, Georg's skills were refined to the point that it was believed that no Queen's Knight, with the possible exception of Ferid, could contest with Prime in a battle without the use of…

trivia:
• You can see Georg's picture as one of the original Six Great Generals in the NTSC-U Suikoden Instruction Manual.
• Georg lives up to his nickname "Deathblow Georg" in Suikoden V by having a randomly occurring instant death strike. This is when his regular attack glows red and there is an "electrical" sound. It is not controlled or activated by a rune of any kind. Sometimes (and during boss battles) the attack simply does about 4x damage.
recruitment: Joins automatically after the events in Gordius.

name: Gijimu
description: Gijimu (ギジム, Gijimu) is a character in Suikoden II who also appears in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2. He is the leader of the Lampdragon Bandits.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Gijimu was the esteemed leader of the Lampdragon Bandits, a renowned group of bandits also led by Koyu and Lo Wen, who were his oath siblings, and the trio was rarely seen apart.

Gijimu was recognized for his aptitude as a bandit, characterized by his assertive and boisterous personality. Under his guidance, the Lampdragon Bandits earned a reputation as a formidable force. However, contrary to their infamous status, they primarily targeted money-driven traders during their exploits and shared their gains in turn. Despite the relentless efforts of Tinto City's forces to suppress the group, they proved largely unsuccessful in their endeavors.

During the Dunan Unification War, the Lampdragon Bandits' stronghold was assailed by Neclord's zombies. Employing a resourceful strategy, Gijimu's group managed to evade capture by igniting the barricades and sabotaging roads and bridges as they made their escape. Though initially reluctant, Gijimu chose to ally with Tinto Mayor Gustav Pendragon to confront Neclord, placing the lives of his comrades above his own ego.

Following the defeat of Neclord, Gijimu, alongside his oath siblings, joined the New Alliance Army, impressed by their leader's efforts against Neclord. In his role as an infantry captain, he skillfully wielded dual axes, leading the New Alliance Army to numerous victories on the battlefield.

After the conclusion of the Dunan Unification War, Gijimu assumed the position of Captain in the Tinto City forces and participated in battles between Tinto and the Grasslands.

Upon Gustav's assignment, Gijimu journeyed to Milit Village to investigate the rumored Fire Bringer presence. There, he collaborated with both Nash Latkje and his former bandit comrades to eliminate the bandits plaguing the small settlement.

recruitment: Joins automatically after the Tinto/Neclord events.

name: Gilbert
description: Gilbert (ギルバート, Girubāto) is a character in Suikoden II who also appears in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2. He is a Zexen native who is also the leader of a mercenary band.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Gilbert was a proud and skilled mercenary who earned his livelihood through his exceptional abilities. Originally serving as a mercenary leader within the Zexen Federation, he chose to leave his family behind and venture to the Dunan region in search of employment opportunities when the fighting died down in Zexen.

Having previously been acquainted with Flik and Viktor, Gilbert had crossed paths with them when he was hired by Muse City on a prior occasion, just over prior to the Dunan Unification War. Later, while in the Highland Kingdom, he found himself hired by the Highland Army.

During the Dunan Unification War, Gilbert participated in the initial assault on Muse City as part of the Highland Army. However, he had a change of heart midway through the battle when Flik and Viktor convinced him to switch sides. Subsequently, Gilbert continued to serve the New Alliance Army as an infantry captain throughout the course of the Dunan Unification War. It is worth noting that he reportedly returned the money paid by the Highland Army for his services.

Despite the questionable action of betraying a contract as a mercenary, Gilbert found it morally unacceptable to follow a leader like Luca Blight, who displayed a disregard for innocent lives. This was particularly troubling to Gilbert as he had left his wife, Mary, and son, Thomas, behind while pursuing employment opportunities in the first place.

After the war, having grown weary of conflict, Gilbert chose to return to his family in Zexen, finding solace and peace of mind in their presence. On his journey back, while stopping in Milit Village, he teamed up with Nash Latkje and his former comrades from the New Alliance Army to confront a group of thieves falsely claiming to be the Fire Bringer.

recruitment: During the first battle for Muse city, damage his unit. *This character is missable and can only be recruited during this battle!

name: Gin
description: Gin (ギン, Gin) is a minor character in Suikoden II. He is a worker in Radat Town.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Gin was in charge of managing the sluice gates in Radat Town early in the Dunan Unification War. He also seemed to serve as a gatekeeper for the sluice gate bridge.

He had a surly reputation and turned down the request of Apple and the hero to shut the sluice gates for them. He seemed to have great trust in Amada, however, and agreed to shut the gates on the sailor's request.

name: Goetsu
description: Goetsu (ゴエツ, Goetsu) is a minor character who appears in Suikoden II. He is a chef who participated in a cook-off.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Goetsu was the second cook to challenge Hai Yo at his restaurant in the New Alliance Army Headquarters. He was a friendly chef with no affiliation with the Black Dragon Cooking Clan and wished to challenge himself in order to master the way of taste.

After his defeat to Hai Yo in a cook-off, Goetsu passed his Buttered Clams recipe to Hai Yo.

name: Gordon (Suikoden II)
description: Gordon (ゴードン, Gōdon) is a character who appears in Suikoden II. He is a trader and the proprietor of Gordon's Mercentile.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Gordon was the sole founder of Gordon's Mercentile, his own trading company run out of Gregminster. Gordon became a wealthy trader within a generation, having experienced many ups and downs, but his true goal was to create "Gordon Group", a chain of trading concerns throughout the land.

When he met the leader of the New Alliance Army during the Dunan Unification War, Gordon realized the potential for expanding his business. As such, he agreed to set up shop within the New Alliance Army on one condition; that its leader prove himself a capable trader.

When the task was complete, Gordon gladly bequeathed his store to his son, Coates, expecting him to maintain Gordon's Mercantile's high standards and success, and moved his business to Jowston.

Following the war, Gordon would remain in the new Dunan Republic, traveling around the nation to expand his business.

recruitment: Speak to Gordon in Iksay with a full party of males, as Chris with any party OR with Augustine in the party.

name: Gorudo
description: Gordeau (ゴルドー, Gorudō) is a supporting antagonist in Suikoden II. He is the leader of the Matilda Knights, whose efforts to consolidate his own power led to his downfall and eventual death.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Gordeau was the leader of the Matilda Knights during the Dunan Unification War. A man driven primarily by self-preservation and the protection of his lands, Gordeau was known for his selfish and arrogant demeanor.

Lamentably, he lacked a sense of justice, often clashing with his subordinate Miklotov due to their differing values, seeking absolute loyalty rather than fostering respect or exemplary leadership among his knights. His uncooperative nature also extended to other members of the City-States of Jowston.

Throughout the war, Gordeau remained skeptical of the Highland Army's intentions to invade Muse Principality, displaying reluctance in committing troops at the crucial Hilltop Conference held in Muse City. He was also insulting and disparaging to his fellow leaders, most notably Greenhill City's Teresa Wisemail.

Ultimately, he dispatched Camus and Miklotov to defend the city, but then swiftly ordered them both to retreat. Following this incident, Gordeau opted for a neutral stance, displaying a cold reception to the leader of the New Alliance Army when presented with the idea of forming an alliance.

However, Gordeau's decisions took a darker turn when he callously refused to provide aid to Muse refugees seeking refuge in Matilda, resulting in their capture and tragic sacrifice in Muse City. This callous act led to the denouncement of his leadership by Camus, Miklotov, and several other knights who renounced their oaths and departed from his service.

Consequently, Gordeau's military power dwindled, and he willingly submitted to the Highland Kingdom, accepting a puppet ruler status in order to maintain his position. That said, he had been in contact with Highland since at least the fall of Muse, indicating a much longer-lasting connection between the two…

trivia: • Gordeau's name was localized as Gorudo in the original version of Suikoden II, which is a very literal romanization of his Japanese name. It should most likely have been consistently romanized as Gordeau, that appears once or twice in the original English-language localization. It was changed consistently to Gordeau in the remaster.

name: Granmeyer
description: Granmeyer (グランマイヤー, Guranmaiyā) is a supporting character in Suikoden II. Granmeyer was the peace-minded mayor of South Window City at the beginning of the Dunan Unification War.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Granmeyer was known as a moderator Mayor of South Window City, well loved by the people of the city.

Granmeyer had been mayor since at least before the destruction of North Window, where he would attempt to keep peace between South Window and Two River City as arms merchants attempted to instigate war between the two powers. When reports came that Two River's forces had entered South Window, he resolved himself to fight but was much relieved when war was averted by the efforts of Viktor, Anabelle, and Zamji.

The most recent cease-fire agreement between the City-States of Jowston and the Highland Kingdom prior to the outbreak of the Dunan Unification War had been largely due to his efforts. His peaceful nature meant he rarely showed angered but he did react bitterly to Gustav Pendragon's dismissal of South Window's military contributions, feeling that others took wars and the resulting loss of life too lightly.

However, once war between Jowston and Highland broke out in 460, his sincere commitment to peace would backfire. When the Highland Army suddenly invaded South Window Principality following the fall of Muse City, Granmeyer had surrendered to Solon Jhee without resistance. His reward for his efforts to avert violence would be his immediate beheading. The following morning, his head would be displayed on South Window's castle wall for all to see.

Later, some would criticize Granmeyer's surrender for indicating that he lacked the sense of responsibility needed for a mayor to take charge of the lives of his citizens, but it is certain that he surrendered to protect the peaceful lives of the citizens he served.

name: Gustav Pendragon
description: Gustav Pendragon (グスタフ・ペンドラゴン, Gusutafu Pendoragon) is a character who appears in Suikoden II. He is the mayor of Tinto City.
originalName: グスタフ・ペンドラゴン

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Gustav, a prominent figure during the Dunan Unification War, held the esteemed position as head of the mining guild in Tinto City, consequently making him the mayor of the mining town, in accordance with local traditions.

As a seasoned miner, Gustav possessed a commanding presence, his physique comparable to seasoned warriors, and his demeanor was marked by a bold and outspoken nature. His charismatic personality instilled a sense of security among the citizens of Tinto, thus earning him significant popularity as the mayor.

In his role as mayor, Gustav staunchly safeguarded Tinto's interests, displaying a marked contrast with his fellow alliance members who were deeply engaged in the war against the Highland Kingdom. Upon the outbreak of the Dunan Unification War, he took the decisive step of closing the borders of Tinto Principality.

Notably, Gustav's demeanor underwent a complete transformation in the presence of his daughter, Lilly. Around her, he assumed the role of a doting and indulgent father, showcasing a more tender and foolish side. During the latter stages of the Dunan Unification War, when Lilly was abducted by Neclord, Gustav found himself in a state of panic.

However, following her rescue by the leader of the New Alliance Army, he swiftly returned to his usual demeanor. He would then agree to align Tinto City with the New Alliance Army for the remainder of the war. He had great respect for the hero, seeing past his slender build, uncommon in the strong miners of Tinto, to the determination and strength in his eyes.

After the conclusion of the Dunan Unification War, Gustav chose not to align with the newly established Dunan Republic. Instead, he declared independence and founded the Tinto Republic, where he assumed the position of its president. By…

name: Gyokuran
description: Gyokuran (ギョクラン, Gyokuran), titled Gyokuran of Suzak (朱雀のギョクラン, Suzaku no Gyokuran, Vermillion Bird Gyokuran), is a minor character who appears in Suikoden II. She is one of the four Grand Masters of the Black Dragon Cooking Clan.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Gyokuran was one of the Black Dragon Cooking Clan's four grand masters and held the title "Gyokuran of Suzak" or "Vermilion Bird Gyokuran."

She appeared to be the one orchestrating a lot of Hai Yo's challenges before stepping out of the shadows after Bashok's defeat. Hai Yo's eighth challenger and the first of the four lords to do so, before their duel, she cowardly conspired to steal most of Hai Yo recipes, leaving him unprepared for their scheduled cook-off.

Lest her tactics suggest otherwise, Gyokuran was an extremely talented chef in her own right. The only woman among the four lords of the Black Dragon Clan, she twirls her staff beautifully while preparing meals.

Despite this, Hai Yo would manage to defeat Gyokuran in the resulting challenge. Realizing that she lost the heart of a true chef, she left on a journey of rediscovery, returning the recipes stolen from Hai Yo as well as leaving behind her specialty recipe, Fried Pork Cutlets.

trivia: • Her title given in Suikoden II (Gyokuran of Suzak) is more properly translated as "Vermilion Bird Gyokuran." The Vermilion Bird is one of the Four Symbols in Chinese constellation groupings and is said to rule over the Southern Heavens. It is described as a red bird that resembles a pheasant with a five-colored plumage and is perpetually covered in flame.

name: Hai Yo
description: Hai Yo (ハイ・ヨー, Hai Yō), titled Hai Yo of Seiryu (セイリュウのハイ・ヨー, Seiryuu no Hai Yō, Blue Dragon Hai Yo), is a character in Suikoden II. Hai Yo is a talented cook who worked for the New Alliance Army while on the run from the Black Dragon Cooking Clan.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Hai Yo was a cheerful, masterful chef who was a member of the Black Dragon Cooking Clan. He had joined the group when it was still an excellent place for training in the culinary arts. His personal speciality was fried eggs.

Hearing about Jinkai's plans for domination using the Moon Bird Recipe, he was entrusted with the recipe by his lover, Shun Min, at the cost of her life and fled with the group on his heels.

He would appear suddenly at the New Alliance Army Headquarters during the Dunan Unification War, offering to open a restaurant at the castle for the New Alliance Army. This was his attempt to hide himself from agents of the Group. He presented him as a bright and cheerful chef but this hid his anxiety and fear of being found.

He would come to be challenged in a series of cook-offs which, though innocuous as first, increased with intensity and danger as the Black Dragon Cooking Clan located him and sent their agents to recover the Moon Bird Recipe.

Finally, Hai Yo defeated Jinkai in a final cook-off and tore up the recipe, stating that recipes alone were not the key to truly great cuisine. He would continue to serve the New Alliance Army for the rest of the war before returning to Black Dragon Island to put flowers on Shun Min's grave and tell her that the fight was finally over.

Following the war, he re-established contact with Bashok, who had challenged him on behalf of the Black Dragon Cooking Clan, and wrote to Huan on his behalf, helping to save the life of Bashok's daughter, Mamie with medicinal dishes prepared under Huan's direction.

trivia:
• Hai Yo has two endings, depending on whether or not the player completes the cook-off minigame. If you do not defeat Jinkai, Hai Yo will flee at the end of the game, continuing his life on the run from the Black Dragon Cooking Clan.
• The order of Hai Yo's opponents is: Yu Kum, Goetsu, Shiki, Ryuki, Bashok, Ryuko, Antonio, Gyokuran, Retso (forced loss), Lester, Retso, and Jinkai. Of these, Yu Kum and Goetsu are traveling chefs with no affiliation to the Black Dragon Cooking Clan. Antonio and Lester make cameos in order to test/help their friend. Everyone else is of the Group.
recruitment: Once your castle is Level 2, he will appear in the kitchen area and ask to join.

name: Han Cunningham
description: Han Cunningham (ハーン・カニンガム, Hān Kaningamu) is a supporting character in Suikoden II. Han is a Highland general and the former bearer of the Black Sword Rune. He was a close friend of Genkaku.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Han Cunningham was a renowned hero and military commander of the Highland Kingdom, celebrated for his valor and leadership on the battlefield.

Born into a distinguished military family, Han rose to prominence as the commander of the Highland 1st Army during the war between Highland and the City-States of Jowston.

He shared a deep friendship and rivalry with Genkaku, a warrior who fought for the City-States, as both hailed from the same village. Their bond was one of mutual respect and admiration, tempered by the fierce competition between them.

During the war, Han and Genkaku encountered the split Rune of Beginning in Toto Village, with Han inheriting the Black Sword Rune. Recognizing each other's strength, the two agreed to a duel to determine the outcome of the war, a testament to their shared desire to test their skills against one another.

However, their duel was thwarted by Darell's conspiracy, resulting in Genkaku's disappearance and Han being falsely hailed as the hero who ended the war. This undeserved acclaim left Han burdened with guilt and a lingering darkness in his heart, as he felt his victory was built on deceit.

Following the war in SY 434, Han and Genkaku returned their runes to the shrine in Toto Village. Han was appointed Commander of the Highland 2nd Army and tasked with defending the Imperial Capital, L'Renouille. Stationed in the capital, he would also serve as Prince Luca Blight's sword instructor for a number of years.

He remained in the capital during the attack on King Agares' royal caravan in SY 443, though he did lead the rescue effort. He also played no active role in the Dunan Unification War until its final stages.

Some speculated that Luca Blight deliberately kept Han out of the public eye, wary of his influence and integrity.…

name: Hanna
description: Hanna (ハンナ, Hanna) is a character in Suikoden II who also appears in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.1. She is a swordswoman on a journey from the distant Nameless Lands.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Hanna was a formidable warrior hailing from the Nameless Lands. Driven by an unyielding desire to wield her sword in battle, she embarked on a journey far from her homeland. Though generally reticent in speech, her imposing presence, honed physique, and stern countenance spoke volumes about her exceptional prowess on numerous battlefields.

While seeking a clash of blades, Hanna found respite in the tranquil atmosphere of Toto Village. However, the outbreak of the Dunan Unification War disrupted her peaceful sojourn. Witnessing the devastation wrought by the Highland Army upon Toto Village ignited a raging fury within Hanna, coupled with a sense of helplessness in being unable to prevent the tragedy. In her quest to halt Highland's advance and avenge the village, she aligned herself with the mercenary forces.

As the New Alliance Army took shape, Hanna assumed the role of an infantry captain, exhibiting an unparalleled prowess that swiftly overwhelmed enemy soldiers, particularly those foolish enough to doubt her strength as a woman.

Despite her usual brevity of speech, Hanna found a kindred spirit in Oulan, their shared attitudes and strength fostering a strong bond. The two warriors frequently engaged in joint training sessions, culminating in the development of the formidable Tackle Attack technique. A true warrior at heart, Hanna favored meat as her go-to food, yet displayed an open-mindedness towards culinary experiences.

With the conclusion of the war, Hanna departed once more on her unending journey, directing her steps toward the Grasslands, eagerly seeking new battles to engage in.

recruitment: After Toto Village is destroyed, you can find her at the entrance. Tell her to fight with you.

name: Hans
description: Hans (ハンス, Hansu) is a character who appears in Suikoden II. He is an armorer by trade who traveled from Zexen to Jowston to make his fortune.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
thumb|left|220px|Two River City, land of free enterprise?
As the second eldest son in his business-owning family, Hans did not receive a good inheritance from his family and so traveled all the way from the Zexen Federation, far west of the Dunan region in order to open a store. He managed to gather the money to do so from his own peddling as well as loans from accquaintences.

Just as he was about to make a name for himself in the City-States of Jowston, he was caught up in the Dunan Unification War. Upon arriving in Two River City, Hans discovered that the city already had an armor store and was at his wit's end.

Luckily, the leader of the New Alliance Army happened upon the armorer at the Green Leaf Inn and gave him the chance to open his own store. Hans was overjoyed at the prospect of having a built-in consumer base for his armor and readily joined, even being willing to pay large amounts of money for the opportunity.

His armor shop became extremely well known for its wide selection of goods and became very popular. Although he had just enough money to establish himself in Jowston, he had also saved up an impressive amount of potch, although it was unknown where he kept it.

He remained at the New Alliance Army Headquarters following the Dunan Unification War, continuing to run his armory there.

recruitment: Speak with him in the inn (2nd floor) of Two-River and he will beg to open a store in your castle. You can either charge him or not. If you charge him, wait several days then talk to him again. If not, he will join right away.

name: Hauser
description: Hauser (ハウザー, Hauzā) is a character in Suikoden II and Genso Suikogaiden Vol.1. Hauser is a talented general and was considered the best military commander in the City-States of Jowston.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Hauser was a talented military officer who served as the commander of the Muse City army. Due to his capable command, he had been able to unite the armies of each city and stave off an invasion of the City-States of Jowston by the Highland Kingdom for some time prior to the outbreak of the Dunan Unification War.

He had served Muse City as a soldier since Darell's mayorship. In SY 446, when he was on hand to help Anabelle when she and Viktor investigated an arms merchant ring attempting to foster war between South Window City and Two River City.

As a soldier, Hauser held a very strict and disciplined attitude and made sure that all his actions as such were made on the basis of the law, refusing to fight as a military officer for any reason other than the law. He had a strong belief that the military should not wield political power and that the military acting on personal feelings invited chaos.

This is the reason why when Muse City fell to the Highland Army, he escaped the city with Jess and fought under him in his role as acting mayor of Muse, rather than joining the New Alliance Army, regardless of his personal confidence in Jess's decisions. It was only with the offical entry of Muse into the New Alliance Army in Tinto City that he would join the force.

After the war, Hauser became the commander of the Dunan Republican Army.

recruitment: Joins automatically after the Tinto/Neclord events.

name: Hoi
description: Hoi (ホイ, Hoi) is a character in Suikoden II who also appears in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.1. Hoi is a con artist who went around the City-States of Jowston posing as the hero.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Hoi, a thief hailing from Kanakan, earned a reputation for engaging in various petty crimes across the region. During the tumultuous period of the Dunan Unification War, he resorted to stealing Ayda's horse in the forests near Greenhill City and attempted to sell it to Nash Latkje. However, Ayda swiftly uncovered the theft and forced Hoi to retreat with a flurry of arrows.

In a brazen attempt to exploit the name of the leader of the New Alliance Army, Hoi began impersonating him on multiple occasions. This slanderous scheme played out in South Window City, Two River City, and Radat Town, allowing Hoi to evade payment for various bills.

During one of his impersonations in Radat Town, the true leader of the New Alliance Army happened to be present, leading to a perplexing case of mistaken identity. Both Hoi and the actual leader were confronted by the crowd and subjected to a beating. Believing his fellow victim to be yet another lookalike, Hoi offered to form an alliance. However, upon returning to New Alliance Army Headquarters, he realized his misjudgment.

Though Hoi faced the possibility of imprisonment, he decided to remain with the New Alliance Army throughout the war. Even while staying with the New Alliance Army, he always looked for an opportunity to indulge in petty thievery and could be found skulking suspiciously in various areas of the castle.

In battle, he demonstrated a proficiency with tonfa, much like the hero, possibly utilizing their similar naming as part of his impersonation strategy. While not quite as skilled as the hero, Hoi showcased competence with these weapons.

Following the war's conclusion, Hoi briefly attempted to retire from a life of thievery, endeavoring to pursue a more honest path. However, he quickly recognized this as a mistake…

recruitment: After defeating Luca Blight, go to the restaurant in Radat and let him join.

name: Huan
description: Huan (ホウアン, Houan) is a character in Suikoden II. Huan is a famous doctor who loaned his talents to the New Alliance Army.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
thumb|left|220px|Take one Tuta and call me in the morning.Huan was a doctor of some fame and was considered to be the best doctor in Muse City. He learned his medical wisdom from Liukan, another famous healer, in the Toran Republic.

As a doctor he was known for his gentle and reassuring bedside manner. Once the Dunan Unification War broke out, Huan was forced to flee his Muse City home, escaped with the help of Georg Prime and found his way to the New Alliance Army's headquarters.

Huan aided the New Alliance Army with his medical knowledge, along with the aid of his apprentice, Tuta, in order to create a peaceful world where doctors would not be needed for war. His love of peace meant he disliked the power over life and death he had as a wartime doctor. Tuta became his student after Huan used his medical knowledge to save the boy's mother, inspiring him to enter the field.

As a member of the New Alliance Army, he worked actively on the battlefield, helping to heal many wounded comrades as a result. Following the Dunan Unification War, Huan returned to Muse, helping to heal veterans of the war.

recruitment: Joins automatically after establishing the headquarters.

name: Jess
description: Jess (ジェス, Jiesu) is a character in Suikoden II. Jess serves as the chamberlain and vice-mayor of Muse City under Mayor Anabelle, whom he greatly respects.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Jess is a politician from Muse City who became a chamberlain and the Vice-Mayor under Mayor Anabelle. Jess was thought of by the citizens of muse as a good, clever and handsome man who would likely be mayor once Anabelle stepped down.

As a bureaucrat, Jess was extremely talented, but his overconfidence would occasionally lend itself to hasty decision making. As such he was more suited to a support role rather than leadership. Some even regarded his ill-thought out decision to send the hero and Jowy Atreides on a reconnaisance mission as inadvertently causing the eventual fall of Muse.

Jess was in no hurry for Anabelle to step aside, as he was in love with her but kept his feelings secret due to his duties as chamberlain. When Anabelle was murdered, Jess mourned her deeply. Seeing the leader of the New Alliance Army at the scene of Annabelle's murder, Jess believed him to have murdered Anabelle and distrusted the New Alliance Army, believing them to be deceiving the City-States of Jowston.

Taking the position of acting mayor, Jess, along with Hauser, assembled a force of volunteers and refugees from Muse and Greenhill in Tinto City. While trying to dispel Neclord from Tinto, Jess forced troops to attack Neclord hastily based on information received from a Muse soldier, without fact-checking the information.

Many soldiers died as a result of Jess' haste, but the New Alliance Army was able to destroy Neclord. Seeing the hero fight so valiantly for the people of Tinto helped Jess realize he was mistaken in his suspicions. He attempted to step down entirely to take responsibility for the loss of so many soldiers but was persuaded by the hero and Hauser to fight with the New Alliance Army.

He would renounce his title as acting mayor of Muse, passing the role to Fitcher,…

trivia:
• Jess only eats a single croissant for breakfast.
• Jess is quite afraid of bugs.
recruitment: After the Tinto events, accept his apology. (if you reject him he will leave forever!)

name: Jillia Blight
description: Jillia Blight (ジル・ブライト, Jiru Buraito) is a supporting character in Suikoden II. Jillia Blight was the Princess of the Highland Kingdom during the Dunan Unification War.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Jillia Blight, the final Queen of the Highland Kingdom, exuded a benevolent and composed demeanor, serving as a stark contrast to her half-brother, the infamous "Mad Prince," Luca Blight. The differences between the siblings gave rise to speculations about their true relation; however, records affirm that they were indeed half-siblings, sharing the same mother.

As a young woman, she would spend summers at the royal family's holiday home in Kyaro Town. As she matured, Jillia began to be presented with potential suitors but she ended up rejecting them all. Though she understood her brother's suffering and tried to restrain him, it became clear she had no more control over him than anyone else. 

Throughout the tumultuous events of the Dunan Unification War, Jillia witnessed the devastating consequences of Luca's unrestrained rampages, which left her feeling apprehensive and powerless to challenge his authority. When Jillia was confronted by the hero and Jowy Atreides during the infiltration of the Highland camp, Jillia said she could well understand his hatred of Highland.

However Jowy would respond that he did not hate Highland, but the conflict that was brewing. It was this realization of Jowy's compassion and desire for peace that resonated in her and led her to entrust herself to him. During the course of the conflict, she found herself entangled in a tragic scheme orchestrated by both Luca and Jowy Atreides, to whom she was eventually betrothed.

The sinister plan aimed to assassinate King Agares, further deepening the complexities of the war. With the death of Agares and the subsequent death of Luca soon after, Jillia would ascend to the position of Queen of Highland, with her bethrowed, Jowy, becoming King at the moment of their marriage. As women were not…

name: Jinkai
description: Jinkai (ジンカイ, Jinkai) is a minor character who appears in Suikoden II. He is the Grand Wazoo of the Black Dragon Cooking Clan.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Jinkai was the leader of the Black Dragon Cooking Clan and one of its four grand masters, known as Jinkai of Genbu. He attempted to use the Moon Bird Recipe along with Retso in his Dragon Plan. His motivation was apparently to give all chefs great power. In order to regain the Moon Bird Recipe, he sent various assassin cooks to challenge Hai Yo but they were thwarted repeatedly.

After Retso was defeated, he would challenge Hai Yo personally. Although Jinkai was extremely skilled and determined, his perverse plans could not overcome Hai Yo's devotion to cooking. After losing to Hai Yo in a cook-off and hearing his impassioned plea, Jinkai regained his pride as a chef and abandoned his Dragon Plan.

name: Joanna
description: Joanna (ジョアンナ, Joanna) is a minor character in Suikoden II. She is the mother of Pilika.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Joanna was Pilika's mother who lived in Toto Village. Her husband was Marx. She was a calm and gentle woman, as well as a good cook.

She kindly nursed Jowy Atreides back to health after he threw himself from Tenzan Pass. She was friendly to both him and the hero on their return to Toto Village, encouraging them both to spend time with her daughter.

Jonanna would be killed in the razing of Toto Village by the Highland Kingdom, led by Luca Blight.

name: Jude
description: Jude (ジュド, Judo) is a character in Suikoden II and Genso Suikogaiden Vol.1. Jude is a sculptor who worked for New Leaf Academy and the New Alliance Army.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Jude was an aspiring sculptor who worked for the New Leaf Academy. Unable to support himself as a sculptor, Jude used his deep knowledge of smithing to work as the blacksmith instructor at New Leaf Academy in Greenhill City.

When the Highland Army attacked Greenhill City, Jude worked diligently to help the protect the city. He would use his smithing skills to forge spears for Nash Latkje who was helping the defenses. When the city was about to fall, he would help Mayor Teresa escape by disguising himself as her bodyguard, Shin, to confuse pursuing Highland soldiers.

Following Greenhill's occupation, Jude would resume his teaching position. However, his true passion was sculpting but with no opportunity to display his works, Jude had never had any works officially published. During the Dunan Unification War, Jude came up with a great idea for a piece but found he was out of clay.

When the hero brought him some clay from his hometown Forest Village, Jude was so grateful he joined the New Alliance Army in thanks. Jude sculpted a Guardian Deity statue, representing his allies' aspirations, in the New Alliance Army's headquarters, as well as a statue of the person who got the most final blows in battle.

Following the war, Jude looked for new inspiration, following his completion of the Guardian Deity. His work on the Guardian Deity, his first displayed piece, had ignited his motivation to continue creating new pieces.

trivia:
• Jude's ending will slightly change depending on whether you complete the Guardian Deity statue or not.
• What items you get will depend on what plans you put together; each combination has a unique item attached to it.
recruitment: Speak with him at the New Leaf Academy. Go to Forest Village and find a villager who has some clay, then bring him said clay.

name: Kahn Marley
description: Kahn Marley (カーン・マリィ, Kān Maryi) is a character in Suikoden II and Genso Suikogaiden Vol.1. Kahn is a vampire hunter in pursuit of the vampire Neclord.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Kahn, a descendant of the Marley clan, belonged to a family renowned for their centuries-long pursuit of vampires, with a particular focus on the vampire Neclord. Their quest began 3 or 4 generations ago, with an ancestor of Kahn losing their wife to Neclord, sparking the Marley family's pursuit for vengeance.

His grandfather and father had previously engaged in hunting Neclord, but lost their lives in their endeavors. Kahn, who was still in training, had been with his father when he fell against Neclord but managed to survive himself. Employing "shadow weapons," compact and easily concealed arms, Kahn mastered the art of combating vampires, a skill cultivated by his family's unwavering dedication to eradicating these creatures.

Upon the resurgence of Neclord in the ruins of North Window during the Dunan Unification War, Kahn united forces with Viktor and the Celestial Sword in an endeavor to vanquish the vampire. Subsequently, when Neclord withdrew, Kahn ventured forth to pursue his independent leads.

During his quest to find Sierra Mikain, the progenitor vampire, Kahn's path intertwined with that of Nash Latkje in Greenhill City. Promptly enlisted by Nash when the city faced siege by the Highland Army, Kahn displayed proficiency in utilizing a Canopy Defense magic scroll, neutralizing the magical prowess of the Highland mages.

Later during the war, Kahn extended assistance to the New Alliance Army during Neclord's attempted takeover of Tinto Principality, collaborating with Sierra. His expertise in vampire hunting techniques proved invaluable, as he employed a technique devised by his grandfather, passed down through the family line, to seal Neclord's Doppelganger Secret.

Following Neclord's ultimate defeat, Kahn continued to stand with the New Alliance Army…

trivia: • After Kahn finishes his vampire hunting business, he would like to become a mineralogist.
recruitment: Joins automatically after the Tinto/Neclord events.

name: Karen
description: Karen (カレン, Karen) is a character in Suikoden II. Karen is a popular dancer in Kuskus Town who joined the New Alliance Army.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Karen was a well-regarded dancer originating from Kuskus Town. She had achieved her proficiency in dancing under the guidance of Mina, a renowned dancer from the Toran Republic and a veteran of the Gate Rune War. She may have learned to fight from Mina also.

Despite her inherent shyness and reserved nature, Karen's performances exuded flamboyance and liveliness, effectively uplifting the spirits of her audience. While dancing, she underwent a remarkable transformation in demeanor, seemingly fueled by the elation of the act itself, presenting an audacious and stunning persona that appeared distinct from her usual self.

During the Dunan Unification War, Karen made the decision to join the New Alliance Army following a dancing contest, in which the young leader of the army proved to be an impressive counterpart to her own talent. It was not that she supported the New Alliance Army over the Highland Army. More that she would side with the friends she made through dancing.

Having been invited to perform at the New Alliance Army Headquarters, Karen contributed significantly to boosting the morale of the soldiers, despite occasionally feeling out of place in a military environment. Demonstrating her adeptness on both the dance stage and the battlefield, her agile footwork and skillful employment of the Twin Ring Rune allowed her to gain an upper hand over her opponents.

Subsequent to the war's conclusion, Karen returned to her hometown of Kuskus, where she sustained her popularity among the male population, albeit not so much with their beleaguered wives.

trivia: • You can win several unique, decorative items from Karen's dancing minigame, including Karen Statue A, Karen Statue B, Karen Statue C, Karen Painting A, and Karen Painting B. The latter prizes are harder to get, as Karen's dance steps on the last two levels of difficulty are random.
recruitment: After Luca Blight's defeat, find her in Kuskus. Dance with her and she'll join you.

name: Kent
description: Kent (ケント, Kento) is a minor character in Suikoden II. He is a delivery boy living in the Highway Village and a friend of Futch.

name: Kiba Windamier
description: Kiba Windamier (キバ ・ウィンダミア, Kiba Windamia) is a character in Suikoden II. Kiba serves as the distinguished general of the 3rd Highland Army, aided by his son and adjutant, Klaus.

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
In Suikoden II, Kiba Windamier is a middle-aged man with brown eyes. He is naturally bald with a well-groomed blond mustache and beard and large eyebrows.

His attire consisted of a full suit of silver plate armor with black underclothes, designed with segmented plates for mobility, as well as a red cape, fastened at the shoulders. In Genso Suikoden Card Stories TCG artwork, he is also depicted wearing chain mail underneath his plate armor.

Additionally, he wore a red scarf around his neck, a brown belt with a golden buckle, and a Highland Kingdom crest on the left side of his chest plate, signifying his patriotism. He also donned black gloves, black trousers under his armor, and sturdy black boots, completing his appearance.

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
Kiba Windamier was a highly respected military commander known for his unwavering loyalty, strong leadership, and deep sense of honor. He was well respected by his men for leading from the front lines, generously rewarding his soldiers, and ensuring that the families of fallen soldiers were taken care of.

Although a proud warrior, he deeply resented the loss of life that war brought, openly admitting to crying upon hearing of his father's death in battle. Despite his great victory at Rhetto Fortress, he held no fond memories of it due to the heavy casualties suffered by his troops.

Kiba was devoted to King Agares Blight, serving with distinction despite receiving little in return for his efforts. He respected Prince Luca Blight as a general but was wary of his excessive brutality.

While a capable battlefield commander, Kiba struggled with administrative duties and often relied on subordinates like Dolenz and his son, Klaus, to manage them.

As a father, Kiba was understanding and had placed no expectations on Klaus to follow in his footsteps, knowing that Klaus had to be his own person. His one major vice was drinking, so much so that Klaus was worried for his father's liver.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Kiba Windamier hailed from a prominent military family in the Highland Kingdom, aspiring to follow in his father's footsteps. Unfortunately, his father died in battle before they could ever serve together.

He first gained fame in SY 434, at the end of the Jowston-Highland War, by defending Rhetto Fortress with only 100 soldiers against an invading force of 3,000 from the City-States of Jowston, successfully protecting King Robert Blight. This victory secured his rise within the Highland Army. He further solidified his reputation by quelling internal revolts and leading joint campaigns with the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia.

At some point he would marry Eliza and in SY 442 his son, Klaus, was born. By the time of the Dunan Unification War, he had attained the rank of general in the 3rd Highland Army.

Supporting the war against Jowston based on historical hostilities and the supposed "surprise attack" on the Unicorn Brigade, he and Klaus executed several military campaigns, including an attempt to take Two River City, which ultimately failed due to intervention by the New Alliance Army.

Later in the war, Kiba occupied Radat Town as a strategic base but was ambushed due to a clever strategy by General Ridley Wizen. His situation worsened when the 4th Highland Army, led by Rowd, refused to provide reinforcements, leading to his capture by the New Alliance Army. 

At first Kiba demanded a warrior's execution, but upon learning that King Agares Blight had been killed by his own son, Luca, Kiba's unwavering loyalty to Agares prevented him from accepting Luca as king. Persuaded by Shu, he chose to defect to the New Alliance Army, realizing that Luca’s rule threatened both Highland and Jowston alike.

Despite the stigma of betrayal, Kiba fought valiantly for his new allies,…

trivia: • In Suikoden II (1998) itself, Kiba's age is given as 37 by Richmond. This is an error, Kiba is 47 years old during the events of Suikoden II.
recruitment: Joins automatically after defeating him.

name: Killey
description: Killey (キリィ, Kiryi) is a recurring character in Suikoden II, Suikoden V and Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2. He is a Sindar hunter who is a rival of Lorelai.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Killey was a renowned treasure hunter hailing from the Queendom of Falena. His distinct red attire is rooted in a cultural tradition of his homeland and is connected to the legend of the Crimson Bird, a revered symbol in his native culture.

Throughout his life, Killey embarked on a global quest to uncover the enigmatic mysteries of the Sindar, an ancient and elusive civilization. His fixation on the Sindar was all-consuming, leading him to pursue any means necessary to obtain information about them. The motivations behind his pursuit and his true identity remain shrouded in mystery.

Generally, Killey exuded a composed and reserved demeanor, yet he could undergo sudden and drastic shifts, transforming into a dangerous and even violent individual when confronted with matters related to the Sindar and their ruins.

Notably, he harbored a longstanding rivalry with Lorelai, another seeker of Sindar secrets. Their paths intertwined within the Deep Twilight Ruins, where they found themselves trapped together until Viki's intervention aided their escape, alongside Nash Latkje and others.

Years later, Killey resurfaced during a gathering organized by the Godwin Faction, aimed at studying the enigmas of the Founder's Site located in the Ashtwal Mountains. Intriguingly, he infiltrated the Sun Palace and was apprehended while investigating the various seals there. Subsequently, he faced imprisonment at Agate Prison, accused of attempting to steal the Sun Rune.

As the Sun Rune War unfolded, Killey encountered the Prince of Falena, and upon recognizing a potential connection between the Sindar and the Falenan royal family, he decided to join the Imperial City Recapture Army. Following the war's conclusion, Killey ventured out of Falena, continuing his relentless quest to explore…

recruitment: Killey has several methods to recruit:

name: Kinnison
description: Kinnison (キニスン, Kinisun) is a character in Suikoden II who also appears in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.1.

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
A handsome youth, Kinnison had short brown hair and brown eyes. He wore a red scarf and a loose yellow coat over a white ensemble. He wore brown boots and arm bracers.

His arrow satchel was slung over his right shoulder. As such, he held his bow with his left hand and drew and fired his arrow with his right.

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
Kinnison was a kind-hearted young man, who hunted for a living. Very quiet and reserved by nature, he was not overly fond of people or large gatherings. He was distressed enough by the destruction of the forest by war to put that aside and fight for the New Alliance Army.

He is able to communicate and converse with Shiro through their heart-to-heart bond. He cannot communicate with any other animal in such a way, however.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Kinnison was a hunter who lived in the forest surrounding Ryube Village along with his dog, Shiro. He had found the young Shiro by the dog's dying mother after a storm and had raised him ever since, the two becoming firm partners.

As a child, Kinnison lived with his mother, having no memories of his father. She passed when Kinnison was around 10 years old and he was taken in by his uncle, Ashino. This was when Kinnison first encountered the forest and learned how to hunt and track.

Seeing the compassion of the hero when he returned a baby bird to its nest, he would speak with him. He would cooperate with the New Alliance Army after seeing the damage the Highland Army caused to the forest he called home on their advance. His faithful hound would also accompany him.

Even as part of the New Alliance Army, Kinnison's calm and peaceful nature meant that even as an archer captain, he would never shoot any more arrows than was strictly necessary. Adjusting to life at the New Alliance Army Headquarters, he would grow close to Ayda, a forest hunter whom he shared quite a lot in common with.

After the Dunan Unification War, Kinnison returned his normal life in the forest near Ryube, though he visited Ayda in the deep forest from time to time.

recruitment: When entering Ryube forest, help the bird's nest on the left fork. Leave and come back; talk to Kinnison.

name: Kinua
description: Kinua (キヌア, Kinua) is a character mentioned in Suikoden II. He is the father of Lucia.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Kinua was the father of Lucia and her predecessor the leader of the Karaya Clan. He is said to have been assassinated in a plot concocted by Teresa's father, Alec Wisemail, and Gordeau of the Matilda Knights.

His death was apparently intended to weaken the position of the Karaya Clan who had competing land claims with Alec and Gordeau. However, Lucia was a strong leader in turn and the death of her father led her to view the City-States of Jowston as an enemy.

name: Klaus Windamier
description: Klaus Windamier (クラウス・ウィンダミア, Kurausu Windamia) is a character in Suikoden II. Klaus is a tactician who served both the Highland Kingdom and the Dunan Republic.
originalName: クラウス・ウィンダミア

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Klaus Windamier, the gifted son of Kiba Windamier, showed early admiration for his father, but his slender physique proved unsuitable for military life. Instead, he inherited the appearance and temperament of his mother, often choosing to immerse himself in solitary studies and rarely speaking unless necessary.

At the age of 14, during a patrol with his father, Klaus faced taunts from a soldier about his perceived weakness compared to Kiba. In response, they dueled at night, and Klaus, recognizing his physical disadvantage, cleverly feigned an injury to one eye. Knocking a torch to the ground, he adapted to the darkness and overcame his opponent, revealing a talent for strategic thinking in battles.

Gifted with the Harmonian edition of "Heroes of Harmonia" by his uncle Greg, Klaus learned about Soledt, a blind strategist absent from the Highland version of the book. Recognizing the pivotal role strategists played, he sought permission from his mother to enroll at the academy Soledt established in the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia.

Upon returning to Highland, Klaus assumed the role of tactician in the 3rd Royal Highland Company, serving under his father during the Dunan Unification War. Despite his youth, Klaus demonstrated a composed analysis of war situations and offered precise instructions to his troops, skillfully extrapolating the enemy's strategies.

He played a part in the attack on Two River City, attempting to secure the city's surrender through manipulation of internal tensions, and contributed to the capture of South Window Principality. Although talented, he fell short of Shu's mastery and was defeated when Highland forces aimed to seize the New Alliance Army Headquarters, subsequently being taken prisoner by the New Alliance Army.

Upon learning of King…

trivia: • Klaus has normal shaped eyes like his father, they're just closed in his artwork.
recruitment: Joins with Kiba.

name: Kokto
description: Kokto (コクト, Kokuto) is a minor character in Suikoden II. He is a guard who served in the army of Tinto Principality.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Kokto was a soldier who served at the guard post in Drakemouth Village. When his mother became pregnant, he asked Koyu to help her with money.

During the Dunan Unification War, Koyu would bring up this act to convince Kokto to allow he and the hero to pass into Tinto Principality despite Mayor Gustav having given the order to let no one enter Tinto.

name: Koyu
