description: Koyu (コウユウ, Kouyuu) is a character in Suikoden II who also appears in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2. He is the youngest of the Lampdragon Bandits.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Koyu was the youngest of the Lampdragon Bandits and is the oath sibling of Gijimu and Lo Wen. He lost his parents at a young age, but Gijimu adopted him as a sworn brother, and he became a bandit with him since then.

Under their influence, he grew to be a dedicated and obliging youth, able to compensate for his lack of strength with quick thinking in battle. Kind hearted like Gijimu, he was well liked by locals, such as Kokto, despite his occupation and was a brave and selfless fighter.

He was also good friends with Marlowe as each has what the other wants, personality wise. He first became friends with Marlowe when he noticed the youth sitting along with his books.

During the Dunan Unification War, Neclord's zombies attacked the Lampdragon Bandits' stronghold and Koyu ran to the New Alliance Army in order to ask its leader for help. After having his request granted, Koyu led a party of New Alliance soldiers to the Lampdragon Bandits' stronghold and to Tinto City. After Neclord's defeat, like Lo Wen and Gijimu, Koyu joined the New Alliance Army.

After the war, Koyu occasionally visited Marlowe in order to marvel at Marlowe's scholarship, while continuing to work under Gijimu.

As such, he accompanied Gijimu to Milit Village on Gustav Pendragon's command when the village was besieged by bandits calling themselves the Fire Bringer and helped to defeat them.

recruitment: Joins automatically after the Tinto/Neclord events.

name: Lebrante
description: Lebrante (レブラント, Reburanto) is a character who appears in Suikoden II. He is an appraiser and noted collector of antiques.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
thumb|left|220px|You'd think he would have found at least one Celadon Urn before now.Lebrante is a Zexen appraiser with absolute confidence in his talents who opened an appraisal store in Radat Town.

During Richmond's investigation into the attempted murder of Milan Fiori, Lebrante would examine Milan's sword at the detective's request. He would reveal it as the Seven Treasure Rising Dragon, a sword with a deep connection to the Scarlet Moon Empire itself, giving Richmond a key clue to solving the mystery of Milan's attacker.

An avid collector of antiques himself, Lebrante's goal was to collect 100 Celadon Urns. He gladly joined the New Alliance Army in exchange for the last vase needed to complete his collection, viewing it as proof of the hero's good character. He hoped to contribute to the war's end, even if only in a small way.

Lebrante was not naturally bald, rather he shaved his head in order to ensure a stray hair would never fall on his precious antiques. Although he was described as easily irritated, he was known to sing loudly, and repeatedly, after a few drinks. His favorite drink was a high grade drink like scotch or whiskey, which he took on the rocks.

After the Dunan Unification War, Lebrante continued his appraising while searching for more Celadon Urns in order to bring his expanded collection to 1,000, and ordering in the shelves to store them on.

recruitment: After Caslte is Level 3, speak to him with a Celadon Urn on your inventory. You can buy these at the Gregminster trade shop, from the Rope Climbing mini-game, or as a rare drop from a BonBon or Flyer.

name: Leona
description: Leona (レオナ, Reona) is a character in Suikoden II who has a cameo in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.1. She is a bartender for the New Alliance Army.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Leona is a flirtatious bartender who worked at the Mercenary Fortress serving drinks as well as occasionally smoothing over tensions in the group. Leona loved taking care of children and was good at talking with customers and doling out advice as sort of an elder sister. Her tender and reassuring nature helped a lot of young soldiers.

She knew Viktor in the past and opened the Mercenary Fortress tavern as she knew it would be a profitable and worthwhile venture. Her charm, as well as the quality of alcohol and food, made her place a very popular meeting place for the group.

When her home, Toto Village, was burned to the ground, she was devastated but was careful not to let it show. After the fall of the Mercenary Fortress, Leona fled with the mercenaries to Muse City, working at an inn there, where she met Nash Latkje. With the fall of Muse, she would make her way to North Window, eventually becoming part of the New Alliance Army.

She opened a tavern at the New Alliance Army Headquarters, tending to her patrons as well as occasionally watching over Pilika. The night before the battle with Luca Blight, Leona kept her bar open late, knowing that people would need a drink and perhaps someone to talk to as the battle approached.

Following the Dunan Unification War, Leona remained at the New Alliance Army Headquarters, where her bar remained extremely popular.

trivia: • After you defeat the final boss, Leona will leave the castle for whiskey, and you will be unable to add anyone except the hero of the first game to the party.
recruitment: Joins automatically after establishing the headquarters.

name: Lilly Pendragon
description: Lilly Pendragon (リリィ・ペンドラゴン, Riryi Pendoragon) is a minor character in Suikoden II and a major character in Suikoden III. Lilly is the spoiled daughter of Gustav Pendragon who, as a young lady, investigates rumors of Fire Bringer raids on Tinto caravans.
originalName: リリィ・ペンドラゴン

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Encounter with Neclord
Lilly was the daughter of Gustav Pendragon, who served as mayor of Tinto City during the Dunan Unification War.

She was the apple of his eye and was a bright and cheerful child who lived a happy, comfortable life. Though in later years, it was said that her father's duties meant she didn't spend much time with her parents.

Her pigtails were her defining feature as a child. She loved her father dearly, though she took after her mother in looks. Her cheerfulness reminded Nanami of Pilika to a certain extent.

When Neclord attempted to take over Tinto in the middle of the Dunan Unification War, Lilly was kidnapped with the intent of making her his bride. Thanks to the efforts of Viktor, Marlowe, and Sierra, among other members of the New Alliance Army, Lilly was safely rescued and returned to her father.
First Daughter of the Republic
As a young woman, Lilly grew up to be both beautiful and clever but also selfish and stubborn due to her spoiled upbringing in Tinto. She was also very hard on her attendants, Samus and Reed, putting too much pressure on them and generally displacing her own failings onto them.

Still, even with all that, she was courageous and not easily scared and willing to fight for her beliefs. She was also very dependable in a crisis. As an adult, Lilly deftly wielded an elegant one-handed sword with a slender blade and guard shaped like a cross.

She first met Chris Lightfellow at the Zexen Federation Day Ball in 470. There, she insisted that Chris dance with her and when Chris, who was not good at dancing at the time, accidentally spilled a glass of wine on Lilly's dress, a brawl soon broke out. Lilly ended up throwing a cake at Chris, who retaliated with a slap.

The fight would finally end after the two finished hurling…

recruitment: Joins Automatically in Hugo Chapter 2.

name: Lo Wen
description: Lo Wen (ロウエン, Rouen) is a character in Suikoden II who also appears in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2. She is a member of the Lampdragon Bandits who was kidnapped by Neclord.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Lo Wen was a spirited member of the notorious Lampdragon Bandits, adopting the appearance and mannerisms befitting a bandit. Yet, among the trio, she stood out as the most well-mannered and composed individual. A skilled warrior, she wielded a heavy ball attached to a chain, employing it with remarkable force to combat her adversaries.

In the past, Lo Wen resided in Two River City. However, her actions caught the attention of the city's Three Houses of Parliament when they discovered her involvement in embezzling money from a high-ranking official. Faced with the consequences, she had no choice but to swiftly depart from the city. It was during this time that she crossed paths with the kindred spirits Gijimu and Koyu, joining forces to form their bandit group.

Amidst the Dunan Unification War, Lo Wen found herself ensnared by the forces of the sinister Neclord during his invasion of Tinto Principality. However, she quickly proved herself resilient, defying her captor as she refused to be taken merely as a "snack" for Neclord.

Eventually rescued from her precarious situation, she was a little upset that her rescue came at the hands of someone other than her sworn siblings, marking their reunion as less than an emotional one. She, along with Gijimu and Koyu, pledged their loyalty to the New Alliance Army, fighting valiantly for the cause of peace.

After the war's conclusion, Lo Wen received a pardon for her service and returned to Two River, finding joy in fulfilling her dream of opening a restaurant.

Content with her life, she embraced her new role as a restaurateur. However, when she learned of her sworn brother Gijimu's battle against bandits in the Grasslands, she could not stand idly by. Determined to aid him, she courageously confronted the fake Fire Bringer…

recruitment: Joins automatically after the Tinto/Neclord events.

name: Long Chan-Chan
description: Long Chan-Chan (ロンチャンチャン, Ron Chan Chan), also known as L.C. Chan, is a character in Suikoden II. Long Chan-Chan is a martial artist as well as the master of Wakaba.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Long Chan-Chan was a martial artist who invented the Dragon-style form of martial arts. As this was his own unique style, it did not hold any traditions or real techniques. He mainly taught techniques he saw in books. That does not mean, however, that his fighting ability was not real. Indeed, he was possessed of a superb talent for fighting. His eventual dream was to have a fighting dojo with over 1,000 students studying under him.

The main points of his style were to maintain good health (through good eating) and to train the body to the utmost limit. The White Tiger Rune that both master and student of the style bear is considered to be a mark of the dojo. He did come off as a bit brazen due to his habit of dine-and-dashing, but his skills and passion for martial arts were genuine.

During the Dunan Unification War, he would join the New Alliance Army after being reunited with his first, and only, student, Wakaba, in Crom Village. At first he was able to persuade Wakaba that his dine-and-dashing was part of his training but eventually came clean after continued scrutiny.

As a member of the army's infantry, he put his Dragon-style martial arts to good use, fighting alongside his student. Together, the two would train daily, never wavering in their efforts or their pure-hearted desire to become stronger.

After the war, Long Chan-Chan continued on his demanding regimen and continued to tutor Wakaba, all for the sake of one day achieving his dream.

trivia:
• Long Chan-Chan is called L.C. Chan on his portrait, likely due to Suikoden II's character limit.
• He named his Dragon-style after himself, with "Long" being the Chinese word for 'dragon'.
recruitment: First recruit Wakaba and have her in your active party, then enter the inn at Crom Village. (You can use the Matilda bug to recruit him at the Rockaxe inn during the peace conference).

name: Lucy
description: Lucy (ルーシー, Rūshī) is a minor character in Suikoden II. She is an innkeeper in the Highway Village.

name: Mairie
description: Mairie (メイリ, Meiri) is a minor character from Suikoden II. She is a young woman working in Greenhill City.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Mairie was a pretty young woman who worked at her family inn in Greenhill City. She had fallen for Pico and his playboy ways and was frequently upset at his flirtatious nature.

When Pico learned that Annallee was safe and at New Alliance Army Headquarters, he would immediately leave, leaving Mairie confused, angry, and heartbroken, all in equal measure.

She seemed to have a habit of crying loudly in response to any stressful situation. When Highland soldiers raided the inn during their occupation of Greenhill, Mairie responded similarly to her confrontation with Pico.

name: Makai
description: Makai (マカイ, Makai) is a supporting character in Suikoden II. Makai was the Plenipotentiary of Two River City during the Dunan Unification War.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Makai served as Plenipotentiary of Two River City during the events of the Dunan Unification War. Although it must be said that Two River's system of governance precluded him from making strong decisions, it is also true that he was lacking in leadership ability.

Due to Two River's segmented political structure, it is difficult to promote united action and this, combined with Makai's indecisive nature, led to the city's lack of unity and growing internal tension. Still, though he may give off the impression of being somewhat unreliable, Makai held a genuine passion for his hometown.

During the war, Makai's efforts to cooperate with the New Alliance Army were delayed when the schemes of Kiba and Klaus Windamier fractured the relations between the Kobold district and the rest of Two River.

However, when his attempts at brokering a peace with Kiba fell through, he was more than willing to die an honorable death defending the city, holding a strong sense of responsibility as Plenipotentiary and a genuine pride and love for his home.

Makai would continue to serve as Plenipotentiary through the Dunan Unification War and its aftermath.

name: Makumaku
description: Makumaku (マクマク, Makumaku) is a character in Suikoden II. He is a Flying Squirrel who is a member of Mukumuku's Flying Squirrel Corps.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Makumaku was a remarkable and sizable flying squirrel hailing from the lush Greenhill Forest, recognized for his distinctive appearance, characterized by a striking blue cape. Like his fellow members of the corps, he was named by Mukumuku.

Serving as the second member of the esteemed Mukumuku's Flying Squirrel Corps, Makumaku's presence added to the corps' strength. Despite his outwardly calm and composed demeanor, Makumaku's inner essence was ablaze with unyielding passion and boundless energy.

In the midst of the Dunan Unification War, Makumaku found himself drawn to the cause of the New Alliance Army, recognizing its leader wandering in the vicinity outside Greenhill City.

Upon the conclusion of the Dunan Unification War, Makumaku's path presumably led him back to his home in Greenhill Forest.

name: Marcel Atreides
description: Marcel Atreides (マルセル・アトレイド, Maruseru Atoreido) is a minor character in Suikoden II. He is the step-father of Jowy Atreides.
originalName: マルセル・アトレイド

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Marcel Atreides was a prominent figure in Kyaro Town. He became the step-father of Jowy after marrying his mother, Rosa. He felt no connection with his step-son and treated him coldly, seeing him as hinderance more than anything else.

When Jowy was arrested as a spy early in the Dunan Unification War, he had no guilt in immediately handing him over to the Highland Army as a traitor, and even denied Jowy's plea to see his mother. Later, Marcel would flee the Highland Kingdom on Jowy's ascension to the throne, fearing retribution.

name: Marco Atreides
description: Marco Atreides (マルコ・アトレイド, Maruko Atoreido) is a minor character in Suikoden II. He is the step-brother of Jowy Atreides.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Marco was the biological son of Marcel Atreides and so was the step-brother of Jowy, unrelated by blood. He did not recognize Jowy as family, being his step-mother's child, and had a distinctly cold attitude towards him.

When Jowy was arrested as a spy early in the Dunan Unification War, he coolly remarked that they were no longer related at all. Like his father, Marco would flee Kyaro Town on Jowy's ascension to the throne, fearing retribution.

name: Marlowe Cody
description: Marlowe Cody (マルロ・コーディー, Maruro Kōdī) is a character in Suikoden II. Marlowe is a tutor of Lilly Pendragon as well as a biographer of the protagonist.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Marlowe was a tutor of the young Lilly Pendragon, daughter of Tinto City Mayor Gustav. He had an extremely timid personality and spent most of his time reading books. In fact, he was entirely self-taught, never having the chance to receive formal education. As books could be very expensive in Tinto, he was very careful with his possessions. Still, along with his learnedness, he long held a desire to be a brave hero as well. 

He was frequently bullied due to his shyness as a child but still managed to become friends with Koyu, who protected him, and the other Lampdragon bandits. To each other, Koyu and Marlowe each had something the other lacked, in Marlowe's case he had studiousness but not strength. They were also both outsiders to the wider world.

During the Dunan Unification War, Marlowe met the leader of the New Alliance Army, whose leadership inspired Marlowe to help out as much as he could during Neclord's attack on Tinto. Though not strong nor particularly brave, he was driven to help in whatever way he could. He would then join the New Alliance Army, in part to work on a biography of his new inspiration, which he entitled The Path of the Warrior.

After the war ended, Marlowe founded a library in Tinto City where he taught many children the joys of learning. He would also become a minister when the Tinto Republic was founded after the war.

trivia:
• Marlowe, along with Koyu, has a somewhat expanded role in the plot if the player chooses to run away from Tinto with Nanami.
• Marlowe is evidently quite a slow writer. His biography of the hero was intended to be accessible in-game but the idea was eventually cut.

name: Marx
description: Marx (マークス, Mākusu) is a minor character in Suikoden II. He is the father of Pilika.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Marx was Pilika's father who lived in Toto Village. His wife was Joanna.

He helped rescue Jowy Atreides after he threw himself from Tenzan Pass, welcoming him with open arms. He was friendly to both him and the hero on their return to Toto Village. He also maintained the village shrine.

Marx would be killed in the razing of Toto Village by the Highland Kingdom, led by Luca Blight.

name: Mazus
description: Mazus (メイザース, Meizāsu) is a character in Suikoden II and Genso Suikogaiden Vol.1. Mazus is a famous sorcerer and the rival of Crowley.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Mazus is a famed sorcerer, known as far as the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia for his talents. In the past, Mazus was a student of Crowley but as his talents grew, Mazus also began to wish to surpass his teacher. At one point in the past, Crowley and Mazus fought, using so much magical power that they leveled three mountains and made it rain for an entire week.

Bursting with self-confidence, Mazus readily proclaimed himself a great and powerful sorcerer and certainly held the ability to back up his words. He was also considered annoying and inconsiderate of his surroundings. His rivalry with Crowley was also one-sided. Crowley had little interest in such matters.

Before he moved to the Tinto Mines, Mazus was spotted in Greenhill Forest, he attempted to create a sea of fire to test the results of his training. The resulting inferno would have been catastrophic, but thankfully the spell failed. Mazus would be discovered by the New Alliance Army in the Tinto Mines during the Dunan Unification War.

There, Mazus was "letting his thoughts flow" which he declared to be the true path to magic. He was convinced to go out into the world by the hero, joining the New Alliance Army as an excuse to test out his magical abilities. He had also heard Crowley had made his return and so was determined not to be outdone by his rival.

He used his magical arts to the great benefit of the New Alliance Army, defeating many a foe, although it was questioned how much Mazus could truly gleam from such battles at his skill level. Thanks to his fame, some young women asked him for love potions, much to his chagrin. He also continued to further his magical experiments, albeit recklessly, potentially putting New Alliance Army Headquarters at risk.

After the Dunan Unification War, Mazus set off for…

trivia: • Just as Crowley was named after a real-life British mystic, Aleister Crowley, Mazus was inspired by Aleister's real-life teacher, Samuel Liddell MacGregor Mathers. Like their Suikoden counterparts, Crowley and Mathers would also fall out over time.
recruitment: Speak to Mazus in the Tinto Mines after liberating Greenhill. You have to have at least 100 recruits.

name: Mekumeku
description: Mekumeku (メクメク, Mekumeku) is a character in Suikoden II. He is a Flying Squirrel who is a member of Mukumuku's Flying Squirrel Corps.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Mikumiku is a large Flying Squirrel who comes from the Greenhill Forest. He wears a distinctive green cape and is a member of Mukumuku's Flying Squirrel Corps. Like his fellow members of the corps, he was named by Mukumuku.

Although he joked around a lot, Mekumeku is also a very diligent worker and oftentimes the heart of the Flying Squirrel Corps. During the Dunan Unification War, he decided to join the New Alliance Army after noticing its leader wandering around the area outside Greenhill City.

name: Melia
description: Melia (メリア, Meria) is a minor character from Suikoden II. She runs Millian's Armor Emporium in Muse City.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Melia ran Millian's Armor Emporium in Muse City during the Dunan Unification War. The blonde-haired Melia was considered quite a beauty, just like her younger sister, Millian. Melia and Millian were both hard workers, unlike their older brother who did little to help the family business at all.

The store was very prosperous due to the efforts of both Melia and Millian. However, Melia was killed during Luca Blight's occupation of the city and Millian ran the store alone from then on.

name: Miklotov
description: Miklotov (マイクロトフ, Maikurotofu) is a character in Suikoden II and Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2. Miklotov is a passionate knight from the Knightdom of Matilda.

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
Miklotov was considered a handsome, if stern, looking man. He had dark eyes, strong eyebrows, and black hair, styled in a short, cropped bowl cut, further emphasizing his seriousness.

As a knight of Matilda, he wore a large blue long coat over a blue tunic, secured with multiple latches and a belt. Beneath this, he wore white pants and knee-high black boots, trimmed with blue. He also wore white gloves. On the left side of his chest, he wore the emblem of the Matilda Knights. During the Dunan Unification War, he removed this for a time due to abandoning his post to join the New Alliance Army.

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
Miklotov was passionate and prone to speaking his mind. In addition to his old-fashioned, serious demeanor, his plainspoken ways did not endear him to women. However, it gave him great empathy towards others.

Miklotov was enraged by Gordeau's decision not to aid Muse City refugees when they tried to cross the border, and even begged Gordeau to allow him to invade Muse, if only with his own Blue Knights. At times, Miklotov found his emotions uncontrollable.

His beliefs could be summarized as "help the weak and deter the strong". He held great respect for knightly virtue and tried to adhere to those standards at all times. Miklotov's passionate, if somewhat reckless, attitude was an inspiration to some of the members of the Blue Knights.

Highly dedicated, Miklotov trained for two hours before breakfast every morning, which was said to be the secret of his Blue Knights' prowess in battle. Miklotov, like Camus, had a reputation for kindness in Rockaxe Castle.

He has a 3-1 record against Camus in training duels, though the two have never fought to their fullest extent and Camus was known to be quick to concede.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Miklotov is a knight in the Matilda Knightdom, who served as the captain of the knightdom's Blue Knights. He was close friends with Red Knight captain Camus, the two having joined the knights on the same day, being comrades ever since. In training, the two set a record for the longest mock battle without a decisive result between them.

Miklotov and Camus became captains of their respective companies at a relatively early age due to a combination of factors. The Matilda Knights meritocracy was a great benefit to them both, as was their being positioned at a key generational change within the order caused in part by Gordeau causing the downfall of more senior leading knights that could rival him.

He would become known as the "Blue Knight" due to his position and prowess. Even those unfamiliar with Miklotov by name would recognize the title. Prior to the outbreak of the Dunan Unification War, Miklotov accompanied Gordeau to the Hilltop Conference at Jowston Hill. He would hurry Camus along after he bumped into Nanami, possibly thinking that Camus was about to engage in his habitual flirting.

Miklotov aided Muse City in their initial defense against the Highland Kingdom when Anabelle called for members of the City-States of Jowston to fulfil their oath but was ordered to withdraw by Gordeau in the middle of battle, much to his distaste.

An attempt by Muse refugees to flee to Matilda was met with an order by Gordeau to mobilize. Thinking this meant action on Gordeau's part, Miklotov was initially buoyed only to be despondent when Gordeau order the knights to protect the border from any incursion, refugee or otherwise, ensuring their slaughter.

Seeking to discover the truth behind the refugee's fate, Miklotov decided to enter Muse with the hero in order to discover the…

recruitment: Joins automatically after the Rockaxe events.

name: Mikumiku
description: Mikumiku (ミクミク, Mikumiku) is a character in Suikoden II. She is a Flying Squirrel who is a member of Mukumuku's Flying Squirrel Corps.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Mikumiku is a large Flying Squirrel who comes from the Greenhill Forest. She wears a distinctive pink cape and is a member of Mukumuku's Flying Squirrel Corps. Like her fellow members of the corps, she was named by Mukumuku.

Gentle-hearted and curious, Mikumiku is the idol of the Flying Squirrel Corps. During the Dunan Unification War, she decided to join the New Alliance Army after noticing its leader wandering around the area outside Greenhill City.

name: Millian
description: Millian (ミリアン, Mirian) is a minor character from Suikoden II. She runs Millian's Armor Emporium in Muse City.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Millian ran Millian's Armor Emporium in Muse City during the Dunan Unification War. Millian herself was considered quite a beauty.

The store was very prosperous, being run with the help of her sister, Melia. Melia and Millian were both hard workers, unlike their older brother who did little to help the family business at all. However, Melia and the other family members was killed during Luca Blight's occupation of the city and Millian ran the store alone from then on.

She gifted the leader of the New Alliance Army some Silver Dragon Armor after Muse's liberation.

name: Millie
description: Millie (ミリー, Mirī) is a character in Suikoden II who also appears in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2. She is a young woman who constantly dotes on her pet groundhog, Bonaparte.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Millie was a young woman in possession of a strange pet creature, Bonaparte. She had caught the creature herself when he was still too young to fight back. Just prior to the Highland Army invasion of the City-States of Jowston, Millie lost Bonaparte in Ryube Village.

The hero agreed to help her find the missing groundhog and eventually found him in Ryube Forest where he had to be subdued in combat before returning to his owner. A grateful Millie then decided to accompany the hero and his companions on their adventure. Her role in the New Alliance Army was a mystery but she could frequently seen at New Alliance Army Headquarters.

Millie always doted on Bonaparte, oblivious to how the strange creature might be viewed by others, declaring him "cute". She also seemed oblivious to Bonaparte's own discomfort and escape attempts. At New Alliance Army Headquarters, she delighted in showing Bonaparte the expansive views of Lake Dunan and would write letters for the Suggestion Box about him also. Sometimes she even slept inside Bonaparte's expansive mouth, much to the shock of Richmond.

Following the Dunan Unification War, Millie decided to travel to the Grasslands in order to find a bride for Bonaparte. She would end up spending part of this journey with Meg, getting caught up in an exhilarating teleporting adventure with Nash, thanks to Viki.

trivia: • Before you defeat Bonaparte, Millie cannot use the Groundhog Rune she has permanently equipped to her person.
recruitment: Speak to her in Ryube Village, and help her retrieve Bonaparte and she will join you.

name: Min Min
description: Min Min (ミンミン, Minmin) is a minor character from Suikoden II. She assists Hai Yo at his restaurant.

sections.title: Story
sections.body: Min Min was a girl who worked at Hai Yo's restaurant at New Alliance Army Headquarters. She got along well with Hai Yo, often encouraging him, and was very cheerful and energetic, soothing the hearts of warriors who were tired of fighting.

name: Mokumoku
description: Mokumoku (モクモク, Mokumoku) is a character in Suikoden II. He is a Flying Squirrel who is a member of Mukumuku's Flying Squirrel Corps.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Mikumiku is a large Flying Squirrel who comes from the Greenhill Forest. He wears a distinctive yellow cape and is a member of Mukumuku's Flying Squirrel Corps. Like his fellow members of the corps, he was named by Mukumuku.

The most powerful member of the Flying Squirrel Corps, Mokumoku was known for his generosity. He also enjoyed curry as his favorite food. During the Dunan Unification War, he decided to join the New Alliance Army after noticing its leader wandering around the area outside Greenhill City.

name: Momo
description: Momo (モモ, Momo) is a minor character in Suikoden II. She is the pet cat of Lotte.

name: Mukumuku
description: Mukumuku (ムクムク, Mukumuku) is a character in Suikoden II. He is a Flying Squirrel who leads a band of his kind called the Flying Squirrel Corps.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Mukumuku is a large Flying Squirrel who comes from the Greenhill Forest. His trademark is his red cloak, as well as his full-blooded sense of justice. He sometimes rode the breeze as far as Kyaro Town in the Highland Kingdom. Part of his like of humans was because of their delicious foods.

In the past, while the hero and Nanami were training in Kyaro, Nanami found the squirrel imitating his moves. She sneaked behind the flying squirrel and leaped upon him. Bringing him back to the hero and Jowy, she named the squirrel Mukumuku after his soft fur and the group have known him ever since. As such, he was something of a childhood friend.

When the hero, Nanami and Jowy were forced to flee from Kyaro under accusation of treason, Mukumuku joined them in their escape, perhaps intending to play with them as he did in the past. Staying with the party, he would eventually reform the Flying Squirrel Corps with four similar flying squirrels. As captain, he was responsible for naming the other members of the squad.

After the Dunan Unification War ended, Mukumuku returned to Greenhill Forest.

trivia:
• Mukumuku's squad is made up of himself, Makumaku, Mekumeku, Mikumiku and Mokumoku.
• Mukumuku appears in the pre-title screen opening video for Genso Suikogaiden Vol.1 but not in the game proper.
recruitment: When going to Kyaro, before entering Riou's house, examine the big tree to the right three times and Mukumuku will join.

name: Nash Latkje
description: Nash Latkje (ナッシュ・ラトキエ, Nasshu Ratokie), also known by the alias Nash Clovis (ナッシュ・クロービス, Nasshu Kurōbisu), is the main character of Genso Suikogaiden Vol.1 and Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2 who also appears in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
Nash, as a first class Harmonian citizen, had the blond hair and blue eyes that were the defining traits of the caste. He was considered to be a handsome man.

He wore loose, dark green clothing as well as a striped scarf. He also wore brown gloves and boots. His loose clothes are worn in order to conceal the equipment he frequently carries. He wields the Twin Snake Sword Großer Fluss. By Suikoden III he wore a less concealing outfit.

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
Nash was said to have an unsoldierly personality and strived to avoid fights. However, when pushed he showed his true power and strength. Many of skills were learned from the Howling Voice Guild and Nash's talents were well regarded even there. Quick-witted and able to adapt quickly, Nash was a master of survival against improbable odds, a key trait for a spy.

He often showed a contrast of thoughtfulness and carelessness. It was said his carelessness was carefully curated to aid in his work as a Harmonian agent. He was also extremely unlucky and fell into all sorts of situations regardless of his intentions, which he tended to react to by blurting out whatever came to mind. 

When he was older, Nash was described as garrulous, elusive, and was something of an idle flirt. Chris regarded him as skilled in wordplay but also cavalier and untrustworthy due to his allegiance and role as a spy. He would also make several cryptic references to a "missus".

The animals of Budehuc Castle had a peculiar relationship with Nash. Both Koroku and Connie tried to urinate on him and Ruby once swooped him up and deposited him in its nest.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Nash was born the eldest son of the Latkje household, which held considerable influence in the People's Faction in Harmonia. In his youth, he spent two years in the Howling Voice Guild as part of his education, learning knowledge of explosives, but not receiving formal training as a gunner.

There, he developed something of a rivalry with Elza, despite their differing roles and background. It was said Nash was never able to defeat Elza, although she was still impressed with his abilities.

Nash's life changed when Zaj Quiroz, a tutor to Nash's sister, Julie, poisoned their father to take over the family estate on the eve of his wedding to Julie. In retaliation, Nash attacked Zaj with the Twin Snake Sword Großer Fluss, severely wounding him. Thinking Zaj dead, Nash fled the household, unwilling to reveal the truth for fearing of hurting Julie further.

Nash joined the Harmonian Southern Frontier Defense Force and was stationed at Caleria, near the Grasslands. On a covert mission to find information regarding the 27 True Runes, he sneaked into the City-States of Jowston during the Dunan Unification War. Entering from Highland, he ended up traveling with Sierra Mikain on his journey to Muse City. The two continued their respective journeys individually after the defeat of the vampire Rein Penenberg.

When Nash arrived in Muse City, he shared a room in the local inn with Templeton. During his stay, he not only met Clive, an old acquaintance from his Howling Voice Guild training, but also several other people who would later form the New Alliance Army. He overheard how Jowy Atreides and his friend were trying to infiltrate a Highland camp near the border.

Deciding to follow them, he stole the uniform of a Highland soldier and investigated the camp. He also met Jillia…

trivia:
• In Suikoden III, Nash uses a nasel bird named Dominguez Junior, named after the original bird he worked with in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.1.
• His partnership with Dominguez brings to mind another pairing with similar names. Nash Bridges was a 1990s police procedural TV series, which saw Nash Bridges and his partner Joe Dominguez team up to solve crimes.
• In Suikoden III, Nash mentions several times that he has a "missus", but it is unknown as to whether or not this is true or part of his cover. Genso Suikoden Genso Shinsho Vol.7 speculated the top three candidates for the role were Sierra Mikain, Lena Suphina, and Aisha.

name: Nina
description: Nina (ニナ, Nina) is a character in Suikoden II who also appears in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.1. Nina is a student at New Leaf Academy who tends to be a hopeless romantic.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Nina is a student at the New Leaf Academy whose bright and congenial personality made her a popular student at the school. She tended to be romantically drawn to foreign men. Her infatuations tend to leave her objects of affections puzzled and withdrawing from her.

When the Highland Army marched on Greenhill City, Nina helped as best she could to organize defenses within the city. During this tense time, she noticed Nash Latkje and immediately became attracted to the Harmonian native.

She also had a deep crush on Shin. It was this prusuit of Shin that brought her into contact with Teresa's hiding place in the city and she became an accomplise to their activites.

During the Highland occupation of Greenhill City, got herself into trouble with a guard, claiming him to be lecherous. The situation was diffused by Flik, which led to the beginnings of her infatuation with the Toran native.

She mistakingly saw Nanami as a rival for his affection during this time, due to the frequent rendezvous between the New Alliance Army in Greenhill and Flik. For the duration of the New Alliance Army's mission in Greenhill, she would attempt to be with Flik whenever possible.

She helped Shin and Teresa Wisemail by purchasing and delivering supplies to their hideout in Greenhill Forest and it was her who helped convince Teresa not to surrender herself to the Highland Army.

She joined the New Alliance Army after Teresa escaped from Greenhill, where she continued to bother Flik. Despite Flik's extreme lack of romantic interest in Nina, the two did become somewhat closer over a series of night-time talks on the roof of the New Alliance Army Headquarters.

Once the Dunan Unification War ended, Nina attempted to follow Flik but eventually lost his trail. She returned to New Leaf Academy…

recruitment: Joins automatically after the Greenhill events.

name: Oulan
description: Oulan (オウラン, Ouran) is a character in Suikoden II who also appears in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2. She is a powerful bodyguard hailing from the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Oulan was a bodyguard for hire who combined her impressive fighting technique with her stylish sensibilities. She wielded the Angry Dragon Rune in combat, making her even more powerful. She was also compassionate and could agree to work to protect a client regardless of payment or compensation if she felt inclined to.

As someone who fought to protect others rather than to attack, Oulan found a special meaning in her work as a bodyguard, as opposed to fighting as a mercenary. She saw a similar promise and heart in the leader of the New Alliance Army after helping him dispatch a gang of thugs who had become notorious for harassing women in Kuskus Town. Afterwards, she would offer to assist the force, free of charge. 

In battle, her bodyguard skills would ensure that any allies defeated on the battlefield would be protected until they could retreat. While at the New Alliance Army Headquarters, her attractiveness and physical strength earned her a fan-club, comprised mostly of women.

Following the war, she would remain in the new Dunan Republic as a captain within the newly formed national guard for a time before embarking on a journey to escort Sheena to the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia in that capacity. In Marid Village, she would help Nash fight against the feral black dragon attacking the village, her strength and Fire Dragon Rune able to daunt even a foe as powerful as a dragon.

trivia:
• Although Oulan's measurement is given as "96-60-89" (38-24-35 in inches) in the original Japanese version, the English-language localisation gives it as 90-60-89, cutting six centimeters from her bust.
• Incidentally, although Richmond reports the memo with Oulan's measurements, the note itself belonged to Sheena and was a visual estimation, not a hard measurement.
recruitment: Enter Kuskus with at least one female in the party and NO males except Riou. Speak with the old lady nearby the entrance to the village, and then cross the bridge next to the inn.

name: Pete
description: Pete (ピート, Pīto) is a minor character in Suikoden II. He is the son of Hilda and Alex.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Pete was the only child of Alex and Hilda. He was a kindly child who cherished his mother and father.

He spent his early years in Muse City with his parents and moved with them when they opened the White Deer Inn outside Muse. He was brought to South Window City by his parents when Muse fell to the Highland Army and he would come along with his folks when they joined the New Alliance Army.

Due to his isolation at the White Deer Inn he generally had a difficult time making friends with other children, though he sometimes played with Pilika, the two being of similar age. At New Alliance Army Headquarters he was able to make friends with some of the other children there.

Following the Dunan Unification War, Pete returned to the White Deer Inn with his parents and the three lived a happy life together.

name: Pohl
description: Pohl (ポール, Pōru) is a minor character in Suikoden II. He is a young man who works at Viktor's Mercenary Fortress.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Pohl was a boy who served at Viktor's Mercenary Fortress in southeastern Muse Principality. His father had been a miner but he had died in an accident in the previous year. With no other family, Pohl would wander for a time, ending up in Ryube Village where he encountered Viktor and joined the company.

He was a gentle lad, assigned to take care of Genkaku's son when he was taken prisoner. He would remind the hero of Sulis, a boy he knew from the Unicorn Brigade.

He took a shine to the hero and helped him acclimatize to his newfound situation, even advocating for better treatment and rations for the hero.

He would be brutally murdered by Luca Blight in a brave attempt to protect Pilika during the Highland Army attack on the fortress.

name: RanRan
description: RanRan (ランラン, Ranran) is an enemy who appears in Suikoden II. She is also a recruitable character in Suikoden STAR LEAP.

name: Raura
description: Raura (ラウラ, Raura) is a character in Suikoden II and Genso Suikogaiden Vol.1. Raura is an directionally challenged scribe who creates magical scrolls.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Raura was a scroll maker, a rare vocation, who made magical scrolls by transferring the power of rune orbs into vellum. She was an old friend of Jeane, though like Jeane her background is shrouded in mystery.

Raura had travelled to the City-States of Jowston during the Dunan Unification War to visit Jeane. However, Raura had an extreme lack of directional sense. Encountering Nash Latkje in the forests near Greenhill City, she would ask directions to Two River City before setting off in the wrong direction. As such, she eventually found herself lost in Tinto City instead.

After meeting the leader of the New Alliance Army by chance, Raura would be guided to the New Alliance Army Headquarters where Jeane was also serving. There, she put her scroll making talents to use by opening a scroll shop in the castle. Like Jeane, many would visit her store just to look at her, with people admiring her from the shop's second floor balcony.

After the Dunan Unification War, Raura headed north out of a misguided desire to reach the southern Toran Republic.

trivia: • In the Italian HDR translation, her name is correctly translated as "Laura".
recruitment: Talk to her in Tinto and tell her you have Jeane recruited. (You'll need to recruit Jeane to do this)

name: Retso
description: Retso (レツオウ, Retsuou) is a character who appears in Suikoden II and Suikoden V. He is one of the four lords of the Black Dragon Cooking Clan and the father of Shun Min.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Suikoden V
Retso was a wandering chef, traveling with his daughter, Shun Min, aspiring to find the fish needed to replicate the legendary "Mythical Fish Sashimi" his cooking master had once prepared for him.

Retso was a specialist in fish dishes, wielding dual knives in order to give himself greater speed and control over his technique. His search for the proper fish for this legendary dish led him to the rivers of Falena, eventually taking a job in Raftfleet.

At Ceras Lake, the Prince of Falena would catch what could only be described as a "strange fish" but was, in actuality, the rare fish needed for Retso's Mythical Fish Sashimi. When prepared, however, the resulting dish tasted awful, Shun Min not even being able to finish her serving.

Puzzled by the disparity in what he had prepared compared to his master's work, he would join the Imperial City Recapture Army in order to investigate the matter further at Ceras Lake.

While serving in the Recapture Army, Retso bounded with Logg, another father in the army, about their daughters. Retso felt a great deal of pride for his daughter Shun Min, who he considered very spirited and quite lovely, but felt guilt that ever since her mother had died, Shun Min had stopped trying to be a kid, and instead tried to act all grown up simply to spare him any trouble, making him feel like a poor father.

Many in the army enjoyed Retso's dishes, especially Nikea, although she sometimes wished Retso would involve more meats and less fish. The detective Oboro considered Retso's knife handling and presentation a sheer work of art, especially when it came to dealing with his specialty, sashimi.

Retso continued to study his master's Mystical Fish Sashimi, making some progress during the war, noting that the secret may have nothing to do…

trivia: • Announced as "Retso of Byako", the phrase is more correctly translated as "White Tiger Retso."
recruitment: After the coronation ceremony and your HQ is upgraded (combined scene), talk to Retso and learn about the Mythical Fish Sashimi. Catch a Weird Fish in Subala's mini-game and bring it to Retso in the Raftfleet Inn.

name: Revier
description: Revier (レビア, Rebia) is a minor character mentioned in Suikoden II. He was the first mayor of Muse City.

sections.title: Story
sections.body: Revier was the first mayor of Muse City once it became independent in 185. His term was marked by border disputes with the Dunan Monarchy.

name: Richmond
description: Richmond (リッチモンド, Ricchimondo) is a character in Suikoden II. Richmond is a detective who aided the New Alliance Army with his investigative skills.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Richmond is a self-styled "hard-boiled detective", though others rarely called him as such, and the self-professed best private eye in Radat Town. Though he appeared and acted odd, his ability and commitment to the job was genuine. His coat was made from thick buffalo leather from the Grasslands.

He was originally from South Window City but preferred the atmosphere in Radat. While visiting the town during the Gate Rune War, he had solved the mystery of who had attempted to take Milan Fiori's life during the noble's visit and made the decision to move there full-time.

Solving that locked-room mystery also helped to boost his reputation as a detective. It is during this case that he met a cat, Bianca, who he took in because no one else would due to her perceived ugliness.

During the Dunan Unification War, Richmond helped the New Alliance Army recruit Shu by gathering info on his habits. But knowing that wasn't enough to complete the job to his client's satisfaction, he would help behind the scenes, planting the coin that Apple would find to recruit the strategist.

Later on in the war, Richmond agreed to join the New Alliance Army provided its leader won a heads/tails coin flip. After losing several times, due to a trick coin, the leader eventually figured out the trick. Richmond then agreed to join the New Alliance Army, as the hero had shown his ability to see through such cheap tricks.

At the New Alliance Army Headquarters, Richmond provided investigations on future recruits, as well as information gathered from its inhabitants. For more bestial comrades, he would have the assistance of his cat, Bianca, and Badeaux's help in interpreting her words.

Following the war, Richmond continued his private eye career in Radat.

trivia:
• One of Richmond's Suggestion Box letters uses the phrase "Loose lips sink ships"; this is a reference to a famous phrase from World War II, which meant Americans should avoid talking about ship movements, lest German submarines use the information against them.
• Suikoden I&II HD Remaster removes Richmond's cigarette from his lips in his portrait (though this was barely noticable in the original PlayStation release due to its resolution). It also removes his ashtray and cigarette stubs from the table on his investigation screen.
recruitment: Speak with him in Radat in front of the appraisal shop and do his coin toss challenge. After losing, go to the tavern and speak to the man straight to the left after entering, and he'll give you a coin. Go back and offer Richmond to use the coin, and he will join.

name: Ridley Wizen
description: Ridley Wizen (リドリー・ワイゼン, Ridorī Waizen) is a character in Suikoden II. Ridley is the General of Two River City and is the leader of Two River's Kobold district.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Ridley Wizen is the leader of the Two River City Kobold district. A motivated soldier and strict and honest to a fault, Ridley didn't miss a training session for years. He held the respect of his soldiers and was considered a general with the highest command ability by Shu.

During the Dunan Unification War, Ridley became ensnared in a Highland Kingdom plot that exploited the tensions present in Two River. Ridley, after finding a "secret communique" between Kiba Windamier and Makai that offered a peace treaty in exchange for Two River's Kobold District.
 
However, Ridley was moved by the attempts of the Winged Horde to fight alone and Ridley realized that despite their differences, the people of Two River were still all citizens who wished to defend their home.

Following the defense of Two River, Ridley joined the New Alliance Army and fought in several of the war's battles. Ridley was a brave officer, unafraid to risk his own life for what he believed in.

This was shown when he and Shu used Highland's own racial tensions plot against them during a battle in Radat Town by having Ridley feint offense at a decision to place Kobold troops on the front line, then attack the Highland Army's flanks during the next battle. He was also not afraid to risk his life to help protect the hero, believing in him greatly.

Following the war, Ridley returned to Two River and continued as the leader of the Kobold army.

trivia:
• Following the Highland Kingdom's conquest of Radat Town, the player has the option of either rescuing Ridley or letting him be captured by Highland forces in one of the war battles. Depending on the outcome, the plot will change very slightly.
• If the player chooses to tun away with Nanami in Tinto, Ridley will be killed searching for the missing hero and Boris, his son, will take his place.
• The above also occurs in the official Suikoden II novelization, Ridley's death and the hero's guilt forming part of his emotional development.
recruitment: Joins automatically after allying with Two-River.

name: Rikimaru
description: Rikimaru (リキマル, Rikimaru) is a character in Suikoden II. He is a wandering swordsman in search for revenge, although he is something of a glutton as well.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Rikimaru was a carefree and some would say listless wanderer who enjoyed sleeping and eating in equal measure. Wandering through the City-States of Jowston, would sometimes visit Ryube Village and Coronet. Rikimaru was found collapsed outside an inn, whining about his hunger.

The hero would take him to an inn where he would proceed to fill his stomach with several courses of food. As Rikimaru had no potch, having apparently given his money to some beggars, he was most grateful when the 3,000 potch tab he had acquired was paid off for him and he joined the New Alliance Army.

His vow to serve the New Alliance Army seemed to be the only thing he took seriously. Although said to be searching for revenge, Rikimaru's primary concern while serving with the New Alliance Army seemed to be food. Before journeying to Jowston, while in Gregminster, he ate for two days straight and had to escape from the inn during the night as he couldn't pay his bill.

In fact, his favourite thing to do was to eat until he just about burst, then spend an entire day sleeping it all off. Despite this, Rikimaru was still a skilled warrior, serving as an infantry captain for the New Alliance Army.

Following the Dunan Unification War, Rikimaru headed north, still in search of the object of his revenge.

recruitment: In Ryube village, you'll find him on the ground next to the town gate. Speak to him and offer to help him, then pay for his meal (3000 potch). Alternatively, he will be in Coronet in the same predicament if you did not get him in Ryube before it was destroyed.

name: Rina
description: Rina (リィナ, Ryina) is a character in Suikoden II who also appears in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2. Rina is a performer in a traveling circus group with Eilie and Bolgan.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Rina was a traveling performer hailing from the Grasslands, although she had been constantly traveling since she could walk. Her and Eilie's parents died in a bandit attack when she was a child and she has been taking care of her sister since, gaining far more experience than her age would suggest.

Since then Rina, along with her sister, Eilie, and later their friend, Bolgan, formed a traveling entertainment troupe in which Rina would perform as a fortuneteller. Her charm as well as her ability to observe people as part of her divination act were often key to the safety and survival of her remaining family.

Rina and the rest of the troupe stopped through the City-States of Jowston. After performing in the town of Ryube Village, Rina, Eilie, and Bolgan travelled briefly with the hero in order to reach the Highland Kingdom. Though briefly stopped by guards, Rina was able to use her sex appeal to convince them to allow the party through.

However, business in Highland proved poor and Rina and her party returned to Jowston, where they once again met the hero and decided to travel onward with him, eventually becoming part of the New Alliance Army. A traveler at heart, following the Dunan Unification War, Rina left with Eilie and Bolgan, leading the group of performers around the world.

Some time later, in Milit Village, she would help confront and defeat a band of thieves styling themselves as the Fire Bringer with Nash Latkje and her former comrades from the New Alliance Army. Having experienced the loss of her parents to bandits, she was determined to prevent a similar disaster from occurring.

trivia: • If you try and pass through the North Swallow Pass after Nanami joins, she will try to replicate Rina's infamous convincing of the guards, but with more violence and less successful results.
recruitment: Joins automatically in Coronet after the events in Muse.

name: RinRin
description: RinRin (リンリン, Rinrin) is an enemy who appears in Suikoden II. She is also a recruitable character in Suikoden STAR LEAP.

name: Robert Blight
description: Robert Blight (ロベール・ブライト, Robēru Buraito) is a minor character mentioned in Suikoden II. He was the 21st King of the Highland Kingdom.

name: Rosa Atreides
description: Rosa Atreides (ローザ・アトレイド, Rōza Atoreido) is a minor character in Suikoden II. She is the mother of Jowy Atreides.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Rosa was the biological mother of Jowy and his only living relative. She would become separated from Jowy's father and would later marry Marcel Atreides under the direction of her parents (Jowy's maternal grandparents).

Her status in the family was quite weak, and although she was concerned about how Jowy was treated by Marcel and Marco, she could do little to help her son. Although he had little connection to his new family, Jowy loved his mother deeply.

name: Rowd
description: Rowd (ラウド, Raudo) is a minor antagonist in Suikoden II, who also appears in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.1. Rowd is the captain of the Unicorn Brigade and later the leader of the Royal Highland Army 4th Company.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Rowd was born in Kyaro Town. When he was a youth, he volunteered for the Highland Army, becoming a member of the Unicorn Brigade.

Due to excellent performance, he would go on to be appointed Captain of the Unicorn Brigade. This was a position with little hope of advancement, however, which frustrated Rowd who had aspired to greatness through his training.

It is said that he was the brother of a blind girl living in Kyaro Town, but the facts are not clear on the matter. The blind girl would talk about how her brother was working hard to improve his situation and make a lot of money so he could take her to the Toran Republic for treatment as her condition was not incurable. As a person, he had a belief in his own strength but lacked a sense of pride.

As captain of the youth brigade, Rowd was respected by his younger subordinates and he in turn treated his charges well enough. He was stricter with older soldiers under his command, such as Jowy Atreides or his friend.

Rowd was a man willing to do anything to advance his career. He had entered the military dreaming of a glamorous life in the Imperial Capital rather than a posting in his small country town and so, he would communicate with Luca Blight, aiding him in his plot to wipe out the boys of his own Unicorn Brigade in order to fabricate a reason for war against the City-States of Jowston.

Still, despite Rowd's lack of hesitation for his actions, he would apologize unemotionally and directly to Jowy and his friend after they were recaptured and sentenced to death. The morning before their scheduled execution, Rowd looked upon the new day as a new dawn for his career, ruminating aloud. Finally feeling fulfilled, he failed to notice the rescue of his prisoners until it was too late.

As a result, for his plotting,…

name: Rulodia
description: Rulodia (ルロラディア, Ruroradia) is a character in Suikoden II who also appears in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2. She is the wife of Abizboah who aided the New Alliance Army during the Dunan Unification War.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Rulodia is a female kraken who lived in the depths of the Tinto Mines. She had a relationship with Abizboah similar to that of a human marriage. During the Dunan Unification War, she followed her husband into the New Alliance Army.

Following the war, she, Abizboah and Chuchara would all return home together as a family. They would eventually settle in Lake Dunan. She may have had more children in the future, though krakens only carried one at a time.

name: Ryuki
description: Ryuki (リュウキ, Ryuuki) is a minor character who appears in Suikoden II. He is a member of the Black Dragon Cooking Clan.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Ryuki was the fourth cook to challenge Hai Yo at his restaurant in the New Alliance Army Headquarters. The chef Ryuko was his younger brother. The two were raised on Black Dragon Island since childhood.

He administered the Sleeping Maiden poison to Barbara in order to force Hai Yo to duel him with the Moon Bird Recipe on the line.

After his defeat to Hai Yo in a cook-off, Ryuki committed suicide via the poison, passing on his pasta recipe.

name: Ryuko
description: Ryuko (リュウコウ, Ryuukou) is a minor character who appears in Suikoden II. He is a member of the Black Dragon Cooking Clan.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Ryuko was the sixth cook to challenge Hai Yo at his restaurant in the New Alliance Army Headquarters. He would challenge Hai Yo both for the Moon Bird Recipe and to avenge his brother, Ryuki. Like his brother, he was raised on Black Dragon Island since childhood and so had no place to return to should he be defeated.

After his defeat to Hai Yo in a cook-off, Ryuko committed suicide via poison, passing on his gratin recipe. This incident affected Hai Yo deeply, leading him to consider renouncing his ideals as a chef.

name: Sasarai
description: Sasarai (ササライ, Sasarai) is a minor character in Suikoden II and Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2 and a major one in Suikoden III. Sasarai is a bishop within the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia. Sasarai is the bearer of the True Earth Rune and is loyal to Hikusaak, the High Priest of Harmonia. He has participated in several wars as a general.

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
Like his brother, Luc, Sasarai has green eyes and brown hair that reaches his shoulders. At 166 centimeters (about 5’5”), he is a little shorter than average. His build is on the thin side of average.

Sasarai is always dressed in a Harmonian bishop’s uniform, but his uniform's appearance differs with each game. In Suikoden II, he wears a blue robe with white trim and the Harmonian insignia on the front and a large bishop’s hat with the same insignia.

In Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2, his hat is smaller, and he also has a short, dark blue cape draped about his shoulders. In Suikoden III, he does not wear a hat, and his robes have been replaced with a blue shirt with white trim with the Harmonian insignia on the front and white pants.

In Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2, he can be seen with a ring on the middle finger of his right hand. In his Suikoden III artwork, he is shown to have one on the left hand as well. His weapon is a ring called “Guardian Eye,” so these may be to enhance his magic.

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
Although Sasarai is a skilled general, he is also a very kind person who dislikes violence, and he has a strong sense of justice. His subordinates place a lot of trust in him. In turn, Sasarai is loyal to Hikusaak and his convictions.

Sasarai is also intelligent and capable of great cunning. An example of this is when, in the aftermath of the Dunan Unification War, he worked behind the scenes to help Lena Suphina save Nash Latkje's sister, Julie. He knows when to question others as well, such as when he suspected Luc (disguised as the Masked Man) had ulterior motives for wishing to serve Hikusaak.

Although Sasarai appears to be formal and fastidious, this is due to his attendants helping him out. He’s actually very nonchalant.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Sasarai was created in SY 444 along with his brother, Luc, as a clone of Hikusaak and a host for the True Earth Rune. He was raised in the One Temple as a bishop and was kept ignorant of his true nature. At some point in his childhood, he and Luc were involved in some sort of incident at the temple. It appears they saw each other when Leknaat appeared to take Luc away from the temple but the exact details are unknown.

Sasarai did not have many friends in his life, but his sub-Commander Dios tried his best to fulfill the role and look out for Sasarai's well-being when he could. 
Involvement in the Dunan Unification War
thumb|220px|Sasarai finds himself attacked by Luc during the [[Dunan Unification War.]]During the Dunan Unification War in 460, Sasarai was sent to aid the Highland Kingdom in the conflict. Even at this young age he was widely considered the second most powerful figure in Harmonia after Hikusaak himself.

At first, he was calm and effective as an ally, but he would begin to feel uneasy due to Luc's presence among the New Alliance Army. Later, Luc confronted him directly on the battlefield, devastating Sasarai’s troops with a powerful spell. Although Sasarai demanded to know what connection Luc had to him, Luc refused to answer Sasarai’s question. Sasarai managed to escape Luc’s attack using his True Earth Rune and did not reappear during the war.

Following the war, back in Crystal Valley, Sasarai discreetly assisted Lena Suphina in saving Nash Latkje’s younger sister, Julie, from Zaj Quiroz. This would not only help keep Harmonia politically stable; Zaj's schemes would have culminated in an attempt to bring down the government, it would also cause Nash to owe a favor that he would have to pay back himself.
War of the Champions
thumb|left|220px|Sasarai…

trivia:
• Sasarai is one of a select few characters whose weapon name does not change as it levels up.
• Sasarai learns the unique Magic skills Precision, Pale Gate Magic, and Magic Repel. A dedicated mage, Sasarai does poorly in all Melee skills but outstanding in Magic. Unsurprisingly he can reach S level in Earth Magic.
• Sasarai was referred to as a “High Priest” (神官長, “chief priest”) in Suikoden II; this would later be corrected to “Bishop” (神官将, “priest-general”). It was also incorrectly stated in Suikoden II that Sasarai gave the Beast Rune to Highland, but this would have happened before Sasarai was born.
recruitment: Joins automatically during Chapter 5.

name: Sasuke
description: Sasuke (サスケ, Sasuke) is a character in Suikoden II. Sasuke is a young ninja who has a crush on Kasumi.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Sasuke was a member of the Rokkaku ninja clan, known for his exceptional skills and dedication to improving his abilities. Unaware of his parentage as per Rokkaku tradition, he was the child of former Rokkaku Vice-Chief, Hien.

As a swallow he was trained by Mondo. Although he sometimes found Mondo's strictness tiring, he looked up to and respected the older ninja as a father figure. As a child he was mischievous and often tried to skive off from more mundane duties like assisting in the reconstruction of Rokkaku.

When Kasumi returned from the Gate Rune War and initially turned down the role of Vice-Chief, Sasuke was shocked. He developed a resentment for the Toran Liberation Army, feeling they had hurt her confidence and bullied her for her inexperience.

In the past, driven by his desire to become stronger, Sasuke pursued the acquisition of a rune. In his quest, he assumed the role of a bandit in an attempt to gain a powerful rune from merchants and black marketeers passing through the Banner Pass.

On one occasion, he crossed paths with the leader of the Toran Liberation Army, engaging in a fight. Though McDohl easily deflected and evaded Sasuke's attacks, it would be Kasumi's intersession and clearing up of the situation that ended the confrontation.

As the events of the Dunan Unification War unfolded, Sasuke, who had been trained as a swallow ninja until the age of 12, lacked practical experience. Despite this, he possessed great talent and a relentless determination to improve his abilities to make Rokkaku a place to rival the great Liberation Army.

Following a visit by the hero, Rokkaku leader Hanzo ordered Sasuke to join the New Alliance Army alongside Mondo. Mondo would tease Sasuke about being happy to work with Kasumi again, much to the flustered youth's…

recruitment: Enter Rokkaku Hamlet, which can be accessed on a hidden path on the south edge of the screen right after the ladders on the forest path to Gregminster from Banner (4th screen). If you have Kasumi in the party Mondo will join, then go outside and talk to Sasuke to get him. If you recruited Valeria, you will have to wait until your castle is Level 4, but, they will both join right away.

name: Seed
description: Seed (シード, Shīdo) is a supporting antagonist in Suikoden II who also appears in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.1 and Genso Suikoden Card Stories.

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body: Befitting his fiery personality, Seed had medium-length red hair and amber, almost red, eyes. He wore a long white long coat, the color of Highland, with red piping throughout over white armor, including gauntlets and greaves, and black pants. He also wielded a sword in his right hand.

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
Seed was an impulsive officer of the Highland Army. He had a strong preference for action over strategizing which occasionally lent him to make hasty decisions. He always fought with his sword at the forefront of battle, befitting his personality. He held great confidence in his own abilities. Although he had a passion for battle, he lacked Luca Blight's ruthless love for slaughter.

Although his hot-headed nature contrasted strongly with that of Culgan, they nonetheless had a strong bond, brought about by their patriotism for the Highland Kingdom. Like Culgan, he was not from a prestigious military family and had worked his way up the ranks from a common soldier. He was said to be slightly superior to Culgan in terms of swordsmanship. It is said that Seed had a lover in Highland.

As someone who benefited from the Highland Army's extreme meritocratic ethos himself, he held no jealousy or envy over Jowy Atreides's rapid rise to prominence and pledged his allegiance freely and without ulterior motive.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Seed was a Highland general, often paired alongside Culgan, his contrasting comrade-in-arms. He had advanced in the Highland Army, despite his relatively young age, due to the extreme meritocracy implemented by Luca Blight.

During the Dunan Unification War, he formed part of the Highland Army offensive, serving under Solon Jhee. Aggressive, he always advocated attack over caution during the early stages of the war.

During the war, Zaj Quiroz on behalf of the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia, requested Luca Blight send men to track down Nash Latkje in Greenhill Forest. Together with Culgan, Seed was able to bring Nash down with their combination attacks but because he had been poisoned during the fight by Nash's Twin Snake Sword Großer Fluss, Culgan agreed to let Nash go in exchange for the antidote, disregarding Luca's orders.

As the war continued, Seed and Culgan came to feel that Luca Blight's continued presence would bring catastrophe to the Highland Kingdom. Thus, in Greenhill City, just after they had warned Rowd off of seeking revenge against his former subordinate, Seed pledged his sword to Jowy Atreides in order to protect Highland.

However, although Jowy would overthrow Luca and Seed would continue to serve to the best of his abilities under King Jowy's rule as commander of the 4th Highland Army, in the end Highland would fall and Seed perished alongside Culgan in L'Renouille Palace attempting to fight off the New Alliance Army.

They both knew that the battle was decided and Highland had fallen but were nonetheless determined to protect what they believed in until the end. As he died, he held onto his joy in serving his country, having lived his life with no regrets.

trivia: • What would become Seed's design was initially submitted for Shin before being repurposed.

name: Shiki
description: Shiki (シキ, Shiki) is a minor character who appears in Suikoden II. He is a member of the Black Dragon Cooking Clan.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Shiki was the third cook to challenge Hai Yo at his restaurant in the New Alliance Army Headquarters. Shiki had an eerie laugh and smile on his face at all times.

After his defeat to Hai Yo in a cook-off, Shiki passed on his cake recipe before committing suicide via poison. Before dying, he revealed to Hai Yo that he was an assassin from the Black Dragon Cooking Clan and that they had tracked him down.

name: Shilo
description: Shilo (シロウ, Shirō) is a character in Suikoden II and Genso Suikogaiden Vol.1. He is a gambler with a penchant for Chinchirorin.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Shilo was a dedicated and seasoned gambler, captivated by the lifestyle akin to a moth to a flame. He established his own gambling den within the Lakewest inn, where he indulged in various games of chance. Notably, he held a long-standing rivalry with Gaspar, engaging in intense gambling battles with him in the Greenhill Forest during the Dunan Unification War.

When Shilo initially encountered the hero in his Lakewest den, he exhibited dismissiveness towards the youth. However, he became intrigued by the hero's unique demeanor and eventually decided to listen to him. Upon receiving an invitation to join the New Alliance Army, Shilo accepted the offer on the condition that the leader could win 5,000 potch from him in a game of Chinchirorin.

After the hero succeeded in the challenge, Shilo agreed to join the army, hoping to ride the hero's wave of good luck, and established a Chinchirorin stall at the New Alliance Army Headquarters. Aside from Chinchirorin, he was also a hanafuda player.

Throughout his tenure with the New Alliance Army, Shilo continued to pursue his passion for gambling, though some accounts suggest occasional instances of cheating. In addition to his gambling skills, he showcased unexpected combat prowess in both major battles and smaller encounters, earning him a position as a special forces member within the army.

Following the conclusion of the Dunan Unification War, Shilo remained at the New Alliance Army Headquarters and achieved considerable success in his gambling business, continuing his run as a skilled and prosperous gambler.

recruitment: Win 5000 potch from him at a game of Chinchorin (Castle Level 2)

name: Shin
description: Shin (シン, Shin) is a character in Suikoden II who also appears in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.1. He is a bodyguard for the House of Wisemail.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Shin served as the bodyguard of Teresa Wisemail, in her capacity as Mayor of Greenhill City, as hired by the Wisemail family. He is a practitioner of the Eight Death Sword and wielded the Tarantula sword, utilizing his Spider Slay technique to great effect in combat.

During the Highland Army advance on the city during the Dunan Unification War, he was the only trained swordsman in the Greenhill army, which was made up entirely of citizen soldiers. He would cooperate with Nash Latkje and when Teresa was captured by the rebellious Muse soldiers, he went with Nash to rescue her.

Following Greenhill's occupation, Shin helped Teresa hide in the forests behind the city and was willing to lay down his life to ensure her safety. Once Teresa had been convinced of the need to leave Greenhill by the New Alliance Army, Shin prevented Highland soldiers from pursuing Teresa, all too willing to risk his life to secure her escape. After this, Fitcher managed to help Shin escape to the New Alliance Army Headquarters, where he was reunited with Teresa.

In reality, Shin's loyalty was to Teresa, rather than her office. He was absolutely loyal to Teresa and was willing to give up his life to protect her. His dedication had grown into a sincere love, but he never fully expressed those feelings as he felt unworthy of her as a swordsman.

As such, following the war, he would leave Greenhill in order to continue honing his skills as a swordsman. He never returned to the city.

trivia: • Shin is the only character in the entire Suikoden series to have a Spider Slay Rune.
recruitment: Joins automatically after the Greenhill events.

name: Shiro
description: Shiro (シロ, Shiro) is a character in Suikoden II. He is a wolfdog who lives in the forest with Kinnison.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Shiro is a wolfdog who lived in the Ryube forest with Kinnison. Shiro, a mutt combination of both wolf and dog, was said to be highly intelligent and able to distinguish between good and evil. His father was likely a white wolf of legend, explaining his gift.

Kinnison had found the young Shiro by the dog's dying mother after a storm and had raised him ever since, the two becoming firm partners. As such, the two had been together for a long time and Kinnison seemed able to understand the meaning of the noises that Shiro made.

His favorite food was, perhaps unsurprisingly, meat. Kinnison would allow him to hunt on his own as needed. The blue cloth tied around his left foreleg was a gift of friendship from Kinnison, with a good luck charm sown inside.

During the early stages of the Dunan Unification War, Kinnison and Shiro encountered the hero in the forest after the hero had rescued a baby bird. Sensing a respect for nature from him and dismayed at the damage the advancing Highland Army were causing, Shiro and Kinnison both agreed to join the New Alliance Army.

In Muse City, he lost Kinnison's trail briefly and was walking along a back street when he ran into Nash Latkje. Perhaps sensing something about the man, he let out a single cry and walked away. He would soon find Kinnison and the two continued to stay with the New Alliance Army.

Shiro struggled with living at the New Alliance Army Headquarters at first, but gradually came to like the castle. He even gave Gengen rides on his back. Shiro's speed and power made him a vicious enemy for the Highland Army to encounter throughout the war.

Following the Dunan Unification War, Shiro returned to Ryube Forest with Kinnison, always by the side of his master.

recruitment: Joins with Kinnison.

name: Shu
description: Shu (シュウ, Shū) is a main character in Suikoden II and Genso Suikoden Card Stories.

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
Shu is a handsome man with a refined and composed appearance. He has long, sleek black hair that is partially tied back, allowing some strands to frame his face. He has sharp, well-defined facial features, including high cheekbones and a strong jawline.

He wears a long, fitted white coat with intricate light blue patterns on one side, buttoned down the front, and layered over a high-collared shirt. A deep red scarf is wrapped loosely around his neck.

Over the coat, he has a dark brown, flowing cloak draped over his shoulders. His outfit is completed with a set of belts around his waist, neatly tailored pants, and black shoes.

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
Shu is a brilliant man and a first-class strategist. While studying under Mathiu Silverberg, Shu met Apple when the latter was 11 years old. He came to think of her as a little sister, while Apple was taken in by Shu, developing a crush on him.

As Mathiu's most senior student, Shu often studied alone and looked after the other children while Mathiu was away. He would also travel to Gregminster on Mathiu's behalf to purchase books, as Mathiu preferred to avoid the city.

Though taught by Mathiu Silverberg, Shu followed a different ideology than his teacher, preferring to use his knowledge for profit. Shu and Mathiu were unable to get along and Mathiu eventually expelled Shu. Shu never saw Mathiu again.

As a trader, Shu developed a vast fortune, trading with places as far as the Island Nations and his fame as a trader spread as far as the Toran Republic. His success was said to be because he handled every detail of his trades himself. Shu also had a reputation for politeness and treated his servants well.
