As a strategist, Shu had absolute confidence in his abilities, and was sometimes seen as a boor because of his strong belief. However, as he knew human habits and emotions better than most anyone else, it could be said that even this was an affectation designed to enhance his prowess as a strategist.

Shu did whatever was necessary to guarantee the success of the New Alliance Army, including sending Pilika with a rescue party during a peace conference ambush in Muse City and asking Kiba Windamier to take on a suicide mission during the attack on the Matilda Knightdom.

In many ways, his ideology was similar to Leon Silverberg's, though the two had not met prior to the Dunan Unification War. Shu's thoughts on this gradually changed as the war progressed.

In the end,…

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Shu is an accomplished strategist and trader. He trained under Mathiu Silverberg but was expelled due to their ideological differences. Shu felt that winning at all costs was the prime goal of a strategist, while Mathiu felt this was selfish and there were goals beyond mere military victory to strive for.

This came to a head when Odessa Silverberg requested Mathiu's help. Feeling that Mathiu was selfish and cowardly in his refusal, he left the school. He did not join the Toran Liberation Army, feeling theirs was a hopeless cause, but eventually settled in Radat Town where he became a wealthy and famous trader.

During the early stages of the Dunan Unification War, Apple approached Shu to ask him to join the New Alliance Army. Though at first hesitant to join the war and hostile to the idea, Shu accepted after being impressed by Apple's dedication and willingness to go to any means to acquire his power.

Arriving shortly thereafter at North Window, Shu's commanding presence and swift analysis of the situation quickly dispelled the gloom hanging over the surrounded forces at the castle.

As the chief strategist of the New Alliance Army, Shu came up with many ingenious strategies to help propel the New Alliance Army to victory. He frequently overcame superior numbers through the use of psychological warfare, such as taking advantage of recently conquered South Window soldiers' fears that they would be executed by the Highland Army during a battle against Solon Jhee.

He would also fake discord between himself and Ridley in order to have Ridley's unit create an ambush in a subsequent battle, and used a fire attack to trap Leon Silverberg after anticipating Leon's movements in the Battle of L'Renouille.

Following the death of Luca Blight and Jowy Atreides' ascension to…

recruitment: Joins automatically after establishing the headquarters.

name: Shun Min
description: Shun Min (シュンミン, Shunmin) is a character who appears in Suikoden V and is mentioned in Suikoden II. She is the daughter of Retso, one of the four lords of the Black Dragon Cooking Clan.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Shun Min was a young girl who helped her father Retso as he toiled in the cafeteria in Raftfleet. She was a gentle, kind girl who loved the fish she raised for consumption, a skill she learned from her mother.

Because of this, she was insistent that people fully eat the fish that had given up their lives. The loss of her mother at a young age also gave Shun Min a maturity beyond her years as she strove to support her father.

She would join the Imperial City Recapture Army along with her father, helping to raise the fish at Ceras Lake for her father's kitchen. At the castle, she worked excitedly but also found time to make friends and relax, taking in Chisato's puppet show and spending time with Eresh, Lelei and Haswar. She would declare that she would only ever marry someone more skilled at cooking than her own father.

Following the Sun Rune War, Shun Min grew into a beautiful young woman, eventually becoming the lover of Hai Yo while her father became one of the Black Dragon Cooking Clan's four grand masters. She opposed Jinkai's Dragon Plan and so stole the Moon Bird Recipe.

However, in her attempt to steal the recipe, Shun Min was fatally wounded and died after passing on the Moon Bird Recipe to Hai Yo, imploring him to keep it out of the hands of the Black Dragon Cooking Clan.

trivia: • If she died in the same year Suikoden II takes place, Shun Min would have died at the age of 23.
recruitment: Joins with Retso.

name: Sid
description: Sid (シド, Shido) is a character in Suikoden II. Sid is a Winged Horde who enjoys teasing children, particularly Chaco.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Sid, a member of the Winged Horde from Two River City's Winged Horde district, grew up with peculiar tendencies that both intrigued and disturbed the townspeople. When he was a child, he would listen to the strange and eerie stories shared by his grandfather of his wanderings.

His routine harassment of Chaco, a fellow Winged Horde, persisted from childhood into adulthood. Sid had a reputation for sharing scary stories with children, leading to unsettling reactions.

That said, many saw Sid's actions as his way of befriending and toughing up children. He had looked after Chaco when he was a baby and had even made a ball for the young boy.

He had agreed to participate as the masked men for the community's Spirit Festival but quickly grew bored of giving the same scares he did in his day-to-day life. Before he continued his scares, he was confronted by the spirit of his deceased grandfather admonishing him. This silenced Sid for a time but he soon perked up with ideas for new scary stories.

At some point, Sid claimed he would leave Two River City but instead moved into the city's sewers, seeking solitude. Few people knew his whereabouts in the sewers, considering him elusive and secretive.

When the hero and Chaco ventured into the sewers, they encountered Sid, and upon learning of Chaco's involvement in the New Alliance Army, Sid decided to join as well. He continued his habit of teasing and scaring Chaco within the army, even extending his eerie tactics to others, such as sharing spine-chilling tales with children and sending cursed chain letters.

Despite his eccentricities, Sid proved to be a formidable fighter, wielding his spear with surprising strength and skill during the Dunan Unification War. After the war's conclusion, he returned to Two River City, where he…

recruitment: Once your castle is at level 2, go to the Two River Sewers with Chaco in the active party and find his room (take the passage behind the waterfall), where he will join.

name: Sierra Mikain
description: Sierra Mikain (シエラ・ミケーネ, Shiera Mikēne) is a character in Suikoden II who also appears in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.1.

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
Sierra's appearance is that of a pretty girl, but her behavior and words convey a power that is extraordinary. She is a vampire, over 800 years old.

She had an exceptionally pale complexion, long white hair, and unusually red eyes. She wore a purple shawl over a short blue cloak. Underneath she wore white frilly clothes, white stockings, and blue shoes.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Sierra Mikain was the original bearer of the Moon Rune, a True Rune of immense power. She discovered the rune in the year -375 and it transformed her into a vampire. With the rune's abilities, Sierra created a coven of vampires, including Rein Penenberg, one of her first sired followers.

For centuries, she and her coven lived in harmony in Blue Moon Village, their existence marked by peace and prosperity. However, this tranquility was shattered when Neclord, a young member of the coven, stole the Moon Rune and fled, unleashing uncontrollable bloodlust among the vampires.

Without the rune's stabilizing influence, many vampires, including Rein, turned to preying on humans. Sierra, determined to restore balance, pursued Neclord and the rogue vampires over the centuries.

During her journey, she encountered Nash Latkje, a young man who joined her as a companion and baggage handler. Together, they confronted and defeated Rein Penenberg, Sierra's former lover, who had succumbed to his bloodlust.

Later, during the Dunan Unification War, Sierra allied with the New Alliance Army to confront Neclord once more after he attacked Tinto Principality. Using her connection to the Moon Rune, she located and reclaimed it from Neclord.

She would leave Neclord's ultimate fate to Viktor, who had his own personal vendetta against the vampire. Sierra's decision to forgo personal vengeance reflected her wisdom and detachment, as well as her confidence in Viktor's ability.

Following the destruction of Neclord, Sierra chose to remain with the New Alliance Army, contributing her immense power to their special forces. After the war's conclusion, she found joy in observing humanity, feeling no urgency to return to Blue Moon Village.

trivia: • Despite regaining the Moon Rune during the game, Sierra never uses it in battle.
recruitment: Joins automatically after the Tinto/Neclord events.

name: Sigfried
subtype: Tenman Star/Tentai Star — Star of Destiny
description: Siegfried (ジークフリート, Jīkufurīto) is a supporting character in Suikoden II. A unicorn living within the Unicorn Woods outside of Kobold Village, Siegfried issues the Test of the Unicorn to those warriors brave enough to attempt it.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Siegfried is a sacred presence that has dwelled in the Unicorn Woods near Kobold Village, known only as a legend. It was said he only appeared in the presence of a pure maiden and that deep within the woods there was a village of unicorns. That said, Siegfried himself had never met another unicorn.

Labeling himself a member of the "White Conclave", he was also the bearer of the rare White Saint Rune. It was kobold lore that he tested the bravery of warriors by means of a trial, the Test of the Unicorn. Siegfried himself had never tested anyone in this manner, however. His only interest was in maidens.

Hix and Tengaar attempted to take the trial during the Dunan Unification War. Although the citizens of the Kobold Village agreed to construct a fake trial, for Hix's benefit, they were shocked when Siegfried really did appear and kidnap Tengaar. Hix stood firm and refused to do as the unicorn demanded, thus passing the trial set out for him. Satisfied in the couple's bond, Siegfried took his leave.

Later during the Dunan Unification War, with a Listening Orb from Badeaux and a maiden in tow, Siegfried was encountered once more and was convinced to join the New Alliance Army for the duration of the war. Following the war, Siegfried simply returned quietly to the forest, having little interest in anything but pure maidens.

trivia:
• His name is spelled as Sigfried rather than the more standard Siegfried in Suikoden II (1998) to fit the eight character limitation for name boxes.
• The full list of girls who can recruit Siegfried: Ayda, Eilie, Karen, Kasumi, Meg, Mikumiku, Millie, Nanami, Nina, Tengaar, Tomo, Viki and Wakaba.
• A special scene can be seen if one brings Sierra Mikain to the Unicorn Woods when attempting to recruit Siegfried. Unfortunately, Siegfried will not join if you do so.
• Siegfried's critical attack is a completely different animation than his normal attack.

name: Simone Verdricci
description: Simone Verdricci (シモーヌ・ベルドリッチ, Shimōnu Berudoricchi) is a character in Suikoden II. He is a Toran Republic aristocrat and friend of Vincent De Boule.
originalName: シモーヌ・ベルドリッチ

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
Simone wears a luxurious red coat with various decorations and a long, loose scarf. The sleeves and collar are both gold colored, and the outfit is thoroughly gorgeous. It is a sophisticated outfit that suits Simone because of his slender and elegant figure.

Simone's good aesthetic sensibilities is further evident in the white socks and pink shoes he wears to prevent it from becoming too obnoxious. He is a vegetarian, claiming it helped his sense of justice, as well as keeping him in shape.

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
Though Simone dresses and acts in a manner similar to his friend, Vincent De Boule, he does not do so in imitation. Simone always had this sense of style and mannerisms, which is what helped the two endear themselves to each other, becoming bosom friends.

That said, Simone seemed to regard almost everyone he met as a bosom friend, so perhaps this term of endearment may not have had much meaning to him. However, his commitment to Vincent, at least, was extremely genuine. He was unwilling to betray his friendship with Vincent for the sake of his own pride, shown by his willingness to drop to his hands and knees and act like a dog to recover the Rose Brooch he lost.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Simone Verdricci was a former noble of the Scarlet Moon Empire who lost his title following the establishment of the Toran Republic. He was known to be dear friends with Vincent De Boule, the two sharing similarities in temperament and fashion choices. The two did not actually know each other very long but bonded quickly due to their shared outlook on life.

During the Dunan Unification War, Simone found himself caught in the conflict while on a journey to study abroad in Crystal Valley. Unfortunately, he lost his prized possession, the Rose Brooch, during his journey.

This loss left him despondent during his stay in Radat Town until it was returned to him by the leader of the New Alliance Army. In gratitude, Simone joined the army and professed his unwavering friendship for its leader.

Later, when reuniting with Vincent, Simone would share his feelings of failure for losing his Rose Brooch. Vincent readily forgave him, saying that their friendship will never change and that was what truly mattered. Simone was moved to tears by his best friend's thoughtfulness and reaffirm his bond of friendship with Vincent.

Despite his appearance, Simone was a skilled swordsman and fought alongside Vincent De Boule against the Highland Army with great dignity. This was not as surprising as one might first assume. As an aristocrat, he had received formal training as a fighter and cavalryman.

After the war, accompanied by his dear friend Vincent, Simone continued his journey to the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia with Vincent. At some point, the two briefly crossed paths with Nash Latkje, who was inadvertently teleported to Simone's location by Viki while they were attending a tea party in a flamboyant Toran Republic residence. This soirée was also attended by Milich Oppenheimer and…

trivia:
• If you choose to ask Simone for money in payment for the Rose Brooch rather than asking him to join your army, Simone will pay you 50,000 Potch. However, you won't be able to recruit him.
• Simone's weapon names all stem from music terms. A prelude is a short piece of music, the form of which may vary from piece to piece. An intermezzo is a composition which fits between other musical or dramatic entities. A rhapsody is a one-movement work that is episodic yet integrated.
recruitment: He is below the Sluice gate in Radat, by the water. Talk to him with a Rose Brooch in your inventory, which can be purchased at the Banner Village item shop at the Rare finds section.

name: Solon Jhee
description: Solon Jhee (ソロン・ジー, Soron Jī) is a minor antagonist in Suikoden II. A member of the powerful Jhee noble family, Solon quickly rose through the military ranks and presided over several early campaigns of the Dunan Unification War.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Solon Jhee was the second son of the esteemed Jhee family, a powerful noble family within the Highland Kingdom. Despite hailing from a prominent family, both his father and elder brother lacked faith in his political acumen, leading him to pursue a career in the Highland Army instead. As a result he had something of a complex about proving himself. He ascended to the position of command within the 4th Royal Highland Army, leading Highland's initial offensives against the City-States of Jowston.

Notably, Solon achieved the rank of general at a young age. However, his relative lack of experience and considerable pride, likely stemming from his noble heritage, became apparent in his leadership style. Though by no means incompetent, he was known to lack prudence and discretion, and his ambitious pursuit of rapid success sometimes rendered him careless.

His position was somewhat insecure as despite being commander of the 4th Army based on Luca's backing even before Luca assumed complete control of the military, some officers, such as Culgan and Seed, seemed to view him as someone who only received his position due to his family connections. However, as Luca continued his backing of him after assuming the position of commander-in-chief, it would seem he had at least some potential as a military leader.

During the Dunan Unification War, Solon faced a series of successive setbacks, with one of the most critical being his ill-fated attack on the old ruins at North Window. Unfamiliar with naval warfare, Solon failed to consider the possibility of being flanked from the lake shore. This oversight proved disastrous, as enemy units arrived by ship, nearly encircling his forces before he was forced to retreat.

In the wake of this crushing defeat, Solon Jhee found himself unable…

name: Susu
description: Susu (スースー, Sūsū) is a minor character who appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Family
facts.value: Chaco (grandson), Marnie (daughter-in-law), Phobo (son), Rainey (son), Unnamed husband

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Susu was the elder (Great Wing in Winged Horde terminology) of the Winged Horde section of Two River City. Although the leader of the Winged Horde, many people, including Chaco, simply refer to her as "Granny".

She had two sons, Phobo and Rainey. Phobo, the eldest, was intended to be her heir but passed away from a sudden illness. This caused Susu a lot of grief but being able to see Phobo's spirit in the annual Spirit Festival may have helped her move on.

She was old enough to remembering coming to Two River City from Tinto Principality. She would share her memories of Genkaku with the hero when he visited her in Two River.

name: Taki
description: Taki (タキ, Taki) is a character in Suikoden II. Taki is a woman with a wide and vast variety of helpful knowledge that she shares with others.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Taki was a venerable woman residing in Lakewest, possessing an extensive repertoire of intriguing stories and knowledge amassed over her long life. This wealth of experience enabled her to provide accurate advice, earning her the reputation of being a reliable source of wisdom among the townsfolk, who sought her counsel on numerous occasions.

Fondly referred to as having a "bag of wisdom," Taki's wisdom was held in high regard. Despite the opportunity to join her sons and grandchildren in Two River City, Taki's deep affection for Lakewest led her to remain in the town, where she cherished her life. In Lakewest, she lived off of her accumulated savings with the help of her family.

During the Dunan Unification War, Taki had an encounter with the leader of the New Alliance Army. He would end up consulting her on several matters. Realizing the inconvenience of the distance between Lakewest and the army's headquarters, she decided to relocate to the New Alliance Army Headquarters, facilitating more convenient access to her counsel.

Taki's vast knowledge, remarkably accurate advice, and its timely nature fueled speculations about the sources of her information. Rumors suggested the existence of an information network or even the secret use of a crystal ball.

Following the conclusion of the war, Taki eventually chose to settle in Two River, where she found contentment living with her daughter's family.

recruitment: Speak to her in Lakewest three times and she will join.

name: Taro
description: Taro (タロウ, Tarō) is a background character in Suikoden II. Taro is a sheep.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Taro was a sheep left in Yuzu's care by her grandfather.

It was Yuzu's favorite, and unlike the other livestock she raised, she was determined not to prepare it for slaughter. This could be seen in its neutral name. Sheep Yuzu raised for slaughter were given food names such as Jingisukan, Hamburger, or Fried Pork Cutlets.

Its collar had the same color as Yuzu's coat, indicating their connection and Taro's importance to her.

name: Tenkou
description: Tenkou (テンコウ, Tenkou) is a character in Suikoden II. He is a retired man who used to be a window maker, whose nostalgia and enthusiasm for his craft led to his joining the New Alliance Army.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Tenkou used to be, by his own admission, a pretty well known window maker. His impressive skills were backed with years of experience, marking him as an expert in the rare field of window making.

Still, he was retired and quietly living out his life alone in Crom Village when the Dunan Unification War broke out. It was rumored he was related to other window makers such as the Warriors' Village's Window.

When the leader of the New Alliance Army encountered him in Crom, Tenkou noticed his possession of a Window Set, which refueled his passion for his craft. He initially offered to buy the Window Set, but joined the New Alliance Army instead when he realized who he was talking to.

Following the end of the Dunan Unification War, Tenkou returned to his quiet life of retirement in Crom, perhaps finally satisfied with his life's work.

trivia: • The player can sell the Window Set to Tenkou for an immediate gain of either 1,000 or 10,000 potch. However, doing so will lose them a Window Set as well as the opportunity to recruit Tenkou.
recruitment: Speak to him in Crom Village, while having a Window Set in your inventory.

name: TenTen
description: TenTen (テンテン, Tenten) is an enemy who appears in Suikoden II. She is also a recruitable character in Suikoden STAR LEAP.

name: Teresa Wisemail
description: Teresa Wisemail (テレーズ・ワイズメル, Terēzu Waizumeru) is a character in Suikoden II who appears in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.1. She is a popular mayor from Greenhill City.
originalName: テレーズ・ワイズメル

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
220px|left|thumb|I'm glad Pahn couldn't hear this.
Teresa Wisemail held an esteemed position as the daughter of Alec Wisemail, the mayor of Greenhill City. When Alec fell seriously ill, Teresa assumed his responsibilities, gaining immense popularity and becoming a symbol of the town.

Despite her charisma and popularity, she actually held little personal interest in warfare or politics. Following her father's demise during the early stages of the Dunan Unification War, Teresa declined the offer to become the mayor. In response, the townspeople established the office of acting mayor specifically for her.

Endowed with intelligence and an unwavering dedication to Greenhill, Teresa's compassion extended to others. Notably, she entrusted Yuri with the care of Nash Latkje when he was discovered collapsed in Greenhill Forest. Additionally, Teresa diligently attempted to negotiate with the Highland Kingdom until the final moments in a bid to prevent an invasion of her beloved city.

Despite her efforts, negotiations ultimately broke down, compelling Teresa to make a momentous decision – with Nash's support – to engage in battle. The concerted efforts of many led to the successful repulsion of the initial Highland Army assault. Nevertheless, the release of 5,000 Muse City soldiers into Greenhill's custody would prove to be the city's undoing. As food supplies dwindled, the Muse soldiers revolted, plunging the city into chaos.

In a courageous attempt to quell the violence, Teresa offered herself to the Muse soldiers, who intended to hand her over to Highland. However, her allies intervened, preventing her from falling into their hands. Fleeing to the forests behind Greenhill alongside her devoted bodyguard, Shin, Teresa's failure to escape the Highland occupation weighed…

trivia:
• What would become Teresa's design was initially submitted for Anabelle before being repurposed.
• Teresa is said to have been Murayama Yoshitaka's favorite character from Suikoden II in particular.
recruitment: Joins automatically after the Greenhill events.

name: Tessai
description: Tessai (テッサイ, Tessai) is a character in Suikoden II. He is a blacksmith looking for a great sword to reignite his passion for his trade.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Tessai was a blacksmith who has made forging weapons his vocation and devoted all of his passion to his work. His passion for great weapons was what initially led him to become a blacksmith.

He had a workshop in Kuskus Town and a single disciple, the rest having been expelled or rejected at one point or another. Like many blacksmiths, he longed for the opportunity to work on a great or legendary sword.

However, such an opportunity did not come along, and eventually his passion for his work waned. By the time of the Dunan Unification War, he found himself jaded and uninspired. He would spend much time away from his workshop in Kuskus, hoping to find a legendary sword such as the Celestial Sword or the Dragon King Sword to inspire him.

Returning to Kuskus as dissatisfied as ever, when he noticed that Viktor wielded the Celestial Sword, his passion returned. He begged to join the New Alliance Army in order to work on the sword with his own two hands. Tessai was a talented smith, able to strength a weapon to its maximum level, providing he had the right tools.

Following the Dunan Unification War, Tessai's stock as a blacksmith rose considerably and he continued to work at the New Alliance Army Headquarters, dreaming of working with famed weapons.

trivia: • Tessai especially likes hot, spicy food.
recruitment: Speak with him at Kuskus Blacksmith while having Viktor in your active party.

name: Tetsu
description: Tetsu (テツ, Tetsu) is a character in Suikoden II. He is a devoted bathmaker and the father of Goro.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Tetsu is a bath maker from Lakewest. Tetsu is extremely passionate about baths and bathing, coming up with new designs for new and different types of baths. He cared for baths more than eating and was very much an old-school craftsman with a stubborn streak. In 457, he had a son, Goro, who would also grow up to become a bath maker and saw his father as his greatest rival.

Tetsu considered himself an out of work bath maker when he met the leader of the New Alliance Army, but he was so impressed with his toasty looks that he offered to make custom baths for the New Alliance Army Headquarters. The sight of people toasty from a warm bath would brighten even his most dour moods.

He encouraged people to decorate their baths, saying the best bath was that which felt like home. His greatest joy was to build such baths and to see people comfortably soaking in them.

Following the Dunan Unification War, Tetsu returned to Lakewest, where he began work on the largest bathhouse in the Dunan Republic. Years later, his son challenged him to a Bath Match which Tetsu won. This would inspire Goro to build a new bath made of new materials.

trivia: • Tetsu has five different bath designs you can choose: Drum, Cypress, Marble, Jungle, and Open Air.
recruitment: Speak to him while having the "toasty" status effect. You can get this status effect by eating fried taco's, which can be purchased at the Kobold District item store in Two-River.

name: Tomo
subtype: Chikyu Star or Tensyo Star — Star of Destiny
description: Tomo (トモ, Tomo) is a character in Suikoden II. Tomo is the daughter of Tsai who wants to reunite her parents.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Tomo was the daughter of Tsai. She was a strong-willed young woman with rustic personality who cared deeply for both her parents. She had inherited her mother's looks and was trained in the use of the spear by her father. After her parents separated, Tomo would try to reunite her mother and father.

She would visit Tsai's home in an attempt to persuade him, only to meet the hero. Hearing from her father that he had joined the New Alliance Army, Tomo would follow Tsai in joining the force so as to allow her to continue pressing the issue. Despite Tsai's attempted objections, she was welcomed into the force.

Although Tomo shoehorned her way into the New Alliance Army with ulterior motives, she fought very hard alongside her father throughout the Dunan Unification War, proving her worth in battle many times over with a spearmanship comparable to that of her father.

As the war continued, she expressed her concern for Tsai's safety. Tsai would agree with the dangers of war but vowed to continue fighting regardless. He would also ask Tomo to tell her mother that she never found him should he die in the upcoming battles, much to her dismay.

Following the war, Tomo succeeded in persuading her father to attempt to make amends with her mother and, together, she was able to live happily at home with both of them.

trivia: • Tomo will have a different star depending on if the player recruits Valeria or Kasumi. She will take the star of the character the player did not recruit.
recruitment: At some point, Tsai will write in the suggestion box asking you to go back to his place in Ryube Forest to get some tools. When this happens, put him in the active party and go to his house. There will be a cutscene between Tsai and Tomo, and she will join.

name: Tony
description: Tony (トニー, Tonī) is a character in Suikoden II and Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2. He is a devoted farmer with a passion for his fields and a hatred of moles.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Originally from the Matilda Knightdom, Tony was a farmer who owned a small field outside of Muse City. He was passionate about farming, cultivating his fields even in his spare time. He was forced to abandon his plot when the Highland Kingdom invaded. Tony fled all the way to Forest Village, where he was taken in by the village elder. 

There, he encountered the New Alliance Army's leader, who asked him to join the force. At first Tony refused, feeling indebted to the people of the Forest Village for taking him in. He would then receive the blessing of the village elder to leave, being persuaded that his work would help the war end that much sooner.

Joining the force, Tony began to grow crops at the New Alliance Army Headquarters. He worked hard at plowing his fields, growing vegetables for the army, and fighting off the moles that would routinely try and destroy his crops. His goal was to one day own a large farmstead of his own.

Following the Dunan Unification War, Tony continued to work hard, attempting to grow vegetables in Tinto's barren fields. At some point, he appeared to have gotten married. Nash Latkje and his friends, who were sent to the future by Viki's teleportation, met Tony and his daughter there. However, it is not entirely clear who his future wife was.

recruitment: Once castle is level 2, speak to him at the elders house in forest village, twice.

name: Tuta
description: Tuta (トウタ, Touta) is a character in Suikoden II and Suikoden III. Tuta is a doctor trained by Doctor Huan.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Medic in Training
Tuta was a young medic who received his training from Doctor Huan. He was the son of one of the doctor's friends and his initial interest in medicine was sparked when Doctor Huan successfully cured Tuta's mother of an illness. In addition to his lessons from Huan, Tuta also assisted the doctor with various tasks, including making deliveries. His medical robes were a hand-me-down from Huan.

Before the outbreak of the Dunan Unification War, Tuta regularly delivered medicines to Viktor's Mercenary Fortress. He was popular among the residents of the base, such as Leona, Gengen, and others. Although a hard-working youth, he was still a child and so was excited at the prospect of visiting new places, such as Ryube Village.

Following the fall of the fortress and the mercenary forces' evacuation to Muse City, Doctor Huan foresaw the unfolding events of the Dunan Unification War. He asked Tuta to serve as a medic within Viktor's forces. When called upon to fight, Tuta utilized a peculiar projectile - a pebble that he wielded skillfully. This seemingly ordinary pebble was unique, as it could be modified and upgraded by a blacksmith.

While Tuta lacked the ability to conduct full-fledged medical procedures, he did his best to assist in any way he could. Eventually, he became an integral part of the New Alliance Army. When Doctor Huan later joined the army as well, Tuta continued supporting him in his medical duties.

After the war concluded, Tuta's dedication to medicine remained unwavering. He pursued advanced training under Doctor Huan, showing even greater determination in his studies.
Doctor on Call
Tuta continued to study medicine under Doctor Huan and would grow to become a skilled doctor. After becoming a full-fledged doctor, he started journeying to…

trivia: • The kanji on Tuta's Suikoden II outfit (薬) is the kanji for medicine.
recruitment: After recruiting Mio, take Mio to the underground of Chisha village and talk to Tuta in the middle room. (You can recruit him without Mio)

name: Wakaba
description: Wakaba (ワカバ, Wakaba) is a character in Suikoden II who also appears in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.1. Wakaba is an energetic young martial artist who trains under Long Chan-Chan.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Wakaba was a skilled martial artist who trained in the dragon-style martial arts under her master, Long Chan-Chan. She was described as a young, unsuspecting woman with an innocent and pure-hearted personality, known for her boundless curiosity and willingness to embrace challenges. Wakaba's infectious enthusiasm served as motivation for her master, Long Chan-Chan, but she was also aware of his imperfections.

Her journey in martial arts began at a local dojo, conveniently located near her home. Driven by the aspiration to become a proficient martial arts master like her mentor, Wakaba subjected herself to rigorous training, utilizing her leg guards both as a weapon and protection during practice.

Combining her dedication to training, innate talent, and the power of the White Tiger Rune she possesses, Wakaba's strength gradually grew, propelling her to the status of a first-class fighter despite her young age. When her master advised her to seek out stronger opponents, he departed, leaving her to set out on her own path.

While on her way to Tinto Principality in search of Long Chan-Chan, Wakaba's exhaustive training led to fatigue, causing her to collapse in the Greenhill Forest. There, she was discovered by Nash Latkje, who assisted her in reaching safety on horseback. Wakaba's naïve and curious nature made her fascinated by the various people they encountered during their journey through the forest.

Subsequently, Wakaba found herself in the Forest Village, where she encountered the leader of the New Alliance Army. Impressed by his strength, she joined the force, eventually reuniting with Long Chan-Chan in Crom Village, where they fought together in the army's infantry regiments, forming a well-coordinated duo on the battlefield. The two would also train daily,…

recruitment: She's in front of the Armor Shop at Forest Village, speak to her after being level 30 or more.

name: Yoshino Yamamoto
description: Yoshino Yamamoto (ヨシノ・ヤマモト, Yoshino Yamamoto), also known as Yoshino ( ヨシノ, Yoshino) in Suikoden II, is a character in Suikoden II. Yoshino is the wife of Freed Yamamoto.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Yoshino Y is the wife of Freed Yamamoto. A gentle, beautiful woman, she first met Freed through Granmeyer's introduction five years prior to the Dunan Unification War. The two were happily married prior to the outbreak of the war. Modest and unassuming, she enjoyed the day-to-day work of maintaining the household, keeping it clean and tidy.

When South Window City was attacked by the Highland Kingdom, Yoshino became worried when Freed did not send word to her and anxiously awaited his return to Radat Town. Yoshino was reunited with Freed when he came to Radat and she decided to join him at the New Alliance Army Headquarters. She was always happiest when at Freed's side and war was no exception to this.

In the New Alliance Army, she showed her marital love by fighting alongside her husband showing her perhaps surprising skill with the naginata. She also had innate talent when it came to rune magic, something that she herself may not have been aware of.
 
Although she served as a soldier in the New Alliance Army she would also help with laundry and sewing duties while on the sidelines. Following the war, Freed lived up to his promise and returned to Radat with her, where they lived happily. Shortly after the war, she would give birth to her daughter, Sanae.

trivia:
• Yoshino's culinary specialty is her meat and potatoes recipe.
• Yoshino suffers at times from a somewhat dodgy translation, where she speaks about herself in third person.
recruitment: Speak with her while Freed Y. is in the party. She is in Radat to the direct right of the south exit.

name: Yu Kum
description: Yu Kum (ウコム, Ukomu) is a minor character who appears in Suikoden II. He is a chef who participated in a cook-off.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Yu Kum was the first cook to challenge Hai Yo at his restaurant in the New Alliance Army Headquarters. He was a friendly wandering chef with no affiliation with the Black Dragon Cooking Clan. He traveled the land in order to hone his cooking techniques.

After his defeat to Hai Yo in a cook-off, Yu Kum passed his Tomato Soup recipe to Hai Yo.

name: Yuzu
description: Yuzu (ユズ, Yuzu) is a character in Suikoden II who also appears in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2. Yuzu is a young shepherdess who participated in the Dunan Unification War.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Yuzu is a young shepherdess originally from the Forest Village in Greenhill Principality. She was separated from her grandfather during the Dunan Unification War. This threw the young girl into disarray. Without her grandfather's help, she had great difficulty caring for her sheep, who had become malnourished.

The leader of the New Alliance Army helped the young shepherdess, who was at her wit's end, find her sheep, who had gotten lost in the forest behind Kobold Village. In gratitude, Yuzu joined the New Alliance Army, where she took care of animals at the ranch, providing food for Hai Yo. 

Yuzu frequently branded her animals based on their use. Only one of Yuzu's animals, Taro, was marked as not for consumption. In her mind, animals for eating and animals for pets were two firmly separate categories. Meat was her favorite food also, reinforcing that divide.

Following the war, Yuzu was reunited with her grandfather, and returned home with Taro. In the future, she would end up running a ranch near Tony's fields. When Viki accidentally teleported Nash and his friends to the future, they briefly met Yuzu as a young adult.

trivia: • In Genso Suikoden Card Stories, Yuzu's sprite rides Taro; she is the only character to appear mounted in the game.
recruitment: After the Greenhill events, talk to her in Kobold village. Find her three sheep in the forest (one is behind the tree) and she will join.

name: Zamza
description: Zamza (ザムザ, Zamuza) is a character in who appears in Suikoden II. He is an egotistical magician who bears the Fire Dragon Rune.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Zamza was a magician who frequented both Toto Village and South Window City. Ever since he could remember, he bore the Fire Dragon Rune on his right hand. Having a unique rune would be part of the motivation behind his self-aggrandizing nature.

In the past, he studied magic under Dale in the hopes of finding a method to use his rune in a practical way, though his arrogance had made it difficult to even ask Dale to help in the matter and prevented him from ever calling Dale his master. He would eventually develop a fighting style incorporating his Fire Dragon Rune during an ivy monster attack on their caravan.

Dale would banish him for this, stating that if he wanted to become a mere brawler he could do it without his tutelage. But Dale also gave him a supply of Medicine for his burns, tacitly giving his approval for Zamza to develop his magic in his own way. Later, Zamza primarily wielded brass knuckles as his weapon of choice. 

He forcibly inserted himself into the ranks of the New Alliance Army when Nanami, offended by his domineering remarks, declared his presence unnecessary. Driven by his high opinion of himself, Zamza joined the army to prove his value.

Due to his conceited and high-handed nature, Zamza spent much of his time in the New Alliance Army complaining about being overlooked, lacking his own room, incessantly talking about himself to anyone who would listen (including Richmond, for five hours), and writing what he referred to as "fantastic letters." He often suggested that less important members should be asked to leave the castle, finding it too hot and crowded.

During the Dunan Unification War, Zamza served as a magic unit captain and a magic instructor. His appointment to the position by Shu was not solely due to his ability, although he was…

trivia: • Equipping Zamza with a Fire Sealing Rune will ensure he takes no damage from his Fire Dragon Rune, although it does mean you cannot equip any other rune as Zamza only ever has two rune slots.
recruitment: Speak to him while Nanami is in the party, either in Toto Village (before Toto's destruction)or the South-Window inn.

name: Zenith
description: Zenith (セネス, Senesu) is a minor character from Suikoden II. She is depicted using a standard rune sage sprite.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Zenith was a female rune sage, reputed to be the most skillful in Muse Principality with the best runes.

It is said that she could be difficult at times, but her talent and bearing meant there were still many people applying to apprentice under her.

trivia: • Erroneously referred to as "he" in the English localization of Suikoden II and again in Suikoden I&II HD Remaster.

name: Ace
description: Ace (エース, Ēsu) is a main character in Suikoden III. Ace is the treasurer of Geddoe's unit in the Harmonian Southern Frontier Defense Force.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Ace was a member of the 12th unit of the Harmonian Southern Frontier Defense Force, working under Geddoe. He was known to be a womanizer and the self-proclaimed sexiest man of the force. He had a certain amount of success with women due to his dry humor and quick wit, but experienced just as much rejection and heartbreak.

He dressed in typical military clothing, so as to be prepared for any mission no matter the location. He was also a voracious reader and something of an aspiring writer, penning the Erk's Adventures series under his penname, Hitman Bravo. However, his writing style was cumbersome and often confusing to the reader.

His passion for reading was also what led to Ace using sai in combat. Ace had learned of the existence of this weapon from an old book and then asked a blacksmith to have it custom-made for him. His sai were made of iron and had three prongs, making them excellent for catching, entangling, and striking back at the enemy.

In addition to being the unit's beleaguered accountant (a job he only received because no one else wanted it), he was also a facilitator and negotiator for the group. He was talented in his role, and was often the one who would push for Geddoe to make decisions for the group.

In the past, he was also a member of Gilbert's mercenaries and fought in the Dunan Unification War as a member of the sellsword group. At some point after the war, he became a member of the Harmonian Southern Frontier Defense Force. He was once in a short-lived romance with Elaine but was dumped after he gave her a gift that cost him an entire mission's wage.

When the War of the Champions began to flare up, Ace would travel as part of Geddoe's group. Eventually, they were confronted by Duke's 14th unit, who were attempting to order the 12th unit to…

trivia: • Ace's Double Tusk Rune is highly similar to the Boar Rune in Suikoden, the effect is the same.

name: Aila
description: Aila (アイラ, Aira) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Aila is a young warrior of the Karaya Clan who is thrust into the world outside Grasslands over the course of the game.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Aila was a young Karayan warrior, skilled with the bow and knowledgeable about magic and the spirits which inhabited the Grasslands.

Raised in the Karaya Village, she tended to adhere to her cultural norms but was nonetheless a curious girl with a somewhat naïve personality due to her lack of experience. She was proud of her heritage and brave to the point of foolhardiness in the face of any danger.

As a member of the Karaya Clan, Aila wore traditional Karayan clothing, mainly made of leather and designed for ease of movement. For her weapon, she wielded a wooden bow named Da Tar. In Aila's case, the bow allowed her to attack from a distance while sensing the location of her target through the power of the earth spirits.

Aila would first encounter Geddoe and his unit of the Southern Frontier Defense Force when they entered Karaya Village. She questioned their motives and was notable affronted when Ace called her "cute", feeling she was being dismissed as a warrior.

When the Karaya Clan was attacked by the Zexen Knights, Aila survived the attack as she had left the village to pursue Sarah, whom her intuition made her suspect.

In order to track her down, Aila would travel with the SFDF. She held great anger against the Zexen Knights for their razing of her village, and had to be restrained on multiple occasions from avenging herself on the knights by Jacques and Queen. Her sense of justice and enthusiasm sometimes shined through, such as when she enthusiastically led the SFDF in their clandestine defense of Budehuc Castle.

Her participation in the War of the Champions awakened Aila's interest in the outside world and she gained valuable experience about the different cultures and ways of life in Zexen, Harmonia, and elsewhere. Aila fell in love with the sodas…

name: Alanis
description: Alanis (アラニス, Aranisu) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Alanis is a young girl about to move away from Vinay del Zexay.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Alanis is the bright, lively daughter of traders and a member of the Saint Loa Knights, a group formed with her friends Melville and Elliot. There, she is known as "The Silver Maiden".

As her parents were traders, she moved a lot in her childhood and rarely had time to make friends before moving on. Still, in Vinay del Zexay she was able to form a close friendship with Melville and Elliot. She explored the North Cavern with her friends and helped to uncover Guillaume's underhanded dealing. These, and other adventures, strengthened her bond with her friends.

Following this, Alanis was supposed to move with her parents to the Nameless Lands but the War of the Champions interfered, stranding her and her family in the Great Hollow. She would be reunited with her friends when Melville visited, and she joined the Fire Bringer.

Following the war, she would resume her move north with her family but with the hope of seeing her friends again someday.

trivia: • Alanis loves insects and animals.

name: Albert Silverberg
description: Albert Silverberg (アルベルト・シルバーバーグ, Aruberuto Shirubābāgu) is an antagonist in Suikoden III. Albert is the strategist of the Destroyers.
originalName: アルベルト・シルバーバーグ

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Albert is a member of renowned strategist family, the Silverbergs, and the brother of Caesar. He is known to be always calm, calculating, and thoughtful. 

He studied abroad in Harmonia, where he was the star student, and came to believe in the practical strategies of his grandfather, Leon. His goal could be said to be the ultimate result of this thought process, the unification of the world into a single nation.

To accomplish this, he chose first to establish himself in Harmonia, feeling its great power would be a perfect base for guiding the world to its ideal state.

While in Crystal Valley, he met Luc and agreed to lend him his power on the condition he be allowed to prioritize his own goals. He was also able to introduce Luc to Yuber as Albert had learned the method of meeting Yuber from the notes of Leon.

Together, the group, known as the Destroyers, planned to obtain the five elemental True Runes while gaining backing from the Harmonian Army. However, the advent of the Fire Bringer forced a change in plans and Albert distanced himself from the group as they forcibly removed the True Earth Rune from Sasarai.

With all five runes collected, Albert had already established himself as a major strategist in Harmonia and so he fed information about Luc's plans to Nash Clovis so that the plot could be thwarted. Since then, his whereabouts have been unknown, although it is said that while he continues to serve Harmonia, he is also working for a power on another continent under an assumed name.

name: Anna Lightfellow
description: Anna Lightfellow (アンナ・ライトフェロー, Anna Raitoferō) is a minor character who appears in Suikoden III. She is the mother of Chris Lightfellow.
originalName: アンナ・ライトフェロー

sections.title: Story
sections.body: Anna was the mother of Chris Lightfellow and the wife of Wyatt. Wyatt loved her dearly, despite being forced to abandon her and his daughter. She would die from a disease several years later.

name: Anne
description: Anne (アンヌ, Annu) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Anne is a member of the Karaya Clan who dreams of opening her own tavern.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Anne is a hardworking, older sister-type who long dreamed of opening a tavern of her own. She was eventually able to open a tavern in Karaya Village but on the very day it opened, the Zexen Knights attacked the village, burning it to the ground. After this, she fled to the Great Hollow with the rest of the Karaya Clan, working at the inn there.

As a tough woman, Anne tried to keep a positive attitude, even in difficult times. Eventually, after joining the Fire Bringer, she was entrusted with the tavern at Budehuc Castle where said attitude helped ease the minds of many. She was also known to be a heavy drinker, easily able to outdrink even Leo, Wan Fu or Joker.

Following the War of the Champions, she was able to return to the restored Karaya Village and reestablished her long-sought bar once more, to great success.

name: Arthur
description: Arthur (アーサー, Āsā) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Arthur is a reporter, always on the look-out for scoops and stories.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Arthur holds a dream of becoming a newspaper reporter, hoping to create a record of the times he lived in. With his soul burning with the desire to find a scoop, he is always first on the scene should he hear about any newsworthy incident. He met Hugo while investigating the secret passage out of Brass Castle and subsequently joined the Fire Bringer.

At Budehuc Castle, he worked on the wall newspaper there, covering many events of the War of the Champions as well as gossip and rumors that had caught on in the castle. Kidd disproved of him, feeling that detectives and reporters were natural enemies and that earning a living writing about private lives was wrong.

Following the war, Arthur continued to travel, tracking down stories to report on.

trivia: • Arthur's recruitment scene differs slightly if you recruit him using Hugo.

name: Augustine Nabor
description: Augustine Nabor (オーギュスタン・ナボール, Ōgyusutan Nabōru) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. He is an aristocrat who bears the Red Rose Rune and prizes his Rose Brooch.
originalName: オーギュスタン・ナボール

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
Augustine had long blond hair, tied into a loose pony tail that extended back his shoulders and blue eyes. He was often seen carrying a red rose in his right hand.

Compared to his fellow narcissists, Augustine wore a more understated outfit consisting of a light blue vest, black hat (with a blue band), and black pants with brown boots. His white shirt and scarf were also key elements of his outfit, keeping the overall presentation refined and preventing it from becoming too drab.

This combination of clothing and appearance in turn created a sophisticated and stylish image that was nonetheless elegant in its simplicity.

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
Elegant and a lover of all things beautiful, Augustine was very particular about fashion and appearance. According to his own words, he is proud of his moustache and prize Rose Brooch, as well as his gorgeous and artistic swordsmanship. It is in part his swordsman's spirit that endeared Vincent De Boule to him as a veteran of the Gate Rune and Dunan Unification Wars.

He would often introduce himself with a wink, being confident in his own charm and irresistible nature. Part of the reason he agreed to join the Fire Bringer was because he genuinely believed the group would simply not give up on recruiting him after recognizing his noble aura and swordsmanship.

Perhaps unsurprisingly, Augustine also tended to get lost in his own world at times, not noticing when people were talking to him, unless it was about a subject he was already invested in.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Augustine is a person from the former Scarlet Moon Empire who loves nobility as well as elegance of beauty. Six years ago, Augustine was also a recipient of a Rose Brooch, an award at Vincent De Boule's beauty contest and the two became "true friends". He had then set out on a journey of sorts, giving the world the privilege of witnessing his stylish swordplay and attention-grabbing outfit.

Six months before the War of the Champions, Augustine would visit Vinay del Zexay and would become so enamored with the vast beauty of the western ocean that he stayed there for over half a year, merely taking in the sea view daily.

Once the war broke out, Augustine joined the Fire Bringer out of gratitude after they recovered the prized Rose Brooch he had misplaced during his travels. His elegant and artistic sword skills lent themselves well to the cause, as did his Red Rose Rune. He was very proud of his appearance and had a slight obsession with removing split ends. 

Augustine was also very fond of the Suggestion Box, writing several letters to the Flame Champion offering his fashion advice, invites for afternoon tea, or asking opinions on flowers to be delivered to the castle. It seems he was always checking out promising new associates at Budehuc Castle, such as Salome and Gordon.

Following the war, Augustine would again travel to the Toran Republic after hearing rumors of a beautiful palace there.

trivia:
• Considering he is already a "true friend" of Vincent De Boule and hails from the Scarlet Moon Empire, one would think he already visited the Toran Republic. His ending, however, implies he has never been there before. His ending might be a reference to the home of Milich Oppenheimer in Gregminster.
• Augustine is the only character who can reach S level in the Parry skill.
• Augustine shows questionable taste in his letter about Kenji having some sort of skill that he too must pursue.
• Augustine is the first user of the Red Rose Rune in the chronology of released titles, however, in the in-game chronology, he is the last known user.

name: Ayame
description: Ayame (アヤメ, Ayame) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Ayame is a ninja, dedicated to hunting down Watari.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Ayame is a mysterious ninja, formerly in the employ of the Highland Kingdom. Stories say that Ayame once served the Highland Kingdom as an assassin before it fell and that she is a member of the Kage ninja group which participated in the Higheast Rebellion that followed, but the exact details are unknown. Her hometown and background are unknown, as she does not appear to be affiliated with the ninja village of Rokkaku Hamlet. Ayame never speaks openly about her involvement in any operation.

In the years that followed, she hunted down the ninja Watari for abandoning the group and challenged him to a duel when the two encountered each other in the North Cavern. Following her defeat, Ayame would join the Fire Bringer. Normally, Ayame was somber and serious but sometimes displayed a fierce tenacity, especially regarding Watari.

She agreed to put aside her orders to kill him until the war was over, although this proved to test her patience. Ayame did not associate much with her fellow Fire Bringers during the war and was particularly annoyed with the detective Kidd, who relented further investigation after she threatened him.

Following the War of the Champions, she would immediately leave Budehuc Castle, already in pursuit of Watari. Any further information or the result of their conflict remains unknown.

trivia:
• Ayame fills the role as Suikoden III's resident "female ninja Shrike Rune user".
• Ayame makes a particularly lousy actress for Nadir's plays, try it!

name: Barts
description: Barts (バーツ, Bātsu) is a supporting character in Suikoden III, a kind-hearted farmer devoted to growing tomatoes. Despite his rakish appearance, he is gentle, often talking to his crops. After his fields are destroyed, he joins the Fire Bringer, supplying fresh produce for their army.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Barts was a man of simple heart and honest labor, a tiller of the earth who found his joy in the nurturing of the soil. Though his appearance was rakish, his true strength lay in his devotion to the land. Many who saw him might have mistaken him as debonair, yet his hands knew only the plow and the vine.

From dawn until dusk, he toiled in the fields, speaking to his crops as though they were dear friends. Above all, he cherished the tomato, pouring his passion into its cultivation, believing that food grown with love would bring happiness to those who ate it. His labor was ceaseless as he rose before the sun and rested only when the rains came, never seeking leisure or idle pleasures.

When the War of the Champions came to Iksay Village, his fields were laid to waste, and he was left with nothing. Yet fortune smiled upon him when he was invited to tend the fields at Budehuc Castle. With gratitude, he took up his hoe anew, his face alight with purpose. There, he supplied the castle's kitchens with the finest produce, with him praising Mamie for being so skilled with his ingredients.

He was known to converse with his plants, to smile upon them as one would a child. A strange habit to some, yet none could deny the bounty he reaped. Mel received his tomatoes with reverence, and Connie followed him as if drawn by his kindness. Kathy and Sasarai also received some of his produce, while Bright occasionally helped him in the fields.

When peace returned, he went back to Iksay Village, where he began anew, contentedly sowing seeds in freshly turned earth.

name: Bazba
description: Bazba (バズバ, Bazuba) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. He is a prominent warrior of the Lizard Clan.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Bazba was one of three warriors of the Lizard Clan, assisting chief Dupa along with Shiba. Although considered slender by lizard standards, he was very strong and adept in combat, wielding a gride, a trident-like spear weapon.

He is also considered very handsome and is a skilled swimmer, winning Lizard Clan swim meets, making him a popular and well-liked member of his clan.

He would fight for the Six Clans during the War of the Champions, eventually joining the Fire Bringer after the establishment of its headquarters at Budehuc Castle.

Following the War of the Champions, he continued to maintain his gride, remaining ever on guard while assisting Dupa.

name: Belle
description: Belle (ベル, Beru) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Belle is a journeying trickster, searching for her mother, Meg.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Belle was a young girl who traveled the land alongside Gadget Z, a remarkable clockwork automaton powered by alchemical mechanisms. Though she possessed the rare skills of a trickster, a craft scarcely seen in the world, her abilities remained unrefined, and none of her own creations had yet functioned properly.

She was the daughter of Meg, a veteran of the Gate Rune and Dunan Unification Wars, who had crafted Gadget Z before mysteriously vanishing. Driven by both a desire to hone her craft and to find her missing mother, Belle wandered the land, her journey leading her to join the Flame Champion's cause during the War of the Heroes after receiving aid in repairing her broken automaton.

Despite her rough speech and brash demeanor, Belle harbored a softer side, particularly around Hugo, for whom she had developed an immediate infatuation. In his presence, she struggled to maintain an uncharacteristically polite and girlish demeanor, often stumbling over her words in flustered embarrassment.

Her letters in the Budehuc Castle Suggestion Box revealed a mix of forced refinement and her usual bluntness. To Hugo she wrote of flower fields and home-made sweaters, while to others she complained about Mel's eccentric bathing habits, and expressed irritation at Aila's familiarity with Hugo.

Belle’s technical skills were questionable at best. Kidd noted that while she could manipulate Gadget Z’s cognitive circuits to make it speak as she pleased, none of her own inventions had ever worked. She was occasionally seen in the company of Viki and Mel, though it was dryly noted that "all three could pass for beauties if they’d just stay quiet."

Following the war, Belle continued her travels with Gadget Z, striving to become a true master of her craft while never abandoning hope of…

name: Billy
description: Billy (ビリー, Birī) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. He is a self-described treasure hunter whose talents seem to lie more in burglary and gambling.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Billy was a treasure hunter and the oft-absent father of Melville.

Always filled with a great wanderlust, Billy once worked on a farm to earn money for his travels. Billy fell in love with the farmer's daughter, but eventually left her to continue his wanderings. Unbeknownst to Billy, this woman was pregnant with Billy's child, who would grow up to be Kathy.

Years later, he would marry another woman in Vinay del Zexay and father Melville. In the Dunan Republic, he would stay at the White Deer Inn, meeting Alex there. He was so impressed with Alex's stories that he too took up the mantle of treasure hunter.

After losing his home due to large gambling debts owed to Shilo (who likely cheated him), his wife left him. At a loss, and raising his son alone, he was able to rent a cheap room from Elliot's mother. He continued his treasure hunting dreams since then, disappearing for great lengths of time.

He was briefly reunited with Melville during the War of the Champions when he agreed to join the Fire Bringer after being caught attempting to steal antiques from Budehuc Castle. Although he encountered Kathy at Budehuc Castle, neither was aware of their relationship.

Following the war, he was dragged back to Vinay del Zexay by Melville.

name: Borus Redrum
description: Borus Redrum (ボルス・レッドラム, Borusu Reddoramu) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Borus is one of the Six Mighty Zexen Knights.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Borus was one of the Six Mighty Knights, the most renowned of the Zexen Knights. He was raised by proud parents in a prominent Zexen family that has produced many fine Zexen Knights but seems to become angry whenever this is brought up. As such, he had apprenticed in the knights from an early age.

He was said to be the best swordsman out of the entire Zexen Knights but was also said to lack composure. He wielded a double-edged, one-handed sword that slightly longer than normal so that it could be used from horseback. His temperament and skill led to him being known as the "Swordsman of Rage." Nevertheless, he was extremely loyal, honest, and passionate person. As a knight, he held absolute confidence in his skill as a swordsman and was unintimidated by any opponent.

He was friends with Percival Fraulein. Though the two graduated at the same time, they were initially assigned to different companies and so did not have much contact. It is only after they both became two of the Six Mighty Knights that they got to know each other. They were initially rivals but eventually forged a bond as comrades.

Borus had harbored strong feelings towards Chris for quite some time, though perhaps these feelings were more of admiration than romance. This has led him to become easily flustered and enraged should he be mocked for it.

During the Zexen attack on the Karaya Village at the outset of the War of the Champions, the resulting massacre cast a dark shadow across his mind. Although the massacre itself was perpetrated by the Destroyers, Borus could not deny that he had been caught up in his anger, bringing out his brutality. Since then, he struggled to forgive himself, even for a moment. His pride as a knight also got in the way of his acceptance of what had happened. 

Borus'…

trivia:
• Borus has a Suggestion Box letter that only occurs if Hugo is selected as Flame Champion and Chris acts in a play, talk about specific.
• Borus first name is likely a reference to Bors (ボールス), one of the Knights of the round table. Their Japanese names only slightly differ. His last name is murder read backwards, probably referencing the Stephen King novel The Shining.

name: Branky
description: Branky (ブランキー, Burankī) is a character and weapon that appears in Suikoden III. He is ostensibly the ventriloquist dummy of Mel.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Branky was a cartoonish wolf puppet that Mel wore on her right hand. Branky tends to speak frequently and coarsely, often reflecting badly or oddly on Mel. Mel herself insisted that she had no ventriloquism talent, and that Branky of its own volition, however.

Following the War of the Champions, Mel would set off on a journey, leaving word that she was visiting Branky's hometown.

name: Brown
description: Brown (ブラウン, Buraun) is a character who is mentioned in Suikoden III. He was the father of Cecile.

sections.title: Story
sections.body: Brown was the guard commander of Budehuc Castle in the past. Following his passing, his daughter Cecile would take up the position, first unofficially then by appointment, out of respect and admiration for her father.

name: Caesar Silverberg
description: Caesar Silverberg (シーザー・シルバーバーグ, Shīzā Shirubābāgu) is a major character in Suikoden III. Caesar is a strategist in training who eventually helps the Fire Bringer's campaign in the War of the Champions.
originalName: シーザー・シルバーバーグ

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Caesar Silverberg is a member of the famed Silverberg family of strategists and the younger brother of Albert. His laid-back personality and propensity for napping tends to obscure his strategic abilities, though his intelligence has never been doubted. Like his brother, Caesar was educated in Crystal Valley. However, unlike Albert, Caesar had little enthusiasm for the studying involved but still managed to impress due to his natural talent and occasional spurts of brilliance.

It wasn't until Caesar met Apple while she was researching for her biography of Mathiu Silverberg that he took a greater interest in military strategy. Apple recounted stories of both Mathiu and Shu to him and Caesar would grow to empathize with their ideals. However, as his brother came to believe more strongly in the principles of their grandfather Leon, the two would grow distant as they grew older.

When Albert left home to help the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia in their aim for eventual global unification, Caesar similarly left with Apple with the goal of thwarting Albert's schemes.

Eventually discovering that Albert was aiding the Harmonian invasion of the Grasslands, sparking the War of the Champions, Caesar would join the resisting Fire Bringer in response. His apparent lack of motivation was nowhere to be found as he confronted Albert, revealing his true feelings and passion for his beliefs.

Although he appears lazy, when action needed to be taken, Caesar was able to show his pride and self-confidence at being a Silverberg. It was Caesar's efforts that helped unite the fractured Zexen and Grasslands at Brass Castle after their retreat from the Duck Village. His determination to prevent his brother's plans was similarly fierce, as he was willing to brave the dangerous Ceremonial Site to…

name: Cecile
description: Cecile (セシル, Seshiru) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. She is the energetic and excitable leader of the Budehuc Castle guard.

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
Cecile is a young adolescent girl of average height. She has large blue eyes and dirty blonde hair. Under her helmet, her shoulder-length hair is tied in twin pigtails with red ribbon.

She is normally in her father's silver torso armor and helmet, with an orange plumage. The armor especially is oversized on her young frame, giving a somewhat comical appearance. She wears an orange and yellow plaid skirt with white fringe, long orange socks, and armored boots. She carries a large shield emblazoned with the Zexen crest as well as her spear.

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
Always full of energy and single-minded, Cecile took it upon herself to follow in the footsteps of her father, Brown, who was once the commander of Budehuc Castle's garrison, and continues to strive to fulfill her self-imposed duties, rain or shine.

Cecile is rarely discouraged in any situation and always speaks positively. She is filled with hope and a strong will to never give up. She could get nervous before battle, but her determination would see her through such obstacles.

She greatly loved her father's armor, seeing in herself the proud image of the father figure she admired so much when she wore it. She never took it off on-duty, regardless of the heat or cold. Her enthusiasm sometimes crossed the line into silliness, especially combined with the image of her wearing her father's oversized armor.

She is also a very good cook, preferring to make hot and spicy foods.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Cecile was a brave girl, filled with boundless energy who served as the gatekeeper of Budehuc Castle. She had unofficially taken over the position after the passing of her father and previous guard commander, Brown. She had grown up in the castle before her father's passing.

She was well aware that she was not the official commander of the castle's garrison. Still, she strove every day to protect her home, which her beloved father had similarly cherished. Accompanying the newly appointed castle lord Thomas to Brass Castle, she would be momentarily stunned when Salome Harras noted that Budehuc had no appointed garrison commander.

With Thomas' intersession and Cecile's own determination shining through, Salome would ensure she was formally appointed as the official gatekeeper, sidestepping the standard Zexen bureaucracy. Thrilled at her pure-hearted dream coming through, Cecile was able to return to Budehuc with a new sense of pride and determination.

Thrilled at her official status, Cecile dedicated herself to protecting Thomas throughout the War of the Champions and aided him in rescuing his namesake from Mt. Hei-Tou. Later, when Zexen Council troops surrounded Budehuc Castle in an attempt to forcibly remove Thomas for his position, Cecile was determined to make a stand.

This was not due to simple optimism or naivety. For Cecile, to protect the things important to her without backing down formed the core of what she truly believed in. Together, the people of Budehuc were able to prevent Thomas's removal as castle governor, securing the future of the castle further. She would continue to support Thomas when Budehuc Castle became the base of operations of the Fire Bringer.

Though a good cook, Thomas fainted when he ate Cecile's food, as he wasn't used to her taste…

name: Chris Lightfellow
description: Chris Lightfellow (クリス・ライトフェロー, Kurisu Raitoferō) is one of the main protagonists of Suikoden III.
originalName: クリス・ライトフェロー

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
As a Zexen Knight, Chris Lightfellow is always clad in her customary silver armor, boots, and greaves. Underneath, she wears black garments. She wears orange and gold bases as befitting her status as one of the Six Mighty Knights. Her silver hair is braided and tied up to keep it from being a detriment in combat. She has violet eyes.

On her journey in the Grasslands, Chris wears practical traveling gear. A green jacket over a white shirt, long navy-blue pants, brown boots, and black gloves. Her hair is also let down, revealing to hang to the small of her back. In both outfits, she wears small teardrop earrings.

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
Chris Lightfellow was known as the Silver Maiden due to her appearance, riding a white horse, clad in silver armor, and with shining white hair. Although celebrated as a hero within her native Zexen for her pride and noble bearing, Chris placed immense pressure on herself due to feelings that she was not worthy of such praise.

As a Zexen Knight, her loyalty was to the institution of Zexen itself, rather than its lands or its people, per se. Chris' dogmatic loyalty to the Zexen Council contrasts with her comrades' personal loyalty to her. This perspective began to shift once she journeyed into the Grasslands and met Yun, who was willing to sacrifice her life for her people.

Eventually Chris comes to believe that people can work together to protect what is important to them, even if their positions and ideas are different. This eventually enables her to accept the titles lauded upon her in order to become a symbol of her beliefs. Although she can be overly serious, she has a gentle personality overall and is greatly trusted and respected both by her subordinates and the people of Zexen.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Chris Lightfellow was born to Anna and Wyatt Lightfellow in Vinay del Zexay. She lost her father at a young age and her mother passed not long after. Growing up, she was skilled in swordplay but disliked more traditionally feminine activities. She was unmelodic and did not last long in school choir because of this. Inspired by her father's ideal and in some way pursuing his memory, Chris would train to be a Zexen Knight.

At the Zexen Federation Day Ball in 470, Chris ended up brawling with Lilly Pendragon, first daughter of the Tinto Republic. Lilly had insisted Chris dance with her and when Chris, who was not good at dancing at the time, accidentally spilled a glass of wine on Lilly's dress, a brawl soon broke out. Lilly ended up throwing a cake at Chris, who retaliated with a slap.

The fight would finally end after the two finished hurling tables at one another. Despite, or rather because of, this incident, the two recognized the strong will in each other and the two, especially Lilly, considered themselves friends.

During a fierce battle against the Grasslands clans, before the War of the Champions, the Zexen Knight commander Galahad was killed. It was Chris who rose up to rally the knights and win the battle. This impressive showing led to her being appointed deputy leader of the Zexen Knights.

Although dedicated to her job and willing to shoulder the heavy responsibility it brought, the plots of the Zexen Council to provoke fighting in Grasslands combined with the invasion from the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia began to take its toll.

In the Zexen attack on the Karaya Village, Chris sighted her father's armor outside a Karaya house. Concluding that Wyatt had been killed and his armor claimed as a battle trophy, she would be filled with malice. When Lulu jumped to…

trivia:
• Chris' weapon upgrades as Letzt, Stendich, Evich. All three are of German origin, being bad transcriptions of Letzte, Ständig and Ewig, which would make her weapons German for Last, Constant, and Eternal.
• Chris is the worst choice for Flame Champion when magic power and rune affinity is taken into consideration, as her Fire Magic rank maxes at C, whereas her Water Magic rank maxes at B+.
• Due to her poor magic growth, she is never able to cast the Level 4 spell of any rune without the abuse of stat stones or the Level 99 trick.

name: Connie
description: Connie (コニー, Konī) is a minor character who appears in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Connie is a white-haired dog who wears a pink wrapping cloth around its neck. Her bark is "Wuffy-wuff-wuff!". She was found by the Fire Bringer deep in the tunnels of the Great Hollow and was subsequently adopted and brought back to Budehuc Castle.

She loves to be dressed up and bathes often as she is concerned about her appearance. She likes to eat orange peels and, unlike the other dogs, she carries strawberries in her wrapping cloth. She really likes Barts and Nash.

name: Dios
description: Dios (ディオス, Diosu) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Dios is Sasarai's aide-de-camp within the Harmonian army.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Dios was a man of sharp intellect and humble origins, born in the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia. Though a capable officer, he was often ill-favored by fortune, burdened with unfavorable duties and overlooked by his peers.

His keen mind was matched only by his lack of political acumen, and thus his merits went unrecognized for many years until the turmoil of the Higheast Rebellion, when his abilities caught the discerning eye of Bishop Sasarai.

Recognizing his worth, Sasarai elevated him to his Chief of Staff, a rare acknowledgment that Dios repaid with unwavering loyalty. From that day forth, he stood steadfastly at Sasarai’s side, serving with quiet efficiency both publicly and behind the scenes. 

A man of pragmatism, Dios believed in the supremacy of wisdom over brute force. This philosophy was displayed during the incident at Chisha Village when Hugo, claiming the title of Flame Champion, challenged Harmonia to single combat. As Sasarai mischievously asked if he would answer the challenge, Dios demurred.

It was neither cowardice nor weakness that stayed his hand, but the simple understanding that some battles were not worth fighting. To those who knew him, this moment was pure Dios; a man who measured his actions by necessity, not pride. 

In this way he was respected for his competence yet never one to seek the spotlight. He even once refrained from bathing when he noticed two of his comrades were engaged in a contest of endurance. His distinctive sideburns were a point of quiet pride for him but when Jefferson proclaimed him the recipient of his 'big nose prize,' he was baffled as to its meaning. 

After the revelation of Luc's true intentions, Dios arrived with reinforcements alongside Sasarai, joining the Fire Bringer as a trusted ally. In the years that…

trivia: • Dios is quite proud of his sideburns.

name: Dominic
description: Dominic (ドミニク, Dominiku) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Dominic is a grumpy, albeit extremely talented, craftsman.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
