Dominic is a smith who runs an armor shop in Vinay del Zexay. He is a highly talented craftsman and when he is inspired, he is capable of crafting everything from highly artistic works of armor to pieces of great practicality and durability.

He is sometimes grumpy and finicky and can sometimes provoke or overcharge non-discerning customers. He can also be very chatty with his clientele, depending on his mood, leading to a dearth of business.

In his own way, he wants people to understand the value of his art, hence his moods. Outside of his work, he does not have much of a social life, heading home quickly and living an ordinary life in his spare time.

While working in Vinay del Zexay, his dream was to expand his store and make it truly great. He would join the Fire Bringer after they purchased his Mole Armor, a piece he was quite proud of.

He would remain at Budehuc Castle following the War of the Champions in order to realize his dream of an expanded store.

name: Duke
description: Duke (デューク, Deyukū) is one of the protagonists of Suikoden III. He is the brash leader of the Harmonian Southern Frontier Defense Force's 14th Unit.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Duke serves as the leader of the 14th Unit of the Harmonian Southern Frontier Defense Force. He is a talented leader and fighter, though his pride occasionally obscures his judgement, such as his one-sided rivalry with Geddoe, leader of the SFDF's 12th Unit.

Initially during the War of the Champions, Duke and his unit worked under the Masked Man and this role would lead to even more conflicts between him and Geddoe, usually with his team coming out on the losing end, fueling the rivalry still further. However, after Luc's attack on Geddoe at Mt. Senai and understanding what the realization of Luc's plans would mean for both the Grasslands and the SFDF, Duke would help Geddoe's unit escape and would fight alongside the Fire Bringer from them on.

Still, his rivalry with Geddoe never abated and the two were never going to get along well. Following the war, Duke married Elaine and headed south on a new mission, hoping for a rematch against Geddoe in the future.

name: Dupa
description: Dupa (デュパ, Deyupa) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. He is the successor to Zepon as Chief of the Lizard Clan.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Dupa served as the warrior Chief of the Lizard Clan during the War of the Champions. Although he is prideful and aggressive, he is also courteous.

Before he became Chief, Dupa served as the Left Fighter in Unit One of the Lizard Clan's Three Bodies Fighters. His headpiece is made up of feathers from birds he personally hunted during a hunting trial, making it a great source of personal pride.

He would become Chief of the Lizard Clan following Chief Zepon's assassination by the Destroyers. As the leader of the Lizard Clan, Dupa played a large role in the War of the Champions, cooperating (and sometimes butting heads) with the Fire Bringer.

It took time for Dupa to trust the Zexen Knights especially. He attempted to repel the Harmonian invasion of Brass Castle alone before accepting their aid.

Dupa would continue to lead the Lizard Clan following the war, leading his people on the battlefield during clashes with the Zexen Federation.

trivia: • Dupa likes Armadillion meat. His jaw is so strong that he even eats the scales.

name: Edge
description: Edge (エッジ, Ejji) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Edge is a wandering swordsman who wields the legendary Celestial Sword.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Edge is a young man who wields the Celestial Sword, an incarnation of the Night Rune, one of the 27 True Runes. His personality is very calm, even when dealing with the often-complaining Celestial Sword, a rare talent. 

In the past, a vampire had attacked Edge's village, kidnapping his older sister. Viktor and Flik came across the village and on hearing Edge's desire to become a true warrior, Viktor allowed Edge to wield the Celestial Sword, defeating the vampire. Since then, he has looked up to Viktor.

Feigning concern about possible vampires still in the area, Viktor "loaned" the Celestial Sword to him before he and Flik departed for the Dunan Republic to aid in the Higheast Rebellion. Of course, Viktor was looking for a way to palm off the fussy sword for quite some time. Edge was unaware of this and gratefully accepted the sword.

As a means of making money, he would duel travelers with his sword on the line for potch. During the War of the Champions, he would challenge Hugo in Le Buque and come up short. Rather than give up the sword, which he saw as a beloved memento from a hero and mentor, Edge instead offered to fight for the Fire Bringer for the remainder of the conflict.

After the war, Edge would travel south in order to defeat a foe sought after by the Celestial Sword.

name: Eike
description: Eike (アイク, Aiku) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Eike is the faithful librarian of Budehuc Castle.

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
Eike is a thin man with grey, pallid skin and bulging eyes with small, dark pupils shadowed by heavy brow ridges. He has no visible eyebrows. His cheekbones are extremely prominent and he had heavy bags under his eyes. His receding, black hair is slightly unkempt, but kept in an approximation of a neat hairstyle.

He wears a brown waistcoat vest and suit pants over a white, full-length shirt and bolo tie. He wears white gloves with black arm bracers and black shoes. He is not often seen without a book of some sort in his hand.

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
Although he appears to be gloomy and eerie, in reality Eike is a polite and helpful person. He is also a voracious reader who once he starts reading a book, loses track of time easily. Although he prefers solitude, he is never rude or dismissive of others. He simply prefers to indulge his preferences when possible.

He also holds odd sleeping habits. When remembering to sleep, Eike would sleep with his eyes half open without turning while speaking in a strange, non-existent language.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Eike was the resident librarian of Budehuc Castle. Harboring a great love of books, he frequently immersed himself in reading and would often lose track of time, disappearing into the castle basement to read in solitude.

One such venture to his reading room in the castle basement led the residents of Budehuc Castle to assume he was missing was being an extensive search for him.

When found, Eike apologized, explaining how easy it was for him to lose track of time when reading. The mystery of how Eike came to be in the basement before the elevator there was repaired remained a mystery, though the residents were mainly relieved to find their friend.

Later, when Geddoe and his team infiltrated Budehuc Castle, they were discovered by Eike. Somehow he was able to surprise the group, despite their being professional mercenaries. He was also unfazed by having weapons drawn on him by Ace, who suspected him of being no ordinary man.

Although he didn't speak much on the matter, he was also a supporter of Thomas as castle lord and stood with him against the efforts of the Zexen Council to remove the young lord.

Eike's talents as a librarian were notable as he was able to find old documents relating to the history of the castle when needed and it was he who first realized the connection between Geddoe and the man who had been present at the negotiations surrounding Budehuc Castle some 50 years prior.

Following the War of the Champions, Eike continued to lead his Silent Reading Society, holding meetings in the deep basement of the castle.

name: Elaine
description: Elaine (エレーン, Erēn) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Elaine is a member of Duke's unit of the Harmonian Southern Frontier Defense Force.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Elaine is the only female member of Duke's unit of the Harmonian Southern Frontier Defense Force. She is extremely confident, not just in her talents as a mercenary but in her own good looks.

She has been in a relationship with Duke for some time and she has long been engaged in a war of words with Queen, the two getting along like cats and dogs.

She always supported Duke and backed him up in battle even after losing in battle to their rivals in the 12th Unit. She would join the Fire Bringer with the rest of her unit after they rescued Geddoe's 12th Unit from Mt. Senai. At Budehuc Castle, she was quite competitive about her looks.

Following the War of the Champions, Elaine married Duke and together the two continued their mercenary lifestyle in the SFDF.

trivia: • The lipstick Elaine uses was designed by Milich Oppenheimer and produced in the Kunan region of the Toran Republic.

name: Elliot
description: Elliot (エリオット, Eriotto) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Elliot is a young, timid child who dreams of adventure.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Elliot is a close friend of both Alanis and Melville and together the three of them formed the Saint Loa Knights. His dream is to become a treasure hunter just like Melville's father, but his mother opposes this. His family runs a bakery.

He has a very timid nature but is able to summon bravery in order to help his friends, such as when the trio investigated the North Cavern to find the ship Melville's father spoke of. Elliot is possessed of a strong sense of smell, which allows him to detect and track all manner of things. In fact, it said that he once defeated a dog in a smelling contest.

He would join the Fire Bringer after convincing his mother to move to Budehuc Castle. Following the War of the Champions, Elliot continued to dream of one day becoming a great treasure hunter, despite the continued opposition of his family.

trivia: • If you recruit Elliot with Hugo he will mention that his father was killed but his ending suggests that his father opposes his treasure hunter dream.

name: Emily
description: Emily (エミリー, Emirī) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Emily is the bright and peppy daughter of Ronnie Bell and Mose, who had both participated in the Gate Rune War some years before her birth.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Emily is the bright and peppy daughter of Ronnie Bell and Mose and is a claw-wielding fighter, much like her mother was during the Gate Rune War. A strong arm-wrestler, she wanted to find a strong person to train with. Seeking an opponent who was stronger than she was, Emily ran away from home and eventually made her way to the Grasslands.

In Chisha Village, she would lose an arm-wrestling contest to Hugo and agreed to join the Fire Bringer. She got along with Juan, the martial arts instructor of Budehuc Castle. Although she was occasionally frustrated by his laziness, the two fought together well. Like her mother, she was interested in homemaking and liked to study cooking. Still, her strength and energy sometimes made it difficult for her to work with delicate ingredients, eggs in particular seemed to frustrate her. 

Emily was ecstatic to be surrounded by so many strong people at the castle, so she opted to train regularly at every free moment. She would often take Koroku jogging, as many others present did not interact with him much, but she would often be seen carrying him back to the castle. Emily would also request arm-wrestling matches, both personally, and sometimes leaving them in the Suggestion Box.

Following the War of the Champions, she would serve as Juan's assistant instructor at the castle while continuing her own vigorous training.

trivia:
• Emily's weapon upgrades as Crow, Tiger Crow, and Dragon Crow. The first can't be seen without a cheating device. "Crow" was a mis-Romanization of "Claw" as her weapon names are identical to her mothers in Japanese.
• Emily can reach S level in her Swing skill.
• Emily has a unite attack with Juan, the Fighting Attack, in which the two dash about striking each foe. She has another with Sharon and Sanae Yamamoto, the Girl Attack, where Emily pummels the foe toward her cohorts who slam the enemy with their weapons in a manner akin to a baseball bat.

name: Erk de Forever
description: Erk de Forever (エーク・ド・フォーエバー, Ēku do Fōebā) is a fictional character who appears in Suikoden III. He is the hero of the Erk's Adventures series.
originalName: エーク・ド・フォーエバー

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Erk is the hero of Ace's Erk's Adventures series, serialized on the wall newspaper at Budehuc Castle. He is the world's strongest adventurer.

In his story, Erk navigated several traps at some Sindar ruins before meeting, and teaming up with, the treasure hunter Marine. They are soon confronted by Erk's archenemy, Baker, who kills Marine but not before she confesses her love. Enraged, Erk kills Baker in combat. He then realizes that the treasure he found would bring Marine back to life, restores her and then the two flee the collapsing ruins.

trivia: • Erk is a rather thinly disguised Mary Sue of Ace himself, down to the nearly identical first names (in Japanese, at least).

name: Ernie
description: Ernie (アーニー, Ānī) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Ernie is a tutor and student of ancient history and archeology.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Ernie was a tutor belonging to the Vinay del Zexay Association of Ancient History and working at Brass Castle. A prodigy from an early age, when she was younger she had studied in Greenhill City, the educational center of the Dunan Republic.

As a member of the Association of Ancient History, she specialized in history and after joining the Fire Bringer, she conducted research into the history and relics of the region, particularly those relating to the Sindar.

Following the War of the Champions, Ernie joined the Archeology Association and discovered the Secret Zexen Ritual Inscription at the Ceremonial Site.

name: Estella
description: Estella (エステラ, Esutera) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Estella is a mage given to almost compulsive levels of lying.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Estella is a magician who claims to be a student of the legendary Crowley. Although her talents are no lie, it is hard to determine anything about her due to her constant lying and teasing of others. She has taken Rody on as her apprentice and gets great joy out of teasing him with her lies and falsehoods.

She would encounter the Fire Bringer at the Alma Kinan Village and would send the group on a wild goose chase looking for an entrance to its all-male counterpart before agreeing to join. Kidd often fell for her lies, believing her bizarre origin story about being born from a rock 50 years ago, traveling to a country or location called Tenjik for an old magic book, and transforming the water imp Rody into a human being after they battled.

While at the castle, Estella would continue her habit of lying, but most people did not take much stock in what she said. She would write letters about Reed's hair, or how Hallec and Dupa were the same person, and generally horrible and/or dangerous advice on improving magic affinity. The highlight of these were suggestions to bury oneself in sand for Earth power, swim 20 laps in a bathtub for Water, and to be exposed to Bright's flame breath for Fire. If there ever was a purpose to any of this, it is not known. It doesn't even appear to be funny to her.

Following the War of the Champions, she would leave on a journey, apparently to find the 109th Star of Destiny.

trivia:
• Estella's weapon upgrades as Ruby Ring II, Ruby Ring IV. The first one, plain Ruby Ring, is not seen without a cheating device. For that matter, what happened to the Ruby Ring III?
• Estella's fanciful backstory as recounted by Kidd is merely the plot of Journey to the West, which is one of China's four great novels, along with the Shui Hu Zhuan, which inspired the Suikoden series.

name: Flame Champion
description: The Flame Champion (炎の英雄, Honō no Eiyū), localized as the Fire Hero in Suikoden II, is a character in Suikoden III. He is the legendary hero of the Grasslands struggle against Harmonian invasion 50 years before the events of the game.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Suikoden II & Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2
In the Dunan region as well as some fringes of the Grasslands, the Flame Champion was said to be the bearer of the True Fire Rune. Stories tell that the Flame Champion managed to steal this rune from the One Temple in Crystal Valley.

Not much information was known about him, save that he led a band called the Fire Bringer, which were often described as thieves, and that he was the larger-than-life hero of a war against the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia.

His identity would be co-opted by a bandit leader following the Dunan Unification War, with such instances possibly adding to this perception of the Flame Champion outside the Grasslands.
Suikoden III
The Flame Champion was the leader of the original Fire Bringer that fought against the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia.

He fought alongside his best friends, Geddoe and Wyatt Lightfellow during the war, bearing the True Fire Rune on his right hand. In 425, he ended the conflict after his True Fire Rune went out of control, decimating both forces, leading to a 50-year truce between the two sides.

During the conflict, he fell in love with Sana. After the war, chose to seal his rune using a Sindar technique and live a normal life in secret with her, bearing the pain this caused as well as losing the eternal youth a True Rune bestows on its bearer.

In fact, he only managed to live a few years in great pain before passing away, but those years with Sana were likely the most meaningful to him.

His legacy would have deep impact on the region, with many turning to the legends of the Flame Champion following the outbreak of the War of the Champions. As a person, he fought for nothing more complicated than the sole purpose of not being bound to anything, his freedom and to protect the important things…

trivia: • The Flame Champion's true name and home are unknown. Even in Suikoden spin-off media, he is only ever referred to as the Flame Champion.

name: Franz
description: Franz (フランツ, Furantsu) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. He is a collaborationist mantor trainer and rider from Le Buque.

facts.label: Origin
facts.value: Le Buque, Holy Kingdom of Harmonia

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
A mantor trainer and rider from Le Buque, even amongst the skilled riders from that village, Franz is a master of his craft. He rides the Mantor Ruby in combat.

As part of the assimilation policy the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia enacts on third-class subjects (subjects from territories that resisted Harmonian annexation), Franz was taken from Le Buque and raised in Crystal Valley from a young age. Still, Franz possessed his own will and has a strong belief in the strength and abilities of his hometown.

Ever protective of his childhood friend, Iku, Franz argued strongly for cooperation with Harmonia during the War of the Champions so that Le Buque may be granted second-class citizenship for their aid in the war.

Still, following Luc's abduction of the people of the Le Buque, Franz would agree to fight with the Fire Bringer for the remainder of the war. Although the people of Le Buque resented his cooperation with Harmonia, Geddoe would argue that he thought more about how to aid the village than anyone else.

Following the war, Franz would return to Le Buque and continue to work hard towards proving the village's worth and earning second-class status.

trivia: • Franz cuts his own hair. Iku usually ends up fixing the uneven mess that results.

name: Fred Maximillian
description: Fred Maximillian (フレッド・マクシミリアン, Fureddo Makushimirian) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Fred is the leader of the once famed Maximillian Knights.
originalName: フレッド・マクシミリアン

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Fred Maximillian is a young warrior who has taken over the mantle of leadership over the Maximillian Knights, the order that served the Scarlet Moon Empire before fighting against them in the Gate Rune War.

Like his grandfather, he envisions himself as a great defender of justice and sometimes this can lead him to be quite severe in his assumptions about other people and situations, causing him to act rashly.

Holding a great respect for his grandfather, Maximillian, the founder of the knights, Fred wishes to revive the good name of the order, hence his journeying across the land along with his squire, Rico.

After meeting and traveling with Chris Lightfellow, Fred judged the Harmonian invasion of the Grasslands as an act of evil and so fought in the War of the Champions as part of the Fire Bringer.

Follow the war, Fred quickly set off once again, hearing rumors that a new evil was once again budding.

name: Fubar
description: Fubar (フーバー, Fūbā) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. He is Hugo's lifelong companion.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Fubar was a particularly intelligent griffin, able to understand human speech and emotion to a great extent. Bearing the Shining Wind Rune perhaps indicated he came from a similar background as Feather. At the very least, his ability to use a rune was proof of his high intelligence.

Fubar had braclets, a favored accessory of the Karaya Clan, on his front legs. Hugo had placed them on Fubar to invoke the blessing of the spirits. Fubar has beautiful fur that is silky to the touch. His front feathers are white while his back feathers were black. Because of this, he was able to take advantage of the darkness when launching surprise attacks.

He first met Hugo when they both were four years old. Fubar had been found as a baby and brought back to Karaya Village. The two have been friends since they met but when he became a fully matured griffin, Fubar was to be imprisoned and sold off by Lucia. Hugo would release Fubar in the night and in return, Fubar rescued Hugo from a pack of wild wolves. Following this, Fubar was allowed to remain with the Karaya.

He faithfully accompanied Hugo on his travels, helping him escape from several perilous situations. In particular, Fubar was able to take advantage of the darkness to save Hugo and the others in Vinay del Zexay when they were seemingly cornered by the Zexen Knights with his signature surprise attack.

Although tame, he was a proud griffin and did not like to carry anyone other than Hugo. Sgt. Joe also seemed to understand him better than most. Fubar cared deeply about Hugo and was always willing to fight for him. He was a clean creature and loved to be brushed after taking a bath. His favorite food was meat and he once put a letter in the suggestion box at Budehuc Castle (handwritten on his behalf by Sgt. Joe) saying that he…

trivia:
• Probably more accurately localized as "Hoover."
• Fubar is one of three characters in Suikoden III who can serve as a mount during battle. The other two characters are Bright and Ruby who serve as mounts for Futch and Franz, respectively.

name: Gadget Z
description: Gadget Z (からくり丸Z, Karakurimaru Z) is a character in Suikoden III. He is an upgraded version of the original Gadget, built by Meg.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Gadget Z is a mechanical doll that accompanies Belle on her journey. It is as sentient as any human and believes itself to be an ally of justice.

Gadget Z previously fought in the Dunan Unification War as Gadget but years after the war, Meg was hit by lightning, striking her with inspiration and prompting a redesign. The result was Gadget being reborn as Gadget Z. It has twice the offensive power of the original Gadget, but it is also apparently three times as vulnerable. It participated in the War of the Champions, accompanying Belle when she joined the Fire Bringer.

Following the war, Gadget Z continued to travel with Belle, hoping to power up even further.

name: Galahad
description: Galahad (ガラハド, Garahado) is a character who is mentioned in Suikoden III. Galahad was the leader of the Zexen Knights just prior to the beginning of the game.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Galahad was the leader of the Zexen Knights just prior to the War of the Champions. He was a talented leader who held the trust and loyalty of his knights, including Chris Lightfellow. He was killed in a battle, along with Vice-Captain Pelize, against the Grasslands clans, prompting Chris' rise to acting captain.

As a leader, he had a strong eye for talent, recommending Roland Lesaurus for the knights despite opposition. Percival Fraulein and Leo Gallen, all future members of the Six Mighty Knights, were also highly recommended and supported by Galahad.

trivia: • In Arthurian legend, Sir Galahad is a knight of King Arthur's Round Table, renowned for his gallantry and purity.

name: Gau
description: Gau (ガウ, Gau) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. He is the kobold member of Duke's unit of the Southern Frontier Defense Force.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Gau is a kobold warrior and a member of the 14th unit of the Harmonian Southern Frontier Defense Force. He wielded a spear with a forked blade in battle.

A rouge kobold, his past is unknown but when leaving his previous line of work, his tail was cut off as compensation. Sometime after that, his life was saved by Duke and since then he has worked with Duke in the SFDF.

During the War of the Champions, he was among the most loyal of soldiers, risking his life time and again for Duke. When Duke was defeated in a duel by Geddoe, Gau would stand up for his leader, fiercely defending Duke as a man in no way inferior to Geddoe. He greatly respected both Duke's leadership and tenacity.

Gau would become a member of the Fire Bringer when Duke aligned the 14th Unit with the group. As a member of the Fire Bringer, he was respectful to its leaders and a great asset on the battlefield alongside his comrades.

He was disdainful of kobold stereotypes, remarking that not all kobolds were like the jovial Muto (though he also admitted there were many like that). He was also determined to settle things with Geddoe's 12th Unit in the future.

After the war, he left the SFDF and began traveling on a journey of training. He would still return to Caleria often to help out Duke.

trivia: • Gau's favorite food is spring rolls and he prefers hard food in general.

name: Geddoe
description: Geddoe (ゲド, Gedo) is one of the protagonists of Suikoden III.

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
Geddoe is a tall, slightly gaunt man with medium-length black hair and a single dark eye. His right eye was lost at some point, necessitating the use of an eyepatch.

He wears a dark long coat, with grey and white vertical straps on the front. He also wears black trousers, as well as brown gloves and boots. He bears the True Lightning Rune on his right hand and his glove conceals it completely. He wielded a wide broadsword in battle.

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
Geddoe is a quiet and unsociable character. Although quiet, he commands a strong presence as a mercenary. His colleagues also trust him deeply despite his unwilling to talk about his past. He was often apologetic about being unwilling to share, as he trusted his comrades and knew they trusted him in turn.

As a man of great experience, Geddoe had something of a sixth sense regarding people especially those of an unsavory nature. He was one of the few people who noticed Mike's hidden dark nature, for example.

He had always admired and was envious of the Flame Champion's free-spirited way of life, the way he bore the True Fire Rune but was never bound by it. He was also jealous of and somewhat resented his friend's decision to seal the rune and live the remainder of his life with Sana. In comparison, Geddoe felt trapped by his fate as a True Rune bearer and abandoned his duty as a hero of the Grasslands.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
In the past, he had sustained the loss of his right eye, requiring the use of a patch. Some 70 years ago, he would inherit the True Lightning Rune, becoming its bearer in some sort of tragedy. This happened near the Karaya Clan and since then he disliked being on Karaya lands due to the negative memories involved, even referred to it as a "cursed land".

He was a close friend of the Flame Champion, helping the original Fire Bringer to fight against the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia 50 years ago. As a member of the Fire Bringer, Geddoe played a key role in the war against Harmonia. He was also the leader behind making the territory of Budehuc Castle common land. Following that war, he travelled the world, through many unnamed countries before becoming a mercenary and hiding his identity.

Geddoe served as the leader of the 12th Unit of the Harmonian Southern Frontier Defense Force. He joined the force some seven or eight years prior to the outbreak of the War of the Champions. Once he was ordered by the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia to investigate the rumors of the Fire Bringer, his situation began to change.

Geddoe and his unit would follow clues to the Great Hollow and the Karaya Village. Jimba was surprised to see him on Karaya lands due to his bad memories of the area, but Geddoe's sense of purpose overcame such sensitivities. He was able to determine that there was a conspiracy inciting war between the Zexen Federation and the clans of the Grasslands.

Realizing that the Masked Man was involved, he followed his trail to Le Buque before traveling to Budehuc Castle and eventually the Flame Champion's Hideaway.

Although he harbored some resentment towards the Flame Champion for his decision to set aside his True Fire Rune, the realization that Luc was planning to obtain the…

trivia:
• He gets the highest rating in Fire Magic of any possible Flame Champion, although most tend to keep him with Lightning, which he is still best at.
• Geddoe's age is revealed as 112, although he had apparently stopped aging somewhere in his 30's.

name: Gezel Hyatt
description: Gezel Hyatt (ゲセル・ハイアット, Geseru Haiatto) is a character who is mentioned in Suikoden III. Gezel Hyatt established the port in the city of Vinay del Zexay.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Gezel Hyatt is said to be the driving force behind the founding of the Zexen Federation. He was originally a trader in deer antlers and was so successful he amassed a substantial fortune in a single generation.

Choosing to invest his wealth in private property, he built a port in the town of Vinay del Zexay, thus increasing its prosperity even further. This led to an increase in trade which would serve as the catalyst of the establishment of the Zexen Federation in 420.

name: Goro
description: Goro (ゴロウ, Gorō) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Goro is a young bath maker eager to surpass his father's techniques.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Goro was born in the Dunan Republic as the son of Tetsu, a master bath-maker who had fought among the 108 Stars during the Dunan Unification War.

From his youth, Goro inherited his father's craft, dedicating himself to the art of bath-making with a singular ambition: to create baths so soothing that bathers would lose themselves in relaxation, drifting into slumber. 

Nineteen years of age when the War of the Champions erupted, Goro was spotted in Zexen Forest where he agreed to go to Budehuc Castle as it was one of the few places both large enough and willing to let him construct a bath. There, he constructed a bath within Budehuc Castle's shipwrecked vessel for his new allies and civilians to enjoy.

His baths became gathering places, where allies and strangers alike shared conversations and unwound their burdens. Yet they were also stages for unexpected dramas such as quarrels between Duke and Edge, Roland’s vow never to share waters with Kenji again, and the mysterious discussions of Ace, Borus, and Nash beneath the steam.

Yet, for all his skill, Goro remained locked in a quiet rivalry with his father, whose mastery he had yet to equal. When the war ended, Goro challenged Tetsu to a contest of craftsmanship. Though he poured his soul into his work, his father's baths still reigned supreme.

Defeat, however, only sharpened his resolve. Gathering his tools, he set forth on a journey across the land, seeking rare woods and new techniques to refine his art.

name: Guillaume
description: Guillaume (ギョーム, Gyōmu) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Guillaume is a crooked appraiser and antiques dealer.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Guillaume is a merchant who ran an appraisal shop in Vinay del Zexay. After a time, he grew tired of the work and started dealing in antiques. Underneath that, he is merely a small, cowardly villain, working in partnership with thieves to recover items from the people he sold them to and then sell them again in his stores.

He sometimes demonstrates bizarre behavior, but it's been suggested that he's not truly a bad person, just childish. He likes to play with small children in an innocent manner, although he has a problem respecting personal boundaries.

After harassing Shabon, he would be defeated in a duel by the Fire Bringer. Later, he would try and break into the underground Sindar ruins before being stopped by the Fire Bringer once more and would end up joining the group as a result.

At Budehuc Castle, he reopened his appraisal shop, bringing financial support to the Fire Bringer. He was rumored, however, to replace valuable items he appraises with fakes, keeping the originals for himself. Following the War of the Champions, he continued to run the shop even though he had quickly tired of the work.

name: Hallec
description: Hallec (ハレック, Harekku) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. He is a warrior in search for the fabled Flame Champion.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Described as a barbarian warrior, Hallec hailed from one of the countries that form the Nameless Lands, north of the Grasslands. Clad in animal skins and possessing a powerful war cry, Hallec was a proud warrior who never lied and had vowed to protect the weak and help the people of his village.

He wielded two twin-headed axes in battle. This axe was so large that each weapon would be considered a two-handed battle axe by most people but Hallec was able to wield them as a pair of one-handed axes due to his size and strength.

In the past, Hallec's village was engulfed in local conflict, and it was the Flame Champion who resolved the situation. So, when an epidemic hit his home during the War of the Champions, Hallec would travel to the Grasslands seeking the Flame Champion's aid once again. He would meet and journey with Mua on his quest before encountering Hugo and joining the Fire Bringer.

Following the war, at Hugo's recommendation, he would return home with Tuta and other physicians, helping to save his village from the epidemic that had plagued it.

trivia: • One of Estella's many wild claims is that Hallec and Dupa are, in fact, the same person.

name: Hikusaak
description: Hikusaak (ヒクサク, Hikusaku) is a character mentioned and referenced in Suikoden II, Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2 and Suikoden III. He is also mentioned in background supplements for Suikoden. He is purportedly the leader of the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia.

name: Hortez VII
description: Hortez VII (ホルテスVII世, Horutesu VII Sei), full name Nefentitiri Radames Ampenhetep Iruiktanas Antikoseus Puto Remaios Kureakias Dimetirios Farasmuseian Ishak Saraj Amenenuhet Tatomes Hatshepsut Toto Ank Amen Hortez VI's son, Hortez VII (ネフェンティティリ・ラダメス・アメンヘテプ・イルイクトーナス・アンティコセウス・プトレマイオス・クレアキオス・ディメティリオス・フアロスムセイオン・イスハーク・サッラージュ・アメンエヌヘト・トウトウーメス・ハトシェプスト・トウト・アンク・アメン・ホルテスVI世の息子、ホルテスVII世, Nefentitiri Radamesu Amenhetepu Iruikutōnasu Antikoseusu Putoremaiosu Kureakiosu Dimetiriosu Fuarosu

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Hortez VII is a haughty scroll maker from a very wealthy foreign nation. His speech patterns are those of someone born from royalty or the aristocracy, but his arrogant personality is somewhat offset by his natural air headedness and his cute habit of getting lost easily due to his extreme lack of a sense of direction. This eclectic nature usually confounds people he meets.

During the War of the Champions, Hortez VII came to the Grasslands in order to open a scroll making store at Budehuc Castle. However, it took the efforts of the Fire Bringer to bring him there as he ended up in Duck Village, Chisha Village, Caleria and the Duck Village once more before finally being guided to Budehuc Castle by being convinced to take what to most people would be the wrong route.

Hortez VII's country, and Hortez VII himself, are almost immeasurably wealthy, causing him to pity almost everyone for their relative poverty. When he caught Guillaume attempting to steal his antiques, for example, he gave the appraiser some gold out of pity, such was his obvious destitution. His store at Budehuc Castle was a success and he was planning to bring over his three wives from his homeland contingent on that success.

However, following the end of the war, he decided to travel to the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia and open a store there. This he did by taking a ship from Vinay del Zexay, a most circuitous route, if nothing else.

name: Iku
description: Iku (イク, Iku) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Iku is a kind-hearted mantor trainer torn between her love for Franz and her village's traditions.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Iku is a young, friendly girl, in training to be a nurse, whose duty it was to take care of the mantors in the village of Le Buque.

She was the childhood friend and later the love of Franz. Although she did not entirely agree with his beliefs over the future of Le Buque, she nonetheless believed in his reasons.

She would join the Fire Bringer along with Franz after the people of Le Buque were rescued from Mt. Senai. There, she would take care of the mantors at Budehuc Castle.

Following the War of the Champions, Iku would continue to take her relationship with Franz slowly, while simultaneously deciding to live with him.

name: Jacques
description: Jacques (ジャック, Jakku) is a major character in Suikoden III. He is the taciturn archer of Geddoe's 12th unit of the Harmonian Southern Frontier Defense Force.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Jacques was the newest member of Geddoe's unit in the Harmonian Southern Frontier Defense Force. He grew up in the mountains inland and had never seen the ocean before joining the SFDF, though he had traveled on his own.

He was recruited into SFDF shortly before the War of the Champions when he helped to snipe a group of pursuers hunting Geddoe when the chase took them to the forest Jacques was living in. Though Jacques had not shot the pursuers to save Geddoe specifically, respecting his temperament and precise marksmanship, Geddoe would recruit Jacques for his unit on the spot. As an expert bow user, he was in charge of the 12th Unit's long-range attacks, a role the unit felt that needed someone for for quite some time.

His weapon of choice was a huge bow gun. Due to its size, it was much more powerful than a normal bow. Though such a large weapon may have seemed too large to handle, it is likely that some effort had been made to reduce its weight so that even the relatively slender Jacques could handle it. He would also hold his bow gun close to the ground to stabilize it. In addition, Jacques wore rather thick clothes, to protect him from tree branches and the like, as he often hide in foliage as a sniper.

Jacques was a very taciturn young man but although he may have seemed aloof or distant at first glance, he was actually a friendly person with very deep feelings. He did not express his opinions often but held a strong conviction when he did so. He liked children and animals, playing with them in his free time. He was known to play with dogs, enjoying himself even though his somber expression never changed.

He would be a part of the 12th unit's investigation of the Flame Champion rumors, seeing the ocean for the first time when their journey took them to…

trivia: • Jacques' Japanese name, Jack, completes the card motif of Geddoe's unit: Ace, Jack, Joker and Queen, with Geddoe as the King. He participates in the spectacular Mercenary B attack with them.

name: Jefferson
description: Jefferson (ジェファーソン, Jefāson) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Jefferson is an "appointer", giving titles to people he encounters.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
A man of rigid military bearing, Jefferson stood as an unyielding advocate for hierarchy, convinced that titles and offices were the foundation of peace and prosperity. His life’s purpose was the imposition of such order.

During the tumult of the War of the Champions, Jefferson marched uninvited into Budehuc Castle and began bestowing ranks upon its inhabitants with the solemnity of a general issuing battlefield commissions. Though many dismissed him as a zealot, none could deny his fervor.

He maintained detailed ledgers, recording the strengths (and perceived failings) of his comrades, all while devising ever more elaborate titles. "Ninja President," "Aesthetic Manager," such designations flowed from his pen with the seriousness of imperial edicts.

His disdain for undisciplined youth was matched only by his obsession with morning drills, which he deemed essential for cultivating both body and spirit. Though not as vital as holding the proper title, of course.

To Jefferson, titles were not mere words but the very framework of civilization. Yet his solemn proclamations often came across as absurd and frivolous, such as when he dubbed Cecil and Joan with nonsensical honors. That he saw no contradiction in this only deepened his infamy.

Even after the war’s end, he remained a fixture in the castle, lying in wait for new arrivals so that he might subject them to his bureaucratic whims.

Following the war, he continued to live at the castle, conferring more titles to new arrivals.

name: Johanna
description: Johanna (ジョアナ, Joana) is a character who is mentioned in Suikoden III. Johanna was the mentor of Salome Harras.

sections.title: Story
sections.body: Johanna was Salome's teacher in the field of military strategy. His book, known as the War Chronicle, is a work that Salome treats as scripture when it comes to tactical matters.

trivia: • The mention of Johanna was cut from the English-language localisation of Suikoden III.

name: Joker
description: Joker (ジョーカー, Jōkā), also known as Wang (ワン, Wan) is a major character in Suikoden III who first appears in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2. Joker is the aging veteran martial artist of the 12th Unit of the Harmonian Southern Frontier Defense Force.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Joker held the position of being the eldest member, second only to Geddoe himself, in the 12th Unit of the Harmonian Southern Frontier Defense Force. As a man of many interests, Joker had an unrivalled drinking ability and was always on the lookout for a pretty girl to spend his time with.

He shared a playful and somewhat comedic dynamic with fellow defense force member, Ace, engaging in banter and belittlement. This relationship showed the understanding and trust between each other and helped to relax tensions in the group, even during dangerous missions. Nevertheless, this did not diminish his occasional displays of insightful wisdom. His unique talent in lip-reading further enhanced his capabilities as an adept information gatherer and he was highly trusted by Geddoe. At heart, he was a kind and loyal man.

Within the unit, he was renowned for his mastery of magic attacks, especially fire magic, a skill that set him apart. Joker also boasted proficiency in the martial arts, making him a consummate professional on the battlefield. He used balls made of iron or steel in battle, holding them to deliver more weight and force to his blows. He eschewed larger weapons like swords as they would impede his movement. This is also the reason why his clothing emphasized mobility and ease of movement.

His passion for combat fueled his boundless energy, and he had been active as a mercenary since his youth. Under the alias "Wang," Joker once encountered Nash Latkje when they both served in the SFDF and even imparted fighting techniques to him. When Nash attempted to escape from Caleria Fortress during his search for Zaj Quiroz, Joker was dispatched to stop him.

Remarkably, Joker, who had the strength and ability to swiftly overpower Nash, deliberately stalled the encounter…

trivia:
• Contrary to the belief Joker uses fist weapons, he carries massaging balls to strengthen his own hands to attack. Please explain how sharpening these helps in any way!
• Joker has the Twister attack with Kenji and Juan, making him the only SFDF member to have one outside of the unit.

name: Juan
description: Juan (ジョアン, Joan) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Juan is the lackadaisical bujutsu instructor of Budehuc Castle.

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
Juan has short blonde hair with a shorn fringe. He is never seen with a leaf in his mouth, which he chews to help him fall asleep easier.

He wears a blue tunic vest with red accents over a white shirt and blue and white pants. Underneath he wears unassuming brown clothing.

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
Although said to be motivated by vengeance, Juan's laid-back attitude and reluctance to involve himself in affairs presents him as lazy and shiftless. Regardless, he is an extremely skilled martial artist and even his propensity to falling asleep in battle was said to be a sign of his confidence in his skills.

Juan is also willing to fight when others are placed in danger and he finds it necessary. His lack of enthusiasm is in part due to being let down by previous lords of Budehuc Castle. When suitable invested, he is willing to risk himself in battle against any manner of opponent, no matter how nervous he is.

His drowsiness is apparently caused by the leaves he chews on that grow around the castle, though his overall laziness is seemingly innate.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Juan was a bujutsu instructor who resided at Budehuc Castle. Although talented at martial arts, he could usually be found merely napping throughout the day. He would often be seen chewing on leaves that were said to help him doze off.

Juan's motivation was supposedly the desire to defeat a traveling drifter who passed through his father's dojo, humiliating him in combat. That is the reason he opened his own dojo at Budehuc Castle, in the hopes that this wandering fighter would come and challenge him. Though some would argue that if he truly sought revenge, he would leave to find this drifter.

Despite his lazy reputation, Juan would fight when he felt he was needed. When a Wild Boar attempted to attack the young Thomas-kun on Mt. Hei-Tou it was Juan who first stepped forward to confront the beast, allowing the others to get the child to safety.

He did not place much faith in Thomas as Lord of Budehuc Castle at first, as the several previous appointed lords had bailed on the castle crew. Juan would, however, grow to admire Thomas' efforts and would support him over time. When the Zexen Council attempted to remove Thomas from the position, Juan would even stand up to Leo and Percival of the Six Mighty Knights to protect the young lord.

Juan's actions in particular who galvanize the people of the castle, giving them the hope they needed to protect their home. When the War of the Champions came to Budehuc, Juan would go on to fight as a member of the Fire Bringer.

As a bujutsu instructor, he helped to develop the talents of the many fighters who resided at the castle during the war. He personally tutored such high-profile Fire Bringer members such as the Castle Master Thomas, Lilly Pendragon, and Fred Maximillian. He would also train with Emily, taking her under his…

trivia:
• The sleep effect of Juan's Waking Rune can be mitigated with a Yellow Scarf or a Taikyoku Tunic, although this prevents the fury status from occurring as well.
• Despite a weapon named Shunmin, he does not have any affiliation with the character Shun Min.

name: Kathy
description: Kathy (キャシィー, Kyashī) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Kathy is a young rancher, wandering the Grasslands with her team of horses.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Kathy is a cheerful rancher who loves horses and is always thinking of their well-being. She lives a nomadic life, bringing her horses to pastures to graze.

She was the daughter of Billy, who had been in a relationship with her mother in between his travels. When he left to continue his wanderings, Kathy's mother was already pregnant with her, though Billy did not know. Either way, Kathy had no particular interest in her father's identity. As a result, she is the half-sister of Billy's other child, Melville.

She encountered the Fire Bringer on the North Amur Plain and agreed to come to Budehuc Castle when they helped her count the horses that had spread across the plain.

As a horse lover, she felt unable to turn down the opportunity to manage a ranch full of horses. There, she ran the Budehuc Castle ranch, admiring the varied breeds of horse there, and following the War of the Champions branched out into holding horse races.

trivia:
• Kathy loves steak, a habit that apparently interrupts her dieting plans.
• She tries to avoid getting freckles whenever possible.

name: Kenji
description: Kenji (ケンジ, Kenji) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Kenji is an enthusiastic gymnastics instructor.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Kenji is an intense gymnastic teacher always willing to push himself, and others, to the limit. He rises at six o'clock every morning and runs through a large training regimen every day, including jogging, aerobics and stretches.

He's a big man but is still very limber and athletic. Although he doesn't speak of it often, Kenji is apparently married with a child, or at least he insists so.

After meeting the Fire Bringer on the Yaza Plain, he would move to Budehuc Castle and teach everyone in gymnastics. Although large, he's very fast although he could not beat Percival's horse in a foot race. Apparently, a big hit with kids, Kenji found lots of time to coach them with all of his downtime at the castle.

Following the war, he would settle at Budehuc and open up a gymnastic school for the children and become even more popular with all the young residents there.

trivia: • Kenji, along with Jefferson, make up some of the most weird, anachronistic characters in the series.

name: Kidd
description: Kidd (キッド, Kiddo) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Kidd is a boy detective whose cases usually turn out to be less than meets the eye.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Kidd is a boy detective who puts on a red eye-mask and starts his investigations whenever an incident occurs. Although possessed with a heavy belief in his abilities, he usually remains unaware that the incidents he investigates inevitably turn out to be nothing worth fussing about.

During the War of the Champions he worked together with Hugo to solve a murder case in the Duck Village (the victim turned out to be asleep after practicing yoga and not, in fact, a mangled corpse). Joining the Fire Bringer after this case, he was available to investigate members of the group, compiling an impressive numbers of profiles. 

His other cases in Duck Village were similar in nature. An allegedly kidnapped girl turned out to be merely wandering and the chief's stolen salted fish turned out to have been eaten by his wife. Still, Kidd remains undeterred. To him, it's the detective's theories and not the reality that mark a detective as great or not.

Following the war, Kidd took up the investigation of a kidnapped lady of the Black Rose Society in the Nameless Lands.

trivia: • Kidd's design is loosely based on Conan Edogawa, the protagonist of the manga and anime series Detective Conan/Case Closed.

name: Kogoro
description: Kogoro (コゴロウ, Kogorou) is a minor character who appears in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Kogoro is a black-haired dog who wears a blue wrapping cloth around its neck. His bark is "A-e-i-o-u!". He was found by the Fire Bringer in the Ruins of Cyndar and was subsequently adopted and brought back to Budehuc Castle.

He hates radishes and his absolute favorite food is cold miso soup. Due to his bizarre behavior and ability to dance on his hind legs, some people think he may not be a dog at all. The only thing in his wrapping cloth is a stone, just like Koroku's.

name: Koichi
description: Koichi (コーイチ, Kōichi) is a minor character who appears in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Koichi is a dog who wears a red wrapping cloth around its neck. His bark is "Ruff! Ruff!". He was found by the Fire Bringer behind a barrel in the North Cavern and was subsequently adopted and brought back to Budehuc Castle.

He likes to takes naps and his favourite food is bones. Some worry about him getting fat due to lack of exercise. The only thing in his wrapping cloth is a stone, just like Koroku's.

name: Koroku
description: Koroku (コロク, Koroku) is a supporting character who appears in Suikoden III. He has his own, optional, chapter in the game which allows him to explore Budehuc Castle.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Koroku is a dog who wears a green wrapping cloth around its neck which contains stones. His bark is "Aroooooo!". His features suggest he is a mongrel mix of some type. He was found by the Fire Bringer at Mt. Hei-Tou and was subsequently adopted and brought back to Budehuc Castle.

His favourite food is bones, and his hobby is marking his territory. He is very friendly with Cecile and forms part of her castle guard along with the other dogs; Connie, Koichi, Kogoro and Kosanji. However, he does not seem to like Nash and will go out of his way to pee on him.

name: Kosanji
description: Kosanji (コサンジ, Kosanji) is a minor character who appears in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Kosanji is a dog who wears a yellow wrapping cloth around its neck. His bark is "O-Oooo!". He was found by the Fire Bringer struggling on a ledge at Mt. Senai and was subsequently adopted and brought back to Budehuc Castle.

He loves to climb cliffs and always works hard so that others might recognise his efforts. His favourite food is breadcrumbs and the only thing in his wrapping cloth is a stone, just like Koroku's.

name: Lanchet
description: Lanchet (ランチェ, Ranche) is a character who is mentioned in Suikoden III. Lanchet was a member of the Zexen Knights.

sections.title: Story
sections.body: Lanchet was a highly esteemed and high ranking member of the Zexen Knights, though he was not one of the Six Knights of Zexen. Lanchet would be killed in battle along with Myriam when the Lizard Clan launched a surprise attack on the knights, breaching their cease-fire agreement thinking that Chief Zepon had been assassinated by the knights.

name: Landis
description: Landis (ランディス, Randisu) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Landis is a gloomy Winged Horde with a penchant for scaring people.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Landis is a brooding Winged Horde who carries a huge scythe reminiscent of the Grim Reaper. In fact, his manner is dark enough that some say he is an acquaintance of Death himself.

When the Fire Bringer passed through Kuput Forest, he just tagged along without introducing and returning with the group to Budehuc Castle, settling in its basement. There, he spent his time unnerving people with ghost stories and his general behavior. He was fond of Elliot, meaning he liked to scare him with ghost stories. His odd behavior included an inclination to walking on his hands and digging a grave for every fish he ate.

He disappeared as suddenly as he joined the Fire Bringer after the War of the Champions.

trivia:
• There is speculation that he and Sid are relatives of some sort, as they share a similar look, demeanor and race, but there is no indication in the games that the two even know each other.
• Landis' weapon upgrades as Calamity to Fatal Calamity. The first name, Bad Luck, cannot be seen without a cheating device.
• Landis gains the unique skills Freeze and Thief, both of which can be upgraded to the S level.
• Landis has a unite attack, Condemnation, with the ninja Ayame. She holds the target still while Landis slices through both of them without hesitation.

name: Leo Gallen
description: Leo Gallen (レオ・ガラン, Reo Garan) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Leo is one of the Six Mighty Zexen Knights.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Leo was one of the renowned Six Mighty Knights, the most skilled of the Zexen Knights. Leo was born the son of a nobleman, and chose to join the Zexen Knights because he had been interested in martial arts since he was a child.

As his appearance suggested, he had a brash personality as well as fearless on the battlefield. He struggled with more nuanced aspects of knightdom such as politics. He was a heavy drinker. He was also competitive and hated to lose at anything. His size and unrivaled strength, combined with his imposing armor, custom made for his frame, led him to gain the nickname "Iron Leo".

When Leo first entered the ranks of the Zexen Knights, he had brute strength but was not much with a sword, leading to many sullen hours practicing futilely. It would be Knight Captain Galahad who would suggest that Leo leverage his power by wielding an axe in battle, restoring Leo's zeal.

As a result of Galahad's advice, Leo was soon one of the most powerful knights. After the death of Galahad, Leo felt guilty for not being there to protect his captain. He would swear to protect Chris Lightfellow with his life to make up for this.

Once the War of the Champions broke out, Leo was one of the knights who led the Zexen raid on the Great Hollow. There, he clashed with Percival Fraulein slightly. Percival cited the need for calm on the battlefield but Leo was determined to rouse his men with a passionate speech following the loss of their countrymen in the surprise attack.

After joining the Fire Bringer, he engaged in a series of long bath matches with Wan Fu and Mua, two men also proud of their power. Leo's strength never faltered in the conflict, even after the final battle at the Ceremonial Site where, surrounded by monsters, Leo vowed to fight his way out…

trivia:
• Leo's weapon upgrades as Schnabel, Schutozahn, Horn, German for beak, "protection tooth" and horn. The second name is incorrectly transcribed, it should be Schutzzahn. It's noteworthy that Schutzzahn is not a word known in German.
• Leo gets up to S level in his Damage and Armor Protect skill. He gets the unique skills Adrenaline Power and Fighting Spirit, able to reach S and A levels in these, respectively. As awesome as his Melee gets, Leo's Magic ability might as well be nonexistent.
• Leo participates in the Knight B attack with Chris, and Borus or Percival. This only works when the party are mounted on horses. Leo also participates in the Manly Attack with Mua or Wan Fu.

name: Louis Keeferson
description: Louis Keeferson (ルイス・キフアーソン, Ruisu Kifuāson) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Louis is a Zexen Knight apprentice and the squire of Chris Lightfellow.
originalName: ルイス・キフアーソン

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Louis was a boy training to be a Zexen Knight and assisting as Chris Lightfellow's squire. He was the youngest of seven children and despite being part of an aristocratic family, his large number of siblings made him unlikely to inherit. As such, he was trained in chivalry and manners as a knight's apprentice.

His father was a friend of Wyatt Lightfellow and the family had helped Chris following the loss of her father, so Louis was especially assigned to Chris' care and training on her request. Due to his age, Louis was not allowed to fight in battle. What he learned from Chris as an attendant were manners and chivalry. Louis was very devoted to Lady Chris and his devotion touched her heart in turn.

Though his mild-mannered and gentle personality made him well-liked and a soothing presence for the battle-fatigued knights, it also lent itself towards Louis being sub-par at swordsmanship. However, bearing his family's expectations, he continued to strive to improve himself and his courage and readiness to serve were certainly worthy of a knight candidate.

Despite his struggles Louis was determined to serve Chris as a squire should. When Chris attacked the Karaya Village at the outbreak of the War of the Champions, he vowed to protect Chris despite being extremely nervous about being on the battlefield himself. In fact, it is possible that his warning cry to Chris is what allowed her to deflect Lulu's attack on her.

Later, Chris would collapse in the Zexen Forest on the way to Vinay del Zexay, worrying Louis greatly. Taking Chris to Brass Castle, Louis would show his worry when she awoke, raising his voice, something very unusual for the polite youth. As a squire, he was well aware that it was impolite to speak his mind so but he did not want to see the knight he…

name: Lowma
description: Lowma (ロウマ, Rouma) is a minor character from Suikoden III. He is a member of the Zexen Council and the father of Thomas.

name: Luce
description: Luce (ルース, Rūsu) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Luce is the maternally inclined mother of Lulu.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Resident of Karaya Village, Luce stood as both the mother of several warrior sons and as a confidant of Lucia, the Karaya chieftain. While her husband journeyed through southern lands, Luce maintained their household.

A woman of hearty laughter and thunderous scoldings, Luce became legendary among the Karaya Clan for her nurturing nature. Her home would became a sanctuary that embraced not only her children but also Jimba, a wandering soul she embraced as her own.

The War of the Champions brought unspeakable tragedy to this vibrant woman. When news came of Lulu's death in battle, she scolded her absent son as though he stood before her, unable to confront the truth of his death. The subsequent death of Jimba, her adopted son, compounded her sorrow.

Yet Luce's strength and largeness of heart endured. Within the walls of Budehuc Castle, her loud voice and ready meals provided solace to warriors and refugees alike. Kidd noted how she would insist on overfeeding visitors, making the tom yum kung that her family once enjoyed together. She also kept busy by helping to prepare props for the castle's theater.

In messages left for the castle's Suggestion Box, she wrote about missing Karayan cooking and longing for the open grasslands. She also articulated her driving purpose after the losses she suffered, to help those who lost family during the war in any way she can.

After the war's conclusion, Luce transformed her home into a refuge for children orphaned by the conflict, creating a new family from the ruins of war. The woman who had lost her sons now became mother to many.

trivia: • If Chris is chosen as the player's Flame Champion, Luce will not write any letters for the Suggestion Box.

name: Lulu
description: Lulu (ルル, Ruru) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Lulu is the precocious best friend of Hugo.

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
Lulu is a Karayan youth with brown skin, black and red hair, and bright blue eyes. His red hair is not dyed and, much like Hugo, is a remnant of his ancestry and is viewed as a blessing by the Karaya.

Lulu wears light Karayan-styled clothing, including short pants, a rarity among the Karaya, as he enjoyed running around in the fields and plains. His bracelets are purple, and his neck accessories were the same color.

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
A bright, lively child filled with curiosity, he is well-liked by everyone and was raised with a lot of love and affection. Unlike his mother, he did not shy away from the sight and smell of iron, finding his brothers' sacrifice a point of great pride rather than sadness.

Lulu wields a jambiya shorter and thinner than those of other Karaya due to his youth. Lulu named his weapon "Sut", which means "Son". The name was given by Lulu to express his desire to fight alongside his deceased brothers.

Lulu holds his weapon backward in order to emphasize the speed with which he could strike. His use of a Sword of Cyclone Rune also emphasizes his focus on speed in battle. He is a fast runner and one of his boasts is that he is even faster than Hugo, who is three years older than him. In truth, the two often trade victories when they race but Lulu's determination means he wins slightly more often.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
thumb|left|220px|Lulu's mother Luce was very protective of Lulu.
Lulu was the loving son of Luce and a close friend of Hugo since childhood.

His older brothers had all previously died in battle with the Zexen Federation, prompting Lulu's desire to become a full-fledged warrior as soon as possible. Like Hugo, he trained in fighting from Sgt. Joe but was not as dutiful as Hugo as he was younger, mischievous, and more prone to slacking off.

Before the outbreak of the War of the Champions, he accompanied Hugo, Sgt. Joe, and Fubar to Vinay del Zexay as part of a peace delegation. Wanting to be recognized as a warrior and bring pride to his mother, he insisted on accompanying the group to help protect his home and further his journey to becoming a true Karayan warrior.

He was vehemently distrustful of the Zexen Knights, having lost his brothers in wars against them. As a result, he felt uncomfortable in Zexen, especially after the frosty reaction he received from bumping into Louis Keeferson in Brass Castle, and frustrated by their reception from the Zexen Council, feeling slighted and disrespected.

Still, he was friendly with the Zexen children Melville, Alanis and Elliot, holding no animosity towards them, and accompanied them on their investigation of the North Cavern. Seeing something of himself in the three St. Loa Knights, he enjoyed helping to battle the bandits in the cavern as a proud Karayan.

thumb|right|220px|Lulu confronts Chris Lightfellow|Chris in the burning [[Karaya Village.]]
Forced to escape Vinay del Zexay due to the outbreak of the war, Lulu and his companions managed to pass through Brass Castle despite Lulu's inability to keep calm, thanks to a forgotten hidden passage. Returning home, Lulu realized that the Zexen Knights had attacked the Karaya…

name: Mamie
description: Mamie (メイミ, Meimi), localized as Mei Mi in Suikoden II, is a supporting character in Suikoden III who is mentioned in Suikoden II. Mamie is a traveling chef.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Mamie is the daughter of the talented chef Bashok. As a baby, she contracted an incurable disease which would have cost far too much potch to treat. The Black Dragon Cooking Clan would fund Mamie's treatment but only in exchange for Bashok's loyalty. And so, Bashok would challenge Hai Yo to a cook-off as commanded.

Following his loss, Bashok would renounce ties with the group, swearing to save Mamie himself. She would eventually be saved by the treatments of Huan and Liukan. Her recovery following their treatments came from medicinal dishes prepared by her father and Hai Yo and she had fully recovered within six months. It is because of this firsthand experience of the wonders of cooking that Mamie aspired to be a cook.

Mamie would grow up to be an enthusiastic young chef, eager to study cooking and follow in her father's footsteps. Her specialty was hamburgers. She was not great at expressing herself verbally, but through her culinary talents, she could communicate feelings other people cannot.

She agreed to open a restaurant at Budehuc Castle when Hugo brought her a rare Crab Rice Bowl recipe she had been searching for as a part of her culinary research, later becoming part of the Fire Bringer.

Following the War of the Champions, she set off on a journey to a new land, searching for the legendary cookbook her father had once searched for.

name: Martha
description: Martha (マーサ, Māsa) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. She's a sharp-tongued lottery vendor living at Budehuc Castle.

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
Martha is a tall, elderly woman with brown skin, white, shoulder-length hair, and grey eyes.

She wears a purple waistcoat over a deep blue vest and a long purple dress. Under her vest and dress, she wears a flowing green tunic, accented with yellow, with blue pants underneath. Much like Piccolo, she also wears black poulaine shoes with orange soles.

For accessories, she wears earrings, a golden choker, and orange armbraces. She also carries a small basket around her waist filled with lottery tickets for sale.

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
Martha is a pragmatic, sharp-tongued, and articulate old woman. As such, her words tends to sting those she is rebuking. Still, she is capable of speaking very flatteringly when she is trying to sell lottery tickets.

She also has a very good memory, and it is said that she remembers all the winning lottery numbers from her business. She is also knowledgeable on the history of Budehuc Castle.

Martha is also said to know how to fight, but is aware of the limitations of her age.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Martha was an elderly woman working as a lottery vendor at Budehuc Castle. She sometimes collaborated with Piccolo to drive business to one another, with Martha encouraging people to visit Piccolo, who would then confirm that this was the perfect lucky day to buy a lottery ticket.

When Thomas first became lord of Budehuc Castle, Martha was wary as several lords had abandoned the castle by this point. Although elderly, she has not lost her pride as a member of the Karaya Clan. She refused to back down or be denigrated as a "barbarian" by the Zexen Council when they attempted to assert their power over the castle. She quickly turned her acerbic tongue on the council representatives, leaving them stunned.

By this point, Martha's confidence in Thomas had grown. When it seemed like he would have to leave, she felt like this would not be their parting. In her long life, she had seen many encounters and partings. She sensed the people of Budehuc Castle still needed Thomas and that they all had the courage to fight for him, herself included. 

As she has been a resident at the castle for some time, she knew a lot about its history and helped Thomas uncover its legal status to ensure its free trade zone status. Martha's memories of the agreement would prove key in finding the document and preserving the castle's autonomy.

She would continue to reside at Budehuc and when the War of the Champions came to the castle, she would join the Fire Bringer alongside Thomas.

Following the war, she would continue to operate her lottery stand, now with no shortage of customers as the castle's population grows, ensuring a permanent smile on her face.

name: Masked Man
description: The Masked Man (仮面の男, Kamen no Otoko), also known as the Masked Bishop (仮面の神官将, Kamen no Shinkan Shō) is a character who appears in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
The Masked Man is a bishop of the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia. He was able to manipulate his way into being appointed to the position on the orders of Hikusaak himself. As Bishop, he had been appointed to oversee the Le Buque forces during the War of the Champions.

His goal was to find the five elemental True Runes. In pursuit of this, he was first seen on the Ancient Highway attempting to find a way into the ruins there but without success. He would also use his influence as a bishop to hunt for the Flame Champion.

With his cohorts, he would later attempt to stop the Alma Kinan soul-sending ritual so that he instead could break the seal on the True Water Rune, giving him access to the Sindar ruins on the Ancient Highway. Once Hugo, Chris and Geddoe made their way to the Flame Champion's Hideaway, the Masked Man was able to track them and would attempt to take the True Fire Rune without success.

With the runes unsealed by the Fire Bringer, the Masked Man was finally able to enter the Ancient Highway where he attempted to secure the True Water Rune. However, he would be thwarted by the Fire Bringer and in the ensuing fight, his mask would be shattered, revealing his identity.

name: Mel
description: Mel (メル, Meru) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Mel is a traveler who sports a large doll, Branky, on her right hand.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Mel is a traveler who journeys with Branky, a cartoonish wolf puppet, on her right hand. Branky tends to speak frequently and coarsely, often reflecting badly or oddly on Mel. Mel herself insists that she has no ventriloquism talent, and that the puppet speaks of its own volition, however.

She was invited to perform at Budehuc Castle while passing through Iksay Village. Despite claiming she had no particular puppetry talents, she still agreed to stay at the castle.

Branky's propensity for speaking its mind tended to anger many inhabitants of the castle. When in her room, Mel was said to be very silent, for unknown reasons.

Following the War of the Champions, she would set off on a journey, leaving word that she was visiting Branky's hometown.

trivia: • Mel is right-handed, mystifying Kidd as to how she ate as she seemingly never removed Branky from her right hand.

name: Melville
description: Melville (メルヴィル, Meruvuiru) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Melville is a pure hearted boy and the leader of the Saint Loa Knights.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Melville is a close friend of both Alanis and Elliot and is the leader of the Saint Loa Knights. He is a straightforward young lad, passionate about his friends and his adventures.

His father is the treasure hunter Billy. Apparently, Meville and his mother were separated a long time ago. Because of Billy's frequent trips away, Melville spends a lot of time on his own, making him very independent and self-reliant. Despite Billy's absences, Melville loves and respects his father greatly, defending him from any criticism. When people in Vinay del Zexay laughed off Billy's claims of finding a grounded ship near the North Cavern, Billy would journey there with his friends to prove his father's honesty, aided by future members of the Fire Bringer.

After the War of the Champions broke out, remembering the help he had received before, Melville would move to Budehuc Castle to aid the Fire Bringer. There, he would be reunited with his father, who had joined the group independently. At the castle, he would explore the area along with Alanis and Elliot.

Following the War of the Champions, Melville would return to Vinay del Zexay, dragging his father behind him.

trivia: • Melville is completely unaware of his relation to Kathy.

name: Mike
description: Mike (マイク, Maiku), also called Leonard (レナード, Renādo), is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Mike is a mysterious figure whose unassuming personality appears to be covering darker motives.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Mike appeared to be a fine young man with an unassuming look who describes himself as a humble arms-dealer but under that smiling face he was a different man with a hidden dark, mysterious nature to him.

In fact, Mike is unlikely to be his real name. He introduced himself as "Leonard" when meeting Luc, for example. He joined the Fire Bringer after losing a game of Kabu to the group in Caleria. Although it appeared like a chance meeting, it seems like Mike had been waiting to encounter the Fire Bringer there.

At Budehuc Castle, his calm, gentle nature made him popular. He would sometimes spend time conversing with Ernie, the two of them being well-educated. But even while popular, there were clues towards a darker nature, such as when he would threaten to kick Koroku under his breath. Geddoe, whose instincts were well honed, also noticed this darker presence.

Rumored to have connections with a secret, foreign organization, he could be spotted sometimes having secret meetings with a group dressed all in black, the members of which saluted him. He and Watari also seemed to hold some connection with each other.

As soon as the war ended, Mike disappeared in the night with the mysterious figures he had been meeting, his true identity and motives remaining a mystery.

trivia:
• If you talk to Mike in Caleria during Luc's chapter, he introduces himself as an armorer named Leonard.
• An NPC in Caleria will describe Mike as a "detective" he lost a card game to.

name: Mio
description: Mio (ミオ, Mio) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Mio is Tuta's nurse, assisting him in healing people across the land.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Mio is a nurse who works alongside Tuta. She is a generous woman with a gentle heart who greatly admires Tuta's medical talents. 

She first met Tuta during the Higheast Rebellion when she asked him to treat her husband. Tuta attempted to treat him without success, and he unfortunately passed away. However, Mio was impressed by both Tuta's medical knowledge and his passion for trying to save lives. Since then, the two have travelled together, with Mio assisting Tuta as his nurse.

After helping Tuta treat people in the Grasslands, Tuta would send Mio on to Budehuc Castle to treat people there before following along later, as he was preoccupied a case in Iksay Village.

Later, Mio found Tuta in the Chisha Clan and the two were reunited. Mio was a very kind nurse who treated many patients in her ward, even those who pretended to be sick just to spend time under her care. 

Following the War of the Champions, Mio would travel to Hallec's village in the Nameless Lands and aid in treatment there. As well as assisting Tuta, she has also begun to study medicine in hopes of becoming a doctor herself.

name: Mua
description: Mua (ムーア, Mūa) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Mua is a Free Knight of Camaro undergoing his own personal saga.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
As a Free Knight of Camaro with a strong and kindhearted personality, Mua embodies the noblest beliefs of the knightly order; with justice as his sword and a personality as straight as an arrow, he strives to be the shield of the weak.

Before the War of the Champions broke out in the Grasslands, Mua journeyed there and could sense the disturbing atmosphere building in the area.

Mua would search for the Flame Champion along with Hallec, meeting Hugo in the process. This meeting would lead Mua to join the Fire Bringer and alongside the Free Knights of Camaro, he would fight in the war and protect the people of the Grasslands.

Following the conflict, his saga completed, Mua would return to his homeland and would settle down and get married.

trivia: • Mua is very particular about his facial hair. He shaves three times a day, uses curlers in his moustache and always sleeps in pajamas and a nightcap.

name: Muto
description: Muto (ムト, Muto) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Muto is the affable Kobold warehouse keeper at Budehuc Castle.

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
Muto is a tall, blond-haired kobold. He has a large scar on his head, between his eyes. He has large brown eyes and a black nose. His ears are long and flop downwards.
