He wears an orange sweater with a large blue stripe decorated with bones. Over this he wears a one-armed overall and blue workman chaps with brightly colored pockets. He also wears a blue hat with a yellow stripe, as well as brown boots.

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
Muto is a very kind and pure-hearted young kobold. Though he always works hard, he often makes mistakes when depositing items in the warehouse. This does not stop people from trusting him however and he is a very popular and well-liked worker at the castle.

He struggles with writing complicated words, so he usually writes simple letters when he can. He also has trouble reading and is sometimes seen struggling over the wall newspaper at Budehuc Castle.

Muto is also incredibly honest, always speaking his mind openly and kindly while taking people at their word. He is sometimes easily confused, such as when Thomas, the castle master, was looking for Thomas, the young boy of the same name.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Muto was a young Kobold possessed with a gentle innocence who maintained the warehouse at Budehuc Castle.

He had a sad past and was abandoned at the castle as a puppy. Since then, he has lived and worked there, seeing the castle as the only home that had ever accepted him. As he had no relatives, the people of the castle were his precious family.

When Thomas became lord of Budehuc Castle, Muto was one of his strongest supporters. On the night before Thomas was due to be removed from his position, Muto stayed up repairing the walls of the castle without being asked. Although he knew Thomas could be removed, Muto sincerely hoped it would not come to pass and simply wished to do his best for Thomas's sake.

With Muto, Thomas, and the others finally stopped the Zexen Council's attack, Muto hugged everyone, sobbing with relief. He would continue to do his best for the castle and went on to work for the Fire Bringer throughout the War of the Champions.

Following the war, he continued to work hard at Budehuc Castle, though he still often handed over the wrong goods to customers.

name: Myriam
description: Myriam (ミリアム, Miriamu) is a character who is mentioned in Suikoden III. Myriam was a member of the Zexen Knights.

sections.title: Story
sections.body: Myriam was a highly esteemed member of the Zexen Knights according to Borus, though they were not one of the Six Knights of Zexen. Myriam would be killed in battle along with Lanchet when the Lizard Clan attacked the knights, breaching their cease-fire agreement thinking that Chief Zepon had been assassinated by the knights.

name: Nadir
description: Nadir (ナディール, Nadīru) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Nadir is a masked, dramatic theatre producer on the lookout for natural talent.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Nadir is a drama producer who wears a mask, covering his face at all times.

He was once part of a three-person troupe which traveled the road far and wide but by the War of the Champions, the company was dissolved. Nadir continued to travel, searching for natural talent.

His true identity is a secret and there have been many contradictory rumors and theories about the motivation behind his wearing a mask. Some stories say that he is a very handsome man, others say that his face has been horrifically burned.

Others suggest that he was a famous actor who was so talented he found it difficult to remain himself. And others, yet again, say he choose to hide his face after a particularly terrible performance. He may just wear the mask to symbolize his dedication to the theater.

In Caleria, he would meet a group of Fire Bringer members whom he saw talent in and so joined the group, opening a theater at Budehuc Castle. Following the war, his theater would prove to be a sell-out, due to rumors that the Flame Champion had performed there.

name: Nei
description: Nei (ネイ, Nei) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Nei is a traveling musician and troupe leader.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Nei was an orphaned elf woman who was raised by an elderly human man who was an entertainer by trade. Due to her unique upbringing, she held no prejudice against humans at all. After her grandfather passed away, Toppo was to first person able to make the crying Nei smile with one of his pantomime performances.

Afterwards, the two began traveling together as performers. While they were performing, they took in the orphaned Shabon after bandits attacked the village they were performing in. The three soon bonded as if they were family and traveled together, performing in different parts of the continent.

She was a talented musician and plays the bouzouki as the leader of the trio's traveling troupe. Her dream was for her troupe to have a theatre of their own in the future. Nei's troupe would perform in Vinay del Zexay just as the War of the Champions broke out.

Later, Nei would join the Fire Bringer with her troupe after Shabon was rescued from the harassment of Guillaume. Having heard of Budehuc Castle, she was delighted to move on to an area with people to bring cheer to with her performances. At Budehuc Castle, she would sometimes talk with Roland.

Following the war, Nei returned to her life of freedom, traveling with Toppo and Shabon.

trivia: • The bouzouki is a Greek string instrument usually made of three or four strings.

name: Nicolas
description: Nicolas (ニコル, Nikoru) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. He is Duke's subordinate in the Harmonian Southern Frontier Defense Force's 14th Unit.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Nicolas serves in the 14th Unit of the Harmonian Southern Frontier Defense Force, under the command of Duke. In the past, he fought alongside Duke's father but before he died, he asked Nicolas to take care of his son. Nicolas has served under Duke ever since.

He is a quiet man but one who is committed to his duty and obligations. In his spare time, his hobby is wood carving. Although he is unflinchingly supportive of Duke and the rest of his unit, Nicolas is also more aware of their flaws and how this could create tension with others.

Nicolas fought bravely for the 14th Unit during the War of the Champions and would serve in the Fire Bringer when Duke saved Geddoe's unit at Mt. Senai. Following the war, he accompanies Duke on his southern mission, acting as his shield in every battle.

name: Peggi
description: Peggi (ペギィ, Pegyi) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. He is a lizard blacksmith who tends to yell, out of both enthusiasm and anger.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Peggi, a skilled blacksmith of the Lizard Clan, hailed from the Great Hollow, where he once plied his trade. Even among the discerning warriors of his clan his craftsmanship was held in esteem.

In his youth, Peggi himself had taken up arms as a warrior, but an injury to his shoulder forced him to abandon the battlefield. Afterwards, he turned his strength to the forge, where his passion and thunderous voice became as legendary as his work.

He held an almost fanatical devotion to the quality of his tools, and the sight of a finely crafted hammer could send him into raptures. One such moment was recorded when he beheld the Iron Hammer in the possession of the Fire Bringer.

His voice, loud enough to echo through the Great Hollow, roared with delight. So overcome was he by its craftsmanship that he pleaded to possess it, agreeing to join the force in exchange. So it was during the War of the Champions that Peggi lent his talents to the cause at Budehuc Castle, where he tirelessly reforged and maintained the weapons of his allies.

His booming exclamations and enthusiasm quickly became a fixture of the castle. Though his manner of speech gave the impression of perpetual anger, it was merely the fire of his spirit, not malice, that drove him. Although he occasionally grumbled about non-weapon requests, he never wavered in his dedication to the craft.

After the war, Peggi remained at Budehuc Castle, his reputation as a master smith only growing with time.

name: Pelize
description: Pelize (ペリーズ, Perīzu) is a character who is mentioned in Suikoden III. Pelize was the Vice-Captain of the Zexen Knights just prior to the beginning of the game.

sections.title: Story
sections.body: Pelize was the deputy leader of the Zexen Knights just prior to the War of the Champions. He was a well-respected knight but was killed in battle, along with Captain Galahad, against the Grasslands clans.

name: Percival Fraulein
description: Percival Fraulein (パーシヴァル・フロイライン, Pāshivuaru Furoirain) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Percival is one of the Six Mighty Zexen Knights.
originalName: パーシヴァル・フロイライン

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Percival is one of the renowned Six Mighty Knights, the most skilled of the Zexen Knights. He wielded a double-edged, one-handed sword, but slightly longer so as to be used from horseback. His talent at handling horses and his speed on horseback has earned him the nickname of the "Swordsman of Gale". He was also popular with women due to his good looks, charming personality, and snappy banter.

Raised in Iksay Village, Percival was still known as "Percy" to its residents. He was one of very few knights to have come from a commoner background, which tended to give him a flexible view on things, allowing him to offer up unique ideas and insights easily.

He joined the Knights as a common soldier but became one of the Six Mighty Knights at the behest of Knight Commander Galahad, who had observed Percival's skills on the field of battle. After becoming one of the Six Knights, he became a rival of Borus Redrum but eventually the two forged a bond as comrades.

During the War of the Champions, Percival became concerned concerned about Chris, who has collapsed from exhaustion, and invited her to his hometown's harvest festival. In the village, he was greeted by his old childhood nickname, Percy. Chris would tease Percival in return by calling him Percy, embarrassing him while cheering up the beleaguered knight commander.

Later, Percival would lead the siege of Budehuc Castle alongside Leo. Although under the orders of the Zexen Council, Percival was reluctant to act after witnessing the resistance of the castle residents to remove Thomas as the castle lord. He would agree to back down, much to Leo's consternation. Percival also realized that Salome most likely had a plan in mind and wanted to wait until it was carried out, showing his flexible attitude.

During the War of…

trivia:
• Percival's weapon upgrades as Einst, Ghegenwelt, Zukenft. These are transcriptions of the German words for Past, Present, Future. The latter two are incorrect, correctly transcribed they would be Gegenwart and Zukunft.
• Percival does relatively well as a combat mage. As far as skill upgrades allow everything reaches B or A levels. He gets the unique skills Continual Attack, Parry and Shield Magic.
• Percival participates in the Knight A attack with Borus when not mountaed. He can also participate in the Knight B attack with Chris, and Borus or Leo. This only works when the party are mounted on horses.
• His first name is a reference to the Arthurian knight Parzival. His last name is…

name: Piccolo
description: Piccolo (ピッコロ, Pikkoro) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Piccolo is a fortune-teller and magician residing at Budehuc Castle.

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
Piccolo is an elderly man with brown skin, a full, white beard, and blue eyes. He wears a black skull cap so whether he has hair on his head is unknown.

He wears orb-shaped earrings, carries a dagger on a golden belt, and wears a necklace comprised of small, golden skulls he claims are his past victims. Clothing wise Piccolo wears an orange coat over a green undershirt and blue pants accented by a purple waistband. He also wears black poulaine shoes with orange soles.

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
Piccolo has a good-natured personality and is always smiling or laughing quietly to himself, even in extremely tense situations. Still, he has pride in himself as a magician and it appears he has hidden depths as a magic user. He resents being called an old man and insists he is a great fortune-teller, knowing that he is no less useful than the others.

Though he often acts young at heart, he uses the knowledge he has gained from his long life to try and guide others. His smile is said to be especially reassuring for the residents of Budehuc Castle.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Piccolo was an elderly Grasslands fortune-teller and self-proclaimed great magician who had previously traveled the world before making his way to Budehuc Castle. There, he ran his divination business out of a tent pitched near the castle gates.

According to Piccolo himself, he was also the third pupil of the great magician Crowley in the past. Though this claim is uncorroborated he did indeed hold impressive skill as a magician and had a wealth of knowledge as a result. He also claimed that the skulls he wore around his neck were the corpses of warriors he had defeated and shrank, but this is another dubious claim.

When Juan called him an old man, Piccolo was quick to counter that he was not an old man but a great fortune-teller. That said, compared to his magic, his fortune-telling abilities seemed to be spotty at best. He also made a habit of recommending patrons to also visit Martha's fortune stand in a pre-arranged business agreement.

At Budehuc, he would help Thomas revitalize the castle's fortunes and stood against the Zexen Council's designed on the castle. When the council's troops marched on the castle, Piccolo used his magic to defeat two Zexen inspectors in an instant. When Juan suggested he should have used this magic sooner, Piccolo claimed with his trademark laugh that it was his once-in-a-decade lucky day.

Like the other residents of Budehuc Castle, he would continue to happily support Thomas and would later aid the Fire Bringer when the War of the Champions spread across the region.

Following the war, he would lead a leisurely life, boasting of his participation in the war.

name: Queen
description: Queen (クイーン, Kuīn) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Queen is a member of Geddoe's 12th unit of the Harmonian Southern Frontier Defense Force.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Queen served as the only female member of Geddoe's unit of the Harmonian Southern Frontier Defense Force. She has a strong temper and a harsh tone of voice, but in fact had a very caring and warm personality under the surface. She was also strong both physically and in terms of will.

She was born to a distinguished family in the country of Sanady but lost that life when the nation was invaded and annexed by Harmonia. As such, she was quite cultured when it comes to music and literature, especially in comparison to her colleagues in the unit.

She tended towards practical clothing, with green as the base color. Her choker was decorated with animal feathers, and her overall fashion tastes tended towards the traditional in that regard.

Queen's weapon was a one-handed sword belonging to the light and easy-to-handle saber family, with a straight blade and a single edge, designed to strike quickly and surely when on a mission. Her sword was named after phases of the moon. She held a Ripple Rune, which could easily poison her targets also.

She felt the closest to Geddoe within the SFDF, helping to support him and deflecting scrutiny during the War of the Champions. The two held a deep respect and trust for one another. Queen's support seemed to help Geddoe cope with and overcome his own emotional wounds during the conflict.

Queen also had her own pain as someone who lost her homeland and felt regret for not doing all she could have done to fight it. It was for this reason she encouraged the Grasslandsers and Zexen to unite and fight against the Harmonian invasion, supporting Geddoe's efforts to unite the forces until the banner of the Fire Bringer.

Her perspective of losing and fighting for one's home surfaced again when helping Franz. Franz would admonish himself for…

trivia:
• Queen learns the unique skills Heavy Damage and Shield Magic. She can generally get any skill up to B or B+, and tends to max her skills early, basically Queen can do anything but not specialize in a particular area.
• Queen was a vegetarian during the events of Suikoden III but gave up the habit following the events of the game.
• It was once theorized that Queen and Jillia Blight were the same person.

name: Reed
description: Reed (リード, Rīdo) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. He is one of the two oft-frustrated attendants to Lilly Pendragon.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Reed was one of two attendants to Lilly Pendragon, daughter of Gustav, President of Tinto. He had a gentle and patient personality. He wielded a slender, one-handed sword with a long blade in battle.

Especially when compared to his fellow attendant Samus, was much more patient about catering to Lilly's whims and demands. The only thing that really made him angry was when someone makes fun of his hairstyle. Even then, he had to suppress it when Lilly teased him.

Reed and Samus accompanied Lilly as her quest to find the Flame Champion took her further and further into the Grasslands. It was a testament to Reed's endurance that he continued to support and aid Lilly as they travelled back and forth between Tinto and the Grasslands before ending up joining the Fire Bringer and fighting in the War of the Champions.

Following the war, Reed was ordered to stop Lilly from leaving Tinto for the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia. In the end, Lilly was able to browbeat him into accompanying her, as usual.

name: Rhett
description: Rhett (レット, Retto) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. He is a warrior of the Duck Clan and compatriot of Wilder.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Rhett is a warrior of the Duck Clan and a friend of Wilder and Sgt. Joe. Even though he's actually three days older than Wilder, he's treated as the youngest of the group. Apparently, he gives Sgt. Joe trouble from time to time.

During the War of the Champions, he and Wilder would help accompany Chris Lightfellow to Chisha Village. After returning to Duck Village, he had intended to stay home for a while but was pressed into the Fire Bringer by Sgt. Joe. At Budehuc Castle, both Hallec and Mamie considered eating him but Rhett was so carefree, he never even noticed.

Following the war, Rhett joined the Zexen Archeology Association, helping to investigate the ruins of the Ceremonial Site.

trivia: • Rhett knitted his own hat and wool scarf.

name: Rick
description: Rick (リック, Rikku) is a minor character who appears in Suikoden III. Rick is a colleague of Franz.

sections.title: Story
sections.body: Rick was a Le Buque soldier who was acquainted with Franz. When the villagers of Le Buque were rounded up and captured under the orders of Luc, Rick was the only person who managed to escape. He warned Franz and the Fire Bringer as they entered Le Buque before passing out.

name: Rico
description: Rico (リコ, Riko) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Rico is the faithful squire of Fred Maximillian.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Rico is the single member of the Maximillian Knights under Fred's command. She is not an official knight, serving as Fred's squire with the official title of Chamberlain and Hall Porter for the Captain of the Maximillian Knights. She is the daughter of Sancho who had, in the past, served in the same role for Fred's grandfather, Maximillian. It is said that Rico's family has served in this manner for generations.

As Fred's loyal retainer, Rico journeying along with Fred, seeking out evil to fight. She would fight alongside Fred throughout the War of the Champions, helping to defend the Chisha Village and in the final battle at the Ceremonial Site. Rico would almost lose her prized medal, a gift from Fred, during that final battle and stayed behind to retrieve it. Fred would also double back to protect her before the duo made their escape.

Following the war, Rico would attempt to persuade Fred to return home many time but, in the end, would continue to assist him in his adventures.

name: Rody
description: Rody (ロディ, Rodi) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Rody is the talented, but naive, apprentice of the magician Estella.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Rody is a young boy training hard under Estella with the aim of becoming a skilled magician. He has a pure and somber personality and holds a great deal of respect and confidence in Estella as his teacher. He is already a talented mage in his own right, but it is unknown whether Estella notices this.

This confidence and pureness of heart means that Rody almost always takes Estella's lies at face value, leading him into absurd situations, such as trying to increase his earth magic prowess by burying himself up to his neck in the ground or running around with his belly button out to increase his lightning magic. He also believed in Estella's claim of a male counterpart to the Alma Kinan Village and after leading the Fire Bringer on a wild goose chase investigating it, joining the group with Estella. Anytime she lied to him, he would take whatever she said as gospel and eagerly hop to it without question, no matter how ridiculous, making him quite probably the most gullible sod in all of Grasslands.

Following the War of the Champions, he continues to train under Estella, striving towards his dream of flying on a broomstick. A goal that is all but impossible to accomplish, sadly.

trivia:
• Rody's weapon upgrades as Fly Rod, Bird Rod, Air Rod.
• Rody participates in an absolutely useless Wizard Attack unite with Estella that burns up his level 1 and 2 MPs to deal 100 damage to all foes.
• Rody learns the unique skills Precision, Blinking Magic and Magic Rationing, with a possible S level in the latter. Unsurprisingly, he has great results with most any type of magic skill but relatively dismal results in melee.
• One look at his runes at it is plainly obvious Estella was using him as a human shield. Both Firefly and Wall Runes equipped make Rody a hopeless damage sponge without any player intervention.

name: Roland Lesaurus
description: Roland Lesaurus (ロラン・レザウルス, Roran Rezaurusu) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Roland is one of the Six Mighty Zexen Knights.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Roland was the only archer and elf in the Zexen Knights, making him a unique asset for the group. Both his loyalty to the Zexen Knights and his skill with a bow were without parallel. It is said he was more loyal to the order as a whole than to Chris Lightfellow as Knight Commander. As an archer, he preferred to wear armor that offers him some mobility.

His history before joining the Zexen Knights is shrouded in mystery as he did not talk much about his past. It is known that Roland was one of a number of mercenaries hired by the Zexen Knights in the past and would eventually join the organization thanks to the strong recommendation of Captain Galahad. Galahad's recommendation was needed due to opposition against the idea of an elf joining the ranks of the Zexen Knights.

He tended not to speak unless necessary but when he did, his words carried weight, perhaps because his elven longevity allowed him to consider the big picture in situations. Despite his aloofness, he held no particular prejudice against humans. He was also not without humor or warmth despite his cold exterior.

He may come off as harsh to people but that is because he felt a Zexen Knight should project authority and he enforced that same discipline on himself. This was the case when he interrupted Hugo as he tried to approach Chris Lightfellow at Brass Castle. He was not a natural leader, but his strict and cool temperament made him highly trusted by his comrades and he trusted them in turn.

When the Six Clans attacked the Zexen Knights at the outbreak of the War of the Champions, Roland's collected nature allowed him to realize that the group needed to retreat before they were surrounded, even before the attack was launched. His composure also stopped Borus from attacking the enemy lines blindly,…

trivia: • Roland's weapon names Night Bow, Moon Bow and Elfin Bow are the same as Sylvina's in Suikoden.

name: Ruby
description: Ruby (ルビ, Rubi) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Ruby is the mantor mount of Franz.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Ruby is the mantor mount ridden by his trainer, Franz. Ruby is not only considered to be strong and fast but also quite handsome by mantor standards.

As Franz's faithful mantor, he is always by Franz's side, participating in the War of the Champions first during the invasion of the Grasslands and later on the side of the Fire Bringer. At Budehuc Castle, he was fond of playing with Nash and Ayame.

Following the war, Ruby would return to Le Buque with Franz, fathering many healthy children.

name: Salome Harras
description: Salome Harras (サロメ・ハラス, Sarome Harasu) is a major character in Suikoden III. Salome Harras is Chris' staff officer in the Zexen Knights.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Salome Harras was one of the Six Mighty Zexen Knights and served under Chris Lightfellow as her staff officer and chief strategist. He came from a disciplined background and studied military tactics from his master, Johanna. He placed great faith in his mentor's War Chronicles as a guide to strategy.

He was generally always cool and collected but his loyalty to Chris and to the Knights was said to burn hotter than anyone else. His calm and appropriate judgment and leadership ability made him well trusted and able to unite the group.

In addition to aiding Chris with tactical considerations, he also took care of the Knight's paperwork and general affairs. To an extent, Salome was the man who keeps the Zexen Knights running. In his spare time, Salome enjoyed drinking tea and was also a skilled cook, regarding it as a talent on par with his tactical acumen.

Skilled at political intrigue, he engaged in information warfare, maintaining contacts throughout the region, as far as the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia. By trading knowledge and information with people such as Nash and Geddoe, Salome further helped the Knights and proved himself indispensable to the order. He also monitored the Zexen Council in much the same manner.

With the help of Nash, he and Salome determined a hidden party was fueling the growing War of the Champions and agreed to work together to try and discover the identity of this group. As such, Salome requested Nash accompany Chris as to investigate this and also to track the Flame Champion, even as he encouraged Chris to depart to follow her own personal desire to discover the fate of her father.

As Geddoe was one of Salome's information contacts, it appeared that Salome had some insight into Wyatt Lightfellow being alive but did not have details of the…

name: Samus
description: Samusu (サムス, Samusu) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. He is one of the two oft-frustrated attendants to Lilly Pendragon.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Samus comes from a land far to the south of the Grasslands, near the southern seas. At some point he moved from the south to the mountains of the Tinto Republic. He is one of two attendants to Lilly Pendragon, daughter of Gustav, President of Tinto. Compared to his fellow attendant Reed, Samus is more likely to talk back to Lilly but, in the end, is just as reflexively obedient as his compatriot.

Samus and Reed accompanied Lilly as her quest to find the Flame Champion took her further and further into the Grasslands. As the accountant of the group, Samus tried to maintain the groups finances as best he could consider Lilly's exuberant spending habits.

Eventually he was able to convince Lilly to return to Tinto for further funds. But after a brief return home, he ended up joining the Fire Bringer and fighting in the War of the Champions.

Following the war, Samus attempted to hand in his letter of resignation, but this was refused by Minister Marlowe Cody.

name: Sana
description: Sana (サナ, Sana) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Sana is the head of the Chisha Clan.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Sana was widely known as the wise chief of the Chisha Clan but she was also the spouse of the Flame Champion in her youth.

She held a mild and gentle temperament by nature but also possessed a strong inner strength, resolutely standing up against invading forces, whether they be from Zexen or Harmonia.

Not only the leader of the Chisha Village, Sana was also the guardian of the Flame Champion's Hideaway, where the True Fire Rune was sealed. She would eventually help guide three heroes to the seal during the War of the Champions and unlock the True Fire Rune.

Following this, she would align the Chisha Clan with the Fire Bringer for the remainder of the war.

name: Sanae Yamamoto
description: Sanae Yamamoto (サナエ・ヤマモト, Sanae Yamamoto), usually referred to as Sanae Y (サナエ・Y, Sanae Y) in-game, is a supporting character in Suikoden III. She is the dutiful, oft-serious scion of the Yamamoto household.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Sanae is the daughter of Freed and Yoshino Yamamoto, two veterans of the Dunan Unification War. She has to wear glasses due to her bad eyesight, a trait both her parents suffer from.

Sanae fights using a naginata, as her mother did, and this combined with her kimono and polite nature causes her to come off as somewhat serious. She was beloved by all of the citizens of her parent's home city of Radat Town before she decided to set out on her own journey to develop her skills.

She originally journeyed to the Grasslands in order to train before finding a husband but would agree to join the Fire Bringer once the War of the Champions broke out in the region. At the castle, she set her spare time aside to develop kitchen skills. Sanae is a talented, if inexperienced chef, with nukazuke pickles being her specialty. She was said to be working on a meat and potatoes recipe as well.

Sanae trained diligently under Juan, hoping to reach the same level of skill as her father. She also found great interest in the theater run by Nadir, and would often read beyond the script, in an effort to 'become' her character.

Following the war, she declared her training complete and returned to her home in the Dunan Republic, ready for marriage.

trivia: • Her outfit and weapon seem to suggest a [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yamato%20nadeshiko Yamato nadeshiko] type, as did her mother, although her attitude does not completely conform to the type.

name: Scott
description: Scott (スコット, Sukotto) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Scott is a trader who travels the world, making a profit along the way.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Scott was a young merchant of unknown origin, accompanied only by his loyal parrot, Waurenhyte. As a young adult, he wandered the lands under the employ of a great trading guild, dreaming of one day establishing his own emporium.

His companion, Waurenhyte, was no ordinary bird. It could speak and comprehend human speech, serving as both confidant and partner in trade. Scott had acquired the parrot in his very first business transaction, exchanging a single candle for the creature in a southern land.

To Scott, the parrot was not merely a pet but an essential ally in commerce. Waurenhyte captured people's attentions, often giving Scott the chance to bring up trading. Waurenhyte's amusing interjections often swayed negotiations in his favor.

He would move to Budehuc Castle from Brass Castle after being impressed by the Fire Bringer's trading acumen. Scott offered counsel to his new allies, advising them on pearls, ancient texts, and gold rushes.

He also advised caution, knowing well that fortunes could be lost, as well as made, on rumor alone. He spoke especially highly of Vinay del Zexay, where luxury goods fetched the highest prices.

When the War of the Champions subsided, Scott, ever restless, turned his gaze westward in search of new trade routes.

name: Sebastian
description: Sebastian (セバスチャン, Sebasuchan) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. He is the fretful, but nonetheless diligent, butler of Budehuc Castle.

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
Sebastian is a portly man, with long blonde hair meticulously set in curls. He also sports a small, neatly trimmed, black moustache. His eyes are rarely seen open.

He normally wears a blue overcoat over a brown waistcoat vest and white shirt, accented with a blue spotted neckerchief. He carried a handkerchief in the upper left pocket of his overcoat. He also wears white striped trousers with brown socks and shoes.

Due to his many roles at Budehuc Castle he is often seen carrying something in hand, be it a business ledger or a tea tray.

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
Sebastian is a hard worker who manages the finances of Budehuc Castle while also handling chores such as preparing meals for everyone.

He is a man who respects convention and discipline, as well as a great worrier. He is also very concerned about his appearance, and is especially particular about his hair, which he spends three hours setting every day.

Though conflict avoidant, Sebastian is a person unwilling to flinch from his duties, whatever they may be. Although timid in many ways, he is at heart a good person determined to support the castle until the end of his days.

He respects Thomas greatly, especially after the young lord's desire to protect and help the beleaguered castle was made clear. He also has feelings for Shizu and these feelings let him to often use the elevator even when he had no great need to.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Sebastian served as the butler of Budehuc Castle under several different administrators. Due to the lack of staff, he had many different duties, from preparing food to managing accounts and anything else that needed doing. Although easily unnerved and highly strung, he was fastidious about his appearance. No matter how busy he was, he would ensure his hair was set neatly every day, even when going to sleep.

When Thomas was appointed castle lord and learned of the grim financial situation there, he proposed to rent out the castle grounds to finance its restoration. Sebastian would object to this, citing that they would never get permission from the Zexen Council. Sebastian understood that such measures were needed, but he was also aware of the nature of the Zexen Council and found great difficulty in opposing them, even after the original common land agreement for Budehuc was unearthed.

Still, despite his severe unease at opposing the Council as they attempted to assert their control over Budehuc Castle, he continued to support Thomas through his hardships. Even when Thomas was formally deposed as lord of Budehuc Castle, Sebastian would choose to stand with him against the Council. Afterward, he would continue to support Thomas as the castle became aligned with the Fire Bringer.

His role continued unchanged following the War of the Champions, running the castle's finances and constantly fretting as Thomas' assistant.

name: Sgt. Joe
description: Sgt. Joe (ジョー軍曹, Jō Gunsō), real name Jordi (ジョルディ, Jorudi), is a major character in Suikoden III. Sgt. Joe is a veteran soldier of the Duck Clan who supports and guides Hugo's development as a warrior.

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
Jordi is a handsome man by duck standards, due to his sharp eyes and fine tail feathers. He was well aware of his attractiveness, boasting about it.

Like most Duck Clan warriors, Sgt. Joe wielded a Duck Halberd in battle. The name of his weapon, Prajuri, comes from the Indonesian word for soldier, being representative of his way of life. His helmet clasps were accented by purple orbs, which matched his coat. He considered him quite fashionable in some regards.

On the battlefield he equipped a green shoulder guard that ran from the left shoulder to the arm as well as a helmet emblazoned with the Duck Clan emblem. Both pieces were designed to mitigate any counterattacks by the enemy after swing his halberd, a heavy weapon.

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
Like most Ducks, Jordi was cheerful and open-hearted by nature but it was his battle experience that makes him more military minded than most.

Always keeping his cool no matter what predicament he found himself in, Sgt. Joe always tried to analyze the situation and face the obstacles in front of him. looking at the situation and facing the difficult obstacles in front of him without giving up. Demonstrating this way of life as a warrior with confidence helped Hugo mature and accept his own growing responsibility as a hero.

He was noted as being able to make sly comments about people without them noticing and his surprisingly jovial sense of humor contributed to his popularity. When Hallec insinuated Jordi looked tasty on their first meeting, he would jovially deflect the comment onto Hugo instead.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Sgt. Joe was a battle-scarred veteran who fought on many battlefields making him a hero of the Duck Clan. The nickname "Sgt. Joe" came from the respect others have for him as a soldier, it was not a rank of the Duck Clan, who were egalitarian warriors much like the Karaya Clan.

It was during the SY 465 war between the Six Clans and the Zexen Federation that he first met Lucia. The two bonded quickly as fellow warriors, with Joe also falling in love with Lucia's home-cooked meals. It was this bond that meant years later he served as something of a tutor to Hugo, supporting him up to and through the War of the Champions.

Jordi used to live with his beautiful wife and two children until tragedy struck the family when his eldest son suddenly became ill and died. At the time, Sergeant Joe was away fighting for the Grasslands in their war against the Zexen. He was informed of his son's illness but was unable to leave the battlefield and so never got to see his son before he died. Torn between his life as a warrior and his inability to protect his family, he left them.

During Joe, Hugo, and Lulu's initial visit to Vinay del Zexay, it was Joe who played the role of elder. When the group were denied entry to the Council, it was Sgt. Joe who urged restraint from the brash youths and negotiated with the Zexen Knight on guard duty, knowing how obstinate they could be.

Soon afterwards, the group would be forced to flee Vinay del Zexay as war broke out between Zexen and the Grasslands once more. Confronted by Percival and Leo, Jordi refused to accompany them for questioning and rebuffed their claim that the Grasslandsers had launched an unprovoked ambush and the armistice.

Racing back to the Karaya Village, the trio would see the village in flames and the Zexen Knights in…

trivia: • In the Suikoden III manga as well as some promotional art, Sgt. Joe is shown to be a heavy smoker, although this is never shown in-game.

name: Shabon
description: Shabon (シャボン, Shabon) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Shabon is the young bodhrán player of Nei's troupe.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Shabon was a young girl who traveled with Nei and Toppo as part of their traveling busker group. She was extremely talented at playing the bodhrán, especially given her young age. She also practiced kenpo and was a talented fighter considering her age and size.

Her parents were killed by bandits and ever since then, Nei and Toppo have taken care of her, the two having been performing in her village at the time. Despite this tragic background, Shabon maintained her childhood and innocence due to the efforts of her friends. She viewed Nei, in particular, as an older sister.

She would accompany Nei and Toppo to Budehuc Castle to perform there after being rescued by the Fire Bringer from Guillaume in Vinay del Zexay. Like a lot of small children, she was fascinated by ducks and was fond of Sgt. Joe. She would spend time at the castle practicing many different tricks and games with Toppo, including tag.

Following the War of the Champions, Shabon has had a difficult choice to make; whether to keep traveling as a minstrel or devote herself full time to her martial arts training.

trivia:
• A bodhrán is a traditional Irish frame drum, open ended on one side so a hand can be used to manipulate the pitch and timbre of the drum and played with the free hand, with or without a tipper, a type of wooden stick.
• At 6 years old, Shabon is the youngest non-beast battle party member in the entire series. Unsurprisingly, she is also the shortest at 110cm (3'7").

name: Sharon
description: Sharon (シャロン, Sharon) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Sharon is an apprentice Dragon Knight under Futch's protection.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Sharon is the daughter of Milia, the commander of the Knights of the Dragon's Den. She is an excessively energetic and wayward young woman. During the War of the Champions, she chased after Futch, without permission, when he was assigned to reconnaissance duties in the Grasslands.

Her behavior is bound only by the limits of her curiosity. That is why she trailed Futch in the first place and it is how, even after joining the Fire Bringer with Futch, her curiosity would lead her to such actions as riding a lizard, bathing in milk and investigating (and continuously riding) Budehuc Castle's elevator. Futch would often have a hard time dealing with Sharon due to her self-centered attitude and fussiness.

During her time with the Fire Bringer, she did not yet have a dragon but fought in battle, nonetheless. She attempted to improve her skills by training with Juan but could never find him awake. It is possible that Juan pretended he was asleep to avoid training her, as he had previously told her she was too much of a child to train with him. She was also highly curious of Estella, and prodded Rody with questions about whether or not she was as powerful a wizard as Mazus.

In her spare time, she would ride Kathy's horses, to get a feel for what riding was like, to prepare herself for getting her own dragon. She had previously attempted this by riding on the back of a Lizard Clan warrior, neither could serve as a substitute for flying. 

Following the war, she would return home for her ceremony and bonded with a Golden Dragon.

trivia:
• Sharon's weapon upgrades as Verserk, Sigmund, Sigrut. Her first one cannot be found without a cheating device.
• Sharon participates in two unite attacks: the Dragon Knight A attack with Futch, in which they jump up high and strike down hard upon one foe, as well as the Girl Attack with Emily and Sanae Yamamoto. Emily pummels the foe toward Sharon and Sanae, who slam the foe with their weapons in a manner akin to a baseball bat.
• Sharon learns the unique skills Parry, Shield Protect and Magic Rationing, although none of her skills in either Melee or Magic field rise above B+ level.

name: Shiba
description: Shiba (シバ, Shiba) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. He is a prominent warrior of the Lizard Clan.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Shiba is a warrior from a southern Lizard Clan who helps to assist Dupa as the Left Fighter in Unit Two of the Lizard Clan's Three Bodies Fighters. He is a strong, prideful warrior, wielding a gride, a trident-like spear weapon traditionally used by the Lizard Clan, but is also rational and realistic. He is prepared to fight and die to protect the Lizard Clan.

Lizards are big eaters in general, but Shiba eats a lot even for a lizard. After joining the Fire Bringer, he began to worry about his weight and asked Kenji for dietary advice.

Following the War of the Champions, Shiba returned to his southern clan and finally got married.

name: Shizu
description: Shizu (シズ, Shizu) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Shizu is the elevator operator at Budehuc Castle.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Shizu was a woman of unknown origins who served as the caretaker of the elevator in Budehuc Castle. Graceful and refined in manner, she carried herself with quiet humility, her gentle demeanor and traditional attire earning her admiration among the castle's inhabitants.

Though she spoke little of her past, it was known that she had once studied under the eccentric inventor Adlai of the Dunan Republic, mastering the workings of the castle's mechanical marvel. She sometimes murmured in her sleep, seemingly recalling her master's peculiar habits.

After arriving at Budehuc Castle and repairing the elevator, she conducted a trial run with Thomas and his companions aboard. When Thomas remarked that it could now bear heavier loads, Shizu softly chided that the elevator should not be overburdened, especially as it was not used to such loads yet.

Her days were spent maintaining the elevator, guiding passengers with practiced courtesy, and ensuring its mechanisms ran smoothly. Yet mystery clung to her. Kidd claimed to have seen her slipping into the forest at night, accompanied by shadowed figures, or training with unseen companions under the moon's pale light.

She would also make frequent requests for extra water and meat, stating only that they were for "acquaintances engaged in strenuous work." These peculiarities and her earlier words indicated that much like her master, Shizu used the strength of the elevator barbarians as the power source of her elevator installations.

She sometimes submitted letters in the castle's Suggestion Box such as polite reminders to use the elevator courteously, warnings against unruly behavior (as Bright had once learned the hard way), and dryly worded cautions about unexpected descents when too many passengers boarded at once.

When the War…

name: Thomas
description: Thomas (トーマス, Tōmasu) is one of the protagonists of Suikoden III. He is a young man from the Nameless Lands who works to revitalize Budehuc Castle.

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
Thomas had medium length brown hair and large, expressive brown eyes. He wore an orange-brown coat over a red vest and white shirt. He also wore short green pants, grey stockings, and brown shoes.

He wielded a short sword as his combat weapon with both hands, holding it timidly.

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
Thomas has a timid personality but has a strong conviction that he will never bend to what is important to him. He quickly grew to care about Budehuc Castle and its inhabitants and was willing to stand against anyone, even the Zexen Knights if it meant securing their rights to live their lives undisturbed.

This bravery as well as his earnest and honest character would draw the people of Budehuc, all from diverse backgrounds, to him.

Thomas was a poor runner but a surprisingly talented swimmer. He also had a sweet tooth and preferred sweet, children's-style curry.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Thomas is the illegitimate son of Zexen Council representative Lowma. His mother was a vendor from a remote country. Her personality was much like Thomas'. She fell in love with Lowma and bore his child, only to be abandoned. He was raised in the Nameless Lands where he grew to have a timid personality and poor fighting ability. However, he is also a quietly determined person, willing to work hard.

He was raised in the Nameless Lands by his mother, but after her death in a Grasslands raid, Thomas journeyed to Zexen per his mother's request to live with his father. Thomas had no grand designs when coming to Zexen, save to see the face of the father he had never met.

However, as an illegitimate child was a political liability, Lowma quickly got rid of Thomas by assigning him lordship over the obscure, crumbling Budehuc Castle in Zexen's northern frontier.

There, without his father's negative influence over him, Thomas worked hard at winning the trust of the castle's residents and revitalizing the area and its economy.

To achieve this, Thomas attempted to make Budehuc Castle a free-trade zone, aided by a 50-year-old treaty which marked the area as common ground for both Zexen and the Grasslands. Although he did not have council approval, Thomas reasoned that the resident's livelihood mattered more than the bureaucratic process and forged ahead with a strong will.
 
As castle lord, he worked closely with Cecile, his guard commander, forming a firm friendship. When Cecile accompanied Thomas to Brass Castle, Salome pointed out that her position was not official, to her dismay. It was Thomas who would stand up for her, convincing Salome to agree to let Thomas appoint her as guard commander until an official appointment could be made. This would be one of Thomas' first…

trivia:
• Thomas is not very good at running but is a surprisingly adept swimmer.
• Thomas has a sweet tooth but has a hard time handling spicy foods, preferring mild curry, which is more popular with small children.

name: Thomas (youth)
description: Thomas, usually referred to as Thomas-kun (トーマスくん, Tōmasu-kun) is a minor character in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Thomas-kun is a small child living in the Village of Nor, in the vicinity of Budehuc Castle. He would wander to Mt. Hei-Tou after hearing stories from Juan and ended up getting lost and attacked by a wild boar. Luckily, Thomas and Cecile would rescue him from his peril.

Since then, Thomas-kun came to deeply respect his namesake and would later become part of Budehuc Castle's garrison under the command of Cecile.

trivia: • "-kun" is a Japanese honorific used, in this circumstance, by anyone when addressing or referring to male children in an affectionate or paternalistic manner.

name: Toppo
description: Toppo (トッポ, Toppo) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Toppo is the quiet, talented fiddler and juggler in Nei's troupe.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Toppo is a traveling entertainer, skilled in juggling and playing the fiddle. He travels with Shabon and Nei, earning money for their performances. 

Toppo and Nei first met after the passing of the latter's grandfather. Toppo made the depressed Nei laugh with a pantomime performance, and the two began traveling together.

While they were performing, they took in the orphaned Shabon after bandits attacked the village they were performing in. The three soon bonded as if they were family and traveled together, performing in different parts of the continent.

Toppo rarely spoke in public, believing that whenever possible that actors should not talk. Instead, he usually communicated through miming. He did sometimes speak with Nei and Shabon but even then, he spoke very softly.

He saw himself as something of a protector for his two companions. He would join the Fire Bringer alongside them after Shabon was rescued from Guillaume.

At Budehuc Castle, he was considered a kindhearted man, making wooden toys for the children who lived there. Following the War of the Champions, Toppo continued to perform with the troupe, dreaming of opening a small theater someday.

trivia: • A fiddle refers to any bowed string musical instrument, including the violin. They are used in folk music in many countries including Ireland, Norway and Hungary.

name: Twaikin
description: Twaikin (トワイキン, Towaikin) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Twaikin is a digging-obsessed dwarf from the Toran Republic.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Twaikin is a dwarf from the Toran Republic who travelled all the way to the Grasslands in order to extend the Great Hollow. Twaikin loves to dig, so much so that he frequently continues to dig without permission, even if his talents were not requested. Ten years ago, he dug so much that he left the Toran Republic entirely.

The feature he is most proud of is the mole on his nose, which he shares with his grandmother. At Budehuc Castle, his digging helped out in a variety of ways, creating garbage disposals for Sebastian and uncovering earthworms for Barts.

Following the War of the Champions, he returned to the Great Hollow to continue his work on expanding the Great Hollow.

name: Wan Fu
description: Wan Fu (ワン・フー, Wan Fū) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. He is a fighter out on a journey of vengeance, with a habit of racking up large debts.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Wan Fu is a polearm fighter easily recognizable due to the large phoenix tattoo on his back. The tattoo is Wan Fu's symbol of his oath of revenge; a friend of his was killed and Wan Fu has apparently been seeking vengeance ever since. His staff is black with a vermillion pattern, reminiscent of his tattoo.

During the War of the Champions, Wan Fu had run up a large amount of debt by eating and drinking heavily without the funds to pay. The Fire Bringer paid off his debt in Chisha Village and he joined the group in gratitude.

Following the war, he continued on his journey of vengeance.

name: Watari
description: Watari (ワタリ, Watari) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. He is a ninja-for-hire currently being pursued for abandoning his clan.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Watari was a member of the mysterious ninja group known as the Kage that once served the Highland Kingdom. Although he appeared to be a cool-headed realist, it seems he abandoned the group due to a promise made to a woman.

Leaving the Kage was not permitted however and ever since he has been marked for death and pursued by Ayame. He joined the Fire Bringer on a 100,000 potch contract during the War of the Champions.

Watari did not associate much with his fellow Fire Bringer, taking extra precaution to avoid the detective Kidd, who attempted to learn about him. Watari carried a sword on his back, but in battle he normally threw shuriken to attack enemies from a distance.

Following the conflict, he would disappear, still pursued by Ayame.

trivia:
• Watari's weapon upgrades as Blue Wolf, to Heavenly Wolf. His earlier weapon, plain Wolf, cannot be seen without a cheating device.
• Watari has a unite attack with Ayame, the Ninja Attack, in which the two ninjas slice multiple foes in a diamond-shape pattern.
• Watari can reach S level in his unique skill, Counter Attack.

name: Wilder
description: Wilder (ワイルダー, Wairudā) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. He is amongst the wisest of the Duck Clan, although not the most active or aggressive sort.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Wilder is a member of the Duck Clan's youth team and the friend and charge of Sgt. Joe. Always seen wearing his glasses and well-versed at the intelligence school, Wilder would much rather read a book or indulge his saxophone hobby than rise to action. Still, he was trained as a warrior, wielding a Duck Halberd with a slender fish-shaped head.

He, and Rhett, would act as a guide for Chris Lightfellow and Nash as they made their way east. It is said that Wilder may have held a slight distrust of humans.

Following this, he would return to Duck Village for a time before being conscripted into the Fire Bringer by Sgt. Joe. After being sent to Budehuc Castle he would frequently spend time at the castle's library and theatre while withstanding the intensive training regimen of Sgt. Joe. Following the War of the Champions, he would make his living as a Grasslands guide, escorting caravans.

name: Wyatt Lightfellow
description: Wyatt Lightfellow (ワイアット・ライトフェロー, Waiatto Raitoferō), also known as Jimba (ジンバ, Jinba), is a major character in Suikoden III. Wyatt is the father of Chris Lightfellow and bearer of the True Water Rune.
originalName: ワイアット・ライトフェロー

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Wyatt Lightfellow was a bearer of the True Water Rune and the father of Chris Lightfellow. Around the year 425, Wyatt fought alongside Geddoe and the Flame Champion against the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia's invasion of the Grasslands.

Following the war, he tried to live a normal life in Zexen, marrying Anna and fathering a daughter, Chris. When the Howling Voice Guild came close to tracking him down and almost killed him in an assassination attempt, Wyatt decided to forsake his old life entirely in order to spare his family any danger. Sealing away his True Water Rune, Wyatt would end up moving to the Karaya Clan and becoming "Jimba", with the help of Lucia.

Even during the War of the Champions, Wyatt maintained his façade as Jimba until he travelled to the True Water Rune seal at the Ruins of Cyndar in order to protect the rune. He would encounter the Destroyers there and be defeated by Luc.

With the arrival of Chris at the ruins, Wyatt would be able to confess to Chris his true identity. Saying that he always loved Anna and Chris, Wyatt would pass on the True Water Rune to his daughter, the last remnant of his life, before dying peacefully.

name: Yuiri
description: Yuiri (ユイリ, Yuiri) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Yuiri is a fierce member of the Alma Kinan.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Yuiri is a member of the Alma Kinan with the ability to commune with the spirits. She is something of a leader for the younger members of the clan, noted for her reliability and perseverance. Although rare for the people of their village, she and Yumi would leave venture outside in order to find Chris Lightfellow at the behest of Yun.

After bringing Chris to the Alma Kinan Village, Yuiri fought the Destroyers in order to allow Yun time to perform her soul-sending ritual. Following this, both she and Yumi would join the Fire Bringer at Chris' request in order to protect the Grasslands, arriving at Brass Castle in time to help the Fire Bringer fight off a Harmonia attack. Yuri was adamantly serious that Yun's sacrifice should be revered and would not openly discuss it thereafter.

At Budehuc Castle, Yuiri was exposed to many different kinds of people she had never imagined. She thought Estella was an amazing magician but considered her clothes to be probably even more so. She also remarked that she had never seen a Winghorde (Landis) until arriving there. Yuiri was also highly suspicious of Guillaume and Mike, considering the former to be not even human.

She was also protective of her fellow Alma Kinan sisters, trying to keep Ace away from Yumi, and anyone else curious about why their village was female-only. In her spare time, Yuri could be found meditating by the lake shore, away from all of the noise coming from the castle.

Following the War of the Champions, she returned to the Alma Kinan where she lived a peaceful life, likely as the new village leader.

trivia:
• Yuiri's weapon upgrades as Yuka Sil, Coola Sil. The first, plain Sil, cannot be seen without a cheating device. These are the same names as Yumi's weapon.
• Yuri learns the unique skills Heavy Damage and Sharp Shoot. She does not reach S level in either Melee or Magic skills but can get between A and B+ level in anything. She is more attack oriented than Yumi.

name: Yumi
description: Yumi (ユミィ, Yumyi) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. Yumi is a demure member of the Alma Kinan.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Yumi is a warrior of the Alma Kinan possessed with a dignity befitting one with the ability to commune with the spirits. As a warrior, she has stunning bow prowess. Unusual for their people, she and Yuiri would leave their village in order to find Chris Lightfellow at the behest of Yun. Innocent and kind-hearted, she had spent all her life in the Alma Kinan Village before their adventure.

Following this mission, both she and Yuiri would join the Fire Bringer at Chris' request in order to protect the Grasslands, arriving at Brass Castle in time to help the Fire Bringer fight off a Harmonia attack.

She would prove to be popular among the men at Budehuc Castle. Not used to the dating scene, she would turn men down because she didn't know how to deal with them.

Among those, Nash, Ace and Joker may have simply been trying to get information from her, so she could have been accidentally protecting her own culture, although Yuiri had to step in to get her away from Ace. Kidd even remarked that he wished he was older, so he could get to know Yumi on better terms. Yumi would often settle matters for the more aggressive Yuiri, though, as she had the better people-skills of the two. 

Following the War of the Champions, she returned to the Alma Kinan but had developed an interest in the outside world that led her to leave the village occasionally.

trivia:
• Yumi's weapon upgrades as Yooka Sil, Coola Sil. The first one, plain Sil, cannot be seen without a cheating device. These are the same names as Yuiri's weapon.
• Yumi learns the unique skills Sharp Shoot and Magic Rationing. She is able to get most of her skills to B+, but only Sharp Shoot to A level. She is slightly more magic oriented than Yuiri.
• Yumi participates in the Alma Kinan attack with Yuiri, where many arrows are fired at the enemies.

name: Yun
description: Yun (ユン, Yun) is a major character in Suikoden III. Yun is a member of the Alma Kinan possessed with the power of clairvoyance.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Yun was a young maiden from the Alma Kinan Village. Blessed with the sacred gift of foresight, she served as a medium of the Alma Kinan people, a vessel through which the threads of fate could be glimpsed.

Gentle and pure of heart, she held a deep affection for all living things, so much so that it was said she would converse with animals as though understanding their voices, and whisper to flowers as if they whispered back.

From the moment she first beheld Chris Lightfellow in her visions, Yun placed in her an unwavering trust. This feeling would only be amplified when conversing with Wyatt about his daughter.

As the War of the Champions spread across the land, Yun summoned Chris to Alma Kinan and revealed to her the secrets of the True Water Rune and the fate of her father. With solemn grace, she then prepared to undertake the soul-sending ritual, a sacred rite in which her spirit would be surrendered to the land's guardian spirits.

In her final hours, Yun spoke with serene certainty about her fate, despite Chris's protests. Though she knew the ritual would claim her life, she did not falter, for she understood that this sacrifice was the only path to unseal the True Water Rune as well as to fulfill her people's contract with the spirits.

The Destroyers would attempt to interrupt the ceremony to try and find the location of the True Water Rune but were thwarted. With dignity, she stepped into the circle of ancient power, her soul merging with the spirits of the Grassland, her essence becoming one with the whispering winds and the rustling trees of Kuput Forest.

name: Zepon
description: Zepon (ゼポン, Zepon) is a supporting character in Suikoden III. He is the honored chief of the Lizard Clan.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Zepon was the Chief of the Lizard Clan who was assassinated at the outset of the War of the Champions as part of the Destroyers plot to incite war between the Zexen Federation and the Six Clans. He had crossed paths with Yuber in the past and Yuber would take pleasure in assassinating him.

In the original invasion of the Grasslands by the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia, 50 years prior to the War of the Champions, Zepon had fought alongside the Fire Bringer and its leader, the Flame Champion. As such, he was also an old friend and comrade of Geddoe, who had visited him before his death.

name: Adrienne
description: Adrienne (アドリアンヌ, Adoriannu) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV who also appears in Suikoden Tactics. Adrienne is a neophyte blacksmith looking to improve her craft.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Adrienne was a young smith working towards becoming a full-fledged blacksmith. She worked for a time in the smithery at Middleport.

She would join the crew of the Stronghold Ship in Obel after traveling there to purchase materials just before the Kooluk attack on the island. Her strong sense of responsibility, as well as her bright, positive personality, led her to apologize profusely to her master in Middleport for this.

As a blacksmith, she was talented, not only being able to temper and hone weapons but also able to use her talents to craft armor and other equipment out of raw ore. In fact, some armors only she was able to craft, and a lot of the armor used during the Island Liberation War was of her hand.

Following the war, she was allowed to open a smithery in the Kingdom of Obel, precluding her from returning to her apprenticeship in Middleport. She made frequent use of the Quest Guild in Middleport to gather rare materials that she used to craft rare pieces of armor.

After Kyril brought her the Legendary Hammer, she was able to hone weapons to perfection, a skill that Kyril and his group made plenty use of.

name: Agnes
description: Agnes (アグネス, Agunesu) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV. Agnes is the tactician student of Elenor Silverberg.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Agnes was a young girl striving to become a strategist, studying under Elenor Silverberg while also taking care of her on Hermitage Island. She was intelligent and usually very calm in demeanor, although Tanya, who also wished to learn from Elenor, brought out her more combative feelings frequently.

She idolized Elenor and as such, readily followed her directions. She would drug the party who had come to visit Elenor at Hermitage Island before helping to revive them at Elenor's say-so. Agnes would readily join the Allied Forces when Elenor agreed to aid the group. As Elenor's aide, she would try and reproach her mentor for her frequent drinking but, knowing the depths of Elenor's pain, was also lenient enough to keep her stocks full.

Although young, Agnes had some measure of ability as a strategist and would mostly help in assigning troops during the Island Liberation War. She was not able to read the weather like Elenor could but hoped to learn the skill from her one day.

She took the role of battlefield strategist during the final battle of the war, as Elenor was leading a mission at El-Eal Fortress itself. The Allied victory in the face of the 1st Kooluk Fleet, led by Troy, was a testament to her ability.

Following the war, she would continue to study strategy, studying abroad in the Scarlet Moon Empire. She would also ensure Elenor's home on Hermitage Island was well stocked with liquor, in anticipation of the missing strategist's return.

name: Akaghi
description: Akaghi (アカギ, Akagi) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. Akaghi is a ninja working under Ramada.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Akaghi was one of Ramada's subordinates, along with Mizuki, as a member of Orark Maritime Trade. Although a ninja, he was very easily moved and prone to outbursts of emotion and was generally a kind-hearted person devoted to his companions. He and Mizuki were from the same village and although he often tried to play the protective big brother, in the end Akaghi was always at her mercy.

Aligned with the Kooluk Empire as part of Orark Maritime Trade, Akaghi and his companions came to Razril when their ship was blown off course after obtaining Rune Cannon ammunition at Middleport. Escorted by the Knights of Gaien, the group evaded a raid by the pirate Brandeau and continued on their way.

Much later, after the destruction of Iluya Island, Akaghi and his group were ordered to obtain the Rune of Punishment, ambushing the the hero in the Kingdom of Obel alongside Mizuki. The three would be defeated and captured, with Ramada surrendering to the hero. Though frustrated and confused by their surrender, Akaghi begrudgingly accepted his new situation. Although initially fearful of being caught in the wrath of the Rune of Punishment, Akaghi would soon come around to aiding the Allied Forces during the war.

As the war continued, Akaghi became more concerned with the effect the Rune of Punishment could be having on the hero, rather than any personal risk. The hero's use of the rune to destroy the suicide privateer fleet particularly affected Akaghi, who felt that regardless of the outcome, it wasn't right to force the hero to go through such an ordeal. He was also worried about Ramada's fate after seeing his bird return alone from an espionage mission at El-Eal Fortress.

Following the Island Liberation War, Akaghi and his companions would enter into the service of Lino En Kuldes.…

name: Aldo
description: Aldo (アルド, Arudo) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV. Aldo is an empathetic archer who tries to reach out to Ted during their time together.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Hermitage Life
Aldo hailed from a secluded forest island east of the Nest of Pirates, where his people, proficent in archery, lived a self-sufficient existence for generations. Defying their elders' wishes, the restless youth built a boat to explore the world, and an intrigued Aldo joined their voyage.

They landed on the nearby Hermitage Island, but their noisy hunting angered its guardian, Gau, who attacked them with maned wolves. In the ensuing panic to flee, Aldo was left behind on the shore as his companions' boat vanished over the horizon.

Aldo would sustain himself on the island through hunting. He would provide meat and other game to Agnes and Elenor Silverberg.

Though it could be a lonesome life, coming from a small community helped Aldo cope. Over time he would feel a bond of friendship with both Agnes and Gau. When the Stronghold Ship arrived on the island, he would join the crew of the vessel.
Friendly Ear
Aldo's initial experience aboard the ship was marked by his unease around the presence of numerous individuals, causing him to be extremely quiet. However, his inherently kind nature began to surface, helping him navigate the challenges of reintegrating into a community of people. Over time, he managed to adapt to the environment and forge connections with his shipmates.

Notably, Aldo demonstrated a strong affinity for Ted, despite the latter's efforts to distance himself from others. Aldo's unwavering commitment to maintaining an open mind and engaging in conversations reflected his empathetic approach.

Following the conclusion of the Island Liberation War, Aldo's deep concern for Ted led him to shadow the enigmatic figure for a time. However, Aldo's fate ultimately remained shrouded in uncertainty.

Aldo's life came to a sudden end, marked by a…

trivia: • If Ted is not recruited, his ending will change. Instead of dying, he returns to the forest.

name: Ameria
description: Ameria (アメリア, Ameria) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. Ameria is an experienced mercenary who is deeply committed to her physical appearance.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Ameria was a beautiful swordswoman who wandered the land, serving as a mercenary and bodyguard. She was very concerned about her personal appearance, rationalizing it as a reward for all the life-risking work she frequently undertook.

Although she could be very cool and realistic, Ameria also had a very kind heart and when Razril was occupied by the Kooluk Empire, she helped protect the villagers there as a bodyguard. Following its liberation by the Allied Forces, Ameria worried about the future of the island's inhabitants but was convinced that the town would now recover, freeing her to accept an invitation by the hero to join the force.

Ameria would fight throughout the Island Liberation War, showcasing the sword skills honed from her mercenary work. Following the war, she attempted to forsake the use of make-up but quickly changed her mind when no one recognized her without it.

Following the order of King Lino En Kuldes himself, she would later help Kyril in his investigations of the Rune Cannons in the Kooluk Empire. She was still very concerned about her outward appearance, as she complained to Maxine and Jeane that the night shifts were bad for her complexion.

trivia: • Ameria is one of three characters in Suikoden Tactics who does not show any form of Good Will with other characters, the other two characters being Wendel and Lalacle.

name: Axel
description: Axel (アクセル, Akuseru) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. Axel is a fiery, young warrior of Na-Nal Island.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Axel was the proud, hot-tempered son of the Na-Nal Island Chief. His stubbornness often led him to make hasty decisions. Like most of the Na-Nal human population, he held a bias against the elven population of the island. Out of all the warriors of Na-Nal, his strength was second only to his father's.

When the leader of the Allied Forces set out to liberate the Island Nations from the Kooluk Empire, Axel and his father initially refused to help, preferring to cooperate with the Kooluk forces led by Troy. Upon the arrival of the hero and Lino En Kuldes on the island and their confrontation with Kooluk troops, Axel would apprehend the group.

Axel and his father would then offer the allied representatives their freedom in exchange for the Elven Remedy kept by Na-Nal's elves. Following the completion of the task, Axel would deliver the remedy to the Kooluk soldiers previously injured before attempting to escort his charges back to their ship.

Before he could, however, Axel and his prisoners would be taken prisoner in turn by Selma. Eventually escaping with Selma's help, Axel would discover that the Kooluk had slaughtered the Na-Nal natives for their perceived culpability in poisoning the Kooluk garrison on the island. The Elven Remedy had been a setup by the Elven Elder to combat the Kooluk occupation.

Inconsolable at the massacre, Axel would eventually agree with his father's decision to support the Allied Forces and would be sent to join the crew of the Stronghold Ship as their official representative. Although undeniably inexperienced, Axel was certainly a skilled warrior. Following the Island Liberation War, he would succeed his father as Chief of Na-Nal.

Two years later, Axel was one of the warriors Lino En Kuldes sent out to support Kyril after the discovery of…

name: Bang
description: Bang (バン, Ban) is a minor character in Suikoden IV. Bang is a lottery booth operator who suffers from a rather unfortunate cat allergy.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Bang was an old man who worked at the lottery booth in the Nay-Kobold Settlement on Nay Island, despite having a cat allergy. He was a compassionate person, but that only made more surprising the fact that he sometimes fixed his lottery by filling the drawing machine with only the least valuable white balls.

Due to his allergies, he found working in the Nay-Kobold Settlement untenable and so jumped at the chance to join the crew of the Stronghold Ship and operate his lottery there. His cold from allergies soon cleared up, although he worried that Jeane would give herself a cold due to her attire.

Following the Island Liberation War, he would return to Nay Island and reopen his lottery booth but this time in the human village on the island.

name: Bartholomew
description: Bartholomew (バーソロミュー, Bāsoromyū) is a character in Suikoden IV. Bartholomew is a fugitive on the run from the Kooluk Empire.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Bartholomew was a man on the run for attacking and slaying a number of Kooluk soldiers in the early stages of the Island Liberation War. Fearing that being a wanted man would mark him as a risk to any surrounding people, he would go on the run, eventually hiding out in the Nay-Kobold Settlement on Nay Island. With his large build and sharp eyes, Bartholomew radiated an intimidating presence but, in reality, he was a calm, polite man.

When the leader of the Stronghold Ship discovered who Bartholomew was, he invited him to join the Allied Forces. Bartholomew would eventually agree to the proposition, joining the crew. As a member of the ship's crew, he would talk with both Tov, eventually discovering an interest in woodcarving, creating small carvings of birds and animals. He would also learn from Gareth, a professional engraver, but made sure to stick to his own methods.

Once the Island Liberation War ended, Bartholomew set out on a journey to the Queendom of Falena in search of battle.

name: Basil
description: Basil (バジル, Bajiru) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV. Basil is a tops player and a good friend of the hero.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Basil was a friend of the hero who lived in Razril. He was not a member of the Knights of Gaien but still played frequently with the hero, especially in his favourite game; koma.

When Razril was occupied by the Kooluk Empire, Basil managed to keep himself safe and following the island's liberation by the Allied Forces would volunteer to join. On board, he would spend his time entertaining others with his games.

He refuted any accusation that he was lazy, countering that he was ensuring everyone was relaxed and taking proper breaks from their work. As time passed, he grew more and more comfortable with the sailing life, especially enjoying Funghi's food for the first time.

Following the Island Liberation War, Basil would spend some time on Nay Island before becoming a crew member on a merchant ship and setting sail once again.

name: Brandeau
description: Brandeau (ブランド, Burando) is an antagonist and supporting character in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics respectively. Brandeau is a pirate feared across the Island Nations for his ruthless nature.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Brandeau was a pirate that was once a close friend of both Kika and Edgar, being called Edgar's left arm. He would often find himself being the voice of reason to Edgar's more passionate nature and was already to pass on his calm advice.

When Edgar decided to attack the pirate Steele, Brandeau and Peck would fight alongside him. After Steele was defeated, he would use the Rune of Punishment, killing Edgar and costing Brandeau his left eye. Brandeau would then inherit the Rune of Punishment from the dead Steele.

With Edgar dead and Peck mutilated by the exploding Evil Eye, Brandeau felt he could not return to the Nest of Pirates and the two survivors would begin their own pirate band.

He would change, according to Kika, when he became the bearer of the Rune of Punishment. Following this, he would become a feared pirate all across the Island Nations region. His six-mast ship would become a symbol of fear for merchants and travelers in the region.

During the Island Liberation War, he would use the rune when he was finally cornered by the hero after an ill-fated attack on the hero's trade escort, losing his life to the rune's curse.

name: Brec
description: Brec (ブレック, Burekku) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV. Brec is a pirate serving under Jango, fighting against the encroachment of the Kooluk Empire on their territories.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Brec was a pirate who served under Jango, who he looked up to as a brother. Although a rough-hearted sort of man, he's not one to rampage but can become violent when provoked.

When the pirate crew found the Kooluk Empire encroaching on territories they considered theirs, Brec and Jango forced their way onto the Stronghold Ship and intimidated their way into the hero's bedroom, joining the crew.

As part of the crew, Brec turned a lot of people against him with his resistance to any form of bathing, especially Taisuke, who had to clean up after the rare baths he and Jango did take.
