Following the Island Liberation War, Brec turned to his pirate lifestyle under Jango, loving the excitement and lack of hygiene standards of his career.

name: Carrie
description: Carrie (キャリー, Kyarī) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. Carrie worked as Doctor Yu's assistant in his practice in the Kingdom of Obel.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
thumb|left|220px|Carrie is a puppet of Big Mega Medicine.
Carrie worked as Doctor Yu's assistant in the Kingdom of Obel and was entrusted with the prescribing of medicine to his patients. She held a great adoration for the doctor and worked hard to arrange his schedule so that he would avoid working without rest and damaging his own health.

She had been performing work for Yu when the Kooluk Empire occupied Obel and so she was trapped there until the island's liberation, after which she would join the crew of the Stronghold Ship, assisting the doctor in his work there. Always busy, she would prescribe and sell medicines at the doctor's office. She was usually cheerful, but she did wish that Maxine, who stayed in the same room as her, would help out sometimes.

Once the Island Liberation War ended, she would return to Obel with Yu and work at the free clinic he established there. 

She would later join Kyril's investigation of the Rune Cannons by command of King Lino En Kuldes, serving as a medic. She also convinced Yu to join them as well and showed great concern for his well-being.

name: Cedric
description: Cedric (セドリック, Sedorikku) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. Cedric is a fast-paced youth who developed a reputation for running out on meals without paying.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Cedric was a young man who was born carrying the Godspeed Rune, which gave him the gift of incredible speed. He was a little timid but overall, the bearer of a pure heart, putting up a brave front in order to use his skills to help people. His naivety angered many as he did not know that potch was needed to eat in restaurants, earning him a reputation for dining and dashing. Because of this habit, Rachel would be called in to collect on his debts.

During the Island Liberation War, he would be caught in the act after he ran aboard the hidden Stronghold Ship and would join the group in order to pay off his debt. However, it is said that after the war, he would continue to run out on his meals in order to get Rachel to chase him.

Indeed, Kyril was later hired by a restaurant in Merseto to help Rachel in catching Cedric again after he did not pay his 5000 potch for his meals. After he is caught, Kyril promises to pay his debts if he helps him in his cause. Cedric gladly agrees and joins his group together with Rachel. The two of them are often seen discussing, but also show signs of a deeper relationship.

trivia:
• In Suikoden IV, Cedric refers to his rune as the True Holy Rune, the same localization used in the first Suikoden. 
• He does not wear any unique rune in Suikoden Tactics but possesses a unique skill which is called True Godspeed, partly fixing the localization error.

name: Chadli
description: Chadli (シャドリ, Shadori) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV. Chadli is the slightly helmet-obsessed item store owner on board the Stronghold Ship.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Chadli was a general merchant who worked on the leader's Stronghold Ship during the Island Liberation War. He was part of the ship's crew from the beginning. As such, he was fully aware of the base's true nature as an ocean-going vessel, but did not open his item shop until after the ship's nature was revealed and had fled the Kingdom of Obel.

He was a talented merchant who whenever he heard of the leader purchasing an item in a store would make sure to add that item to his stock immediately. He was also in charge of logistical support during the war.

Following the war, Chadli would use the profit he accrued to buy himself a brand-new helmet.

name: Champo
description: Champo (チャンポ, Chanpo) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. Champo is a friendly Nay-Kobold with ambitions to become a great merchant.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Champo was a young Nay-Kobold who admired and looked up to his friend, Nalkul. Although he tended to move at a leisurely pace, giving the impression of someone who was slow, he was a prodigy at catching rats. In fact, he was the greatest ratcatcher on all of Nay Island. In other respects, he was a typical Nay-Kobold, fond of fish and disdainful of baths.

When Nalkul stole the Golden Seal, Champo was initially reluctant to part ways with it, being entranced by its beauty. It was also Champo who would lose it, accidentally knocking it into the warehouse at the Nay-Kobold Settlement on Nay Island due to Nalkul's sudden toss when confronted on the matter. 

However, it was also Champo who recovered the seal from the warehouse it was lost in, after realizing that the seal belonged to the Allied Forces and Champo and Nalkul's hero, Chiepoo. His prodigious rat catching skills allowed him to quickly corral all the creatures and the seal they had taken. Following this, he would join the Allied Forces along with Nalkul, eager to learn from the great Chiepoo.

Following the Island Liberation War, he would open a business along with Nalkul under the tutelage of Chiepoo, putting in the hard work necessary to turn the early struggles into future success.

name: Charlemagne
description: Charlemagne (シャルルマーニュ, Sharurumānyu) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. Charlemagne is a highly refined gentleman from Middleport and friend of Schtolteheim Reinbach III.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Charlemagne ran the Lovely Ringlet Cottage inn on Middleport island around the time of the Island Liberation War. He was a gentle hearted man who was unfaltering polite to everyone. He also practiced swordplay as a hobby, having discovered a taste for it at some point.

He would find himself involved with the Allied Forces when they recruited Helga, who had previously damaged his inn. Charlemagne would cover the costs of Helga's damages, freeing her to join them. Having heard of the leader's accomplishments, he would ask to join the crew after taking some time to sort out his personal affairs.

Aboard the Stronghold Ship, Charlemagne's refined attributes along with finely honed aesthetic sense would lead him to become a close friend of Schtolteheim Reinbach III. Following the war, Charlemagne would be given peerage as a nobleman by Reinbach for his service.

Two years later, Charlemagne traveled alone into Kooluk territory, following bandits who had stolen Reinbach's Rose Crest. His friendship was such that he was willing to risk his life alone to recover it. Kyril would be hired by Reinbach to assist him, and, fighting together, Charlemagne was able to retrieve the precious object, but he was devastated that Reinbach apparently doubted his ability.

They returned the item to its rightful owner who explained that he did not doubt Charlemagne's skills but was worried that something might have happened to his trusted friend, bringing Charlemagne much relief. When they learned of Kyril's mission to destroy the Rune Cannons, they joined his endeavor. During their journey, the two of them deepened their friendship once again.

trivia: • If you do not recruit Reinbach in Suikoden IV, his ending will change, and he becomes the new owner of Lovely Ringlet Cottage.

name: Chiepoo
description: Chiepoo (チープー, Chīpū) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. Chiepoo is a Nay-Kobold with grand ambitions to become a great merchant.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Chiepoo was a young Nay-Kobold who broke with the tradition of his village on Nay Island and left with the goal of becoming a great merchant. Filled with curiosity, Chiepoo had a bright and cheerful personality and eventually found his way to Razril. He would work at the item store there, stocking shelves.

During the Kindling Ritual ceremony for newly graduated Knights of Gaien, Chiepoo would alert the fledgling knights to a kidnapping attempt in the town's back streets. Chiepoo's call for help would allow the child to be rescued and the Kidnapper to be vanquished.

Later, when the hero was exiled, Chiepoo would stowaway on his boat, unaware of its nature, hoping to find somewhere new to explore and learn about business. When the ship washed up on the Deserted Island, he would curious as to whether the various crabs on the island would make for good food. This curiosity eventually drew the ire of the Boss Crab, and Chiepoo would have to be rescued by his crew mates.

Following their departure from the Deserted Island and subsequent rescue by the Kingdom of Obel, Chiepoo would continue to journey with the hero, eventually becoming part of the Stronghold Ship crew and a member of the Allied Forces. Chiepoo's help would prove vital in recovering the Golden Seal when it was stolen by Nalkul on Nay Island.

During this adventure, Chiepoo was surprised to find out he was something of a folk hero to some at the Nay-Kobold Settlement, including Nalkul and Champo. He would spend the Island Liberation War learning about the specialties of every island the ship visited, expanding his business acumen. As the war progressed, he continued to show concern for the hero's continued use of the Rune of Punishment.

Once the war ended, Chiepoo continued to travel for a time before…

name: Colton
description: Colton (コルトン, Koruton) is a supporting antagonist in Suikoden IV. Colton served as commander of the Kooluk Empire's second fleet during the Island Liberation War.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Colton served as a subordinate of Troy and the commander of the Kooluk Empire's second fleet during the Island Liberation War. He had sworn allegiance to the Empire and all his actions were taken out of his loyalty to his country.

This loyalty meant that only did Colton fight for the Empire during the Island Liberation War, he would also investigate and, with Troy, try to maneuver around the disturbing plans of Graham Cray as well. As commander of the 2nd Kooluk Fleet he was also a lead figure in the Kingdom of Obel's occupation.

Following his defeat, Colton would be captured by the Allied Forces and taken aboard the Stronghold Ship where he would remain silent for the remainder of the war, although he was not unhappy to see Graham Cray defeated.

He was the father of Helmut, who would visit him sometimes after his capture. Colton held no resentment towards his son for the choices he made and following the war, he would disappear along with his son.

name: Cray's son
description: Cray's son (クレイの息子, Kurei no musuko) is a minor character in Suikoden IV. He is the unnamed son of antagonist Graham Cray who perished due to the Rune of Punishment some years before the beginning of the game.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
220px|left|thumb|The spirit of Graham Cray's son turns to ash in front of the hero.|rightThe son of Graham Cray came into the possession of the Rune of Punishment when his father, the previous rune bearer, cut off his left arm to escape the curse, not realizing it would target his son next. Following this, Graham would try and keep his son's rune a secret for his own safety, having him promise never to use the rune.

However, Cray's son would end up using the Rune of Punishment during the Man-Hunting Incident, destroying those nobles attacking his village and would die due to the rune's curse soon after. Cray would end up taking responsibility for the incident and be exiled from the Scarlet Moon Empire.

Years later, Cray's son would appear as a memory of the past to the newest bearer of the rune, after he used the power of the Rune of Punishment to defeat the Boss Crab living on the Deserted Island. The memory of Cray's son would apologize for using the rune against his father's wishes before wishing he could taste the bread his father used to bake one more time. The memory then turned to ash.

name: Dario
description: Dario (ダリオ, Dario) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. Dario is the impulsive, self-proclaimed right-hand man of Lady Kika.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Early Life
Dario was a notorious delinquent raised in a rough port town, where he learned to brawl and drink from a young age. Convinced of his own invincibility, he spent his nights picking fights in taverns and dragging opponents into the streets.

He eventually became a sailor's apprentice, though he constantly schemed to avoid actual work. During one voyage, he stole and guzzled the captain's prized liquor, draining nearly the entire bottle.

Enraged, the captain beat Dario severely and threw him overboard to drown. As he drifted helplessly, Dario was saved by a compassionate mermaid who ignored her companions' pleas to leave him.

He washed ashore and claimed to have drifted in with the tide after falling overboard. Most who ever heard his tale dismissed it as a drunken hallucination. Still, he maintained a life-long affection and protectiveness over mermaids.
Pirate For Hire
Dario would eventually become a pirate serving under Kika as her, self-proclaimed, right-hand man. He was also the father of Nalleo and although they have a true parent-child bond, Dario had not confided in him that they are not blood-related nor the identity of his mother, Louise.

Louise, an old friend of Dario, had thrusted Nalleo upon him soon after his birth. Louise's only motivation was that she didn't want to raise a child. At first Dario viewed her actions as selfish but as he grew to love Nalleo, chose to see him as a treasure instead.

Thinking with his heart over his head, Dario was often a source of strife for Kika, Sigurd, and Hervey due to his hot-temper and propensity to start fights. Still, his blunders were usually forgiven as Dario's motivations were usually pure and in pursuit of justice.

He would be hired by Ramada to lead an attack on Razril, following the death of…

name: Deborah
description: Deborah (デボラ, Debora) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV. Deborah is a stately woman who possesses some degree of clairvoyant talents.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
220px||thumb|Debroah spies a Hero (Suikoden IV)|boy with a special fate.|leftDeborah was a woman who possessed a strange ability to "see" the intangibles of fate surrounding a person, giving her a talent for fortune-telling. Her abilities were not outright future sight. She could, however, "see" people who would impact a person, both past, present and future. This talent was something she always had.

She also possessed an impeccably graceful demeanor and could be found spending much of her time in Middleport, enjoying tea and spending time with her close friend, Oskar. When the leader of the Stronghold Ship came to Middleport, Deborah became interested in the aura surrounding the youth and so would volunteer to join the crew, using her give for foresight to offer advice when she could, helping him to recruit even more fellows to his cause.

Following the Island Liberation War, she and Oskar would return to Middleport, continuing their lives of quiet gossip and friendship.

trivia: • She has a habit of calling the hero "Little Hero" (Hero-chan in Japanese), with Hero being whatever name you gave to the main character.

name: Desmond
description: Desmond (デスモンド, Desumondo) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV. Desmond is the assistant to Princess Flare and a statesman working under Setsu in the Kingdom of Obel.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Desmond served as an assistant to Flare in her position as Princess of Obel. Although skittish and nervous, the polite and serious Desmond was a man who would tackle any task given to him with hard work and dedication.

As Flare's assistant, he accompanied her on her patrols of Obel's waters, and it was on one of those patrols that he would first meet the exiled hero. Noticing a rune on his left hand, Desmond would examine it and discover that it was, in fact, the Rune of Punishment. This would lead to the group returning to Obel and meeting with King Lino En Kuldes.

Loyal to Flare and the Kingdom of Obel, he was one of the first people to join the crew of the Stronghold Ship. During the Island Liberation War, he would develop a strong crush on Louise, often visiting her in the ship's saloon. He would become jealous of Ornan, worried that he had similar feelings for her, even writing a letter complaining to that effect.

After the war, he because depressed for a time after Louise vanished but recommitted himself to his stately duties under Setsu.

name: Elenor Silverberg
description: Elenor Silverberg (エレノア・シルバーバーグ, Erenoa Shirubābāgu) is a major character in Suikoden IV. Elenor is a deeply troubled master strategist exiled from the Scarlet Moon Empire.
originalName: エレノア・シルバーバーグ

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
Elenor was a woman of average height, thin and gaunt with sallow skin and brown eyes. Her hair was also brown, and unkempt in style and showed signs of recession around the scalp and temples. Only a very loose ponytail, kept in place by a green bow, kept her long hair in check.

Clothing wise, she dressed in all black. A black coat, complete with a black scarf wrapped around her neck and shoulders, covered her dark vestments and she also wore a pair of black trousers. The only splashes of color came from the off-color white bits of trim on her clothing. Her boots were similarly black. She was also rarely seen without a bottle of liquor in her right hand.

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
As a master strategist of the Silverberg family, Elenor had no little sense of esteem for her own tactical skills. Still, in many ways she despised her talents, finding herself disheartened by the decisions she had to make as a military strategist and leader. Her inability to reconcile the pragmatism of war with her personal compassionate nature brought her much grief.

Elenor was also consumed with regret and guilt over the expulsion of Graham Cray. As Cray had adamantly refused to explain his actions, Elenor would end up ruminating on the incident that led to their expulsion again and again over the years. It was partly this drive for an answer that led her to eventually become the chief strategist of the Allied Forces.

These two aspects of her personality pushed Elenor into a life of drinking. Kika herself would describe Elenor as a troubled woman and because of this seemed reluctant to involve her in the Island Liberation War initially despite the respect she held for Elenor's capabilities.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Elenor Silverberg was an esteemed tactician and one of the famous Silverberg line of tacticians. She had served the Scarlet Moon Empire in the past and had won great fame and renown with her service. As a strategist of the Empire, she had worked to repel attacks from the Kooluk Empire.

Still, her service would come to a halt when the involvement of her subordinate, Graham Cray, in the Man-Hunting Incident led her to be expelled from the Empire. As she had high hopes for Cray, this incident hit her harder than it otherwise would have. 

Following this, she moved to the isolated Hermitage Island where she became a recluse, giving herself to excessive drinking. Her understudy, Agnes, would serve sake to her silently as she ruminated over the past. She would meet Kika at some point with the two having a relationship of mutual respect.

Several years later, Kika would refer the crew of the Stronghold Ship to Elenor but not before noting that Elenor had her own problems and that she may not choose to help them repel the Kooluk forces. Upon arriving, the crew were drugged by Agnes on Elenor's orders. She would then learn from the group that Graham Cray was behind the Kooluk Empire's Southern Expansion Policy and its invasion of the Island Nations.

Her interest piqued, she had the hero collect a restorative for the sleeping drug and a Silverberg crest to prove his dedication. Wishing to correct her student, who had changed so much in countenance since the incident that led to their expulsion, as well as to discover his motives, she would agree to serve as the tactician of the Allied Forces.

Her influence on the Island Liberation War was insurmountable. Under her direction, the various islands of the region were able to unite into a single force, capable of liberating the…

trivia:
• Elenor holds a preference towards wines from Kanakan.
• Elenor is the earliest known female Silverberg, pre-dating Odessa by almost 200 years. She is also the oldest known Silverberg that has made an appearance in a game. Julian Silverberg, the first Silverberg strategist ever known, never appeared outside of publications.

name: Elven Elder
description: The Elven Elder (エルフの長老, Erufu no Chōrō) is a minor antagonist in Suikoden IV. The Elven Elder is the leader of the Na-Nal Island elven community.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
The Elven Elder was the leader of the elven community that lived on Na-Nal Island. He looked down on and hated humans as a whole and his suspicions of the race were only furthered when the humans of Na-Nal handed over the island to the invading Kooluk Empire.

In anticipation of an attempt to steal the fabled Elven Remedy, the Elder replaced it with a poison that afflicted the Kooluk stationed at Na-Nal, resulting in a retaliatory massacre of the humans by the enraged Kooluk.

trivia: • The Elven Elder's title in Japanese is identical to the Japanese title of Suikoden's Chief of the Elves.

name: Ema
description: Ema (エマ, Ema) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. Ema is the devoted wife of Gary.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Ema was a caring wife who helped to support her husband, Gary, whose enthusiasm often got the best of him. With an easy-going personality and a gentle heart, she would watch over him, smiling, although she would not hesitate to chide him if necessary. Together, they travelled around the Island Nations only to find themselves in the Kingdom of Obel just as the Island Liberation War broke out.

She would remain on the island with Gary, despite having the chance to evacuate, as she wanted to help Princess Flare and boost the morale of the citizens who remained there during the Kooluk occupation. Following the island's liberation, she would join the crew of the Stronghold Ship alongside her husband.

Once the war ended, Ema and Gary resumed their travels, seeking a place where they could live in peace. They would eventually journey to Merseto where, after witnessing Kyril seeing off a bunch of unscrupulous merchants in the town, Gary decided to join Kyril's fight against the Rune Cannons and Ema gladly accompanied him.

name: Etienne
description: Etienne (エチエンヌ, Echiennu) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV. Etienne is a minstrel who served Schtolteheim Reinbach III.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Etienne was a bard who served the Reinbach household in Middleport. He held a large, sincere respect especially for Reinbach III.

As such, Etienne was quite frustrated at not being able to perform for Reinbach once he joined the crew of the Stronghold Ship but the two would be reunited when Etienne was invited aboard the vessel also.

Aboard the ship, Etienne would perform in the saloon there, healing the hearts of weary fighters with his songs, his repertoire growing throughout the war. Following the Island Liberation War, he would return to Middleport where he continued to play for Reinbach while composing many beautiful and beloved songs.

name: Eugene
description: Eugene (ユージン, Yūjin) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. Eugene is a timid but skillful spear user from Na-Nal Island.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Eugene was a timid young spear user who, though possessed with natural talent, found it hard to utilize due to his shy nature. As he bore the Firefly Rune which made him a frequent target for enemies, it is possible that his timid nature may not have been so unreasonable.

Eugene's mother had planned to leave Eugene in the care of the Na-Nal Island Chief in order to toughen her son up, after hearing rumors of the Stronghold Ship, she entrusted him into its leader's care. From Eugene's standpoint, he had just been evicted but by experiencing the Island Liberation War, the youth did become stronger, both mentally and physically.

Although still suffering from confidence issues, he trained hard and looked up to both his leader as well as Lino En Kuldes as a fellow spear user. Rachel also saw the potential Eugene bore.

Following the war, he returned home to Na-Nal Island filled with newfound courage.

name: Flare
description: Flare (フレア, Furea) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. The Princess of Obel, Flare is a skilled warrior, as well as a respected leader of her people.

facts.label: Family
facts.value: Hero (Suikoden IV) (brother), Lino En Kuldes (father), Queen of Obel (mother)

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Flare was the daughter of Lino En Kuldes and as such was Princess of the Kingdom of Obel. As a princess, her cheerful, friendly attitude led her to be beloved by the people. She was also an active leader, well able to take action herself, as shown by her patrols of Obel waters which she led personally, much to the worry of Desmond and Setsu.

It was during one of these patrols that Flare first encounter the hero and his companions. Shocked by the discovery of the Rune of Punishment on his hand, Flare had him brought to Obel immediately to meet her father. As she had lost her mother when still young, she had little memory of her and even less of her brother who was lost at sea. She still cherished her mother's music box, however, and would listen to it on occasion.

When Obel was occupied by the Kooluk Empire, she would help her father and other citizens to escape the island but would remain on Obel herself, along with Setsu, in order to support the citizens, there during the difficult period. Later during the Island Liberation War, during the liberation of Obel, she would coordinate with Elenor Silverberg to prevent 2nd Kooluk Fleet reinforcements from engaging in a pincer attack by taking over their ship.

Following the success of the battle and Obel's freedom, Flare would join the Allied Forces. Reunited with her father, the two would fight alongside each other for the remainder of the war. As a fearless leader in her own right, she didn't hesitate to carry out even the most dangerous of Elenor's plans. Following the foundation of the Island Nations Federation, Flare would resume her patrols of Obel's territorial waters, frightening any who would earn her wrath.

name: Fog Ship Guide
description: The Fog Ship Guide (霧の船の導者, Kiri no Fune no Dōsha) is a antagonist in Suikoden IV. The Fog Ship Guide appears on board the Fog Ship as part of the recruitment process for Ted.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
The Fog Ship Guide is a mysterious being from a parallel world. Filled with bitterness for unknown reasons, they seek the power of the 27 True Runes to enact revenge on this world.

While they have the appearance of an enormous monster, this is a magical shape that has no true substance, much like their vessel, the Fog Ship. The Guide's plan was to invite True Rune bearers to the Fog Ship where they would then take their power for their own.

Ted, who bore the Rune of Life and Death, was one such person but as he wanted to escape the curse of the "Soul Eater", boarded the ship voluntarily. During the Island Liberation War, the Guide would attempt to persuade the the leader of the Stronghold Ship to give up his Rune of Punishment but the leader instead convinced Ted to reclaim his rune, the Fog Ship Guide would turn on Ted and his newfound allies and attacked.

The Fog Ship Guide has extremely potent battle magic and proved to be a formidable foe. However, when Leknaat appeared, she urged the Guide to return to its "Eternal City".

Ted's Soul Eater would then dispatch the creature.

name: Frederica
description: Frederica (フレデリカ, Furederica) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. From the border between the Scarlet Moon Empire and Kooluk, she is investigating Graham Cray's links to the incidents there.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Frederica was a female archer obsessed with tracking down Graham Cray. Investigating Cray, as well as the Man-Hunting Incident, was her burning obsession, in order to find out the truth of what really happened to those villages on the Kooluk-Scarlet Moon border. Her search went fruitless, however, as those not directly involved showed little interest in her goals.

Knowing that Cray was a common enemy, she would offer to join the Allied Forces after meeting its leader in Razril, realising that traveling together would likely help her investigation.

Following the Island Liberation War, she finished her research on Cray and traveled north towards the Scarlet Moon Empire in order to help resolve tensions between the two nations.

name: Funghi
description: Funghi (フンギ, Fungi) is a minor character in Suikoden IV. Funghi is a cook who works for the Knights of Gaien in Razril.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Funghi was a young cook working the kitchen of the Hall of Knights for the Knights of Gaien. As the kitchen was very close to the Protagonist's room, the two held a close relationship. He was an excellent cook, to the point that some knights would claim they signed up just to experience his cooking.

He remained on Razril when it was occupied by the Kooluk Empire in the Island Liberation War but following its liberation, he would join the Allied Forces, where he would create a variety of dishes to nourish and inspire the crew. That said, not everyone loved his cooking. Konrad, in particular, found his food to be too salty.

Once the war ended, he returned to Razril where he would continue to develop his culinary talents, this time for the newly formed Razril Marine Knights.

name: Gareth
description: Gareth (ガレス, Garesu) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV. Gareth was a talented and renowned engraver.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Gareth was a sculptor and engraver, renowned for his talents throughout the Island Nations. Reinbach III's prized Rose Brooch was one of his works. However, the brooch was apparently made for display purposes and Gareth was surprised to hear that Reinbach was actively wearing it in battle.

During the Island Liberation War, he would request to join the Stronghold Ship crew so that he could maintain the Rose Brooch. Gareth refrained from fighting in the war itself but did sculpt an engraving for the vessel. Following the war, his ship engravings would become popular with visitors to the hot springs on Mordo Island.

name: Gary
description: Gary (ゲイリー, Geirī) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. Gary is the husband of Ema and a believer in justice.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Gary was a man who travelled the Island Nations together with his wife, Ema. They also have a son which they have not seen for years. The couple arrived at the Kingdom of Obel shortly before it was occupied by the Kooluk Empire. Gary's strong character and sense of justice meant that he turned down the chance to escape the island, choosing instead to remain and offer support to Princess Flare and the people of Obel.

Once Obel was liberated, he would join the hero and help to fight the Kooluk Empire. Following the Island Liberation War, he and Ema would begin a journey towards the Scarlet Moon Empire, hoping to find a place to settle down.

During a stay in Merseto, Gary and Ema observed how Kyril saved a small child from some violent merchants at the port. Impressed by his responsibility and sense of justice, Gary decided to lend him his strength in his investigations of the Rune Cannons.

On their journey, Gary was greatly inspired by all the young people around him fighting for justice, something that reminded him of his own son. His contacts with the royal family of Obel still seemed to be intact, as Gary was sometimes seen discussing the current situation regarding the Kooluk with Lino En Kuldes.

name: Glen Cott
description: Glen Cott (グレン・コット, Guren Kotto) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV. Commander Glen was the leader of the Knights of Gaien just prior to the outbreak of the Island Liberation War.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Glen Cott served as the Commander of the Knights of Gaien immediately prior to the Island Liberation War and was a veteran of the Dukedom of Gaien's battles against the Kooluk Empire. 

He was the instructor of both Snowe Vingerhut and his friend, whom he cared for due to his status as an orphan. He was the father of Katarina but very few people knew of this. He himself remained unaware of their connection all his life.

Although he could be warm, Glen was also a harsh and demanding leader working to make the best out of his young charges. He was spurred to do so by his own experiences in battle against Kooluk, especially the Gaien attack on El-Eal Fortress that saw four Gaien ships decimated by Troy alone.

Glen was critical of Snowe's abilities, attempting to instil improvement through harsh words while also being pressured by Vincent Vingerhut, Lord of Razril, to promote his son beyond his abilities. He found Snowe to be lacking in awareness and reliability as a ship captain, easily boarding his training vessel during their final training exercise. He would also work to try and ensure the hero's independence post-graduation. He would even go so far as to contact Schtolteheim Reinbach II in Middleport regarding the matter, but the lord was resistant to the matter.

Despite some misgivings, Glen would be pressured to appoint the newly graduated Snowe as a ship captain quickly. He chose to have Snowe lead the mission to escort Ramada's vessel to Iluya Island, reasoning that it was unlikely to be a perilous task. He would have the hero accompany Snowe, confident that his talents would make up for Snowe's shortcomings.

When Snowe's ship was attacked by the pirate Brandeau, Glen would quickly arrive on the scene after receiving reports of the pirate's movements. Finding…

name: Governor
description: The Governor (総督, Sōtoku) is a minor antagonist in Suikoden IV. He is a member of the Kooluk Empire government and so, in theory, directs the forces of Graham Cray, Troy and Colton throughout the game.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
The Governor served as commander of the Kooluk navy during the Island Liberation War. With an ambition to annexation the southern Island Nations, he worked with Graham Cray in planning the invasion and installing the Magnus Rune Cannon at El-Eal Fortress which would go on to destroy the surface of Iluya Island.

Although he was officially in command of Kooluk's Southern Expansion Policy, the Governor lacked the necessary stature to command the various officers under his command and seemed to be less than capable. Due to this, he would be manipulated by Graham Cray throughout the war only to be imprisoned once his usefulness was at an end. In the prisons of El-Eal Fortress, he would be discovered by Ramada and pleaded for help, but the conversation was overheard by Cray and the Governor was killed soon after.

name: Graham Cray
description: Graham Cray (グレアム・クレイ, Gureamu Kurei) is the primary antagonist of Suikoden IV. Cray is a former strategist of the Scarlet Moon Empire now working to further the Kooluk Empire's Southern Expansion Policy for his own ends.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Early Life and Service under Elenor
Graham Cray was a brilliant, sharp-minded native of a village on the Scarlet Moon Empire's border with Kooluk. His strategic acumen earned him a position as the first and brightest apprentice to the renowned strategist, Elenor Silverberg.

However, the two were often at odds due to their conflicting philosophies; while Elenor believed in using any means necessary to win, including deceit and dirty tricks, Cray was highly principled and argued against such methods. Despite their clashes, Elenor found their debates exhilarating and considered him her only true intellectual challenger.
The Man-Hunting Incident
Unaware that it was his homeland, Elenor sent Cray to investigate rumors that Scarlet Moon nobles were secretly slaughtering border villages and blaming Kooluk. Cray arrived with his young, adopted son to find his birthplace and surrounding villages utterly devastated by the so-called "man-hunts" conducted by the nobility.

At some point, he inherited the Rune of Punishment and, desperate to rid himself of the rune’s cursed influence, severed his left arm, later replacing it with a iron prosthetic. The Rune of Punishment then chose Cray’s son, as its next host. Cray would tell others that he had damaged his hand playing with his son and following the incident rarely spoke of him.

Cray conducted his investigation with stoic precision, internalizing his immense grief and fury. His son, witnessing the suffering through innocent eyes, was unable to stand by. In a fateful moment, the boy unleashed the power of the Rune of Punishment embedded in his left hand, immolating the noble perpetrators. 

Cray found his son collapsed in the burning village. The boy apologized for breaking his promise not to use the rune before dissolving into…

name: Gretchen
description: Gretchen (グレッチェン, Guretchen) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. Gretchen is a talented swordswoman who spends most of her time immersed in her training regimen.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Gretchen was a dedicated swordswoman who spent all her time training in the mountains. An absolutely scrupulous person, she had complete confidence in her sword fighting abilities.

During the Island Liberation War, she would hear rumors of the person said to be leading a resistance against the Kooluk Empire and would descend from the mountains to investigate. She would meet the leader in Razril following its liberation and would gladly join the Allied Forces.

On board the Stronghold Ship, Gretchen would serve as part of the leader's personal guard and was stationed outside his room constantly. She would harbor some frustration with Millay, whom Gretchen felt spoke only about the hero, though she was reluctant to speak of it, even in private. Her talent with a sword served the navy well and, following the war, she would go on to serve as a swordsmanship instructor at the Marines Academy in Razril. 

She would later join Kyril's investigation of the Rune Cannons by command of King Lino En Kuldes. Even though she was still confident in her skills, she also showed some modesty when approached by Millay who was very impressed by her swordsmanship, saying that she still had to learn a lot of the art.

name: Gunter
description: Gunter (ギュンター, Gyuntā) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV. Gunter was a traveling gambler.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Gunter was a gambler who traveled the Island Nations while earning his keep with the dice games he played. During the Island Liberation War, he would encounter the leader of the Stronghold Ship three times; at Middleport, Na-Nal Island and the Kingdom of Obel and would end up joining the Allied Forces, believe this series of meetings was fate.

On board ship, he would wrack up frequent wins in Chinchirorin and other games against the sailors there, making himself some nice earnings in the process, especially when compared to Igor. He was not a fan of the sea breeze, frequently bathing to remove the odor. He ranked third in Lo Fong's ranking of men on board. He was also not above petty theft, sometimes stealing spare change. 

Still, he became attracted to the idea of settling down in one spot and following the Island Liberation War would retire from the gambling life, opening a restaurant in Middleport.

name: Haruto
description: Haruto (ハルト, Haruto) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV. Haruto is the head navigator of the Stronghold Ship.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Haruto was a member of the original crew who worked on the Stronghold Ship when it left the Kingdom of Obel. He was technically in charge of steering the ship but he seemed to leave this duty to his subordinates, spending most of his time in the ship's library working on charts.

Despite this, he claimed he was a hard worker and only refrained from steering directly due to a bad arm. He also had to deal with a possessive Tanya who was very territorial about her library space. Haruto claimed he had all his charts memorized so there was never any worry about the ship going off course.

Following the Island Liberation War, Haruto would begin work on a brand new ship, assisting Tov in his designs.

name: Helga
description: Helga (ヘルガ, Heruga) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV. Helga is a powerful warrior prone to accidental property damage due to her exuberance.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Helga was an axe-wielder who was possessed with an almost superhuman level of strength. Although she could be a rough, blunt speaker, she was also gentle-hearted and, surprisingly, quite shy.

It is when Helga would try to overcompensate for this shyness that trouble would arise, usually in the form of panicked, accidental destruction of property. She was in Middleport when she accidentally dealt a great deal of property damage to the Lovely Ringlet Cottage inn there.

Hearing of the incident, the hero attempted to recruit her but found that Helga would have to pay for the damage. When Charlemagne offered to pay off her debt, she would agree to join the Allied Forces.

Aboard the Stronghold Ship, she would serve in the hero's personal guard along with Gretchen and Millay. Despite her best efforts, she would occasionally damage the ship. The most notable incident being when Lo Hak called her cute, causing her to jump in surprise and making a hole in the floor.

Following the Island Liberation War, her incredible skill and strength led to her becoming a lumberjack in the Kingdom of Obel.

name: Helmut
description: Helmut (ヘルムート, Herumūto) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV. Helmut is a captain with the Kooluk Empire's navy.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Helmut was the son of Colton and captained the Razril occupation forces during the Island Liberation War. As an Kooluk officer, he was considered highly competent, which is why he was entrusted with such a responsibility despite his young age.

As overseer of the Razril occupation, he had to order his men to disperse Razril protestors when they shot fire arrows at Snowe Vingerhut on his return from his failed anti-pirate endeavor.

Later during the Island Liberation War, when the Kooluk Mixed Fleet was defeated in battle, Helmut was also shown to be an officer who cared for his men when he agreed to surrender peacefully in exchange for the lives of his men. In exchange, he would not be executed himself but would join the Allied Forces for the remainder of the war. 

He would later be reunited with his father, who was taken prisoner after the liberation of Obel but chose not to reveal this information to his new allies. Helmut apologized to his father for what he saw as his betrayal but would be absolved by Colton who remarked that there was more to the world than Kooluk.

Once the war ended, he disappeared alongside his father and was not seen since.

trivia: • Helmut's weapon upgrades in a way similar as the swords of those from the Warriors' Village, but without a special name: Officer's Sword, Officer's Sword +, and Officer's Sword ++.

name: Hero
description: The hero (主人公, Shujinkou), known by several different names, is the protagonist of Suikoden IV. He is a washed-up orphan who rises to become the liberator of the Island Nations. He also appears in Suikoden Tactics as an obtainable character should the player upload Suikoden IV data.

name: Hervey
description: Hervey (ハーヴェイ, Hāvuei) is a major character in Suikoden IV who also appears in Suikoden Tactics. Hervey is a young pirate who works under Lady Kika.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Hervey was a pirate who worked closely alongside Kika. Like Sigurd, he had been washed away during a battle against the pirate Steele and was rescued by Kika. Since then, he has loyally worked under her to repay that life-long debt. Skilled at fire magic, he had dubbed himself "Hervey the Furious".

As a novice pirate, he had served under Dario but found the relationship never became more equitable. Even by the time of the Island Liberation War, Dario would still try and pull rank in order to have Hervey cook for Dario and even massage his feet.

Hervey could be hot-tempered and aggressive, making him quite the contrast to his former rival, Sigurd. Together, however, they formed a formidable team. When Kika agreed to join forces with the leader of the resistance against the Kooluk Empire, Hervey would join as well. Once the conflict ended, Hervey returned to the Nest of Pirates with the rest of Kika's crew and continued to work hard to assist their operations.

He also later joined Kyril on his mission to destroy the Rune Cannons together with Sigurd. The two of them were asked by Dario to train Nalleo and they would be impressed with how much the youth improved. He continued to serve loyally under Kika after the Rune Cannons were destroyed.

name: Igor
description: Igor (イーゴリ, Īgori) is a minor character in Suikoden IV. Igor was the manager of the hot springs on Mordo Island.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Igor managed the hot springs located on Mordo Island until the unfortunate man had his business hijacked by the Lo siblings. Following that, he was forced to make a living by challenging tourists to a coin toss game on the island.

Even after being invited to join the crew of the Stronghold Ship, Igor would continue to play his coin game on board. He would end up despondent after comparing his income to Gunter's, however.

Following the end of the Island Liberation War, he would continue to attempt to earn potch with his stingy coin games.

name: Izak
description: Izak (イザク, Izaku) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV. Izak is a veteran warrior, looking for a home to settle down in.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Izak was an aging warrior who had been looking for a place of tranquility. While he was a large warrior, he was also a very calm and gentle man with a love for nature. He visited Iluya Island while looking for a peaceful place to settle down in but arrived just before the Kooluk Empire's giant Rune Cannon destroyed the surface.

Losing the place he might have called home and remembering a young girl, Dana, he had met there who had loved flowers, and planting some seeds in her memory, he agreed to join the Allied Forces in order to defeat the Kooluk and revive Iluya.

Following the Island Liberation War, he devoted his life to the reconstruction of Iluya, helping it to become a place covered in flowers.

name: Jango
description: Jango (ジャンゴ, Jango) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV. Jango is a coarse pirate leader who wants to fight against the encroachment of the Kooluk Empire on his territories.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Jango was the boss of a pirate gang whose territory lay within the Island Nations. Although brutish and daring, he was also incredibly adept at ship handling and so was trusted by his peers as a leader and a captain.

Wanting to fight together with those opposing Graham Cray and the Kooluk Empire, he forced his way onto the Stronghold Ship alongside Brec and joined the crew. His pirate skills honed from years in the profession helped sink many enemy ships during the battles of the Island Liberation War.

Following the war, he and Brec would go on to chase down and capture the remnants of Cray Trading Company throughout the area.

name: Jeremy
description: Jeremy (ジェレミー, Jeremī) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. Jeremy is a swordsman looking for a rematch against Mitsuba in order to prove the superiority of his technique.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
thumb|left|220px|Only such a powerful technique could combine being a mediocre swordsman with having a Water Rune.Jeremy was a swordsman who had inherited the Flowing Strike Sword technique from his family which was known for its swordmanship. Even though he was taught to fight by his father, he claims that his mother was the strongest in the family. 

In the past, he had been taken in by Mitsuba's con and defeated and since then he had been searching for her in order to get a rematch. He wasn't the type of person to think about things too deeply or to get caught up on details, nor was he particularly good at writing. He was also something of a sore loser, refusing to admit that he had, in fact, lost to Mitsuba before.

He eventually found Mitsuba on Na-Nal Island, accompanying the leader of the Stronghold Ship. Mitsuba turned down his challenge so instead he decided to show off his sword technique by joining the crew of the vessel himself.

Following the Island Liberation War, Jeremy would become a swordsmanship instructor for the Kingdom of Obel's army. Later, he was also part of a group that was sent by Lino En Kuldes to help Kyril in his mission to investigate the Rune Cannons. 

He fought at the side of a lot of his old comrades, including Trishtan about whom he showed great concern, and Millay, fellow instructor at Obel. He also confronted Mitsuba and Reinhold accusing them of being up to their old tricks.

name: Jewel
description: Jewel (ジュエル, Jueru) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. Jewel is a Knight of Gaien trainee and close friend of the hero.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Jewel was originally from Na-Nal although she would come to Razril after a disagreement with the Na-Nal Island Chief and train to become a Knight of Gaien. She would develop a friendship with the hero during this time. She was a cheerful, attractive, and precise person whose patience allowed her to work past difficult times, often setting the mood among her comrades.

She had developed a crush on Snowe Vingerhut, having a weakness for attractive young men, and was troubled by his decisions throughout the Island Liberation War. She had always believed in the innocence of the hero, and she fought alongside him as part of the naval resistance against the Kooluk Empire following his banishment.

Following the war, she went back to Razril for a time but eventually returned home to Na-Nal Island. Later, she was asked by Lino En Kuldes to help Kyril in his mission to destroy the Rune Cannons.

Together with Paula and Selma she came to the Small Border Village to help him in his battle. She also met her old comrades from the Academy again and was particularly impressed with how Keneth has developed. She promised that she would come and pay Razril a visit after the Rune Cannons were destroyed.

trivia: • In the Suikoden IV novelization, Jewel is one of the two Knights of Gaien who joins the hero during his exile. The other is Tal.

name: Karl
description: Karl (カール, Kāru) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. Karl is a fiercely dedicated martial artist.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Karl was a passionate martial artist who devoted his life to training and strengthening his body. It was said that he never laughed which wasn't exactly true, he merely did not have time to laugh due to the intensity of his training. Despite his intensity, he was a kindhearted man with a goodly sense of justice.

He would meet the hero in the backstreets of Razril following the town's liberation from the Kooluk Empire and would subsequently join the Allied Forces. On board the Stronghold Ship, Karl would spend his time training on deck and sometimes worried he might be disturbing everyone else on deck. He was also prone to letting out war cries which would later earn him the ire of Rachel when the two journeyed together in Kooluk.

Throughout the Island Liberation War, he defeated enemy after enemy without much satisfaction and following the conflict he would leave on a journey to train and hone his body even further.

Karl would end up in Kooluk where his strong sense of justice would lead him to try and defend a group of children against a monster attack at the Collanbal Ruins alone. Luckily, he would be backed up by Kyril and, after rescuing the children, would accompany Kyril's group on their journey through the Empire.

name: Katarina
description: Katarina (カタリナ, Katarina) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. Katarina serves as the Vice-Commander of the Knights of Gaien.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Katarina served as the Vice-Commander of the Knights of Gaien under the command of Glen Cott. She was trained by Konrad in the use of magic. Commander Glen was, in truth, her father but this was something she never revealed, even to him. She was a strong, serious woman and an able officer but could also be said to lack flexibility. As such, she was taken in by Snowe's accusations and agreed to exile his friend for the murder of Commander Glen.

Following the Kooluk occupancy of Razril, Katarina would end up at the Nest of Pirates, wounded. Encountering her exiled charge once again, she finally believed in his innocence and once recovered, join the Stronghold Ship crew and would reunite with Konrad. Following the Island Liberation War, Katarina would become the Commander of the new Razril Marine Knights, successor to the old Gaien order.

name: Kate
description: Kate (ケイト, Keito) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. Kate is a taciturn ninja, looking for revenge after her contract was betrayed by her employer.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Kate was a ninja who had been hired by a leading figure in the Kooluk Empire. She was a native of the same village as Akaghi and Mizuki and the three of them form the last survivors of their homeland. Although usually cool, collected and very aloof, Kate could be slightly personable around her acquaintances. Otherwise, she could or would not engage in friendly conversation.

During the Island Liberation War, she was sent to Iluya Island just prior to its destruction by the giant Rune Cannon. Realizing that her employer had meant to eliminate her, she terminated her contract and joined the Allied Forces once she met Mizuki and the leader on the island.

Eager to hunt down her former employer, Kate would head north to the Kooluk Empire following the war.

During her stay in Kooluk, she found out that her former employer already died a long time ago. Kyril met her during a visit in Frontier Town Haruna where she was persecuted by Kooluk soldiers. After Mizuki had explained that she was an old acquaintance of hers, he decided to help her. After the battle, Kate decided to join forces with Kyril as they have a common enemy. 

She mostly socialized with Mizuki and Akaghi, saying that she was impressed how both of them are currently working as bodyguards for Lino En Kuldes. She constantly joked about their relationship (or lack thereof) and even proposed a marriage to make sure that the bloodline of their village would go on. Even though Mizuki invited her to join her and Akaghi at their job, Kate planned to start traveling on her own again after the battle against the Kooluk was over.

name: Keen
description: Keen (キーン, Kīn) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV. Keen was an assassin hired to take out Sigurd.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Keen was an assassin hired to kill Sigurd due to his desertion from the Middleport fleet. He was said to feel a joy in judging and punishing people. He was convinced to temporarily stay his hand by the hero and joined the force in order to keep an eye on his target.

He would construct a confessional on the Stronghold Ship during the Island Liberation War and judge members of the navy based on their confessions. He would eventually tire of judging people, however, and following the war would retire to Middleport without executing his contract on Sigurd.

name: Keneth
description: Keneth (ケネス, Kenesu) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. Keneth is a knight of Razril and friend of the hero.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Keneth was a Knight of Gaien trainee who graduated at the same time as Tal, Jewel, Paula, Snowe and his friend, whom he got along well with. He was an easy-going type of person who valued calm and collected decision making, earning him the trust of his friends that he returned in kind.

He held a strong belief in his friend's innocent after he was accused of the murder of Glen Cott, supporting him covertly as well as part of the Stronghold Ship crew. As befitting his personality, Keneth would rarely rush forward hastily and so readily deferred to his friend's command during the Island Liberation War.

Following the end of the war, Keneth would take up a leadership role as the Vice-Commander of the new Razril Marine Knights, serving under Katarina.

Two years later, a group of fake knights would spread throughout the Island Nations. These frauds would offer their help to travelers only to steal their valuables, sullying the good name of the Knights of Razril. Kyril accepted a request at the Quest Guild in Middleport from the Razril Marine Knights and they would come across and battle against the mercenaries at El-Eal Fortress. After the battle, Katarina and Keneth would join forces with Kyril, Lino En Kuldes having already requested of them to lend him their support once they had completed their current mission.

During this journey, Keneth was reunited with his old friends from the Marine Academy and invited them to a tour to show them how the Order has changed after they left the Knights. He also showed some concern on how naval warfare would change after the Rune Cannons will be dissembled but accepted that this is for the greater good. He was also very supportive of Katarina, assuring her that she was doing a good job as Commander of the Knights of Razril.

name: Kevin
description: Kevin (ケヴィン, Kevuin) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV who is mentioned in Suikoden Tactics. Kevin runs a café on Iluya Island along with his wife, Pam.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Kevin was a man who ran a café on Iluya Island alongside his wife, Pam, prior to the Island Liberation War. Usually a very serious man, Kevin also held an unusually strong passion for food, especially steamed buns, the cafe's specialty.

When the Kooluk Empire fired their Magnus Rune Cannon at Iluya, both Kevin and Pam were washed out to sea before eventually washing up on Nay Island. The two would spend their time convalescing in Nay and, wanting to help fight the Kooluk, Kevin would agree to join the Stronghold Ship upon his recovery. On board ship, he would run a restaurant, serving food to the ship's crew with pride.

Following the war, Kevin would return to Iluya alongside Pam and reopened their café as part of the reconstruction efforts there. Their business seems to flourish very well, and they are actively looking for rare and hard to get ingredients via the Quest Guild in Middleport to create new steamed buns.

name: Kika
description: Kika (キカ, Kika) is a major character in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. Kika is the respected and honorable leader of a pirate band operating in the Island Nations.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Kika was a famed pirate leader who operated out of her stronghold at the Nest of Pirates island. She captained the feared pirate vessel, The Grishend. Acknowledged as the premier pirate in the region by her contemporaries, she was known for only attacking ships belonging to greedy and unscrupulous merchants or nobles. As a leader, she inspired great loyalty, with Dario, Hervey and Sigurd serving her faithfully and looking after her welfare. 

Kika was once close with Brandeau and was the lover of Edgar, the Pirate King, but following his death and Brandeau's inheriting of the Rune of Punishment, that bond dissolved. She was also acquainted with Lino En Kuldes and so when the Kingdom of Obel fell to the Kooluk Empire, she was willing to join forces to fight against their common enemy. She considered Cray Trading Company to be a hostile force and so they and her band were enemies even prior to the Island Liberation War. 

With a similar fighting style to the leader of the Stronghold Ship, Kika fought in many battles of the war and was adept at boarding enemy vessels. Her work in introducing the leader to Elenor Silverberg would also play a huge part in the eventually success of the alliance in their struggles against the Kooluk.

Following the war, Kika would return to the Nest of Pirates, returning to her traditional business. It is said that she would meet her end on the high seas.

name: Konrad
description: Konrad (コンラッド, Konraddo) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV. Konrad is a retired magician who once tutored Katarina and was a friend of Commander Glen.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
220px||thumb|Konrad held some concern for Katarina's well-being.|left
Konrad, a respected magician, played a crucial role as a magic instructor within the Knights of Gaien prior to his retirement.

His expertise in magic was shared with numerous students, including Katarina, during his tenure as an instructor. As a friend of Glen Cott he knew of the commander's parental relationship with Katarina, but never spoke of it to her.

During the Island Liberation War, Konrad emerged from retirement to contribute his talents to the conflict against the Kooluk Empire, highlighting his dedication to causes he found worthy.

Despite his age, Konrad's commitment to his craft remained unwavering. He demonstrated a willingness to engage in combat when motivated, showcasing his enduring skill and determination. His proficiency rivaled that of Katarina, making him a potent force on the battlefield.

Following the war's conclusion, Konrad continued to be an active figure, assuming the role of magic instructor for the new knights stationed in Razril. His proficiency and commitment to nurturing magical prowess earned him admiration and recognition, solidifying his legacy.

name: Lilan
description: Lilan (リーラン, Rīran) is a minor character in Suikoden IV. Lilan is the youngest of the five mermaid sisters.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Lilan was the youngest of the five mermaid sisters who were separated and forced into hiding when Cray Trading Company began its mermaid-hunting activities in the Island Nations region. Following this, she attempted to find her sisters again, making her way across the Kuknasi Sea.

She would be caught up in Shiramine's nets aboard the Stronghold Ship when they were both making their way through the waters near Nay Island. Her accidental capture would prove to be a blessing in disguise as she was reunited with her sister Lilin, who was already a member of the ship's crew. Lilan would then join the ship's crew to remain close to her sisters and would work in the accessory shop on board.

Following the Island Liberation War, reunited with her four sisters, Lilan would return to the Deserted Island with her siblings.

name: Lilen
description: Lilen (リーレン, Rīren) is a minor character in Suikoden IV. Lilen is the second of the five mermaid sisters.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Lilen was the third of the five mermaid sisters who were separated and forced into hiding when Cray Trading Company began its mermaid-hunting activities in the Island Nations region. She held the strongest curious streak of all the sisters, which led her to be not so fearful of humans. This would leave to be slightly naive, however, and she would easily believe whatever she was told.

She would eventually cross paths with the leader of the Stronghold Ship when she returned to the Deserted Island and learning that some of her sisters were already aboard, agreed to join the ship's crew. Reunited with her sisters, she would work in the accessory shop on board.

Following the Island Liberation War, reunited with her four sisters, Lilen would return to the Deserted Island with her siblings.

name: Lilin
description: Lilin (リーリン, Rīrin) is a minor character in Suikoden IV. Lilin is the second youngest of the five mermaid sisters.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Lilin was the fourth of the five mermaid sisters who were separated and forced into hiding when Cray Trading Company began its mermaid-hunting activities in the Island Nations region. She was a rare breed among mermaids for her willingness to engage with humans despite her understandable fear of them.

Following this, she attempted to find her sisters again. She would first meet the future leader of the Stronghold Ship on the Deserted Island, helping him and his friends to get off the island. She would later encounter them again in the waters off of Obel, being hunted by Cray's underlings. After being rescued, she would join the crew of the ship, eventually opening an accessory shop on aboard the vessel.

Following the Island Liberation War, reunited with her four sisters, Lilin would return to the Deserted Island with her siblings for a time before setting out once again to find other mermaids who had gone missing in the wake of the mermaid hunts.

name: Lilon
description: Lilon (リーロン, Rīron) is a minor character in Suikoden IV. Lilon is the eldest of the five mermaid sisters.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Lilon was the eldest of the five mermaid sisters who were separated and forced into hiding when Cray Trading Company began its mermaid-hunting activities in the Island Nations region. She was the most knowledgeable of the five sisters and was the most eloquent writer.

She would eventually cross paths with the leader of the Stronghold Ship in the waters outside the Nest of Pirates and learning that some of her sisters were already aboard, agreed to join the ship's crew. Reunited with her sisters, she would work in the accessory shop on board.

Following the Island Liberation War, reunited with her four sisters, Lilon would return to the Deserted Island with her siblings.

name: Liloon
description: Liloon (リールン, Rīrun) is a minor character in Suikoden IV. Liloon is the third of the five mermaid sisters.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Liloon was the third of the five mermaid sisters who were separated and forced into hiding when Cray Trading Company began its mermaid-hunting activities in the Island Nations region. Because of this, she held a deep fear of humans as she explored the region, looking for her sisters.

She would eventually cross paths with the leader of the Stronghold Ship on the docks of the ruined Iluya Island and learning that some of her sisters were already aboard, agreed to join the ship's crew. Reunited with her sisters, she would work in the accessory shop on board.

Following the Island Liberation War, reunited with her four sisters, Liloon would return to the Deserted Island with her siblings before setting out to find other mermaids lost during the war.

name: Lino En Kuldes
description: Lino En Kuldes (リノ・エン・クルデス, Rino En Kurudesu) is a major character in Suikoden IV who also appears in Suikoden Tactics. Lino is the rough but open-minded ruler of the Kingdom of Obel.
originalName: リノ・エン・クルデス

facts.label: Family
facts.value: Flare (daughter), Hero (Suikoden IV) (son), Queen of Obel (wife)

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Lino En Kuldes was the King of the Kingdom of Obel. Years before the Island Liberation War, he had lost his son and wife to a pirate attack. Since then, he had diligently prepared Obel to defend against the Kooluk Empire, having noticed their increasing interest in the lands of the Island Nations.

As a king, he was known to be unfazed by almost anything and was respected as a fierce warrior, wielding his spear masterfully on the battlefield with courage and conviction. He was also known as a great king who cherished his people who, in return, cherished him back and gave him their trust and devotion. In spite of his appearance, he was also a skilled politician and would be one of the driving forces behind the creation of the Island Nations Federation following the war.

220px|thumb|Lino restored Kingdom of Obel|Obel and showed off this outfit in the same party. Efficient.|leftWhen the hero was brought to Obel, Lino saw the potential for greatness and had him begin the task of rounding up recruits for what would become the Stronghold Ship. Once the Kooluk Empire began their invasion of Obel, Lino would join the crew of the ship and leave the island, swearing to return to liberate its citizens. Following the evacuation from Obel, he would take Elenor Silverberg's advice and duel the hero for leadership of the vessel, in order to ensure a strict hierarchy. After losing the duel, he would gift the hero with the Golden Seal, the symbol of his position as King of Obel.

Once the Stronghold Ship had gathered enough allies, Obel would be liberated, and the people freed from their occupation. Lino would continue to fight for the remainder of the war, wielding his spear without fear all the way up to the final battle at El-Eal Fortress. Following the end of the war, he would…

name: Lo Fong
description: Lo Fong (ロウフォン, Roufon) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV. Lo Fong is an idle con artist and the middle child of the three Lo siblings.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Lo Fong was the middle sister of the three Lo siblings and the sister of Lo Hak and Lo Seng. Like her brothers, Lo Fong disliked work greatly and so collaborated with their plans to hijack Igor's hot springs on Mordo Island in order to make some potch. 

In addition to this, he also tried to work a fraud in the name of the leader of the Stronghold Ship to further increase their profits but would eventually be visited by the real deal. After he and his siblings were defeated by the real leader and realized who he was, Lo Fong was the first of the siblings to decide to join the crew.

Lo Fong's tastes in men tended to skew towards more mature men. She rated Izak, Konrad and Gunter as her favorites, in that order. She sometimes found the ship dull and wished to go shopping at the ports they visited.

Following the Island Liberation War, after falling in love with the Kingdom of Obel, she chose to split apart from her brothers and began a life there.

name: Lo Hak
description: Lo Hak (ロウハク, Rouhaku) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV. Lo Hak is a layabout con artist and the youngest of the three Lo siblings.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Lo Hak was the youngest of the three Lo siblings and the younger brother of Lo Fong and Lo Seng. Like his brother, Lo Hak was a lay about who hated work. He was an overconfident, but skilled, archer who possibly could have become a respectable archer if he had been willing to put the effort in.

With his siblings, he tried to hijack Igor's hot springs on Mordo Island in order to make some potch. In addition to this, he also tried to work a fraud in the name of the leader of the Stronghold Ship to further increase their profits but would eventually be visited by the real deal.

After he and his siblings were defeated by the real leader and realized who he was, all three offered to join the Allied Forces. As a member of the crew, Lo Hak became a fan of Pam's steamed buns. He also spent his time ranking the female members of the ship, ranking Katarina, Maxine and Helga especially highly.

Following the Island Liberation War, he would return to Mordo Island with Lo Seng and would work to make the island into a popular tourist spot, eventually buying a boat to nap in on his days off.

name: Lo Seng
description: Lo Seng (ロウセン, Rousen) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV. Lo Seng is a layabout con artist and the eldest of three Lo siblings.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Lo Seng was the eldest of the three Lo siblings and the elder brother of Lo Fong and Lo Hak. Like his siblings, he had a lazy temperament and so tried to hijack Igor's hot springs on Mordo Island in order to make some potch. In addition to this, he also tried to work a fraud in the name of the leader of the Stronghold Ship to further increase their profits but would eventually be visited by the real deal.

After he and his siblings were defeated by the real leader and realized who he was, all three offered to join his crew. As a member of the crew, Lo Seng took advantage of his position to net himself free wine and when that wasn't an option, thought little of breaking into Elenor Silverberg's room and stealing hers.

Following the Island Liberation War, he would return to Mordo Island with Lo Hak and would work to make the island into a popular tourist spot.

name: Louise
description: Louise (ルイーズ, Ruīzu) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV. Louise is a bartender and innkeeper with a certain air of mystery surrounding her.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Louise was the person who ran the saloon on the Stronghold Ship before it was even revealed as a vessel, making her one of its earliest crew members.

She was a caring person of good character who would warmly welcome people to her inn, but she also had something of a mysterious background. She could see the strength that laid in the eyes of the ship's leader.

She was an old acquaintance of Dario and had apparently entrusted him with the care of her son, Nalleo, soon after his birth. She also said that her husband, Nalleo's birth father, had already died.

She would be reunited with Nalleo during the Island Liberation War and, while glad to see her son growing into a fine man, chose not to reveal the truth of their relation. She would describe herself as a failure of a mother and ask for Nalleo to be told that his mother had died.

During the war, she would attract attention from both Ornan and Desmond. Desmond would develop a crush on her, but Ornan seemed to find a similarity between Louise and the woman he was searching for, although nothing ever came of it.

Once the war ended, Louise remained in the Kingdom of Obel for a short time but vanished unnoticed once Obel began to regain its old vigor.

name: Manu
description: Manu (マニュ, Manyu) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV. Manu is an inventor who created a variation on the Elevator.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Manu was an inventor who created a device known as an Elevator. He promoted this invention as a great way to navigate high places quickly, such as the cliffs which overlooked the docks on Obel.

Despite its advantages, Manu had a hard time generating interest in his creation, mainly due to his own slightly smarmy personality. He would volunteer to install the Elevator on the Stronghold Ship and so would join the ship's crew. On board, he harbored a slight resentment towards those who would rather take the stairs as well as envy towards how exciting Viki's teleportation seemed in comparison to his device.

Following the Island Liberation War, likely because his pitching on the Elevator continued to fail, he would isolate himself in his home, beginning work on another invention.

name: Mao
description: Mao (マオ, Mao) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV. Mao is a bizarre horticulturist with a passion for mushrooms.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Mao was a mysterious man who appeared behind a locked door on the Stronghold Ship fifth deck. The door itself was mysterious as Tov had no recollection of planning or building such a room but Mao offered no explanation for this, or his presence on board, acting as if he had always been on board. His potted mushroom plants would take up one half of the entire room.

Later, Nao would also appear and begin to cultivate mint on the other side of the room, much to each other's chagrin. He would very rarely leave the cultivation room, engrossed as he was in his passion. Following the Island Liberation War, he would run off and hide, muttering about hating bright places and seeking a darker place for his work.

name: Maxine
description: Maxine (マキシン, Makishin) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. Maxine is an assassin who is contracted to kill the hero.

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
Maxine is the epitome of a professional assassin, possessing a very high degree of professional pride. This is demonstrated by her refusal to give up even after being defeated, continually challenging Lazlo, and her steadfast refusal to reveal the identity of her employer while she was under contract. 

She is unsociable, cool, and openly states that she dislikes being in groups, preferring to operate alone. Her loyalty, once given via a contract or agreement, is absolute and only broken by betrayal on the part of her employer.

Beneath her cold exterior, however, lies a moral compass, as noted by Simeon. She is a powerful combatant, skilled in both physical assassination techniques and magical combat.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Assassination Attempts
Maxine was encountered by Lazlo and his party on Na-Nal Island in the town square and Nay Island as they prepared to depart via the Stonecutter’s Field. She launched a surprise attack aimed directly at Lazlo.

Her assault was swift and lethal, utilizing a crescent-tipped staff with expert precision. As depicted in the Suikoden IV novelization, though her agility initially gave her an edge, the combined vigilance of Lazlo, Bartholomew, and Izak forced her to break off the attack and retreat after realizing she was outflanked.

The party speculated she was either a Kooluk pursuer or, more likely, another agent of the Cray Trading Company, similar to previous assailants like Mizuki and Akaghi.
Joining the Allied Forces
220px|thumb|Following the liberation of Razril, Maxine revealed [[Ramada as her employer.|left]]
Maxine later appeared again in Razril, still intent on fulfilling her contract. However, she was intercepted by Ramada, another former Cray assassin who had defected to Lazlo's cause.

Ramada revealed to her the true nature of her mission: the target, the Rune of Punishment, could only be taken by it transfering to the one who killed its previous host. Ramada explained that their employer, Graham Cray, had deliberately withheld this information, intending to use the assassins as disposable vessels for the rune.

Shocked by this revelation and the true, cynical nature of her employer, Maxine agreed to a truce. Meeting Lazlo, she formally renounced her contract and requested to join his army to fight against the Cray Trading Company and the Kooluk Empire.
Island Liberation War and Departure
Maxine fought alongside the Allied Forces throughout the Island Liberation War, adhering strictly to the terms of the new contract she had forged.

She…

trivia:
• Maxine is one of the few characters who does not participate in Keen's confessional booth in Suikoden IV.
• In the Suikoden IV novelization Maxine only attacks the party once. In the game, she must be fought twice to recruit her.
• Her reasons for joining Kyril's party in Suikoden Tactics remain unknown due to the secret contents of Ramada's letter. Though given their connection, it may have been a simple personal request.
