name: Micky
description: Micky (ミッキー, Mikkī) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV. Micky is the dedicated, kowtowing servant of Schtolteheim Reinbach III.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Micky was a servant of Schtolteheim Reinbach III in Middleport as part of the Reinbach house's staff. He had also been entrusted the care of Reinbach II's pet Little Daisy. After Little Daisy was defeated by the Stronghold Ship and Reinbach III renounced his father's deeds, Micky found himself out of work and so would join the crew of the Stronghold Ship along with his master. 

On board, he would become jealous of Charlemagne for his budding friendship with Reinbach and also attracted criticism from both Etienne and Reinbach himself for his supposed neglect of his master in the throes of seasickness. In reality, he was very dedicated to Reinbach, even going so far as to pen a novel, The Rose Swordsman, which would be published in Perrault's newspaper. 

He would continue to serve Reinbach faithfully following the Island Liberation War while his novel would go on to become a classic tale of heroism.

name: Millay
description: Millay (ミレイ, Mirei) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. Millay is a talented swordswoman on the run from the goons of Schtolteheim Reinbach II.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Millay was a swordswoman of considerable dedication and zeal whose physical beauty caused her to be sought by Schtolteheim Reinbach II as a potential wife for his son. Polite but straight-forward in terms of personality, she refused the proposal with the reasonable excuse of not wanting to marry a stranger, only to be hounded by Reinbach II's lackeys.

She would eventually be chased to Na-Nal Island, where these goons would be run off by Reinbach III, who had no knowledge of his father's marriage schemes, and the leader of the Stronghold Ship. In return, she would ask to join his crew and for the remainder of the Island Liberation War, would serve as one of the hero's bodyguards, along with Helga and Gretchen. It seems that during the war she would develop a strong bond with the leader and also reconsidered her views on the younger Reinbach, who she had previously assumed was part of his father's plans.

Following the war, her skills as a soldier led her to being appointed as a General in the Kingdom of Obel. As a general, she was very dutiful and serious, finding herself unable to refer to Princess Flare without her honorific, even when requested to do so. After Kyril decided to head to the Kooluk Empire, Lino En Kuldes ordered Millay, among others, to accompany him.

name: Mitsuba
description: Mitsuba (ミツバ, Mitsuba) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. Mitsuba is a duelist who uses Reinhold to set up duels for potch wagers under fraudulent circumstances.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Mitsuba was a cheerful young woman with a sunny disposition who earned a living by engaging in pseudo-fraudulent duels. These would be set by her accomplice, Reinhold, enticing passers-by to avenge some fabricated beating on him by challenging Mitsuba to a duel. She would then accept the duel as a high potch wager and would, normally, win handily, being a strong and talented swordswoman. 

Reinhold participated under the false assumption that Mitsuba had uncovered some secret of his to blackmail him with. Mitsuba just happened to pass him by as he was reading a letter and Reinhold erroneously thought she had seen the sensitive information in the letter, forcing him into a life of servitude.

Jeremy was one of her past victims and he harbored a grudge against her because of this. She, on the other hand, just dismissed him as a weakling. Axel had also been a victim in the past, further proving both her skill and unscrupulous ways.

During the Island Liberation War, Mitsuba set up her operation on Na-Nal Island only to be unexpectedly defeated by the leader of the Stronghold Ship. She agreed to join the crew after her defeat, frustrated with Reinhold for sending such powerful marks against her.

She suspended her operation for the remainder of the war, but once it had finished, she continued to make a living using Reinhold to set up her duels.

Eventually the two would make their way to the Kooluk Empire where Reinhold would send Kyril to duel Mitsuba on the Terana Plains. She lost to Kyril but readily agreed to join his group, thinking that the journey could be profitable for her.

name: Mizuki
description: Mizuki (ミズキ, Mizuki) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. Mizuki is a ninja who fights alongside Akaghi and Ramada.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Mizuki served under Ramada in his role as leader of Orark Maritime Trade. She came from the same community as Akaghi and became Ramada's companion, along with Akaghi, after Ramada saved their lives in the past. She held a very stoic personality, which could be calming and reassuring in stressful situations. But she was also able to put on a bright, cheerful smile in order to deceive any enemies.

She attacked the bearer of the Rune of Punishment, along with Akaghi and Ramada, under Graham Cray's orders but was defeated. She would then join the crew of the Stronghold Ship, following the defection of Ramada, in contrast in Akaghi who required more cajoling. She would be reunited with Kate, another member of their ninja community, who would tease her about her relationship, or lack thereof, with Akaghi.

Mizuki would serve faithfully for the remainder of the Island Liberation War and, along with Akaghi, would become a bodyguard of Lino En Kuldes in Obel.

trivia:
• Befitting her silent nature, Mizuki will not send the player any letters in Suikoden IV.
• Mizuki's weapons, Damnacanthus (アリドウシ Aridoushi), Citrus Tachibana (タチバナ Tachibana), and Ardisia Crispa (カラタチバナ Karatachibana), are all names of plants that are native to East Asia and Japan. Mizuki's name herself is the Japanese name of the genus of Cornus, a genus of plants which is especially abundant in Japan, or the Cornus controversa in particular, a plant of said genus that's mostly found in Japan.
• Mizuki's eye color changes from green in Suikoden IV to blue in Suikoden Tactics.

name: Na-Nal Island Chief
description: The Na-Nal Island Chief (ナ・ナル島長, Na Naru-jima Chō) is a minor character in Suikoden IV. He is the leader of the Na-Nal Island human community.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Befitting the Na-Nal reputation for strength, the Chief was the strongest on the island. He was well acquainted with Lino En Kuldes as both were rulers of their respective islands.

During the Island Liberation War, the Chief was able to build friendly relations with the Kooluk Empire through Troy and held fast to a policy of not interfering in the war. When he attempted to gift the Kooluk soldiers stationed on Na-Nal the Elven Remedy, however, things fells apart. The remedy had been switched by the elves of the Elven Settlement with poison. The resulting poisoning of several Kooluk soldiers led to a violent retaliation which was stopped by the crew of the Stronghold Ship.

Following this massacre, the Chief regretted his relationship with the Kooluk and agreed to cooperate instead with the resisting forces. He would have his son, Axel, join their crew as a show of this new-found cooperative spirit. Following the war, he would ceede leadership of the island to his son.

name: Nabokov
description: Nabokov (ナボコフ, Nabokofu) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV. Nabokov is a dedicated appraiser looking to improve his skills.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Nabokov worked as an appraiser in the Kingdom of Obel prior to its occupation by the Kooluk Empire. Although he was a serious, hard-working man, he was not yet an expert at appraisal.

His talents would expand when he joined the crew of the Stronghold Ship in order to help appraise any items salvaged by the vessel. He would open an appraisal shop on board the ship, coming across a great variety of items and antiques from across the Island Nations. His view of appraisal was that it should focus on determining the physical nature of items, not their value. This methodology frustrated Lilin, who brought him a seashell in the expectation of having a value assigned to it.

Once the Island Liberation War ended, Nabokov left on a journey in order to spread the art of appraisal.

name: Nalkul
description: Nalkul (ナルクル, Narukuru) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. Nalkul is a rough Nay-Kobold hoping to make a big splash in the world.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Nalkul is a young Nay-Kobold who is the close friend of Champo. Although he and Champo often engaged in petty theft and other roguish activities, Nalkul is, at heart, a friendly person, taking care of Champo even while berating him. Like most Nay-Kobolds, he did not like baths and avoided them when he could.

Like Champo, Nalkul looked up to Chiepoo for his leaving the village and traveling the world, although even Chiepoo was surprised by this hero-worship. Nalkul would steal the Golden Seal from the leader of the Stronghold Ship but when he realized that the Nay-Kobold accompanying him was Chiepoo himself, he offered to return the Golden Seal immediately and joined the Allied Forces.

After the Island Liberation War ended, Nalkul and Champo would begin a business on Nay Island as apprentices of Chiepoo.

name: Nalleo
description: Nalleo (ナレオ, Nareo) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. Nalleo is an apprentice pirate who grows into a skilled young warrior under the tutelage of his father, Dario.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Nalleo was a young boy training under his father, Dario, to become a pirate. He respected Dario greatly and dreamed of becoming a great pirate in his image. In truth, Dario is Nalleo's adopted father. His birth mother was Louise who gave him up for reasons unknown. Nalleo is unaware of this and often dreamed about finding his mother someday, if she was still alive.

When Dario joined the crew of the Stronghold Ship, Nalleo followed along, supporting his father and keeping him from getting into too much trouble. Although he could fight in naval battles due to his sailing experience, he was still only a trainee when it came to weapons and magic, meaning he could not help out in pitched battles, much to his frustration.

Following the Island Liberation War, Nalleo continued to train hard, growing into a great pirate, recorded in legends.

name: Nao
description: Nao (ナオ, Nao) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV. Nao is a puzzling horticulturist with a passion for mint.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Nao was a mysterious man who appeared behind a locked door on the Stronghold Ship fifth deck. The door itself was mysterious as Tov had no recollection of planning or building such a room but Nao offered no explanation for this, or his presence on board, beyond a desire to cultivate mint. His mint collection would take up one half of the entire room.

Mao's cultivation of mushrooms on the other side of the room would distress and annoy Nao greatly. He would very rarely leave the cultivation room, engrossed as he was in his passion. Following the Island Liberation War, he would run off and hide, muttering about hating dry places and seeking a more humid place for his work.

name: Nataly
description: Nataly (ナタリー, Natarī) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV. Nataly is a window maker caught up in the attack on Iluya Island.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Nataly was a window maker who had a reputation for diligent work and faultless politeness. She was working on Iluya Island when it was attack by the Kooluk Empire's Magnus Rune Cannon and was one of the few survivors of the attack.

She met the leader of the Stronghold Ship when he came to Iluya and offered her services, joining his crew. On board, the grateful Nataly would work hard to install new windows on the Stronghold Ship and following the Island Liberation War would return to Iluya to help aid in the reconstruction. Her own home there would have the most brilliant of window frames.

name: Nico
description: Nico (ニコ, Niko) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV. Nico is the Stronghold Ship exceptional lookout.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Nico was a young man who served as the lookout aboard the Stronghold Ship. He was well trusted by his fellow sailors due to his serious diligence when it came to his work coupled with his polite demeanor. As a lookout, he had exceptional talent, his amazing eyesight gaining him the adulation, and jealousy, of Wendel in particular.

He was one of the original crew members of the Stronghold Ship and served both Lino En Kuldes and its leader faithfully throughout the Island Liberation War. His uncanny talents allowed him to spot enemy ships quickly, making him a vital asset during the war.

On board ship, Nico's diligent nature made him doubt Haruto's commitment to his job although he did have high standards for everyone on board. He also had trouble shaking off Wendel, even after stating he was not looking for an apprentice.

Once the war ended, he continued to serve as a lookout on the Kingdom of Obel patrol ships.

name: Noah
description: Noah (ノア, Noa) is a character in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. Noah is a grifter who impersonates a Nay-Kobold for her own amusement.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Noah was a mischievous girl, petty thief and gambler who pretended to be a Nay-Kobold, delighting in the reactions she would garner from people with her tricks. Her ears, paws and tail are all fake and custom-made for herself. Although she could be sweet, she seemed to suffer from an almost compulsive urge to grift people, similar to Nalkul.

She had been a hungry child, not having eaten for three days when she was discovered by the leader of the Stronghold Ship in the Kingdom of Obel. Noah was then invited on board the ship where she could eat heartily every day. She would also become close friends with Rita and Rene during her time on board.

Following the Island Liberation War, Noah would give up her life of petty crime and would enter into business with Champo and Nalkul.

name: Oleg
description: Oleg (オレーグ, Orēgu) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV. Oleg is a skilled inventor whose work usually goes over the heads of most people.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Oleg was an inventor who created a box able to capture an image of whatever it was pointed at. He was also the first person to create and use a video camera in the Island Nations. As an inventor, he was peerless but the inability of others to comprehend his work would often have him regarded simply as an eccentric.

During the Island Liberation War, Lino En Kuldes wished for an audience with Oleg. The same night he was met at Nay Island, his camera would be coincidentally pointed at Iluya Island during its destruction by the giant Rune Cannon, capturing the moment. Bringing his footage to Obel, Oleg wished to sell his invention and Lino promised him eventual compensation in exchange for his continued service during the war.

Following the war, Oleg would continue to attempt to sell his inventions but found few buyers as no-one would take him seriously.

trivia: • The character design of Oleg is a small tribute to a character of the same name from another Kawano Junko-produced game; Shadow of Memories (Shadow of Destiny in North America). That Oleg is a movie producer while Suikoden IV's Oleg invents the video camera.

name: Ornan
description: Ornan (オルナン, Orunan) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV. Ornan is a traveler, searching for a past love who left him.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Ornan was a man caught in the shadow of an ex-lover he met in the past. He fought using throwing daggers to take down his enemies and was held in high regard as a warrior. However, he bore the Waking Rune which had him sleep in battle, making him somewhat unreliable when combined with his mild personality.

As he was searching for a past love, he decided to join the Stronghold Ship crew as he reasoned that a group gathering many people may offer up some potential leads in his search.

Apparently, he did not meet the person he was searching for during the Island Liberation War, although he felt Louise resembled her, and following the conflict, he would continue his search.

name: Oskar
description: Oskar (オスカル, Osukaru) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV. Oskar is the close friend of Deborah who has a mysterious past.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
220px|thumb|Oskar was impacted deeply by the loss of Deborah|his tea-drinking friend.|left
Oskar was an androgynous-looking fellow whose daily routine consisted of a cup of tea and a chat with his friend Deborah at the inn in Middleport.

It seems that in the past Oskar was involved in illegal activities, but details are unknown due to his reluctance to speak about his past. In fact, Oskar was not his original name. At that time, he was helped out by Deborah and the two of them soon forged a close friendship. Once Deborah joined the Allied Forces, Oskar found himself extremely lonely and would force himself onto the Stronghold Ship at the earliest opportunity, reuniting with his friend.

Following the Island Liberation War, Oskar would return to Middleport, resuming his daily routine with Deborah at the inn.

name: Pablo
description: Pablo (パブロ, Paburo) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. Pablo is a man passionate about Rune Cannon development and research.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Pablo was responsible for the Rune Cannon control room on board the Stronghold Ship and was the ship's resident expert on the weapons. He had poured his heart and soul into the study of Rune Cannons and especially looked up to Warlock, the original creator of the weapons.

He was usually quite blunt and unfriendly to people, but his personality would completely shift when the conversation would turn to Rune Cannons.

Once the Stronghold Ship left the Kingdom of Obel, Pablo was in charge of maintaining the Rune Cannons installed on the ship. Once Warlock also joined the crew of the ship, he would attempt to study under him and learn as much as he could, despite Warlock's extreme reluctance to divulge any information on the weapons he regretted creating.

Following the Island Liberation War, with the sudden death of Warlock, Pablo returned to studying Rune Cannons alone, even as they began to vanish from the world.

name: Pam
description: Pam (パム, Pamu) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV. Pam runs a cafe on Iluya Island along with her husband, Kevin.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Pam ran a cafe on Iluya Island alongside her husband, Kevin, prior to the Island Liberation War. Compared to her husband, she was very quiet and willing to stay in the shadows and support her husband.

When the Kooluk Empire fired their Magnus Rune Cannon at Iluya, both Kevin and Pam were washed out to sea before eventually washing up on Nay Island. The two would spend their time convalescing in Nay and, wanting to help fight the Kooluk, Pam would agree to join the Stronghold Ship upon her recovery.

On board ship, she would run a restaurant, serving food to the ship's crew with pride. She would spend her time conceiving of new types of buns to make and even carried dough and a steaming basket so as to make buns on the go.

Following the war, Pam would return to Iluya alongside Kevin and reopened their cafe as part of the reconstruction efforts there. Their business seemed to flourish very well, and they actively looked for rare and hard to get ingredients via the Quest Guild in Middleport to create new steamed buns.

name: Paula
description: Paula (ポーラ, Pōra) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. Paula is a taciturn elf as well as a close friend of the the protagonist.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Paula was an elf who served as a trainee, and later a graduate, in the Knights of Gaien. She once lived in the Elven Settlement on Na-Nal Island with her mother, but was exiled along with her mother, Romina.

Although a very quiet person, she is a friendly person and, as a rarity, does not hold a racial bias from her elven heritage although she was troubled that despite having lived so many years in human society, she didn't know how to help reconcile the two races.

Even when her friend was exiled from Razril on the charge of killing Glen Cott, she would continue to believe in his innocence and would fight alongside him against the Kooluk Empire in the Island Liberation War. On board the Stronghold Ship, she would serve faithfully but would worry that she needed to train harder in order to avoid becoming a liability.

Her achievements were recognized after the war ended, with Paula being granted permission to return home to the Elven Settlement on Na-Nal Island. She remained there for a time before returning to her position in the Knights of Razril. 

She was later sent along with Jewel and Selma to assist Kyril's group on their infiltration of the Kooluk Empire, arriving during a battle at the Small Border Village. By this time, Paula had also become a skilled rider of Giant Owls, which proved to be of great help to Kyril's group on their mission.

name: Peck
description: Peck (ペック, Pekku), known as Assassin (暗器使い, Kuraki Tsukai) in Suikoden IV, is a supporting character from Suikoden Tactics and an antagonist in Suikoden IV. Peck is a young pirate devoted to Brandeau.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Peck was a young pirate of the Nest of Pirates who adored Brandeau deeply. He respected Brandeau enough that he would put himself in harm's way to impress the elder pirate without concern for his own safety.

This loyalty would cost Peck dearly when he would fight alongside Brandeau during their battle with Steele. He would be caught in the explosion of Steele's Evil Eye, transformed into something half-human and half-fishman. Dismayed by his disfigurement, he would volunteer to accompany Brandeau in his self-imposed exile, to keep him from being lonely.

As part of Brandeau's pirate band, he would serve as an assassin, using his knife throwing skills to eliminate targets. He would be killed by Brandeau when he used the Rune of Punishment following his own defeat in battle. Caught in the range of the rune, Peck would be disintegrated, seemingly without a care from the man he worshipped.

name: Pecola
description: Pecola (ペコラ, Pekora) is a minor character in Suikoden IV. Pecola is an interior decorator always willing to showcase and improve her talents.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Pecola was a professional interior decorator whose book on the subject had sparked interest in the hobby in Middleport. In Middleport, she had become something of a celebrity, being surrounded by admirers of her work.

An obliging person, she accepted an offer to bring her skills to the Stronghold Ship, taking a room on the vessel's third deck and using it to showcase her talents. She was also impressed at how tidy the rooms on board tended to be kept.

Following the Island Liberation War, Pecola journeyed south in order to study the latest trends in interior design.

name: Perrault
description: Perrault (ぺロー, Perō) is a minor character in Suikoden IV. Perrault is a journalist as well as an aspiring author, looking to reach the masses with his work.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Perrault was a journalist who wished to entertain and help the masses with his work. Although he could be slightly nervous, he was also a bright young man with a flourishing sense of curiosity.

He was staying at the Nay-Kobold Settlement on Nay Island when he met the leader of the Stronghold Ship and decided to produce a newspaper on board the ship. This newspaper would cover a variety of topics from news to light entertainment and even fiction. However, his serialized novel, Madame Depression, proved to be quite unpopular leaving him very depressed himself.

Following the Island Liberation War, he would move to the Kingdom of Obel in order to write articles on the Island Nations.

name: Phil
description: Phil (フィル, Firu) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV. Phil was a skilled tailor who feels responsible for making good use of the materials he is given.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Phil was a tailor who made clothes using a variety of materials, both standard and unusual. His prowess was considerable, with some of his tailored clothing even possessing powerful magical-like effects on its wearer. Because the materials needed for his work were difficult to gather, he had become extremely work-focused in order to create garments worthy of the materials he had gathered.

Finding himself wanting to work in a place where a large variety of people would gather, he eagerly accepted the hero's offer to join the crew of the Stronghold Ship. Throughout the Island Liberation War, he would create dozens of new outfits on board the ship.

On the ship, he shared a room with Tov, who unnerved him slightly due to his silence. He also offended Lilin when he requested a scale from her, not realizing what she had been through in her past. He wished for a chance to reconcile with her.

Following the war, with his technique now more refined than ever, it was said that no greater tailor existed than Phil.

name: Queen of Obel
description: The Queen of Obel (オベル王妃, Oberu Ōhi) is a minor character in Suikoden IV. She passed away some 15 years prior to the events of the game.

facts.label: Family
facts.value: Flare (daughter), Hero (Suikoden IV) (son), Lino En Kuldes (husband)

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
The Queen of Obel was the wife of Lino En Kuldes and the mother of Flare and her son. She lived a normal life with her family in the Kingdom of Obel.

Some 15 years prior to the Island Liberation War, Obel was attacked by pirates. On board ship with her family, the Queen would summon the Rune of Punishment from within the Ruins of Obel and use it to repel the attack on their ship. Although her son would be thrown overboard due to the pirate attack, Flare would be saved. The Queen then passed away due to the rune's curse.

name: Rachel
description: Rachel (レイチェル, Reicheru) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. Rachel is a debt collector hunting down the frequent bill-skipper, Cedric.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
220px|left|thumb|Considering I paid 10,000 for Fu Su Lu, this is a steal.
Rachel was a spear-wielding warrior who worked as a debt collector. Although she could be stubborn, she had a good, bright personality. She was committed to her work and was chasing down Cedric, who ran run out on several bills during the Island Liberation War. She was slightly nosy so when Cedric was caught on board the Stronghold Ship, she would offer to join as well in order to keep tabs on him.

Some theorized that Rachel had a crush on Cedric and even Rachel admitted that she found it hard to resist teasing him even when he was not doing anything criminal. She was also critical of Eugene but only because she believed he had good potential as a spear user like herself.

Following the Island Liberation War, she began to chase down Cedric again as he renewed his habit of bilking bills across the land.

name: Rakgi
description: Rakgi (ラクジー, Rakujī) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV. Rakgi is a kind-hearted boy traveling with his mother after his father was lost at sea.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
thumb|left|220px|Rakgi with a hot take on blockchains or the Rune of Punishment.Rakgi was a young boy who helped his mother, Rikie, guard the mound where the Rune of Punishment once stood in the Ruins of Obel while harboring hope that his father, a previous bearer of the rune, would return.

He and Rikie had traveled far in search of news of his father and after arriving in Obel, Rakgi would put his knowledge to good use by becoming a tour guide for hire in the ruins there.

When the new bearer of the Rune of Punishment arrived in Obel, Rakgi accompanied his mother when she decided to join him in order to be close to the rune.

On board the Stronghold Ship, Rakgi worked incredibly hard, impressing Basil greatly. Rakgi would also look up to both Dario and Nalleo, admiring the latter's goal of becoming a pirate.

Following the Island Liberation War, Rakgi would follow in his father's footsteps, becoming a great fisherman.

name: Rakgi's father
description: Rakgi's father (ラクジーの父, Rakujī no chichi) is a minor character in Suikoden IV. He is the unnamed father of Rakgi who perished due to the Rune of Punishment some years before the beginning of the game.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
The husband of Rikie and the father of Rakgi, Rakgi's father ended up inheriting the Rune of Punishment under unknown circumstances while out on a fishing trip.

Burdened by the True Rune and unwilling to put his family in danger by remaining in proximity to them, he would leave set off on a journey alone to protect them.

The curse of the rune would eventually overtake him and he would die in a distant land. Years later, Rakgi's father would appear as a memory of the past to the newest bearer of the rune.

name: Ramada
description: Ramada (ラマダ, Ramada) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV. Ramada is a trader, spy and assassin who leads the umbrella company Orark Maritime Trade.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
220px|thumb|Ramada first appeared to work loyally for Graham Cray and his interests.|left
Ramada was a spy who worked for Cray Trading Company, as leader of the umbrella company Orark Maritime Trade. The ninja Akaghi and Mizuki served as his subordinates. In the past, Ramada had prevented the two from being sold as slaves, and the two had pledged their loyalty to him ever since.

Working under Graham Cray, his first role in the Island Liberation War was to smuggle Magnus Rune Cannon shells from Middleport to El-Eal Fortress. Despite being blown off course to Razril and attacked by the pirate Brandeau, this task was successful, and the shells would later be used to attack Iluya Island. 

Following this success, he would hire a fleet of pirates to attack Razril in order to test the Rune of Punishment. Ramada was then ordered, along with Akaghi and Mizuki, to recover the Rune of Punishment from its current bearer. 

He would hire the assassin Maxine to complete this objective but would also take a direct hand in matters, ambushing the rune bearer in the Kingdom of Obel. After failing in his attempt to assassinate the rune bearer, he would surrender immediately, knowing that Cray would have him and his companions killed for his failure.

Learning of the true nature of the Rune of Punishment, and how if he had been successful, the rune would have moved to one of them in turn, would completely remove any notion of loyalty to Ramada's former employer. Requesting to join the crew of the Stronghold Ship, he was accepted on the condition that his companion's lives were contingent on his behavior.

220px|thumb|Unable to convince Graham Cray|Cray that his change of sides was a feint, Cray would poison Ramada.|right
He would maintain his loyalty throughout the war, engaging in…

name: Reinhold
description: Reinhold (ラインホルト, Rainhoruto) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. Reinhold is a talented warrior and teacher who finds himself under the commander of the con-artist Mitsuba.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Reinhold was a spear-wielder who helped Mitsuba in her dueling scams by playing a victim of hers who would beseech passers-by to extract revenge on his behalf. Contrary to his somewhat scary appearance, he held a dutiful, polite personality.

He was under the impression that Mitsuba had read a letter of his, exposing a secret and so was unable to go against her plans but, in actuality, Mitsuba did not have knowledge to blackmail Reinhold with, she just happened to pass by at the same time Reinhold was reading said letter.

During the Island Liberation War, the two operated on Na-Nal Island where Reinhold managed to send the hero to duel her, only for Mitsuba to lose. As a result, she joined the crew of the Stronghold Ship, bringing Reinhold along for the ride. There, he opened a training hall, drilling those who visited him throughout the war. In addition to aiding Mitsuba's cons, he was under her thumb in every way, even pinching various passengers on the ship under her orders.

Following the war, he continued participating in Mitsuba's scams, ever fearful of the secret he imagined she held over his head. He eventually ended up in Merseto, sending his latest victims to the Terana Plains to duel Mitsuba. This plan would backfire once again when Kyril managed to defeat Mitsuba and so Reinhold found himself dragged into traveling with Mitsuba as she became a colleague of Kyril.

name: Rene
description: Rene (ルネ, Rune) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. Rene is a treasure hunter whose passion lied solely in the investigation part of the job.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Rene was a treasurer hunter who loved the thrill of scavenger hunts and searching for buried treasures. Although she loved to find treasure, however, she had no interest in keeping the spoils for herself. Able to quickly identify buried items and an expert at dowsing, she awaited someone to bring her new treasure maps. To that effect, she sent a message in a bottle, which washed up on Nay Island.

When the leader of the Stronghold Ship met Rene on Donut Island with such a map, she would help him uncover the treasure there before agreeing to join the crew of his ship. Over the course of the Island Liberation War, she would help him uncover treasures on many different islands throughout the Island Nations region.

On board ship, she would spend her time decoding maps, rebuffing Louise's attempts to have her enjoy herself in the saloon. She would, however, become friends with Rita and Noah, helping them create an amulet for their leader prior to the final battle at El-Eal Fortress.

After the war, mumbling about subterranean water channels, she left on a journey with her divining rods in her hands.

name: Rikie
description: Rikie (リキエ, Rikie) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV. Rikie is the solemn but compassionate mother of Rakgi.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Rikie was the mother of Rakgi, who lost her husband after he became the bearer of the Rune of Punishment. Over time, she and Rakgi searched for any trail of her husband and eventually found their way to the Kingdom of Obel where she learned of the history of the Rune of Punishment and came to accept her husband's death.

A strong woman, she raised Rakgi by herself from a young age and together the two lived in the Ruins of Obel. When the hero comes to Obel, Rikie discovers he has become the latest bearer of the Rune of Punishment and, wanting to be close to the rune her husband once bore, agreed to leave the island and join him.

Following the Island Liberation War, Rikie would return to her home on Na-Nal Island, leading a quiet life with her son.

name: Rita
description: Rita (リタ, Rita) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. She is an ambitious and excitable young woman and the creator of the games, Ritapon! and Ritaland!.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Rita was a young enthusiast of games who developed her very own creation called Ritapon!, a game designed for competitive play with others. Eager to showcase her invention, Rita encountered the hero during a chance meeting on Na-Nal Island. She promptly challenged the hero to a match, but was defeated. As a consequence, she agreed to join the hero's journey by boarding the Stronghold Ship as a form of payment.

Despite being the creator of Ritapon!, Rita was not particularly skilled at playing the game. Her misfortunes extended beyond her defeat against the hero, as she often found herself on the losing end when facing Noah in matches, leading to great frustration. Moreover, in one instance, Rita resorted to cheating during a game against Rakgi in order to evade defeat, though she later came to regret her actions.

Always enthusiastic about challenging the hero to games, Rita formed strong bonds with both Noah and Rene, sharing their admiration for their commander. Before the final assault on El-Eal Fortress, Rita and her friends Noah and Rene presented the hero with an amulet they crafted together.

Following the conclusion of the Island Liberation War, Rita set her sights on creating a new game, driven by the desire to earn more potch. Seeking test players for her yet-to-be-named game, she sought the assistance of the Quest Guild. Kyril, a member of the guild, agreed to playtest it with her in Razril, resulting in Rita's defeat. In turn, she pledged to aid Kyril in his journey.

Throughout her adventure, Rita reunited with old acquaintances, such as Millay, Noah, and Rene, while also forming a new friendship with Corselia, the Princess of the Kooluk Empire. Ever eager to promote her new game to her friends, Rita often emerged victorious in their matches. Her passion…

name: Romina
description: Romina (ロミナ, Romina) is a character who is briefly mentioned in Suikoden IV. She was an elf and mother of Paula.

sections.title: Story
sections.body: Romina was the mother of Paula and together they lived in the Elven community on Na-Nal Island. For unknown reasons, she collaborated with the human population of the island and was subsequently banished from the island by the Elven Elder, together with her daughter. She died many years before the beginning of the Island Liberation War.

trivia:
• Romina's name is only mentioned during a brief event when the player visits the Elven Elder with Paula in the party prior to the liberation of Na-Nal Island. 

de:

name: Schtolteheim Reinbach II
description: Schtolteheim Reinbach II (シュトルテハイム・ラインバッハ2世, Shutorutehaimu Rainbahha 2-sei) is a minor character in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. Reinbach II is the lord of Middleport and father of Schtolteheim Reinbach III.
originalName: シュトルテハイム・ラインバッハ2世

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Schtolteheim Reinbach II was the nobleman who governed Middleport as the second-generation head of the Reinbach family. When Middleport was forced to declare independence from the Dukedom of Gaien following occupation by the Kooluk Empire, Reinbach II was able to develop the town greatly, gaining him the trust of the people under his rule.

Although he appeared to be a worthy, if haughty, lord on the surface, much of his fortune and Middleport's prosperity stemmed from his secretive trading of Rune Cannon material and ammunition to any and all interested parties. Very few people knew of Reinbach II's darker practices and he would ensure this by having his private fleet attack and kill anyone who knew too much or asked too many questions.

In the past, Sigurd worked under him and he had sent Sigurd to silence Walter and his friends after they started inquiring about rune cannons in Middleport. This attack was thwarted by a raid from the pirate Steele, however.

It also appears the assassin Keen was hired by Reinbach II to silence Sigurd in turn once he abandoned his job. As his wealth and position came from trading with all parties, during the Island Liberation War he pursued a policy of neutrality and Middleport would join the new Island Nations Federation following the war's conclusion.

His son, Schtolteheim Reinbach III, would condemn his father's practices during the war and would succeed him following the conflict.

name: Schtolteheim Reinbach III
description: Schtolteheim Reinbach III (シュトルテハイム・ラインバッハ3世, Shutorutehaimu Rainbahha San-sei), also known simply as Reinbach, is a supporting character in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. Reinbach is the aristocratic son of the lord of Middleport, Schtolteheim Reinbach II.
originalName: シュトルテハイム・ラインバッハ3世

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Reinbach was the scion of Middleport, being the son of the town's lord, Schtolteheim Reinbach II. He behaved much like a typical member of the aristocracy, wearing overly flamboyant clothing and frequently breaking into histrionics.

Although he had a fondness for those who share his aesthetic sense, he also has a stubborn side, shown when he disassociated himself with his father due to the latter's shady business deals concerning Rune Cannon components as, unlike his father, Reinbach III possessed a pure and noble heart.

When Reinbach discovered the actions of his father, he would leave his household, accompanied by his faithful servant Micky before being offered a place on the Stronghold Ship by its leader. Although eager to aid the Allied Forces, he suffered from extreme seasickness, causing him to spend most of his time on ship laying down.

Following the Island Liberation War, Reinbach reconciled with his father and succeeded his father, taking up the mantle of Lord of Middleport. Some years later, he would encounter Kyril through their mutual use of the Quest Guild where, upon learning of Kyril's journey to destroy rune cannons, he would accompany the youth's party on their quest, along with his good friend Charlemagne.

A biography on Reinbach by Micky, titled The Rose Swordsman, would enshrine Reinbach as a noble hero into posterity.

He would be referenced in legends told as far as the City-States of Jowston. In future generations, his name would be used as an alias on several occasions, including by Viktor during the Gate Rune War as he tried to pass through the Fortress of Kwaba undetected.

trivia: • Suikoden V is the only main title that does not feature a Reinbach gag.

name: Selma
description: Selma (セルマ, Seruma) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. Selma is a taciturn elf whose exile leads her to joining the protagonist's forces.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
thumb|left|220px|Burning Mirror|Found one!Selma was an elf who lived in the Elven Settlement on Na-Nal Island. She was a very quiet person and, although not without bias, generally believed in the pointlessness of the human-elf conflict on Na-Nal, marking her as something of an eccentric among her people.

When Lino En Kuldes and the leader were imprisoned by the Elven Elder following their theft of the Elven Remedy, Selma ended up releasing them. This action incurred the wrath of the elder, leading to her exile. Following this, she volunteered her services in order to fight the Kooluk Empire and joined the crew of the Stronghold Ship.

Following the Island Liberation War, she was allowed to return to the Elven Settlement due to her deeds in fighting the Kooluk. Two years later, at the request of Lino En Kuldes, she and Paula would travel to the Kooluk Empire to aid Kyril, reinforcing his group at the Small Border Village.

name: Setsu
description: Setsu (セツ, Setsu) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV who also appears briefly in Suikoden Tactics. Setsu is the worrisome retainer to Lino En Kuldes in the Kingdom of Obel.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
thumb|220px|left|Setsu attempts to distract the Kooluk Empire|Kooluk fleet to aid the [[Stronghold Ship escape.]]Setsu served as the loyal retainer to the royal family of Obel. His main priority in life was the prosperity of the kingdom and its royal family, which caused him sometimes to dismiss any other concerns. He was something of a do-good worrier and often clashed with Lino En Kuldes and Flare about their risk-tasking.

When the Island Liberation War began, he was against any military conflict between Obel and the Kooluk Empire but his concerns were overridden by Lino. He would stay behind on the island when it was occupied by the Kooluk, in order to protect Flare and the kingdom's citizens. Following the island's liberation, he would join the crew of the Stronghold Ship.

Following the war, Setsu would continue to serve Lino and Flare loyally from the shadows.

name: Shiramine
description: Shiramine (シラミネ, Shiramine) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV. Shiramine is a skilled dragnet fisher and friend of Ugetsu.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Shiramine was a talented fisherman and a close friend of Ugetsu. He was a gentle and caring sort who cared a lot for his friend. Shiramine also looks up to Ugetsu's fishing abilities and strives to be more like him.

When Ugetsu joined the crew of the Stronghold Ship, Shiramine ended up being caught by his fishing rod and dragged aboard. Once cut loose, he agreed to also join the crew, working with Ugetsu at the rear of the ship to catch fish in his net. He would end up accidentally catching the mermaid Lilan with his net.

Following the Island Liberation War, Shiramine would end up becoming a fishing instructor in Razril, taking on Tal as one of his students.

name: Sigurd
description: Sigurd (シグルド, Shigurudo) is a major character in Suikoden IV who also appears in Suikoden Tactics. Sigurd is a pirate and one of Kika's closest assistants.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Sigurd was a pirate who worked closely alongside Kika. He had come into her service when Kika had saved his life and since then he's worked to repay that personal debt he felt he owed. Before that, he had worked for the Middleport fleet, leading the fleet for Schtolteheim Reinbach II and carrying out his dirty work. He would be grievously injured in the battle against the pirate Steele and was rescued by Kika, to whom he pledged his loyalty. Since then, the assassin Keen had been tracking him for some time, in order to ensure that any secrets Sigurd knew of the fleet would remain hidden.

He was rather prudent and calm, making him quite the opposite of his colleague Hervey. The two were enemies in Sigurd's past life in Middleport and Hervey had sworn loyalty to Kika in much the same manner as Sigurd following the battle with Steele. Still, the two worked well together and trusted each other, serving faithfully under Kika. When Kika agreed to join forces with the leader of the Allied Forces fighting against the Kooluk Empire, Sigurd would join as well. 

During the major battles of the Island Liberation War, Sigurd often took the helm of The Grishend. He would also encounter Keen in Middelport during the war but was able to persuade him to not kill him. Once the conflict ended, Sigurd returned to the Nest of Pirates with the rest of Kika's crew and continued to work hard to assist their operations.

He also later joined Kyril on his mission to destroy the Rune Cannons together with Hervey. The two of them were asked by Dario to train Nalleo and they are impressed how much he improved. He still remained loyal to Kika after the Rune Cannons were destroyed.

name: Snowe Vingerhut
description: Snowe Vingerhut (スノウ・フィンガーフート, Sunō Fingāfūto) is a major character in Suikoden IV who also appears in Suikoden Tactics. Snowe is the spoilt but generally well-intentioned son of Vincent Vingerhut, lord of Razril.
originalName: スノウ・フィンガーフート

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Snowe was the only son of Vincent Vingerhut, lord of the Gaien island of Razril. At an early age, the household adopted an orphan to be Snowe's caretaker and the two would become childhood friends, almost brothers, despite the inherent inequality in their relationship.

Growing up with a lavish and spoiled lifestyle, Snowe did not grow into a bad person, necessarily, but had an inflated sense of pride which did not always go together well with his timid nature and lack of dependableness in times of crisis.

He attended the Knights of Gaien academy with his friends and graduated peacefully enough. However, his mishandling of his first mission; escorting an Orark Maritime Trade ship which came under attack by the pirate Brandeau led to Commander Glen inheriting the Rune of Punishment.

When Snowe later saw Glen die after using the rune, he accused his childhood friend of being responsible in his testimony, resulting in Katarina's expulsion of the hero from Razril.

Following this, Lord Vingerhut ensured that Snowe would succeed Glen as commander of the knights, regardless of his inexperience. In an attempt to foster peace, he would swiftly surrender Razril to the Kooluk Empire and cooperate with Graham Cray's hunt for the Rune of Punishment.

However, the anger of Razril's citizens meant that he was unable to return to the port after leaving to attack a pirate band (in actuality the Stronghold Ship under the command of his old friend).

With his position lost for good with the liberation of Razril by the Allied Forces, Snowe turned to piracy and ended up leading an attack on his old friends in an attempt at revenge for the slights he felt he suffered. Easily repelled, his small measure of authority would not last long and he was eventually abandoned at sea by his pirate…

name: Taisuke
description: Taisuke (タイスケ, Taisuke) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV. Taisuke is a passionate bath maker, always looking to perfect his techniques.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Taisuke was a man with an unprecedented passion for baths. The leader of the Stronghold Ship came across Taisuke sampling the hot springs on the Deserted Island. Inviting him aboard, Taisuke jumped at the chance to install a bath on the enormous vessel.

On board, Taisuke's bath proved instantly popular as he maintained a very comfortable bath that was kept heated at all times with boiling water. Despite his success though, he was frustrated that people like Jango and Brec, as well as the ship's compliment of Nay-Kobolds, either disliked baths or created a large mess for him to clean up on the rare occasions they did use his baths.

Following the Island Liberation War, Taisuke continued his travels, spreading the joy of baths throughout the land, building a succession of luxurious bath houses.

name: Tal
description: Tal (タル, Taru) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. Tal is a knight of Razril and friend of the hero.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Tal was a Knight of Gaien trainee who graduated at the same time as Keneth, Jewel, Paula, Snowe and his friend, whom he got along well with. As the eldest member of the group, he was something of the glue holding the others together with his good nature and frank personality coupled with his impressive physique.

When his friend was accused of the murder of Glen Cott, Tal believed strongly in his innocence, even after he was exiled from Razril, fighting to try and prove his innocence. As well as a strong fighter, Tal was a passionate fisherman, sometimes working with Shiramine on board the Stronghold Ship and marveling at the quality of fish served aboard.

Following the Island Liberation War, Tal quit his military service and became a fisherman in Razril, studying under Shiramine.

trivia: • In the Suikoden IV novelization, Tal is one of the two Knights of Gaien who joins the hero during his exile. The other is Jewel.

name: Tanya
description: Tanya (ターニャ, Tānya) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV. Tanya is a scholar and librarian with a view to becoming a student of the strategist Elenor Silverberg.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Tanya was a scholar possessing clear thought and rational thinking. She was willing to enter dangerous situations to satisfy her thirst for knowledge which led to her journeying to the Nest of Pirates in search of Elenor Silverberg. There, she encountered the Allied Forces and joined the crew of the Stronghold Ship, presiding over the ship's library.

As a crewperson, she would often enter into conflict with Agnes over receiving the teachings of Elenor with Agnes acting very protective of her position as Elenor's protégé. Tanya, on the other hand, would often be jealous of Agnes' role in Elenor's life.

On board ship, she would manage the ship's library by taking care of the Old Books passed onto her. She took to this duty with delight, expressing joy in the size of her facilities. She was also somewhat possessive of her room, seemingly encouraging Haruto to leave the library to her.

Over the course of the Island Liberation War, Tanya would develop an interest in the history of the Island Nations. Following the war, she would begin research into the region's history and began writing up her findings with a view to publication.

name: Tov
description: Tov (トーブ, Tōbu) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV. Tov is the man who designed the Stronghold Ship used throughout the game.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Tov was a talented shipwright who was commissioned in secret by Lino En Kuldes to construct the Stronghold Ship in the Kingdom of Obel. Tov actually came in second in a contest for shipwrights held in Obel but something about him impressed Lino enough that he was tasked with this monumental job. He was not very affable and was a very taciturn person but, in his own way, was a kind, respectful figure, just lacking in the social graces.

As the shipwright, he was one of the very first crew members of the Stronghold Ship and accompanied the vessel when it fled Obel. Throughout the Island Liberation War, Tov would work to maintain the vessel to the best of his abilities, even upgrading what parts of the ship he could to make it even more effective in its war against the Kooluk Empire. That said, even Tov's considerable talents and knowledge failed to explain the presence of the strange room where Mao and Nao would take residence, much to his consternation.

Following the war, Tov would be commissioned once again by Lino to begin work on another secret ship.

name: Travis
description: Travis (トラヴィス, Toravuisu) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV. He is a quite youth who would prefer nothing more to being left alone.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Travis was a youth found standing at the mound with the Rune of Punishment had once been kept at the rear of the Ruins of Obel. Although a tall and handsome man, he was painfully quiet and had apparently secluded himself in the ruins in order to take advantage of its solitude.

When he was approached by the leader of the Stronghold Ship, Travis would ask him to show him the proper use of an Escape Talisman, sending the hero out of the ruins and out of Travis' hair. Once the hero made his way back, however, Travis would agree to join his forces as an apology for his trickery.

Despite his constant desire to be alone, he did hold a fondness for Nay-Kobolds, eulogizing over how fluffy they were. He also enjoyed the food aboard ship, pestering Pam for the recipe to her buns without success.

Once the Island Liberation War concluded, Travis would quickly grow tired of the hectic pace of town life and would return to the ruins in order to continue his isolation, this time as an official manager of the Ruins of Obel.

name: Trishtan
description: Trishtan (トリスタン, Torisutan) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. Trishtan is a hypochondriac warrior who manages to fight despite his fluctuating health problems.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Trishtan was a former soldier in the Kingdom of Obel who resigned his position due to a psychosomatic illness that continued to plague him over the years. As such, he began treatment under Doctor Yu. Yu judged him to be healthy in every respect and identifying Trishtan as a hypochondriac, decided to indulge him and began treatment. Of course, the medicine prescribed to him was a placebo, made from wheat flour, but it nonetheless had the effect of relieving Trishtan's symptoms. 

His treatment was forced to be discontinued when Yu joined the crew of the Stronghold Ship as they fled the Kingdom of Obel from the approaching Kooluk fleet. Once Obel was liberated from occupation by the Kooluk, Trishtan met Yu again and joined the crew of the Stronghold Ship at Yu's urging to continue his treatment. Aboard ship, Trishtan continued his treatment although he found fault with Carrie for giving him real medicine which, predictably, did nothing to relieve his symptoms. He also claimed that Nay-Kobold fur aggravated his condition and took up an all-carb diet to try and stay healthy.

Following the Island Liberation War, Trishtan returned to service in Obel, becoming captain of the royal guard.

name: Troy
description: Troy (トロイ, Toroi) is an antagonist in Suikoden IV. Known as the "Child of the Sea God", Troy serves as commander of the Kooluk Empire's first fleet.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Troy was a member of the Kooluk Empire's Imperial Faction who, as well as being a skilled warrior, also served as the talented and resourceful commander of the 1st Kooluk Fleet. His reputation and regard were such that he was spoken of as the "Child of the Sea God".
Youth and Mermaid Encounter
As a young cadet, Troy displayed exceptional academic and tactical promise, yet his reserved nature and difficulty with social integration were noted by his instructors. He often sought solitude along the shore to escape the stifling atmosphere of peer rivalries, finding peace in the rhythm of the waves.

His first documented encounter with a mermaid occurred during one such retreat. Having commandeered a small, abandoned dinghy to row offshore, Troy fell asleep and awoke to find himself far out to sea. He quickly realized the boat was being propelled by an unseen force. Prepared for a confrontation, he was astonished when the entity revealed itself not as a monster, but as a young mermaid with distinctive red hair.

This initial meeting was not an isolated incident. The same mermaid began to appear consistently when Troy was at sea, maintaining a cautious distance but clearly recognizing him. Over time, their interactions developed into a recurring, if silent, rapport, with the mermaid often performing graceful leaps near his vessel.

The encounters fostered within him a noted sense of longing for the serene and beautiful world beneath the waves, a stark contrast to the complexities of human society. This unique relationship continued even after his graduation and rise through the military ranks.
Naval Captain of Kooluk
Much of his reputation came from the SY 299 Gaien attack on El-Eal Fortress. Captaining a single ship, he was able to put to rout several ships belonging to the…

name: Ugetsu
description: Ugetsu (ウゲツ, Ugetsu) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV. Ugetsu is a skilled fisherman and friend of Shiramine.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Ugetsu was a fisherman who yearned to be as skilled as his close friend, Shiramine. Pole and line fishing was his specialty, and he would always eat what he could, not letting anything go to waste.

He would join the crew of the Stronghold Ship at Na-Nal Island as he wanted to experience fishing from the enormous vessel. On board, he established a small fishing nook at the rear of the ship where he spent his time. The fish he and Shiramine caught were brought to Kevin and Pam who prepared them for the ship's crew.

Following the Island Liberation War, Ugetsu would return to Na-Nal where he would continue to spend his days fishing.

name: Vincent Vingerhut
description: Vincent Vingerhut (ヴィンセント・フィンガーフート, Vuinsento Fingāfūto) is a minor character in Suikoden IV. Vincent is the Lord of Razril and the father of Snowe.
originalName: ヴィンセント・フィンガーフート

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Vincent Vingerhut was a member of the Dukedom of Gaien's aristocracy and served as lord of Razril. The father of Snowe, Vincent provided great financial support to the Knights of Gaien, allowing him a large amount of influence which he would leverage to his son's benefit.

As such, he would push his son into the leadership position in the order following the death of Commander Glen Cott. When Snowe sold out Razril to the Kooluk Empire during the Island Liberation War, Vincent would flee to Gaien amidst the resulting civil disorder, leaving it to its fate.

He would return to Razril following its liberation and attempted to assert his authority as the island's lord but was quickly turned upon by its angry inhabitants and was chased from the island once more.

name: Warlock
description: Warlock (ウォーロック, Uōrokku) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV. Warlock is the inventor of the Rune Cannon and a powerful magician.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Warlock was the great magician who originally created Rune Cannons. Roughly 10 years before the outbreak of the Island Liberation War, Warlock summoned a Giant Tree from another world and was able to discover a way to utilize that creature's magical abilities to create a new weapon; the rune cannon. This new weapon would quickly spread far and wide, quickly becoming indispensable in naval combat around the Island Nations region.

A stubborn craftsman, he would not answer any questions about their creation or design. This was because Warlock regretted having ever created such a weapon. In order to prevent further information about rune cannons from becoming known, he would decide to live without company in his hidden laboratory in Middleport.

When the Island Liberation War, broke out Warlock would join the crew of the Stronghold Ship after hearing about the destruction of Iluya Island by Kooluk's giant Rune Cannon. He wished to help to end the war swiftly and atone for his sins. Pablo would become something of an apprentice of his during his time on the ship but he was cagey about the subject of Rune Cannons even with him.

Although Warlock fought to atone for his sins, many found them not so easily absolved. Following the war, he was accosted by a militant group determined to make him pay for his crimes and was killed.

name: Wendel
description: Wendel (ウェンデル, Uenderu) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. Wendel is the aggressively straightforward apprentice of Nico.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Wendel was a tomboyish and overly honest person who idolized Nico, the leader of the lookouts on board the Stronghold Ship. She pursued Nico with the dream of becoming a fully-fledged lookout like him.

Following her dreams, she was able to board the vessel once it returned to the Kingdom of Obel following the island's liberation from the Kooluk Empire and join the Allied Forces, wishing to learn under Nico.

She would learn a lot from Nico, despite his unease at having such a fanatical apprentice, and following the Island Liberation War, she would continue to study hard, determined to surpass Nico in nautical knowledge in the future.

Following the order of King Lino En Kuldes himself, she would later help Kyril in his investigations of the Rune Cannons in the Kooluk Empire. She showed her frank attitude in confronting Kyril, asking him why he did not join them during the Island Liberation War.

She thought of his injury as just a pitiful excuse but added that he had become strong and "cleaned up well" since then.

trivia: • Wendel is one of three characters in Suikoden Tactics who does not show any form of Good Will with other characters, the other two characters being Ameria and Lalacle.

name: Yu
description: Yu (ユウ, Yū) is a supporting character in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. Yu is a doctor working in the Kingdom of Obel with a strong financial streak.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Yu was a highly regarded physician who dedicated his life to the practice of medicine within the Kingdom of Obel. Yu's reputation for seeking proper compensation for his medical services might have conveyed a sense of callousness.

However, this disposition was rooted in practicality rather than insensitivity. He recognized the importance of charging fees that covered the expenses of necessary medical supplies, ensuring the continuity of effective care.

Amid the fall of the Kingdom of Obel to the Kooluk Empire, Yu chose to join the crew of the Stronghold Ship, recognizing its role as a refuge for the sick and injured. Yu's contribution extended beyond the realm of traditional medicine. He demonstrated his skill as a physician by addressing Trishtan's hypochondria through the use of placebo medicine.

During the Island Liberation War, Yu chose not to charge for his services directly, benefiting from the compensation provided by Lino En Kuldes. Following the liberation of Obel, he would be reunited with his assistant, Carrie, and the two worked together for the remainder of the Island Liberation War. This commitment continued post-war, when Yu collaborated with Carrie to establish a free consultation office on Obel.

Yu's later involvement in Kyril's investigation of the Rune Cannons was also instigated by Carrie's persuasive influence. Throughout their shared mission, she demonstrated her concern for his well-being, underlining their strong bond.

name: Alenia
description: Alenia (アレニア, Arenia) is an antagonist in Suikoden V. She is a member of Falena's Queen's Knights who supports the policies of the Godwin Faction.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Alenia was a skilled swordswoman who served in the Queen's Knights. Her father had also served in the elite group before being killed in the New Armes invasion of 441. That said, she did not join the organization to follow her father's wishes or to follow in his footsteps. Instead, she saw her father's death as a sign of his weakness, inviting her disdain.

She was also a stringent follower of the beliefs of the Godwin Faction, believing their focus on military power was best for Falena. As such, she was complicit in the Godwin coup on the Sun Palace in 449, remaining a member of the Queen's Knights under the Godwin puppet regime.

During the Sun Rune War, she would often fight alongside Zahhak. She would be chosen to bear the Twilight Rune, revealing it to the members of the Imperial City Recapture Army at Doraat Fortress. Still, she had trouble controlling the rune's power. Because of this, and other failures in battle, she would have the rune removed from her and given instead to Sialeeds.

Alenia would continue to fight faithfully for the Godwin regime throughout the war, defending the Sun Palace to the last even as the war came to a close. Alongside Zahhak, she would take a large dose of Raging Nostrum, vastly increasing her strength and vitality. Still, even that was not enough to prevent defeat and she would die a violent death from the side effects of the drug.

name: Alhazred
description: Alhazred (アズラッド, Azuraddo) is a supporting character in Suikoden V. Alhazred is a highly dedicated collector of rare and wonderful books.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Alhazred is a man in search of rare and interesting books who has traveled the world, collecting tomes from the countries he passes through. He was apparently wealthy enough to spend extraordinary amounts of potch on books he desired and could be sometimes found clutching them as if he were in love. He dismisses any claims that he is a "paddler" as he does not actually sell any works.

During the Sun Rune War, he became interested in the Book of Condemnation, which was said to pass from owner to owner with every unnatural death. He would travel to the Queendom of Falena, seeking clues about this book when he met the Prince of Falena. After recovering this legendary book with the help of the Prince, he would then join the Imperial City Recapture Army out of gratitude, establishing a library within their headquarters at Ceras Lake.

Following the war, Alhazred would head towards Nagarea in his pursuit of rare books but was refused entry. That said, this seems unlikely to dissuade him from his path.

name: Alison Barows
description: Alison Barows (アリスン・バロウズ, Arisun Barōzu) is a background character in Suikoden V. She is the reclusive mother of both Euram and Luserina Barows.

facts.label: Family
facts.value: Euram Barows (son), Hiram Barows (son), Luserina Barows (daughter), Salum Barows

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Alison is the wife of Salum Barows, leader of the Barows Faction. She had four children and had lost one due to illness but ever since the death of Hiram, her eldest son, in the Falenan succession struggles she would isolate herself out of fear and despair in the Barows manor at Rainwall.

She would baby Euram throughout his childhood but following the fall of the Barows Faction in the Sun Rune War, would admonish Euram, helping him to overcome his confusion and childishness.

Following the war, she would aid both Euram and Luserina in their efforts in rebuilding Falena.

name: Arshtat Falenas
description: Arshtat Falenas (アルシュタート・ファレナス, Arushutāto Farenasu) is a major character in Suikoden V. Arshtat is the ruling Queen of Falena as well as the mother of the game's protagonist.
originalName: アルシュタート・ファレナス

facts.label: Family
facts.value: Falzrahm (mother), Ferid (husband), Haswar (cousin), Hero (Suikoden V) (son), Kauss

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Arshtat was the reigning Queen of Falena at the outset of the Sun Rune War. She was the wife of Ferid and the strict, but gentle, mother of both Lymsleia and the Prince of Falena. She had met Ferid in a visit to the Island Nations as a youth and the two had quickly fallen in love. In the year 433, Arshtat would sponsor him in the Sacred Games and his victory in this tournament would allow him to ask for her hand in marriage.

Arshtat would become queen in SY 440, after the sudden death of her mother, Falzrahm. The following year, with the aid of strategist Lucretia Merces, she would fight off an invasion from the New Armes Kingdom and would later institute a ban on cruelty to slaves. These, and other acts, would lead to Arshtat being lauded as a virtuous, benevolent ruler.

This would all change in 447 when, during the Lordlake Uprising, Arshtat would become the bearer of the Sun Rune on Lucretia's advice, to prevent it from being stolen by the Godwin Faction. Although this would indeed protect the rune from being stolen, it would also make Queen Arshtat increasingly unstable in her rule. She ended the Lordlake riots by using the Sun Rune to dry up the waters in the region, forcing them to live in desperation and squalor. Since that time, bursts of anger were not uncommon.

In 449, when the Godwin Faction began their coup at the Sun Palace, beginning the Sun Rune War, Arshtat's rage would be so great that she would accidentally kill Ferid in one of her outbursts as she fought off the Godwin invaders. The one person able to reach her in her anger killed, the devastated and enraged Arshtat decided to wipe out all Falena itself with her powers. She would be stopped before she could do so, cut down by Georg Prime with a single thrust of his sword.

Before she died, she…

name: Ax
description: Ax (アックス, Akkusu) is a minor character in Suikoden V. He is a young dragon horse and the mount of Nick.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Ax is a dragon horse who is the companion of Nick, the Feitas Dragon Cavalry apprentice. Compared to other dragon horses, Ax stands out in that he has three horns, which is a rarity. This as well as strength and stamina, which allows him to fight alongside Nick despite his youth, would help him stand out as having the makings of a first class dragon horse.

During the Sun Rune War, he would fight alongside Nick in the Imperial City Recapture Army. He excelled in water battles, showing a measure of success comparable to fully grown dragon horses such as Lance and Flail.

Following the war, he would continue to train and would grow into a magnificent three-horned dragon horse, making his partnership with Nick one to watch for in the Dragon Cavalry.

name: Babbage
description: Babbage (バベッジ, Babejji) is a supporting character in Suikoden V. Babbage is an incredibly intelligent, if eccentric, inventor obsessed with gears and clockwork mechanics.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Babbage is a highly talented inventor who works constantly from his workshop in Estrise. Known as the "Gear King", he is obsessed with gears and most all of his creations use them in some fashion. Over the years, he has created a wide variety of items, but his two greatest known creations are the windmills which overlook Estrise and the Revolving Bridge, dividing the East Palace from eastern Falena. He has an apprentice, Sorensen, who essentially takes care of his day-to-day needs, allowing Babbage to devote himself to his work.

Once the Sun Rune War broke out, it became clear the Revolving Bridge could only be controlled from the western side, giving a great advantage of the Godwin Faction. The Prince asked Babbage to stop the Revolving Bridge and the inventor accompanied the Prince and his group to the bridge.

Inside the inner portion of the Revolving Bridge, Babbage removed the crucial gear that kept the entire Bridge running, thus stopping the mechanism that allowed it to revolve. Back at Estrise, an impressed Prince asked Babbage to join the Imperial City Recapture Army, but instead, Babbage asked Sorensen to go with the Prince instead.

Babbage later assisted the Recapture Army once again, helping to create a mechanism to help with the destruction of Hatred Fortress. The mechanism, when placed on a Raftfleet ship, helped propel a barrel of oil on board into the fortress, starting a fire which forced the Godwin soldiers to withdraw before the fortress was destroyed by the tidal wave stemming from Ceras Lake.

After the water level at the Ceras Lake receded, a previously submerged room was discovered which contained a large gear from the Sindar civilization. Babbage was informed and so the inventor insisted on being taken to see the gears for himself. Impressed…

name: Bahram Luger
description: Bahram Luger (バフラム・ルーガー, Bafuramu Rūgā) is an antagonist in Suikoden V. Bahram Luger is the Admiral of Falena's Royal Navy and a loyal member of the Godwin Faction.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Bahram Luger was the stern, conscientious Admiral of the Queendom of Falena's Royal Navy. He was a reliable, calm commander who received commendations from Raja, leading to his appointment. Bahram had served under Raja during the Armes Invasion and at first felt that she, being the daughter of a fisherman, was below him, until he saw her in action. After that, he served loyally and the two formed a close bond, leading to Luger succeeding Raja when the latter retired following the invasion.

Following the Queen's Assassination and the outbreak of the Sun Rune War, Luger supported the Godwin Faction, believing that greater cultural homogeneity as well as the proper utilization of the Sun Rune could only bring further prosperity and stability to Falena. Luger's fleet was sent to bring Raftfleet under complete control, rather than leaving it as the autonomous collection of ships it was. Still, Lucretia Merces managed to use her knowledge of the Feitas River, and the debris underneath the waters, to ground most of Luger's fleet, forcing him to order a retreat.

Later during the war, Luger served under Queen's Knight Childerich as the Godwin faction pushed back against the Imperial City Recapture Army, reclaiming lost territories such as Doraat Fortress. Completely disgusted by Childerich's methods, Bahram did his best to stomach them as the Godwin Army seemed to be making progress.

But once their reclaimed territories began falling, Bahram let the new Queen's Knight know of his disdain for his methods and derided his own stupidity in allowing the situation to happen in the first place, denouncing him as a maniac and a fool. Childerich retorted by suggesting that Bahram was, in fact, a traitor and that he was the reason Doraat was falling around them. Bahram vowed to prove…

trivia: • Bahram (or Vahram), in Avestan Verethragna, is a divinity of Zoroastrianism, representing victory. It is also a name meaning "smiting of resistance" or "victorious" in Persian. Several Kings of the Sassanid Empire also held the name.

name: Bastan
description: Bastan (バシュタン, Bashutan) is a minor character in Suikoden V. Bastan is an appraiser, forced to put his own, finely honed, aesthetic sense on hold in order to support his family.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Bastan is an appraiser who moved to Haud Village in order to raise money for his family. Still, although he actually held a great skill in appraisal, he was forced to pander to the eclectic tastes of both his fellow villagers and Salum Barows, giving him a certain measure of resentment. His sense of self-worth also suffered, with Bastan considering himself a con man of sorts for his actions.

When the Prince first arrived in Haud, he confided this in him, noting that the Prince was an outsider. Later, when the Prince brought him a rare Ivanov Painting to be appraised, an excited Bastan agreed to join the Imperial City Recapture Army, moving his business to Ceras Lake. Despite his reaction to the Ivanov painting, he was noted to be less interested in painting and sculptures than in pottery and dishes, as he considered the latter as being useful in everyday life as well as artistically valuable.

Bastan's spirit was rejuvenated at the castle as he was able to honestly appraise many kinds of wonderful creations. He sometimes talked with Josephine, where she would complain about her home country. Still, Bastan thought that no culture could be called backwards, stating that different cultures bloomed in different regions. Cornelio didn't think highly of him, viewing him as yet another 'mediocrity' due to 'putting prices on pieces of art'. In contrast, Norden thought that he and Bastan would get on well, both having had trouble with the Barows family in the past.

Following the Sun Rune War, he returned to Haud Village to appraise art, but now seemingly at peace with its eccentric citizenry.

name: Belcoot
description: Belcoot (ベルクート, Berukūto) is a supporting character in Suikoden V. Belcoot is a former gladiator hoping to put an end to Falena's institutionalized slavery.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Belcoot is a wandering swordsman with a keen sense of justice. In his past, he was a gladiator slave, fighting for his life in the Stormfist Arena. A very poor gladiator initially, Belcoot managed to escape around SY 438, eventually fleeing the Queendom of Falena entirely.

He eventually made his way to Kanakan, where he studied the Falcon style of swordplay under the famous Roundia Haia. Upon the completion of his training, Belcoot returned to Falena in SY 449 in order to enter the Sacred Games.

His goal in entering the Sacred Games was to abolish the gladiatorial system completely upon his entering the Falenan royal family. Although thankful for the steps that Queen Arshtat and Ferid had made in securing greater care for slaves, Belcoot was aware that things would revert back to their previous state should a nobleman win the tournament.

While staying in Stormfist, Belcoot was accosted by jingoistic and ×enophobic thugs who sought to put him out of the competition after he had convincingly defeated Lord De Beers' representative, Jinku, in the opening round. With the help of the Falenan Prince, Belcoot was rescued.

Still, the thugs then kidnapped Marina, a young woman working at the inn in Stormfist where Belcoot stayed, who had taken a shine to the swordsman. Belcoot, with the aid of the Prince and his companions managed to rescue Marina from her captors in the Western Woods.

While she was held captive, Dolph had actually given Marina a sample of Dark Arcanum, an undetectable drug used by Nether Gate.
