Sagiri is a quiet, docile young woman who works as an investigator, along with Oboro and Shigure, in the Oboro Detective Agency. She had been previously trained from childhood to be an assassin for the covert group Nether Gate. 

Sagiri's trainer noted that a smiling little girl approaching a target would have them lower their guard consistently and so Sagiri was trained to always smile, without pause. Even following the dismantlement of Nether Gate and her being taken in by Oboro, Sagiri was unable to make any facial expression beyond her single, fixed smile. Very few people can tell when this smile is genuine as opposed to a result of her condition. Shigure, who was very protective of Sagiri, was one of those few.

While still a child operative, Sagiri had assassinate Nakula's father around SY 436. Nakula tracked down Sagiri during the Sun Rune War but was persuaded to stay his hand, noting that the real enemies were the Godwin Faction. Talking with Nakula reforged Sagiri's dedication to fighting alongside the Imperial City Recapture Army, having previously only assisted in investigative work for Oboro.

Following the war, Nakula would leave her unharmed, stating that he would "get her last" before leaving to track down other Nether Gate members. She would also continue to work for the Oboro Detective Agency, now under Fuyo's leadership. It is said that once in a great while her smile slips just a little.

name: Sairoh
description: Sairoh (サイロウ, Sairou) is a minor character in Suikoden V. Sairoh is a retired trader whose trading network once extended through all Falena and beyond.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Sairoh is a retired old man, the grandfather of the trader Shinro, who enjoys spending his days peacefully in Raftfleet. Although he may not look it, he was once an incredibly prosperous and powerful trader in his own right.

He had traded with Beaver Lodge in the past, trading with Fuwalafuwalu's father, who was leader at the time. Wasil's father also traded with Sairoh, noting that he was an aggressive, though fair, trading partner. His grandson, Shinro, particularly looked up to him as an inspiration.

His legendary success was known throughout the land and there were dark rumors about him being involved in the weapons trade during times of war, as well as having a large pirate fleet under his employ in the Island Nations.

He retired soon after the death of Shinro's mother and father, Sairoh's son. It is said that this or simply the dark rumors that had trailed him, led him to retire. Whether these mutterings are true, and how accurate they may be, is currently unknown.

During the Sun Rune War, he encouraged the Prince to get involved with trading and once he saw his success in making a profit from salt, decided to join the Imperial City Recapture Army. At Ceras Lake, he would open a Trading post, making a lot of potch in the process.

Following the Sun Rune War, Sairoh would make his final retirement, spending his days relaxing in Raftfleet with Raja and occasionally helping his grandson out when need be.

name: Salisha Raulbel
description: Salisha Raulbel (サリーシャ・ラウルベル, Sarīsha Rauruberu) is a background character in Suikoden V. She is the daughter of Lord Solis Raulbel.
originalName: サリーシャ・ラウルベル

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Salisha is the daughter of Solis Raulbel, the Lord of Sable. She was involved in a relationship with Dinn, the young swordsman who served the House of Raulbel, and though he would rarely speak of it, he was devoted to her.

Following the Sun Rune War it was rumored that Dinn had won Salisha's hand in marriage.

name: Salum Barows
description: Salum Barows (サルム・バロウズ, Sarumu Barōzu) is an antagonist in Suikoden V. He is the opulent and loquacious head of the illustrious Barows Faction.

facts.label: Family
facts.value: Alison Barows (wife), Euram Barows (son), Hiram Barows (son), Luserina Barows (daughter),

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Salum Barows was the head of the great Barows Faction, one of the most powerful noble factions in the Queendom of Falena. He was also the father of Euram and Luserina Barows, as well as his two older children who had died previously. He was a skillful politician and a talented orator who could usually get his own way through his verbal talents.

In SY 438, the Falenan royal family began a bitter struggle over succession to the throne with Queen Olhazeta's death. Salum Barows was one of the major players in pushing Falzrahm to claim the throne as her husband, Kauss, was a member of his house. Following the ascension of Falzrahm to the throne, the Barows Faction became one of the most powerful houses in Falena, rivaled only by the Godwin Faction. Over the years, the two factions would clash in the senate.

When the Sun Rune War broke out in SY 449, Salum Barows offered refuge to the escaping Prince and his companions. During the Prince's time in Rainwall, Salum would try and encourage him not only to marry his daughter, Luserina, but to crown himself king also. Despite his obvious plays for power, the Prince had no option but to continue to cooperate even if he did reject Salum's more extreme proposals.

However, once it was revealed that Salum was responsible for not only the Lordlake Uprising but the theft of the Dawn Rune from the East Palace, the Prince finally left his protection. Salum also had Luserina abandon him and saw his once stalwart ally, Boz Wilde, leave him also. In the wake of this desertion, Salum would bluster about revenge but would mostly stew in Rainwall, fearful of attacks from the now-ruling Godwins.

Towards the end of the Sun Rune War, however, he would be murdered in his home by Sialeeds, who wished to wipe out both the Barows and the Godwins…

name: Sanji
description: Sanji (サンジ, Sanji) is a background character in Suikoden V. Sanji is a stray dog.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Sanji was a dog that had taken shelter at the Imperial City Recapture Army base at Ceras Lake. There, they would be cared for by a youth there.

Once Sol-Falena was liberated by the Recapture Army, Sanji would leave on a journey but not before the youth tied a wrapping cloth filled with treats around their neck.

trivia: • A pretty clear reference to the dogs from Suikoden III, most notably Kosanji.

name: Shahrewar
description: Shahrewar (シャスレワール, Shasurewāru) is a background character mentioned in Suikoden V. She was the eldest daughter of Queen Olhazeta and Crown Princess of Falena prior to the succession struggles which plagued the nation.

facts.label: Family
facts.value: Arshtat Falenas (neice), Falzrahm (sister), Haswar (daughter), Mardas (husband), Olhazeta

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Shahrewar was the sister of Falzrahm and the mother of Haswar. She was also the eldest daughter of Queen Olhazeta, making her the successor to throne of Falena.

However, over the years, Falzrahm would try and usurp this position and undermine Shahrewar's claims to the throne. When their mother died, their feud turned into a bloody succession conflict, rife with assassinations and murders. Eventually, Shahrewar would tire of all the bloodshed, including the death of her husband, Mardas, and would cede the throne to her sister.

Falzrahm, paranoid and not trusting her defeated sister, would have her assassinated by Nether Gate soon afterwards.

name: Shamshir
description: Shamshir (シャムシール, Shamushīru) is a background character in Suikoden V. He is the mount of Craig Laden.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Shamshir is a dark-skinned dragon horse who is the companion and battle mount of Craig Laden, commander of the Feitas Dragon Cavalry.

His strength and ability is such that he is often nicknamed the "Dragon Horse King".

trivia: • A shamshir is a Persian saber with a radically curved blade.

name: Sharmista
description: Sharmista (シャルミシタ, Sharumishita) is a supporting character in Suikoden V. Sharmista is a high-ranked officer in the Armes Western Marine Corps, working under Shula Valya.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Sharmista served as the secretary and bodyguard to Shula Valya, helping to lead the Armes Western Marine Corps as one of the unit's senior officers along with the equally capable Nifsara.

A beautiful and intelligent woman, she was a member of one of the leading families in the Madra Clan. Her quiet, flexible form of thinking marked as an intellectual sort. She and Shula have known each other since childhood and she was also very close to Nifsara.

Following the withdrawal of the New Armes Kingdom from Falena in the Sun Rune War, she and Nifsara would join the Imperial City Recapture Army along with Shula Valya himself in order to help foster more peaceful relations between the two countries post-war. In battle, Sharmista not only fought exquisitely alongside Nifsara but also specialized in rune magic herself.

Following the war, she and Nifsara would be stationed alongside Shula Valya in his new position as New Armes ambassador in Sol-Falena. It was said that her wisdom and grace soon won over the hearts of Falena's people.

name: Shigure
description: Shigure (シグレ, Shigure) is a supporting character in Suikoden V. Shigure is an investigator who was raised as an assassin by the covert group Nether Gate.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Shigure is a rough, plain-speaking young man who works as an investigator, along with Oboro and Sagiri, in the Oboro Detective Agency. Although he was an exceptionally lazy person, even by his own admission, he was also a skillful investigator well capable of operating in the shadows. Like Sagiri, Shigure had been previously trained from childhood to be an assassin for the covert group Nether Gate. 

Following the dismantlement of Nether Gate, Shigure was taken in by Oboro. In comparison to Sagiri's smile, Shigure was trained to be expressionless and had an extremely neutral voice. However, his concern and desire to protect Sagiri would sometimes allow him to break through this conditioning, showing the gentle, watchful side to his personality. He wears his bangs specifically to cover his eyes.

He would join the Imperial City Recapture Army along with Oboro. He would often fight with Nakula, due to Shigure's protectiveness regarding Sagiri. His talents in espionage were also no match for Zerase, who somehow spotted him on repeated occasions, causing him to quit that particular job in frustration.

Although he became the primary investigator for the Oboro Detective Agency after the Sun Rune War with the absence of Oboro, his lazy attitude remains as steadfast as ever.

name: Shinro
description: Shinro (シンロウ, Shinrou) is a minor character in Suikoden V. Shinro is a hard working merchant, hoping to make a big name for himself.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Shinro is a young man trying to follow in his grandfather, Sairoh's, footsteps as a great merchant. Shinro lost his parents at a young age, at around the same time his grandfather retired from the trading business himself. Training in Raftfleet, Shinro was a hard worker, but his conscientious personality and straight forward nature might make him ill-fitted for the merchant lifestyle. Still, Shinro doesn't let these flaws get in his way and, carrying the huge abacus given to him by his grandfather, continues to work at his craft.

He would prove useful to the Imperial City Recapture Army, joining the force during the Sun Rune War and using his talents to quickly distribute and sell goods from the places the army had visited in his store. His well-stocked store at Ceras Lake was frequently filled with customers.

Following the war, Boz Wilde's trade expansion policies enabled Shinro to build a trade route with the New Armes Kingdom, causing his business to take off in a flash. His grandfather would continue to gently assist with the business as well.

name: Shoon
description: Shoon (シュン, Shun) is a supporting character in Suikoden V. Shoon is a young gladiator who fights for a living in the arenas of Stormfist.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Shoon was a young gladiator with a strong sense of justice. Born in a village so small that it isn't marked on any maps, he volunteered to become a gladiator in order to use his proficient martial arts talents to help his poor family. As a gladiator, he learned to harbor antipathy towards the Falenan aristocracy for the abuse they heaped down on slaves and gladiators in the past while holding a measure of respect and trust towards the royal family for their part in curbing abuses.

It was this trust that led Shoon to sneak out of captivity to warn the Prince and his comrades of the whispers he had heard which suggested someone was planning to assassinate Queen Arshtat during the Sacred Games. Helping the Prince uncover the conspiracy, the two became friends and for the festival opening, Shoon would be chosen to duel the Prince in a special exhibition match.

Shoon would remain in captivity in Stormfist during the Sun Rune War but when the Imperial City Recapture Army attacked the city, he would help launch a surprise attack with the aid of his fellow gladiators and slaves and joined the rebel force, hoping to use his fists to carve out a future for the released gladiators. With the Godwin Faction crumbling, he was free to do as he wished without worrying about reprisals against his family. 

During his time with the army, he would continue to look up to Belcoot and Zegai, both of whom had strong reputations as gladiators themselves. He also got along well with Nikea as they both held an affinity for the martial arts.

Once the Sun Rune War ended, Shoon set out on a journey to find and rescue gladiators and slaves who were sold to other countries, all whilst continuing to train.

name: Shula Valya
description: Shula Valya (シュラ・ヴァルヤ, Shura Vuaruya) is a supporting character in Suikoden V. Shula Valya is the commander of the Armes Western Marine Corps and a member of the moderate Madra Clan in New Armes politics.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Shula Valya served as the young commander of the Armes Western Marine Corps. However, despite his relative youth, he was more than capable of leading such a unit, being a highly clever, savvy person as well skilled at political intrigue and diplomacy.

In addition, he was a scion of the prestigious Valya family, part of the moderate Madra Clan as well as the brother of Josephine (her real name being Yuma Valya). He was also assisted in his work by the highly capable Nifsara and Sharmista.

Initially during the New Armes Kingdom's entry into the Sun Rune War, he would be ordered to take the city of Estrise and to maintain a close watch over the Armes Southern Mountain Corps, in order to prevent any massacres from occurring. Realising that this war with Falena was not to Armes' benefit, he would treat Estrise as a vacation for his men before withdrawing peacefully. 

When New Armes withdrew from the war entirely, Shula would make overtures to the Imperial City Recapture Army, requesting to cooperate as a form of apology. The true reason behind this show of generosity, of course, was to prevent the dangerously belligerent Marscal Godwin from attacking New Armes with the power of the Sun Rune should he emerge victorious.

With the support of King Jalat and the clan chiefs, Shula would serve as an unofficial goodwill ambassador to the rebel forces. Joining the force, he would berate his sister, Josephine, for her antics, stating that if anyone discovered her identity she could jeopardize the Vayla family.

Following the war, Shula would work hard to forge a pact of friendship with the new Falenan government, becoming the New Armes ambassador to Sol-Falena.

name: Sialeeds
description: Sialeeds (サイアリーズ, Saiarīzu) is a major character in Suikoden V. Sialeeds is a princess of Falena and the aunt of the game's protagonist.

facts.label: Family
facts.value: Arshtat Falenas (sister), Falzrahm (mother), Haswar (cousin), Hero (Suikoden V) (nephew),

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Sialeeds was the younger daughter of Queen Falzrahm and her husband Kauss, a member of the Barows Faction. In her youth she was arranged to be married to Gizel Godwin but in the aftermath of the Falenan succession crisis and the Armes Invasion, the relatives of the newly crowned Arshtat agreed not to marry in order to avoid a repeat of those struggles. It is around this time that Sialeeds started dyeing her white hair, to distance herself from the royal line.

She served under her sister, performing inspections around the country and generally living a carefree, if slightly directionless, lifestyle. When the Sun Rune War broke out, she escaped with the Prince and Georg Prime, helping to form the Imperial City Recapture Army and providing counsel, particularly when it came to noble political matters, a subject in which she was well versed. She begrudgingly agreed that Lucretia Merces would be a valuable asset to the army, having harbored resentment towards the tactician for years due to her role in persuading Arshtat to bear the Sun Rune.

As the war dragged on, although she believed in her nephew's cause, she also decided that he lacked the nous to sweep away the corrupt political structure even if he should win the war. So, when Queen Lymsleia Falenas was on the verge of being rescued, Sialeeds would betray her comrades, taking her back to her Godwin captors. Sometime after, she would inherit the Twilight Rune from Alenia.

When the Prince's army marched on Stormfist, Sialeeds was sent to guard the city. Sialeeds used the Twilight Rune on behalf of her former allies, thwarting Childerich’s ambush in the Arena but also against them in battle, before returning to Sol-Falena. Following this, Sialeeds traveled to Rainwall, where she met with Salum and Euram Barows. There,…

name: Silva
description: Silva (シルヴァ, Shiruvua) is a supporting character in Suikoden V. Silva is a curt but compassionate doctor working overtime to help the people of the devastated Lordlake area.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Silva is a doctor who, following the razing of Lordlake by Arshtat's Sun Rune, forsook her medical license to violate orders and traveled to the withered city to treat its many patients. Possessed with a sober, severe personality, she also had a kind heart and true empathy for her patients which earned her great trust from the people she helped.

In her youth, she had studied medicine alongside Murad under a great doctor who instilled in both of them strict humanitarian principles which would influence her choices later in life. She traveled a lot when she was younger, never staying in one clinic for long. She volunteered to be an army doctor and saved lots of wounded troops on the battlefield, eventually meeting Galleon. The two were married but quickly divorced, their personalities just clashing too much to make their relationship viable.

After the destruction of Lordlake, she harbored a great deal of bitterness towards the royal family and treated the Prince and his comrades harshly when they were sent to the town by Arshtat for an inspection. Silva was not receptive to their platitudes, ordering them to get out of her infirmary.

Later, during the Sun Rune War, once the Prince helped the town recover, Silva accepted that the Prince was different from the other royals, and his mother, and agreed to serve as a doctor in the Imperial City Recapture Army now that Lordlake could recover, and supplies and treatment brought to the town easily. It is said that her talents as a battlefield medic saved many lives and contributed greatly to the Recapture Army's eventually victory in the war.

Following the war, Silva would turn down an invitation to become the Sun Palace's court physician and instead reopened her clinic in Lordlake. There, she continues to dedicate herself…

name: Skald Egan
description: Skald Egan (スカルド・イーガン, Sukarudo Īgan) is a supporting character in Suikoden V. Skald Egan is the admiral of the Island Nations Federation fleet as well as the father of Ferid.

facts.label: Family
facts.value: Bernadette Egan (daughter), Ferid (son), Hero (Suikoden V) (grandson), Six unnamed

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Skald Egan is the leader of the Island Nations Federation fleet and captain of the vessel Lino En Kuldes. He is also the father of eight children, including his youngest child, Bernadette, as well as Ferid. This would also make him the Prince's grandfather. Ferid's parentage, however, was kept quiet once he entered the Sacred Games in Falena in order to not bring up accusations of the Island Nations having undue influence in Falenan politics.

During the Sun Rune War, the Imperial City Recapture Army arranged a meeting with Skald on Nirva Island as the admiral traveled to Falena for Princess Lymsleia. The Recapture Army requested an alliance with the Island Nations Federation, represented by Skald, but the admiral turned the request down, citing the Obel Maritime Council's line on the matter. However, when he was impressed with how the Prince helped deal with a band of pirates threatening to burn down the Nirva Lighthouse, he did convince Bernadette to serve as his representative before continuing his journey to Sol-Falena.

As a leader, Skald carries himself with a very proactive type of attitude, driving himself and his officers hard but also treating them with dignity. He is also a very decisive figure and quick to decide on a course of action. This reputation was partly so great because Skald would play up these traits in order to manipulate people and events. For example, by pretending to have decided to burn the Nirva Lighthouse down in order to thwart the pirates, he forced Bernadette to accept the help of the Prince in capturing them instead. Similarly, his rash choice to take his navy into battle against the Godwin Faction was another ploy designed to have Bernadette quickly volunteer to join the Imperial City Recapture Army instead, proving that Skald was a…

name: SoLaTi
description: SoLaTi (ソラシ, Sorashi) is a minor character in Suikoden V. SoLaTi is one of Cornelio's rogue DoReMi Elves.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
SoLaTi was the blue-garbed member of Cornelio's DoReMi Elves. The conductor had captured each of them as part of his attempts to create a DoReMi orchestra. When the Prince inadvertently released the DoRemi Elves, SoLaTi was one of those who escaped.

SoLaTi was eventually found on the mountain path leading to the Beaver Lodge where they were successfully recaptured.

name: Solis Raulbel
description: Solis Raulbel (ソリス・ラウルベル, Sorisu Rauruberu) is a minor character in Suikoden V. Solis Raulbel is the popular, soft-spoken Lord of Sable.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Solis Raulbel is the head of the House of Raulbel who traditionally ruled the southern Falenan town of Sable. He has a wife, Melissa, and a daughter, Salisha. He was possessed of a soft-spoken demeanour unusual for a member of the aristocracy. However, he also had an inner strength that allowed him to stick to his convictions. He held the trust and loyalty of his people, including Dinn, due to his dedication to his noble duties and his disdain for the vices that had gripped other nobles such as Salum Barows.

During the Sun Rune War, once the Prince had cleared up the confusion regarding the bandit Roy's impersonation of him, Solis vowed to cooperate with the Imperial City Recapture Army, dispatching Dinn to the army as a representative of Sable.

Following the war, he gave his blessing to the union of Dinn and his daughter Salisha.

name: Sorensen
description: Sorensen (ソレンセン, Sorensen) is a supporting character in Suikoden V. Sorensen is the extremely dedicated apprentice of the great inventor, Babbage.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Sorensen is the heavily dedicated, selfless apprentice of the inventor, Babbage. Living in Babbage's Estrise workshop, Sorensen worshiped the eccentric inventor, dreaming of a day when he too would become a great inventor like his master. Holding an innocent personality, he had complete faith in Babbage, allowing even his master's most eccentric or selfish commands wash over him easily. A large part of his duties involved him taking care of Babbage's housework and meals while also cleaning up after Lu.

Sorensen, along with Babbage and Lu, first became involved in the Sun Rune War when the Prince paid the Estrise inventor a visit in hopes of getting him to help shut down the Revolving Bridge. Seeing that his invention was not used for the reason he had invented it, Babbage agreed, Sorensen and Lu following in tow. The three managed to remove an essential gear from the Revolving Bridge, thus disabling it. When they returned to Estrise, an impressed Prince asked Babbage to join his forces; Babbage turned him down but offered to send Sorensen in his stead. The Prince accepted, and Sorensen joined the Imperial City Recapture Army.

Later in the war, the Recapture Army would call upon Sorensen and Babbage’s help once again, this time in the taking of Hatred Fortress. The two created a mechanism that launched a barrel of oil into the fortress, starting a fire that would draw most of the Godwin forces away from Hatred Fortress before the fortress itself was overwhelmed by a tidal wave.

Further on in the war, once the water drained out of the lower level of the Sindar castle, a new room was discovered – and inside of it, a gear from the days of the Sindar. Growing excited at this prospect, Sorensen begged the prince to bring Babbage back so that his teacher could look at this…

name: Subala
description: Subala (スバル, Subaru) is a supporting character in Suikoden V. Subala is a Raftfleet fishing master and the arch-rival of Lun.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Subala was the self-proclaimed fishing master of Raftfleet. She was a rough, coarse, and energetic young woman who wasn't afraid to brag about her skills.

Those skills were ample as, despite her slender frame, Subala could pull up enormous fish in as if they were nothing. Her skill at fishing was well admired; the famous chef Retso even used to buy fish off of her to serve in his restaurant.

In her youth, she was a close friend of Lun but the two had a falling out and have been fierce rivals ever since. Subala claims that this animosity exists because as children, Lun stole Subala’s first love away. However, Lun denies this ever happening. Subala still treats Lun's mother, Kisara, with respect, however.

Subala became involved in the Sun Rune War upon encountering the Prince in Raftfleet while accompanied by Lun. Subala was already furious that Raftfleet had moved without anyone telling her while she was gone and seeing her rival with a pretty boy only increased her ire.

When the Prince asked her to join the Imperial City Recapture Army, Subala stated she would only join him if he could prove he was the better fisherman. Having an unfair advantage in Raftfleet, Subala offered to move the competition to Ceras Lake. Amazingly, he managed to beat her – and so Subala joined him, helping to feed her new allies with her proven fishing talents.

After the war ended, Subala returned to her job as a fisherwoman, this time in search of the Big One. Though one day she disappeared at sea, everyone's faith in her abilities reassures them that she's safe.

name: Takamu
description: Takamu (タカム, Takamu) is a minor character in Suikoden V. Takamu is a talented cartographer with a truly awe-inspiring sense of direction.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Takamu was a former court official, and cartographer blessed a truly exceptional sense of direction. Believing that maps should be made freely available to all, Takamu was ordered by Ferid, who agreed with his views, to create and publish the most detailed map of Falena to date.

Once the Queen's Assassination occurred, Takamu was arrested and his maps seized, the Godwin Faction maintaining that maps were breaches of national security. He later noted that the Barows Faction shared similar views on the matter. Imprisoned in Agate Prison, Takamu was eventually released by the Prince. Grateful, and wishing to repay Ferid's belief in the freedom of mapping, Takamu joined the Imperial City Recapture Army, presenting him with a map of Falena to aid him on his travels.

His maps are considered to be perfect, even Shigure was unable to find any flaws in his work. Also, his keen eye for detail and superb sense of direction meant that he would never get lost even in caves and forests. In fact, he was even able to navigate through elven wards in the Lunas North Woods, much to the shock of Isato, who demanded to know how he had done it. A stunned Takamu had no reply as he had used no tricks or magic, just relying on his senses and experience.

As a perfectionist, Takamu was always looking for opportunities to expand and improve his maps. He would ask Shoon to guide him around Stormfist's underground passages in order to improve his maps, but not before asking Egbert Aethelbald for assistance. He soon decided to ask Shoon instead, however, when forced to put up with Egbert's rants regarding Godwin plots and the like. He was also highly embarrassed that he had known nothing about the cave within the Big Hole that the Prince and Jeane had explored.

Following the war, he took on the…

name: Talgeyl
description: Talgeyl (タルゲイユ, Tarugeiyu) is a supporting character in Suikoden V. Talgeyl is a prominent figure in Lordlake, serving as its anchor point as the people struggle to survive.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Talgeyl had served as a former noble with a deep commitment to aiding and supporting the people of Lordlake following the devastating events of the Lordlake Uprising. Talgeyl once held the position of steward for Lord Rovere in Lordlake. However, after the town suffered destruction due to the power of the Sun Rune and Lord Rovere was executed for treason, Talgeyl renounced his noble status.

Initially overwhelmed by grief and despair, Talgeyl contemplated taking his own life. However, he reconsidered this course of action, realizing that his actions might lead others in Lordlake to follow suit in their distress. Instead, he used his natural leadership abilities, even without noble standing, to become a de facto leader for the people of Lordlake in the years that followed the tragic incident.

By the year SY 449, Talgeyl harbored resentment toward Queen Arshtat and the Falenan government for treating Lordlake as an outcast, subjecting it to harsh punishment and isolation at the hands of both the Barows and Godwin factions.

Nevertheless, he held no direct animosity toward the Prince, recognizing that the young ruler had no involvement in the events that transpired. When the Sun Rune War erupted, Talgeyl refused to align with the Prince, who was then allied with the Barows, as he believed Lordlake lacked the strength to participate in the conflict while facing its own ongoing struggles.

However, Talgeyl's perspective changed when he learned the truth behind the Lordlake Uprising. Filled with anger, he found himself at a loss for how to respond. It was only later, when the Prince successfully restored water to Lordlake through his actions, that Talgeyl became emotional, realizing the Prince's sincerity and determination to effect positive change. Inspired by the Prince's…

name: Taylor
description: Taylor (テイラー, Teirā) is a supporting character in Suikoden V. Taylor is a justice-seeking reporter who uses the truth as his weapon in fighting corruption.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Taylor is a journalist and news reporter known for his unusual and varied ways of gathering information. Taylor used to believe that a reporter had to impartial and fair at all times, but after being arrested in Stormfist for being too aggressive in his investigations into the faction, his view began to slowly change. Although his beliefs slowly shifted, he always maintained his journalistic integrity, balking at creating "propaganda pieces" no matter the cause.

After conducting some interviews with the Imperial City Recapture Army and realising the lack of press restrictions there, Taylor agreed to join their cause, creating the forces official newspaper, the Dawn Times, in order to counteract the Godwin propaganda emanating from Sol-Falena during the Sun Rune War. His reports on the Godwin razing of Lelcar proved vital in the securing of Doraat Fortress as it was the spread of this information via the Dawn Times that convinced a panicked populace threw open to gates to Doraat, throwing the Godwin Army into confusion.

Although he was dedicated to printing the truth whenever possible, Taylor was able to restrain himself for the sake of the army, for example, he never revealed the idea of a double for the Prince. He's known Oboro for a quite a while, the two often exchanging information, although as the two both have wildly different occupations, Oboro keeping secrets and Taylor exposing them, sometimes things would get a little awkward but the two were generally friends. 

Shula Valya noted that a man like Taylor could be worth over a hundred thousand soldiers. He also attempted to interview Georg Prime, Yahr and Richard as part of a special on foreigners fighting in the war but did not get many results. His planting of a news story in a special edition of the Dawn…

name: Toma
description: Toma (トーマ, Tōma) is a supporting character in Suikoden V. Toma is a young boy from Lordlake, filled with resentment over the town's fortunes.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Toma is an energetic, rough young boy from Lordlake. He lost his parents in the Lordlake riots and since then has harbored a deed grudge against the Falenan royal family for the destruction Queen Arshtat unleashed on their town.

During a royal inspection of Lordlake, Toma would go to the Withered Forest just outside town to look for water for the town's inhabitants. When Talgeyl and Goesch noticed the youth was missing, the Prince volunteered to find him. Discovering Toma had been ambushed by a pack of hungry Ulses, he was rescued quickly but bore no gratitude, stating that they weren't doing enough to help Lordlake considering its dire state.

Once the Sun Rune War broke out, the Prince would return to Lordlake to try and broker an alliance. After witnessing the true devastation of Lordlake, which prevented them from helping even if they wanted to, the Prince ran into Toma, who once again angrily demanded the Prince fix Lordlake. This time, the prince told him he was going to, and Toma demanded to be let along. Lyon had some reservations, but eventually gave in and allowed Toma to follow along.

Toma followed the Prince to the Ceras Lake Ruins, where they discovered the Sindar sluice gate. Toma followed along as the Prince and the others managed to use the sluice gate in the ruins to flood Hatred Fortress and bring life back to Lordlake. Impressed by the Prince’s dedication to Lordlake, Toma decided to tag along with the Prince until the war was over.

Toma is actually fairly popular among kids of his own age; in Lordlake, he is treated as their leader and all the younger kids call him their older brother. Toma is also Maroon's friend, as both travelled together for a time during the war. He has surprisingly good instincts and was even able to notice when Shigure…

name: Urda
description: Urda (ウルダ, Uruda) is a supporting character in Suikoden V. Urda is an elven archer with a disdain for humans.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Urda is a young elf from the hidden village of Alseid who left her homeland in order to find and bring Isato home. On the surface, she presents an ice-cold demeanor but when it comes to Isato, she can get very emotional. The two have been friends since childhood and it seems Urda had unspoken feelings towards him.

Although she harbored strong prejudice against humans, when Urda found out Isato had joined Haswar in the Imperial City Recapture Army, she too would join in order to stay close by. As part of the army, she would frequently get into arguments with human officers. She was particularly opposed to guns, arguing at length with Cathari on the matter.

After the Sun Rune War, Urda would return to Alseid without Isato but would frequently commute between her home and Lunas in order to visit him, her true feelings still hidden.

name: Volga
description: Volga (ヴォリガ, Vuoriga) is a supporting character in Suikoden V. Volga is an influential person in Lelcar local politics.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Volga held the influential informal position of "boss" over the eastern bank of Lelcar during the tumultuous Sun Rune War, a conflict that divided the town between various factions vying for control.

Despite his aggressive and fierce appearance and demeanor, Volga's true nature was that of a remarkably caring and compassionate individual. For many years, he dedicated himself to the well-being of orphans in the town, providing them with care and support in their vulnerable circumstances. Among the fortunate recipients of his benevolence was a young boy named Kyle, who, like many others, found solace and care under Volga's watchful eye.

As a leader in Lelcar, Volga found himself at odds with Orok, a fellow authority figure, over the administration and governance of the town. However, when the moment demanded unity and cooperation, Volga willingly set aside his differences with Orok and joined forces with him as they both became part of the Imperial City Recapture Army in response to the Godwins' abandonment of Lelcar.

Despite his outwardly assertive demeanor, Volga was not naturally confrontational. Rather, it was his unwavering commitment to his principles and beliefs that fueled his passion for maintaining his stance, even if it meant standing firm against prevailing opinions.

In the aftermath of the Sun Rune War, Volga's actions and character garnered him a substantial following of supporters who believed him the ideal candidate to assume the role of Mayor of Lelcar. Although reluctant to accept the position, Volga ultimately acquiesced to the wishes of his supporters, taking on the mantle of leadership with a sense of responsibility and dedication.

name: Wabon
description: Wabon (ワボン, Wabon) is a supporting character in Suikoden V. Wabon is the cantankerous, mining cart-obsessed cave dwarf of the Dwarf Camp.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Wabon was an elder cave dwarf who dedicated his life to building his tunnel that stretched from the Dwarf Camp all the way to the Deep Twilight Forest in the far west. His pride and joy was his long mine cart track that traveled at incredible speeds. 

Although the plans were devised by him, a lot of the work was performed by his apprentice Dongo, whom he had taught a great deal in the art of smithing. Over the course of the Sun Rune War, he tried to prevent Dongo from joining the Imperial City Recapture Army on several occasions, but eventually fired his apprentice once he grew tired of him, which Dongo interpreted as his "blessing".

When the Godwin Faction attempted to exterminate the dwarves, Wabon joined the Recapture Army and gave them safe haven within the Dwarf Camp until they could reclaim their headquarters. Following the Sun Rune War, he was offered the position of delegate to the New Falenan Parliament, but instead he passed the job on to Gunde and returned to working on his tracks, taking Bergen on as his new apprentice.

name: Wasil
description: Wasil (ワシール, Washīru) is a supporting character in Suikoden V. Wasil is an influential person in Lelcar local politics, providing a moderate influence between that of Orok and Volga.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Wasil served as the de facto leader of Lelcar's central islet as the town became increasingly divided along political lines in the run-up to the Sun Rune War. Wasil's diplomatic acumen helped keep the peace between the town's eastern and western islet who were often at odds with each other.

Neither a Barows nor a Godwin supporter, Wasil could more accurately be described as a supporter of the monarchy itself, much like Lord Rovere. In fact, following the Lordlake Uprising in SY 447, Wasil signed a petition to save him from execution to no avail.

Still, he continued to support the monarchy as best he could as well as helping to mediate between Orok and Volga with their opposing political views. As the Sun Rune War began to spread throughout Falena, Wasil sent a request to the Imperial City Recapture Army to help Lelcar out of its plight. With Lord De Beers having fled the town, the tensions in Lelcar had stretched to breaking point between supporters of various factions. His enthusiasm upon meeting the Prince and Kyle, however, practically blew their cover. 

Still, Wasil's work helped place the Recapture Army in a position to earn the trust of the citizens of Lelcar, first by rescuing several kidnapped 'draftees' to the Godwin Army and then by repelling Zahhak's forces which prepared to occupy the town. Swift action in dealing with the fire started by Zahhak to aid his retreat also earned the goodwill of the people. Following this, Wasil managed to forge an uneasy peace between Volga and Orok, the three of them agreeing to join the Prince's forces. 

There, he developed a crush on the Island Nations Federation officer, Bernadette Egan, noting that he had always had a 'thing' for Island Nations women. In the past, Wasil's father had business dealings with the trader,…

name: Wilhelm
description: Wilhelm (ヴィルヘルム, Vuiruherumu) is a supporting character in Suikoden V. Wilhelm is a captain within the Lindwurm Mercenary Brigade.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Wilhelm is the captain of the self-titled Wilhelm detachment of the Lindwurm Mercenary Brigade. Although he appeared to have an extremely frivolous attitude, he was also a capable leader, assisted by his deputy, Mueller.

He knew Mueller before becoming a member of the Lindwurm Mercenary Brigade, having cooperated on several jobs in the past, walking that fine line being mercenary work and out-and-out banditry. Early in the Sun Rune War, he was hired by the Barows Faction, fulfilling the terms of their contract to the letter. He would later be hired by the Prince directly, becoming part of the Imperial City Recapture Army.

Following the war, he had no shortage of work with his reputation having soared due to his efforts in the conflict. However, nowhere ever seemed to have enough women for him.

name: Yahr
description: Yahr (ヤール, Yāru) is a supporting character in Suikoden V. Yahr is an officer of the Island Nations Federation charged with the investigation and recovery of Rune Cannons.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Yahr was a secret intelligence officer serving in the navy of the Island Nations Federation. Charged with finding and recovering Rune Cannon components on behalf of the Federation, he is the direct superior of Nelis. Although he appears to be neglectful when it comes to work, he is actually highly skilled and has a particularly good memory for details.

Like Nelis, over time he grew to view Rune Cannons as having a negative influence on the world. He even went so far as to not report and even destroy rune cannons he found. Yahr originally started this task alone; however, Nelis was later assigned to help him.

Yahr would join the Prince along with Nelis when he helped them stop some pirates who were intent on making off with an old Rune Cannon found upon the missing Island Nations Federation vessel, the Kerzalik. After they had seen the power of the device, the Prince offered to help Nelis and Yahr destroy it. Nelis and Yahr were deeply impressed by this and decided to join the Imperial City Recapture Army in return.

Yahr enjoyed his time in Falena, learning about the new culture and even taking the time to watch some of his fellow countrywoman Chisato's puppet plays.

After the Sun Rune War ended, Yahr and Nelis returned to the service of the Island Nations, but not for long. Together, they snuck into the place where the rune shells were kept and destroyed them all. They quickly fled the Island Nations, but Skald Egan was said to be right on their heels.

name: Yoran
description: Yoran (ヨラン, Yoran) is a minor character in Suikoden V. Yoran is a gifted dragon horse caretaker studying in the Feitas Dragon Cavalry.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Yoran is a dragon horse caretaker trainee, undergoing his apprenticeship is Gordius. He is the best friend of Nick, a Dragon Cavalry trainee. Though he is the younger of the two, he is the one who looks after Nick, managing to keep him out of trouble. Although Yoran is very timid, he is also bright and caring. He is also a talented flutist, usually playing the less gifted Nick's flute for him.

Yoran and Nick become involved in the Sun Rune War when Godwin forces stormed Gordius, taking the dragon eggs hostage and thus stopping the Dragon Cavalry from interfering in the war even after the Godwin forces allied with Armes.

Ironically enough, it would be Nick’s terrible flute playing that would come to save the day as Nick’s playing caused such a diversion that Georg Prime was able to get between the Godwin agent Dolph and the eggs, thus neutralizing the Godwin threat and allowing Craig Laden to ally his force with the Imperial City Recapture Army.

Along with the senior members of the Dragon Calvary, Yoran joined the army, becoming the caretaker to all the dragon horses there. Yoran got along quite peacefully with most of the castle and ran into few difficulties.

However, Yoran had spent almost all his time in Gordius, leading him to be rather inexperienced when it came to interacting with women. Yoran also had troubles with Haleth gambling on the dragon horse races. However, once he saw how the riders were improving, he became at ease with it.

After the war ended, Yoran would go to Sauronix Castle to tend to the dragon horses there. It is said that he treats them with great care and earns their respect.

name: Zahhak
description: Zahhak (ザハーク, Zahāku) is an antagonist in Suikoden V. He is a member of Falena's Queen's Knights who supports the policies of the Godwin Faction.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Zahhak was a skilled swordsman who served in the Queen's Knights. He was distantly related to the Godwin Faction and it was Marscal Godwin himself who noted Zahhak's excellent talents with a sword and nominated him to the Queen's Knights. It was said his skill with a sword was only second to those of Georg Prime and the Commander of the Queen's Knights, Ferid. His calm judgement was said to be another of his key attributes that led him to be a talented Queen's Knight.

He agreed with many of the Godwin's social and political beliefs and so would betray the royal family, participating in the Godwin coup at the Sun Palace which ignited the Sun Rune War. Zahhak's cold nature meant that he was fully prepared to sacrifice innocents in order to achieve his objectives, such as when he razed the town of Lelcar in order to stymie the advance of the Imperial City Recapture Army.

Remaining a member of the Queen's Knights, now under the command of Gizel Godwin, Zhahak would clash with the Imperial City Recapture Army several times over the course of the war. He would be defeated alongside Alenia in the final battle as the attempted to protect the Sun Palace. He would perish from the effects of the Raging Nostrum he took liberally before the battle.

name: Zegai
description: Zegai (ゼガイ, Zegai) is a supporting character in Suikoden V. Zegai is a powerful and fearsome gladiator, enrolled as part of the Barows Faction' plan to secure victory in the Sacred Games.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Zegai was born a wandering nomad. His community claimed no land as their home but as he was born in the territories of the New Armes Kingdom, he was designed an Armesian by both those in Armes and elsewhere.

One day, Zegai crossed the border into Falena while hunting and was soon sighted by guards who attempted to arrest him. This arrest attempt soon turned into a huge battle. What had started as a small patrol grew to over a hundred men, and eventually the warrior was subdued and imprisoned in Barows territory. While he may have been locked in prison, his parole came quickly as word spread of his superb fighting skills.

Salum Barows would take Zegai on as a gladiator, set on using him to win the Sacred Games for his son, Euram. Shoon especially admired Zegai's skills during their time together in the Stormfist Arena. Zegai was later involved in a plot by the Godwin Faction, which ended in his disqualification as a contestant for the Sacred Games. Instead of being executed as a spy and traitor, Zegai was assisted by the Prince and freed; in return, Zegai later helped the Prince escape from the Sun Palace during the Queen's Assassination incident in Sol-Falena.

Later during the Sun Rune War, Zegai reacquainted himself with the Prince north of Lunas where he was hunting wild animals before agreeing to join the Imperial City Recapture Army. Urda detested Zegai for his hunting and his actions didn't improve her opinions of humans. During the war, Zegai proved to be an extremely effective fighter on the battlefield. He also served as a bujutsu teacher, giving new insight into his soldiers' own abilities. 

Once the war ended, Zegai returned to the wild nomadic lifestyle he had always known.

name: Zerase
description: Zerase (ゼラセ, Zerase) is a major character in Suikoden V. Zerase is a supremely powerful magician and bearer of the unique Star Rune. She serves as something of a guardian for the three runes of Falena, possibly due to her connection to the Night Rune.

sections.title: Appearance
sections.body:
Zerase is a pale, slender woman clad exclusively in black with gold trim. According to designer concept art, Zerase's long black hair reaches to the small of her back where it is tied together in a low, loose ponytail. She has cool blue eyes, emphasised with purple eye shadow. Under her flowing, dark robes, she wears a sheer dress, held in place by her choker. On each wrist she wears five dark bangles.

Zerase also carries a muff hand-warmer, dangling from her neck by a chain, which she normally keeps her hands in unless she is attacking or using a rune. Her feet are never seen.

In battle, Zerase wields a long, pointed needle which she throws at her enemies with her right hand. She apparently summons these needles with a flick of her wrist. The Star Rune is attached to her forehead according to game mechanics but she has no marking to indicate this, although they may be covered by her fringe.

sections.title: Personality
sections.body:
Zerase is secretive and holds a sharp tongue, being quick to ready a barb or admonishment towards friend or foe for their shortcomings. She has a strong sense of duty regarding the safeguarding of the three runes of Falena, especially the Sun Rune itself.

It is this that leads her to be quick to insult the royal family for what she sees as their lapse in their duties and to state that she would not hesitate to kill the Prince of Falena if he proved to be unworthy of the Dawn Rune and to attempt to remove the Twilight Rune from Alenia and then Sialeeds.

Keeping her knowledge close to her chest, Zerase rarely provided answers unless she felt it directly important to her goals. That said, she is never actively dishonest, preferring to bluntly admit that she will not answer questions rather than obscuring the truth with lies. Her curtness comes off as rude.

When Lyon was vitally wounded in the New Queen's Campaign, Zerase refused to help, stating that the Dawn Rune simply wasn't powerful enough in its current state to heal her. Although dismissive, this turned out to be exactly the case, allowing the Prince to eventually increase the ability of the rune and heal Lyon.

As Zerase's greatest concern is that of the three runes of Falena, her greatest emotional outbursts come when those runes are misused. Although she was dismissive of Alenia for being "unworthy" of the Twilight Rune, she was also upset at its misuse. This only intensified when the rune chose Sialeeds as its bearer and was able to wield much more of the rune's powers.

Her attitude towards the Prince would soften slightly as she came to believe he was a worthy host for the Dawn Rune, further empathizing how her attitude centered around the three runes. When the three runes were restored to their proper…

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Zerase is a mysterious magician with intimate knowledge of the three runes of Falena, the Night Rune, as well as the Sindar civilisation. She is also the bearer of the Star Rune, a rune born from the Night Rune, which was stated by Levi to be a unique, one-of-a-kind rune, like the Dawn Rune itself. This rune, as well as her knowledge of the Celestial Sword makes it likely she has some connection to the Night Rune.

Leknaat regarded her as a "traveler of the night and bearer of stars" and as someone who could see "the stars that guide people's destinies". The two apparently know each other, with Leknaat exercising a sense of authority over the magician.
Early Appearances and Warnings
thumb|220px|right|Zerase demands to know the location of the Dawn Rune.Zerase first appeared to the Prince, Sialeeds and Lymsleia at the East Palace, where the Dawn Rune had been housed prior to the Lordlake Uprising. Demanding to know if the rune had been found, Zerase chided the Falenan royal family for its incompetence, much to Lymsleia's anger, before disappearing without a trace.

She would not be seen again until the Prince and his party had fled to Rainwall, following the Queen's Assassination. She was seen in the town, apparently attempting to concentrate on the Dawn Rune's location and later, when the Prince uncovered the Dawn Rune in the Barows' mansion, Zerase appeared once more, remarking that the Prince had better live up to the expectations of the rune, or she would kill him before vanishing once again.
The Twilight Rune and Alenia
As the Sun Rune War began, the Sindar ruins at Ceras Lake had been uncovered and chosen as the headquarters of the Imperial City Recapture Army, Zerase appeared following the vision shown in the sealed room at the top of the ruins. She would…

name: Zunda
description: Zunda (ズンダ, Zunda) is a minor character in Suikoden V. Zunda is the gruff and responsible, perhaps the only responsible, cave dwarf of Falena's Dwarf Camp.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Zunda is one of the cave dwarves living in the Dwarf Camp. Zunda's responsibility lies in preventing cave-ins as well as in the creation of 'air holes' in the caverns that are dug by the male cave dwarves. She took on this job because dwarven men usually are so obsessed with their work that they are known to pass out from lack of air from time to time. Because of how well she takes care of all the dwarves, they all refer to her as 'Sis' as a sign of affection.

During the Sun Rune War, Zunda demanded that the Prince show her an example of human windows, something she was not all that familiar with. She became so interested in them that she joined the Imperial City Recapture Army in order to study windows better. Initially she had little respect for humans, typically referring to everyone as just 'human', but her attitude would gradually change over the course of the war.

Following the war, Zunda became the dwarven leader since she reasoned that none of the men were responsible enough to take such a role seriously.

name: Zweig
description: Zweig (ツヴァイク, Tsuvuaiku) is an important character in Suikoden V. Zweig is an archaeologist harboring an intense intellectual curiosity regarding the Sindar.

sections.title: Story
sections.body:
Zweig is an archeologist who specializes in studying and researching the Sindar civilization. Over the years, he had researched and explored many Sindar ruins in the Queendom of Falena while working in academia on the matter. Unlike Lorelai and Killey, Zweig insists that his interest in the Sindar is purely academic, though he is just as obsessed as they are with the idea of the Sindar. He is also proficient with a bo staff, which aids his explorations of ancient Sindar ruins.

Zweig was invited to Marscal Godwin's gathering of Sindar experts just prior to the outbreak of the Sun Rune War, but, suspicious of such an invitation, he turned it down. Lucretia Merces heard about this and hired the Oboro Detective Agency to track him down in secret. They were not the only ones who tracked him down, however – it was thanks to their tracking of Zweig that Zweig met Lorelai, who would pester him about his knowledge of the Sindar, though he would refuse to share much with the young girl. Zweig actually had a secret laboratory somewhere in Falena where he kept all of his research materials on the Sindar. Lorelai constantly demanded for him to take her there, but he played ignorant with her, insisting that such a thing didn’t exist.

After becoming the Imperial City Recapture Army's strategist, Lucretia directed them to Zweig to go explore the Ceras Lake ruins that Zweig had discovered. After exposing the castle submerged there, Zweig decided to join the force in order to study it. While serving in the army, he would meet Killey, who told him a story about the Crimson Pilgrims. 

Analyzing Killey’s story, Zweig thought he had uncovered the home of the Crimson Pilgrims, and after the war, followed Killey’s story; however, all he found was a single, towering tree. Adding to his bad…

name: Crow
description: The Crow (クロウ, Kurō) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 30 · DEX 70 · DEF 7 · SPD 27 · MAG 5 · LCK 24

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This ferocious crow appeared in the Arus region early in the Gate Rune War, attacking people. Although fast and capable of striking a target with a quick, nimble strike, it was not really strong enough to pose much of a threat to anyone but the most defenseless people.

name: Mosquito
description: The Mosquito (モスキート, Mosukīto or ビックモスキート, Biggu Mosukīto) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 30 · DEX 50 · DEF 6 · SPD 29 · MAG 0 · LCK 35

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This mosquito monster, which somehow became huge, could be found around Gregminster in the early days of the Gate Rune War. It sucked the blood of travelers through its needle-sharp mouth. Still, due to its low physical strength, it was said to be easy to defeat.

name: Furball
description: The Furball (ふさふさ, Fusafusa) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden. In the original 1996 English language localization, it was known as the BonBon.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 31 · DEX 5 · DEF 5 · SPD 16 · MAG 9 · LCK 12

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Defective Urn, Octopus Pot, Celadon Urn

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A mysterious creature that lived around Gregminster in the early days of the Gate Rune War. It attacks by ramming, but reports suggest it is quite weak and would not challenge any adventurer seriously in combat.

Furballs are one of the earliest monsters encountered in the game. It is generally not a threat, even to a lone protagonist at the beginning of the game, unless partnered with more dangerous foes.

trivia: * "Fusafusa" (ふさふさ), this enemy's Japanese name, is a Japanese onomatopoeia used to denote fluffiness.

name: Boar
description: The Boar (いのしし, Inoshishi) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 65 · DEX 15 · DEF 19 · SPD 20 · MAG 0 · LCK 14

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: Believed to be feral wild boar, as its name implies. Wild Boar had been witnessed in Arus during the Gate Rune War and infested the trail to Tsai's house during the Dunan Unification War. These two types of boar are assumed to be both the same species. Although they don't use any particular attack, their powerful charges are enough to blow away most any target.

trivia:
* The Wild Boar is the first of three boar-type enemies in Suikoden. The others being the Black Wild Boar and the Death Boar.
* It can be difficult to distinguish between the Wild Boar and the Black Wild Boar due to their similar appearance and the short length of time between the introduction of one, then the other. They are practically identical, except for the Black Wild Boar having slightly higher LCK and a reduced item drop rate.

name: Red Soldier Ant
description: The Red Soldier Ant (赤兵隊アリ, Akaheitaiari) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 48 · DEX 18 · DEF 10 · SPD 22 · MAG 0 · LCK 3

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Red Soldier Ant was a crimson ant creature characterized by its exoskeletal structure. These creatures were frequently observed in the vicinity of Mt. Seifu during the Gate Rune War, where they formed swarms and posed a threat to travelers and adventurers.

Despite not possessing considerable physical strength, the Red Soldier Ants compensated for this with their numbers and coordinated attacks. While individual encounters may not have proven challenging for seasoned adventurers, the ants could become formidable adversaries for those caught off guard or unprepared.

trivia: * The Red Soldier Ant appears earlier in the game than the monster its a palate swap of, the Soldier Ant.

name: Holly Boy
description: The Holly Boy (ひいらぎこぞう, Hīragikozō) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 29 · DEX 5 · DEF 7 · SPD 15 · MAG 10 · LCK 70

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Holly Boy is not a very difficult enemy to defeat and generally didn't enjoy fighting, being prone to retreating from the battlefield. They were dismissively said to be as strong as they look.

Holly Boys usually appear in a group, with other Holly Boys on Magician's Island and under the command of Holly Spirits in the Great Forest. The Holly Boys on Magician's Island are weaker than those in the Great Forest but neither provide anything approaching a challenge.

name: FurFur
description: The FurFur (もさもさ, Mosamosa) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 31 · DEX 5 · DEF 5 · SPD 16 · MAG 7 · LCK 5

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: A hairy monster that lives on Magician's Island. It tackles its enemy, but its fur acts as a cushion and so it doesn't deal much damage on impact. What it looks like under its thick fur coat is unknown.

trivia: * "Mosamosa" (もさもさ) is a Japanese onomatopoeia used to represent the sound of plants growing rapidly.

name: Clay Doll
description: The Clay Doll (クレイドール, Kureidōru, Clay Doll) is an enemy in the first Suikoden. The Clay Doll is the first boss of the game and is encountered on Magician's Island.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 40 · DEX 21 · DEF 2 · SPD 17 · MAG 25 · LCK 20

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This Clay Doll was summoned by Luc with the power of his True Wind Rune in order to "test" McDohl and his entourage following their arrival on Magician's Island in order to retrieve Leknaat's Astral Predications.

The Clay Doll's attacks included punching the ground to hit a foe with a tremor as well as throwing its boulder-like head at an opponent. Its defeat forced Luc to admit that McDohl and his entourage were the real deal and to escort them to Leknaat.

trivia: * In the 1996 English language localization of Suikoden, this enemy's name was localized as the Golem. In the Japanese version and the 2025 English localization of Suikoden I&II HD Remaster, it and the Clay Doll share the same name. * A golem is a being from Jewish mythology. It is a statue made of clay which is turned to life by means of magic.

name: Soldier Ant
description: The Soldier Ant (兵隊アリ, Heitaiari) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 48 · DEX 18 · DEF 10 · SPD 22 · MAG 0 · LCK 6

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Soldier Ant was a large ant monster. It was confirmed that many lived at Mt. Seifu, near Gregminster during the early Gate Rune War. They always appear in groups and attack simultaneously with their spears. However, the strength of a single ant was very low.

It is similar to the Red Soldier Ant with no meaningful ability difference, easy to defeat even for a fledgling adventurer.

trivia: * Although described as being basically identical to its counterpart, the Red Soldier Ant, the Soldier Ant has a slightly higher LUCK stat, but gives out less potch.

name: Bandit
description: Bandits (山賊, Sanzoku) are an enemy which appear in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 49 · DEX 15 · DEF 6 · SPD 18 · MAG 14 · LCK 18

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These common bandits attacked travelers in different mountain paths across the land, stealing their money, their goods, and sometimes even their lives. There are nothing exceptional as fighters, unable to use runes and carrying either a sword or bow into combat.

name: Black Wild Boar
description: The Black Wild Boar (黒イノシシ, Kuro inoshishi) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 65 · DEX 15 · DEF 19 · SPD 20 · MAG 0 · LCK 20

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This variation on the Wild Boar had been witnessed around Mt. Seifu. These breed of boar is assumed to be the same species as the Wild Boar, although it is slightly more vicious than its counterpart that roams outside Gregminster.

trivia:
* It can be difficult to distinguish between the Black Wild Boar and the Wild Boar due to their similar appearance and the short length of time between the introduction of one, then the other. They are practically identical, except for the Wild Boar having slightly lower LUCK and an increased item drop rate.
* The Black Wild Boar is not black, although its later palate swap, the Death Boar is.

name: Queen Ant
description: The Queen Ant (クィーンアント, Kwīnanto) is an enemy in the first Suikoden. The Queen Ant first appears as the game's first unwinnable battle on Mt. Seifu and is later encountered as a regular enemy in Seek Valley.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 75 · DEX 25 · DEF 50 · SPD 20 · MAG 55 · LCK 55

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Queen Ant is a giant monster with the upper body of a humanoid female attached to the lower body of a flying ant. They were considered highly intelligent as they were capable of leading and commanding groups of lesser ants.

Incredibly powerful, these creatures are usually seen around the Seek Valley area but during the Gate Rune War, one marked its territory on Mt. Seifu and began using the caves there as a nest, forcing Ted to use the Soul Eater to blow it away.

name: Imperial Soldier
description: The Imperial Soldier (ていこくへい, Teikokuhei) is an enemy in the first Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 65 · DEX 40 · DEF 7 · SPD 15 · MAG 10 · LCK 12

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Headband, Defective Urn, Flower Vase, Blue Dragon Urn, Chest Plate

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Imperial Soldiers are often found in large groups of four or more but usually do not pose a threat to the party unless you are already injured or low on spells. There are ten distinct Imperial Soldiers in the game, encountered in four different areas.

The first four are encountered in Rockland when infiltrating Grady's mansion, the next three are met in the sewers of Lenankamp shortly thereafter, two are fought in Seika and the final type are fought in the military commander's mansion in Kouan.

They are sometimes accompanied by an Imperial Captain. These units were frequently stationed around the Lake Toran area and used a variety of weapons in combat. Female troops were also present among the soldiers. Soldiers such as these formed the majority of the the Imperial Army's strength.

name: Imperial Captain
description: The Imperial Captain (たいちょう, Taichō, Commanding Officer) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 95 · DEX 45 · DEF 15 · SPD 10 · MAG 15 · LCK 25

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These Imperial captains commanded members of the Imperial Army assigned to protect the military government of Rockland. They are chosen out of the rank and file Imperial soldiers due to their excellent leadership and combat abilities.

Their skill with a sword in particular hampered the efforts of McDohl as he tried to rescue Varkas and Sydonia.

name: Blade Bunny
description: The Blade Bunny (カットバニー, Kattobanī, Cut Bunny) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 65 · DEX 30 · DEF 10 · SPD 29 · MAG 6 · LCK 6

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These vicious and intelligent rabbits live in the heights of Mt. Tigerwolf. Wielding hand-axes, they are able to use them akin to boomerangs, flinging them at their targets before catching them on the rebound. They usually appear in large numbers.

trivia: * In Japanese, they share the same name as the Suikoden II enemy Blade Bunny and the Suikoden III enemy BladeBunny.

name: Killer Slime
description: Killer Slime (這い寄る粘菌, Haiyoru Neneki, Creeping Slime) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 72 · DEX 20 · DEF 34 · SPD 22 · MAG 55 · LCK 23

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This liquid creature was found on Mt. Tigerwolf. It changes its appearance to get closer to its prey and attacks when its target is off guard. Still, it is said that few adventurers struggled against it regardless due to its low stamina.

Water Rune Pieces could sometimes be found its remains.

trivia: * Due to technical limitations on the Sega Saturn, the Killer Slime in the Saturn version of the game no longer has any transparency properties.

name: Giant Snail
description: The Giant Snail (マイマイ, Maimai, Snail) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 95 · DEX 35 · DEF 24 · SPD 22 · MAG 18 · LCK 30

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: Found on Mt. Tigerwolf, this giant snail catches prey by stretching its neck. It is said that those who defeated this monster could sometimes obtain a Defense Rune Piece.

name: Killer Bunny
description: Killer Bunny (ころしやうさぎ, Koroshiyausagi) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 90 · DEX 30 · DEF 15 · SPD 29 · MAG 7 · LCK 7

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Hex Doll, Japanese Dish, Peeing Boy

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These dark-furred, axe-wielding rabbits infested the Gouran region during the Gate Rune War, attacking from a distance with their throwing axes. There are reports that they carry various antiques on their person.

trivia: * It shares the same Japanese name as Suikoden III's RabidRabbt.

name: Flying Squirrel
description: The Flying Squirrel (むささび, Musasabi) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 99 · DEX 40 · DEF 25 · SPD 35 · MAG 9 · LCK 30

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Flying Squirrels are known to many people due to its broad habitation range and were witnessed during both the Gate Rune War and the Dunan Unification War. They are believed to be hostile, and have a surprising mean streak.

While flapping its cloak, they swoop towards their target to attack. They also commit pranks by covering the target's head with a bucket.

trivia: * The Flying Squirrel is based on a real-life animal, the [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Japanese_giant_flying_squirrel Japanese giant flying squirrel].

name: Beast Commander
description: The Beast Commander (ターグットレディ, Tāguttoredi, Target Lady) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 60 · DEX 30 · DEF 40 · SPD 70 · MAG 35 · LCK 60

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The Beast Commander is a female-shaped monster that appeared in the Gouran region during the Gate Rune War. They were usually spotted with groups of Flying Squirrels, giving them commands and identifying foes for them to target. They rarely engage in attacks themselves.

trivia: * The Beast Commander shares the same Japanese name as Suikoden II's Target Lady and Suikoden III's Nariqua.

name: Roc
description: The Roc (ロック, Rokku) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 125 · DEX 55 · DEF 55 · SPD 60 · MAG 16 · LCK 20

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This incredibly large bird monster lived in the Gouran region. According to eyewitness accounts, the bird was so huge that a cow looked the size of a field mouse when compared to it.

It would swoop down from the sky and attack its prey with its sharp, pointed beak. It was known to be weak against wind-based attacks.

trivia: * The Roc's Japanese name is identical to that of original Suikoden character Roc.

name: Giant Slug
description: The Giant Slug (スラッグ, Surragu, Slug) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 95 · DEX 35 · DEF 35 · SPD 22 · MAG 15 · LCK 26

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These giant snails lived in the Toran Lake Castle. Their bodies are covered by a highly durable gem-like shell and but their retractable necks mean they can attack their prey from a distance without approaching the enemy. Their weakness was fire magic.

trivia: * Slugs can in fact have shells, although not as depicted in the Giant Slug's sprite. They are usually very small, vestigial or internalized however. Semislugs have larger shells which are not quite vestigial but are still too small to retract into.

name: Oannes
description: Oannes (さかなモンスター, Sakana Monsutā or バトルフィッシュ, Batorufisshu) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 90 · DEX 10 · DEF 30 · SPD 45 · MAG 17 · LCK 12

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This monstrous fish was spotted at Toran Lake Castle. The appearance of a gigantic fish with limbs was truly alarming. It stabbed opponents with the trident it held, fighting to repel intruders within the castle.

It was said that even a novice adventurer could easily defeat it by attacking it with fire magic, which is its weak point.

name: Ghost Armor
description: Ghost Armor (亡霊ヨロイ, Bourei Yoroi) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 110 · DEX 30 · DEF 30 · SPD 40 · MAG 35 · LCK 27

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This armor-type monster that attacked those who entered Toran Lake Castle in the early days of the Gate Rune War. It is said that each part of the eerie, floating armor attacked independently.

As can be inferred from its name, it was believed to be the ghost of a warrior who died on the battlefield. It was rumored to have been a warrior of some renown because of its shining armor. Like many other undead monsters, it was weak to holy magic.

name: Zombie Dragon
description: The Zombie Dragon (ドラゴンゾンビ, Doragonzonbi) is an enemy in the first Suikoden. The Zombie Dragon first appears as the fifth boss of the game and is fought at the Toran Lake Castle.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 175 · DEX 50 · DEF 35 · SPD 65 · MAG 130 · LCK 40

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This zombified dragon spewed blue flames, burning down any intruders who approached his lair. This beast was summoned by Imperial General Sonya Shulen as a trap to prevent Toran Lake Castle from being occupied by lake pirates.

Its skin and wings were decomposing although, as expected of a former dragon, its tremendous strength had not diminished in the slightest.

Because this dragon was undead, it is said that its weak point was fire and holy-type attacks. This Zombie Dragon seems to have been the cause of the fog enveloping Toran Castle with it disappearing upon its defeat.

name: Robot Soldier
description: Robot Soldiers (機械兵, Kikaihei, Machine Soldier) are an enemy which appear in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 135 · DEX 20 · DEF 70 · SPD 45 · MAG 0 · LCK 0

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Defective Urn, Flower Vase, Persian Lamp

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These trickster contraptions, each equipped with a sword or spear, were designed to guard Lepant's mansion in Kouan. They were not capable of complex behavior or thought and so would attempt to fight off any intruders incessantly.

Its strong armor was difficult to break through, thought it was weak to lightning magic. The spear-wielding soldier was also considered slightly stronger and more skilled than its sword-wielding counterpart.

It is unknown if Juppo or another trickster was responsible for these particular machines.

name: Slot Man
description: Slot Man (スロットマン, Surottoman) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 180 · DEX 70 · DEF 50 · SPD 80 · MAG 110 · LCK 30

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Defective Urn, Flower Vase, Persian Lamp

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These strange creatures appeared in Lepant's mansion during the Gate Rune War. They were faces and limbs which protruded from treasure chests, giving them a most bizarre appearance. It is possible they were trickster creations of Juppo.

Their attacks were determined by slots, making them unpredictable, and they could use both attack and recovery magic. Rare antiques could occasionally be found inside their chests after their defeat.

name: Assassin
description: The Assassin (アサシン, Asashin) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 120 · DEX 60 · DEF 9 · SPD 35 · MAG 30 · LCK 50

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The name Assassin is a widely used alias for many different hired killers. This particular assassin, clad in a ninja-like costume, was hired by Windy during the Gate Rune War to attempt to murder the hero but the attempt was unsuccessful although the still-young hero was unable to defeat his attacker directly. The assassin fled once the alerted Toran Liberation Army came to help.

trivia: * The Assassin retreats after 5 turns, making it very unlikely you will whittle down his disproportionately high HP.

name: Kobold
description: Kobolds (コボルト, Koboruto) appear as an enemy in the first Suikoden.

facts.label: Location
facts.value: Great Forest, Area C (Great Forest region)

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 150 · DEX 40 · DEF 48 · SPD 49 · MAG 20 · LCK 35

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Kobolds were generally known as a gentle, friendly race but these kobolds had their wild blood awakened by the power of the Conqueror Rune. Operating as bandits, they seemed to regard anyone in the forest as an invader, needlessly and viciously attacked travellers around the Great Forest region during the Gate Rune War.

They are unable to be reasoned with and wielded a variety of weapons, including short swords and bows. They held a weakness to fire magic, save for the mages, possibly due to their fur. Kobold mages were rare but those that existed were skilled and adept at defending against magic attacks.

name: Holly Spirit
description: Holly Spirit (ひいらぎの精, Hīragi no Sei) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 150 · DEX 55 · DEF 55 · SPD 65 · MAG 60 · LCK 70

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These holly creatures were sighted in the Great Forest during the Gate Rune War and the Greenhill Forest during the Dunan Unification War. Looking like a person dressed in holly, these creature's attacks can inflict the Balloon status on unaware foes.

trivia: * They have the same Japanese name as the Suikoden II's Holly Elf.

name: Strong Arm
description: The Strong Arm (ストロングアーム, Sutoronguāmu) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Location
