facts.value: Great Forest, Area C (Great Forest region)

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 200 · DEX 50 · DEF 60 · SPD 40 · MAG 20 · LCK 29

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Strong Arms are a breed of monster who had infested the region around the Great Forest and the Village of the Elves during the Gate Rune War. Either its confidence or its sturdy body stature leads to it being draped in only a piece of cloth. 

t has a relatively high life force as well as formidable physical strength, being able to physically throw its allies at the enemy as well as generating shock waves, but Earth attribute attacks are effective against this creature.

name: Stray Dwarf
description: The Stray Dwarf (はぐれドワーフ, Hagure Dowāfu, Stray Dwarf) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 170 · DEX 58 · DEF 55 · SPD 56 · MAG 25 · LCK 36

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This belligerent dwarf roamed the Dwarf Trail. As soon as he found his target, he closed the gap and attacked with a forceful swing of his single-edged hatchet.

Dwarves are usually an amiable enough people, but some of them were known to be violent. Like the Crimson Dwarves, they were strong opponents, but their weakness was wind, which made them easy to defeat.

name: Eagle Man
description: The Eagle Man (イーグルマン, Īguruman) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 165 · DEX 75 · DEF 55 · SPD 68 · MAG 40 · LCK 40

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Eagle Man's name comes from the fact that it looks like an anthropomorphic eagle. Their main habitat is around the Village of the Dwarves in the Toran Republic but they have also been seen in the Kobold Village forests during the Dunan Unification War. There is not much difference recognized between the two but it is said that the Eagle Man found in the Kobold Village forest have greater agility.

These Eagle Men attack with powerful kicks from their legs, using their wings to float in the air as they strike. Some have reported looting paintings from defeated foes, indicating that they may hold some sort of artistic taste.

name: Wild Boar
description: The Wild Boar (ワイルドボー, Wairudobō) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 182 · DEX 40 · DEF 45 · SPD 35 · MAG 10 · LCK 25

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This monster, which inhabited the Dwarf Trail during the Gate Rune War is known for its high amount of life force. It has weak magic attacks, but vigilance is needed as like the Wild Boars, it has a powerful rushing attack.

It has low resistance to most magical elements so it is recommended to exploit this in battle. It is reported that Boar Rune Orbs have been found in the remains of this monster.

trivia: * The Wild Boar is the third, and most powerful, of the three boar-type enemies in Suikoden. Its compatriots are the Boar and the Black Wild Boar.

name: Crimson Dwarf
description: The Crimson Dwarf (あばれドワーフ, Abare Dowāfu, Fighting Dwarf) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 175 · DEX 45 · DEF 35 · SPD 60 · MAG 27 · LCK 38

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These Crimson Dwarves have been sighted in the Dwarves' Vault during the Gate Rune War. While dwarves are known to be an amicable race and usually very seldom attack people, more violent dwarves do exist. During the Gate Rune War, many such rogue dwarves were witnessed. Wielding a hand ax, this difficult enemy is certainly a skillful warrior.

trivia: * The Crimson Dwarf is simply a palate swap of the enemy Dwarf.

name: Death Machine
description: Death Machines (デスマシーン, Desumashīn) are an enemy which appear in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 180 · DEX 40 · DEF 60 · SPD 55 · MAG 0 · LCK 0

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Designed to guard the interior of the Dwarves' Vault, these mechanical soldiers are well protected by their metal bodies, giving them considerable durability. These robust machines wield both sword and spear. Their weakness to lightning magic would prove to be their undoing, however.

The spear-equipped machines in particular have rarely been known to carry Trick Orbs on their person. Some theorize that they are in fact creations of the dwarves.

name: Gigantes
description: The Gigantes (ギガンテス, Gigantesu) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden. The Gigantes is the fifth boss of the game and is encountered at the bottom of the Dwarves' Vault.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 260 · DEX 60 · DEF 25 · SPD 80 · MAG 120 · LCK 55

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The Gigantes is a two headed demon with both an upper and lower torso. During the Gate Rune War it served as the keeper of the Dwarves' Vault. Its two torsos connected at the waist and the upper half is responsible for physical attacks while the lower half is responsible for magic attacks. They flip over 180 degrees when attacking, so that the attacking torso is always on top. Fire attacks have no effect on this enemy whatsoever, but it is weak to lightning and wind magic.

name: Veteran Soldier
description: The Veteran Soldiers (ベテラン兵, Beteran Hei) are enemies which appear in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 175 · DEX 50 · DEF 45 · SPD 80 · MAG 38 · LCK 47

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These Imperial troops were guarding Pannu Yakuta Castle during the Gate Rune War. These battle-seasoned soldiers excelled with weapons such as bows and spears and were capable anywhere on the battlefield. Among the soldiers only the women were trained in sword use.

name: Devil Armor
description: Devil Armor (ゴーストアーマー, Gōsutoāmā, Ghost Armor) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 210 · DEX 40 · DEF 60 · SPD 50 · MAG 50 · LCK 40

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This armor type monster was seen in Pannu Yakuta Castle, summouned by and under the control of Windy. The contents of the armor are entirely empty, with no lower body, the armor being propelled by its evil will.

The helmet, left arm and right arm of the Devil Armor can detach, allowing it to attack multiple targets simultaneously. As a suit of armor, it had high defense but held a weakness to lightning magic. Rarely, the sword it carries could be used as a Main-Gauche if recovered from its remains.

name: Devil Shield
description: Devil Shield (ゴーストシールド, Gōsutashīrudo, Ghost Shield) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 195 · DEX 56 · DEF 55 · SPD 45 · MAG 90 · LCK 30

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This shield monster was seen in Pannu Yakuta Castle during the Gate Rune War. It is a metal shield imbued with the face of a demon and viciously attacks any intruders. The Devil Shield can release a powerful shockwave from its mouth, striking all of its opponents.

Defeated, its vengeful spirit may be driven out, leaving behind a Chaos Shield.

name: Dragon
description: The Dragon (ドラゴン, Doragon) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden. The Dragon is the sixth boss of the game and is encountered at Pannu Yakuta Castle.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 250 · DEX 60 · DEF 35 · SPD 40 · MAG 150 · LCK 65

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This dragons entire body is covered with scales and it also possesses sharp claws and fangs. This beast is widely known for breathing fire from its mouth. During the Gate Rune War, this creature was spotted at Pannu Yakuta Castle under the control of Kwanda Rosman's Conqueror Rune, blocking the way of the Toran Liberation Army with its fearsome lightning attacks.

It boasts a very high life-force and because it is resistant to most attacks, other than wind, it is very difficult for even a veteran soldier to defeat it. It is said that this dragon posses a strength and dignity truly worth of the name king of beasts.

name: Holly Master
description: The Holly Master (ひいらぎマスター, Hīragi Masutā) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 240 · DEX 50 · DEF 35 · SPD 80 · MAG 75 · LCK 90

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This humanoid holly creature was seen in the Kunan area during the Gate Rune War and attacked in the same manner as the Holly Spirit. Due to its higher strength, it is believed to be a matured form of the Holly Spirit. This creature is known to rarely drop a Nameless Urn.

Like the Holly Spirit, it could inflict the bothersome Balloon effect. Because of their high magical resistance, some warriors could struggle to defeat them before they were sent flying by said balloons.

name: Mad Ivy
description: The Mad Ivy (怒れるツタ, Ikareru Tsuta) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 235 · DEX 75 · DEF 25 · SPD 70 · MAG 35 · LCK 33

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The Mad Ivy is a plant-type monster that appeared around Teien during the Gate Rune War. It grows from the ground and so no one has confirmed what it looks like apart from its vines, which it uses to attack like whips. A typical plant-based monster, it has low durability and vulnerable to attack by fire. It is a common tactic when a group of these monsters appear to burn them down with a wide-ranged fire magic attack.

name: Creeper
description: Creeper (カズラー, Kazurā) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 210 · DEX 90 · DEF 25 · SPD 73 · MAG 40 · LCK 25

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: Creepers are anthropomorphic plant monsters who, as well as being able to attack with their ivy limbs, are able to swallow a person or animal whole, draining their health away. They resemble carnivorous plants in that regard. They were spotted around the town of Teien during the Gate Rune War and were also found in the Sindar ruins behind the White Deer Inn near Muse City.

name: Delf
description: Delf (つぼ, Tsubo, Jar) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 180 · DEX 30 · DEF 85 · SPD 30 · MAG 40 · LCK 30

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These cobras live inside small jars and are manipulated by the flutes of the Cobra Charmer. Their jars functioned as a shell, increasing their defense.

Although they usually follow the Cobra Charmer's orders, they do not require the Cobra Charmer's flute to attack and can do so of their own volition. They are not venomous creatures, for the record.

name: Scarlet Slime
description: Scarlet Slime (ハイドロゲル, Haidorogeru, Hydrogel) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 225 · DEX 50 · DEF 55 · SPD 60 · MAG 60 · LCK 45

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Hex Doll, Chinese Dish, Bonsai Tree

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This liquid monster had no fixed shape and was seen in Soniere Prison. It is said that it could change his shape at will and deafen intruders. It held a severe weakness to fire and wind magics.

Chinese Dishes, a difficult to obtain antique, could sometimes be found in its remains.

name: Cobra Charmer
description: Cobra Charmer (コブラ使い, Kobura Tsukai, Cobra Tamer) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 203 · DEX 45 · DEF 60 · SPD 70 · MAG 55 · LCK 56

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These snake tamers were charged with the guarding of Soniere Prison during the Gate Rune War. They carried Delfs in jars, manipulating with their flutes.

Normally they focus on manipulating their minions but when cornered, can use their flutes to emit ultrasonic waves to hurt intruders. They resisted fire attacks but had a weakness against earth magic.

name: Nightmare
description: Nightmare (マジシャン, Majishan, Magician) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 250 · DEX 60 · DEF 45 · SPD 90 · MAG 240 · LCK 70

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A clown-type monster hiding in a card that was seen in Soniere Prison. It can freely manipulate fire, wind, and water magic, but decides which one to use by the picture on the card that appears. It is therefore considered to have a very capricious personality, although still dangerous due to its high magical ability.

Nightmares are also the only source of the Opal, necessary to recruit Esmeralda in Antei.

name: Dagon
description: The Dagon (うおがみ, Uogami, Fish God) is an enemy in the first Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 230 · DEX 70 · DEF 50 · SPD 75 · MAG 80 · LCK 35

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The Dagon is a giant mysterious fish with limbs. During the Gate Rune War, many were seen around Kalekka and the Secret Factory. As well as attacking with the spear in its hands, it can also poison a target with its tail. It does not have outstanding abilities and should be easily defeated if you take advantage of its weakness to fire and wind attribute attacks.

name: Siren
description: Sirens (セイレーン, Seirēn) are an enemy in the first Suikoden.

facts.label: Location
facts.value: Area E (Dana region), Floating Fortress of Shasarazade

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 260 · DEX 60 · DEF 20 · SPD 90 · MAG 160 · LCK 40

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Named for the way they lure victims with their beautiful singing voices, these fairy creatures appeared around the Floating Fortress of Shasarazade, using their powerful scream-like singing to inflict the Silence status on enemies.

There was also a type of Siren seen around the Secret Factory but they are slightly different creatures with a different form of attack. These pseudo-Sirens looked very similar to their regular counterparts with even seasoned adventurers struggling to tell the difference. These creatures occasionally dropped the very valuable Prosperity Rune.

name: Grizzly Bear
description: The Grizzly Bear (アームべア, Āmubea, Arm Bear) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 280 · DEX 50 · DEF 30 · SPD 50 · MAG 35 · LCK 10

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The Grizzly Bear is a kind of bear known for its quick temper. It came to be known as the Arm Bear from the powerful attacks unleashed from its limbs, using their sharp claws to kill their prey. This beast was feared by adventurers as well as hunters and loggers but sightings decreased during the Gate Rune War and today their numbers have lessened.

trivia: * The Grizzly Bear has one of the few animated enemy sprites in Suikoden, in order to capture its drool in full motion.

name: Hawk Man
description: The Hawk Man (ホークマン, Hōkuman) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 245 · DEX 40 · DEF 55 · SPD 90 · MAG 99 · LCK 55

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Defective Urn, Wide Urn, Black Urn

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: Haunting the ruins of Kalekka, this humanoid birdman uses the sharp claws on its legs as a weapon to launch continuous kicks. It is said that they possess various types of pots and urns, which they probably collected in the abandoned town.

name: Shadow
description: Shadow (シャドウ, Shadō) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 260 · DEX 120 · DEF 45 · SPD 88 · MAG 60 · LCK 90

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These humanoid creatures were seen in the ruins of Kalekka. Their technique usually involves grappling their target before leaping into the air and pile driving them into ground, causing great damage. They were highly accurate and would rarely lose sight of their prey.

It is said that although it looked almost human, it was actually some form of malevolent spirit.

name: Demon Hound
description: Demon Hound (地獄の番犬, Jigoku no Banken) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 250 · DEX 70 · DEF 60 · SPD 65 · MAG 75 · LCK 45

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The Demon Hound is a double-headed monster that was spotted in the ruins of Kalekka. Its entire body is bathed in pale blue flames, making it immune to fire magic as well as difficult to fight off. It had a weakness to earth and water magic, though this did little to reduce its danger.

trivia: * Demon Hound's Japanese name translates as Hellhound while the Hell Hound enemy's Japanese name is something different entirely: Jackal.

name: Hell Hound
description: Hell Hound (ジャックル, Jakkuru) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 258 · DEX 70 · DEF 65 · SPD 70 · MAG 30 · LCK 40

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The Hell Hound is a ferocious wolf-esque two-headed monster that was seen around Moravia Castle and the Lorimar region during the Gate Rune War. They usually attack on the commands of their Whip Wolf or Whip Master but in the absence of orders are capable of attacking targets independently.

name: Whip Wolf
description: Whip Wolf (ウィップマン, Uippuman, Whip Man) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 240 · DEX 90 · DEF 70 · SPD 150 · MAG 75 · LCK 55

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This wolf-like monster with a human body was seen in the Lorimar region during the Gate Rune War. It usually commanded a number of Hell Hounds, having them attack intruders with a crack of its whip although it could also use the whip as a weapon under duress. In defeat, it could occasionally drop the very rare Resurrection Rune.

More powerful Whip Wolves were called Whip Masters and were regarded as being much more dangerous.

name: Grave Master
description: Grave Master (グレイブマスター, Gureibumasutā) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 270 · DEX 30 · DEF 80 · SPD 50 · MAG 75 · LCK 55

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Grave Master is a golem-like creature made of steel, instead of rock, which appeared in the rural region of Lorimar during the Gate Rune War. It is quite slow, but its powerful fists which it uses to mercilessly attack its opponents more than make up for any sluggish movements.

They were not strong against magic, especially against lightning, but held some resistance to fire and wind magic.

name: Sorcerer
description: Sorcerer (ソーサラー, Sōsarā) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 270 · DEX 100 · DEF 30 · SPD 90 · MAG 190 · LCK 90

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This evil practitioner of high magic was said to have immolated many adventures with the flames emanating from their hands. They were seen in the Lorimar region during the Gate Rune War. The robes they wear are Master's Robes and are valuable pieces of equipment if you can salvage one from a fallen Sorcerer.

name: Banshee
description: Banshee (バンシー, Banshī) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 270 · DEX 110 · DEF 40 · SPD 90 · MAG 180 · LCK 70

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This grotesque spirit, neither resembling a young or old woman, cried out in a sad voice, appearing in front of people when misfortune struck. They were feared as harbingers of bad luck. Its appearance was confirmed in the Cave of the Past behind Qlon Temple, and it is said that those who heard its cry lost their voices in fear.

It also seems that Landscape Scrolls could sometimes be found on its person, indicating it had an appreciation for art.

name: Wisp
description: Wisp (ウィスプ, Uisupu) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 250 · DEX 90 · DEF 40 · SPD 130 · MAG 150 · LCK 86

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These shining, floating spherical creatures appear in the Cave of the Past. Despite their unassuming appearance, they were quite formidable. They attack by firing projectiles from within the sphere itself.

It had low vitality and was vulnerable to fire magic, so mages rarely had a difficult time against their monster. Reports say that this creature can leave behind a rare Speed Rune Piece upon defeat.

name: Clay Doll
description: The Clay Doll (クレイドール, Kureidōru) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 280 · DEX 35 · DEF 70 · SPD 65 · MAG 160 · LCK 35

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Clay Doll is a golem-type of monster that appeared at the Cave of the Past, behind Qlon Temple. It has a strong attack, able to rain down stone on its opponents with its large fists, and being made up of rocks, high defense.

It also has a strong resistance against magic, especially wind magic. However, it shows some weakness against lightning element attacks. One other drawback is its slow movements.

trivia:
* The Clay Doll is the second of four golem-type enemies in Suikoden.
* In the 1996 English language localization of Suikoden, the boss variant of the Clay Doll had a different name, the Golem.

name: Specter
description: Specters (スペクト, Supekuto) are an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 260 · DEX 90 · DEF 80 · SPD 110 · MAG 100 · LCK 50

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Defective Urn, Blue Dragon Urn, Fine China

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These spirits of the dead haunted Neclord's Castle during the Gate Rune War. Floating eerily through the air, they would approach their targets while bobbing and swaying before stealing their life essence with a touch.

It resisted most forms of magic, but had a particular weakness to Holy spells. Some reports state that these creatures can leave behind fine antiques when vanquished.

name: Zombie Sorcerer
description: The Zombie Sorcerer (ソーサラーゾンビ, Sōsarāzonbi, Sorcerer Zombie) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 320 · DEX 70 · DEF 50 · SPD 100 · MAG 290 · LCK 60

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These zombie sorcerers haunted Neclord's Castle during the Gate Rune War. As the creature has three heads, it is believed to be the remains of several sorcerers combined together rather than something created by sorcerers.

They were considered far more powerful and dangerous than sorcerers in most every respect. Wielding powerful magic such as its blue flame attack, it aims to defeat and consume the souls of any intruders in the castle.

name: Zombie Unicorn
description: Zombie Unicorn (ユニコーンゾンビ, Yunikōnzonbi, Unicorn Zombie) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 380 · DEX 85 · DEF 60 · SPD 90 · MAG 265 · LCK 100

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This unicorn zombie appeared in Neclord's Castle during the Gate Rune War. It is said that in addition to charging with its horn against intruders, it also used huge blocks of ice to attack. Rumor has it these beasts sometimes dropped the valuable Star Earrings.

trivia: * This enemy shares the same Japanese name as Suikoden II's Zombie Unicorn and Suikoden III's ZombUnicrn.

name: Ephemero
description: Ephemero (かげろう, Kagerō) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 270 · DEX 100 · DEF 60 · SPD 140 · MAG 240 · LCK 66

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This sphere-like monster, whose makeup remains a mystery, was spotted in the Dragon's Den during the Gate Rune War. It fires a green high-speed projectile from within itself, allowing it to attack intruders from a distance.

Although physically weak, its magic prowess made up for its fragility. It has been reported that rare Magic Rune Pieces have been found in its remains.

name: Magic Shield
description: Magic Shield (マジックシールド, Majikkushīrudo) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 280 · DEX 80 · DEF 130 · SPD 100 · MAG 230 · LCK 37

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This shield-shaped monster was seen in the Dragon's Den. As it looks like a shield, its defense is extremely high. It is said that it generated shockwaves in a wide area, stunning adventurers it came across.

This creature was also rumored to drop (or perhaps be) the Earth Shield, the best shield available in the Toran region during the Gate Rune War.

name: Sun King
description: The Sun King (日輪魔王, Nichirinmaō) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 340 · DEX 95 · DEF 90 · SPD 95 · MAG 280 · LCK 50

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These golden-skinned giants were encountered in the Dragon's Den. This monster's strength and ability absolutely lived up to their imposing stature. They wielded a powerful attack that generates a large amount fire rings all at once at the target. They also apparently had an interest in windows.

trivia: * This enemy shares the same Japanese name as the Suikoden II enemy Sun King and the Suikoden III enemy Bronze Sun.

name: Killer Ivy
description: Killer Ivy (這い寄るツタ, Haiyoru Tsuta) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Location
facts.value: Area G (Dragon Knight's Domain), Seek Valley

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 290 · DEX 70 · DEF 80 · SPD 110 · MAG 170 · LCK 25

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Hex Doll, Peeing Boy, Knight Statue

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This plant-type monster grew naturally in Seek Valley. It was difficult to distinguish from ordinary plants. It can manipulate its ivy tendrils to attack anyone who enters its territory.

Although it had low vitality, it was very durable and so it was much more effective to attack with fire magic than engage it in physical combat. Antiques could sometimes be found in its gnarled remains.

name: Shadow Man
description: The Shadow Man (影男, Kageotoko) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 310 · DEX 150 · DEF 60 · SPD 130 · MAG 60 · LCK 90

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These strange creatures were encountered outside the Dragon Knights' Fortress during the Gate Rune War. They move quickly and can grab an opponent and drop them from a great height.

Although they share the same name as another creature that were sighted in North Window, they are clearly different despite both sharing an eerie appearance. Their faces are wrapped in cloth from which two eyes vacantly look out. If there is some connection between the two, it remains unknown for now.

name: Mirage
description: Mirage (ミラージュ, Mirāju) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Location
facts.value: Area G (Dragon Knight's Domain), Area H (Senan region)

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 330 · DEX 130 · DEF 95 · SPD 120 · MAG 175 · LCK 70

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: A being wearing full body armor and carrying a greatsword, often seen around Moravia Castle. They could disappear instantly and suddenly reappear before their target, bringing their sword to bear. It seems these creatures possess Speed Rings, and it is believed that this ring's reverberation causes them to be able to disappear in an instant.

name: Rock Buster
description: The Rock Buster (ロックバスター, Rokkubasutā) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 300 · DEX 75 · DEF 90 · SPD 80 · MAG 240 · LCK 39

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These humanoid monsters had been spotted in the area around the Dragon Knight's Domain during the Gate Rune War. It has a very similar appearance to the Clay Doll, but its life force, power and magic are all superior.

It is resistant to fire and wind magic but as with Clay Dolls, is weak against lightning magic.

trivia: * The Rock Buster is the third of the four golem-type enemies in Suikoden. Its compatriots are the Clay Doll, the Clay Doll and the Earth Golem.

name: Wyvern
description: Wyvern (ワイバーン, Waibān) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 320 · DEX 110 · DEF 85 · SPD 120 · MAG 260 · LCK 75

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This winged beast breathes fire and cold air from its mouth, killing all foes in the area. It boasts of high physical and magical power, and is said to have tormented many a skilled adventurer.

Wyverns were often equated with dragons because of their similar appearance but they did possess the comparatively advanced intelligence of a dragon. However, like dragons, they were powerful beasts that boasted high vitality and magical power.

name: Crystal Core
description: The Crystal Core (クリスタルコア, Kurisutarukoa) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden. The Crystal Core is the eleventh boss of the game and is encountered at Seek Valley.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 350 · DEX 70 · DEF 90 · SPD 65 · MAG 275 · LCK 80

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Crystal Core is a mysterious giant crystalline life form that appears to have been summoned through magic. It seems to have been called by Windy to block the path in Seek Valley. It attacks with the crystals that grow in Seek Valley and once its core is revealed will unleashed powerful fire magic attacks on its enemies.

Holy magic has no effect and it is also resistant to fire, lightning and wind magic, but weak against water and earth. It also has powerful magic of its own, so overall it's quite formidable.

name: Rabbit Bird
description: Rabbit Bird (ラビットバード, Rabittobādo) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 305 · DEX 110 · DEF 50 · SPD 60 · MAG 160 · LCK 110

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This odd bird-shaped monster was seen in the area around Moravia Castle. Its body was essentially comprised of its beaked-head and wing-shaped ears.

It has been observed to stomp the ground after attacking, but its meaning has yet to be determined. Although it had no outstanding features besides its bizarre appearance, it was considered auspicious to encounter one.

name: Earth Golem
description: The Earth Golem (アースゴーレム, Āsugōremu) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 340 · DEX 70 · DEF 110 · SPD 70 · MAG 230 · LCK 35

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The Earth Golem is comprised of clods of earth and its appearance had been confirmed around Moravia Castle during the Gate Rune War. It is proud of its robustness and excels in defense and magic as well as attack, boasting a formidable resistance against Fire and Wind elemental attacks. A Lightning elemental attack is said to be its only weakness. Some cases of Master's Garb can be found in the remains of Earth Golems.

trivia:
* The Earth Golem sprite is a recoloring of the Clay Doll sprite.
* The Earth Golem is the fourth (and final) golem-type enemy in Suikoden, along with Clay Doll, Clay Doll and Rock Buster.

name: Whip Master
description: Whip Master (ウィップマスター, Uippumasutā) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 320 · DEX 85 · DEF 90 · SPD 180 · MAG 175 · LCK 55

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Hex Doll, Bonsai Tree, Goddess Statue

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This wolf-like monster with a human body was seen in Moravia Castle during the Gate Rune War. It usually commanded a number of Hell Hounds, having them attack intruders with a crack of its whip. In defeat, it could occasionally drop a rare work of art, such as the impressive Goddess Statue which was worth a great deal of potch.

It appeared to be a more developed version of the Whip Wolf as it was regarded as superior in most every way.

name: Elite Soldier
description: The Elite Soldier (エリート兵, Erīto Hei) is an enemy in the first Suikoden.

facts.label: Location
facts.value: Moravia Castle, Floating Fortress of Shasarazade

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 340 · DEX 190 · DEF 120 · SPD 120 · MAG 110 · LCK 60

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
During the late Gate Rune War, these soldiers guarded Moravia Castle and the Floating Fortress of Shasarazade. These soldiers are carefully selected from the strongest in the Imperial Army, with their skills as a soldier on par with anything else.

These troops mercilessly cut down any intruders. Even though it is tough, some have managed to defeat the Elite Soldiers, finding the rare Rage Orb as well as the Horned Helmet, which boasts the highest strength of all helmets.

trivia: * There are actually two types of Elite Soldier: a standard officer-type at Moravia Castle, as well as a commanding officer-type at the Floating Fortress of Shasarazade. The one at Shasarazade is slightly stronger.

name: Ninja
description: The Ninja (ニンジャ, Ninja) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 340 · DEX 140 · DEF 70 · SPD 160 · MAG 60 · LCK 90

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: Ninja are known to be a skilled professional who conduct spy missions, and do not often reveal their appearance in public so their existence is shrouded in mystery. During the late Gate Rune War, the presence of these foreign warriors have been verified in Moravia Castle. Wielding shuriken, their attack techniques are exceptional and their swift, aggressive behavior is a sight to behold.

trivia: * The ninja is one of three ninja enemies in Suikoden made from the same template, the others being the Assassin and the Ninja Master.

name: Magus
description: Magus (メイガス, Meigasu) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 270 · DEX 90 · DEF 50 · SPD 125 · MAG 290 · LCK 120

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This highly powerful sorcerer was spotted within Moravia Castle during the Gate Rune War. They manipulate fire magic and mercilessly burn to death any intruders found within the castle.

Some say that due to their pale skin and the fact that their feet are not visible underneath their robes that these Magi may be dead, the ghosts of famed magicians past, but the truth remains unclear.

It is said that there are those who have defeated this monster and obtained the precious Fortune Rune in the process.

name: Cerberus
description: Cerberus (ケルベロス, Keruberosu) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 370 · DEX 150 · DEF 70 · SPD 180 · MAG 160 · LCK 90

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These two-headed demon dogs could be found at the Floating Fortress of Shasarazade. Most likely summouned by magic, these creatures could shrug off any and all fire-based attacks thanks to its flame coated body but water and earth magic had some effect on it.

name: Venus Shell
description: The Venus Shell (シェルビーナス, Sherubīnasu) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden. The Venus Shell appears as the twelfth boss of the game and is fought in the Floating Fortress of Shasarazade.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 470 · DEX 80 · DEF 40 · SPD 80 · MAG 350 · LCK 100

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Venus Shell is a shell monster that lived in the Floating Fortress of Shasarazade during the late Gate Rune War. The monster was said to have been powerful, and it was only sighted the once. Its ecosystem is unknown but from its use of powerful magic, it is clear that it had high intelligence.

Usually, it remains closed, firing pearls at its foes but when struck with lightning magic, it could open up to reveal the figure of a beautiful woman, capable of unleashing powerful spells. It is far more powerful than any other creature on Lake Toran, making it incredibly difficult to subdue.

trivia: * The Venus Shell has the highest HP of any enemy, boss or no, in all the game and the highest Level and potch outside of the final boss.

name: Ninja Master
description: The Ninja Master (マスターニンジャ, Masutāninja) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 330 · DEX 140 · DEF 30 · SPD 170 · MAG 120 · LCK 60

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These Ninja Masters, clad in crimson-dyed clothing, appeared near Gregminster at the end of the Gate Rune War. Their defenses are inferior to that of the regular Ninja but their magic and agility is superior.

They are in possession of a Crimson Cape, dyed in the same manner as their costumes. With the title "master" in their name, it suggests they have a higher standing that the regular Ninja.

trivia: * The Ninja Master is simply a palate swap of the Ninja enemy.

name: Simurgh
description: The Simurgh (テンプルホーク, Tenpuruhōku, Templehawk) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 315 · DEX 80 · DEF 45 · SPD 130 · MAG 160 · LCK 55

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These hawk-like monsters appeared towards the end of the Gate Rune War in the Arus region surrounding Gregminster. Soaring high in the air, they would swoop down with tremendous speed at their targets.

Rarely, Thunder Orbs could be found in the carcass of this monster.

name: Orc
description: The Orc (オーク, Ōku) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 340 · DEX 30 · DEF 5 · SPD 15 · MAG 30 · LCK 30

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Graffiti, Lovers' Garden, Beauties of Nature

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The Orc appears to be a Human sub-race, with its human-like figure, venomously green skin color and highly violent nature. Late in the Gate Rune War, these monsters had been spotted around Gregminster, attacking travellers with its spiked ball and chain. The Beauties of Nature painting has been discovered on its body, raising the question as to whether it has the intelligence to admire art.

trivia: * Although the Orc has one of the highest (non-boss) PWR stats in the game, outmatched only by the Colossus and Echidna, it has one of the lowest DEF stats, beating only the Bandit (archer) and the opening boss, Clay Doll.

name: Imperial Guard
description: The Imperial Guard (近衛兵, Konoehei) are an enemy in the first Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 330 · DEX 100 · DEF 130 · SPD 90 · MAG 200 · LCK 110

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These soldiers fought to the best of their ability to protect Gregminster Palace at the end of the Gate Rune War. Comprised of both male and female soldiers, the men wielded two-handed swords while the women carried both sword and shield into combat. Though the male soldiers were physically stronger, the female soldiers utilized runes.

name: Phantom
description: The Phantom (ファントム, Fantomu) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 335 · DEX 150 · DEF 70 · SPD 120 · MAG 190 · LCK 95

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These enigmatic foes manifested within the confines of Gregminster Palace, often found in the company of other monsters. Though not exceptionally challenging adversaries, their ability to assault the back row of a party posed a significant threat due to their formidable strength.

Phantoms presented themselves as jet-black warriors, exhibiting an intriguing behavior as they materialized in front of their intended targets with sudden movements, wielding their swords to strike.

The true nature and origins of these entities remain shrouded in mystery, with some speculating that they might be the spirits of the deceased. Despite being cloaked in full armor, their physical substance appears limited, resulting in relatively thin armor.

Notably, Phantoms are known to possess a valuable item known as the Earth Boots, a prized piece of equipment sought after by adventurers.

name: Colossus
description: The Colossus (コロッサス, Korossasu) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 350 · DEX 80 · DEF 90 · SPD 60 · MAG 100 · LCK 75

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A Colossus is a type of blue-skinned giant. It is considered very dangerous as it combines extensive magic attack with a very powerful physical strength, able to create shockwaves by driving its fist into the ground to eliminate intruders.

With extremely high stamina, one-on-one combat would be a pretty tough challenge. There is a theory that it is wide-open to earth and lightning attacks. They seemed to have been used as an escort in the Imperial court of the Scarlet Moon Empire.

name: Echidna
description: The Echidna (エキドナ, Ekidona) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 370 · DEX 100 · DEF 40 · SPD 90 · MAG 310 · LCK 60

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
During the late Gate Rune War, following the Last Battle, many monsters appeared in Gregminster Palace.

Of these, the Echidna is said to be one of the most formidable of all enemies. Even a battle-tested warrior would struggle against the height of its vitality and magic power.

Its crescent blasts of energy were able to bring down even the hardiest fighters. But those who were able to defeat the Echidna had a chance to obtain the Windspun Armor, the best armor at the time. Warriors fortunate to get this armor unscathed would have obtained impregnable defensive power.

name: Golden Hydra
description: The Golden Hydra (黄金竜, Ougonryū) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden. It is the final boss of the game, and the transformed form of Barbarossa Rugner.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 520 · DEX 105 · DEF 55 · SPD 75 · MAG 370 · LCK 80

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
In the final battle of the Gate Rune War, the Scarlet Moon Emperor Barbarossa, with the help of the Dragon King Sword, which had the Sovereign Rune embedded in its hilt, turned into a three headed Golden Hydra. Each of the heads held a fire, lightning and wind weakness, respectively, but also held resistances to many forms of magic.

It could best be described as a very powerful and formidable enemy, and very difficult to beat, even with the best selection of warriors in the Toran Liberation Army.

trivia:
*Even though it is the final boss, the heads of the Golden Hydra do not have the highest HP of all of Suikoden's enemies. The Venus Shell has even more HP (15,000).
*The Golden Hydra has the highest level and potch reward of any enemy in the game. However, after the battle the characters won't actually gain any level or potch.
*The right head is, with the exception of Neclord in his invincible form, the only enemy in the game that is invulnerable to the lightning element.

name: Highland Soldier
description: Highland Soldiers (おうこくへい, Ōkoku Hei, Kingdom Soldier) are an enemy which appear in Suikoden II. There are no less than 35 different Highland Soldier enemies the player can encounter in the game. They were called Highlands in the 1999 English language localization of the game.

facts.label: Location
facts.value: Tenzan Pass, Kyaro Town, Mercenary Fortress, Muse-Highland Checkpoint, Muse City, Two River City

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 15 · DEX 5 · DEF 6 · SPD 10 · MAG 14 · LCK 18

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
They normally appear in groups of five or six, often accompanied by a Commander. They usually wielded either a sword, spear, or bow.

Well trained and organized, their skill was impressed upon the New Alliance Army many a time during the Dunan Unification War. Broadly speaking, the spearmen were considered the strongest individuals and the archers provided support.

name: Commander
description: The Commander (しょうたいちょう, Shoutaichou, Platoon Leader) is an enemy which appear in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Location
facts.value: Tenzan Pass, Mercenary Fortress, Muse City, Two River City, Greenhill Forest, Rockaxe Castle

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 20 · DEX 5 · DEF 8 · SPD 6 · MAG 12 · LCK 16

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These Commanders usually led groups of Highland Soldier in battle and were very active fighting for the Highland Army during the Dunan Unification War.

As a squad leader, they had superior fighting skills and equipment when compared to the average Highland soldier and were also capable leaders.

name: Killer Bee
description: The Killer Bee (キラービー, Kirābī) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 18 · DEX 50 · DEF 0 · SPD 30 · MAG 0 · LCK 35

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Killer Bees were spotted in droves, living on the North Swallow Pass during the Dunan Unification War, indiscriminately attacking people. Rarely appearing alone, these bee monsters were more likely to attack in swarms but, as such, were susceptible to large-scale magic attacks.

Although they are not very strong, they are able to attack from afar so travellers should remain on guard.

name: Wolf
description: The Wolf (ウルフ, Urufu) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 48 · DEX 25 · DEF 10 · SPD 30 · MAG 0 · LCK 3

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This dangerous pack animal was spotted on the North Swallow Pass early in the Dunan Unification War. It is not technically a monster, merely a feral wild wolf. As such, they do not generally attack adventurers. They still sometimes attack people, especially when in groups, but are fairly easy to repel.

name: Mist Shade
description: The Mist Shade (ミストシェイド, Misutosheido) is a boss enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 65 · DEX 30 · DEF 10 · SPD 15 · MAG 6 · LCK 6

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This mist monster was well feared by people living near the North Swallow Pass early in the Dunan Unification War. It sometimes appeared in the form of a demonic man and sometimes as a naked female.

Both forms are made of wind and the Mist Shade had no solid form in either guise nor did its abilities differ on its form, save for the woman version being skilled at using attack magic.

The monster was killed by the future leader of the New Alliance Army and Rina's traveling troupe as they journeyed to Kyaro Town.

name: Spider
description: The Spider (スパイダー, Supaidā) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Location
facts.value: Area J (Kyaro Town area), Ryube Forest

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 22 · DEX 10 · DEF 4 · SPD 15 · MAG 5 · LCK 20

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This large arachnid monster was spotted in the Kyaro Town area and in the Ryube Forest during the early Dunan Unification War. It appears to be able to poison its enemies and is resistant to venom itself. Although Antitoxin is useful for countering this monster, it is not a particularly difficult opponent.

trivia: *This enemy shares the same Japanese name as Suikoden III's Arachnvore.

name: Woodpecker
description: The Woodpecker (キツツッキー, Kitsutsukkī) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Location
facts.value: Area A (East Muse Principality), Ryube Forest

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 38 · DEX 30 · DEF 10 · SPD 25 · MAG 20 · LCK 20

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Woodpecker is a cute, but aggressive avian monster. It had wings but they were so small that the creature was assumed to be incapable of flight, walking everywhere.

It was spotted during the Dunan Unification War in Ryube Forest. Sometimes it would drop the rare Killer Rune upon defeat.

name: Shadow Dog
description: The Shadow Dog (シャドウドソグ, Shadoudosogu) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 52 · DEX 36 · DEF 15 · SPD 27 · MAG 0 · LCK 18

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Shadow Dog is a monster that appeared in the areas surrounding the Mercenary Fortress early in the Dunan Unification War. A Shadow Dog once attempted to attack Templeton on his travels but it was driven off by Colin, a local youth.

It is believed that its name comes from its dark, shadowy fur. Resembling a wolf more than a dog, these ferocious, charging creatures would sometimes drop the rare Haziness Rune once defeated in combat.

name: Poison Spider
description: The Poison Spider (どくぐも, Dokugomu) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 40 · DEX 25 · DEF 10 · SPD 24 · MAG 7 · LCK 13

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Poison Spider was a huge arachnid with a powerful poison attack that was spotted around Muse Principality during the early Dunan Unification War. As its name suggests, it is a huge, fierce spider that attacks people indiscriminately.

Potentially dangerous due to its poisonous attacks, if properly prepared, this monster should prove to be of little difficulty.

name: Sickle
description: The Sickle (カマイタチ, Kamaitachi) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 60 · DEX 53 · DEF 25 · SPD 65 · MAG 35 · LCK 28

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Sickles were monsters that inhabited Muse Principality during the early Dunan Unification War. It is very fast and moves like a whirlwind, its attacks cutting like a gale. Its attacks are believed to be where this creature got its name.

It was invulnerable to wind elemental attacks. Some adventurers have found Gale Runes on the bodies of these monsters.

name: Gold Boar
description: The Gold Boar (ゴールドボー, Gōrudobō) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 65 · DEX 30 · DEF 15 · SPD 35 · MAG 10 · LCK 27

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The Gold Boar is huge, wild boar covered in a coarse gold fur. Inhabiting the fields around Muse City, it is very fierce and will attack those who wander into its territory at random with its charge attacks. That said, it is not too difficult to defeat with a little bit of skill.

name: Mega Watt
description: The Mega Watt (メガワキ, Megawaki) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 60 · DEX 40 · DEF 25 · SPD 38 · MAG 45 · LCK 27

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
During the Dunan Unification War, this monster appeared in the Sindar Ruins near Muse City. It is shaped like a person, except instead of a head, it has a single huge eye.

Sharp claws grow at the end of its tentacle-like arms and its lower body resembles the root of a tree. It is believed to be related to the Devil Eye, due to their similar appearance.

Although it is unsettling, this monster is weak to fire and should not cause a party of warriors to struggle greatly.

name: Salamander
description: The Salamander (サラマンダー, Saramandā) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 63 · DEX 33 · DEF 35 · SPD 36 · MAG 37 · LCK 37

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These fire-cloaked lizards appeared in the Sindar Ruins located behind the White Deer Inn. They are said to be the incarnation of fire spirits.

They support themselves in the air by use of their small wings and, as could be inferred from their appearance, are completely immune to, and wields, fire magic and are also susceptible to water. Sometimes a rare Fire Lizard Rune can be recovered from their bodies.

name: Mr. Venus
description: The Mr. Venus (げっこうろうし, Gekkōrōshi, Moonbeam Sage) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 53 · DEX 25 · DEF 26 · SPD 27 · MAG 52 · LCK 16

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This bizarre creature appeared in the Sindar Ruins behind the White Deer Inn. It is shaped like a crescent moon, with a beard and face attached. It uses a variety of magic to attack, marking it as quite intelligent, and it seems to carry rarities such as the Titan Rune and the Unicorn Rune.

name: Double Head
description: The Double Head (ダブルヘッド, Daburuheddo) is a boss enemy that appears in Suikoden II. It is encountered by the hero, Jowy, Nanami, and their comrades in the Sindar Ruins.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 70 · DEX 32 · DEF 15 · SPD 27 · MAG 35 · LCK 29

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This double-headed serpent was found in the deepest parts of the Sindar Ruins in Muse Principality during the Dunan Unification War, guarding the treasure of the ruins. Statues depicting it could also be seen in the ruins.

It attacks with water and fire attacks but despite this, fire magic was effective against it. It is also notable for dropping a Spark Orb when killed.

name: Armadillon
description: The Armadillon (アルマジロン, Arumajiron) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 85 · DEX 28 · DEF 50 · SPD 30 · MAG 17 · LCK 28

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These armadillo-like monsters appeared on the Muse-Highland Checkpoint in the Dunan Unification War. Their entire body is covered with a stiff carapace. Because of this, damaging it through normal weapon attacks can be difficult. It can also hurt its foes by bouncing off its opponents with its tough shell. If you want a short encounter, fire magic will hurt this monster. 

A similar creature, the Armadilloid, appears at the same time but it is clear that they are different creatures.

trivia: * In the Japanese Suikoden II, Armadillon and Armadilloids share the same name. The Armadillon is spelt out in Katakana rather than Hiragana like the Armadilloid.

name: Armadilloid
description: The Armadilloid (あるまじろん, Arumajiron) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 75 · DEX 100 · DEF 32 · SPD 35 · MAG 30 · LCK 5

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Defective Urn, Flower Vase, Blue Dragon Urn

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Armadilloids appeared on the Muse-Highland Checkpoint early in the Dunan Unification War. Although their name is very similar to the Armadillon, it is clear that they are different creatures. It appears to have a shell trapped on in order to mimic the Armadillon but details are unclear.

It has less defense when compared to the Armadillon but its ability to evade attacks makes up for this deficiency. Since it's a troublesome monster, one must try and not focus on its cute appearance. It was said to be very soft, save for its shell.

Nash Latkje would be briefly distracted by this creature during his infiltration of the Muse-Highland Checkpoint camp.

trivia: * In the Japanese Suikoden II, Armadilloids and Armadillon share the same name. The Armadilloid is spelt out in Hiragana rather than Katakana like the Armadillon.

name: Black Bunny
description: The Black Bunny (ブラックバニー, Burakkubanī, Black Bunny) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II. It was called the DarkBunny in the 1999 English language localization of the game.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 110 · DEX 75 · DEF 45 · SPD 65 · MAG 23 · LCK 29

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Dark Bunny looks like a one-eyed rabbit whose entire body has been dyed black. It was spotted wielding a sickle and attacking people indiscriminately around South Window Principality during the Dunan Unification War.

Its high attack made it a force to be reckoned with, despite its cute appearance. It is thought to be a subspecies of the Blade Bunny.

name: Target Lady
description: The Target Lady (ターグットレディ, Tāguttoredi) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 85 · DEX 52 · DEF 27 · SPD 72 · MAG 65 · LCK 38

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Dryad Orb, Nymph Orb, Pixie Orb

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Target Lady appears to be a derivative of the Target Girl, slightly less adept than it in combat. Like its more powerful counterpart, its bewitching ways made it an threat.

It too carries a variety of valuable runes that can be looted after its defeat.

trivia: * It shares the same Japanese name as Suikoden's Beast Commander.

name: Zombie
description: The Zombie (ゾンビ, Zonbi) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II. They are the enemy with the second most amount of variants in the game, after the Highland Soldier.

facts.label: Location
facts.value: North Window, North Window Castle, Lampdragon Mountain, Tinto Mines, Tigermouth Village, Crom Village

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 122 · DEX 37 · DEF 40 · SPD 18 · MAG 0 · LCK 6

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Hex Doll, Defective Urn, Half Helmet, Silver Necklace, Escape Talisman

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Zombies were infamous creatures, being haunting decaying corpses of the deceased. They are generally believed to only become animated under the power of someone else's magic and held no will or spirit of their own as opposed to ghosts and spirits.

During the Dunan Unification War, many of these zombies were seen in Tinto Principality under the control of the vampire Neclord. According to eyewitness accounts, their skin was rotting, their clothing was in tatters, and some were missing hair and eyeballs.

name: Griffin
description: The Griffin (グリフオン, Gurifuon) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Location
facts.value: Cave of the Wind, Forest Village

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 115 · DEX 38 · DEF 45 · SPD 45 · MAG 74 · LCK 39

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These vicious griffins were found inhabiting the Cave of the Wind as well as being seeing around the Forest Village. Unlike the intelligent and amiable griffins such as Feather, they creatures showed no hesitation in attacking people without provocation.

name: Minos
description: The Minos (ごず, Gozu) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 130 · DEX 40 · DEF 50 · SPD 42 · MAG 45 · LCK 55

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Mega Medicine 3, Gozu Orb, Grilled Beef 3

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This monster, human from the neck down and bull from the neck up, inhabits the Cave of the Wind in South Window Principality. It is very muscular and powerful and strikes using a large, spiked mace.

It tends to enjoy fighting so there's no way to avoid combat, besides running away. On top of its resistance to lightning and fire magic, it is also immune of sleep, poison and instant death techniques.

Still, outside of its resistances, it is generally weak to magic and sometimes carries the interesting Gozu Orb.

trivia: * This creature's Japanese name is a reference to the Gozu (or Ox-Head), a type of underworld guardian in Chinese mythology.

name: Celestial Sword
description: The Celestial Sword (星辰剣, Seishin Ken) is a boss enemy who appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 135 · DEX 47 · DEF 40 · SPD 72 · MAG 68 · LCK 54

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Celestial Sword is fought in the depths of the Cave of the Wind. As well as the normal status and elemental resistances, the Celestial Sword also resists normal, physical attacks.

Its group magic attacks can be distressing but, overall, it is not a difficult opponent and be ground down rather quickly.

trivia: * This enemy's name is given as "S.D. Sword" due to character limitations in the enemy name field in the 1999 localization of Suikoden II.

name: Abomination
description: The Abomination (ヘカトンケイル, Hekatonkeiru, Hecatoncheir) is a boss enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 150 · DEX 45 · DEF 25 · SPD 75 · MAG 65 · LCK 37

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Abomination was a giant, misshapen monster, comprised of countless arms and five heads, summoned by Neclord at North Window Castle during the Dunan Unification War. This monster that will remain notable not only for its destructive ability but also for the impact of its appearance.

Although its magic ability was not said to have been particularly high, its fire magic was reported to have been intense and wide-reaching nonetheless.

name: Rockadillo
description: The Rockadillo (イワマジロン, Iwamajiron) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 145 · DEX 42 · DEF 67 · SPD 54 · MAG 22 · LCK 35

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Rockadillo is a monster with a robust shell, making it difficult for anyone trying to hit a killing blow on such a creature. It appears in Two River Principality but is different from the Sandillo that appear in the same area.

Due to their physical resemblance, it is speculated to be related to the Armadillon. It should be noted that the Rockadillo has a chance of dropping one of the elusive Guardian Deity statue plans once when defeated.

trivia: * In the Japanese Suikoden II, Rockadillo and Sandillo share the same name. The Rockadillo is spelt out in Katakana rather than Hiragana like the Sandillo.

name: Spikebeak
description: The Spikebeak (ちゆうちゆう, Chiyūchiyū) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 160 · DEX 58 · DEF 40 · SPD 75 · MAG 57 · LCK 87

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Spikebeaks are a type of bird monster that balance deftly on a single leg. At first glance it looks harmless, but it has an unexpectedly violent temper and can be dangerous if provoked.

During the Dunan Unification War, they were spotted in the areas surrounding the Kobold Village. Balance Orbs can be sometimes recovered from the corpse of this creature.

name: Sandillo
description: The Sandillo (いわまじろん, Iwamajiron) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 135 · DEX 182 · DEF 55 · SPD 63 · MAG 45 · LCK 77

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Japanese Dish, Chinese Dish, Peeing Boy

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Sandillos are a creature that inhabit the areas around Lakewest and Two River City. It is surprisingly stronger than its namesake, the Rockadillo, although its defensive strength is not quite as powerful. Also, because it has a superb ability to avoid attacks, many warriors struggle to fight it.

Due to their physical resemblance, it is speculated to be related to the Armadilloid.

trivia: * In the Japanese Suikoden II, Sandillo and Rockadillo share the same name. The Sandillo is spelt out in Hiragana rather than Katakana like the Rockadillo.

name: Devil Eye
description: The Devil Eye (デビルアイ, Debiruai) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 140 · DEX 57 · DEF 42 · SPD 53 · MAG 47 · LCK 82

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: During the Dunan Unification War, these vicious creatures dwelled in the sewers beneath Two River City. It has a bizarre appearance, with spindly limbs connected by a large eyeball. Its weakness is fire magic, making it a wise choice in a battle.

name: Land Shark
description: The Land Shark (マッドシャーク, Maddoshāku, Mad Shark) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 135 · DEX 115 · DEF 35 · SPD 72 · MAG 35 · LCK 53

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These man-eating shark monsters lived in the sewers beneath Two River City during the Dunan Unification War, always searching for prey. Normally, only its dorsal fin can be glimpsed from the surface, attacking without revealing itself. Its evasion is very good and warriors who insist on attacking wildly may struggle against these creatures.

name: Pest Rat
description: The Pest Rat (ペストラット, Pesutoratto) is a boss enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 160 · DEX 52 · DEF 39 · SPD 85 · MAG 58 · LCK 44

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This monster was witnessed in the Two River City's underground sewers during the Dunan Unification War. As it was only sighted once, its origin is unknown. As ferocious as it looked, it was a very dangerous foe, unleashing earth magic and poisoning its enemies.

It also had a powerful lunging attack that could deal serious damage to its targets. According to the New Alliance Army soldiers who managed to defeat this creature, a Poison Orb could be found on its corpse.

name: Ragged One
description: The RaggedOne (びんぼうがみ, Binbōgami, God of Poverty) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 160 · DEX 62 · DEF 40 · SPD 98 · MAG 74 · LCK 0

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This strange creature has taken on the appearance of a disheveled old man, cloaked with an ominous aura. This, and the meagre amount of potch recovered from its remains, gave rise to its name.

During the Dunan Unification War, these monsters infested Greenhill Principality. It has a dangerous Poison attack but, if defeated, can drop the extremely rare Turtle Plans #2. For some reason cute toy Chicks have also occasionally been found on their person.

name: Sauroid
description: The Sauroid (ビッグフロッグ, Biggufuroggu, Big Frog) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 195 · DEX 59 · DEF 48 · SPD 75 · MAG 37 · LCK 82

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These monsters were seen around Greenhill Principality during the Dunan Unification War. It appears to be a combination of a fish and amphibian and it is unknown whether it is a mutation or simply an evolved organism. As it can knockdown and unbalance its foes, people who encounter it must be alert.

name: Melonzoo
description: The Melonzoo (スイカズラー, Suikazurā) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 165 · DEX 62 · DEF 50 · SPD 87 · MAG 73 · LCK 75

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The Melonzoo was a plant-type monster that lived in the Greenhill Forest during the Dunan Unification War. It appears very similar to the Creeper but yet is distinctive in its own right. It is believed that the Melonzoo may have grown or evolved from the Creeper somehow.

name: Holly Elf
description: The Holly Elf (ひいらぎのせい, Hīragi no Sei, Holly Spirit) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 158 · DEX 88 · DEF 45 · SPD 113 · MAG 83 · LCK 98

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These holly creatures were sighted in the Great Forest during the Gate Rune War and the Greenhill Forest during the Dunan Unification War. Looking like a beautiful woman dressed in holly leaf clothing, these creature's attacks can inflict the Balloon status on unaware foes. They excel at magic attacks, as is typical of spirit-like creatures.

The rare Dryad Orb can sometimes be found after dispatching this monster.

trivia: * They have the same Japanese name as the first Suikoden's Holly Spirit.

name: Monwer
description: The Monwer (おばけばな, Obakebana, Goblin Flower) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 170 · DEX 145 · DEF 47 · SPD 0 · MAG 56 · LCK 82

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These plant-type monsters infested the Greenhill Forest during the Dunan Unification War. They never actively undertake aggressive behavior, though their petals do have sharp fangs and they can counter attacks made by anyone in proximity. Like most plants, fire will ravage it.

trivia: This enemy, like the Eye Flower, never attacks in the game and does not even have an attack animation.

name: Papa Holly
description: The Papa Holly (ひいらぎパパ, Hīragipapa) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 190 · DEX 57 · DEF 57 · SPD 15 · MAG 47 · LCK 75

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Seed Potato, Heavy Collar, Canopy Defense 1

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This plant type monster is very similar to the Grand Holly, albeit slightly weaker. During the Dunan Unification War, these creatures were spotted on the Road to Matilda.

It is likely that over the years, the Holly Boy would grow into a Papa Holly and from there a Grand Holly. Incidentally, Seed Potato can sometimes be recovered from the remains of this monster.

name: DoReMi Elf
description: The DoReMi Elf (ドレミのせい, Doremi no Sei) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II. There are six different versions, based on the color of their clothing, but their stats are identical.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 83 · DEX 82 · DEF 47 · SPD 93 · MAG 97 · LCK 45

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The DoReMi Elves were forest-dwelling sprites. During the Dunan Unification War, they were seen both on the Road to Matilda and the Greenhill Forest. There are six types: red, blue, yellow, green, pink, and aqua, and each uses a different magical attack.

Despite their cute appearance, they are extremely vicious, often appearing in groups and attacking towns and as such require extreme caution. Their varied attacks could cause several status effects, making them very troublesome to deal with.

There have been reports of them dropping recipes, suggesting that they may have been quite gourmet.

name: Zombie Slug
description: The Zombie Slug (ナメゾンビ, Namezonbi) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 150 · DEX 55 · DEF 38 · SPD 83 · MAG 124 · LCK 67

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This creature, a giant slugged enveloped in a blue flame, was spotted in the Road to Matilda during the Dunan Unification War. Unaffected by flames, holy magic seemed to damage it greatly, reflecting its status as a zombie-type creature. Powerful magically, these monsters could blind or paralyse foes with their attacks.

trivia: * The monster shares the same Japanese name as Suikoden III's Soul Slug.

name: Pink Bird
description: The Pink Bird (ピンクバード, Pinkubādo) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 177 · DEX 72 · DEF 52 · SPD 112 · MAG 82 · LCK 105

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
As its name would suggest, this creature is a pink-colored bird. Appearing in the Matilda Knightdom, it strangely balances itself on one foot, but it has unimaginable agility even then.

Bold warriors who could challenge these monsters would find they held strange runes, including the Sylph Orb and the Skunk Orb.

name: Spiker
description: The Spiker (いずな, Izuna) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 202 · DEX 88 · DEF 38 · SPD 150 · MAG 42 · LCK 33

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Defective Urn, Wide Urn, Black Urn

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Spikers infested Rakutei Mountain during the Dunan Unification War. Both their hands are scythe-like spikes, making their attacks quite dangerous. Their speed also makes them difficult to escape from.

However, although nimble, their actual stamina is quite low. If attacked with full force immediately on encountering them, they should be defeated quickly.

name: Harpy
description: The Harpy (ハーピー, Hāpī) is a boss enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 203 · DEX 85 · DEF 45 · SPD 127 · MAG 95 · LCK 65

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Harpy is a terrifying creature with the upper body of a woman but the wings and lower body of a bird. It was spotted on Rakutei Mountain during the Dunan Unification War and was a difficult beast to defeat, even for the New Alliance Army.

Striking with wind element magic and stunning its foes with diving attacks, this creature held no glaring weakness, making it a difficult foe to bring down.

trivia: * If you leave the Highway Village sidequest until after you liberate Rockaxe, then the Harpy will have boosted stats.

name: Clock Knight
description: The Clock Knight (タイムナイト, Taimunaito) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 145 · DEX 62 · DEF 79 · SPD 87 · MAG 82 · LCK 96

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Clock Knight was a diminutive monster characterized by its adorable knight-like appearance, bearing a clock-shaped shield that symbolizes time. It is presumed that the name "Clock Knight" originates from this distinctive feature.

Despite its charming appearance, the Clock Knight possessed formidable defensive capabilities. The creature exhibited a high proficiency in guarding against magical assaults. As a result, those who specialized in magic found themselves struggling when faced with this creature.

name: White Tiger
description: The White Tiger (ホワイトタイガー, Howaitotaigā) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 160 · DEX 67 · DEF 82 · SPD 98 · MAG 43 · LCK 58

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These aggressive rare tigers are covered with white fur, as its name suggests. During the Dunan Unification War, they were spotted around Muse City. It is believed that these tigers were feral but the truth remains unclear.

Double-Jab Orbs could be found on its body on occasion.

name: Tiger
description: The Tiger (トラ, Tora) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 175 · DEX 67 · DEF 85 · SPD 98 · MAG 67 · LCK 60

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These feral tigers with huge fangs resided in the Banner Pass to the Toran Republic during the Dunan Unification War. Without excelling in any one area, it is still a very dangerous foe due to its powerful biting attack and was feared by those traversing the pass.

A Window Set #1 could be recovered from this beast, but why a tiger would have such an item is a mystery to everyone.

name: Samurai
description: The Samurai (サムライ, Samurai) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 180 · DEX 69 · DEF 65 · SPD 105 · MAG 75 · LCK 65

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Hex Doll, Chinese Dish, Bonsai Tree

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These sword practitioners seem to inhabit the Banner Forest that spreads between the Dunan and Toran Republics.

Supposedly human underneath their armor, attacking travelers is apparently part of their sword training, marking them as extremely bloodthirsty. Their roots seem similar to those of the ninja of Rokkaku Hamlet and they carry many fine items that the ninja would favor, such as Bonsai Trees.

They are very powerful and their fighting spirit makes them very dangerous. Their unique sword techniques allow it to stun its opponents in close quarters.

name: Worm
description: The Worm (ワーム, Wāmu) is a boss enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 185 · DEX 65 · DEF 57 · SPD 76 · MAG 113 · LCK 65

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This creature is the larvae of a huge mother monster called the Poison Moth. As such, it is weak in comparison to the Poison Moth but still holds great magic resistance and life force in its own right.

It was believed that because it had little magical ability that it was not very intelligent, but it was able to use lightning magic in battle. It also used its bulk to roll into its foes, hurting them in the process.

It was encountered by the New Alliance Army twice during the Dunan Unification War. The second time was while rescuing Ko in the Banner Pass. The Worm would transform into the Poison Moth after a short time in that encounter.

name: Poison Moth
description: The Poison Moth (ポイズンモス, Poizunmosu) is a boss enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 237 · DEX 72 · DEF 54 · SPD 126 · MAG 136 · LCK 65

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Poison Moth could be considered merely a huge monster moth but it is too large to be a true moth. It has hidden agility that most could not guess at, considering its bulk.

It appeared on the Banner Pass, first as a Worm, before transforming into the Poison Moth. In addition to spreading poisonous spores, it could also emit long range electric shocks from its body.

name: White Wolf Soldier
description: The White Wolf Soldiers (はくろうぐん, Hakurōgun, White Wolf Army) are an enemy which appear in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 172 · DEX 89 · DEF 75 · SPD 120 · MAG 95 · LCK 67

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These are soldiers of Luca Blight's infamous White Wolf Army. Comprised of elite soldiers from the Highland Army, they were highly skilled and equipped with the best, marking them as outstanding compared to their fellow soldiers and making them extremely difficult opponents to defeat.

name: Killer Dog
description: The Killer Dog (キラードソグ, Kirādosogu) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 165 · DEX 98 · DEF 45 · SPD 103 · MAG 35 · LCK 78

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Killer Dog is a monster that was spotted frequently on the Lampdragon Mountain during the Dunan Unification War. This creature has a face like a demon and although shaped like a hound, it is clear this is no ordinary dog gone feral.

It is a very dangerous monster with a high level of cruelty and occasionally Chaos Shields have been found in its remains as a testament to its twisted existence.

name: Air Lizard
description: The Air Lizard (フライリザード, Furairizādo, Fly Lizard) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 197 · DEX 85 · DEF 63 · SPD 78 · MAG 89 · LCK 68

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
During the Dunan Unification War, these winged lizards appeared on the Lampdragon Mountain to Tinto Principality. It attacks with fire techniques and is immune to fire itself. Conversely, it is weak to water-based attacks.

Rarely, a Firefly Orb can be discovered on the monster's corpse.

trivia: * This enemy shares the same Japanese name as Suikoden IV's Fly Lizard.

name: Skeleton
description: The Skeleton (スケルトン, Sukeruton) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 185 · DEX 83 · DEF 62 · SPD 65 · MAG 103 · LCK 0

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These undead monsters appear to be the skeletal remains of soldiers and have seemingly died and been revived in the same manner as Zombies. During the Dunan Unification War, these fiends haunted the areas outside Tinto City, attacking the living with their rusted swords and spears.

name: Ghost Slug
description: The Ghost Slug (ゴーストスラッグ, Gōsutosuraggu) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 205 · DEX 53 · DEF 62 · SPD 85 · MAG 115 · LCK 72

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Ghost Slug was a giant slug that appeared in the area around Tinto City. Coated in flame, it was resistant to fire and darkness magics as well as instant death and sleep techniques, although it was weak to holy magic.

Its attacks could also plunge its target into darkness or paralyze them completely, marking it as a foe not to be underestimated.

name: Sierra
description: Sierra (シエラ, Shiera) is an event enemy who appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 200 · DEX 93 · DEF 95 · SPD 125 · MAG 127 · LCK 78

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A grouchy Sierra Mikain would challenge the New Alliance Army in Tigermouth Village. Although she is herself strong, she can easily be overwhelmed by a full party, tiring her out and ending the encounter.

Following this, Sierra will join the party, determined to aid in the destruction of Neclord.

name: Neclordia
description: The Neclordia (ネクローディア, Nekurōdia) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 150 · DEX 65 · DEF 82 · SPD 153 · MAG 122 · LCK 36

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Hex Doll, Master's Garb, Knight Statue

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These Master's Garb-clad undead women appeared in the Tinto Mines during the Dunan Unification War. Just like Neclord, they have the ability to command zombies and so long as they have zombies to manipulate, will rarely attack themselves.

name: Stone Golem
description: The Stone Golem (ストーンゴーレム, Sutōngōremu) is a boss enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 230 · DEX 62 · DEF 100 · SPD 82 · MAG 85 · LCK 65

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Stone Golem is a terrifying humanoid monster formed out of rock by magic. This creature has been seen only once: in the tunnels of the Tinto Mines during the Dunan Unification War. Extremely powerful, it could lay waste to enemies with its powerful fists and its superior fire and earth magic.

Its only weakness was a susceptibility to lightning magic, it barely even reacts to physical attacks. The New Alliance Army woke this creature while traversing the mine and were barely able to overcome it.

name: Eye Flower
description: The Eye Flower (アイフラワー, Aifurawā) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 230 · DEX 240 · DEF 52 · SPD 0 · MAG 72 · LCK 98

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These plant-type monsters infested the areas around Greenhill City in the middle of Dunan Unification War. They never actively undertake aggressive behavior but can attempt to counter any attacks taken against it within its limited range. Because of this, it is a relatively harmless creature despite its fearsome looking appearance.

However, should any 'brave' warriors decide to battle these monsters, they may pick up a rare Spinach Seedling in the process.

trivia: This enemy, like the Monwer, never attacks in the game and does not even have an attack animation.

name: Kookaburra
