description: The Kookaburra (ボールバード, Bōrubādo, Ball Bird) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 230 · DEX 90 · DEF 42 · SPD 145 · MAG 45 · LCK 77

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Kookaburra is a plump avian monster that most consider cute. Despite this, it was all a vicious creature.

It appeared late in the Dunan Unification War in Greenhill Principality. Its round figure belies its quick attacks, catching its foes off guard. It is also strong enough to inflict a Knockdown effect, a bitter experience for an unprepared opponent.

trivia: * A kookaburra is a type of kingfisher bird, native to Australia and New Guinea. It is also the title of an Australian nursery rhyme about the bird.

name: Target Girl
description: The Target Girl (ターゲットガール, Tāgettogāru) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 200 · DEX 89 · DEF 73 · SPD 127 · MAG 123 · LCK 83

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Waking Orb, Nymph Orb, Pixie Orb

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This monster was spotted in the Greenhill Forest towards the end of the Dunan Unification War. Its method of attack is to target a foe and then unleash a concentrated barrage of attacks with its allies. This strategy makes it a very troublesome enemy. Its beauty also helped it attract the attention of passers-by.

Those who do defeat it, however, have a chance of recovering rare runes from the remains.

name: Grand Holly
description: The Grand Holly (ひいらぎじい, Hīragijii, Holly Grandpa) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 220 · DEX 66 · DEF 72 · SPD 54 · MAG 52 · LCK 103

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Grand Holly is the same type of creature as the Holly Boy and the Holly Master but at an advanced age. It was perhaps this advanced age that made them so powerful.

They were spotted in the Greenhill Forest in the late Dunan Unification War. They are very similar to the Papa Holly but much stronger, as would be apparent to any person who fought one.

They were said to drop a Cape of Darkness on rare occassions.

name: Karayan Soldier
description: Karayan Soldier (カラヤのせんし, Karaya no Senshi, Karaya Fighter) are an enemy which appear in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 270 · DEX 95 · DEF 47 · SPD 127 · MAG 58 · LCK 89

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These are valiant warriors of the Karaya Clan dressed in distinctive costumes. During the Dunan Unification War, they would ally with the Highland Kingdom and fight the New Alliance Army under the command of Lucia. They wield curved swords in combat that are coated with a poisonous substance.

name: Bone Dragon
description: The Bone Dragon (ボーンドラゴン, Bōndoragon) is a boss enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 350 · DEX 85 · DEF 78 · SPD 112 · MAG 140 · LCK 32

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Bone Dragon was an enormous monster, summoned by Yuber to thwart the New Alliance Army's efforts to liberate Greenhill City. It existed as only white bone, with no muscle tissue or skin, showing the dignity of the dragon's form at a glance.

Incredibly powerful and boasting a variety of magic attacks, fighting this monster proved to be a deep struggle, even for the bravest warriors of the New Alliance Army.

trivia: * The Bone Dragon has the highest HP of any single enemy in the game.

name: Golden Wolf
description: The Golden Wolf (ゴールドウルフ, Gōrudourufu) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II and Genso Suikogaiden Vol.1.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 370 · DEX 101 · DEF 66 · SPD 163 · MAG 126 · LCK 107

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Golden Wolf is a formidable monster born from the Beast Rune. It was released following Luca Blight's ritual to unseal the Beast Rune in Muse City. Controlling these monsters is difficult as they tend to attack anything in their sights.

They have a resistance to all magic as well as most staus aliments and have a mastery over lightning magic, making them very difficult to defeat.

Nash Latkje and Elza also encountered one of these monsters in the woods near Greenhill City, which was released by Seed and Culgan. Despite being overwhelmed by its speed and power, Nash and Elza were able to drop it from a cliff to its doom.

name: Fisheye
description: The Fisheye (アイフィッシュ, Aifisshu, Eye Fish) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 275 · DEX 85 · DEF 104 · SPD 143 · MAG 85 · LCK 99

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This distinctive creature has large, deep red shining eyes and sharp teeth reminiscent of carnivorous fish. Many of these creatures infested the area around Muse City late into the Dunan Unification War. Seemingly a combination of amphibian and fish, its physical form belies its impressive agility and it is able to stun its prey into submission.

name: Iron Moon
description: The Iron Moon (アイアンムーン, Aianmūn) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 280 · DEX 82 · DEF 85 · SPD 135 · MAG 137 · LCK 75

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These strange monsters appeared around Muse City towards the end of the Dunan Unification War. It has the appearance of an anthropomorphic blue crescent moon bearing the face of an old man and can skillfully use two different types of attack magic.

Its origin is a mystery, but seeing as it may carry a Landscape Scroll or a Flower Painting, it evidentially has the capacity to appreciate art.

name: Copper Sun
description: The Copper Sun (かッパーサン, Kappāsan) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 290 · DEX 77 · DEF 150 · SPD 107 · MAG 125 · LCK 88

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These humanoid monsters with copper-colored bodies were seen outside Muse City towards the end of the Dunan Unification War. These creatures have high intelligence and specialize in wide-ranging magic. Also, its skin is as hard as the copper it is named after and it can be difficult to bring one down physically. According to the few who managed to bring down these monsters, Rage Orbs can be found on their corpses.

name: Fei Yu
description: Fei Yu (フェイユ, Feiyu) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 290 · DEX 115 · DEF 103 · SPD 167 · MAG 79 · LCK 150

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Fei Yu was a female warrior, sighted around Rockaxe in the late Dunan Unification War, along with her compatriots, Li Lan and Shiu Lin. Do not let her graceful figure mislead you as she is very powerful. She held an excellent resistance to magic attacks while her staff could poison her opponents, coated as it was with venom.

As she carries a Gold Bar, a valuable trading item, it seems unlikely these three were adventurers filled with a sense of justice. She also appeared to be worried about maintaining her graceful figure as she could been seen carrying a Diet Lunch.

name: Li Lan
description: Li Lan (リィラン, Ryiran) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 300 · DEX 105 · DEF 105 · SPD 143 · MAG 88 · LCK 158

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Li Lan was a female warrior, rumored to be the sister of Fei Yu and Shiu Lin. She was spotted with her two comrades in the area around Rockaxe late into the Dunan Unification War.

Her staff could poison her opponents, coated as it was with venom. All three had a powerful life force and each, including Li Lan, was a formidable opponent in and of themselves.

name: Shiu Lin
description: Shiu Lin (シュアロン, Shuaron) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 260 · DEX 122 · DEF 98 · SPD 152 · MAG 102 · LCK 172

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Shiu Lin was a female warrior, sighted around Rockaxe in the late Dunan Unification War, along with her compatriots, Li Lan and Fei Yu. Her graceful figure belied her power and skill as a fighter.

She held an excellent resistance to magic attacks while her staff could poison her opponents, coated as it was with venom. Once defeated, she may drop the valuable Flowing Rune.

name: Black Tiger
description: The Black Tiger (ブラックタイガー, Burakkutaigā) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 330 · DEX 86 · DEF 75 · SPD 127 · MAG 52 · LCK 59

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These ferocious, black-haired tigers infested the area surrounding Rockaxe during the late Dunan Unification War. They were feral and often attack people, just like regular Tigers but their abilities were superior, overall.

name: Cockatrice
description: The Cockatrice (コカトリス, Kokatorisu) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 150 · DEX 75 · DEF 74 · SPD 137 · MAG 115 · LCK 89

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Cockatrice is a monster that has the feathered body and head of a chicken, the wings of a bat and the tail of a lizard. It has been seen lurking in Rockaxe Castle and has the power to paralyze the person it glares at. It is very swift and has a strong resistance to poison and sleep attacks.

They have been known to carry Thunder Rune orbs on rare occasions.

name: Hippogriff
description: The Hippogriff (ヒポグリフ, Hipogurifu) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 280 · DEX 92 · DEF 69 · SPD 142 · MAG 95 · LCK 76

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: According to one theory, this creature, with the upper body of an eagle and the lower body of a horse, is the child of a Griffin. Encountered at Rockaxe Castle late into the Dunan Unification War, this monster was extremely agile and possessed a large life-force. Wind Amulets could be occasionally found on its corpse.

name: Matilda Knight
description: The Matilda Knight (マチルダきし, Machiruda Kishi) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 310 · DEX 97 · DEF 102 · SPD 103 · MAG 108 · LCK 79

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Their white uniforms indicated that these are the White Knights, Gordeau's own branch of the Matilda Knights and were very confident in their abilities. They fought against the New Alliance Army in Rockaxe Castle, towards the end of the Dunan Unification War.

They are said to be talented knights and comparable in skill to the Highland Army. Some are equipped with the Knight Rune.

name: Gordeau
description: Gordeau (ゴルドー, Gorudō) is a boss enemy who appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 300 · DEX 115 · DEF 107 · SPD 98 · MAG 98 · LCK 57

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Gordeau is fought following the fall of Rockaxe Castle. He is fought with a party comprised of the hero and Jowy Atreides.

Although he is fairly strong and has a range of attacks, the power of the duo's unite attack and the Bright Shield and Black Sword Runes make him an easy opponent to defeat.

name: Sky Knight
description: The Sky Knight (スカイナイト, Sukainaito) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 308 · DEX 90 · DEF 87 · SPD 148 · MAG 75 · LCK 120

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Sky Knight was a birdman-like monster that lived on Rakutei Mountain late into the Dunan Unification War. Its sharp claws on its hands and feet not only make it dangerous as an attacker but also give it the ability to ensnare an enemy and carry them away.

Apparently, it is possible to find a single copy of the elusive Turtle Plans on its person.

name: Leathercut
description: The Leathercut (レザーカット, Rezākatto, Razorcut) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 325 · DEX 175 · DEF 46 · SPD 172 · MAG 53 · LCK 56

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Defective Urn, Blue Dragon Urn, Fine China

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Leathercut, with its scythe-like spikes for arms and high agility, seem to be a type of sickle-shaped monster, such as the Spiker. These creatures had been seen on Rakutei Mountain, late into the Dunan Unification War. Still, although nimble, their actual stamina is quite low. If attacked with full force immediately on encountering them, they should be defeated quickly.

It appeared to like antiques as several could be found on its remains.

name: Iron Claw
description: The IronClaw (アイアンクロー, Aiankurō) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 307 · DEX 92 · DEF 78 · SPD 128 · MAG 103 · LCK 143

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Iron Claws infested Rakutei Mountain towards the end of the Dunan Unification War. They are capable of unleashing a terrible blow, bringing instant death to their targets. This is due to the poison in their sharp claws that grow out of both hands.

There is speculation that Iron Claw may have evolved from the similar Assassin creature that also inhabits the area.

name: Minotaurus
description: The Minotaurus (ミノタウロス, Minotaurosu) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 280 · DEX 87 · DEF 125 · SPD 116 · MAG 89 · LCK 103

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This monster, human from the neck down and bull from the neck up, was spotted around the L'Renouille area towards the end of the Dunan Unification War. Its life force isn't very high, but it is very powerful, and its hide is as tough as bark.

Known for its brutality, it strikes using a large, spiked weapon that can inflict the Stun status on its foes. Some creatures carry the rare Mother Earth Rune.

name: Chimera
description: The Chimera (キメラ, Kimera) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 337 · DEX 105 · DEF 99 · SPD 116 · MAG 115 · LCK 89

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Chimera is a monster with a bizarre shape. It has three heads: a bird's, a goat's and a lion's. It also has bat-like wings and a snake's tail. These fearsome creatures were spotted at L'Renouille Palace towards the end of the Dunan Unification War. Since it has only been seen in the Imperial Palace, it is assumed that it is a magical beast created by some kind of mystical power.

Its powerful fire magic and other attacks make it difficult to defeat, even for veteran warriors. Difficult to come by items such as the Lucky Ring and Blood Armor have been discovered on the bodies of Chimera that such warriors have managed to fell.

trivia: * Either due to a programming error or a conscious effort to remove the data, the second Chimera (at Tenzan Pass) is actually impossible to encounter. The full data is present, and it is even listed, with full stats, in game guides.

name: Bronzem
description: The Bronzem (ブロンズゴーレム, Buronzugōremu, Bronze Golem) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 355 · DEX 85 · DEF 106 · SPD 97 · MAG 112 · LCK 89

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Bronzem is a powerful, giant humanoid monster, seemingly artificially created using magic by persons unknown. It was encountered in L'Renouille Palace at the very end of the Dunan Unification War.

Its entire body was made up of bronze and it was a very powerful monster; resistant to most magic and talented at unleashing its own earth magic. In rare cases, it has been reported that it has dropped Master's Garb, an armor known for increasing combat capability.

trivia: * The Bronzem has the highest STR of any enemy in the game.

name: Silver Wolf
description: The Silver Wolf (シルバーウルフ, Shirubāurufu) is an enemy which appear in Suikoden II. It is the final boss of the game and the incarnation of the Beast Rune.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 300 · DEX 97 · DEF 85 · SPD 95 · MAG 170 · LCK 0

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Silver Wolf was the incarnation of the Beast Rune, one of the 27 True Runes. This monster was given form from the sacrifice of the people of Muse City that Luca Blight performed during the Dunan Unification War. At the end of the war, it was summoned by the blood of Leon Silverberg in the throne room of L'Renouille Palace, turning the seat of the King of Highland into the final battlefield of the war.

The staggeringly massive beast resembled a two-headed wolf and was armed with a vast array of attacks and magic and was bolstered by the power of the Beast Rune itself. Its moon attack was particularly destructive and it was immune to damage unless the rune floating in its center was destroyed.

Even the greatest officers of the New Alliance Army found it extremely difficult to rip victory away from such a formidable opponent.

name: Arachnvore
description: The Arachnvore (スパイダー, Supaidā) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden III.

facts.label: Location
facts.value: Amur Plain, North Amur, Brass Castle, Yaza Plain (Chapter 1-3)

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: PWR 30 · SKL 50 · MAG 47 · PDF 20 · MDF 45 · SPD 42 · LUK 45

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This giant spider monster sports a vivid yellow and black striped pattern on its body. It attacks by jumping at its foe and latching on with its claws. It was mostly seen on the various plains of the Grasslands during the War of the Champions.

trivia:
* This enemy shares the same Japanese name as Suikoden II's Spider.
* The enemy's name is derived from the greek word arachnē (ἀράχνη, spider) and the latin suffix -vorus (eating, devouring). Thus, its name literally means spider eating.

name: Azzodess
description: The Azzodess (エムプーサ, Emupūsa) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden III.

facts.label: Location
facts.value: Ceremonial Site (Chapter 5), Flame Champion's Hideaway (Chapter 3 & 4, 6th POV)

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This monster was known to inhabit ruins, such as the Ceremonial Site and the Flame Champion's Hideaway. This creature had the appearance of a woman with wings, and in addition to attacking by flapping her wings, she also used high-level lightning magic.

The Azzodess could even summon monsters from another world to attack her foes, and those who were so attacked are said to be silenced out of fear.

trivia: * This monster's Japanese name refers to the Empusa, a shape-shifting female being in Greek mythology, said to possess a single leg of copper and commanded by Hecate.

name: BladeBunny
description: The BladeBunny (カットバニー, Kattobanī, Cut Bunny) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden III.

facts.label: Location
facts.value: Amur Plain, Budehuc Castle basement, Mt. Hei-Tou, Yaza Plain (Chapters 1-3)

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This creature resembles a cute, white rabbit but the stone hatchet it holds in its paws should show that it is really a fearsome monster. Swinging its hatchet wildly, these rabbits are mostly found on plains.

trivia: * This enemy has the same Japanese name as the Blade Bunny from the original Suikoden and the Blade Bunny from Suikoden II.

name: Blue Svire
description: The Blue Svire (エアドルフィン, Eadorufin, Airdolphin) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Blue Svire was a creature with a snake-like body. They swam through the air as if they were underwater, attack with their tail fins, and could charge at high speeds.

Their preferred habitats were dark and damp places and as such they were spotted on Mt. Senai and in the basement of Budehuc Castle during the War of the Champions.

name: Bronze Sun
description: The Bronze Sun (にちりんまおう, Nichirinmaou, Demon Sun King) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden III.

facts.label: Location
facts.value: Ancient Highway (Chapters 2-3), Flame Champion's Hideaway (Chapters 3-4, Luc Chapter)

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: PWR 120 · SKL 115 · MAG 102 · PDF 35 · MDF 110 · SPD 75 · LUK 66

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Fury Rune, Shield Rune, Minced Hot Pot, Minced Hot Pot

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This huge, humanoid monster floats in the air with its legs crossed and has a green pattern emblazoned on its yellow-hued skin. Floating in the air, it attacks with pillars and rings of light and was spotted at the Ancient Highway and the Flame Champion's Hideaway during the War of the Champions.

trivia: * This enemy has the same Japanese name as the Sun King from the original Suikoden and the Sun King from Suikoden II. It also shares the same English name as the Suikoden V enemy Bronze Sun, but their Japanese names differ.

name: Daggerwing
description: The Daggerwing (アローバード, Arōbādo, Arrowbird) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden III.

facts.label: Location
facts.value: North Cavern (Hugo Chapter 1), Great Hollow (Geddoe Chapter 1)

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This creepy bird-shaped monster was noted for its huge, skull-like head. In addition to attacking by grabbing the target and dropping them on the ground, it could also attack by shooting projectiles from its mouth, giving it its name.

Its main habitat was dark place and as such, it was spotted in areas such as the North Cavern and the Great Hollow during the War of the Champions.

name: Furrfly
description: The Furrfly (もさもさ, Mosamosa) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden III.

facts.label: Location
facts.value:
* Amur Plain
* Mt. Hei-Tou
* Yaza Plain

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: Furrflies are creatures covered in thick, shaggy fur. They were commonly found on the Amur Plain as well as the Yaza Plain. Their attack power is low, so it's not a particularly dangerous monster to any careful traveler.

trivia:
* Otherwise localized as the Furball, the Hairball, the Furfur, or the Laggart depending on the game.
* "Mosamosa" (もさもさ) is a Japanese onomatopoeia used to represent the sound of plants growing rapidly.

name: Guardian
description: The Guardian (ガーディアン, Gādian) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These enigmatic beings existed in a Gray World that had come to an end due to the battle between chaos and order. They resemble large eyeballs and used lasers and lightning magic to eliminate any foreign objects they came across. They were the only active beings in a world consumed by death.

name: HollyShrub
description: The HollyShrub (ひいらぎこぞう, Hīragikozō) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden III.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: PWR 3 · SKL 10 · MAG 13 · PDF 2 · MDF 10 · SPD 9 · LUK 3

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Medicine D ×6, Throat Drop ×4, Nukazuke Pickles

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These holly leaf creatures regularly appear in Zexen Forest. 0.5 meters tall (roughly 1.5 feet), they attack by jumping and launching their body at their foes. They are not very strong.

trivia: * This enemy has the same Japanese name as the Holly Boy from the original Suikoden and Suikoden II.

name: Karaya Fighter
description: The Karaya Fighter (カラヤ戦士, Karaya Senshi) is an event enemy that appears in Suikoden III. It is rendered as KarayaFtr during battle in the English localization of the game due to space limitations.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These skilled fighters were warriors of the Karaya Clan. With high combat skills, these jambiya-wielding warriors would attack swiftly and surely to fell their opponents.

They fought in several battles before and during the War of the Champions as part of the Six Clans as well as the Fire Bringer.

name: Lightning Rune Crystal
description: The Lightning Rune Crystal (雷の紋章球, Kaminari no Monshō-kyū) is a boss enemy that appears in Suikoden III. It is rendered as LgtngRCrys during battle in the English localization of the game due to space limitations.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This gigantic lightning rune orb protected the Wind Rune Incarnation during the final battle between the Fire Bringer and the Destroyers during the War of the Champions.

Born from the True Lightning Rune, it targeted and attacked the enemy with Furious Blow, an intermediate level lightning magic, and Hammer of Raijin, the highest level lightning magic.

name: Magician
description: The Magician (マジッシャン, Majisshan) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden III.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: PWR 70 · SKL 80 · MAG 96 · PDF 35 · MDF 92 · SPD 80 · LUK 59

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The Magician is a humanoid monster that floats through the air. It attacks with acrobatic kicks and by summoning magic attacks from its cards. It was seen on Mt. Senai and in the North Cavern during the War of the Champions.

trivia: * This enemy has almost the same Japanese name (with the same intended meaning) as the Nightmare from the original Suikoden and the creature of the same name from Suikoden II.

name: Malifaux
description: The Malifaux (顏柱, Kaobashira) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden III.

facts.label: Location
facts.value: Ancient Highway (Hugo 2&3, Chapters 4&5), Ruins of Cyndar (Chapter 4&5, 6th POV)

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These pillar-like monster with engraved faces appears on the Ancient Highway and in the Ruins of Cyndar during the War of the Champions They were known to jump up and attack by crushing their foes, or attack with objects floating in orbit around the pillar itself.

name: Mantikra
description: The Mantikra (カニパンチ, Kanipanchi) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This crab-like monster appeared in the Zexen and Kuput Forests during the War of the Champions.

It attacked with its giant pincers and could also spit out paralyzing bubbles. The rare delicacy Crab Rice Bowl could be made from its remains.

name: Megawaki
description: The Megawaki (メガワキ, Megawaki) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This bizarre monster has a giant eye for a head and is in fact some sort of plant creature. In addition to attacking with its sharp claws, it is also able to fire a laser from its eye. They were spotted in various underground paths at Budehuc Castle and Brass Castle during the War of the Champions.

trivia: * This enemy has the same Japanese name as Suikoden II's Mega Watt.

name: Nariqua
description: The Nariqua (ターゲットレディ, Tāgettoredi, Target Lady) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden III.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: PWR 95 · SKL 110 · MAG 120 · PDF 40 · MDF 130 · SPD 120 · LUK 160

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These tailed humanoid monsters attack both with their own attacks and by commanding its allies to attack enemies it targeted. During the War of the Champions, they were spotted in the vicinity of the Flame Champion's Hideaway.

trivia: This enemy has nearly the same Japanese name (with the same intention) as the Beast Commander from the original Suikoden and the Target Lady from Suikoden II.

name: Peckles
description: The Peckles (ペッカルス, Pekkarusu) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden III.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: PWR 75 · SKL 85 · MAG 65 · PDF 35 · MDF 60 · SPD 90 · LUK 60

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This ivory-colored wild boar was spotted on the plains of the Grasslands during the War of the Champions. They rush towards their enemies and ram them with their large bodies.

name: Purple Creeper
description: The Purple Creeper (むらさきカズラー, Murasaki Kazurā) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden III. It is rendered PrplCreeper in-game due to space limitations.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These Purple Creepers appeared on the Amur Plain and in the North Cavern. Anyone sucked into its maw is likely to find themselves in an unappealing and unfriendly state. Incidentally, perhaps because it is known to swallow anything, it's said that screws from Gadget Z were found inside it during the War of the Champions.

name: RabidRabbt
description: The RabidRabbt (ころしやうさぎ, Koroshiyausagi) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden III.

facts.label: Location
facts.value: Kuput Forest, Yaza Plain (Chapters 1-3), Mountain Path, North Amur Plain (Chapter 4)

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: PWR 79 · SKL 110 · MAG 75 · PDF 25 · MDF 70 · SPD 140 · LUK 63

facts.label: Drops
facts.value:
Kuput Forest
: Mega Medicine D ×4
Mountain Path
: Killer Rune
North Amur Plain
: Viper Rune
Yaza Plain
: Skunk Rune, Surprise Stew

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This ivory-haired rabbit creature is missing part of its ear and has a crushed eye socket. It attacks by swinging its single-edged axe in battle. It mainly appeared on the plains of the Grasslands during the War of the Champions.

It is known as the killer rabbit due to its ability to target enemy weaknesses.

trivia: * It shares the same Japanese name as Suikoden's Killer Bunny (Suikoden) and Suikoden II's Killer Bunny.

name: Sarah
description: Sarah (セラ, Sera) is an enemy boss who appears several times in Suikoden III.

facts.label: Location
facts.value:
, 
* Mt. Senai (Geddoe Chapter 2)
* Alma Kinan Village (Chris Chapter 3)
* Flame Champion's Hideaway (Chapter 4)
* Outside Duck Village (Cha

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: PWR 75 · SKL 110 · MAG 135 · PDF 60 · MDF 125 · SPD 95 · LUK 68

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: Sarah can be fought a total of eight times over the course of the game. She is a powerful mage, capable of healing her comrades in battle with the Kindness Rain spell. As such, she should usually be taken out first in any battle the player has with her.

name: SongSprite
description: The SongSprite (ドレミのせい, Doremi no Sei, Doremi Sprite) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden III.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: PWR 60 · SKL 85 · MAG 110 · PDF 15 · MDF 110 · SPD 120 · LUK 60

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These cheerful sound sprites wear bright multi-colored clothing and attack through the music, either individually or as part of a chorus, which hits multiple targets. They were seen mostly in the Zexen Forest during the War of the Champions.

trivia:
* This creature shares the same Japanese name as Suikoden II's DoReMi Elf.

}

name: Soul Slug
description: The Soul Slug (ナメゾンビ, Namezonbi, Slug Zombie) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden III.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: PWR 55 · SKL 75 · MAG 72 · PDF 40 · MDF 75 · SPD 50 · LUK 49

facts.label: Drops
facts.value:
Ancient Highway, Chapter 4+
:Hex Doll
Great Hollow, Geddoe Chapter 1
:Hex Doll, Peeing Boy, Grilled Fish

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This monster is known for its translucent light blue body. It can breath blue fire from its mouth and also has the ability to absorb the health of its opponents. It was mainly spotted on the Ancient Highway during the War of the Champions.

trivia: * This creature shares the same Japanese name as Suikoden II's Zombie Slug.

name: Wind Rune Incarnation
description: The Wind Rune Incarnation (風の紋章の化身, Kaze no Monshō no Keshin) is a boss enemy that appears in Suikoden III. It is the final boss of the game. It is rendered as WindRIncar during battle in the English localization of the game due to space limitations.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This massive creature was the embodiment of Luc and the power of the True Wind Rune he bore. The incarnation looked like a phoenix shining in emerald, emitting a divine light from its corporeal body.

It possessed not only the power of the True Wind Rune, but also the power of the other elemental True Runes, which materialized as gigantic rune orbs. In terms of attacks, it had high-speed body strikes, could shoot countless lasers from its wings, and could even combine the power of the five elemental runes to devastating effect.

name: Yellow Svire
description: The Yellow Svire (イッカク, Ikkaku) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden III.

facts.label: Location
facts.value: Budehuc Castle (Chris 3 / Geddoe 2&3 / Hugo 3), Kuput Forest (Chris 3), Mountain Path (Chris 3 / Geddoe 1-3 / Hugo 3), Mt. Hei-Tou (Chris 3

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Yellow Svire was a snake-like monster with large horns. It moved as if swimming through the air and attacked with both its tail and horns.

Their habitat was wide, and during the War of the Champions they could be found in varied locations from Mt. Hei-Tou and the Mountain Path, to Kuput Forest and even under Budehuc Castle. There were also reports that if defeated, a Banshak Sake Set could be found in its remains.

name: ZombUnicrn
description: The ZombUnicrn (ユニコーンゾンビ, Yunikōnzonbi, Unicorn Zombie) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden III.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: PWR 90 · SKL 100 · MAG 110 · PDF 25 · MDF 100 · SPD 120 · LUK 70

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This creature is a unicorn-type monster that is about 1.5 times the size of a regular horse. They tend to thrust forward and skewer opponents with their horn or cast powerful lightning magic. They were spotted at the North Cavern during the War of the Champions.

trivia: * This enemy shares the same Japanese name as the original Suikoden's Hell Unicorn and Suikoden II's Zombie Unicorn.

description: ??? (？？？, ???), real identity Glen (グレン, Guren), is an event enemy that appears in Suikoden IV. He fights alongside ???? in the first battle of the game.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 10 · SKL 10 · MAG 14 · PDF 10 · MDF 10 · EVA 2 · SPD 8 · LUK 8

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Commander Glen would board Snowe Vingerhut's Gaien Ship towards the conclusion of their training exercise.

Engaging the trainees alongside his adjutant Katarina, Glen proved to be readily able to deal with his students attacks.

trivia:
* He is referred to as '???' in-game but as '??? (Glen)' in strategy guides.
* His HP is essentially infinite and he cannot be defeated, except with cheats. The battle will eventually just end.

description: ??? (???, ???), real identity Troy (トロイ, Toroi), is an event enemy that appears in Suikoden IV. He fights alongside Colton aboard the Mystery Merchant Ship.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 380 · SKL 500 · MAG 500 · PDF 107 · MDF 500 · EVA 500 · SPD 500 · LUK 500

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Following the hero's exile from Razril, he and his friends would eventually be spotted by a Kooluk ship posing as a merchant vessel.

When Troy and Colton discovered the group overhearing their conversation with their spies, they would attempt to silence the group. Although easily overwhelmed, Troy would let the group escape, reasoning that, as exiles, they could not warn Gaien and that the sea should decide their fate.

trivia:
* He is referred to as '???' in-game but as '??? (Troy)' in strategy guides.
* His HP is essentially infinite at 50000, and he cannot be defeated, save for cheats. His stats are similarly unknown, even in the official strategy guide.
* Despite being unbeatable, he has a coded potch payout and item drop rate.

description: ???? (？？？？, ????), real identity Katarina (カタリナ, Katarina), is an event enemy that appears in Suikoden IV. She fights alongside ??? in the first battle of the game.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 5 · SKL 4 · MAG 20 · PDF 5 · MDF 10 · EVA 8 · SPD 7 · LUK 6

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Vice-Commander Katarina would board Snowe Vingerhut's Gaien Ship towards the conclusion of their training exercise.

Engaging the trainees alongside her superior, Glen Cott, Katarina proved to be readily able to deal with her students attacks.

trivia:
* She is referred to as '????' in-game but as '???? (Katarina)' in strategy guides.
* Like Glen, her HP is essentially infinite and she cannot be defeated, except with cheats. The battle will eventually just end.

name: Ancient Crab
description: The Ancient Crab (古代ガニ, Kodaigani) is a fixed boss enemy that appears in Suikoden IV. It can be fought on the Deserted Island after the Regaining Razril naval battle. Unusually for a boss encounter it respawns every time the player leaves the island.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 240 · SKL 160 · MAG 120 · PDF 150 · MDF 160 · EVA 160 · SPD 100 · LUK 160

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Giant Crab's Shell, Fire Rune Piece, Crab Bun

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This giant crab, a survivor of ancient times, appeared atop the hills of the Deserted Island. Its strong shell offered it enhanced defenses which could not be penetrated by half-hearted attacks. Even strong wind magic would be like a soft breeze to this shell.

Its pincers were powerful, as was the flames it could breathe from its mouth. Still, those flames also gave a clue to its weakness, a large weakness to water magic. It is said that any who can defeat this Ancient Crab will find a large amount of potch as well as perhaps a Giant Crab's Shell.

name: Angel Hairball
description: The Angel Hairball (光もさもさ, Hikari Mosamosa, Light Furball) is a fixed enemy that appears in Suikoden IV. They can be fought three times in the game, each time hidden in a treasure chest.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 350 · SKL 150 · MAG 200 · PDF 300 · MDF 150 · EVA 150 · SPD 150 · LUK 150

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This shining, brilliantly haired hairball can be found hidden in treasure chests on Iluya Island and in the Ruins of Obel. This dangerous foe has high strength, high defense against most types of magic, and a variety of spells at its disposal.

Treasure chests that contain these creatures hide valuable treasures inside for those able to defeat it.

name: Armored Wolf
description: The Armored Wolf (よろいおおかみ, Yoroi Ōkami ) is an enemy in Suikoden IV.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 265 · SKL 110 · MAG 100 · PDF 125 · MDF 100 · EVA 100 · SPD 90 · LUK 90

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A wolf-like beast whose sturdy fur is akin to armor. It hid in the darkness of the Ruins of Obel, jumping out at its prey, attacking with vicious bites.

The fangs of this creature contain a weak venom and when bitten, their target may become paralyzed and unable to move.

name: Bloody Behemoth
description: The Bloody Behemoth (ブラッディコング, Buraddikongu, Bloody Kong) is an enemy in Suikoden IV.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 190 · SKL 80 · MAG 145 · PDF 120 · MDF 80 · EVA 80 · SPD 50 · LUK 145

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This monster lurked the ruins of Iluya Island. Normally, these creatures lived peacefully among abundant nature, but its docile side has since disappeared due to the destruction of the island.

The horns of these behemoths are very rare materials that are not widely available.

name: Blue Rodent
description: Blue Rodent (むささびブルー, Musasabi Burū) is an enemy in Suikoden IV.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 68 · SKL 40 · MAG 40 · PDF 63 · MDF 40 · EVA 40 · SPD 80 · LUK 38

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Stone of Defense, Water Rune Piece, Steamed Bun

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These blue-haired flying squirrels could be found on Obel, as well as nearby Mordo Island. They can inflict poison or balloon on struck opponents, making them troublesome despite their low attack power.

Its attacks were infused with water magic and Water Rune Pieces could sometimes be found in its remains. Even more rarely, a Stone of Defense could even be found.

name: Boss Crab
description: The Boss Crab (ヌシガ二, Nushigani) is a boss event enemy that appears in Suikoden IV. It is fought on the Deserted Island after repairing the wrecked Exile Ship. When first fought, it was accompanied by two Wild Crabs. After its defense is dropped, it is fought alongside six Wild Crabs.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 50 · SKL 40 · MAG 20 · PDF 35 · MDF 50 · EVA 20 · SPD 20 · LUK 33

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This giant monster served as the boss of the crabs that lived on the Deserted Island. It seems to be very protective of its crabs and attacking any of its children will enrage it. It fights with powerful water breath attacks.

The Boss Crab had very strong defenses and giant pincers to attack with. When defending itself, it could repel most any attack with ease but when brought into an attack posture, its defenses would drop quite considerably. The hero had to use his Rune of Punishment to drop the creature's defenses and rescue Chiepoo.

It is said that Boss Crab meat tastes very good. When the exiled hero and his friends defeated the Boss Crab on the island, they cooked the body and had an enormous, hearty meal before leaving the island.

name: Brandeau's Shadow
description: Brandeau's Shadow (ブランドの影, Burando no Kage) is an event enemy that appears in Suikoden IV. He is "fought" following the Defending Obel naval battle.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 0 · SKL 0 · MAG 0 · PDF 0 · MDF 0 · EVA 0 · SPD 0 · LUK 0

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The ghost of the pirate Brandeau, after his soul was devoured by the Rune of Punishment. Like the other Mysterious Shadows, he suffered, trapped in the realm of the rune.

Released from his suffering by the hero, the rune grew in power.

trivia: * Unsurprisingly, Brandeau's Shadow ties for lowest stats in all of Suikoden IV with the other shadows.

name: Captain's Staff
description: The Captain's Staff (船長部下, Senchō-buka) are an event enemy that appears in Suikoden IV. They are fought after rescuing Lilin. There are six different enemy types but they are identical in terms of enemy data.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 130 · SKL 55 · MAG 55 · PDF 90 · MDF 55 · EVA 55 · SPD 55 · LUK 55

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These goons served as sailors on a Cray Trading Company vessel. They were sent to attack the hero by their captain after he refused to release Lilin into their nefarious clutches.

Each of the staff had rune pieces attached to their weapon. It must also be noted that they were highly armed which seemed slightly unnecessary for a mere trading vessel. Each of the staff also held Medicine and could be dropped by them on their defeat.

name: Damp Hairball
description: The Damp Hairball (海もさもさ, Umi Mosamosa) is an event enemy that appears in Suikoden IV. It is an early game event enemy found in the seas around Razril and is later fought as a regular encounter in the seas surrounding Obel

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 40 · SKL 9 · MAG 8 · PDF 35 · MDF 35 · EVA 9 · SPD 15 · LUK 9

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This creature was an ocean-residing furball. Contrary to its soft, furry appearance, it was a surprisingly powerful enemy that resisted weapons as well as lightning and fire magic attacks.

There was once a Damp Hairball residing in the waters near Middleport, annoying fishermen and keeping them from their work, but it would be exterminated by the Knights of Gaien.

name: Dead Blade
description: The Dead Blade (死せる剣士, Shiseru Kenshi) is an enemy in Suikoden IV. It is only encountered on the Fog Ship.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 220 · SKL 74 · MAG 76 · PDF 120 · MDF 80 · EVA 72 · SPD 77 · LUK 71

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These skeletons of dead swordsmen were seen, moving again, on the mysterious Fog Ship. An excellent swordsman before their death, they have a very well-made blade, equipped with a Wind Rune Piece.

It has a strong regeneration ability, and to truly vanquish it, all enemies have to be defeated at the same time, so as to not give it time to regenerate.

name: Demon Frog
description: The Demon Frog (鬼ガエル, Oni-gaeru) is an enemy in Suikoden IV.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 250 · SKL 20 · MAG 30 · PDF 50 · MDF 50 · EVA 20 · SPD 50 · LUK 50

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Nay Island was the home of this huge, solidly built monster. Incredibly strong, this Demon Frog would strike with its giant club, which could stun its target upon impact.

Due to its sheer imposing strength, it proves to be a difficult foe for whoever encounters it.

name: Demon Hairball
description: The Demon Hairball (鬼もさもさ, Oni Mosamosa) is an enemy in Suikoden IV.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 126 · SKL 126 · MAG 20 · PDF 198 · MDF 126 · EVA 126 · SPD 126 · LUK 126

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Stone of Strength, Unicorn's Horn, Salad

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This breed of Hairball had been spotted in the Kuknasi Sea, especially around Mountain Mass Island. Just as most hairballs, its sturdy body made it difficult for standard weapons to damage it heavily.

Although its physical attacks were simple, they could occasionally confuse the target. Demon Hairballs were also able to use fire magic to attack multiple foes at once.

name: Demon Wolf
description: The Demon Wolf (たてがみおおかみ, Tategami Ōkami ) is an enemy in Suikoden IV.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 135 · SKL 55 · MAG 55 · PDF 105 · MDF 55 · EVA 55 · SPD 55 · LUK 55

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This bizarre creature lived on Hermitage Island. It had a face resembling a humans and gave an unsettling impression to those who encountered it.

Its attacks were simple leaps and bites, but it could sometimes cause the balloon effect. Alone, the Demon Wolf is not very imposing but it can be terrifying if one is attacked by a pack.

name: Dry Fly
description: The Dry Fly (ドライフライ, Doraifurai) is an enemy in Suikoden IV.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 220 · SKL 83 · MAG 83 · PDF 122 · MDF 83 · EVA 83 · SPD 83 · LUK 83

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This huge bee-like creature was encountered in the dense forests of the Deserted Island. It buzzes quickly through the air, piercing foes with the needle protruding from its bottom.

These needles are very poisonous and so are likely to inject poison into anyone who is pierced by one. Although not such a great threat alone, in a large group and a protracted battle, they can be quite dangerous.

name: Evil Bat
description: The Evil Bat (イビルバット, Ibirubatto) is an enemy in Suikoden IV.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 185 · SKL 83 · MAG 83 · PDF 132 · MDF 83 · EVA 83 · SPD 83 · LUK 83

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These bat creatures were spotted on Mountain Mass Island. They fly easily and attack with their sharp talons.

These talons are highly venomous, symbolizing their evil, and they can silence or poison their targets with them.

name: Fish Fiend
description: The Fish Fiend (インスマウス, Insumausu, Innsmouth) is an enemy in Suikoden IV.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 190 · SKL 83 · MAG 70 · PDF 144 · MDF 83 · EVA 83 · SPD 83 · LUK 83

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Scale, Water Rune Piece, Broiled Fish

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These land-dwelling fishmen were seen on Mountain Mass Island. They use their legs to jump and close the distance on their foes and then swiping with their thick tail or using their ice magic.

Rare Water Rune Pieces can be found on their bodies, due to their affinity for the ocean.

trivia: * The Japanese name for this enemy, "Innsmouth", is a reference to the H.P. Lovecraft work "The Shadow Over Innsmouth", which features malign, occult fish creatures.

name: Fishman
description: The Fishman (魚人, Gyojin) is an enemy in Suikoden IV.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 180 · SKL 83 · MAG 70 · PDF 132 · MDF 83 · EVA 83 · SPD 83 · LUK 83

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Scale, Water Rune Piece, Broiled Fish

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These land-dwelling Fishmen were seen on Donut Island. They use their legs to jump and close the distance on their foes and then swiping with their thick tail or using their ice magic.

They tend to get in the way of adventurers searching for treasure on the island.

name: Fly Lizard
description: The Fly Lizard (フライリザード, Furairizādo) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden IV.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 35 · SKL 6 · MAG 10 · PDF 22 · MDF 10 · EVA 6 · SPD 8 · LUK 10

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These common enemies can be found in a variety of locations, mainly around the Kingdom of Obel. With its bat-like wings, it flies over its prey's head and attacks with its talons.

It's not a very powerful monster, but is merciless when it finds weakened prey, such as criminals left adrift on the seas. Fly Lizards such as these attacked the hero and his friends when they were exiled from Razril.

trivia: * This enemy shares the same Japanese name as Suikoden II's Air Lizard.

name: Fossil Beast
description: The Fossil Beast (化石獣, Kasekijū) is an enemy in Suikoden IV.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 10 · SKL 50 · MAG 20 · PDF 40 · MDF 50 · EVA 60 · SPD 50 · LUK 50

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The bizarre moving fossils of an ancient creature, this beast existed on Hermitage Island. It rarely attacked directly, preferring to use its powerful lightning strikes.

The prevailing theory is that it was some form of unicorn before this form, as Unicorn's Horn can be found on its defeated body.

name: Gaien Soldier
description: The Gaien Soldier (ガイエン兵, Gaien-hei) is an enemy in Suikoden IV. They are only encountered a handful of times in the game.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 23 · SKL 13 · MAG 13 · PDF 22 · MDF 13 · EVA 13 · SPD 13 · LUK 13

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
One Gaien Soldier was a fully-fledged Knight of Gaien assigned to the Gaien Ship for an escort mission to Iluya Island. Such knights are better equipped than trainees and their swords usually have some rune attached.

Such knights are usually busy with their assigned duties but this one was able to make time for training during the journey and would spar with the hero and Snowe if requested.

Two Gaien Soldiers would accompany Lord Vingerhut when he tried to reassert control of Razril following its liberation by the Allied Forces. These soldiers were easily dispatched and quickly left with Lord Vingerhut.

trivia: * I'm pretty sure the Regaining Razril naval battle variant of this enemy cannot be encountered as the Gaien Ship always defects to your army on turn two. Though it might be possible to avert this or engage the ship on turn one somehow.

name: Giant Bat
description: The Giant Bat (おおこうもり, Ōkōmori) is an enemy in Suikoden IV.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 65 · SKL 29 · MAG 29 · PDF 29 · MDF 39 · EVA 25 · SPD 50 · LUK 29

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These creatures dwelled in the caves of the Deserted Island. Highly agile, they were able to swoop at their prey before attacking with the sharp talons on their feet.

Although they had no special attacks as such, their high speed meant they could attack before their target could act, making the troublesome opponents. In rare cases, Wind Rune Pieces could be found when they were defeated.

name: Giant Tree
description: The Giant Tree (巨大樹, Kyodai-ju) is an event boss enemy that appears in Suikoden IV. It is accompanied by two Seeds of Light, which it will periodically replenish if they are defeated. It is the final boss of the game.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 250 · SKL 160 · MAG 160 · PDF 120 · MDF 200 · EVA 160 · SPD 160 · LUK 160

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The final weapon of the "Merchant of Death", Graham Cray, this monstrosity was located at the top of El-Eal Fortress. Its daunting appearance was that of a giant dragon with two large trees growing from its back.

It used a vast variety of attacks in battle, based on the five elements. There was a noted connection between the color of its leaves and the attack method it would use. Its most powerful attack was a ray of light called God's Grace, that could destroy almost any opponent in one blow, no matter their strength.

The abilities of this Giant Tree were reported to have been used for powering the Magnus Rune Cannon mounted at El-Eal Fortress. Upon its defeat, the Giant Tree's ammunition began to explode, eventually destroying the fortress completely as the Allied Forces rushed to evacuate the area.

trivia: * The Giant Tree has the highest LVL, HP, MGC, MDF, and SPD totals of any enemy in the game. It also has by far the most attacks for a single enemy in the game at seven.

name: Glen's Shadow
description: Glen's Shadow (グレンの影, Guren no Kage) is an event enemy that appears in Suikoden IV. He is "fought" following the Mysterious Fleet naval battle.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 0 · SKL 0 · MAG 0 · PDF 0 · MDF 0 · EVA 0 · SPD 0 · LUK 0

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The ghost of the Glen Cott, Commander of the Knights of Gaien, his soul devoured by the Rune of Punishment after using all its power to drive away the pirate fleet that attacked Razril.

Like the other Mysterious Shadows, he suffered, trapped in the realm of the rune. Released from his suffering by the hero, the rune grew in power.

trivia: * Unsurprisingly, Glen's Shadow ties for lowest stats in all of Suikoden IV with the other shadows. However, his Death resistance is zero, meaning he could be considered the weakest of all the shadows.

name: Golden Hairball
description: The Golden Hairball (金もさもさ, Kin Mosamosa) is a fixed enemy that appears in Suikoden IV. They can be fought twice in the game, both times hidden in treasure chests.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 10 · SKL 50 · MAG 100 · PDF 30 · MDF 50 · EVA 50 · SPD 50 · LUK 50

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Stone of Evasion, Unicorn's Horn, Salad

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This shining, golden haired hairball can be found hidden in treasure chests on Na-Nal and Mountain Mass Island. It usually attacks by slamming its body into its foe, or by using fire magic. It can confuse the enemy when it strikes, but it is not very powerful besides that.

It may drop a Stone of Evasion upon its defeat.

name: Golden Wing
description: The Golden Wing (ゴールドウイング, Gōrudouingu) is an enemy in Suikoden IV.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 220 · SKL 83 · MAG 83 · PDF 122 · MDF 83 · EVA 83 · SPD 83 · LUK 83

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These bat-like creatures lurked the Deserted Island. Highly agile, they were able to swoop at their prey with their gaudy-colored wings before attacking with the sharp talons on their feet.

Although they had no special attacks as such, their high speed meant they could attack before their target could act, making the troublesome opponents. Caution is advised as they are strong, especially in packs, and are able to counterattack their foes.

name: Golem
description: The Golem (ゴーレム, Gōremu) is an enemy in Suikoden IV.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 140 · SKL 140 · MAG 80 · PDF 150 · MDF 140 · EVA 140 · SPD 80 · LUK 140

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This was a guardian made to protect the Ruins of Obel. It boasts tremendous physical strength and has the ability to repair damage.

It is very strong in attacking, and can unleash powerful punches as well as beams of energy. Diamonds and Platinum are sometimes found after battling it.

name: Grain of Light
description: The Grain of Light (光のつぶ, Hikari no Tsubu) is an enemy in Suikoden IV.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 210 · SKL 129 · MAG 129 · PDF 207 · MDF 129 · EVA 129 · SPD 129 · LUK 129

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The mysterious foe appeared on the spiral staircase within El-Eal Fortress. It was a powerful enemy who used strong magic, being able to both cure allies and harm foes with their spells.

Luckily for any who come across them, their durability was not very high and so they could be easily destroyed under concentrated attacks.

name: Guard
description: The Guard (番人, Ban'nin) is a boss event enemy that appears in Suikoden IV. It is fought within the Ruins of Obel, before meeting Rikie, and again, after meeting Rikie.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 130 · SKL 71 · MAG 68 · PDF 100 · MDF 69 · EVA 71 · SPD 50 · LUK 73

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This powerful guardian protects the Ruins of Obel. Equipped with powerful weapons such as eye beams and ultrasonic waves, it endeavored to repel any intruders with its relentless attacks.

The Guard is also able to heal itself, but the repair function is built into its hands, so if it fights without its hands, it is unable to repair itself. When it was damaged by the hero, it would retreat before attacking again later, now with its hands.

Upon its destruction, a blacksmith's Iron Hammer can be found in the rubble.

name: Hairball
description: The Hairball (ふさふさ, Fusafusa) is an enemy in Suikoden IV.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 220 · SKL 90 · MAG 5 · PDF 130 · MDF 90 · EVA 80 · SPD 70 · LUK 90

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These furry, soft-looking creatures inhabit the Secret Underground Path beneath Middleport.

Although they looked cute, this creatures could confuse their opponents with their attacks.

trivia:
* "Fusafusa" (ふさふさ) is a Japanese onomatopoeia used to denote fluffiness.
* This creature shares its Japanese name with the Furball enemies of Suikoden and Suikoden II.

name: Horned Behemoth
description: The Horned Behemoth (ホーンドコング, Hōndokongu, Horned Kong) is a boss event enemy that appears in Suikoden IV.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 175 · SKL 100 · MAG 122 · PDF 112 · MDF 122 · EVA 90 · SPD 122 · LUK 90

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Horn, Earth Rune Piece, Steamed Bun

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This huge beast, covered in muscles, lived on Hermitage Island. Normally, these behemoth moved on four legs but when attacking, could strike its target with its forearm like a log.

This particular Horned Behemoth attacked the hero as he traveled to complete Elenor's tasks and wake his comrades. Forced to fight the beast alone, the hero would eventually triumph.

The horn of this behemoths, one of its more striking features, can be obtained after its defeat.

name: Ice Bird
description: The Ice Bird (アイスバード, Aisubādo) is an enemy in Suikoden IV.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 188 · SKL 94 · MAG 94 · PDF 120 · MDF 94 · EVA 94 · SPD 94 · LUK 94

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Giant Bird's Feather, Water Rune Piece

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These bird creatures, which glowed with a white, cold aura, were often seen in the waters around Razril. They do not fly with their wings. Instead these Ice Birds ran up to their targets, striking them or attacking with an ice breath.

Occasionally, these creatures would drop Water Rune Pieces upon defeat.

name: Jellyfish Man
description: Jellyfish Man (くらげおとこ, Kurage Otoko) is an enemy in Suikoden IV.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 150 · SKL 53 · MAG 53 · PDF 80 · MDF 53 · EVA 40 · SPD 53 · LUK 53

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Lightning Rune Piece, Stone of Technique

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This creepy monster has a jellyfish-like transparent body which encased a glowing, red nucleus. It was found in many parts of the Kuknasi Sea around Mordo Island.

It has lightning running through its body and could attack by swinging its appendages or by showing a laser from its head nucleus at multiple targets. Stones of Technique could occasionally be found in its remains.

name: Jellyman
description: The Jellyman (ゼリーマン, Zerīman) is an enemy in Suikoden IV.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 95 · SKL 46 · MAG 45 · PDF 40 · MDF 47 · EVA 47 · SPD 42 · LUK 43

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These humanoid-shaped creatures was seen at the Ruins of Obel. Its sword has a Wind Rune Piece attached and it can poison or send its foe to sleep when struck.

Its soft, jelly-like body gives the ability to heal wounds quickly and to stand up even after being defeated. To truly vanquish it, all enemies have to be defeated at the same time, so as to not give it time to regenerate.

name: Kidnapper
description: The Kidnapper (人さらい, Hitosarai) is an event enemy that appears in Suikoden IV. He is fought alongside his minion, the Kidnapper Underling.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 8 · SKL 8 · MAG 12 · PDF 18 · MDF 16 · EVA 6 · SPD 8 · LUK 6

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Mackerel Miso Stew, Pot of Razril

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This villain attempted to kidnap a young girl in Razril during the town's Kindling Ritual, taking advantage of the mostly secluded back streets for his scheme. He works alongside his minion, the Kidnapper Underling.

Although tougher than the town's regular thugs, he is still not much to handle for a formally trained Gaien Knight. When defeated, he drops a Pot of Razril, which can be assumed to be stolen goods.

name: Kidnapper Underling
description: The Kidnapper Underling (人さらい子分, Hitosarai Kobun) is an event enemy that appears in Suikoden IV. He is fought alongside his boss, the Kidnapper.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 4 · SKL 4 · MAG 6 · PDF 12 · MDF 10 · EVA 4 · SPD 4 · LUK 4

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This villain was an accomplice in the Kidnapper's plot in Razril during the town's Kindling Ritual.

Although involved in this crime, he only has the ability of a regular thug. When defeated, he may drop some Graffiti, but the item is nearly as worthless as the criminal holding it.

name: Killer Fish
description: The Killer Fish (キラーフィッシュ, Kirāfisshu) is an enemy in Suikoden IV.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 170 · SKL 77 · MAG 77 · PDF 140 · MDF 90 · EVA 60 · SPD 77 · LUK 77

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These fish monsters were found in the waters around the island of Razril. They use their legs to jump and close the distance on their foes and then swiping with their thick tail.

Although it has no special means of attack, such as poison or magic, one should still be wary of any creature that has earned the name "Killer Fish".

name: Killer Fly
description: The Killer Fly (キラーフライ, Kirāfurai) is an enemy in Suikoden IV.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 130 · SKL 52 · MAG 52 · PDF 80 · MDF 52 · EVA 52 · SPD 52 · LUK 52

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This huge bee-like creature was encountered mostly on Nay Island. It buzzes quickly through the air, piercing foes with the needle protruding from its bottom.

These needles are very poisonous and so are likely to inject poison into anyone who is pierced by one. If one had no means of detoxification, a protracted fight could be very dangerous.

name: Killer Golem
description: The Killer Golem (キラーゴーレム, Kirāgōremu) is a fixed boss enemy that appears in Suikoden IV. It is fought within the Ruins of Obel clearing. Unusually for a boss encounter, it respawns every time the player leaves the ruins.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 275 · SKL 120 · MAG 140 · PDF 150 · MDF 140 · EVA 96 · SPD 86 · LUK 50

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This was the strongest guardian protecting the Ruins of Obel. It boasts inexhaustible physical strength several times that of its counterpart, the Guard, and has the ability to repair damage.

The punch from its giant fist can kill even a well-prepared adventurer with a single blow, and the beams emitted from its left hand wreak destruction over a wide area. It appears to be made of higher quality materials than a normal golem, and if it can be defeated, it seems that there is a high probability that Diamonds and Platinum can be obtained.

name: Killer Ray
description: The Killer Rays (殺人エイ, Satsujinei) are a boss event enemy that appears in Suikoden IV. They are fought after sailing away from the Deserted Island and are the final bosses of the Exile Ship segment of the game.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 50 · SKL 30 · MAG 30 · PDF 35 · MDF 25 · EVA 30 · SPD 50 · LUK 50

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These ray-shaped monsters that lives in the waters around the Deserted Island. Two of these creatures were encountered, a brown ray and a green ray, acting in concert.

They were powerful enemies that not only used body slams and beam attacks, but also had a powerful combo attack in which the two soared high before striking their opponents simultaneously. In addition, the green Killer Ray also held the ability to heal wounds.

As the green Killer Ray can heal, and also has a strong weakness to the Rune of Punishment, it is recommended that you strike down the green, left-most, Killer Ray first.

name: Killer Rodent
description: Killer Rodent (ころしやむささび, Koroshiya Musasabi) is an enemy in Suikoden IV.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 76 · SKL 46 · MAG 39 · PDF 70 · MDF 47 · EVA 36 · SPD 46 · LUK 46

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Stone of Technique, Lightning Rune Piece, Steamed Bun

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These black flying squirrels lived on Obel, as well as nearby Mordo Island. Its name is no exaggeration as its swift gliding attack can sometimes strike a foe's critical point, felling them with one blow.

In rare cases, Stones of Technique can be found on their bodies, providing a chance to increase one's technique.

name: Kooluk Archer
description: The Kooluk Archer (クールーク弓兵, Kūrūku-yumihei) is an enemy in Suikoden IV. They are fought at various strengths several times over the course of the game.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 190 · SKL 78 · MAG 78 · PDF 120 · MDF 100 · EVA 78 · SPD 78 · LUK 78

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These were standard soldiers of the Kooluk Empire and appeared in various locations during the Island Liberation War, usually in occupied territories as well as El-Eal Fortress.

Soldiers were usually equipped with either swords or bows with runes attached, so enemies could have a variety of attack methods. In defeat, pieces of art could often be found.

name: Kooluk Captain
description: The Kooluk Captain (クールーク隊長, Kūrūku Taichō) is an enemy in Suikoden IV. They are fought at various strengths several times over the course of the game.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 87 · SKL 145 · MAG 87 · PDF 87 · MDF 87 · EVA 87 · SPD 87 · LUK 87

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Maestro Graffiti, Celadon Pot, Meat Bun

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A Kooluk military commander, distinguished from ordinary rank-and-file soldiers by the prominent decoration on their helmets.

These captains are more skilled than ordinary soldiers and can also treat wounded allies with their magic, making them very troublesome on the battlefield. It seems Kooluk Captains have an interest in art; many fine pieces can be found after they are defeated.

name: Kooluk Soldier
description: The Kooluk Soldier (クールーク兵, Kūrūku-hei) is an enemy in Suikoden IV. They are fought at various strengths several times over the course of the game.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 50 · SKL 20 · MAG 29 · PDF 20 · MDF 29 · EVA 20 · SPD 20 · LUK 29

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These were standard soldiers of the Kooluk Empire and appeared in various locations during the Island Liberation War, usually in occupied territories as well as El-Eal Fortress.

Soldiers were usually equipped with either swords or bows with runes attached, so enemies could have a variety of attack methods. In defeat, pieces of art could often be found.

name: Land Dragon
description: The Land Dragon (陸竜, Riku Ryū) is a fixed boss enemy that appears in Suikoden IV. It is fought in the Secret Underground Path, just outside Warlock's Underground Laboratory. Unusually for a boss encounter, it respawns every time the player leaves the cavern.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 210 · SKL 166 · MAG 140 · PDF 100 · MDF 166 · EVA 166 · SPD 140 · LUK 166

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This rare dragon lurked in the Secret Underground Path found underneath a vacant home in Middleport. The attacks of this dragon were imbued with powerful earth magic. 

This foe was very powerful in every sense of the word and was able to cut its enemies with the wind force of its tail swipes as well as having powerful breath attacks. Its body could sometimes be found to hold rare materials such as the Dragon Scale and Dragon's Bone.

name: Land Shell
description: The Land Shell (大陸貝, Tairikukai) is an enemy in Suikoden IV. It is fought alongside four tentacles.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 150 · SKL 150 · MAG 150 · PDF 150 · MDF 150 · EVA 150 · SPD 150 · LUK 150

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This giant monster earned its name as those who saw it compared it to the size of a continent. This creature was rarely encountered but seemed to hide in the waters near Middleport.

Extremely powerful, the Land Shell could attack anyone on deck with its tentacles and by spraying ink. It would also ram the ship with its body, shaking the entire vessel violently. No mean feat, considering the size of the Stronghold Ship itself. This foe was also immune to all manner of status affecting magics.

Due to its size and relative distance from the ship, short ranged fighters cannot engage the Land Shell. Instead, ranged fighters and magicians are recommended for this encounter. In battle, removing its tentacles is imperative so as to remove the powerful fire attack from its arsenal.

name: Leviathan
description: The Leviathan (リヴァイアサン, Rivuaiasan) is an enemy in Suikoden IV.

facts.label: Location
facts.value: Sea aboard Mountain Mass Island (late game)

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 130 · SKL 130 · MAG 130 · PDF 130 · MDF 130 · EVA 130 · SPD 130 · LUK 130

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Leviathan was a giant monster that resided in the Kuknasi Sea, in waters close to Mountain Mass Island.

It had many powerful attacks, such as knocking prey asleep with lightning as well as highly damaging breath attacks. It resisted many forms of magic but did hold a susceptibility to lightning magic.

Precious materials, such as the Dragon Scale and Dragon's Bone could be obtained from its remains.

name: Metal Crab
description: The Metal Crab (メタルクラブ, Metarukurabu) is an enemy in Suikoden IV.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 120 · SKL 95 · MAG 95 · PDF 170 · MDF 95 · EVA 95 · SPD 95 · LUK 95

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Metal Crab is a rarely seen giant crab that is said to have been sighted in the waters around the Deserted Island. It has been compared to a fortress due to its high physical power as well as its strong shell offering it enhance defense.

Its pincers hold the power of lightning magic and can discharge electricity over a wide area. Despite being a dangerous enemy, some might find it good luck to encounter one as they can drop expensive materials such as Platinum and Giant Crab's Shells when defeated.

name: Moss Behemoth
description: The Moss Behemoth (こけゴリラ, Kokegorira, Moss Gorilla) is an enemy in Suikoden IV.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 280 · SKL 120 · MAG 120 · PDF 120 · MDF 120 · EVA 80 · SPD 60 · LUK 120

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Horn, Earth Rune Piece, Steamed Bun

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This giant beast, body covered in moss, was rarely seen on the Deserted Island. Normally, these behemoth moved on four legs but when attacking, could strike its target with its forearm like a log. It could also strike the ground which such force as to hurt its foes.

Although incredibly strong, the Moss Behemoth could be easy to handle due to its susceptibility to sleep spells.

name: Moving Isle
description: The Moving Isle (動く島, Ugokushima) is a boss event enemy that appears in Suikoden IV. It is fought alongside four tentacles.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 120 · SKL 95 · MAG 95 · PDF 100 · MDF 130 · EVA 95 · SPD 80 · LUK 95

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This giant monster, apparently named Little Daisy, was the pet of Schtolteheim Reinbach II, Lord of Middleport. His son, Schtolteheim Reinbach III, hated this creature because it was not beautiful in his eyes. Attacking the Stronghold Ship as it approached Middleport, the Allied Forces were forced to engage it.

Extremely powerful, the Moving Isle could attack anyone on deck with its tentacles and by spraying ink. It would also ram the ship with its body, shaking the entire vessel violently. No mean feat, considering the size of the Stronghold Ship itself.

Due to its size and relative distance from the ship, short-range fighters cannot engage the Moving Isle. Instead, ranged fighters and magicians are recommended for this encounter. In battle, removing its tentacles is imperative so as to remove the powerful fire attack from its arsenal.

name: Muddy Fish
description: Muddy Fish (ドロドロウオ, Dorodorouo) is an enemy in Suikoden IV. It is an early game enemy found in the seas around Razril.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 10 · SKL 6 · MAG 6 · PDF 8 · MDF 6 · EVA 4 · SPD 6 · LUK 6

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This fish-type monster's entire is covered with dirty mud, hence its name. These fishmen inhabit the waters around Razril and Middleport.

Although bothersome to merchants who sail the seas for trading, they do tend to make good practice for newly-graduated Knights of Gaien who may be on patrol.

name: Mysterious Shadow
description: Mysterious Shadows (謎の影, Naze no Kage) are an event enemy that appears in Suikoden IV. They are "fought" periodically throughout the events of the game.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 0 · SKL 0 · MAG 0 · PDF 0 · MDF 0 · EVA 0 · SPD 0 · LUK 0

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These are the shades of Rakgi's father and Cray's son, who both bore the Rune of Punishment in the past. Their souls trapped within the memories of the rune. They desire to be released as soon as possible.

Each time the desperate victims are released from their curse, the Rune of Punishment grows stronger in the hand of the current bearer.

trivia: * Unsurprisingly, the Mysterious Shadow ties for lowest stats in all of Suikoden IV with its other, named, shadows.

name: Ogre Frog
description: The Ogre Frog (ビッグフロッグ, Biggufuroggu, Big Frog) is an enemy in Suikoden IV.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 240 · SKL 150 · MAG 150 · PDF 200 · MDF 100 · EVA 80 · SPD 83 · LUK 80

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This rarely seen beast was sighted in the Secret Underground Path beneath Middleport. Its huge body shows its extremely high physical power.

It attacked with the giant club it held in its hand, and was able to sue a sweeping attack over a wide area, making this Ogre Frog a highly dangerous, destructive force.

name: Paradise Bee
description: The Paradise Bee (ゴクラクバチ, Gokurakubachi) is an enemy in Suikoden IV.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 125 · SKL 50 · MAG 51 · PDF 80 · MDF 50 · EVA 50 · SPD 50 · LUK 50

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This huge bee-like creature was encountered mostly on Na-Nal Island. It is a common pest in settled areas that threatens the health of residents.

Its four wings beat violently at a high enough speed to leave an afterimage. It flies through the sky, using its needle to attack. Fortunately, its stinger holds no poison, so there is no need for undue panic should one be stung.

name: Pearl Crab
description: The Pearl Crab (パールクラブ, Pārukurabu) is an enemy in Suikoden IV.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 60 · SKL 21 · MAG 21 · PDF 21 · MDF 21 · EVA 14 · SPD 21 · LUK 21

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This crustacean creature could be found in the caves of the Deserted Island. Although its shell was sturdy, it could easily be torn apart by skilled warriors.

Very rarely, a Pearl could be found in its remains.

name: Pirate
description: Pirates (海賊, Kaizoku), and their superiors, the Pirate Boss (海賊親分, Kaizoku Oyabun) are an event enemy that appears in Suikoden IV. They are fought in the aftermath of the Retribution naval battle.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 40 · SKL 15 · MAG 15 · PDF 22 · MDF 28 · EVA 15 · SPD 24 · LUK 24

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These pirates are the ones who attacked Razril and made landfall at the town's port. Each one had a sword equipped with a Lightning Rune.

Although none of these pirates had particularly good fighting skills, they did have the ability to cast sleeping magic so letting them act unchecked would be dangerous.

Each group was led by a Pirate Boss, these veteran pirates were tougher opponents, their swords also enhanced through the use of Lightning Rune pieces.

trivia: * The English-language localization of Suikoden IV simply calls both enemy types "Pirates" with no distinction. In the original Japanese, they are divided into "Pirate" and "Pirate Boss" enemy types.

name: Pirate Brandeau
description: The Pirate Brandeau (海賊ブランド, Kaizoku Burando) is an event boss enemy that appears in Suikoden IV. He is fought alongside his underling, Assassin.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 45 · SKL 26 · MAG 6 · PDF 20 · MDF 10 · EVA 16 · SPD 26 · LUK 26

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Pirate Brandeau fights against you in this battle alongside the Assassin. In the party is the hero and a singular Knight of Gaien 1.

The optimal path is likely to use the Knight of Gaien's Wind Rune to attempt to put the enemy group to sleep. If Pirate Brandeau is put to sleep, you can then attack the Assassin with impunity. However, even should Pirate Brandeau not be affected (as his resistance is quite high) it is still best to focus on the Assassin first as he can be eliminated quickly, even if Brandeau runs healing.

Following the battle, Pirate Brandeau will engage the hero in a duel.

name: Pirate Seaman
description: The Pirate Seaman (海賊兵, Kaizoku-hei) is a naval battle enemy that appears in Suikoden IV. They can be fought in three different naval battles over the course of the game.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 25 · SKL 10 · MAG 10 · PDF 20 · MDF 50 · EVA 10 · SPD 10 · LUK 10

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These rough pirates form the crew of many a Pirate Ship, boarding enemy vessels and repelling enemy boarding attempts in turn. Despite being low ranked pirates, these seamen's weapons are enhanced with rune pieces and so they should not be underestimated.

Of course, if one were to sink their vessels before coming into boarding range, then these pirates would be completely ineffective.

name: Poison Bird
description: The Poison Bird (ポイズンバード, Poizunbādo) is an enemy in Suikoden IV.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 240 · SKL 94 · MAG 94 · PDF 140 · MDF 94 · EVA 94 · SPD 94 · LUK 94

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These bird creatures inhabit the Secret Underground Path beneath Middleport. They rush at their foes with their tough feet and then attempt to pierce the enemy with their sharp beak.

Their body color is indicative of the poison that oozes from their body and they can be poisonous to the touch. Sometimes they will unleash a breath attack, but that is not poisonous, thankfully.

name: Poison Lizard
description: The Poison Lizard (ポイズンリザード, Poizunrizādo) is an enemy in Suikoden IV.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 75 · SKL 45 · MAG 44 · PDF 67 · MDF 45 · EVA 30 · SPD 30 · LUK 45

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This lizard-type creature appeared in the waters around the Kingdom of Obel. It tries to poison its foes by striking them with its poisonous tail.

It seems to be resistant to death and sleep spells, but it's not a very powerful foe if you pay attention to its poison.

name: Prophet
description: The Prophet (よみする者, Yomisurumono) is an enemy in Suikoden IV. It is only encountered on the Fog Ship.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 106 · SKL 106 · MAG 106 · PDF 106 · MDF 100 · EVA 106 · SPD 106 · LUK 106

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These spectral monsters were found dwelling inside the mysterious Fog Ship found drifting on the open sea. Filled with darkness, it has control over many dark magics. It has nothing approaching a heart and so the Charm Rune is useless against it.

On rare occasions, Stones of Defense may be found in its remains.

name: Queen Sprout
description: The Queen Sprout (カズラークイーン, Kazurākuīn) is an enemy in Suikoden IV.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 160 · SKL 94 · MAG 94 · PDF 100 · MDF 94 · EVA 80 · SPD 94 · LUK 94

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This plant-like creature resembled a giant insectivorous plant in appearance and was seen on the Deserted Island. These creatures were very strong and held a resistance to death magic.

Fire Rune Pieces come sometimes be obtained from their remains.

name: Red Ray
description: The Red Ray (赤エイ, Aka Ei) is an enemy in Suikoden IV.

facts.label: Stats
