facts.value: STR 160 · SKL 135 · MAG 135 · PDF 110 · MDF 135 · EVA 80 · SPD 115 · LUK 135

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Fire Rune Piece, Pot of Nay

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These ray-like creatures were found lurking in the seas around Iluya Island. This creature could attack with a wide-ranging beam, hitting multiple targets.

The Red Ray also appeared to have some sort of unique aesthetic quality, and so the power of the Charm Rune has no effect against this creature.

name: Red Rodent
description: Red Rodent (むささびレッド, Musasabi Reddo) is an enemy in Suikoden IV.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 90 · SKL 40 · MAG 40 · PDF 63 · MDF 40 · EVA 40 · SPD 40 · LUK 40

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Stone of Strength, Fire Rune Piece, Steamed Bun

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These red-haired flying squirrels could be found on Obel, as well as nearby Mordo Island. Though not as accurate as the Rodent Leader, it too could hit opponents with the balloon status effect.

Its attacks were infused with fire magic and Fire Rune Pieces could sometimes be found in its remains. Even more rarely, a Stone of Power could even be found.

name: Rock Crab
description: The Rock Crab (カニモドキ, Kanimodoki and 岩ガニ, Iwagani) are two enemies that share the same name in Suikoden IV.

facts.label: Location
facts.value:
* Sea around Nest of Pirates
* Na-Nal Island

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 153 · SKL 48 · MAG 48 · PDF 100 · MDF 48 · EVA 38 · SPD 68 · LUK 48

originalName: カニモドキ / 岩ガニ

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
One form of these crustacean creatures lived in the waters around the Nest of Pirates. They are well protected by their shells and can attack with their pincers. One can gain such materials as shells and seashells from their bodies.

Another Rock Crab lived on the beaches of Na-Nal Island and was a troublesome crab that could appear even in residential areas. Like its compatriot, it was well protected by its shell. The Stone of Defense it sometimes left behind in defeat was proof of that.

trivia: * A messy localization incident, for sure. The first crab's Japanese name "Kanimodoki" would probably be better off localized as "Mock (or Pseudo-) Crab".

name: Rodent Leader
description: Rodent Leader (むささびリーダー, Musasabi Rīdā) is an enemy in Suikoden IV.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 85 · SKL 48 · MAG 45 · PDF 80 · MDF 20 · EVA 48 · SPD 48 · LUK 48

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Stone of Speed, Wind Rune Piece, Steamed Bun

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These are the leaders of the flying squirrels that lived on Obel, as well as nearby Mordo Island. They are slightly stronger than the other flying squirrels.

They were able to inflict the balloon status with a high degree of probability. Its attacks were also infused with wind magic and Wind Rune Pieces could sometimes be found in its remains. Even more rarely, a Stone of Speed could even be found.

name: Savage Frog
description: The Savage Frog (まだらガエル, Madaragaeru, Mottled Frog) is an enemy in Suikoden IV.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 200 · SKL 180 · MAG 60 · PDF 50 · MDF 150 · EVA 83 · SPD 83 · LUK 83

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Frog's Skin, Water Rune Piece, Steamed Bun

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This large beast was seen in the dense forests of the Deserted Island. Its huge body shows its extremely high physical power and its strikes could even paralyze a foe.

It was also able to use a sweeping attack over a wide area, making this Savage Frog a highly dangerous, destructive force despite the simplicity of its attacks.

name: Savage Root
description: The Savage Root (カズラーパパ, Kazurāpapa, Creeper Papa) is an enemy in Suikoden IV.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 120 · SKL 94 · MAG 94 · PDF 150 · MDF 94 · EVA 80 · SPD 94 · LUK 94

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This plant-like creature resembled a giant insectivorous plant in appearance and was seen on Iluya Island. Although this version of the creature was not as strong as a Queen Sprout, it was still much stronger than a normal Savage Sprout and would not be easily felled.

These creatures were adept at attacking multiple opponents with abilities such as pollen dispersal, creating intense vacuums with its maw and poisoning all at its disposal.

name: Savage Sprout
description: The Savage Sprout (カズラー, Kazurā) is an enemy in Suikoden IV.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 116 · SKL 76 · MAG 76 · PDF 130 · MDF 76 · EVA 76 · SPD 76 · LUK 76

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This plant-like creature resembled a giant insectivorous plant in appearance and was seen on Nay Island. These creatures were adept at attacking multiple opponents with abilities such as pollen dispersal, creating intense vacuums with its maw and poisoning all at its disposal.

In rare cases, the expensive material, Silk, may be dropped on its defeat.

trivia: * The Savage Sprout has the same Japanese name as the Creeper enemy from the first three main Suikoden games. Those versions never seemed so large, however.

name: Scoundrel
description: Scoundrels (ならずもの, Narazumono) are an event enemy that appears in Suikoden IV. They are fought as part of Millay's recruitment. Reinbach is required for this battle.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 180 · SKL 96 · MAG 96 · PDF 127 · MDF 96 · EVA 96 · SPD 96 · LUK 96

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These rogues were hired by Schtolteheim Reinbach II in order to find a suitable fiancée for his son. They may have looked brutal, but their weapons were of a good make and their use of runes marked them as bankrolled by their employer.

They had cornered Millay on Na-Nal Island as a prospective wife and were very aggressive on the matter. When the younger Reinbach came across the scene, unaware and highly disapproving of his father's plans, he would dispatch the scoundrels post-haste.

name: Seed of Light
description: The Seeds of Light (光の種, Hikari no Tane) are an event enemy that appears in Suikoden IV. They are fought as the minions of the Giant Tree in the final boss fight of the game.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 150 · SKL 68 · MAG 68 · PDF 68 · MDF 68 · EVA 68 · SPD 68 · LUK 68

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: In addition to its fire magic, the Seed of Light can also cure wounds suffered by the Giant Tree. Even if destroyed, the Giant Tree will simply make more, without end. As such, there's no fully stopping them, but they can be put to sleep as it holds a beast's instinct.

name: Senior Knight
description: Senior Knights (先輩騎士団員, Senpai Kishidanin) are an event enemy that appears in Suikoden IV. They are an early game enemy in the Razril Hall of Knights.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 14 · SKL 8 · MAG 1 · PDF 10 · MDF 18 · EVA 6 · SPD 8 · LUK 12

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These are regular Knights of Gaien who served as training partners at Razril's Hall of Knights. Apparently dueling with new graduates is something of a rite.

These knights are much more skilled than pre-graduate trainees. They are good-hearted seniors who will engage you in mock battles as many times as you need, until you have determined who you wish to take in the party.

name: Snobby Shrew
description: The Snobby Shrew (じゃじゃ馬, Jajauma, Unmanageable Woman) is an enemy in Suikoden IV. She is an early game enemy in the back alleys of Razril.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 6 · SKL 3 · MAG 2 · PDF 2 · MDF 16 · EVA 3 · SPD 6 · LUK 6

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These Snobby Shrews were part of a group of rogues lurking in the backstreets of Razril. As they lacked anything in the way of proper armor, they were vulnerable to regular attacks from ordinary weapons.

Overall, they made something of a perfect opponent for the fledgling Knights of Gaien.

name: Speckled Ray
description: Speckled Ray (ぶちエイ, Buchi Ei) is an enemy in Suikoden IV.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 126 · SKL 66 · MAG 66 · PDF 70 · MDF 66 · EVA 40 · SPD 50 · LUK 66

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Earth Rune Piece, Pot of Failure

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These ray-like creatures were found lurking in the seas around the Kingdom of Obel. They soar through the air gently and fly towards their foes as if they were gliding.

They can fire beams from their eyes that are not especially harmful but can nonetheless be bothersome as they hit multiple targets.

name: Spectral Leaves
description: Spectral Leaves (ひいらぎゾンビ, Hīragizonbi, Holly Zombie) is an enemy in Suikoden IV.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 225 · SKL 90 · MAG 90 · PDF 130 · MDF 90 · EVA 90 · SPD 90 · LUK 90

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This strange enemy was a zombified creature, found in the Secret Underground Path beneath Middleport.

Its color, which was once a vivid green, has since withered and turned brown. But the creature itself is stronger than before, making it a much more formidable enemy.

name: Spiny Gecko
description: The Spiny Gecko (トゲヤモリ, Togeyamori) is an enemy in Suikoden IV.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 170 · SKL 145 · MAG 145 · PDF 115 · MDF 145 · EVA 80 · SPD 80 · LUK 70

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This lizard-type creature appeared in the waters around Iluya Island. It tries to attack its foes by striking them with its venomous tail.

This tail is extremely dangerous as the venom within can not only poison its target, but can induce sleep and confusion as well.

name: Stun Lizard
description: The Stun Lizard (マヒとかげ, Mahitokage) is an enemy in Suikoden IV.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 170 · SKL 75 · MAG 75 · PDF 110 · MDF 75 · EVA 60 · SPD 80 · LUK 75

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This lizard-type creature lived on Iluya Island. Its tail, which it keeps raised up like a scorpion's, held a strong paralytic venom.

If attacked by a swarm of these creatures, your entire party may be swiftly paralyzed, making them easy prey for the Stun Lizard's attacks. Occasionally, Lightning Rune Pieces may be found on their bodies.

name: Thug
description: The Thug (チンピラ, Chinpira) is an enemy in Suikoden IV. He is an early game enemy in the back alleys of Razril.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 5 · SKL 2 · MAG 4 · PDF 3 · MDF 12 · EVA 2 · SPD 4 · LUK 4

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Pot of Failure, Pot of Razril, Meat Bun

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These small-time thugs engage in petty criminality in the back alleys of Razril. Although they are threatening to the town's citizenry, they are easily cowed by the Knights of Gaien.

They seem to often carry pots, most of which are worthless, but occasionally a high quality Pot of Razril can be found in their possession.

name: Trainee
description: Trainees (訓練生, Kunren-sei) are a fixed enemy that appears in Suikoden IV. They are an early game enemy in the Razril Hall of Knights.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 3 · SKL 1 · MAG 3 · PDF 3 · MDF 3 · EVA 1 · SPD 1 · LUK 3

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These trainees were students, hoping to one day graduate and become Knights of Gaien themselves. They would request combat training from the newly graduated Snowe Vingerhut and his friend.

These training bouts seemed to show they had roughly the same ability as the ruffians in Razril's backstreets, showing that they needed more training.

name: Unicorn
description: The Unicorn (ユニコーン, Yunikōn) is an enemy in Suikoden IV.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 130 · SKL 50 · MAG 50 · PDF 66 · MDF 50 · EVA 50 · SPD 50 · LUK 50

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This legendary beast was spotted at the Ruins of Obel. It controlled lightning freely and discharged it at its foes to attack.

This beast was a sturdy foe, not because of its physical strength, which was somewhat lacking, but because of its use of lightning magic and its strong resistance to death and sleep magics.

name: Unicorn Zombie
description: The Unicorn Zombie (ユニコーンゾンビ, Yunikōn Zonbi) is an enemy in Suikoden IV.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 143 · SKL 103 · MAG 103 · PDF 120 · MDF 103 · EVA 103 · SPD 103 · LUK 103

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Seen at the Ruins of Obel, this is the corpse of a Unicorn previously killed by adventurers and reanimated through magic. Perhaps due to its death it can no longer feel pain, making it a much stronger and more durable foe.

Defeating it can yield the Unicorn's Horn, as well as, rarely, a Stone of Magic.

trivia: * This enemy has the same Japanese name as Suikoden's Hell Unicorn, Suikoden II's Zombie Unicorn, and Suikoden III's ZombUnicrn.

name: Water Dragon
description: The Water Dragon (ウオータードラゴン, Uōtādoragon) is a boss event enemy that appears in Suikoden IV. It is fought on the seas after escaping from Troy and Colton on the Mystery Merchant Ship.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 33 · SKL 33 · MAG 33 · PDF 20 · MDF 50 · EVA 20 · SPD 20 · LUK 33

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Water Dragon was a giant monster that dwelled in the Kuknasi Sea and could attack any ship that passed near it.

It had many power attacks, such as knocking prey asleep with lightning as well as highly damaging breath attacks. It resisted many forms of magic but did hold a susceptibility to lightning magic.

Precious materials, such as the Dragon Scale and Dragon's Bone were obtained from its corpse when it was defeated by the hero and his friends aboard the Exile Ship.

name: Wild Crab
description: The Wild Crab (あばれガニ, Abare-gani) is an enemy in Suikoden IV.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 65 · SKL 25 · MAG 25 · PDF 25 · MDF 25 · EVA 18 · SPD 25 · LUK 25

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The crustacean creatures lived in the dense forests of the Deserted Island. They are well protected by their shells and can attack with their pincers.

The flesh under their sturdy shell is delicious and can be used as a source of crab meat, but they also tend to group up when threatened so be careful.

name: Wild Leaves
description: Wild Leaves (ひいらぎこぞう, Hīragikozō) is an enemy in Suikoden IV.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 110 · SKL 54 · MAG 54 · PDF 90 · MDF 54 · EVA 54 · SPD 54 · LUK 54

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This enemy appeared on Hermitage Island and was actually a cluster of small creatures, looking like small, green leaves.

These Wild Leaves has no remarkable abilities and is no great threat on its own, but may be surprising when encountered in groups of about eight, which is not uncommon.

trivia: * This monster has the same Japanese name as the Holly Boy and Holly Shrubs in previous Suikoden games, though they were not clustered together.

name: Wild Seaweed
description: Wild Seaweed (ワカメこぞう, Wakame Kozō, Seaweed Kid) is an enemy in Suikoden IV. It is an early game enemy found in the seas around Razril.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 15 · SKL 6 · MAG 10 · PDF 12 · MDF 10 · EVA 6 · SPD 8 · LUK 10

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This enemy frequently appeared in the waters around Razril and Middleport. Upon closer examination, it can be determined that it is actually a cluster of small creatures, similar to green seaweed.

This Wild Seaweed has no remarkable abilities, and offers those who are new to the ocean an opportunity to hone their skills.

name: Yellow Rodent
description: Yellow Rodent (むささびイエロー, Musasabi Ierō) is an enemy in Suikoden IV.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: STR 80 · SKL 40 · MAG 40 · PDF 63 · MDF 40 · EVA 100 · SPD 40 · LUK 40

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Stone of Technique, Lightning Rune Piece, Steamed Bun

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These yellow-haired flying squirrels could be found on Obel, as well as nearby Mordo Island. Its gliding strikes were able to confuse a hit opponent.

Its attacks were infused with earth magic and Earth Rune Pieces could sometimes be found in its remains. Even more rarely, a Stone of Magic could even be found.

name: Ancient Creeper
description: The Ancient Creeper (エンシェントカズラー, Enshentokazurā) is a boss enemy that appears in Suikoden V. It is fought within the Deep Twilight Forest Ruins during the game's Chapter 5.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 225 · MAG 150 · PDF 240 · MDF 330 · TEC 220 · ACC 250 · EVA 60 · SPD 100 · LUK 110

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Pale Gate Rune Piece, Silver White Robe, Cyclone Rune Piece, Matsutake Rice

originalName: エンシェントカズラー

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This giant carnivorous plant lived in the ruins of the Deep Twilight Forest. It was highly resistant to magical attacks, and the mucus and spores it spits out caused deadly poison and sleep.

Many adventurers who were caught when these sleep spores were spread are said to have fallen into an eternal slumber.

name: Armes Bodyguard
description: Armes Bodyguards (アーメス護衛兵, Āmesu Goei-hei) are an enemy that appear in Suikoden V. They are fought during the boss battle against Jidan Guisu.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 245 · MAG 100 · PDF 155 · MDF 200 · TEC 270 · ACC 300 · EVA 100 · SPD 150 · LUK 104

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These were soldiers trained to guard high-ranking officials of the New Armes Kingdom and were highly skilled in both swords and bows. They fought alongside General Jidan Guisu against the Imperial City Recapture Army but were defeated alongside their commander.

name: Armes Soldier
description: Armes Soldiers (アーメス兵, Āmesu Hei) are an enemy that appear in Suikoden V. They can be fought in Sable should the player choose to participate in the Armes Pursuit Battle over the Childerich Pursuit Battle.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 225 · MAG 112 · PDF 150 · MDF 200 · TEC 270 · ACC 300 · EVA 120 · SPD 140 · LUK 75

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These were soldiers of the New Armes Kingdom, serving in the Armes Southern Mountain Corps. They were divided into swordsmen and archers, and
during the occupation of Sable, they were stationed throughout the city. It's said that swordsmen served as the vanguard, while archers served as the rearguard.

name: Arrow Bat
description: Arrow Bats (アローバット, Arōbatto) are an enemy that appear in Suikoden V. They can be fought in the Stormfist Arena underground passage.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 25 · MAG 16 · PDF 18 · MDF 20 · TEC 40 · ACC 100 · EVA 10 · SPD 60 · LUK 24

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These bat-like monsters inhabited the underground passages of Stormfist. It bites its prey with swift, arrow-like movements. Those bitten may occasionally become poisoned.

name: Avolo Pestis
description: Avolo Pestis (飛行蟲, Hikochiyū, Flying Bug) are an enemy that appear in Suikoden V. They can be fought on the Mountain Path in Chapter 3.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 110 · MAG 115 · PDF 40 · MDF 90 · TEC 130 · ACC 150 · EVA 70 · SPD 90 · LUK 70

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Mega Medicine, Great Firefly Rune Piece, Spiked Helmet, Broiled Eel

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This flying insect-like creature inhabited the Mountain Path leading to Beaver Lodge. It attacks with its pincer-like appendages. Occasionally, when this monster is defeated, a Great Firefly Rune Piece could be discovered.

name: Bahram
description: Bahram (バフラム, Bafuramu) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden V. He is potentially fought by the player in an optional boss battle should they choose to participate in the Childerich Pursuit Battle over the Armes Pursuit Battle.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 280 · MAG 200 · PDF 200 · MDF 350 · TEC 270 · ACC 300 · EVA 100 · SPD 160 · LUK 106

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: The Shredding scroll, Resurrection Rune Piece

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: Once Doraat Fortress fell for the second time to the Imperial City Recapture Army, Bahram Luger would steer his flagship, the Kulsasper, into the Dahak, in order to buy time for his comrades to escape. Bahram boarded the Dahak, engaging in combat with the Prince, his allies, and Raja. He was eventually defeated and fell in battle.

name: Barows Soldier
description: Barows Soldiers (バロウズ兵, Barōzu Hei) are a boss enemy that appear in Suikoden V. They are fought several times through the game as the main fighting units of the Barows Faction.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 80 · MAG 82 · PDF 35 · MDF 50 · TEC 150 · ACC 150 · EVA 40 · SPD 70 · LUK 50

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These were private soldiers of Salum Barows. They attacked the Prince and his companions when they returned to the southern wharf from Lordlake, accusing them of being Godwin spies. Another unit were ordered by Euram Barows to attack the Prince, Isabel, and Mathias in Haud Village. Their units appears to consist of six soldiers: three swordsmen and three archers.

name: Big Creeper
description: Big Creepers (デカズラー, Dekazurā) are an enemy that appear in Suikoden V. They can be fought in forested regions of the world from Chapter 4 onward.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 190 · MAG 115 · PDF 100 · MDF 130 · TEC 160 · ACC 180 · EVA 80 · SPD 70 · LUK 76

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Baked Cheesecake, Bamboo Bonsai, Black Warrior Garb, Grilled Salmon

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These gigantic Creepers tend to lay claim to vast forest territories. Its suction attacks are said to be difficult to avoid, and those sucked in can be afflicted with various negative effects.

name: Billioneye
description: Billioneyes (ビリオンアイ, Birion'ai) are an enemy that appear in Suikoden V. They can be fought in various caves and forests across the land.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 280 · MAG 210 · PDF 210 · MDF 250 · TEC 300 · ACC 300 · EVA 120 · SPD 150 · LUK 116

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Magicial Rune Piece, Fortune Rune Piece, Deluxe Salad

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These bizarre creatures were found in ancient ruins, such as those in the Ashtwal Mountains. It emits rays from its eyes, paralyzing opponents. It can also freely control flames, burning to death any intruders into the ruins.

name: Blight
description: Blights (ペインバード, Peinbādo, Painbird) are an enemy that appear in Suikoden V. They can be fought across the plains of the world map.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 180 · MAG 111 · PDF 80 · MDF 90 · TEC 160 · ACC 180 · EVA 50 · SPD 140 · LUK 70

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This peacock-like monster inhabited many a plain. It hunts its prey by stabbing with its sharp beak and by repeatedly kicking from the air.

name: Blue Scissors
description: Blue Scissors (ブルーシザー, Burūshizā) are an enemy that appear in Suikoden V. They can be fought in cave and water areas. A version appears as an Area Boss during the game's Chapter 4.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 210 · MAG 100 · PDF 120 · MDF 150 · TEC 200 · ACC 250 · EVA 75 · SPD 60 · LUK 74

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Silver Bangle, Fire Sealing Rune Piece, Crab, Crab Rice Bowl

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: A blue, crab-like creature that inhabited the Ship Graveyard. It attacks intruders into its territory using its claws and bubbles spewed from its mouth. Based on its appearance and attack methods, it is speculated to be a variant of the Mantikra.

name: Bodyguard
description: Bodyguards (用心棒, Yōjinbō) are a boss enemy that appear in Suikoden V. They fight against the Prince in two different boss battles in the game's Chapter 4.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 145 · MAG 105 · PDF 70 · MDF 100 · TEC 180 · ACC 200 · EVA 50 · SPD 100 · LUK 240

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These mercenaries will side with anyone as long as their price is met. Such bodyguards were hired by Orok and Euram Barows, fighting against the Prince on multiple occasions. Perhaps because they prioritize agility, they favor a short sword and slash their enemies with swift movements.

name: Bone Soldier
description: Bone Soldiers (ボーンソルジャ, Bōnsoruja) are an enemy that appear in Suikoden V. They can be fought in several of the ancient ruins and caves throughout the game.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 220 · MAG 120 · PDF 165 · MDF 170 · TEC 220 · ACC 250 · EVA 90 · SPD 135 · LUK 90

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Bone Braclet, Double-Edged Orb, Carp Soup

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These skeletal creatures appeared in the Deep Twilight Forest Ruins and other such ancient sites. An evil spirit resides within these warrior's remains, and it attacks intruders with the axe they wielded in life.

name: Book of Condemnation
description: The Book of Condemnation (断罪の書の化身, Danzai no Sho no Keshin, Book of Condemnation Incarnation) is an optional boss enemy that appears in Suikoden V. It is fought in Yashuna Village as part of Alhazred's recruitment.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 130 · MAG 140 · PDF 100 · MDF 200 · TEC 300 · ACC 300 · EVA 50 · SPD 80 · LUK 65

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This incarnation of the Book of Condemnation materialized by absorbing the life force of the person who opened it, namely Euram Barows. Perhaps because the book is packed with arcane knowledge, all of its attacks have a dark element.

name: Centipede
description: Centipedes (センチピード, Senchipīdo) are an enemy that appear in Suikoden V. They can be fought outside Estrise on the world map in Chapter 6.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 260 · MAG 167 · PDF 190 · MDF 250 · TEC 300 · ACC 300 · EVA 150 · SPD 190 · LUK 110

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Great Firefly Rune Piece, Broiled Eel

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This winged insect creature inhabited areas such as the Estrise region. It is a subspecies of the Dragonfly, but is incomparably more ferocious. Its pincer attacks can inflict deadly poison and paralysis.

name: Condor Zombie
description: Condor Zombies (コンドルゾンビ, Kondoruzonbi) are an enemy that appear in Suikoden V. They can be fought in mountainous areas.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 220 · MAG 131 · PDF 165 · MDF 180 · TEC 250 · ACC 250 · EVA 70 · SPD 160 · LUK 90

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These zombified condors inhabited areas such as the Estrise area. Their decaying bodies contained a deadly poison, and anyone attacked by their beaks or wings could be infected by it.

name: Dark Bunny
description: Dark Bunnies (ブラックバニー, Burakkubanī, Black Bunny) are an enemy that appear in Suikoden V. They can be fought across the plains of the world map.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 70 · MAG 20 · PDF 30 · MDF 80 · TEC 100 · ACC 110 · EVA 40 · SPD 70 · LUK 80

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: Contrary to its appearance, this plains-dwelling rabbit is actually quite ferocious. They were highly intelligent and wields an axe like a boomerang to attack adventurers.

name: Diamond Sun
description: The Diamond Suns (ダイアモンドサン, Daiamondosan) are boss enemies which appear in Suikoden V. They are fought in the Ceras Lake Ruins in Chapter 3.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 150 · MAG 120 · PDF 160 · MDF 80 · TEC 250 · ACC 300 · EVA 10 · SPD 75 · LUK 70

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: A giant with an angry expression, guarding the central section of the Ceras Lake Ruins. It splits itself into two bodies, A and B, to fight. A is strong against physical attacks but weak against magical attacks, while B is weak against physical attacks but strong against magical attacks. Both use powerful magic, and some adventurers have reported losing many allies to their repeated use of Earthquake.

trivia: * In Japanese, the differentiator between the two Diamond Suns is 阿 and 吽, not A and B. When combined, 阿吽 is the transliteration in Japanese of the syllable "aum" (or "om"), sacred to the Indian religions of Hinduism, Buddhism, and Jainism. The original Sanskrit term is composed of two letters, the first and the last of the Sanskrit alphabet. Together, they symbolically represent the beginning and the end of all things. In Japanese Mikkyō Buddhism, the letters represent the beginning and the end of the universe.

name: Differential Gear
description: The Differential Gear (デファレンシャル・ギア, Defarensharu Gia) is an optional boss enemy that appears in Suikoden V. It is fought at the Revolving Bridge as part of Sorensen's recruitment.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 100 · MAG 75 · PDF 135 · MDF 120 · TEC 200 · ACC 200 · EVA 10 · SPD 50 · LUK 60

originalName: デファレンシャル・ギア

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This enormous security contraption was created by Babbage, the creator of the Revolving Bridge. Its front legs were equipped with sickles, its hind legs with drills, and its tail with a saw. Its stamina was impressive, and its armor was thick. Lacking in obvious weak spots, pity anyone who had to fight against this mechanical creation.

name: Dilber
description: Dilber (ディルバ, Diruba) is a boss enemy that appears in Suikoden V. He is fought alongside four Imperial Guards in the game's Chapter 6.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 285 · MAG 180 · PDF 190 · MDF 350 · TEC 270 · ACC 300 · EVA 100 · SPD 150 · LUK 185

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: Towards the end of the Sun Rune War, following the Stormfist Siege, Dilber Novum stood as the guardian of Godwin Castle in Stormfist. Dilber never surrendered, even though he and a handful of men were the only ones left standing. He fought the Imperial City Recapture Army there to his last breath.

name: Dragonfly
description: Dragonflies (ドラゴンフライ, Doragonfurai) are an enemy that appear in Suikoden V. They can be fought on the Mountain Path in Chapter 3.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 110 · MAG 85 · PDF 45 · MDF 30 · TEC 150 · ACC 180 · EVA 40 · SPD 90 · LUK 54

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Medicine, Great Firefly Rune Piece, Broiled Eel

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: A flying insect-like creature that inhabits the Mountain Path leading to Beaver Lodge. It attacks with its pincer-like appendages. Occasionally, when this monster is defeated, a Great Firefly Rune Piece could be discovered.

name: Elite Trooper
description: Elite Troopers (親衛隊, Shin'eitai, Personal Guard) are an enemy that appear in Suikoden V. They are fought in the Ashtwal Mountains near the end of the game.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 290 · MAG 173 · PDF 180 · MDF 250 · TEC 270 · ACC 300 · EVA 120 · SPD 140 · LUK 114

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These Godwin Faction soldier accompanied Marscal Godwin to the Ashtwal Mountains. While they still use swords and crossbows, their skills are recognized by Marscal and are unmatched by other soldiers under his command.

name: Embryo
description: Embryos (エンブリオ, Enburio) are an enemy that appear in Suikoden V. They can be fought in the Ceras Lake Ruins.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 110 · MAG 125 · PDF 100 · MDF 100 · TEC 130 · ACC 150 · EVA 50 · SPD 110 · LUK 58

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This bizarre wheel-shaped monster inhabited the Ceras Lake Ruins. It used its round shape to attack by spinning.

Despite initiating combat, it would often flee battles when hurt.

name: Enmesharra
description: Enmesharra (エンメシャルラ, Enmesharura) is a boss enemy that appears in Suikoden V. It is fought within the Ashtwal Mountains during the game's Chapter 6. It is the penultimate boss of the game and one of three bosses in the ruins alongside Girtablulu and Gudalim.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 350 · MAG 125 · PDF 320 · MDF 390 · TEC 350 · ACC 400 · EVA 130 · SPD 150 · LUK 130

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Immortal Medicine, Water Ring, Seafood Rice Bowl

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This humanoid-bird hybrid monster appeared before the Prince's party in the Ashtwal Mountains.

Its heat ray, called "Ripple Laser," not only has terrifying destructive power but also has the nasty effect of paralyzing or stunning opponents it hits.

trivia:
* [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Enmesharra Enmesharra] was a Mesopotamian god associated with the underworld.
* If the player turns around and leaves the ruins after defeating Enmesharra without triggering the final portion of the game, Enmesharra, Girtablulu, and Gudalim will respawn. This allows for ample training for experience points, SP, and guaranteed drops.

name: Euram
description: Euram (ユーラム, Yūramu) is a boss enemy that appears in Suikoden V. He is fought in Sable alongside five Bodyguards in the game's Chapter 4.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 130 · MAG 100 · PDF 100 · MDF 80 · TEC 120 · ACC 200 · EVA 50 · SPD 80 · LUK 74

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: Humiliated by the reveal of his father, Salum Barows' duplicity, Euram's attempt for revenge resulted in him hiring Roy and his friends to commit acts of banditry in Sable in the Prince's name, which resulted in a negative portrayal the Prince and his army. Euram was foiled yet again, this time by Sialeeds, with help from the bandits whom Euram hired, and bested in combat.

name: Evil Toad
description: Evil Toads (イビルトード, Ibirutōdo) are an enemy that appear in Suikoden V. They can be fought in cave and water areas, such as the hot springs in Yashuna Village.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 110 · MAG 124 · PDF 50 · MDF 50 · TEC 150 · ACC 180 · EVA 50 · SPD 90 · LUK 56

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These deformed, frog-like monsters inhabit water areas, such as the underground waterways of the Dwarf Camp, and caves. It attacks by smacking its prey with its outstretched tongue and crushing its prey with its body.

While there have been reports of people obtaining Prosperity Rune pieces from this creature, such instances are extremely rare.

name: Ghost Holly
description: Ghost Hollies (ゴーストツリー, Gōsutotsurī, Ghost Tree) are an enemy that appear in Suikoden V. They can be fought in the Deep Twilight Forest.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 220 · MAG 200 · PDF 180 · MDF 220 · TEC 220 · ACC 250 · EVA 20 · SPD 100 · LUK 90

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Pine Bonsai, Truffles, Matsutake, Rolled Cabbage

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This tree monster appears in places like the Deep Twilight Forest. It can extend its roots and vines to attack, and it can also use the magic spell Earthquake. It's said that it's best to burn it with fire attacks.

name: Ghost Shield
description: Ghost Shields (ゴーストシールド, Gōsutoshīrudo) are an enemy that appear in Suikoden V. They can be fought in the Ceras Lake Ruins.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 115 · MAG 88 · PDF 115 · MDF 130 · TEC 160 · ACC 170 · EVA 50 · SPD 100 · LUK 56

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These shield-shaped monster that appears in the Ceras Lake Ruins. The shield itself is imbued with evil, eliminating intruders by ramming into them. There have also been reports of Steel Rune pieces being discovered within this monster.

name: Giant Snake
description: The Giant Snake (大蛇, Orochi) is an optional boss enemy that appears in Suikoden V. It must be fought to recruit the character Byakuren.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 160 · MAG 105 · PDF 130 · MDF 130 · TEC 200 · ACC 200 · EVA 50 · SPD 120 · LUK 64

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: When Byakuren's life feeding on the fish at the bottom of Ceras Lake was disturbed by the Imperial City Recapture Army lowering the water levels and making their home there, she would grow more and more irritated. Furious, she slithered onto the dock of the castle, attacking the Prince.

name: Girtablulu
description: Girtablulu (ギルタブルウル, Girutaburūru) is a boss enemy that appears in Suikoden V. It is fought within the Ashtwal Mountains during the game's Chapter 6. It is the penultimate boss of the game and one of three bosses in the ruins alongside Enmesharra and Gudalim.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 315 · MAG 125 · PDF 320 · MDF 390 · TEC 350 · ACC 400 · EVA 100 · SPD 150 · LUK 130

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Immortal Medicine, Tachibana Greaves, Seafood Rice Bowl

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This monster with thick limbs that appeared before Lyon's team in the Ashtwal Mountains. The blows it unleashes from its arms are powerful. However, even more threatening is the diffused beam of light that spreads over a wide area that can paralyze or stun its targets.

trivia:
* [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Scorpion_man Girtablulu] were mythological creatures that feature in several Akkadian-language myths from ancient Mesopotamia.
* If the player turns around and leaves the ruins after defeating Girtablulu without triggering the final portion of the game, Enmesharra, Girtablulu, and Gudalim will respawn. This allows for ample training for experience points, SP, and guaranteed drops.

name: Godwin Soldier
description: Godwin Soldiers (ゴドウィン兵, Godōuin Hei) are an enemy that appear in Suikoden V. They are fought several times through the game as the main fighting units of the Godwin Faction.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 70 · MAG 82 · PDF 25 · MDF 50 · TEC 130 · ACC 150 · EVA 30 · SPD 70 · LUK 40

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: A typical soldier stationed in buildings and towns that support the Godwin Faction. While their actions are limited to simple attacks such as slashing with a sword and firing arrows with a crossbow, they split into front-line and rear-line units and launch coordinated attacks.

name: Golden Bunny
description: Golden Bunnies (ゴールデンバニー, Gōrudenbanī) are an enemy that appear in Suikoden V. They can be fought across the plains of the world map.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 58 · MAG 10 · PDF 30 · MDF 80 · TEC 100 · ACC 100 · EVA 36 · SPD 65 · LUK 36

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This golden Blade Bunny appears across a variety of ​​plains. It attacks by throwing its axe like a boomerang and can control lightning.

It is said to possess a Prosperity Rune Piece, but these have rarely been seen.

name: Gray Ulse
description: Gray Ulse (グレイウルス, Gureiurusu) are an enemy that appear in Suikoden V. They can be fought around the Lordlake region, including in the Withered Forest.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 190 · MAG 111 · PDF 100 · MDF 90 · TEC 160 · ACC 200 · EVA 60 · SPD 130 · LUK 68

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Violence Rune Piece, Boost Rune Piece, Carpaccio Salad

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This standing-beast creature lived in the Withered Forest and the lands around Lordlake. It was considered the most ferocious of Ulse creatures. It attacks directly with its claws and tail. Its rapid attack combo is also dangerous.

name: Greater Salamandy
description: Greater Salamandies (ハイウッパ, Haiuppa, High Wooper) are an enemy that appear in Suikoden V. They can be fought on the world map between the Mountain Path and Beaver Lodge.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 115 · MAG 85 · PDF 45 · MDF 100 · TEC 130 · ACC 150 · EVA 40 · SPD 65 · LUK 56

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Fire Sealing Rune Piece, Carp Soup

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These axolotl-like creatures, stronger variants of the regular Salamandy, inhabited waterside areas such as around Beaver Lodge. When it spots an adventurer, it will stab them with its harpoon or attempt to sweep them away with its tail.

name: Guardian Heat Sprite
description: The Guardian Heat Sprite (熱の守護精, Netsu no Shugosei) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden V. It is the final boss of the game and the incarnation of the Sun Rune.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 380 · MAG 60 · PDF 175 · MDF 450 · TEC 350 · ACC 400 · EVA 40 · SPD 170 · LUK 120

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This floating spirit was summoned by the Sun Rune Incarnation along with the Guardian Light Sprite. True to its name, its body is red.

Not only is it summoned multiple times each time it's defeated, but its Firebird ability, which burns its target to ashes with scorching flames, is also a threat.

name: Guardian Light Sprite
description: The Guardian Light Sprite (光の守護精, Hikari no Shugosei) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden V. It is part of the final battle of the game against the Sun Rune Incarnation.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 370 · MAG 60 · PDF 175 · MDF 450 · TEC 350 · ACC 400 · EVA 40 · SPD 170 · LUK 120

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This jellyfish-esque floating spirit was summoned by the Sun Rune Incarnation along with the Guardian Heat Sprite. The Sun Rune Incarnation would revive if it were ever struck down.

Its Energy Absorption ability allows it to absorb the life force of its attacking opponent and send it to the Sun Rune Incarnation.

name: Gudalim
description: Gudalim (グドアリム, Gudoarimu) is a boss enemy that appears in Suikoden V. It is fought within the Ashtwal Mountains during the game's Chapter 6. It is the penultimate boss of the game and one of three bosses in the ruins alongside Enmesharra and Girtablulu.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 350 · MAG 125 · PDF 320 · MDF 390 · TEC 350 · ACC 400 · EVA 95 · SPD 150 · LUK 130

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Immortal Medicine, Wind Ring, Seafood Rice Bowl

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This monster appeared before Zweig's party in the Ashtwal Mountains. It specializes in attacks using the blades attached to the sides of its hands. It also swings its tail, sending enemies flying in a wide range, and torments its foes with a beam of light that causes burning.

trivia:
* [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kusarikku Gudalim], usually rendered as Kusarikku, was a mythological bull-man that feature in ancient Mesopotamian myths.
* If the player turns around and leaves the ruins after defeating Gudalim without triggering the final portion of the game, Enmesharra, Girtablulu, and Gudalim will respawn. This allows for ample training for experience points, SP, and guaranteed drops.

name: Hellhound
description: Hellhounds (ヘルハウンド, Heruhaundo) are an enemy that appear in Suikoden V. They can be fought on the plains on the world map.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This twisted dog-like creature was seen in the Withered Forest, around Lordlake, and on various plains. Its bite attacks poison its prey, and its scratch attacks slows their targets down.

name: Hellsteed
description: The Hellsteed (火馬, Hiuma) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden V. They can be fought in the Underground Ruins as an Area Boss from Chapter 4 onward.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 320 · MAG 245 · PDF 245 · MDF 350 · TEC 300 · ACC 300 · EVA 90 · SPD 150 · LUK 200

facts.label: Drops

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This fiery unicorn appeared in ancient ruins. Even a simple thrust attack using its horn deals increased damage due to the heat generated. It has also been reported that it can use the highest level of fire magic, Final Flame, to turn the surrounding area into a sea of ​​fire.

name: Hermilio Crab
description: Hermilio Crabs (ヤドゴロー, Yadogorō) are an enemy that appear in Suikoden V. They can be fought in the Ship Graveyard.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: A crab-like monster that claims the Ship Graveyard as its territory. It has an extremely hard shell but it's well-known among travelers that if you hit it hard with a blunt object, it will jump out in surprise.

name: Hermisio Crab
description: Hermisio Crabs (ヤドクロー, Yadokurō) are an enemy that appear in Suikoden V. They can be fought in the Ship Graveyard.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 180 · MAG 101 · PDF 120 · MDF 110 · TEC 160 · ACC 160 · EVA 30 · SPD 80 · LUK 66

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: A crab-like monster that claims the Ship Graveyard as its territory. Its shell is made from a human skull discovered in a shipwreck. Compared to the Hermilio Crab, its stamina and defense are significantly increased.

name: Horohoro
description: Horohoro (ほろほろバード, Horohoro Bādo) are an enemy that appear in Suikoden V. They can be fought across the plains of the world map.

facts.value: ATK 75 · MAG 78 · PDF 30 · MDF 40 · TEC 80 · ACC 100 · EVA 30 · SPD 80 · LUK 40

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This peacock-like monster inhabited the plains around Estrise. It hunts its prey by stabbing with its sharp beak and by repeatedly kicking from the air. Its meat was sometimes used locally as Premium Chicken.

name: Huge Legs

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This spider was found in a wide range of forests and caves. It bites its prey with a poisonous bite and spits a web that slows its movement.

name: Hydra
description: The Hydra (ハイドラ, Haidora) is an optional boss enemy that appears in Suikoden V. It can be fought at the Ship Graveyard after recruiting Nelis and Yahr.

facts.label: Stats

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The Hydra was a water dragon that ruled over the Ship Graveyard following the defeat of Dagon. It was similar enough to its predecessor that it was considered a subspecies and could similarly control water at will.

name: Jidan
description: Jidan (ジダン, Jidan) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden V. He is potentially fought by the player in an optional boss battle should they choose to participate in the Armes Pursuit Battle over the Childerich Pursuit Battle.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 280 · MAG 180 · PDF 190 · MDF 320 · TEC 270 · ACC 300 · EVA 100 · SPD 100 · LUK 106

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Vengeful Child Scroll, Mother Earth Rune Piece

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
When the Armes Southern Mountain Corps were pushed back by the Imperial City Recapture Army, Jidan Guisu would be abandoned in Sable when Maha Sparna blocked the gates that divided Sable from New Armes behind her in order to secure her own retreat.

Trapped, he would try to fight his way out alongside his Armes Bodyguards but would be killed in battle.

name: Killer Bald Eagle
description: Killer Bald Eagles (殺し屋ハゲタカ, Koroshiya Hagetaka) are an enemy that appear in Suikoden V. They can be fought in mountainous areas.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 195 · MAG 111 · PDF 130 · MDF 120 · TEC 160 · ACC 180 · EVA 68 · SPD 140 · LUK 72

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These dangerous vultures have been spotted in places like Ranro Mountain and the greater Sable area. In addition to attacking with its beak, it scatters its feathers all over the area, blinding its enemies. Defeating this monster has a rare chance of yielding an Equilibrium Rune Piece.

name: Killer Tiger
description: Killer Tigers (キラータイガー, Kirātaigā) are an enemy that appear in Suikoden V. They can be fought in plains and forested areas.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: A tiger-like monster that inhabits forests, such as the Deep Twilight Forest, as well as plains. Its sharp fangs deliver deadly poisonous bites, while its sharp claws cause paralysis with their scratching attacks.

name: King Mantik

sections.body: These dangerous condors have been spotted in places like the Mountain Path to Beaver Lodge. It attacks by stabbing with its beak from the air. Defeating this monster has a rare chance of yielding an Equilibrium Rune Piece.

name: Mad Dog

name: Millioneye

sections.body: These bizarre creatures were found in locations such as the Ceras Lake Ruins. It can emits rays from its eyes, paralyzing opponents, as well as commit to a dangerous self-destruct attack.

name: Pirate Captain

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This figure was the leader of the pirates holed up in the Great Lighthouse on Nirva Island. In addition to slashing with his sword, he often used his Water Rune to restore his own and his subordinates' stamina.

name: Pirate Lackey

sections.title: Profile

name: Prison Guard
description: Prison Guards (看守, Kanshu) are an enemy that appear in Suikoden V. They are fought in Agate Prison.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 90 · MAG 98 · PDF 35 · MDF 50 · TEC 130 · ACC 150 · EVA 60 · SPD 70 · LUK 50

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These prison guards were responsible for monitoring, guarding, and maintaining discipline at Agate Prison. When the Prince's party infiltrated Agate Prison to rescue Lucretia Merces, these guards mistook the party them for Godwin's assassins and attacked. Among them are those wielding bows and swords, including Cius and Lelei.

name: Raging Alenia
description: Raging Alenia (アレニア・烈身, Arenia Retsumi) is a boss enemy that appears in Suikoden V. She is fought after defeating her regular form in the Sun Palace near the end of the game.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 340 · MAG 230 · PDF 220 · MDF 450 · TEC 270 · ACC 300 · EVA 120 · SPD 180 · LUK 120

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Silver White Emblem, Rage Rune Piece

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
After being pushed to the brink of defeat in battle against the Prince and his comrades, Alenia and Zahhak would resort to taking doses of Raging Nostrum, turning them into supercharged, but insanely violent, versions of themselves.

Though physically far stronger than before, the madness brought on by the drug meant that Alenia would only attack with simple, but vicious, sword strikes. The two would fail to defeat their foes and soon died from the effects of the Raging Nostrum drug.

name: Raging Assassin
description: Raging Assassins (暗殺者・烈身, Ansatsusha Retsumi) are an enemy that appear in Suikoden V.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 290 · MAG 120 · PDF 200 · MDF 290 · TEC 230 · ACC 280 · EVA 110 · SPD 160 · LUK 106

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Crazed Orb, Thunder Rune Piece, Marbled Steak

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These professional assassins were members of Nether Gate who had ingested the Raging Nostrum drug, increasing their strength and vitality at the cost of their sanity and lives. As such, they only used basic attacks but with far greater strength than normal.

name: Raging Zahhak
description: Raging Zahhak (ザハーク・烈身, Zahāku Retsumi) is a boss enemy that appears in Suikoden V. He is fought after defeating her regular form in the Sun Palace near the end of the game.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 360 · MAG 190 · PDF 230 · MDF 380 · TEC 270 · ACC 300 · EVA 120 · SPD 170 · LUK 120

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
After being pushed to the brink of defeat in battle against the Prince and his comrades, Alenia and Zahhak would resort to taking doses of Raging Nostrum, turning them into supercharged, but insanely violent, versions of themselves.

Though physically far stronger than before, the two would fail to defeat their foes and soon died from the effects of the Raging Nostrum drug.

name: Razorback
description: Razorbacks (レイザーバック, Reizābakku) are an enemy that appear in Suikoden V. They can be fought on the plains on the world map.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 200 · MAG 105 · PDF 110 · MDF 140 · TEC 100 · ACC 180 · EVA 50 · SPD 120 · LUK 74

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Power Rune Piece, Marbled Beef, Power Belt, Beef Stew

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This incredibly aggressive boar inhabited the plains of Falena. Its charge attacks are a threat, as is its super-charged Hurricane Mixer, which is said to be so powerful that no adventurer has been able to avoid it.

name: Red Ulse
description: Red Ulse (レッドウルス, Reddourusu) are an enemy that appear in Suikoden V. They can be fought around the Lordlake region, including in the Withered Forest.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 78 · MAG 82 · PDF 25 · MDF 40 · TEC 100 · ACC 100 · EVA 35 · SPD 80 · LUK 50

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Mega Medicine, Violence Rune Piece, Boost Rune Piece, Carpaccio Salad

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This standing-beast creature lived in the Withered Forest and the lands around Lordlake. Its claws and rapid attack combo are dangerous and it can also sweep away enemies with its tail.

name: Rock Golem
description: Rock Golems (ロックゴーレム, Rokkugōremu) are an enemy that appear in Suikoden V. They can be fought in forested areas and ruins from Chapter 5 onwards.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 250 · MAG 135 · PDF 310 · MDF 100 · TEC 250 · ACC 280 · EVA 120 · SPD 50 · LUK 85

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Magical Rune Piece, Angel Statue, Earth Ring, Grilled Chicken

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These giant stone-bodied construct inhabited places such as the Deep Twilight Forest. Its attacks may be simple, like swinging its fists, spinning its arms, or throwing rocks but their destructive power is immeasurable.

name: Royal Mummy
description: Royal Mummies (ロイヤルマミー, Roiyarumamī) are an enemy that appear in Suikoden V. They can be fought in many of the ancient ruins throughout the game.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 225 · MAG 120 · PDF 150 · MDF 170 · TEC 220 · ACC 250 · EVA 90 · SPD 135 · LUK 90

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Magic Absorb Rune Piece, Boundary Rune Piece, Carp Soup

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These living corpses appeared in ancient ruins across the land. It has high strength and stamina but even so its danger is limited due to their lack of intelligence and inability to do more than simple attacks.

name: Saber Fang
description: Saber Fangs (サーベルファング, Sāberufangu) are an enemy that appear in Suikoden V. They can be fought in plains and forested areas.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 230 · MAG 173 · PDF 160 · MDF 180 · TEC 250 · ACC 250 · EVA 90 · SPD 190 · LUK 90

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Fur Clothes, Byakko Chain Mail, Beef Stew

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: A tiger-like monster that inhabited areas such as the Deep Twilight Forest. It possesses sharp claws and fangs, and can kill its prey with a deadly poisonous bite or a paralyzing scratch.

name: Sable Patrol Guard
description: Sable Patrol Guards (セーブル警備隊員, Sēburu Keibitaīn) are an enemy that appear in Suikoden V. They are fought several times through the game as the main fighting units of the Godwin Faction.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 170 · MAG 111 · PDF 110 · MDF 80 · TEC 180 · ACC 200 · EVA 50 · SPD 100 · LUK 132

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These soldiers guarded the Sable, East Checkpoint that led to Sable proper. Specializing in sword attacks, they once mistook the Prince for his bandit doppelgänger, Roy, and attacked to try and restore peace to their home.

name: Salamandy
description: Salamandies (ウッパ, Uppa) are an enemy that appear in Suikoden V. They can be fought on the world map between the Mountain Path and Beaver Lodge.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 110 · MAG 82 · PDF 40 · MDF 70 · TEC 130 · ACC 150 · EVA 40 · SPD 65 · LUK 56

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Medicine, Fire Sealing Rune Piece, Carp Soup

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These axolotl-like creatures, inhabited waterside areas such as around Beaver Lodge. When it spots an adventurer, it will stab them with its harpoon or attempt to sweep them away with its tail.

name: Shepcesska
description: Shepcesska (シェプセスカ, Shepusesuka) is an optional boss enemy that appears in Suikoden V. It is fought within the Underground Ruins as part of Eresh's recruitment, meaning it can not be encountered if the player has recruited Euram Barows.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 270 · MAG 250 · PDF 220 · MDF 350 · TEC 300 · ACC 300 · EVA 80 · SPD 130 · LUK 81

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Sacred Oath Epic, Cyclone Rune Piece, Mother Earth Rune Piece, Marbled Steak

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The guardian deity of the throne room in the deepest part of the Underground Ruins. He wields two gigantic red swords, slicing through intruders with violent spins. He also uses the powerful lightning spell Furious Blow to stun and paralyze enemies. According to a brave adventurer who claims to have defeated Shepcesska, the being holds the legendary Sacred Oath Epic.

trivia: * [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shepseskare Shepseskare] was an Ancient Egyptian king, the fourth or fifth ruler of the Fifth Dynasty (2494–2345 BC) during the Old Kingdom period.

name: Skull Army
description: Skull Armies (スカルアーミー, Sukaruāmī) are an enemy that appear in Suikoden V. They can be fought most commonly in the Underground Ruins accessible through the Big Hole.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 270 · MAG 150 · PDF 230 · MDF 250 · TEC 300 · ACC 300 · EVA 120 · SPD 155 · LUK 116

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Bone Braclet, Strike-Back Orb, Carp Soup

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This skeleton creature was found guarding ancient ruins. Perhaps a former warrior, it possesses high combat ability, slashing with its sword and even wielding the magic spell Blazing Wall. However, despite its armor, its body is made of bones, making it vulnerable to physical attacks.

name: Snail
description: Snails (マイマイ, Maimai) are an enemy that appear in Suikoden V. They can be fought in the Stormfist Arena underground passage.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 43 · MAG 33 · PDF 40 · MDF 40 · TEC 40 · ACC 100 · EVA 10 · SPD 25 · LUK 24

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These snail-like creatures inhabited Stormfist's Underground Passage and the Western Woods. It attacks with a body ramming attack, but its power is low, so it poses little threat.

name: Sun Rune Incarnation
description: The Sun Rune Incarnation (太陽の紋章の化身, Taiyō no Monshō no Keshin) is an enemy that appears in Suikoden V. It is the final boss of the game and the incarnation of the Sun Rune.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 370 · MAG 280 · PDF 300 · MDF 520 · TEC 350 · ACC 400 · EVA 95 · SPD 150 · LUK 130

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This was the incarnation of the Sun Rune, which Marscal Godwin transformed into in the depths of the Sindar ruins in the Ashtwal Mountains.

It looked similar to a giant butterfly, with the figure of a woman, visually similar to its previous bearer, Queen Arshtat in its center. In battle, it is supported by a Guardian Heat Sprite and a Guardian Light Sprite, who both attacked the party and healed the Sun Rune Incarnation. These guardian sprites would also revive themselves eventually even if defeated.

The Sun Rune Incarnation specialized in powerful sun magic attacks.

trivia: The Sun Rune Incarnation's attacks are named after deities in ancient Akkadian literature.

name: Suspicious Man
description: Suspicious Men (怪しい男, Ayashī Otoko) are an enemy that appear in Suikoden V. The player fights a group of five of these in the middle of the game's first chapter.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 43 · MAG 33 · PDF 18 · MDF 30 · TEC 60 · ACC 100 · EVA 10 · SPD 50 · LUK 24

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These spies from the New Armes Kingdom were found sneaking around in the basement of Stormfist Arena. Their main role was covert operations, so they were easily defeated by the Prince and his comrades when they pursued and confronted the shady characters.

name: Tarantula
description: Tarantulas (タランチュラ, Taranchura) are an enemy that appear in Suikoden V. They can be fought in various caves and forests across the land.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 160 · MAG 137 · PDF 80 · MDF 120 · TEC 160 · ACC 180 · EVA 60 · SPD 100 · LUK 60

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Mega Medicine, Venom Orb, Eyeball Ring, Chicken Curry

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This highly venomous spider was found in a wide range of forests and caves. Anyone bitten by this monster will be infected with deadly poison or become paralyzed.

name: Terrible Armor
description: Terrible Armors (テリブルアーマー, Teriburuāmā) are an enemy that appear in Suikoden V. They can be fought in the Ceras Lake Ruins.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 185 · MAG 100 · PDF 140 · MDF 150 · TEC 180 · ACC 180 · EVA 60 · SPD 80 · LUK 66

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This armored monster found in the Ceras Lake Ruins. Perhaps the grudge of a warrior resides within its armor. It can eliminate intruders with its greatsword and armor; In addition to slashing with its sword, it can also separate the various parts of its armor to simultaneously attack nearby targets.

name: Terror Armor
description: Terror Armors (テラーアーマー, Terāāmā) are an enemy that appear in Suikoden V. They can be fought in the Ashtwal Mountains.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 300 · MAG 180 · PDF 220 · MDF 250 · TEC 300 · ACC 300 · EVA 120 · SPD 140 · LUK 116

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This armored monster found in the Ashtwal Mountains. This armor possessed a will of its own, wielding its powerful greatsword to eliminate intruders. The armor can be separated into its individual parts, each capable of attacking intruders. Its strength is unparalleled among the creatures found in the Queendom of Falena.

name: Ulse
description: Ulse (ウルス, Urusu) are an enemy that appear in Suikoden V. They can be fought around the Lordlake region, including in the Withered Forest.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 38 · MAG 33 · PDF 15 · MDF 20 · TEC 100 · ACC 100 · EVA 10 · SPD 25 · LUK 20

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Medicine, Violence Rune Piece, Boost Rune Piece, Carpaccio Salad

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This standing-beast creature lived in the Withered Forest and the lands around Lordlake. It attacks directly with its claws and tail. Its rapid attack combo is potentially dangerous, but its power is weak and it misses easily.

These creatures were noted not to normally attack people, but with the lack of food and water around Lordlake, had begun attacking people to survive.

name: Vampire Bat
description: Vampire Bats (ヴアンパイアバット, Vuanpaiabatto) are an enemy that appear in Suikoden V. They can be fought in cave areas.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 110 · MAG 101 · PDF 45 · MDF 50 · TEC 150 · ACC 180 · EVA 50 · SPD 100 · LUK 56

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These vampire bats lived in areas such as the Dragon Horse Lair and the Waterfall Basin upstream of Beaver Lodge. They suck blood from their prey, not only restoring their own stamina but also poisoning the victim.

name: Visage Box
description: Visage Boxes (顏箱, Kaobako) are an enemy that appear in Suikoden V. They can be fought in the Ashtwal Mountains.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 290 · MAG 230 · PDF 215 · MDF 290 · TEC 300 · ACC 350 · EVA 90 · SPD 150 · LUK 118

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Mega Medicine, Magical Rune Piece, Tuna Rice Bowl

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: A gigantic, box-shaped monster that appeared in the Ashtwal Mountains. Each face is said to contain the evil will of a human.

name: Wizard Tree
description: Wizard Trees (ウィザーツリー, Uizātsurī, Wither Tree) are an enemy that appear in Suikoden V. They can be fought in and around Lordlake.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 80 · MAG 60 · PDF 50 · MDF 100 · TEC 100 · ACC 120 · EVA 40 · SPD 50 · LUK 40

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This strange tree monster appeared in places like the Withered Forest and around Lordlake. It freely grows long, beard-like roots and vines, attacking adventurers. There have been rare reports of truffles being harvested from it.

name: Zadom
description: The Zadom (ザドム, Zadomu) is an enemy in Suikoden V. It is fought first as a boss before reappearing later in the game as a common enemy encounter.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 50 · MAG 45 · PDF 20 · MDF 45 · TEC 50 · ACC 100 · EVA 15 · SPD 45 · LUK 45

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Medicine, Fire Sealing Rune Piece, Breath of Ice Scroll, Carpaccio Salad

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Zadom was fought by the Prince's party en route to Stormfist. The creature was fended off, and its comrades repelled by the arrival of Gizel Godwin's ship. Sialeeds suspected that the incident had been set up by Gizel to show his valor.

These creatures can be found in many locations throughout the Queendom of Falena, including the forests north of Lunas and the Mountain Path leading to Beaver Lodge.

They can attack with their long tails, but also have several long-ranged attacks, being able to breath poison and spit saliva at their foes.

name: Zadom King
description: Zadom Kings (ザドムキング, Zadomukingu) are an enemy that appear in Suikoden V. They can be fought at the Ship Graveyard.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 195 · MAG 85 · PDF 120 · MDF 150 · TEC 160 · ACC 180 · EVA 50 · SPD 130 · LUK 70

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Premium Chicken, Zadom Leather, Carpaccio Salad

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This giant, white lizard inhabited the Ship Graveyard. Like regular Zadoms, it attacks enemies with its saliva, tail, and poisonous breath. However, its destructive power and strength are far greater than its normal variant. After all, that's what makes it the king.

name: Zombie Snail
description: Zombie Snails (マイマイゾンビ, Maimaizombi) are an enemy that appear in Suikoden V. They can be fought mainly in cavernous areas.

facts.label: Stats
facts.value: ATK 75 · MAG 30 · PDF 45 · MDF 50 · TEC 100 · ACC 120 · EVA 20 · SPD 30 · LUK 44

facts.label: Drops
facts.value: Mega Medicine, Breath of Ice, Sashimi Combo

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These zombified Snails were found in places such as the Baska Mine and Agate Prison. In addition to ramming attacks, it could also spread poisonous breath around.

name: Ancient Text
description: Ancient Texts (こもんじょ, Komonji-yo) are a trade item in several games of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These ancient documents describe long-forgotten knowledge and were valued as trading goods. In Kobold Village, they were in low demand selling for some 700 potch whereas in the Forest Village, they could sell for as much as 35,000 potch.

name: Angry Blow Scroll
description: The Angry Blow Scroll (怒りの一撃の札, Ikari no Ichigeki no Fuda) is a magic scroll available in Suikoden II and Suikoden III. In Suikoden III, it is called the Berserk Blow Scroll.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The scroll was embedded with the low level attack magic of the Lightning Rune, "Angry Blow.". It was intended for use by those not proficient in runes and as such did not require a Lightning Rune to use.

If used in battle, it could unleash a small lightning strike on a single enemy, inflicting damage.

name: Antitoxin
game: Suikoden I, II, III, IV & V
description: Antitoxin (毒消し, Dokukeshi) is the standard Poison recovery item throughout the Suikoden series. It removes the Poison status effect and is usually bought in bundles of 4.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This inexpensive item is used to heal people afflicted with forms of poison. It is wise to have this item readied in advance, especially if you expect to encounter any venomous monsters on your journey.

It was not universally effective against all forms of toxin. For example, it could do nothing against the poison pollen of the monstrous Antoinette during the Gate Rune War.

name: BBQ Meat Bun
description: BBQ Meat Bun (にくまんじゅう, Nikumanjū) is a recipe in Suikoden II. It is Recipe #7 in the game.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This baozi-based dish is made by kneading flour and steaming it filled with meat or other ingredients. The cooking method is not very difficult, and it is said to have been an easy dish for quality chefs such as Hai Yo.

name: Bandana
description: The Bandana (バンダナ, Bandana) is a type of equipment available in several titles of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A bandana is a swath of cloth, folded and wrapped around the head. It has almost no protective ability but is often used as a fashion accessory, being produced in a wide array of colors and patterns.

It tends to cost in the region of 50 potch.

name: Beauties of Nature
game: Suikoden I, II, III, IV & V
description: The Beauties of Nature (花ちょう風月, Kachō Fūgetsu) is a recurring antique in the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: A masterful painting depicting beautiful natural scenery as represented by flowers, birds, etc. A first-class work of art, pieces such as this can fetch as much as 200,000 to 400,000 potch on the open market. It is a rarely seen masterpiece as a result.

name: Black Urn
description: The Black Urn (黒いつぼ, Kuroi Tsubo) is a recurring antique in the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: A beautiful black glistening urn. Its smooth and fine texture makes it a highly sought-after antique. It is sold for as much as 20,000 potch in the Toran Republic and 40,000 potch in the Dunan region.

name: Blinking Mirror
description: The Blinking Mirror (またたきの手鏡, Matataki no Tekagami) is a recurring item in the Suikoden series. The mirror has the power to return the user to their home base under most conditions.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
When the Toran Liberation Army recruited Hellion in Teien, she gave them the Blinking Mirror. By looking into the mirror, the holder could return to its counterpart, a larger mirror tjhat appeared at Toran Castle with Hellion's recruitment. The Blinking Mirror itself looked like a small hand mirror.

It said that the Gate Rune War's end was accelerated thanks to this magical hand mirror. The mirror was kept in Gregminster Palace following the war.

The leader of the New Alliance Army would receive the Blinking Mirror in turn during the Dunan Unification War. Seeing something of McDohl in his guest, President Lepant would gift the mirror to him during their meeting, considering it part of their alliance.

name: Blood Armor
description: The Blood Armor (ブラッドアーマー, Buraddoāmā) is a type of equipment available in Suikoden II and Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This was an armor of cursed power with outstanding defensive ability, but it drains the user's blood and continues to drain their strength little by little as they continue to wear it.

Because of this curse, it is rarely seen by the general public, not that many would be fearless enough to wear it. During the Dunan Unification War, it could only be obtained from Chimeras in the Highland capital of L'Renouille.

name: Blue Dragon Urn
description: The Blue Dragon Urn (青竜のつぼ, Seiryū no Tsubo) is a recurring antique in the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This was a beautiful vase depicting a majestic dragon soaring through the sky. It was quite precious, fetching prices upwards of 22,000 potch in locations such as Crom Village.

name: Bolt of Wrath Scroll
description: The Bolt of Wrath Scroll (天雷の札, Tenkaminari no Fuda) is a magic scroll available in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The scroll was embedded with a spell from the Lightning and Thunder Runes, "Bolt of Wrath". When used, this spell scroll would generate storm clouds which would drop a thunderbolt on a single enemy, inflicting intense scorching damage.

name: Bonsai Tree
game: Suikoden I, II, III, IV & V
description: Bonsai Tree (ぼんさい, Bonsai) is a recurring antique and trade item in the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Bonsai are generally pine and other evergreen trees. They are planted in small pots and their branches meticilously pruned and curated.

Tending to bonsai trees was considered a calming and meditative activity. They were especially popular in Rokkaku Hamlet and other areas such as South Window City.

In Gregminster, most of them sold for roughly 25,000 potch, but those with fine branches could fetch as much as 50,000 potch at a trading post.

name: Book
description: Books (ふるい本, Furui Hon) are a trade item in several games of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These tradeable books, available in many places, contained various kinds of knowledge. As trade goods, handwritten and woodblock-printed books were generally quite expensive, selling for 3,500 potch in the Forest Village and up to 7,000 potch in Rockaxe.

Still, they were often purchased for the libraries established by both the Toran Liberation and New Alliance Armies. Such books often contained information on history, political affairs, and so on.

name: Buttered Clams
description: Buttered Clams (かいバター, Kaibatā) is a recipe in Suikoden II and Suikoden III, where it is localized as Butter Sauted Clam. It is Recipe #8 in the former game and Recipe #5 in the latter.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: A dish made with fresh shellfish. Depending on the seasonings used, diners can enjoy a wide variety of flavors, from rich to light. However, the Mai Mai??? is considered a very peculiar dish, so perhaps choose a more normal meal instead.

name: Cake
description: Cake (ケーキ, Kēki) is a recipe in Suikoden II and Suikoden III. It is Recipe #17 in the former game and Recipe #10 in the latter.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Cake is a confection made by kneading an egg into a sponge-like texture, then topping it with cream. It can be round or rectangular in shape and cut into pieces for eating. The way to eat it varies from person to person, but it is generally eaten with a fork. As a treat, it is highly favored by women, but does not keep well.

Cake holds great appeal in both shape and taste, but unusual ingredients can easily turn this delicious treat into a dangerous gambit.

name: Candle
description: Candles (ロウソク, Rōsoku) are a trade item in several games of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: Candles made by hardening vegetable wax into a cylindrical shape using a strand of beeswax as a wick. As a traded commodity, it was sold for 200 potch in Rokkaku Hamlet and 750 potch in Crom Village.

name: Canopy Defense Scroll
description: The Canopy Defense Scroll (守りの天蓋の札, Mori no Tengai no Fuda) is a magic scroll available in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The scroll was embedded with a spell from the Earth and Mother Earth Runes, "Canopy Defense". When used, a strong aura of light would envelop the entire party, completely repelling enemy magic.

Early in the Dunan Unification War, when the Highland Army led by Rowd attacked Greenhill City, Nash Latkje's strategy was to place these magic scrolls on the walls of the city to protect it from attacks by the Highland Army magicians.

name: Celadon Urn
game: Suikoden I, II, III, IV & V
description: The Celadon Urn (せいじのつぼ, Seiji no Tsubo), localized as Celadon Pot in Suikoden IV and Suikoden V, is a recurring antique in the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Celadon Urn is a porcelain vase fired in a blue-green color. The beautiful color of the porcelain is very popular in many countries. The selling price tended to range from 10,000 to 20,000 potch, with Celadon Urns in Gregminster in particular fetching up to 23,000 potch.

The Zexen appraiser Lebrante was an avid collector of Celadon Urns and once gladly joined the New Alliance Army in exchange for the last vase needed to complete his 100 urn collection. Following the Dunan Unification War, he would decide to expand his collection to 1,000 Celadon Urns.

name: Chain Mail
description: The Chain Mail (チェインメイル, Cheinmeiru) is a type of equipment available in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: A chain mail armor made by linking a series of gold or other metal rings together. It was one of the more popular armors as it was easy to move around in and had a fairly high defensive effect.

name: Chaos Shield
description: The Chaos Shield (こんとんのたて, Konton no Tate) is a type of equipment available in Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
One of the best shields available in the Toran region, this shield was only readily available for sale in the port town of Kirov. They were also available in Tinto City in the City-States of Jowston.

The origin of its name is unknown, but one theory is that it stems from its rarity, being often obtained from monsters.

name: Chest Plate
description: The Chest Plate (ブレスアーマー, Buresuāmā) is a type of equipment available in Suikoden and Suikoden II. It was originally localized as the Brass Armor.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This was an easy-to-forge breastplate made of large metal plates connected by strings. It protected both the front and the back of the wearer. It was widely used for light armor due to its inexpensive cost, especially by new military recruits.

name: Chinese Banquet
description: The Chinese Banquet (まんがんぜんせき, Mankanzenseki, Man-Han Imperial Feast), localized as the Kaiseki Dinner in the 1999 English localization of Suikoden II, is a recipe and item in Suikoden II and Suikoden III. It is Recipe #40 in the former game and Recipe #20 in the latter.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This extravagant and expansive dish has been historically enjoyed mainly as court food. Because it requires many ingredients and the cooking method is complicated, it requires a first-class chef like Hai Yo or Jinkai to prepare.

Jinkai favored the Chinese Banquet as his main course during his battle with Hai Yo and gifted the recipe to Hai Yo following his ultimate defeat.

name: Chinese Dish
game: Suikoden I, II, III, IV & V
description: The Chinese Dish (中国絵皿, Chūgoku Ezara) is a recurring antique in the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These were unusual painted plates made with a unique dyeing technique from a foreign country. They tended to depict various subjects such as dragons and monkeys. They are sold for about 12,000 potch in Dunan and about 6,000 potch in Toran.

name: Chowder
description: Chowder (ポタージュ, Potāju) is a recipe in Suikoden II. It is Recipe #6 in the game.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Chowder and potage are general terms for a thick, creamy soup. They can vary greatly depending on the ingredients and seasonings used.

The Dark Chowder seasoned with Soy Sauce is so tasteless that the diner will fall into a state of Panic after eating it.

name: Circlet
description: A Circlet (サークレット, Sākuretto) is a type of equipment available in several games of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This woman's head accessory is a metal band fitted to the wearer's forehead. Usually decorated, its defensive capabilities are quite thin seeing as its primary function is as a fashion piece.

name: Clay Guardian Scroll
description: The Clay Guardian Scroll (土の守護神の札, Tsuchi no Shugoshin no Fuda) is a magic scroll available in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The scroll was embedded with a spell from the Earth Rune, "Clay Guardian". This spell would improve the defensive capabilities of the target by envolping them with the protection of the earth.

name: Copper Hammer
description: The Copper Hammer (カッパーハンマー, Kappāhanmā) is a hammer available in several games of the Suikoden series. It is usually the second lowest powered optional hammer in the game.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The Copper Hammer is a hammer used by blacksmiths in order to forge weapons. This hammer is normally used by full-fledged blacksmiths who have moved past their beginning apprenticeships. One such hammer was seen in Gregminster Palace during the Dunan Unification War. It was a superior tool to the Iron Hammer.

name: Coral
game: Suikoden I, II, III, IV & V
description: Coral (サンゴ, Sango) is a recurring antique and trade item in the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This item could be found at sea and required an appraisal as an antique, but could also be bought at trading posts.

It would normally sell for about 40,000 potch at an item shop, but it fetched a high price of 55,000 potch at the Rockaxe trading post.

name: Croquettes
description: Croquettes (コロッケ, Korokke) are a recipe in Suikoden II. It is Recipe #18 in the game.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A croquette is a delicious deep-fried dish made with potatoes. There are many variations depending on the ingredients and seasonings. Among them, the Eilie Croquettes made with salt are apparently so flavorful that they cause food poisoning and can lead to people fainting in agony.

Croquettes were considered a delicacy of Radat Town in South Window Principality.

name: Crystal Ball
