description: The Crystal Ball (光るたま, Hikaru Tama) is a trade item in several games of the Suikoden series. It normally attracts the attention of non-human races.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: An orb that shines in a mysterious color. They are often bought and sold as trade goods. They are not very rare, but they seem to be very attractive to kobolds. Normally, they can be purchased in human towns for around 300 to 500 potch, but in kobold settlements, they fetch as much as 7,000 potch.

name: Curry Rice
description: Curry Rice (カレー, Karē) is a recipe in Suikoden II. It is Recipe #28 in the game.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Curry is powdered mixture of various spices such as pepper and mustard. Its unique yellowish-brown color, appetizing aroma, and stimulating spiciness make it a popular menu item. High recommended is the Mayo Curry, which packs a lot of energy. It is especially loved by warriors because they can become invincible immediately after eating it.

Gregminster, capital of the Toran Republic, was known for its curry dishes.

name: Dancing Flames Scroll
description: The Dancing Flames Scroll (おどる火炎の札, Odoru Kaen no Fuda) is a magic scroll available in Suikoden II and Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This scroll was embedded with a spell from the Fire and Rage Runes, "Dancing Flames.". It was a very useful scroll that caused great damage to entire enemy groups by producing huge flames, but one should always remember that creatures immune to fire attacks exist. Still, it was a useful scroll for those who were poor at magic, or simply didn't have a Fire Rune.

Nash Latkje used one these magic scrolls to wipe out a horde of attacking zombies at Rein Penenberg's manor. He would also attempt to use one against Elza only to have it blocked by her Silent Lake Scroll.

name: Deer Antler
description: Deer Antlers (鹿のツノ, Shika no Tsuno) are a trade item in several games of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: Literally, the antlers of an adult deer. They are either cut off from the animal or are shed naturally. They are grinded and used as a medicine. They are traded in many trading posts, and were sold for around 1,000 potch in the Forest Village and 5,500 potch at New Alliance Army Headquarters.

name: Defective Urn
game: Suikoden I, II, III, IV & V
description: The Defective Urn (しっぱいのつぼ, Shippai no Tsubo), also known as the Failure Urn and in Suikoden IV the Pot of Failure, is a recurring antique in the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: A failed piece of pottery that has been deformed or miscolored due to some error in the manufacturing process. Normally, they are sold at a throwaway price of about 10 to 20 potch, but in Kobold Village, they can sell for around 330 potch due to their eccentric shapes and colors intriguing the residents.

name: Defense Rune Piece
description: The Defense Rune Piece (守の紋章片, Mori no Monshō Hen) is a rune fragment item from Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This rune fragment can affect the robustness of the user. When used, it permanently improves one's physical durability and making them more resistant to physical attacks.

name: Dragon Armor
description: Dragon Armor (竜鎧, Ryū Yoroi) is a type of equipment available in Suikoden and Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This armor is said to have been crafted from the scales of a dragon, harder than iron. It boasts no special effects but its defensive power is very high despite being lightweight and its rare materials make it priced at a premium. It generally costs somewhere in the region of 23,000 potch.

name: Dragon Incense
description: Dragon Incense (竜印呑炉, Ryūinkōro), also known as Dragon Seal Incense, is an item featured in several games of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This incense burner is engraved with a facsimile of a dragon. As soon as the lid is attached, an incredibly pure scent fills the area, helping to heal the wounds of those near it. It is considered a magic incense burner because it retains its scent no matter how many times it is used.

One such incense was kept in the roulette room of Lepant's mansion, and it is said that many adventurers have spent much time trying to obtain it through the luck of the spinning wheel. The Guardian Deity statue at the New Alliance Army Headquarters could also gift this rare item.

name: Dream Robe
description: The Dream Robe (夢幻はおり, Mugen Waori) is a type of equipment available in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This is a mysterious robe that shimmers in seven colors. In addition to its extremely high defensive properties, it also has the effect of straining the eyes of the wearer's opponent, making it more difficult for them to land a blow.

name: Drops of Kindness Scroll
description: The Drops of Kindness Scroll (守りの霧の札, Mori no Kiri no Fuda) is a magic scroll available in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The scroll was embedded with a spell from the Water Rune, "Drops of Kindness". This spell would cause water droplets to rain down and completely heal the wounds of one ally and restore their strength.

name: Earth Armor
description: The Earth Armor (霧のローブ, Daichi no Yoroi) is a type of equipment available in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This armor had outstanding defensive power. It was said to contain the power of the Earth Rune and in addition to reducing damage from earth-based attacks, it also protected against poison and other abnormal conditions.

It is rarely distributed to the general public, on rare occasions it can be found in the armor shops of Muse City.

name: Earth Rune Piece
description: The Earth Rune Piece (土の紋章片, Tsuchi no Monshō Hen) is a rune fragment item from Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This is a rune shard that was separated from a Earth Orb. By embedding it in a weapon, it is said to imbue attacks with earth elemental properties as well as enhancing the defensive capabilities of the weapon bearer.

name: Earth Shield
description: The Earth Shield (大地のたて, Daichi no Tate) is a type of equipment available in several games of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The strongest shield, boasting immense strength. This shield is said to harbor the power of the Earth Rune and is the finest shield available in the entire Toran region.

Its rarity was such that during the Gate Rune War, it could only be obtained by either defeating the Magic Shield creatures that appeared in the Dragon's Den or purchasing it in Gregminster at the very end of the war.

It was also very rare in the Dunan region, with only one being kept in a treasure chest in Rockaxe Castle. It reduces damage from earth elemental attacks and prevents abnormal status effects such as poison.

In the Grasslands these shields could only be found in dangerous locations such as the Ruins of Cyndar.

name: Escape Talisman
game: Suikoden I, II, III, IV & V
description: The Escape Talisman (すり抜けの札, Surinuke no Fuda) is a standard item throughout the Suikoden series. It allows the player to teleport to the entrance of a dungeon.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
When used in such ominous locations as caves and mountain paths, this charm can warp the user and their companions back to the entrance. When used in a location with multiple entrances, it takes the user to the entrance they used themselves.

As such, these charms are good for shortening travel time or in emergency situations and were considered essential, but occasionally, due to some mysterious force in the area, they are rendered ineffective.

name: Famous Urn
description: The Famous Urn (名のあるつぼ, Na no Arutsubo) is a unique antique in Suikoden II. It was localized as the Famous Vase in the 1999 English localization of Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This medium-sized jar was placed in Genkaku's dojo. It apparently originally belonged to Genkaku and when Nanami and the hero attempted to escape from Kyaro Town it was one of the vases she considered taking to help fund their journey.

This unique item was valued at 12,000 potch, bringing in 6,000 potch when sold, making it the best choice.

name: Fine China
game: Suikoden I, II, III, IV & V
description: Fine China ( 景徳鎮 ( けいとくちん ), Keitokuchin), also known as Fine Bone China, is a recurring antique in the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This legendary antique is said to have been made in Jingdezhen, a city far out at sea where the pottery arts flourished. These pieces are generally sold for between 60 to 120,000 potch.

name: Fire Rune Piece
description: The Fire Rune Piece (火の紋章片, Hi no Monshō Hen) is a rune fragment item from Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This is a rune shard that was separated from a Fire Orb. By embedding it in a weapon, it is said to imbue attacks with fire elemental properties as well as causing additional damage to enemies through its residual flames and heat.

name: Fire Wall Scroll
description: The Fire Wall Scroll (炎の壁の札, Honō no Kabe no Fuda) is a magic scroll available in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The scroll was embedded with a spell from the Fire and Rage Runes, "Fire Wall". This spell would cause a huge wall of flame to emerge from the earth, inflicting damage on all enemies standing in a horizontal line above it.

name: Fish Fry
description: Fish Fry (フライ, Furai) is a recipe in Suikoden II. It is Recipe #9 in the game.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: A dish made by frying fish and other ingredients in oil, this recipe was popular in several areas but most prominently in Kuskus Town. The Fried Tacos made with Red Pepper seasoning can leave the diner feeling Toasty after eating.

name: Flame Helmet
description: The Flame Helmet (炎のフルヘルム, Honō no Bōshi) is a type of equipment available in several games of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This helmet has intricate flame patterns carved into it and is considered one of the most effective helmets. It also has magical powers, protecting the wearer from fire attacks and increasing their physical strength. It was quite a rare piece of equipment.

name: Flaming Arrows Scroll
description: The Dancing Flames Scroll (火炎の矢の札, Kaen no ya no Fuda) is a magic scroll available in Suikoden II and Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This scroll was embedded with a spell from the Fire Rune, "Flaming Arrows." The spell shot spears of fire out in a wide area, burning the enemy forces. It was considered one of the more basic fire magic scrolls and was very convenient for those who could not use a Fire Rune or did not own one.

Nash Latkje tried to use this scroll against Wang at Caleria Fortress, but Wang quickly dashed forward and snatched the scroll from Nash's hand before he could use it.

name: Flower Painting
game: Suikoden I, II, III, IV & V
description: The Flower Painting (花の絵, Hana no E) is a recurring antique in the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: Beautiful paintings such as these featured a variety of flowers, including morning glories, sunflowers, and chrysanthemums. These works sold for as little as 7,000 potch and upwards of 16,000 potch in cities such as Gregminster.

name: Flower Vase
description: The Flower Vase (花びん, Kabin), also known as simply as Vase, is a recurring antique in the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A ceramic vase used to hold flowers, this Flower Vase was a masterpiece crafted with the utmost care and attention.

Normally, it is sold for 2,500 potch in the Toran Republic and about 5,000 potch in the City-States of Jowston, but in the Forest Village, it was sold at a slightly higher price of 6,700 potch.

name: Flute
description: Flutes (ふえ, Fue) is a trade item in several games of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These handmade bamboo flutes of various shapes and sizes were common trade goods and available at trading posts through the Dunan region. However, their price did not rise or fall much in any one area, selling between 200 and 700 potch depending on location and market conditions.

name: Fog Robe
description: The Fog Robe (霧のローブ, Kiri no Rōbu) is a type of equipment available in Suikoden II and Suikoden III. It is also known as the Robe of Mist.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This enchanted robe is imbued with the power of water and grants high defensive protection, especially against water-based attacks. Surrounding the wearer in a fine mist, it also has the effect of repelling enemy attack magic to a certain degree.

It was a rare piece of equipment, only scarcely being seen for sale in Rockaxe.

name: Fried Pork Cutlets
description: Fried Pork Cutlets (トンカツ, Tonkatsu), Tonkatsu in the 1999 localization, is a recipe in Suikoden II. It is Recipe #27 in the game.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A cut of pork fillet dipped in egg and bread crumbs and deep fried in oil. Cream Cutlets, which uses Mayonnaise as a seasoning, was a favorite dish of warriors because it could make them feel Invulnerable when consumed during battle.

This recipe was the specialty of the Black Dragon Cooking Clan's Gyokuran.

name: Fried Rice
description: Fried Rice (チャーハン, Chāhan) is a recipe in Suikoden II and Suikoden III. It is Recipe #24 in the former game and Recipe #15 in the latter.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: Fried rice is a relatively simple dish of rice mixed with eggs and vegetables and is a frequent meal in many locations. In the hands of a professional chef such as Hai Yo or Bashok, however, more subtle and varying flavors can be brought out.

name: Fritters
description: Fritters (からあげ, Karaage) is a recipe in Suikoden II. It is Recipe #15 in the game. In the 1999 English localization of Suikoden II, it was called the Fried Fish Balls recipe.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This karaage dish is made by deep frying chicken or fish without batter. If prepared by a skilled chef such as Hai Yo, the diner will enjoy a crispy outside and juicy inside.

The Fiery Fritters are so spicy that if it is used during a battle, it will somehow cast the Dancing Flames spell. Unsurprisingly, it is not recommended for those who do not enjoy spicy foods.

name: Full Armor
description: Full Armor (フルアーマー, Furuāmā) is a type of equipment available in Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This metal armor covers the entire torso. It is very heavy, but very effective in defense. It is sold in the Warriors' Village and is considered the best armor sold around Lake Toran. It is called Full Armor, as opposed to the highly similar Full Plate, around the Toran region.

name: Full Course
description: The Full Course (フルコース, Furukōsu) is a recipe in Suikoden II and Suikoden III. It is Recipe #35 in the former game and Recipe #18 in the latter.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: A relatively difficult series of dishes to prepare using a variety of ingredients. All the courses are satisfying and tailored to specific regions or races. Only the Kobold Course is said to have lacked wide appeal with other diners feeling it lacked in taste.

name: Full Helmet
description: The Full Helmet (フルヘルム, Furuherumu) is a piece of equipment in Suikoden and Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Full Helmet completely covers the head and thus greatly prevents attacks to the head. It is heavy and has a narrow field of view, but has a high defensive effect.

Because it is a costly item, it was not widely available, but it is said to be sold in rare cases at Rockaxe and the Warriors' Village.

name: Full Plate
description: The Full Plate (フルプレート, Furupurēto) is a type of equipment available in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This set of metal armor covered the entire body. Known widely as a very well-made set of armor, it was adopted as equipment for soldiers of the Highland Army.

It was generally difficult to obtain due to limited production numbers but could occasionally be found for sale in Banner Village, of all places.

A very similar type of armor was called the Full Armor in the Toran region.

name: Fur
description: Furs (けがわ, Kegawa) are a trade item in several games of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These animal pelts are used for clothing, ornaments, and other items. They were common trade goods and were available at trading posts through the Dunan region from between 350 and 800 potch depending on location and market conditions.

name: Fur Robe
description: The Fur Robe (けがわのふく, Kegawa no Fuku) is a type of equipment available in Suikoden and Suikoden II. It was localized as the Leather Coat in the 1996 English localization of Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This large coat is sown together from animal skins. The leather softens the impact of enemy attacks to a certain extent as well as providing a thick winter coat for cold weather. As such they were quite popular with travelers.

name: Goddess Statue
game: Suikoden I, II, III, IV & V
description: The Goddess Statue (女神像, Magami Zō) is a recurring antique in the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These were beautiful, carved statues of goddesses based on regional mythology. Some such statues were very old and as such were extremely valuable.

They could sell for as much as 100 to 200,000 potch and were regarded as being among the finest antiques around.

name: Gold Bar
description: The Gold Bar (金かい, Kinkai) is a trade item in several games of the Suikoden series. It is usually a very pricey trade item that can bring great wealth is sold at the right time.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This bar of pure gold was one of the most valuable trading goods. In the Dunan region, it could be bought at one of three trading posts - Crom Village, Rokkaku Hamlet, and the Highway Village.

It could be purchased for about 17,000 potch in Crom and sold for upwards for 40,000 potch elsewhere.

name: Golden Hammer
description: The Golden Hammer (ゴールデンハンマー, Gōrudenhanmā) is a hammer available in several games of the Suikoden series. It is usually the most powereful hammer in the game.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Golden Hammer is said to be the finest hammer used by blacksmiths. If one could master the use of this hammer, it would allow them to maximize the power of the weapons they work on. The leader of the New Alliance Army managed to find this rare hammer in Sajah Village and with it Tessai was able to level weapons to Lv. 16 with this hammer.

During the War of the Champions, Peggi received such a hammer, allowing the lizard blacksmith to strengthen weapons to their highest level at Budehuc Castle.

name: Graffiti
game: Suikoden I, II, III, IV & V
description: Graffiti (らくがき, Rakugaki) is a recurring antique in the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This artwork was a form of graffiti that was hard to identify the artist of. Their simplicity and undisciplined style meant that anyone could draw one, but any individual piece was likewise difficult to imitate. They sold for small amounts of money, usually between 100 and 200 potch.

name: Gratin
description: Gratin (グラタン, Guratan) is a recipe in Suikoden II and Suikoden III. It is Recipe #22 in the former game and Recipe #12 in the latter.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: Gratin is prepared by baking meat, vegetables, and other ingredients in a white sauce. Among these dishes, the Creamy Gratin, made with mayonnaise is considered the most delicious. Hai Yo received this recipe after defeating the chef Ryuko in a Cook-off.

name: Grilled Beef
description: Grilled Beef (やきにく, Yakiniku) is a recipe in Suikoden II. It is Recipe #29 in the game.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A dish in which bite-sized, thinly-sliced beef is cooked on a griddle. There are many different types of dishes, depending on the ingredients and cooking method, but all are known for their high nutritional value and delicious taste.

Although most variants are main courses, Meat Salad, served with Mayonnaise, is also served as an appetizer.

name: Grilled Fish
description: Grilled Fish (焼きざかな, Yakizakana) is a recipe in Suikoden II and Suikoden III. It is Recipe #21 in the former game and Recipe #11 in the latter.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A dish made by roasting fish over a fire. It is highly nutritious and a common household favorite.

However, Salted Fish is a more dangerous dish that emits such a strong odor that even those who enjoy seafood may fall into a state of Panic, in spite of its deliciousness.

name: Guard Robe
description: The Guard Robe (ガードローブ, Gādorōbu) is a type of equipment available in several games of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This commonly available cloak has been subjected to a strong defensive magic. It also has the ability to boost the wearer's magic power. As such, the Guard Robe is a common garment of magicians everywhere. It is also popular with general travelers, despite its price, due to its protectiveness being greater than ordinary robes.

name: Gyoza
description: Gyoza (ギョーザ, Gyōza) is a recipe in Suikoden II. It is Recipe #5 in the game.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Gyoza is a type of dumpling made by mixing flour, spreading it into a thin layer, and wrapping it around ingredients such as pork. It is a dish that can be served either fried or boiled. It is widely popular as a common household dish, but its flavor is so enjoyed that some fancy restaurants even have chefs who specialize in gyoza.

Coronet Town was known for its gyoza.

name: Half Armor
description: Half Armor (ハーフアーマー, Hāfuāmā) is a type of equipment available in Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: It is pretty much identical to its counterpart, the Half Plate. It is an armor made of metal, wrapping around nearly the entire upper body. Its protective capabilities are quite decent, though it forgoes lower body protection to keep its weight manageable, and so it is often used by warriors. It is called Half Armor, as opposed to Half Plate, around the Toran Republic.

name: Half Helmet
description: The Half Helmet (ハーフヘルム, Hāfuherumu) is a type of equipment available in Suikoden and Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: A lightweight helmet designed not to obstruct vision, as oppossed to the Full Helmet. It is often used by both professional soldiers and militiamen because it is strong enough to protect the head while being easy to wear and relatively inexpensive.

name: Half Plate
description: Half Plate (ハーフアーマー, Hāfupurēto) is a type of equipment available in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
It is pretty much identical to its Toran counterpart, the Half Armor. It is an armor made of metal, wrapping around nearly the entire upper body, as compared to the Full Plate which offers total body protection.

Its protective capabilities are quite decent and it was quite easy to find, so it was often used by travellers.

name: Hamburger
description: Hamburger (ハンバーガー, Hanbāgā) is a recipe in Suikoden II. It is Recipe #31 in the game.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: A baked dish of ground meat mixed with bread crumbs, onions, and other ingredients. It is prepared in many households and is an especially popular dish among children. The Kobold Burger, which uses salt as a seasoning, is also said to have Boosted the person eating it when used in battle.

name: Headband
description: The Headband (はちまき, Hachimaki) is a type of equipment available in Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: A long, thin cloth wrapped around the head. It has little protective effect, but is used to unify the mind and improve one's spirits. It is sold in several towns in the Toran region.

name: Headgear
description: Headgear (ひたいあて, Hitai-ate), also known as Head Gear, is a piece of equipment in Suikoden and Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This piece of head equipment was made mainly to protect the forehead. By inserting a thin plate of copper or iron in the forehead of the headband, it helps to prevent fatal injuries when the head is attacked from the front.

It is a reasonably effective piece of equipment and is popular as it does not block the peripheral vision of the wearer.

name: Healing Wind Scroll
description: The Healing Wind Scroll (いやしの風の札, Iyashi no Kaze no Fuda) is a magic scroll available in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The scroll was embedded with a spell from the Wind and Cyclone Runes, "Healing Wind". This spell could restore the health of any ally.

Warriors sometimes carried a select of these scrolls so they could heal themselves reliably in combat.

name: Hex Doll
game: Suikoden I, II, III, IV & V
description: Hex Dolls (のろい人形, Noroi Ningyō) are a recurring antique in the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Hex Dolls are unearthed straw and clay figures, dressed in sinister costumes. It is said that these objects were made by ancient peoples for magical and ritual purposes.

They did not have much appeal as trade goods, selling for as little as 120 potch in the Dunan region and for 60 potch in Toran.

name: Holly Berry
description: Holly Berries (ひいらぎの実, Hīragi no Mi) are a trade item in several games of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These are small black or red berries found on holly branches. They are used for various purposes, such as as decorations or for use in talismans. They are traded for 550 potch in the Forest Village and 1,100 potch in Rockaxe.

name: Horned Helmet
description: The Horned Helmet (ホーンヘルム, Hōnherumu) is a piece of equipment in Suikoden and Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A helmet with large horns attached to a reinforced Full Helmet. Though mostly decorative, it nonetheless boasts a very high defensive effect.

It was not available for general purchase in the Toran region. Its use was monopolized by the Scarlet Moon Empire, which procured supplies through its own connections. As such, it is a custom-made item that only so-called elite Imperial soldiers were allowed to wear.

They were also rare in number in the Dunan region with only a few being seen, very rarely, in Sajah Village for an astonishing 65,000 potch. It was even rarer in the Grasslands with Horned Helmets only been uncovered in treasure chests on Mt. Senai.

name: Ice Cream
description: Ice Cream (アイスクリーム, Aisukurīmu) is a recipe and item in Suikoden II and Suikoden III. It is Recipe #10 in the former game and Recipe #8 in the latter.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This frozen treat is made up of sugar, egg yolk, milk, and flavoring. Ice cream was popular as a dessert at the restaurant of the New Alliance Army Headquarters, especially with women and children. Any variation of ice cream is recommended for those with a sweet tooth, with the only exception being Nanami Ice Cream, due to its liberal use of salt.

name: Iron Hammer
description: The Iron Hammer (アイアンハンマー, Aianhanmā) is a hammer available in several games of the Suikoden series. It is usually the lowest powered optional hammer in the game.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Iron Hammer is a hammer used by blacksmiths in order to forge weapons. This hammer is used by a large number of blacksmiths, making it something of a beginner's tool for apprentices.

During the War of the Champions, the Lizard Clan blacksmith Peggi sought this hammer, having grown dissatisfied with his own. After receiving this hammer, he would move his operations to Budehuc Castle in thanks.

name: Iron Shield
description: The Iron Shield (鉄のたて, Tetsu no Tate) is a type of equipment available in several games of the Suikoden series. It was localized as the Steel Shield in the 1996 English localization of Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This iron shield holds sufficient strength and durability to block an enemy's attack. These shields have only been seen for sale in the armor shops of Kouan, at least in Toran.

In the City-States of Jowston, it was one of the more popular shields and widely used among ordinary soldiers. In the Grasslands, the shield was considered worth its price, but otherwise not particularly effective.

name: Japanese Dish
game: Suikoden I, II, III, IV & V
description: The Japanese Dish (中国絵皿, Kazu Ezara) is a recurring antique in the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: Among decorative plates, these ones with subtle colors were popular in Rokkaku Hamlet among other places. They often sold for between 3,000 and 6,000 potch but in Rokkaku they sold for as much as 8,500 potch due to their popularity.

name: Japanese Stew
description: Japanese Stew (なべもの, Nabemono) is a recipe in Suikoden II. It is Recipe #34 in the game.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This hot pot dish is made from meat, fish, and vegetables stewed together, served while still boiling. There are several variants of this dish and one in particular, Sweet Tofu, which uses sugar as a seasoning, is loved by many women as a dessert.

Recipe #34 could only be obtained from the DoReMi Elves in Greenhill Forest in the latter stages of the Dunan Unification War.

name: Jingisukan
description: Jingisukan (ジンギスカン, Jingisukan), localized as Genghis Khan in the 1999 English localization of Suikoden II, is a recipe in Suikoden II. It is Recipe #36 in the game.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Jingisukan is mainly created by roasting lamb on an iron spit or wire mesh. The Coopa, or rice casserole with salt as a seasoning, has a reputation for being very tasty, but the regular Jingisukan has a very particular taste, so it is a dish that people tend to either love or hate.

The dish was a Rockaxe specialty and its recipe, Recipe #36, could only be obtained from the Highland Soldiers at Rockaxe Castle in the latter stages of the Dunan Unification War. Yuzu, who branded names on her ranch animals depending on their ultimate destination, branded one sheep with the name Jingisukan.

name: Karen Painting A
description: The Karen Painting A (カレンのえA, Karen no E A) is a unique antique in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This painting could be sold as an antique at the item shop. One could obtain it by clearing the fourth stage of the Dance with Karen minigame at the New Alliance Army Headquarters.

name: Karen Painting B
description: The Karen Painting B (カレンのえB, Karen no E B) is a unique antique in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This painting could be sold as an antique at the item shop. One could obtain it by clearing the fifth stage of the Dance with Karen minigame at the New Alliance Army Headquarters.

name: Karen Statue A
description: The Karen Statue A (カレンのぞうA, Karen no Zō A) is a unique antique in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This statue could be sold as an antique at the item shop. One could obtain it by clearing the first stage of the Dance with Karen minigame at the New Alliance Army Headquarters.

name: Karen Statue B
description: The Karen Statue B (カレンのぞうB, Karen no Zō B) is a unique antique in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This statue could be sold as an antique at the item shop. One could obtain it by clearing the second stage of the Dance with Karen minigame at the New Alliance Army Headquarters.

name: Karen Statue C
description: The Karen Statue C (カレンのぞうC, Karen no Zō C) is a unique antique in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This statue could be sold as an antique at the item shop. One could obtain it by clearing the second stage of the Dance with Karen minigame at the New Alliance Army Headquarters.

name: Knight Armor
description: The Knight Armor (ナイトアーマー, Naitoāmā) is a type of equipment available in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This was a set of full plate armor with the name reflecting its intended use. It was sold in Rockaxe and other locations that were home to knightly orders, but it was so expensive that it was out of reach of ordinary soldiers.

They were heavy and required considerable training to handle. It was used by the members of the Matilda Knights, who were based out of Rockaxe Castle.

name: Knight Statue
game: Suikoden I, II, III, IV & V
description: The Knight Statue (騎士のぞう, Kishi no Zō) is a recurring antique in the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A statue of a stalwart knight. These sculptures range in size from small hand-held statues to larger ones, and are popular among boys.

Some are based on historical knights and others are modeled after the Matilda Knights. Some of them, such as those based on the Maximillian Knights, are said to be rare.

There are various types of knights, from those sold in the Scarlet Moon Empire, which cost about 30,000 potch to those sold in the Dunan region, which could cost up to 60,000 potch.

name: Landscape Scroll
game: Suikoden I, II, III, IV & V
description: The Landscape Scroll (山水画, Sansui-ga or 山水図, Sansui-zu) is a recurring antique in the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This elegant painting depicted scenery of mountains and rivers. As an antique it could be sold to item shops and was normally valued at 40,000 potch. It could also be displayed in public baths.

For some reason, it appears that Banshees tended to carry them.

name: Large Urn
description: The Large Urn (おおきいつぼ, Ōkītsubo) is a unique antique in Suikoden II. It was localized as the Large Vase in the 1999 English localization of Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This large-sized jar was placed in Genkaku's dojo. It apparently originally belonged to Genkaku and when Nanami and the hero attempted to escape from Kyaro Town it was one of the vases she considered taking to help fund their journey.

This unique item was valued at 6,000 potch, bringing in 3,000 potch when sold, making it a decent choice.

name: Leather Armor
description: Leather Armor (レザーアーマー, Rezāāmā) is a type of equipment available in several titles of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This armor is made of thick, hardened animal skin. It is lightweight, but can withstand light impacts. Although less protective than metal armor, it is easier to move in and can be worn by almost everyone. As such, it has many wearers among ordinary civilians.

name: Leather Cape
description: The Leather Cape (皮のマント, Kawa no Manto) is a type of equipment available in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: A fine cloak made of animal leather. It is sturdier than a cloth cloak and can be used as protective gear against enemy attacks. Fur Capes were generally regarded as more popular and more effective, however.

name: Leather Hat
description: The Leather Hat (皮のぼうし, Kawa no Bōshi) is a type of equipment available in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: A hat made of tough leather. Although it does not offer much protection, it is inexpensive and is readily available to most, being work as a casual cap.

name: Lightning Rune Piece
description: The Lightning Rune Piece (雷の紋章片, Kaminari no Monshō Hen) is a rune fragment item from Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This is a rune shard that was separated from a Lightning Orb. By embedding it in a weapon, it is said to imbue attacks with lightning elemental properties as well as cause additional lightning damage to enemies.

name: Lovers' Garden
game: Suikoden I, II, III, IV & V
description: The Lovers' Garden (ニ人の花畑, Ni Hito no Hanabatake) is a recurring antique in the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This very rare and precious painting was regarded as something of a masterpiece. It depicted a man and a woman frolicking in a field of flowers.

name: Luck Rune Piece
description: The Luck Rune Piece (運の紋章片, Un no Monshō Hen) is a rune fragment item from Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This rune fragment can affect the luck of the user. When used on a person, it can permanently increase their luck. The effect seems to be real, but it is so slight that it may be difficult for one to notice the increase in good fortune.

name: Magic Robe
description: The Magic Robe (マジックローブ, Majikkurōbu) is a type of equipment available in Suikoden and Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A cloak with a magic that increases its defensive effect. It is lightweight, but its magic effect offers greater protection than ordinary armor. It could be purchased in Antei and the Village of the Elves.

During the Dunan Unification War, the Magic Robe was also available in Gregminster and, on rare occasions, Radat Town.

name: Magic Rune Piece
description: The Magic Rune Piece (魔の紋章片, Ma no Monshō Hen) is a rune fragment item from Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This rune fragment enhances the ability of the user to handle magic. When used, it permanently increases one's magic concentration, giving their spells stronger power.

name: Martial Arts Robe
description: The Martial Arts Robe (けんぽうぎ, Kenpō Gi) is a type of equipment available in several games of the Suikoden series. It was localized as the Karate Uniform in the 1996 English localization of Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These clothes have been designed for martial arts practitioners and designed to be light and easy to move in, making it easier for the wearer to land blows. There is thick padding designed to protect key areas of the body, giving it considerable defensive capabilities.

name: Master's Garb
description: The Master's Garb (マスターガーブ, Masutāgābu) is a type of equipment available in Suikoden and Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This armor is beautifully decorated and detailed and also magically enhanced to the utmost defensive effect. It could also even boost the wearer's dexterity and strength.

Those who were fortunate enough to obtain a Master Garb from the Earth Golems that wandered outside Moravia Castle in the Senan region during the Gate Rune War can be said to have been extremely lucky.

The Master's Garb was not generally available to be purchased but one piece was sighted in the Tinto Mines during the Dunan Unification War. They also very rarely appeared for sale in Vinay del Zexay.

name: Master's Robe
description: The Master's Robe (マスターローブ, Masutārōbu) is a type of equipment available in Suikoden and Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A strong robe enchanted to increase its defensive effectiveness. It was originally created for physically vulnerable Sorcerers and other such magicians and has since become a favorite of most skilled mages. It is said to have been worn by evil Sorcerers who appeared in the Lorimar region during the Gate Rune War.

In addition to its excellent defensive properties, it also naturally restores the user's health.

name: Mayonnaise
description: Mayonnaise (マョネーズ, Mayonēzu) is a trade item in several games of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: An uncommon condiment made from eggs and other ingredients. It was highly prized by gourmets and used in salads and other dishes. It was also delicious when served over rice. It was sold for 400 potch in South Window City and 2,200 potch in the Highway Village.

name: Meat Pie
description: The Meat Pie (ミートパイ, Mītopai) is a recipe in Suikoden II. It is Recipe #13 in the game.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A dish made by kneading flour, rolling it out thin, wrapping the ingredients inside, and steaming it. Depending on the exact ingredients used, it can be either a main dish or a dessert. 

A kobold inside the Kobold Village chief's house held a copy of this recipe.

name: Medicine
game: Suikoden I, II, III, IV & V
description: Medicine (おくすり, Okusuri) is the standard HP restorative item throughout the Suikoden series. It usually heals 100 HP and is normally purchased in bundles of six.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This inexpensive item is the most widely used medicine used to treat injuries. It is possible to buy anywhere with relative ease. It is usually sold together in batches, a necessity for any traveler.

In the Grasslands, medicine can also be graded based on effectiveness from D to A, with Medicine A being the highest and most potent type.

name: Mega Medicine
game: Suikoden I, II, III, IV & V
description: Mega Medicine (特効薬, Tokkōyaku, Wonder Medicine) is the advanced HP restorative item throughout the Suikoden series. It usually heals 500 HP and is normally purchased in bundles of three.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: Mega Medicine is a more powerful form of the regular Medicine item with a much higher healing property, able to cure most injuries. As such, it is more expensive, costing 500 potch for three portions.

name: Mole Helmet
description: The Mole Helmet (もぐらヘルム, Mogura Herumu) is a type of equipment available in Suikoden II ans Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This helmet was a prize given for completing the novice courses of Tony's Whack-A-Mole event held at the New Alliance Army Headquarters.

In addition to having good defense, it also had the special effect of preventing allies from becoming unfriendly to the person wearing it.

A version of this helmet was part of the Mole Set available in the Grasslands. This Mole Helm was not very good in comparison to its southern counterpart but was comparatively more valuable.

name: Mole Suit
description: The Mole Suit (もぐらスーツ, Mogura Sūtsu) is a type of equipment available in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This piece of body armor was a prize given for completing the intermediate courses of Tony's Whack-A-Mole event held at the New Alliance Army Headquarters. It was a brown mole costume that covered the entire body.

Wearing this suit prevents enemies from targeting the wearer in battle. It was very effective as a piece of armor and was particularly resistant to earth attacks.

A Mole Suit was also seen upstairs in the Radat Town item shop, though how to access it is not well known.

name: Musk
description: Musk (ジャコウ, Jakō) is a trade item in several games of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This was a valuable fragrance derived from the body of a male musk deer. It was sold at trading posts for 4,500 potch in the Highway Village and 8,500 potch in Crom Village.

name: Nanami's Vase
description: Nanami's Vase (ナナミのつぼ, Nanami no Tsubo) is a unique antique in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This small jar was placed in Genkaku's dojo. It was cherished by Nanami and when she and the hero attempted to escape from Kyaro Town it was one of the vases she considered taking to help fund their journey.

Despite being the only one of its kind, the vase was only worth approximately 10 potch, making it a poor choice.

name: Needle
description: Needles (はり, Hari) are a recurring item in the Suikoden series. They are used to cure the Balloon status effect.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These special needles can burst cursed balloons attached by enemies. Three balloons will cause someone to drift away. The only way to break such balloons is to use recovery magic with a rune or by using one of these needles. They are usually sold in bundles of four.

name: Ninja Garb
description: The Ninja Garb (しのびの服, Shinobi no Fuku) is a type of equipment available in several games of the Suikoden series. It was localized as the Ninja Suit in the 1996 English localization of Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These clothes have been designed for use by ninja and are woven with a special thread. In order to blend into the darkness, these clothes are dyed colors such as a black and navy blue. As well as being protective, they were lightweight, allowing for unencumbered movement and were also designed to boost the speed of the wearer.

This grab was not generally available in the Dunan region but were rarely dropped by Tenten who appears in the Banner Forests near the Toran Republic. It was also a rare piece of equipment in the Grasslands.

name: Obento
description: Obento (おべんとう, Obentō) is a recipe in Suikoden II and Suikoden III, where it is localized as Lunch Box. It is Recipe #32 in the former game and Recipe #13 in the latter.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Mainly for eating on the go, Obento dishes are normally carried around in containers. Unlike many such portable foods, there are many forms of preparation and contents can vary wildly.

The most popular variety is the Chili Shrimp Bento, but for those concerned about proper nutrition, the Ladies Lunch is recommended. As a village visited by many passing merchants, the Highway Village is known for Obento recipes.

name: Octopus Pot
game: Suikoden I, II, III, IV & V
description: The Octopus Pot (たこつぼ, Tako Tsubo), also known as the Octopus Urn, is a recurring antique in the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Originally used to catch octopi, these contains were submerged in water where the catcher waited for the octopus to enter.

They usually sold for between 500 and 1,000 potch. However, during the Dunan Unification War, with the appearance of Abizboah at the New Alliance Army Headquarters, the price of such pots at the trading post there rose dramatically, to some 3,500 potch.

name: Ohitashi
description: Ohitashi (おひたし, Ohitashi) is a recipe in Suikoden II and Suikoden III, where it is localized as Nukazuke Pickles. It is Recipe #3 in the former game and Recipe #2 in the latter.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A delicacy of Muse City, Ohitashi is a simple salad-based side dish consisting of spinach with bonito flakes and prepared in a soy broth. However, there are many variations depending on the seasonings and cooking methods used.

Even those who are not fond of spinach may be surprised by the beautiful looking Spinach Cake dessert based on this recipe.

name: Pasta
description: Pasta (パスタ, Pasuta) is a recipe in Suikoden II. It is Recipe #19 in the game.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A general term for dishes made by kneading flour. It is widely known as a common home cooking, but it is said that the level of preparation given by a professional chef cannot be imitated by ordinary people.

Hai Yo obtained a copy of this recipe by defeating the chef Ryuki in a Cook-off.

name: Pearl
description: Pearls (パール, Pāru) are a trade item in several games of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: A ball of secretion that forms inside a shell. It is a beautiful gemstone, and true pearls are especially rare. They are traded for 18,000 potch at New Alliance Army Headquarters and up to 35,000 in the trading post at Rockaxe.

name: Peeing Boy
game: Suikoden I, II, III, IV & V
description: The Peeing Boy (しょうべん小僧, Shouben Kozō) is a recurring antique in the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: A statue of an angel doing its business. They are popular because they are cute and quite expensive because of this. They are sometimes installed as fountains with a pipe running through them. In Dunan, the price is usually around 32,000 potch, but can fluctuate depending on demand. In Rockaxe, the price can be even higher due to its high popularity. In Toran, the price is about 16,000 potch, about half the price.

name: Persian Lamp
game: Suikoden I, II, III, IV & V
description: The Persian Lamp (けいとくちん, Perusharanpu) is a recurring antique in the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This metal lamp originates from a country far beyond the sea. Shaped something like a tea pot, they are filled with oil and are lit at the mouth. 

Legend has it that if one were to gather a number of these lamps together, a mysterious djinn would appear and grant the person's wish. They normally sold for between 7,500 to 16,000 potch but prices could go as high as 22,000 potch in Rockaxe.

name: Pizza
description: Pizza (ピザ, Piza) is a recipe in Suikoden II. It is Recipe #25 in the game.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A form of pie dough topped with tomatoes and other ingredients and baked. It is loved by people of all ages because it can be topped with a wide variety of ingredients to suit one's own tastes.

It was popular among students at New Leaf Academy in Greenhill City so it is not surprising the recipe was occasionally available at the item shops there. Meg also enjoyed Anchovy Pizza.

name: Pointy Hat
description: The Pointy Hat (とんがりぼうし, Tongaribōshi), also called the Pointed Hat in prior localizations, is a type of equipment available in Suikoden and Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: A cone-shaped hat made from a flimsy material, often paper or cloth. It is not very effective in defense as it designed purely for aesthetics, but if a monster attacks with a Balloon, the hat's pointed peak can be used to burst it.

name: Power Rune Piece
description: The Power Rune Piece (力の紋章片, Chikara no Monshō Hen) is a rune fragment item from Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This rune fragment enhances the physical strengther of the user. When used, it permanently increases one's muscle strength, allowing them to deal more damage with physical attacks in combat.

name: Protection Mist Scroll
description: The Protection Mist Scroll (守りの霧の札, Mori no Kiri no Fuda) is a magic scroll available in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The scroll was embedded with a spell from the Water and Flowing Runes, "Protection Mist". This spell would cause a magical fog to envelop the group, potentially repelling harmful magic from the enemy for a certain period of time.

name: Quiche
description: Quiche (ドリア, Doria) is a recipe in Suikoden II. It is Recipe #11 in the game.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This dish consists of rice topped with gratin and baked. The exact nature of the dish varies depending on the ingredients, but Lasagna made with Red Pepper was especially popular for its delicious taste.

Kuskus Town's quiche was highly recommended and the recipe was sometimes available from the item shop there.

name: Rain of Kindness Scroll
description: The Rain of Kindness Scroll (守りの霧の札, Mori no Kiri no Fuda) is a magic scroll available in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The scroll was embedded with a spell from the Water and Flowing Runes, "Rain of Kindness". This spell would create gently ebbing water to surround all allies and heal their wounds, restoring their strength.

name: Ramen
description: Ramen (ラーメン, Rāmen) is a recipe in Suikoden II and Suikoden III. It is Recipe #30 in the former game and Recipe #16 in the latter.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Ramen is a dish consisting of noodles simmered in a broth with vegetables and other ingredients. It is a favorite of many people throughout the land because of its smooth texture and easy preparation method.

Hai Yo was awarded the Ramen recipe by Antonio after defeating him in a Cook-off.

name: Red Pepper
description: Red Pepper (レッドペッパー, Reddopeppā) is a trade item in several games of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Unlike ordinary pepper, this spice was reputed to be extremely hot. It was sometimes used in Red Curry and other such dishes.

It was sold for 1,200 potch in Crom Village and for up to 3,200 potch in Rockaxe.

name: Revenge Earth Scroll
description: The Revenge Earth Scroll (復讐の札, Fukushū no Fuda) is a magic scroll available in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The scroll was embedded with a spell from the Earth and Mother Earth Runes, "Revenge Earth". This spell would cause numerous earth swords to emerge from the earth around one ally, giving them the power to counterattack against enemy attacks with the earth's fury.

name: Rice Omelet
description: Rice Omelet (オムライス, Omuraisu) is a recipe in Suikoden II. It is Recipe #23 in the game.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A thin layer of prepared rice wrapped in an egg and baked, this dish is especially popular among children and young women. Although there are many varieties of this recipe, many egg lovers say that nothing beats the basic Rice Omelet.

Recipes are sometimes available for purchase at the Two River City item shop.

name: Robe
description: The Robe (ローブ, Rōbu) is a type of equipment available in Suikoden and Suikoden II. The robe is the weakest piece of body equipment.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: Sleeved, loose-fitting garment that covers both the upper and lower body in cloth. This everyday clothing can obscure the exact position of the body, leading to foes missing attacks against someone wearing this, but it is very weak in defensive properties.

name: Rolled Omelet
description: Rolled Omelet (たまごやき, Tamagoyaki) is a recipe in Suikoden II and Suikoden III. It is Recipe #1 in both games. It was localized as Tamago-Yaki in the 1999 English localization of Suikoden II and Egg Roll in the 2002 localization of Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: A general term for an omelet dish made by baking eggs. As a dish, it is quite rudimentary and easily prepared by cooks such as Hai Yo. The Eggsplosive variant, made with Red Pepper, had magical effects and could be used in battle.

name: Sacrificial Buddha
game: Suikoden I, II, III, IV & V
description: The Sacrificial Buddha (身代わり地蔵, Migawari Jizō), also known as the Sacrificial Jizo, is a recurring item in the Suikoden series. They usually block or auto-cure the Unconscious status effect when equipped.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This jizo can save the life of its holder by taking their place if they becomes incapacitated in battle. The item is somewhat valuable in the Toran region because it was usually only sold by the Toran Liberation Army through Chandler or at Kirov's item shop.

It is said that Viktor survived his confrontation with Neclord during the North Window tragedy due to a Sacrifice Buddha he had bought at Daisy's request. The thief Hoi, who was frequently accousted for his activites, also carried several of these for safety purposes.

name: Salad
description: Salad (サラダ, Sarada) is a recipe in Suikoden II. It is Recipe #4 in the game.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A simple dish consisting of an assortment of raw vegetables and seasonings such as dressing. Depending on the seasoning, there are several variations such as Sweet Salad, Pickled Cabbage, Island Nation Salad, Green Salad, and even Veggie Stir Fry.

All are relatively simple to make and can be easily prepared by anyone. Cabbage is the main ingredient.

name: Sandwich
description: The Sandwich (サンドイッチ, Sandoitchi) is a recipe in Suikoden II. It is Recipe #12 in the game.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A food consisting of thinly sliced bread with meat, vegetables, and/or other ingredients between the slices. The Teri-Sand is especially delicious and is one of the most popular dishes around.

It is even said that after eating this satisfying dish, diner's bad moods were instantly lifted.

name: Sashimi Platter
description: The Sashimi Platter (さしみもりあわせ, Sashimi Moriawase) is a recipe in Suikoden II. It is Recipe #38 in the game. It was called the Sashimi Combo in the 1999 English localization of Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A sumptuous dish made with whitefish, shellfish, shrimp, salmon, and other ingredients. Depending on the way it is prepared, it can be served in a variety of elaborate ways, such as on a small boat or dandy style. The price is relatively high with prices ranging from 1,400 to 6,000 potch per dish.

At the New Alliance Army Headquarters, Retso would give his Sashimi Platter recipe to Hai Yo after losing to him in their second Cook-off battle.

name: Scale Mail
description: Scale Mail (スケイルメイル, Sukeirumeiru) is a type of equipment available in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: Armor made from a leather tunic base with metal pieces overlayed like scales. Very popular in Rockaxe, it was considered both easy to manufacture and a highly protective piece of equipment.

name: Shredding Wind Scroll
description: The Shredding Wind Scroll (切り裂きの札, Kirisaki no Fuda) is a magic scroll available in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This scroll was embedded with a spell from the Wind Rune, "Shredding Wind". It was known as the most powerful wind magic scroll.

Using it would create a tornado that enveloped the entire enemy, tearing through their bodies and inflicting great damage.

The maid Ril once used a Shredding Wind Scroll in an attempted murder, activating it from outside a locked room in an attempt to kill Milan Fiori.

name: Silver Dragon Armor
description: The Silver Dragon Armor (銀糸の竜鎧, Ginshi no Ryū Yoroi) is a type of equipment available in several games of the Suikoden series. It was originally localized as the Silver Armor. In Suikoden III it localized as the Dragon Scale.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This magic armor was said to be made of thin, sacred silver threads and dragon scales. The wearer of this armor automatically heals wounds and recovers strength during battle. It also has a high level of defense, especially against attacks imbued with darkness. It could also help improve the wearer's chain attack skills.

Millian's Armor Emporium in Muse City would stock this superb piece of armor at the zenith of the Dunan Unification War.

name: Silver Hammer
description: The Silver Hammer (シルバーハンマー, Shirubāhanmā) is a hammer available in several games of the Suikoden series. It is usually the second most powerful optional hammer in the game.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Silver Hammer is a hammer used by blacksmiths in order to forge weapons. It is said to be able to bring out a great deal of power from a weapon and was very much sought after.

After the liberation of Greenhill City during the Dunan Unification War, the students of New Leaf Academy gave their Silver Hammer to the New Alliance Army. By handing it over to Tessai at New Alliance Army Headquarters, it was possible to raise a weapon's level to Lv. 15.

During the War of the Champions, Peggi received such a hammer, allowing the lizard blacksmith to strengthen weapons to Lv. 15 at Budehuc Castle.

name: Silver Hat
description: The Silver Hat (銀の帽子, Gin no Bōshi) is a type of equipment available in several games of the Suikoden series. The Silver Hat is a mid-game, powerful accessory which gives the wearer a high DEF boost.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This metal hat has been lavished with valuable silver. Although it is quite expensive, its defensive benefits make it well worth the asking price. In addition to its protective qualities, it has the magical ability to slowly heal the wounds of the wearer and also resists dark attacks.

name: Simmered Fish
description: Simmered Fish (にざかな, Nizakana) is a recipe in Suikoden II. It is Recipe #14 in the game.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: Fish stewed in soy sauce, salt, and other seasonings, this dish was popularly eaten in Radat Town and elsewhere. Though a relatively simple dish to prepare, true masters of cooking such as Hai Yo could create a dish that even those who did not like seafood were able to eat without issue.

name: Skill Rune Piece
description: The Skill Rune Piece (技の紋章片, Waza no Monshō Hen) is a rune fragment item from Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This rune fragment can enhance the reflexes of the user. When used on a person, it can permanently increase their reflexes and poise in combat.

This fragment was not sold by merchants and would disappear when used.

name: Special Stew
description: The Special Stew (とくせいシチュー, Tokusei Shichū) is a recipe in Suikoden II and Suikoden III. It is Recipe #39 in the former game and Recipe #19 in the latter.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Meat and vegetables are cooked for a long time and seasoned with salt and butter. There were many variations depending on the ingredients and seasoning, and all were known to be nutritious and delicious. Some stews were said to be so tasty that they could even restore a person who had lost the ability to fight.

Gremio was the originator of this particular stew recipe. He was highly regarded for his cooking, especially his stews, the recipes for which were originally inspired by his own mother's cooking.

name: Speed Rune Piece
description: The Speed Rune Piece (速の紋章片, Haya no Monshō Hen) is a rune fragment item from Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This rune fragment can affect the speed of the user. When used on a person, it can permanently increase their speed and reaction times.

This fragment was not sold by merchants and would disappear when used.

name: Steak
description: Steak (ステーキ, Sutēki) is a recipe in Suikoden II. It is Recipe #37 in the game.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Steak is a meat dish made by grilling beef or other meat on a griddle. It is especially favored by warriors because of its high nutritional value.

Gordeau, leader of the Matilda Knights had a copy of this recipe in his room at Rockaxe Castle during the Dunan Unification War.

name: Stone of Luck
description: The Stone of Luck (運の石, Un no Ishi) is a stat increasing item that appears in Suikoden II and Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This stone was a charm believed to increase the user's luck. It would disappear once prayed to, bringing good luck to its owner. The higher one's luck, the easier it is to land a critical hit, and the higher a person's chances of escaping from enemies.

During the War of the Champions this rare stone was sometimes found in the Ghost Knight's treasure trove in the North Cavern.

name: Stone of Magic
description: The Stone of Magic (魔の石, Ma no Ishi) is a stat increasing item that appears in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This mysterious stone, imbuded with runic power, increases the user's Magic slightly. It disappears once used. The higher one's magic, the more powerful their rune attacks.

name: Stone of Magic Defense
description: The Stone of Magic Defense (魔守の石, Mamoru no Ishi) is a stat increasing item that appears in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This mysterious stone has been imbued with the power of a rune and when used increases the user's Magic Defense slightly. It disappears once used. The higher one's magic defense, the more they can resist magic attack damage.

name: Stone of Power
description: The Stone of Power (力の石, Chikara no Ishi) is a stat increasing item that appears in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This stone, empowered by a rune, increases the user's strength slightly when used. It disappears once used. The higher one's strength, the more powerful their physical attacks. It was a rare and highly valuable stone.

name: Stone of Defense
description: The Stone of Defense (直守の石, Chokumamoru no Ishi) is a stat increasing item that appears in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This rune-influenced magic stone increases the user's defense slightly. This rare stone disappears once used. The higher one's defense, the less damage they will receive from physical attacks.

name: Stone of Skill
description: The Stone of Speed (技の石, Waza no Ishi) is a stat increasing item that appears in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This stone was said to increase the user's dexterity by praying to it. It disappears once used. The higher one's dexterity, the easier it was to connect with physical attacks.

name: Stone of Speed
description: The Stone of Speed (速の石, Haya no Ishi) is a stat increasing item that appears in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This rare stone increases the user's speed by lightening the user's body. It disappears once used. The higher one's speed, the quicker one can attack in battle.

name: Sugar
description: Sugar (さとう, Satō) is an item in several games of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Salt is one of the basic seasonings of cooking. It can be used in combination with recipes to create a variety of dishes. It was also important as a trade good, being sold at trading posts throughout the land.

During the Gate Rune War, when McDohl first met Sarah in Kirov she agreed to join the Toran Liberation Army under one condition: That he procure her some soap. To do this, he had to embark on a trading spree within the city, and as part of this, had to purchase this common seasoning in the item shop in order to exchange it for a Yardstick.

name: Sunomono
description: Sunomono (すのもの, Sunomono) is a recipe in Suikoden II. It is Recipe #16 in the game.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A simple dish of fish and vegetables soaked in vinegar, there are many varieties depending on the ingredients used. It was a favorite dish of the elderly, and perhaps unsurprisingly the recipe was found in Taki's home in Lakewest.

There are also reports that it had a special effect of removing Rust and causing other status effects.

name: Sushi
description: Sushi (すし, Sushi) is a recipe in Suikoden II. It is Recipe #33 in the game.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Rice seasoned with vinegar and salt and mixed with fish and vegetables. The rice is formed into a rice ball and topped with fish, shellfish, or other ingredients. The Fancy Rice Ball is especially popular for its delicious taste.

Hai Yo was awarded the Sushi recipe by Lester after defeating him in a Cook-off.

name: Tai Chi Garb
description: The Tai Chi Garb (太極の服, Taikyoku no Fuku), also known as the Taikyoku Tunic, is a type of equipment available in several games of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These strange clothes of foreign make is comprised of two heavily contrasting colors; black and white. This is meant to represent the virtues of yin and yang.

Although it appears to be a normal gi, it was imbued with some form of exotic mystical power. As such, it heavily reduces the amount of damage the wearer takes and even increases the speed of the person wearing it. Some forms of the tunic even protected again abnormal status effects.

During the Gate Rune War, the Rock Buster monsters of Seek Valley could sometimes be found carrying this garment. It was generally very rare but could be seen for sale in Tinto City on rare occasions.

name: Tempura
description: Tempura (てんぷら, Tenpura) is a recipe in Suikoden II. It is Recipe #20 in the game.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: Fish or shellfish is dipped in a flour-based batter and deep-fried in oil. In the hands of a top chef such as Hai Yo, tempura would be crispy and very tasty. Snack Tempura, which used Sugar as a seasoning, was popular among children.

name: Teriyaki
description: Teriyaki (てりやき, Teriyaki) is a recipe in Suikoden II. It is Recipe #26 in the game.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Teriyaki is a dish made by baking meat or fish fillets in a mixture of mirin (sweet sake), soy sauce, and other seasonings so that they become glossy. The BBQ Bomb, with Red Pepper as a seasoning, had magical effects when used in battle.

The Highway Village in the Matilda Knightdom was known for its teriyaki.

name: Throat Drops
description: Throat Drops (のどあめ, Nodoame), also localized as Cough Drops, are a recurring item in the Suikoden series. They are used to cure the Silence status effect.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These lozenges are made by infusing herbs and are designed to soothe the throat. If one were to take a drop when they have a throat injury and cannot speak, it would provide immediate relief.

name: Thunder God's Garb
description: The Thunder God's Garb (雷神のふく, Raijin no Fukuro) is a type of equipment available in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This garb is said to have the blessing of the god of thunder. In addition to its high defensive power, it also increases the accuracy of the wearer's attacks and their resistance to lightning attacks.

These clothes are rarely sold to the general public, but some were seen very rarely at the armor shop in Rockaxe.

name: Thunder Runner Scroll
description: The Thunder Runner Scroll (走る雷撃の札, Hashiru Raigeki no Fuda) is a magic scroll available in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The scroll was embedded with a spell from the Lightning and Thunder Runes, "Thunder Runner". When used, When used, an electric shock would run through the earth in a straight line, damaging all enemies who come into contact with it.

name: Tomato Soup
description: Tomato Soup (トマトスープ, Tomatosūpu) is a recipe in Suikoden II and Suikoden III. It is Recipe #2 in the former game and Recipe #3 in the latter.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Tomato soup is a type of soup made by boiling ingredients and was mainly served as an appetizer. As the name suggests, tomatoes were used as a primary ingredient. This recipe and some of its variants were said to have detoxifying properties.

The wandering chef Yu Kum gave Hai Yo this recipe after Hai Yo defeated him in a friendly Cook-off.

name: Traditional Clothing
description: Traditional Clothing (民族いしょう, Minzoku Ishō) is a trade item in several games of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These are traditional clothing items of various ethnic groups, especially foreign nations with unique cultures, such as the Karaya Clan. They have a certain stylistic charm, such as their innovative use of color and patterns not seen in the immediate region.

Such clothing traded for 800 potch in Gregminster and up to 2,500 potch in the Highway Village.

name: Trio Painting
description: The Trio Painting (3人のえ, San-ri no E) is a unique antique in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This item was considered an antique and so could be sold at an item shop. It could be found by examining the large tree behind the Genkaku family home in Kyaro Town following the defeat of the Silver Wolf in L'Renouille Palace.

The painting depicted the trio of the hero, Jowy, and Nanami.

name: Tunic
description: The Tunic (チュニック, Chunikku) is a type of equipment available in several games of the Suikoden series. The Tunic is a very weak early game piece of equipment.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: A normally sleeveless leather coat made of slightly thicker than normal materials, common among the general public. Although it is supposed to absorb blows, its low defensive properties usually mean it is worn underneath more effective armor.

name: Water Rune Piece
description: The Water Rune Piece (水の紋章片, Tsuchi no Monshō Hen) is a rune fragment item from Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This is a rune shard that was separated from a Water Orb. By embedding it in a weapon, it is said to imbue attacks with water elemental properties as well as providing the bearer with a small healing effect.

name: Wide Urn
description: The Wide Urn (口ひろつぼ, Kuchihiro Tsubo) is a recurring antique in the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
As its name suggests, this antique was an urn with a wide mouth.

As an antique, it usually sold for between 4,000 and 8,000 potch. Though it could sell for up to 9,500 potch in the Forest Village and 13,000 potch in Crom Village.

name: Wind Feather Ornament
description: The Wind Feather Ornament (風のはねかざり, Kaze no Hane Kazari) is a type of equipment available in several games of the Suikoden series. It is also known as the Feathered Hat.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This feather ornament is imbued with the power of the Wind Rune. In addition to having a limited protective effect, it also makes the wearer quicker as well as more resistant to wind attacks.

name: Wind Hat
description: The Wind Hat (風のはねかざり, Kaze no Bōshi) is a type of equipment available in several games of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This hat is imbued with the power of the Wind Rune. In addition to having a great protective effect, it also makes the wearer quicker as well as more resistant to wind attacks.

It was rarely seen in stores, with the Tinto City market occasionally having it in stock for 35,000 potch.

name: Wind Rune Piece
description: The Wind Rune Piece (風の紋章片, Kaze no Monshō Hen) is a rune fragment item from Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This is a rune shard that was separated from a Wind Orb. By embedding it in a weapon, it is said to imbue attacks with wind elemental properties as well as allow for quicker handling of the weapon.

name: Wind of Sleep Scroll
description: The Wind of Sleep Scroll (眠りの風の札, Nemuri no Kaze no Fuda) is a magic scroll available in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The scroll was embedded with a spell from the Wind Rune, "Wind of Sleep". When used, this magic scroll could put groups of enemies to sleep by produced a sweet scented air causing extreme drowsiness.

name: Windspun Armor
description: The Windspun Armor (風のつむいだ鎧, Kaze no Tsumuida Yoroi ) is a type of equipment available in several games of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Windspun Armor is known throughout the Toran region as the greatest armor of all. During the Gate Rune War, the powerful monsters known as Echidna sometimes carried this incredible armor, which boosted both defense and agility.

It is imbued with the power of the Wind Rune, making it especially effective against wind magic, and was known as an extremely expensive piece of armor. It was very rarely seen for sale in Sajah Village for an eye-watering 210,000 potch.

The armor was also highly prized and rarely seen in the Grasslands as it had the additional property of enhancing one's Holy Dash technique.

name: Wine
description: Wine (ワイン, Wain) is a trade item in several games of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: Wine is a grape wine produced in various regions. Various types are widely traded depending on the region. It was traded for 650 potch in Gregminster and up to 1,900 potch in Rockaxe.

name: Wooden Amulet
description: The Wooden Amulet (木ぼりのお守り, Kibori no Omamori) is a trade item in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These wooden amulets were bought and sold at various trading posts throughout the land. It was considered a trade good and so could not be sold at an item shop.

name: Wooden Shield
description: A Wooden Shield (木のたて, Ki no Tate) is a type of equipment available in several games of the Suikoden series. The Wooden Shield is a low level, basic early game defensive equipment.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This is a generic shield made from wood. They are inexpensive shields and can be used easily due to their light weight.

As they are mainly used for training purposes, they do not provide a great defensive benefit. They have been seen for sale in Kaku and Rockland among other places.

name: Wooden Shoes
description: Wooden Shoes (木ぐつ, Kigutsu) are a type of equipment available in several games of the Suikoden series. Wooden Shoes are low level, basic early game defensive equipment.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: These shoes are carved from hard wood and sold in a variety of shapes and are then customized to better fit a person's feet. Rather than being used as armor, they are usually used as everyday wear by people and as such are commonly available.

name: Alert Rune
description: The Alert Rune (かくせいの紋章, Kakusei no Monshō), localized as Alertness Rune in Suikoden III, is a rune that appears in several games of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This rune has the power to awaken the magical talents of its bearer. During an extended battle, this rune will increase the magic attack prowess of its bearer through the Awakened status, making it useful for stubborn enemies.

name: Angry Dragon Rune
description: The Angry Dragon Rune (乱れ竜の紋章, Midare Ryū no Monshō) is a rune that appears in Suikoden II and Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This rune was borne by the bodyguard Oulan during the Dunan Unification War. The rune allowed its bearer to attack with a great many turbulent blows, inflicting twice the normal damage.

Normally, the rune could only be used once per battle. But when enraged, the rune could be used indefinitely, as anger was the source of its power. Equipping a rune such as the Fury Rune alongside it would make the Angry Dragon Rune always available.

Oulan would use her Angry Dragon Rune to unleash her Angry Dragon uppercut on the black dragon terrorizing Milit Village.

name: Balance Rune
description: The Balance Rune (バランスの紋章, Baransu no Monshō), localized as Equilibrium Rune in Suikoden V, is a rune that appears in several games of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This rune enhanced the balance of the rune bearer's body. With this rune, the bearer would never lose their balance during battle. Such a rune showed its true value when used in combination with other runes or unite attacks that would knock a bearer off-balance.

name: Banshee Rune
description: The Banshee Rune (バンシーの紋章, Banshī no Monshō) is a rune that appears in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This rune allowed the bearer to steal the vitality of their ally in order to restore their own. The name comes from the creature of the same name and similar foes such as the Siren, who scream loudly and steal their target's stamina.

As such, it was not a very popular rune with the bearer's companions.

name: Barrier Rune
description: The Barrier Rune (バリアーの紋章, Bariā no Monshō) is a rune that appears in Suikoden II and Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This rune generated a transparent barrier around the bearer, which could deflect enemy magic attacks. The barrier itself stems from the magical power of the rune bearer, so the more skilled with magic the bearer, the more effective the barrier.

Note this rune does not stack with the water spell Protection Mist. The +20% effect from Protection Mist takes precedence over the effect of the Barrier Rune, regardless of how much its adding.

name: Beast Rune
description: The Beast Rune (獣の紋章, Kemono no Monshō) is one of the 27 True Runes. It is the national rune of the Highland Kingdom and appears in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Beast Rune has the ability to show and draw out raw power; passion, the violent emotions and spirits that dwell within all creatures. The official story states that High Priest Hikusaak gifted the Beast Rune to Mauro Blight in SY 236 to represent the right of the Blight family to rule the newly established Highland Kingdom. However, the truth is that the Beast Rune moved to L'Renouille of its own accord and the truth was covered up and the rune's movement presented as a gift.

Although the rune functions as a symbol of Highland's power and was considered the nation's most important national treasure, it was never practical to attempt to use its power in anything more than a symbolic function.

During the Dunan Unification War, Highland Prince Luca Blight plotted the destruction of the world by releasing the Beast Rune, invading the City-States of Jowston and slaughtering the citizens of Muse City in order to awaken it.
