Many Golden Wolves appeared in Muse City as the Beast Rune manifested itself there.

After Luca Blight's death the rune was re-sealed in L'Renouille Palace and just before the end of the Dunan Unification War, Leon Silverberg released the Beast Rune with his own blood. The rune manifested itself as a two-headed Silver Wolf but the New Alliance Army managed to defeat it.

Since the fall of Highland, the rune's whereabouts has been unknown.

name: Beast Rune
description: The Beast Rune (獣魔の紋章, Kemono Ma no Monshō, Demon Beast Rune) is a rune available in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: Ernst bears this unique rune, whose curse transforms the bearer into the form of leopard. During the full moon, the rune's curse is weakened and the person reverts to human form, though it is possible for the rune bearer to suppress the curse in very short bursts, allow the bearer access to the rune's powerful magic spells.

name: Blaze Rune
description: The Blaze Rune (ほむらの紋章, Homura no Monshō, Blaze Rune) is a rune that appears in the first Suikoden. It was localized as the Hate Rune in the 1996 English language localization of Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Blaze Rune is a unique rune and is permanently equipped to Ronnie Bell. The Hate Rune gave Ronnie Bell the ability to attack with shockwaves from a distance. It was similar to the White Tiger Rune in many respects. The rune mark depicted a ball of flame such as the bearer of the rune could attack with.

Ronnie Bell seemed to try and refrain from using the rune as she found it unladylike but when confronted in combat, she made full use of its power. The rune ensured Ronnie Bell had no weak points, being able to attack both at long and short distances.

name: Blinking Rune
description: The Blinking Rune (瞬きの紋章, Mabataki no Monshō) is a rune that appears in several games in the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This rune allows the bearer to use teleportation magic. Viki was especially adept with the rune and was said to be the one who named each spell.

Its spells did such things as teleport enemies away or teleport a variety of objects above them to drop on their heads. It did not come without risk; the rune could backfire with its intended affects targeting allies instead of enemies.

name: Blue Drop Rune
description: The Blue Drop Rune (青いしずくの紋章, Aoi Shizuku no Monshō) is a rune that appears in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A unique rune borne by both Rulodia and Abizboah. It grants the Blue Drop water-element attack which upgrades (single, horizontal row, whole) as the magic level of the bearer grows.

Unfortunately, using the rune causes the bearer to become Unbalanced.

name: Boar Rune
description: The Boar Rune (ししの紋章, Shishi no Monshō) is a rune that appears in the first Suikoden game.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Once activated, the Boar Rune granted the bearer the ability to unleash a brief but potent flurry of attacks. However, the powerful assault delivered by the user resulted in significant recoil, causing the user to be knocked off balance.

Adventurers seeking to acquire the Boar Rune could find it within a chest concealed in the Village of the Hidden Rune. Additionally, it could be obtained from defeated Death Boars encountered throughout Great Forest region. The Boar Rune could be equipped by those proficient in wielding claw weapons, allowing them to harness its might.

Although the rune was permanently equipped by Pahn, it was not a unique rune. Notably, Ronnie Bell had a Blaze Rune permanently affixed to her, rendering her unable to equip the Boar Rune. This restriction left only Eikei and Morgan as suitable candidates capable of attaching and removing the Boar Rune in the Toran Liberation Army.

name: Boronda Hawk Rune
description: The Boronda Hawk Rune (大鷹の紋章, Ōtaka no Monshō) is an attack rune that appears in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: That was a unique rune borne by Jacques. When used, its bearer leaps into the sky and fires countless arrows down on the battlefield. The power of these attacks are about half that of normal, but it held the great advantage of being able to attack all enemies. It could only be used once per battle.

name: Champion's Rune
game: Suikoden I, II, III, IV & V
description: The Champion's Rune (王者の紋章, Ōja no Monshō) is a rune that appears in several titles of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Champion's Rune is a rare rune which gave its bearer an enhanced aura of intimidation, suppressing the appearance of enemies that would pose little danger when compared to the overall strength of the party accompanying the rune bearer.

In Toran, the rune could be purchased in the Warriors' Village but was very expensive. It was even rarer in the region of Dunan, with only one being found in a chest deep within L'Renouille Palace during the Dunan Unification War.

During the War of the Champions this rune could be found in the possession of Copper Sun at the Ruins of Cyndar.

name: Chimera Rune
description: The Chimera Rune (カーミラの紋章, Kāmira no Monshō, Carmilla Rune) is a rune that appears in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This rune, seen during the Dunan Unification War, could transfer negative effects such as poison and berserk to an ally, curing the bearer from the afflictions. The rune is apparently named after a vampire woman that once existed.

name: Circle Rune
description: The Circle Rune (円の紋章, En no Monshō) is one of the 27 True Runes. It is the rune of Hikusaak, High Priest of the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Circle Rune is said to embody law and be able to administer Dharma, creating both order and stagnation. As for its more specific abilities, nothing is known but it is said to hold great power.

The rune is borne by Hikusaak, High Priest of the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia, and is consider the country's national rune.

name: Clone Rune
description: The Clone Rune (わけみの紋章, Wakemi no Monshō) is a rune that appears in Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This rune allows the bearer to attack with an avatar multiple times, dealing twice the normal damage. However, the burden on the bearer's spirit is great, and will throw them off balance, leading them to be unable to attack for a time.

name: Conqueror Rune
description: The Conqueror Rune (支配の紋章, Shihai no Monshō, Control Rune), also known as the Black Rune (ブラックルーン, Burakku Rūn) is a rune that grants its bearer control over monsters while simultaneously weakening their resolve.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Conqueror Rune is a rune that gives its users control over monsters, but simultaneously weakens their willpower, slowly ensuring their loyalty to Windy.

The Conqueror Rune can affect the user so long as it remains attached to their body; however, the user will break free of the rune's control should the rune melt and all effects caused by the rune will vanish. The melting of the rune can be painful and illicit a burning feeling. When bearers break free of the rune, they may not remember what happened to them during the time they were under the rune's control. 

The Conqueror Rune was utilized by Windy in an attempt to control Kwanda Rosman, Milich Oppenheimer and Barbarossa Rugner. Save Barbarossa Rugner, who was unaffected by the Conqueror Rune due to his Sovereign Rune, the Conqueror Rune affected both Kwanda Rosman and Milich Oppenheimer during the Gate Rune War. Both were freed from the rune's influence following their defeat in battle.

Kwanda Rosman used the power of the rune in order to push the Kobolds of Kobold Village into a feral state, but the curse was broken by the melting of Kwanda's rune. Ted also had a Conqueror Rune attached to him when he was sent to confront the hero at Seek Valley but the proximity of the Soul Eater allowed him to regain full control of his senses.

name: Counter Rune
game: Suikoden I, II, III, IV & V
description: The Counter Rune (見きりの紋章, Mikiri no Monshō) is a rune that appears in many titles of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The Counter Rune increases the visual acuity of its bearer, allowing them to more easily avoid the opponent's attack, and increasing the chances of counterattacking. It is an effective rune, especially for those who fight in the front row, as it both increased the number of opportunies for the bearer to attack while helping them to evade blows.

name: Cyclone Rune
game: Suikoden I, II, III, IV & V
description: The Cyclone Rune (旋風の紋章, Senpū no Monshō, Whirlwind Rune) is a rune that appears in many games in the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Cyclone Rune is one of the runes of the five elements. Its lower order variant is the Wind Rune. This rune allows its bearer to manipulate a mixture of both attack and support magic. Its effects mainly help to recover the stamina of the rune bearer and their allies.

When attached to a weapon, it affixes the wind element to the weapon bearer's physical attacks. Sometimes attaching it to a weapon could instead increase the chances of the wielder repelling a magic attack.

name: Darkness Rune
description: The Darkness Rune (闇の紋章, Yami no Monshō) is a rune that appears in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The Darkness Rune is a lower-order variant of the Rune of Life and Death, the Soul Eater, one of the 27 True Runes. It holds the power of darkness and several of its spells can even steal the enemy's soul instantly through said power.

name: Dawn Rune
description: The Dawn Rune (黎明の紋章, Reimei no Monshō) is a unique magic rune. It is one of the three runes of Falena, playing an integral role in the plot of Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Dawn Rune is one of the three runes of Falena, and is normally housed in the East Palace. It was believed to be the only rune borne of fragments of both the Sun Rune and the Night Rune. It was said the rune could soften the harsh brightness of the Sun Rune.

However, the rune was stolen from the East Palace during the Lordlake Uprising by Salum Barows, who used the riots as a cover to obtain the rune, which was then stored in the Barows mansion in Rainwall. This plot would be uncovered when Norden revealed the truth of the Lordlake Uprising and the Prince of Falena would take the rune into his possession, becoming its bearer.

The Dawn Rune remained in the possession of the Prince during the Sun Rune War. Its abilities would grow during the war, and the Prince would use it multiple times to help and heal people, such as Lyon. It was also used in tandem in Lyon's Twilight Rune and the Sun Rune itself several times during the war.

name: Double-Jab Rune
description: The Double-Jab Rune (かえし刃の紋章, Kaeshiha no Monshō, Barbed Blade Rune) is a rune that appears in Suikoden and Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Double-Jab Rune increases the speed at which the bearer's weapon can strike their opponent, allowing them to hit twice in a single attack. This speed does make the bearer take more damage should they be successfully countered, however.

This very powerful ability makes it a very precious rune for warriors. A rare rune, it was not seen for sale anywhere. The rune itself looked like two blades clashing, befitting its name.

name: Double-Strike Rune
description: The Double-Strike Rune (倍返しの紋章, Baigeshi no Monshō), localized as Double-Edged Rune in Suikoden V, is a rune that appears in several games of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The Double-Strike Rune was a high-risk rune that multiplied both the strength of the rune bearer and their vulnerability to attacks. The rune is most effective when used to overwhelm an opponent, defeating them before they can strike back.

name: Down Rune
description: The Down Rune (ダウンの紋章, Daun no Monshō) is a rune that appears in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This rune could not be borne by an individual, and had to be attached to a weapon. An opponent struck with a weapon that has this rune attached had a chance of having their nervous system shocking, causing them to be knocked down by the blow.

name: Dragon Rune
description: The Dragon Rune (竜の紋章, Ryū no Monshō) is one of the 27 True Runes. It is the rune of Joshua Levenheit and Milia.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Dragon Rune is the rune that controls the existence of dragons within the world. Since dragons are of a different world, the loss of the rune would result in their death. The bearer of the Dragon Rune is also able to manipulate dragons more directly.

Traditionally, the rune is borne by the leader of the Knights of the Dragon's Den and is only passed on should its bearer die in an accident or voluntarily choose to pass away. The safety of the rune is so important that the leader of the knights never takes to the front lines, it is the vice-captain who always leads the Dragon Knights in battle.

Joshua Levenheit held the rune for some 200 years but by the time of the War of the Champions, the rune and leadership of the Dragon Knights had passed to Milia. The circumstances surrounding this are unknown.

name: Drain Rune
description: The Drain Rune (ドレインの紋章, Dorein no Monshō), localized as Draining Rune in Suikoden II, is a rune that appears in several titles of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The Drain Rune is a rare rune that, for the most part, can only be attached to a bearer's right hand. When the bearer manages to strike a critical hit, the rune automatically absorbs that damage dealt and turns a portion of it into healing for the bearer.

name: Dryad Rune
description: The Dryad Rune (ドリアドの紋章, Doriado no Monshō) is an attack rune that appears in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This mysterious rune had the power to change the number of times magic could be used. It could expend the uses of more powerful spells in order to increase the uses of lesser spells.

Its name was said to derive from that of a forest spirit.

name: Earth Rune
game: Suikoden I, II, III, IV & V
description: The Earth Rune (土の紋章, Tsuchi no Monshō) is a rune that appears in many games in the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Wind Rune is one of the runes of the five elements. Its higher order variant is the Mother Earth Rune. This rune allows its bearer to manipulate a mixture of both attack and support magic. Its effects mainly help to aid and strengthen your allies. The spell Canopy Defense was said to be an especially useful spell, nullifying enemy attack magic.

When attached to a weapon, it affixes the earth element to the weapon bearer's physical attacks. It could also raise the defense of the bearer and aid in the healing of small wounds when attached to a weapon.

name: Eightfold Rune
description: The Eightfold Rune (八鬼房の紋章, Yatsufusa no Monshō) is one of the 27 True Runes. It is the rune of the dark knight, Yuber.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Eightfold Rune is one of the 27 True Runes and is said to be in the possession of Yuber. Much like its owners, its effect and nature are a complete mystery.

It is difficult to say to what extent the fearsome Yuber's abilities are due to the power of this rune and to what extent they are due to Yuber's own enigmatic abilities. Despite the similar name, it is currently not known whether Yuber's Eight Devil Rune has any connection to the True Rune.

name: Exertion Rune
description: The Exertion Rune (どりょくの紋章, Doriyoku no Monshō) is a rune that appears in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This rune could not be borne by an individual, and had to be attached to a weapon. This rune had the ability of increasing the attack power of the weapon user as the battle steadily progressed.

The warrior Hix used this rune to great effect during the Dunan Unification War.

name: Falcon Rune
description: The Falcon Rune (はやぶさの紋章, Hayabusa no Monshō) is an attack rune that appears in several games of the Suikoden series. It is always permanently attached to a sword fighter.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This rune was used by Valeria, who had received it after training with the famed Roundia Haia in Kanakan. The rune was considered a sign of mastery of Roundia Haia's falcon-style. Anita, who had also trained under Master Haia, also bore this rune. The rune mark itself depicts a falcon speeding through the sky.

Using the rune would slightly decrease the user's hit rate but would allow them to strike quickly and rapidly in quick succession, dealing great damage to the enemy.

name: Fire Breath Rune
description: The Fire Breath Rune (火ふきの紋章, Hifuki no Monshō) is an attack rune that appears in Suikoden II. It is the rune of Bolgan.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This unique rune was borne by the traveling entertainer Bolgan during the Dunan Unification War. In battle, Bolgan used the rune to blow fire out of his mouth at the opponent, causing high damage.

However, after using the rune, Bolgan would be knocked off-balance and unable to act for a time.

name: Fire Dragon Rune
description: The Fire Dragon Rune (炎の竜の紋章, Honō no Ryū no Monshō) is a rune that appears in Suikoden II and Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A unique rune borne by Zamza. It allowed Zamza to engulf his fists with flames and attack his opponent with blazing blows. Zamza's master, Dale, held great distaste for this rune, focusing as it did on physical prowess.

Zamza had borne the rune as long as he could remember and no rune sage had ever been able to remove it. In fact, only one elderly rune sage had even been able to identify it.

This combination of magic and martial arts was very cool, but the rune would also burn its wielder due to their proximity to the flames. Although this could be mitigated by a Fire Sealing Rune at the cost of using all of Zamza's rune slots.

name: Fire Lizard Rune
description: The Fire Lizard Rune (火とかげの紋章, Hi to Kage no Monshō) is a rune that appears in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This rune could not be borne by an individual, and had to be attached to a weapon, causing it to emit flames. An opponent struck with a weapon that has this rune would be greatly damaged by the burning flames generated from the strike. However, the backdraft would also harm the weapon wielder.

The name of the rune was inspired by a creature encountered in the Sindar Ruins behind the White Deer Inn.

name: Fire Rune
game: Suikoden I, II, III, IV & V
description: The Fire Rune (火の紋章, Hi no Monshō) is a rune that appears in many games in the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The Fire Rune is one of the runes of the five elements. Its higher order variant is the Rage Rune. This rune allows its bearer to use the power of fire to attack groups of enemies with magic. When attached to a weapon, it affixes the fire element to the weapon bearer's physical attacks and can also add additional fire-elemental damage.

name: Fire Sealing Rune
description: The Fire Sealing Rune (火封じの紋章, Hifūji no Monshō) is a rune that appears in Suikoden II, Suikoden III, and Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This rune completely nullified fire-attribute attacks. No fire spell or command could hurt a bearer of this rune. On the other hand, the rune doubled damage received from water-attribute attacks, such as spells or commands, making it something of a double-edged sword.

The Fire Sealing Rune could prevent Zamza from taking damage from his Fire Dragon Rune. It could also be used to block damage from some of Luca Blight's attacks.

name: Firefly Rune
description: The Firefly Rune (ほたるの紋章, Hotaru no Monshō) is a rune that appears in several titles of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Firefly Rune is a rune with a mysterious effect, glowing softly and making it so that enemies are much more likely to attack the bearer. It is a symbol of the spirit of self-sacrifice, to protect one's peers even if you put yourself at risk.

Ordinarily a very risky rune to bear, combined with something like the Knight Rune, it could make an individual a near-invincible bastion protecting their allies.

name: Flowing Rune
game: Suikoden I, II, III, IV & V
description: The Flowing Rune (流水の紋章, Ryūsui no Monshō, Flowing Water Rune) is a rune that appears in many games in the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The Flowing Rune is one of the runes of the five elements, its lower order variant being the Water Rune. Its effects mainly help to recover the stamina of the rune bearer and their allies. When attached to a weapon, it can help to slow recover the weapon wielder's wounds.

name: Fortune Rune
game: Suikoden I, II, III, IV & V
description: The Fortune Rune (幸運の紋章, Kōun no Monshō) is a rune that appears in several titles of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Most fortunate is the person who bears this rare rune as it gives the bearer extra experience from combat, encouraging swift growth. In a way, it is a counterpart to the Prosperity Rune, which it shares similarities with in how it affects the bearer's fortunes in battle.

Tales say that the hero alone was gifted this rune by a man in Sarady.

name: Friendship Rune
description: The Friendship Rune (友情の紋章, Yūjō no Monshō) is a rune that appears in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This rune could not be borne by an individual, and had to be attached to a weapon. This mysterious rune had the effect of turning the bonds of friendship into physical strength. The more friends the bearer had brought to their side, the more powerful this rune became.

A very rare rune, it was only spotted a handful of times, found nestled in some South Window City bonsai during the Dunan Unification War or as a boon from the New Alliance Army's Guardian Deity statue.

name: Fury Rune
description: The Fury Rune (怒りの紋章, Ikari no Monshō) is a rune that appears in several games of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This normally passive rune would enrage its bearer during battle. It would drive the bearer into a frenzy, having them deal much more damage in battle. As such it was useful for those who fought with physical weapons or their bare hands, allowing them to overcome their natural limitations.

name: Gadget Rune
description: The Gadget Rune (からくりの紋章, Karakuri no Monshō), translated as Trick Rune in Suikoden and Suikoden II, is a rune that appears in several games of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This rune was attached to Juppo during the Gate Rune War. The rune gave the bearer the ability to create, control, and manipulate a mechanical doll. These dolls do not function without the use of the rune.

The origin of this rune is unknown, but some theorize that it is a mechanical being created by Juppo in the shape of a rune. The rune mark itself looks as if it has a segmented head, torso, and hands, almost like a diagram.

Meg would later inherit the rune from Juppo, using it during the Dunan Unification War. Meg's use of the rune was restricted to once per battle and with more power behind it, perhaps due to her own tinkering.

During the War of the Champions, Gadget Z would wield this rune. It would allow him to merge with a giant robot, causing great destruction to his foes, but at the cost of his own health and balance.

name: Gale Rune
description: The Gale Rune (しっぷうの紋章, Shippū no Monshō) is a rune that appears in several titles of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Gale Rune greatly increases the speed of the bearer's reflexes, as oppossed to their sprinting, making them act and react much faster in battle.

The rune is not very common in the Toran region, being unavailable in stores there. However, one of these runes can be found in Pannu Yakuta Castle. The rune was also extremely uncommon in Dunan, with a handful of beasts near Muse City dropping the rare rune.

name: Gate Rune
description: The Gate Rune (門の紋章, Mon no Monshō), also rendered as the Rune of the Gate, is one of the 27 True Runes. It is the rune of both Leknaat and Windy, having been split between the two sometime prior.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Gate Rune was safeguarded by the Clan of the Gate and when that clan was destroyed, the rune was used by Windy and Leknaat to escape the massacre. Leknaat considered the Gate Rune to hold terrible power.

The rune is, in fact, two runes, the "front" and "back" (also called the "entrance" and "exit") runes. The rune was split between the two clan sisters with Leknaat bearing the "back" rune and Windy the "front." The "front" rune was said to be able to summon from other worlds, while the "back" rune was able to reverse such summons. Their powers are considered to be connected.

Combined with the power of the 108 Stars, the Gate Rune was used to bring Gremio back from the dead, although Leknaat stated this to be a unique event. The Rune of the Gate could also return monsters to the nether world although, like all runes, it is nullified completely by the Sovereign Rune.

Windy attempted to use her rune to achieve political control of the Scarlet Moon Empire to further her hunt for the Rune of Life and Death. Towards the end of the Gate Rune War, she summoned thousands of monsters to bolster the Empire's forces before the final battle but had her creatures returned by the combined power of Leknaat's "back" rune and Joshua Levenheit's Dragon Rune.

The whereabouts of Windy's "front" half of the rune is unknown following the Gate Rune War, along with Windy herself. The "back" half remained in the possession of Leknaat.

name: Gozu Rune
description: The Gozu Rune (ゴズの紋章, Gozu no Monshō), localized as Minotaur Rune in Suikoden V, is a rune that appears in several titles of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The rune, named after the enemies who dropped it, was only borne by those who wielded axe-based weapons in combat. This rune boosted the abilities of the bearer's axe, allowing them to damage an entire row of enemies at once, but leaving them off-kilter afterwards.

name: Great Hawk Rune
description: The Great Hawk Rune (オオタカの紋章, Ōtaka no Monshō) is an attack rune that appears in several games of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This rune could only be borne by those who wielded bows and similar weapons in combat. The Great Hawk Rune would allow its bearer to execute a powerful attack, rapidly firing arrows at all enemies on the battlefield simultaneously.

The damage to each single enemy would not as great as a more focused shot, but when fighting against many foes at the same time, this technique was very efficient as well as effective.

name: Groundhog Rune
description: The Groundhog Rune (山ねずみの紋章, Yamanezumi no Monshō) is a rune that appears in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This rune was borne by Millie during the Dunan Unification War. The rune was named as such by Millie, but most are skeptical to her claim that Bonaparte is a common groundhog.

The rune encouraged her pet, Bonaparte, to fight and using it would have Bonaparte attack the enemy with its gargantuan maw, chewing the enemy up before spitting them out. The rune could only be used once per battle, however. When Bonaparte ran away from Millie, the rune was useless and unable to be used until he was recovered.

name: Hazy Rune
description: The Hazy Rune (おぼろの紋章, Oboro no Monshō), localized as the Haziness Rune in Suikoden III, is a rune that appears in several titles of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Hazy Rune is a rune which causes the bearer to become enshrouded in a fine mist, making it difficult for enemies to focus their attacks on the bearer. Because of this, the rune bearer has an easier time evading melee attacks from the enemy.

Although its bearer could not avoid every attack with this rune, it was still a very reliable enhancement for those on the battlefield.

name: High-Speed Rune
description: The High-Speed Rune (真神行法の紋章, Shin Shinkōhō no Monshō, lit. "True Spirit Travelling Method Rune") is an effect rune featured in several games of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The High-Speed Rune grants its bearer significantly increased speed, leg strength, and stamina, allowing for swift traversal. The rune's abilities make it invaluable for covering long distances quickly and escaping dangerous encounters with ease. 

Cedric, a participant in the Island Liberation War was associated with the rune. Cathari, a member of the Howling Voice Guild and participant in the Sun Rune War also bore this rune.

During the Gate Rune and Dunan Unification Wars, the rune was equipped by Stallion.

A lesser variant of the rune, known as the Speed Rune, exists but is rarely encountered. While the concept of runes does not exist in the world of Suikoden Tierkreis, the Godspeed Talisman was a rare piece of equipment with a similar power.

name: Howling Rune
description: The Howling Rune (ほえたける紋章, Hoetakeru Monshō) is an attack rune that appears in Suikoden II and Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This rune was borne by the beastmaster Badeaux during the Dunan Unification War. The rune allowed Badeaux to increase the fighting spirit of beast allies, and greatly increases their attack power.

name: Hunter Rune
description: The Hunter Rune (ハンターの紋章, Hantā no Monshō) is a rune that appears in several games of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: In the Dunan region, this rune was attached to a weapon. It allowed the bearer of said weapon to steal an item from an opponent when struck. Unfortunately, the attack power and accuracy of the weapon is also greatly reduced by the rune.

name: Isshin Rune
description: The Isshin Rune (一真の紋章, Isshin no Monshō) is a unique attack rune in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This was a rune unique to the mercenary Joker. This rune was only used by Joker, who was an expert in both magic and martial arts. When used, it gathered power in both hands before releasing it to attack.

Not only was it very powerful, it had a good change of knocking the enemy off balance. However, use of the rune would also make its bearer unbalanced for a time.

name: Jongleur Rune
description: The Jongleur Rune (吟遊詩人の紋章, Ginyūshijin no Monshō) is a rune that appears in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: Reciting the songs of the Jongleur Rune gave the rune bearer access to magic that could have different effects on those that hear it. The songs are very useful magic, but the drawback is that the song's effects last only so long as the rune bearer keeps singing. If they stop, or are made silent, the spell's effects end.

name: Killer Rune
game: Suikoden I, II, III, IV & V
description: The Killer Rune (ひっさつの紋章, Hissatsu no Monshō) is a rune that appears in several titles of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The Killer Rune increases the concentration of the bearer during combat, allowing the wielder to spot enemy key points. This allows the bearer to deliver a critical hit to the enemy's vital points at a greater frequency than usual.

name: Kindness Rune
description: The Kindness Rune (好意の紋章, Kōi no Monshō) is a rune that appears in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This rune could not be borne by an individual, and had to be attached to a weapon. The rune had the effect of turning the kindness of friendship into power for the weapon's wielder. The more favoured the person, the higher the power gained and the more powerful the bearer's attacks.

name: Kite Rune
description: The Kite Rune (トンビの紋章, Tonbi no Monshō) is an attack rune that appears in Suikoden II, Suikoden III, and Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This rune could only be used by those wielding shuriken weapons, or similar weapons like darts. This rune allowed the bearer to expand their attack range, throwing their projectiles at all enemies, by improving their leaping and handling abilities. Still, the less concentrated attack meant less damage would be dealt to each individual foe.

name: Knight Rune
description: The Knight Rune (騎士の紋章, Kishi no Monshō) is a rune that appears in Suikoden II and Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: Bearing this rune, when a wounded comrade is targeted by a foe, the bearer shields them from the potentially fatal blow. This rune is considered to be a rune filled with the essence of chivalry and honor.

name: Lightning Rune
game: Suikoden I, II, III, IV & V
description: The Lightning Rune (雷の紋章, Kaminari no Monshō) is a rune that appears in many games in the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The Lightning Rune is one of the runes of the five elements with its higher order variant being the Thunder Rune. The magic of this rune is geared towards powerful lightning attacks against single foes and when applied to a weapon allows the wielder to deal additional lightning elemental damage.

name: Lion Rune
description: The Lion Rune (レオの紋章, Reo no Monshō, Leo Rune) is a rune that appears in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The Lion Rune is a rune which can only be borne by claw-wielders. It allows the user to perform a powerful attack against an enemy but will reduce the user's ability to wield and protect against magic while it is equipped. It can be found in Two River City for 10,000 potch.

name: Lion's Blade Rune
description: The Lion's Blade Rune (ししせんじんの紋章, Shishisenjin no Monshō) is a rune mentioned in the Sega Saturn release of the original Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Lion's Blade Rune was said to be a rune used by Eikei to summon monsters and other such creatures for training purposes.

Some obscure records suggest that Eikei used this rune to summon monsters for the officers of the Toran Liberation Army to test their strength and battle prowess against.

name: Magic Absorb Rune
description: The Magic Absorb Rune (魔力吸いの紋章, Maryoku Sui no Monshō), localized as Magic Drain Rune in Suikoden II, is a rune that appears in Suikoden II and Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This rune was attached only to weapons in the Dunan region. Hitting an attack with a weapon that bears this rune would possibly recover lower level magic for the wielder. On the other hand, the rune also lowered the accuracy of the weapon.

name: Mayfly Rune
description: The Mayfly Rune (かげろうの紋章, Kagerō no Monshō) is an attack rune that appears in Suikoden II. It is the rune of Mondo.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This unique rune was borne by the ninja Mondo during the Dunan Unification War. When he used the rune, Mondo would split into three people, allowing him to attack multiple enemies at the same time.

name: Medicine Rune
description: The Medicine Rune (おくすりの紋章, Okusuri no Monshō) is a rune that appears in Suikoden II, Suikoden III, and Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Tuta prominently bore this rune during the Dunan Unification War, though it was not exclusive to the youth. The Medicine Rune allows the bearer to rapidly apply medicine to the wounds of severely injured allies in the middle of combat.

Although this was the only ability the rune granted, it could often be the difference between victory and defeat in a dangerous battle. However, to be effective, the bearer would have to have a number of appropriate healing items on hand.

name: Moon Rune
description: The Moon Rune (月の紋章, Tsuki no Monshō) is one of the 27 True Runes. It is the rune of Sierra Mikain.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Moon Rune is the rune of Sierra, the first vampire. It is unknown how Sierra found the rune but it seems to have strong side-effects seeing as Sierra became mentally unstable after inheriting the rune.

The rune grants the magic of eternal youth to the bearer but at the expenses of the desire to feed on human blood, transforming those who are fed on into vampires. However, the rune also gives compassion to those same creatures, curbing their desires.

In the past, the Moon Rune was enshrined in the Blue Moon Village, giving energy and comfort to its inhabitants until the rune was stolen by Neclord. The villagers, other than Sierra, were then forced between feasting on blood to survive or a painful death. The now-immortal Neclord traveled the world with the rune, killing maidens and destroying villages but during the Dunan Unification War, he was defeated.

Sierra reclaimed the Moon Rune from Neclord before his death and continues to carry it.

name: Mother Earth Rune
game: Suikoden I, II, III, IV & V
description: The Mother Earth Rune (大地の紋章, Daichi no Monshō) is a rune that appears in many games in the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Mother Earth Rune is one of the runes of the five elements. Its lower order variant is the Earth Rune. This rune allows its bearer to manipulate a mixture of both attack and support magic.

Embedding this rune to a weapon could increase the weapon wielder's defensive strength and even allow them to heal wounds naturally in the middle of battle.

name: Night Rune
description: The Night Rune (夜の紋章, Yoru no Monshō) is one of the 27 True Runes. It is currently in the form of the Celestial Sword.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Celestial Sword stated that he was the Night Rune itself, but transformed. The Night Rune is said to have the power of the darkness of night, as well as the ability to see through the darkness in people's hearts.

The Night Rune has strong influence over the Moon Rune. It is said that it hides its power as the Celestial Sword in order to defeat the vampires that the Moon Rune created.

Viktor would discover the rune in its sword form in the Cave of the Past and wield it during the Gate Rune and Dunan Unification Wars.

name: Nymph Rune
description: The Nymph Rune (ニンフの紋章, Ninfu no Monshō) is an attack rune that appears in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This mysterious runes effects varied depending on the luck of its bearer. For a lucky individual, the rune could damage their opponents and heal their allies. On the other hand, an unfortunate soul might find that the rune damages their comrades, rather than their enemy, when used.

name: Pale Gate Rune
description: The Pale Gate Rune (蒼き門の紋章, Aokimon no Monshō), localized as Blue Gate Rune in Suikoden II, is a rune that appears in Suikoden II, Suikoden III, and Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Pale Gate Rune is a rune that can only be attached to the bearer's forehead. Whoever bears this rune is able to summon entities from a different world, called the "Empty World", to attack their enemies.

These envoys from another world are extremely powerful and boast varied impressive attacks, unattached to the traditional five elements. However, its most powerful magics made no distinction being friend or foe and so must be used with caution.

name: Phero Rune
description: The Phero Rune (フェロの紋章, Fero no Monshō) is a rune that appears in several titles of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The Phero Rune is a rune that exerts a mysterious charm, attracting people to the bearer. The bearer of this rune, when attacked, will find men protecting them from blows if they are women, and vice-versa. Its name was inspired by the pheromone usage of insects and other such creatures.

name: Phoenix Rune
description: The Phoenix Rune (白鳳の紋章, Hakuhō no Monshō) is an attack rune that appears in Suikoden III. It is the unique rune of Chris Lightfellow.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This unique rune belonged to Chris Lightfellow, who had it embeded on her left hand.

When used, the bearer would fire countless thrusts at the enemies at high speed. The power of those thrusts would equal twice that of a normal attack, but would unfortunately throw the user off-balance afterwards.

name: Poison Rune
description: The Poison Rune (毒の紋章, Doku no Monshō) is a rune that appears in Suikoden II and Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This rune was not borne by individuals during the Dunan Unification War and was instead attached to a weapon. An opponent struck with a weapon that has this rune attached had a chance of being poisoned by the blow.

Because of its toxic nature, it was not a popular rune among the general public.

name: Prosperity Rune
description: The Prosperity Rune (金運の紋章, Kin'un no Monshō) is a rune that appears in several titles of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Prosperity Rune blesses the luck of the bearer, making them earn twice the amount of potch they normally would. The Prosperity Rune is known to be very rare, but it is said that the hero and Ted once received one from visiting a man in the isolated village of Sarady.

During the Dunan Unification War, a particular form of the New Alliance Army Headquarters Guardian Deity statue was known to bless the New Alliance Army with such a rune.

name: Rabid Fang Rune
description: The Rabid Fang Rune (狂牙の紋章, Kyōkiba no Monshō) is a rune that appears in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This rune was borne by Bob during the Dunan Unification War and was how the werewolf was able to maintain a human body. During battle, Bob could weaken the rune, allowing himself to transform into a full werewolf. That said, since werewolves were extremely rare, whether that was the case or that the rune cursed him to become a werefolf can not be said with absolute certainty.

Regardless, the transformation would turn Bob into a savage, powerful beast. However, when the rune reasserted itself, he would be left weakened.

name: Rage Rune
game: Suikoden I, II, III, IV & V
description: The Rage Rune (烈火の紋章, Rekka no Monshō) is a rune that appears in many games in the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Rage Rune is one of the runes of the five elements. Its lower order variant is the Fire Rune. This rune allows its bearer to use the power of fire to attack groups of enemies with magic. When attached to a weapon, it affixes the fire element to the weapon bearer's physical attacks and can also add additional fire-elemental damage.

Seed, in particular, bore a Rage Rune during the Dunan Unification War, using it in battle both against the New Alliance Army and Nash Latkje.

name: Red Rose Rune
description: The Red Rose Rune (赤いバラの紋章, Akai Bara no Monshō) is an attack rune that appears in several games of the Suikoden series. It is always permanently attached to a narcissist character.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Red Rose Rune is a rune usually found attached to such aristocratic-styled individuals as Schtolteheim Reinbach III, Augustine Nabor, and Josephine.

When used, the rune allows the bearer to wield a splendid attack, whereby the bearer elegantly attacks with their chosen weapon, complete with the scattering of roses. Although useful, it appears using the rune can leave other members of a battle party disgruntled or angry with the wielder.

Reinbach bore the rune on his head, while Augustine and Josephine both had the rune attached to their right hand. Reinbach, in particular, could also put enemies to sleep with his Red Rose Rune.

name: Resurrection Rune
description: The Resurrection Rune (破魔の紋章, Hama no Monshō, Exorcism Rune) is a rune available in Suikoden, Suikoden II and Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Resurrection Rune uses magic of the holy element, so elemental affinities and weaknesses are based off of that. It is highly effective against the undead and other such demonic forces.

It is a fairly rare rune in the first Suikoden with Fukien being the only character who has it attached upon recruitment. He was considered highly adept at wielding the rune. Only one enemy in the game drops the Resurrection Orb, that being the Whip Wolf which can be found on the field map in the Lorimar region.

In Suikoden II and Suikoden V, the rune is even more rare with a maximum of four being available in the second game through character recruitment, chests and the Guardian Deity statue, and three being available in the fifth game, should you take advantage of every opportunity to find Resurrection Rune pieces.

Both Kahn Marley and Gantetsu wielded this rune with skill during the Dunan Unification War.

name: Rune of Beginning
description: The Rune of Beginning (始まリの紋章, Hajimemari no Monshō) is one of the 27 True Runes. It is normally split into two runes: the Bright Shield and Black Sword Runes.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Rune of Beginning is said to be the rune representing sword and shield and is believed to have been present at the beginning of the world. It is normally divided into two runes: the Bright Shield and Black Sword Runes.

Along with the power of eternal youth, the rune is also said to judge order and chaos, calling back to its role as representing sword and shield. However, when separated, the rune does not provide eternal youth and the use of the individual rune's powers could end up taking the life of both bearers.

The rune is said to appear at critical turning points in history, placing the burden of destiny on two heroes. Two such heroes appeared during the war between the City-States of Jowston and the Highland Kingdom, which began in 428. The Bright Shield Rune went to Genkaku, fighting for the City-State, and the Black Sword Rune went to Han Cunningham, soldier of Highland. Realizing the danger and hardships the runes could cause, the two friends eventually sealed the runes in Toto Village.

Over 30 years later, the runes would be discovered by Genkaku's adopted son and Jowy Atreides, influencing the Dunan Unification War with the two friends on opposing sides in the conflict. This use of the rune risked both bearer's lives. Jowy Atreides was fully aware of the consequences but was determined to create a lasting peace through the rune.

Following the war, the two bearers would face each other at Tenzan Pass, with Jowy content to die to preserve peace and also to save the life of his friend. After Jowy collapsed and died, however, the rune was united once more and Jowy was revived thanks to the will and desires of his friend.

name: Rune of Punishment
description: The Rune of Punishment (罰の紋章, Batsu no Monshō) is one of the 27 True Runes. It is the rune of the protagonist of Suikoden IV and is also usable in Suikoden Tactics.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Rune of Punishment is said to be the rune representing forgiveness and atonement. As a cursed rune, it drains the life of its host in exchange for its considerable power. Once the host dies, their body is turned to ash and their soul absorbed by the rune, which then passes on to a new host. It is said that if a host can overcome this "time of atonement" then the curse will be lifted, but the truth of this is not clear.


No one knows when the rune first appeared. In times past, it was coveted for its power and so passed through countless hands. Much blood has been spilled over it. Eventually, one man bearing the rune ventured to the depths of the Ruins of Obel. Hoping to end the curse himself, he turned himself to stone with magic, perishing and sealing the rune there.

The rune remained sealed for many years before being released by the Queen of Obel. She had been seemingly drawn to the ruins by curiosity, although Lino En Kuldes suspected that the rune itself drew her there to set itself free. Soon after, she would perish after using the rune to prevent a pirate attack on her and her children. 

Since then, the rune has passed to many bearers, from Rakgi's father to Graham Cray to his son and then to the pirates Steele and Brandeau before ending up in the possession of Glen Cott. Except for Graham Cray, who cut off his arm to escape the rune's curse, all the other bearers died using the rune.

After Glen died using the rune to repel a pirate attack on Razril, his successor, who would go on to lead the Stronghold Ship during the Island Liberation War, would end up with the rune. Although he would use it several times during the war to ward off the Kooluk Empire, he would not succumb to its curse and finally, with his strong will and the aid of all the friends and companions he made, he would overcome its curse and achieve the salvation spoken of in legends of the

name: Shining Wing Rune
description: The Shining Wing Rune (輝く羽の紋章, Kagayaku Hane no Monshō) is an attack rune that appears in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: That was a unique rune borne by Ruby. When used, the bearer fires a heat ray at its target, hitting all foes in a straight line between themselves and the target. It has high power, but the bearer would lose balance after attacking with this rune.

name: Shrike Rune
game: Suikoden I, II, III, IV & V
description: The Shrike Rune (もずの紋章, Mozu no Monshō) is a rune that appears in many titles of the Suikoden series. This rune was wielded by ninja and assassins.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The rune was wielded by Kasumi during the Gate Rune War and Dunan Unification War, allowing her to use the Izuna Drop technique. When used, the bearer leaps forward, grabs their opponent's body, leaps high in the air, and attacks by slamming the foe down into the ground.

name: Sickle-Weasel Rune
description: The Sickle-Weasel Rune (大鷹の紋章, Kamaitachi no Monshō) is an attack rune that appears in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: That was a unique rune borne by Sanae Yamamoto. When Sanae used this rune, after slashing with her naginata, countless sickle monsters would attack the enemy, damaging the target and all enemies in a straight line.

name: Silence Rune
description: The Silence Rune (ちんもくの紋章, Chinmoku no Monshō) is a rune that appears in Suikoden II, Suikoden Tactics, and Suikoden V. It also makes an appearance in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This rune could not be borne by an individual, and had to be attached to a weapon. An opponent struck with a weapon that has this rune attached had a chance of having their speech suppressed, rendering them silenced by the blow and unable to use rune magic.

When fighting against the fake Fire Bringer in Milit Village, Camus would use this rune to silence Clant, the fraudulent Flame Champion.

name: Skunk Rune
description: The Skunk Rune (スカンクの紋章, Sukanku no Monshō) is a rune that appears in several games of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This rune ensured that person who bore it was less likely to be attacked by enemies. It does this by keeping foes at bay with a strong stench, much like that of a skunk. It is presumed that that is the origin of the rune's name.

name: Sleep Rune
description: The Sleep Rune (眠りの紋章, Nemuri no Monshō) is a rune that appears in Suikoden II and Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: During the Dunan Unification War, this rune was not borne by individuals, instead it was attached to a weapon. An opponent struck with a weapon that has this rune attached would be made drowzy, having a chance of being put to sleep by the blow. It was priced at 18,000 potch in Rockaxe.

name: Sound Rune
description: The Sound Rune (音の紋章, Oto no Monshō) is a rune that appears in the first Suikoden and is referenced in Suikoden II and Genso Suikogaiden Vol.1. It is used by the characters Melodye and Connell.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Sound Rune is a highly rare rune that only the acoustician Connell is currently known to be in possession of, although Melodye was known to have one in the past, gifted to her by the leader of the Toran Liberation Army who found it in Kalekka.

As the name suggests, it gives its bearer the ability to freely manipulate sounds. It seems like the rune can also be used for offensive purposes but the details surrounding that are unclear.

name: Sovereign Rune
description: The Sovereign Rune (覇王の紋章, Haō no Monshō) is one of the 27 True Runes. It is the rune of Barbarossa Rugner.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Sovereign Rune is a heirloom of the Rugner family that is in the form of the Dragon King Sword. It is said that the rune came into possession of the Scarlet Moon Empire around the time of its founding, but the details are unclear.

The Sovereign Rune protects its user from all forms of magic, even that of magical coercion, such as the Conqueror Rune and the power of the other True Runes such as the Gate Rune. It also enables its user to possess the form of the Golden Hydra.

The fact that the Sovereign Rune was, in fact, embedded in the Dragon King Sword was a secret of the Rugner family, known only by direct members of the family line. As such, Windy did not know of the rune's location until Barbarossa revealed it to her.

In some sources, Barbarossa elaborated that the Sovereign Rune carried its own curse, leaving its bearer feared and respected but never truly loved and even taking the lives of those who loved the bearer, like Claudia. Its whereabouts following Barbarossa's leap from Gregminster Palace are unknown.

name: Spark Rune
description: The Spark Rune (しでんの紋章, Shiden no Monshō) is a rune that appears in Suikoden and Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This rune produces a curious effect where the rune bearer's allies will try and immediately follow them once an attack is made. This rune can be used to have allies attack a target quickly, before the enemy has a chance to respond.

One of these runes were discovered on the body of the Double Head snake defeated in the Sindar Ruins close to Muse City.

name: Speed Rune
description: The Speed Rune (神行法の紋章, Shinkōhō no Monshō), known as Holy Rune in the 1996 localization of Suikoden, is a rune that appears in the original versions of Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This rarely seen rune boosts the walking speed of its bearer, making it possible to traverse great distances with ease. Its effect did not last long and was mainly used for traversing shorter distances. Its higher order variant was Stallion's High-Speed Rune.

Stallion also once gifted a Speed Rune to Hou and it would eventually be used by his son.

name: Spider Slay Rune
description: The Spider Slay Rune (クモ斬りの紋章, Kumokiri no Monshō) is a rune that appears in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This rune was borne by Shin during the Dunan Unification War. Shin's southern Eight Death Sword style of fighting utilized this rune in a powerful attack.

Once used, the rune would become unusable for a time, but its power was immense.

name: Star Rune
description: The Star Rune (星の紋章, Hoshi no Monshō) is a unique magic rune. It appears in Suikoden V as the rune of Zerase.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Star Rune is a unique rune, said to have been born from the Night Rune itself. Its full capabilities are unknown but it possesses magical powers that rival that of the Twilight Rune, making the Star Rune close in power to one of the 27 True Runes.

The rune is borne by Zerase who was reticent to discuss anything about herself or the rune's nature.

name: Sun Rune
description: The Sun Rune (太陽の紋章, Taiyō no Monshō) is one of the 27 True Runes. It is one of the three runes of Falena, playing an integral role in the plot of Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Sun Rune is a True Rune that governs over the great power of the sun. It has traditionally been the property of the Queendom of Falena and is normally sealed away in the Sun Palace in a bust. It had always been thought by the Falenan people that the Sun Rune was too powerful for any one person to be in control of it. According to legend, the Sun Rune was once synchronised with the Night Rune. However, once the Night Rune abandoned the Sun Rune, it would appear that the Dawn and Twilight Runes would instead be used to keep the Sun Rune's balance in power. 

With the powers of the Dawn and Twilight Runes to balance it, the Sun Rune was said to be capable of nurturing life as well as destroying it. Legends also state that it was the Sun Rune’s out of control power that destroyed the Ancient Armes Dynasty and that a miraculous Sindar survivor would emerge from this devastation, bearing the rune and founding the Queendom of Falena.

When Queen Arshtat Falenas took the Sun Rune in order to prevent it from falling into the hands of her political enemies, she became more and more unstable and violent due to the rune's influence and power, becoming uncontrollably violent in moments where she was threatened or questioned. With nothing holding her back, she threatened to destroy Falena, but was slain by the swordsman Georg Prime before she could. The Sun Rune, without a host, returned to the bust in the Sealed Room in the Sun Palace. 

Throughout the Sun Rune War, the Godwin Faction would attempt to gain access to the rune's powers and by the war's end, Marscal Godwin fled to the Ashtwal Mountains with it. Godwin sacrificed his life to the Rune so that it could take on a form to destroy his enemies. This attempt was foiled, and the rune returned to a peaceful state. With the war over, the Sun Rune was returned to the Sealed Room, and has remained there since.

name: Sunbeam Rune
game: Suikoden I, II, III, IV & V
description: The Sunbeam Rune (こもれ日の紋章, Komorebi no Monshō) is a rune that appears in several titles of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This rune envelops the bearer in a strange warmth reminiscent of sunshine, hence the rune's name, which slowly heals any wounds the bearer might have as they travel.

The Sunbeam Rune was not sold in Toran or Dunan, though they could be rarely uncovered there.

name: Swallow Rune
description: The Swallow Rune (つばめの紋章, Tsubame no Monshō) is a rune that appears in Suikoden II and Suikoden V

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This rune was borne by the wandering samurai Genshu during the Dunan Unification War. The rune allowed its bearer to attack as swiftly as a swallow while having a possibility of outright felling the foe in a single blow.

However, Genshu would lose balance after an attack and would be unable to act for a while.

name: Sylph Rune
description: The Sylph Rune (シルフの紋章, Shirufu no Monshō) is an attack rune that appears in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This mysterious rune reduced the vitality of its bearer when activated in order to help heal the rest of the battle party. It is said that it was a very rare rune and could be obtained by defeating the Pink Birds that appeared around Rockaxe during the Dunan Unification War.

The rune was named after the sylph, a type of spirit said to be related to the Wind Rune somehow.

name: Technique Rune
description: The Technique Rune (手技の紋章, Shugi no Monshō) is a rune that appears in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This rune could not be borne by an individual, and had to be attached to a weapon. Increasing hand dexterity, it gives the bearer a chance of stealing potch from the enemy when they attack, making for a vicious mugging.

name: Thunder Rune
game: Suikoden I, II, III, IV & V
description: The Thunder Rune (雷鳴の紋章, Raimei no Monshō) is a rune that appears in many games in the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Thunder Rune is one of the runes of the five elements, its lower order variant being the Lightning Rune. The magic of this rune is more geared towards powerful lightning attacks against single foes and when applied to a weapon allows the wielder to deal lightning elemental damage.

Glenshiel, the "Lightning General" wielded a Thunder Rune during the Gate Rune War. Culgan, in particular, bore a Thunder Rune during the Dunan Unification War, using it in battle both against the New Alliance Army and Nash Latkje.

name: Titan Rune
description: The Titan Rune (タイタンの紋章, Taitan no Monshō) is an attack rune that appears in several games of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Titan Rune is a rune that can be used only by those who wield two-handed swords. The rune makes it so that when used, the rune bearer's attack order is last in the party, but in exchange, the bearer delivers a more powerful blow than usual.

The name of the rune is said to derive from a form of slow-moving giant.

name: True Earth Rune
description: The True Earth Rune (真なる土の紋章, Shin naru Tsuchi no Monshō) is one of the 27 True Runes. It is the rune of Sasarai and it appears in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The True Earth Rune is one of the five elemental True Runes and governs the earth and soil. The rune was borne by Sasarai, Bishop of the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia. Just like Luc, Sasarai was created as a duplicate of Hikusaak in order to bear the True Earth Rune already in his possession.

Although Sasarai was not seen to use this rune during the Dunan Unification War, it was surmised that it had similar defensive properties to its lower order variants, the Mother Earth and Earth Runes.

During the War of the Champions, Luc would remove the rune from Sasarai as part of his plan to destroy the True Wind Rune but Sasarai would regain it at the Ceremonial Site prior to the final confrontation with Luc.

name: True Fire Rune
description: The True Fire Rune (真なる火の紋章, Shin naru Hi no Monshō) is one of the 27 True Runes. It is the rune of the Flame Champion and it appears in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The True Fire Rune is one of the five elemental True Runes and governs fire. It was once in the possession of the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia but was stolen some 50 years prior to the War of the Champions by the Flame Champion who would go on to use the rune in the Grasslands's struggles against Harmonia. 

The rune would go out of control during that conflict's final battle, decimating both forces and ending the war. After that, the Flame Champion would seal the rune away. 50 years later, the leader of the new Fire Bringer would unseal the rune and inherit the title of the Flame Champion. The True Fire Rune holds great power but is also very dangerous due to its ability to hurt friend and foe alike.

name: True Lightning Rune
description: The True Earth Rune (真なる雷の紋章, Shin naru Kaminari no Monshō) is one of the 27 True Runes. It is the rune of Geddoe and it appears in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The True Lightning Rune is one of the five elemental True Runes and governs the power of thunder. For over 70 years, this rune was in the possession of Geddoe, who fought alongside the Flame Champion and Wyatt Lightfellow in the original Fire Bringer's struggle against the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia. It appears that Geddoe inherited the rune near the location of the Karaya Village.

During the War of the Champions, Luc would remove the rune from Geddoe as part of his plan to destroy the True Wind Rune but Geddoe would regain it at the Ceremonial Site prior to the final confrontation with Luc.

name: True Water Rune
description: The True Water Rune (真なる水の紋章, Shin naru Mizu no Monshō) is one of the 27 True Runes. It is the rune of Wyatt Lightfellow and it appears in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The True Water Rune is one of the five elemental True Runes and governs water. It was once in the possession of the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia but was stolen some 50 years prior to the War of the Champions and inherited by Wyatt Lightfellow of the Zexen Federation. Wyatt would fight with the original Fire Bringer in their fight against Harmonian invasion.

In the years following the conflict, the Howling Voice Guild would hunt the True Water Rune under the command of Harmonia and Wyatt would eventually seal the rune in the Ruins of Cyndar in Grasslands before taking refuge with the Karaya Clan under the pseudonym Jimba. When the War of the Champions broke out, Wyatt would attempt to reclaim the rune but would be killed by the Destroyers. His daughter, Chris would inherit the rune and wield it throughout the war.

name: True Wind Rune
description: The True Wind Rune (真なる風の紋章, Shin naru Kaze no Monshō) is one of the 27 True Runes. It is the rune of Luc and it appears in Suikoden III. It is also mentioned in Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The True Wind Rune is one of the five elemental True Runes and governs the wind. As such, it is the parent rune of both the Wind Rune and the Cyclone Rune.

It was borne by Luc, the apprentice of Leknaat. Luc was created as a copy of Hikusaak, leader of the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia, in order to carry the True Rune.

Along with powerful battle magic, the True Wind Rune could also be used to summon Clay Dolls and was used for teleportation purposes. Luc rarely used the rune but during the Dunan Unification War, the rune's terrible power was called up in the battle against Luca Blight in order to obliterate the reinforcements from the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia, who were aiding the Highland Army at that time.

As Luc continued to carry the rune, his soul would become intertwined with it, allowing him to see visions of the Gray World, the True Wind Rune's memories of the future. Desperate to avert this future, Luc would attempt to destroy the True Wind Rune, igniting the War of the Champions in the process. Following his defeat, the location of the rune is unknown.

name: Turtle Rune
description: The Turtle Rune (かめの紋章, Kame no Monshō) is a rune that appears in Suikoden and Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Turtle Rune can prevent the bearer from suffering any abnormal condition such as silence, poison, paralysis and so on. It is this excellent role as a defense rune that has led to it earning the name "Turtle Rune".

It was a rare rune, only seen for sale in the Toran region.

name: Twilight Rune
description: The Twilight Rune (黄昏の紋章, Tasogare no Monshō) is a unique magic rune. It is one of the three runes of Falena, playing an integral role in the plot of Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Twilight Rune is one of the three runes of Falena and is normally housed in the West Palace. This rune was said to be one of the fragments shed from the Night Rune when it discarded its runic form to take on that of a sword. The Twilight Rune, with the Dawn Rune, is able to unlock certain powers of the Sun Rune. Rather than simply being a harbinger of death, the Sun Rune can be used to heal and renew. The Twilight and Dawn Runes can also combine their powers to create unique spells that can selectively wound and heal.

When the Sun Rune War broke out, the Godwin Faction began trying methods of extracting the Twlight Rune from its bust. The Rune did not willingly choose a host, so methods were taken to find an expert who extricate and attach the rune. The Rune Sage, Jeane was summoned, and was successful in embedding the Rune onto Alenia. The Queen's Knight then left for Doraat Fortress, to aid in its defense.

When the Imperial City Recapture Army breached the city gates, Alenia called upon the power of the Twilight Rune. The spell, although spectacular, did not overly damage the attacking forces, and they were able to regroup and take the town. Alenia remained in the castle, awaiting the Prince. She was repelled by the power of his Dawn Rune, and Zerase stepped forward to seize the Rune from her. However, Dolph intervened, and managed to flee with Alenia.

The Rune remained with Alenia until Sialeeds betrayed her allies to work with the Godwin Faction. The Twilight Rune willingly chose the Prince’s aunt, who left for the defense of Stormfist. As a chosen host for the rune, Sialeeds' attack was much more dangerous to the besieging army. The Prince was able enough to defeat Sialeeds, but again, failed to take the rune from its bearer.

Towards the war's end, Zerase confronted Sialeeds in Sol-Falena, but Sialeeds' power was too intense for her to overcome, even with

name: Twin Ring Rune
description: The Twin Ring Rune (双輪の紋章, Sōrin no Monshō) is a rune that appears in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This rare rune was borne by the dancer Karen during the Dunan Unification War. The rune allowed its bearer to attack while circling the opponent in a rhythmic fashion, inflicting twice the normal damage through their dance-like movements.

The user performs a series of attacks with blinding speed, ending with a blow from a spectacular aerial spin technique. The damage is twice as effective as a normal attack, but perhaps because of the intensity of the movements, the bearer may lose balance after an attack and be unable to act for a while.

name: Unicorn Rune
description: The Unicorn Rune (イッカクの紋章, Ikkaku no Monshō) is an attack rune that appears in several game of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This rune could only be borne by those who wielded spears and similar weapons in combat. The Unicorn Rune would add sharpness to the bearer's thrust, allowing its bearer to execute a powerful attack, penetrating a column of enemies. That said, the distributed damage per enemy could be less than a single attack on the same opponent would be.

However, the bearer would be left wide open following the attack, with zero defense, due to having to pull their spear back out. This meant that if the enemy survived the assault, a fatal counterattack could be on the horizon.

name: Violence Rune
description: The Violence Rune (げき怒の紋章, Geki ika no Monshō) is a rune that appears in several games of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This rare, normally passive rune would activate and enrage its bearer when a certain amount of damage was taken during battle, accumulating strength with each blow taken.

It would drive the bearer into a frenzy, while also allowing them to damage the enemy three times more than normal with their next strike before returning to normal.

name: Viper Rune
description: The Viper Rune (バイパーの紋章, Baipā no Monshō) is an attack rune that appears in several games of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This rune could only be used by a bearer who wielded a one-handed sword in battle. When used, the rune would render the bearer's accuracy extremely low, but on a successful hit, the Viper Rune would kill its opponent instantly.

name: Waking Rune
description: The Waking Rune (寝起きの紋章, Neoki no Monshō) is a rune that appears in several titles of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The Waking Rune is a rune that puts the bearer to sleep at the beginning of battle. Normally a disadvantage, the bearer of this rune will be enraged upon being woken, allowing them to fight with stronger attacks than usual.

name: Wall Rune
description: The Wall Rune (ぬりかべの紋章, Nurikabe no Monshō) is an effect rune that appears in Suikoden II, Suikoden III, and Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This rune greatly improved the defense of its bearer. However, this came with a large cost. The bearer of this rune cannot take any action other than defending attacks.

As the name implies, the Wall Rune allows the bearer to become a wall for their allies, but nothing more. Combining the Wall Rune with other runes, such as the Firefly Rune and the Knight Rune may give the bearer of the Wall Rune more utility.

The rune was said to be inspired by a demon that looked like a wall.

name: Warrior Rune
description: The Warrior Rune (力強化の紋章, Chikarakyōka no Monshō), localized as Power Rune in Suikoden V, is a rune that appears in several games of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This rune lowered the bearer's defense and boosted their strength in return. Although its benefits in increase the power of a warrior's physical attacks are obvious, the steep drop in the bearer's defense made this a potentially harmful rune for those who have not mastered its use.

It was rarely seen for sale in the Two River City rune shop for a princely 32,000 potch.

name: Water Rune
game: Suikoden I, II, III, IV & V
description: The Water Rune (土の紋章, Tsuchi no Monshō) is a rune that appears in many games in the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Water Rune is one of the runes of the five elements. Its higher order variant is the Flowing Rune. This rune allows its bearer to use water magic in order to heal themselves and their allies.

When attached to a weapon, it affixes the water element to the weapon bearer's physical attacks. It can also heal the wielder's wounds during battle when attached.

name: White Saint Rune
description: The White Saint Rune (白き聖女の紋章, Shiroki seijo no Monshō) is a rune that appears in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The White Saint Rune was a rune borne by the unicorn Siegfried. It allowed Siegfried to wield impressive lightning magic, which he used to attack his foes.

The actual rune itself was also noted to be very beautiful itself.

name: White Tiger Rune
description: The White Tiger Rune (白虎の紋章, Byakko no Monshō) is an attack rune that appears in Suikoden II and Suikoden Tactics. It is a rune used by martial artists.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This rune was borne by the master-student duo of Long Chan-Chan and Wakaba during the Dunan Unification War. It was considered something of an emblem for Long Chan-Chan's dojo. In addition to the technique of firing a ball of ki, the bearer can also attack with a powerful series of strikes.

Long Chan-Chan would use the rune to release energy from his hands while Wakaba released energy from her feet to attack the enemy. It could be used even from the rear guard, and inflicted double the normal damage on the target. However, there are cases where the user would be knocked off-balance and unable to act for a time.

This undesired effect could apparently be mitigated with training. As someone with more experience, Long Chan-Chan was better at handling the effects of this rune than his enthusiastic protege.

name: Wind Rune
game: Suikoden I, II, III, IV & V
description: The Wind Rune (風の紋章, Kaze no Monshō) is a rune that appears in many games in the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Wind Rune is one of the runes of the five elements. Its higher order variant is the Cyclone Rune. This rune allows its bearer to manipulate a mixture of both attack and support magic.

Its effects mainly help to recover the stamina of the rune bearer and their allies though it is not as effective at healing as the Water Rune. When attached to a weapon, it affixes the wind element to the weapon bearer's physical attacks as well as increasing resistance to magic.

name: Window Rune
description: The Window Rune (まどの紋章, Mado no Monshō) is a rune that appears in the first Suikoden. It is used by Window.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This rune was essential for window makers, a rare occupation in the world. Such people manipulate the power of the rune to create windows. One was hidden inside the Cave of the Past at Qlon Temple.

It is said that McDohl found it and handed it over to Window, who was in the Warriors' Village, to encourage him to join the Toran Liberation Army.

name: Wizard Rune
description: The Wizard Rune (魔力強化の紋章, Maryoku Kyōka no Monshō) is a rune that appears in several titles of the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This rune enhances the bearer's magical power. The Wizard Rune does this by channeling the bearer's magical defenses and funneling it into power instead.

The rune is risky to bear because of this, but is potentially very useful for the right bearer. It was rarely spotted for sale in Two River City.

name: Wrath Rune
description: The Wrath Rune (げきりんの紋章, Gekirin no Monshō) is a unique attack rune in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This was a rune unique to Hallec, warrior of the Nameless Lands. When used, the rune would cause Hallec to roar, filling him with rage and recovering some of his strength in the process. It could only be used once per battle.

name: Agate Prison
description: Agate Prison (アゲイト監獄, Ageito Kangoku) is a location that appears in Suikoden V. It is a secluded prison used by the Queendom of Falena to house political prisoners.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Agate Prison was a prison built on a sandbar in the middle of one of the Feitas River's eastern branches. It was a facility that housed political prisoners and other such inconvenient enemies of the state, rather than convicted criminals.

Maintained by the Godwin Faction, Lucretia Merces, who served as a military strategist during the Armes Invasion was imprisoned here on the direct orders of Arshtat Falenas. Lucretia eventually won the sympathy and loyalties of her captors and with the aid of the Imperial City Recapture Army was able to escape.

Killey was also imprisoned in Agate Prison for infiltrating the Sun Palace under the charge of attempting to steal the Sun Rune.

name: Alma Kinan Village
description: The Alma Kinan Village (アルマ・キナンの村, Aruma Kinan no Mura) is a location that appears in Suikoden III. It is an isolated settlement located in the north of the Grasslands.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This village of the Alma Kinan clan, is located behind a protective barrier deep in Kuput Forest. Isolated from the outside world, the village is populated entirely by women.

Buildings in the village are made of wood and are decorated inside with furs, showing their life of self-sufficiency which is primarily fueled by hunting.

In the rear of the village is an altar used for the soul-sending ritual. The altar is surrounded by the roots of a huge tree that towers high over the rear of the village, lending it an even more mysterious atmosphere.

name: Alseid
description: Alseid (アルセイド, Aruseido) is a location mentioned in Suikoden V. It is a village of elves located on the southern continent.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Alseid is a village inhabited entirely by elves. Located in the forests north of the Holy Land of Lunas, it is the home of both Isato and Urda.

It is said that since the dawn of time, humans have never set foot on these elven lands.

name: Altar of the Mantor Trainers
description: The Altar of the Mantor Trainers (虫使いの祭壇, Mushi Tsukai no Saidan), formerly known as the Altar of the Flame Champion (炎の英雄の祭壇, Honō no Eiyū no Saidan), is a location in Suikoden III. It is a shrine located deep within Mt. Senai in the eastern Grasslands.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Altar of the Mantor Trainers is a shrine located deep in the caves of Mt. Senai. At the time of the first Harmonian invasion of Grasslands, Le Buque was a Grasslands territory and created the Altar of the Flame Champion in his honor during the war.

Once Le Buque was annexed by the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia, the people there began to refer to the shrine as the Altar of the Mantor Trainers in order to ensure it would not be destroyed by the occupying Harmonians.

name: Amur Plain
description: The Amur Plain (アムル平原, Amuru Heigan) is a location in Suikoden III. It is a grass plain located in the southern Grasslands.

sections.title: Profile
