sections.body: The vast Amur Plain forms the southern part of the Grasslands. Due to its flat, clear view, it is difficult to organize an ambush on this land, which is the reason why it was chosen as the location of the armistice between the Six Clans and the Zexen Federation just prior to the outbreak of the War of the Champions.

name: Ancient Highway
description: The Ancient Highway (高速路, Kōsoku-ji) is a location in Suikoden III. It is a collection of connected underground passages running through the central Grasslands.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Ancient Highway was a series of underground passages connecting the ruins near Duck Village to the Great Hollow. These passages originally came from ancient ruins and one sealed doorway along the path led to the Ruins of Cyndar.

During the War of the Champions that particular door was sealed and the True Water Rune was hidden inside. The Lizard Clan made the most frequent use of the highway as the underground passages connected many various parts of the Grasslands, which they utilized to great effect in warfare.

name: Antei
description: Antei (アンテイ, Antei) is a town that appears in Suikoden. Known as Bier Blanche (ビィル・ブランシェ, Byiru Buranshe) for a short period of time, Antei is a large township in the region of Kunan.

facts.label: Nation
facts.value: Scarlet Moon Empire (until 457), Toran Republic (457 onwards)

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Antei can be considered the center of Kunan and is known as a trading town. The town is also known for producing several artists and skilled engineers. Surrounded by seasonal flowers, the town is a popular tourist destination.

During the Gate Rune War, the town was renamed "Bier Blanche" due to the uniform white walls of the town, as per Milich Oppenheimer's command. Perhaps predictably, the new name proved unpopular and difficult to remember, and a protest was organized, which marched on Scarleticia Castle. However, the group was repelled by the poisonous Antoinette surrounding the citadel.

After Milich was defeated and the region liberated by the Toran Liberation Army, the town's name reverted back to Antei.

name: Arus
description: Arus (アールス, Ārusu) is a region of the Toran Republic, and its predecessor, the Scarlet Moon Empire. It is the region containing the nation's capital, Gregminster.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Arus refers to the lands surrounding the capital of Gregminster. Both the Fortress of Kwaba and the Floating Fortress of Shasarazade protect Arus as the heart of the nation. It has a higher level of culture compared to other regions and is very active in commerce.

Arus is said to have a mild and temperate climate all year round. The region is bordered by the very high Banner Mountains to the west and the fortresses of Kwaba and Shasarazade in the south, making Arus very difficult to invade through neighboring regions.

Instead, it was the corrupt internal politics that felled the Scarlet Moon Empire, with its defensive isolation allowing political corruption to fester in the final years of Emperor Barbarossa's reign.

name: Ashtwal Mountains
description: The Ashtwal Mountains (アストラル山脈, Asutoraru Sanmyaku) are a location in Suikoden V. They are a mountain range in far northern Falena.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Ashtwal Mountains lie to the far north of the Queendom of Falena, beyond even the holy land of Lunas. Incredibly frigid, these frozen mountains form the source of the Feitas River that runs throughout the country.

Within the mountains lay the Founder's Site, a glacial area containing lost Sindar ruins. Following the defeat of the Godwin Faction during the Sun Rune War, Marscal Godwin threatened to use the Sun Rune to melt the glaciers of the Ashtwal Mountains, causing the Feitas to flood and devastate Falena.

name: Banner Forest
description: The Banner Forest (バナーの森, Banā no Mori) is a location in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Banner Forest is a forest which spreads around the Banner Mountains. It stretches from behind Banner Village all the way to the border with the Toran Republic. 

The Banner Pass runs through this forest. Also nearby is the hidden ninja commune of Rokkaku Hamlet.

name: Banner Mines
description: The Banner Mines (バナーの鉱山, Banā no Kōzan), localized as Banaar Mines in Suikoden, is a location mentioned in the first game.

facts.label: Nation
facts.value: Scarlet Moon Empire (until 457), Toran Republic (457 onwards)

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Banner Mines are located in the far northern Banner Mountains at the border with the Dunan Republic, in the region of Dana. It was owned by the Scarlet Moon Empire and was rich in minerals. It was also known for being a forced labor camp for criminals and dissidents.

During the early Gate Rune War, the Imperial Army attempted to press Mathiu Silverberg to return to military service by threatening to send a student of his to the mines.

name: Banner Mountains
description: The Banner Mountains (バナー山脈, Banā Sanmyaku) are a mountain range which appear in Suikoden and Suikoden II. Although rarely referred to directly, the mountain range is visible in both games.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The rugged Banner Mountains stretch from southern South Window Principality in Dunan to the north-west of Arus in Toran. In Toran, Mt. Tigerwolf is the most prominent mountain in the mountain range, with the town of Sarady lying in the range.

In Dunan, Banner Village is located near the range and the Banner Pass takes travelers through part of the mountain range before reaching the border with the Toran Republic. The Banner Mines are located in this mountain range, on the border with the Dunan Republic.

name: Banner Pass
description: The Banner Pass (バナーの峠, Banā no Tōge) is a location in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Banner Pass is a mountain path located behind Banner Village that eventually leads to the Toran Republic. It is surrounded by the Banner Forest.

In the past, it was a dangerous area due to the bandits present there but since the intervention of both the leader of the New Alliance Army and the hero of the Gate Rune War, the pass has been much calmer.

name: Banner Village
description: Banner Village (バナーの村 Banā no Mura) is a location that appears in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Banner is a small fishing village located on the bank of the Dunan River, which flows from Lake Dunan into Lake Toran. It lies close to the border with the Toran Republic. Fishing is the lifeblood of the village.

Behind the village lies a large forest in which the Banner Pass is located. This pass must be navigated to reach Gregminster, capital of the Toran Republic.

During the Dunan Unification War, the the leader of the New Alliance Army and the hero of the Gate Rune War met each other in this small village. It would appear that McDohl would visit here sporadically to fish.

name: Barows Mansion
description: Barows Mansion (バロウズ邸, Barousu-tei) is a location seen in Suikoden V. It is a large mansion located in Rainwall.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This expansive manse was the home of the Barows family, which ruled Rainwall. When the Sun Rune War first broke out, the prince and his entourage fled to Rainwall and with Salum Barows' backing, raised an army using the Barows Mansion as their headquarters.

However, once the truth of the Lordlake Uprising and the theft of the Dawn Rune were revealed, the prince and his forces left the mansion.

name: Baska Mine
description: The Baska Mine (バスカ鉱山, Basuka Kōzan) is a location seen in Suikoden V. It is a mine located in southern Falena.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Baska Mine is a once-prosperous mine located to the west of the Dwarf Camp. In the past, the mine was known to possess high quality ore but by the time of the Sun Rune War, these reserves were depleted and the mine was mostly abandoned.

The earliest tunnels of the Baska Mine also lead to the Dwarf Camp itself.

name: Beaver Lodge
description: The Beaver Lodge (ビーバーロッジ, Bībārojji) is a location seen in Suikoden V. It is a beaver village located in the south of the Queendom of Falena.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Beaver Lodge is a beaver settlement located in the southeast Falena. 

As befitting the beavers affinity for water, the village is a series of wooden buildings laid atop a series of interconnecting rafts. Because of this, the settlement can be moved and rearranged quite easily, in a manner similar to Raftfleet.

name: Big Hole
description: The Big Hole (大穴, Ōana), also known as the Ancient Ruins (古代遺跡, Kodai Iseki) or Abandoned Ruins (廃墟の遺跡, Haikyo no Iseki), is a location in Suikoden V. It is a mysterious cavern that appeared during the Sun Rune War.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Big Hole appeared in northeastern Falena, prompting investigation by both Jeane and Eresh. Levi and Bergen were also fascinated by the hole.

In the back of a series of caves found at the bottom of the hole lay a set of ancient underground ruins. There is a theory that these ruins were once the center of the Ancient Armes Dynasty. A vision, similar to the one experienced at Ceras Lake Ruins about the Dawn Rune, was experienced there but any connection is unknown.

It is said that these ruins suddenly became inaccessible after the conclusion of the Sun Rune War, once Eresh had disappeared.

name: Blue Moon Village
description: Blue Moon Village (蒼き月の村, Aoki Tsuki no Mura) is a location mentioned in Suikoden II and Genso Suikogaiden Vol.1.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Blue Moon Village was a village of vampires that was founded and ruled by Sierra Mikain, bearer of the Moon Rune. Thanks to the Moon Rune's power giving them vitality, vampires were able to live here in peace and solitude, without having to feed on humans.

However, some 385 years ago, the Moon Rune was stolen by Neclord, a resident who had hidden his intentions. Forced to choose between feeding on humans or dying in the village, many vampires chose the latter and left though most were said to have chosen death. Following the loss of the Moon Rune and her home, Sierra left to destroy those vampires who chose to prey on the innocent.

After destroying Neclord and reclaiming the Moon Rune during the Dunan Unification War, it seems Sierra has no plans to return to her village.

name: Brass Castle
description: Brass Castle (ブラス城, Burasu Jō) is a location in Suikoden III. It is a large fortification on the border between Zexen and the Grasslands.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Brass Castle functions as the home base of the Zexen Knights and was constructed in 422, with construction beginning immediately after the establishment of the Zexen Federation.

It is located on the border with the Grasslands and is at the forefront with the intent of safeguarding the Zexen nation from Grasslands raids.

In addition to the castle itself and the knights stationed there, a large civilian population has emerged around the facility, bringing shops and trade to the area. Several hidden passageways are said to exist inside the castle itself.

name: Budehuc Castle
description: Budehuc Castle (ビュッデヒュッケ城, Byuddehyukke-jō) is a location in Suikoden III. It is ostensibly common land, meaning that neither the Zexen Federation nor any clan of the Grasslands hold sole dominion over it.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Budehuc Castle is a sprawling complex lying on the banks of a vast lake in the northern Zexen Federation. Due to only meager funds being assigned to it, the castle was dilapidated and on the verge of becoming completely deserted. It was not considered a desired posting and many lords assigned to the castle had abandoned it soon after.

The appointment of Thomas as the new castle lord helped begin its rejuvenation. As the land was negotiated into becoming common land between Zexen and the Grasslands in the past, Thomas was able to give property to those who wished to trade, regardless of race and nationality. Hearing of this, the Zexen Council declared the common land treaty invalid.


The Council tried to take the land by force, but Thomas was able to protect the castle thanks to the strategies of Caesar Silverberg. Following this, Thomas sold the land in his last days as Zexen-appointed lord to Lucia who would lease it back to Thomas for a token fee, thwarting the Council's ambitions.

Later during the War of the Champions, its strategic position led to the castle becoming the base of the Fire Bringer, attracting even more citizens and merchants to its walls.

name: Caleria
description: Caleria (カレリア, Kareria) is a location in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2 and Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Caleria is a region of the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia that is located near the Grasslands border. The town is protected by the Caleria Fortress, where Nash Latkje once served.

The town is where the Harmonian Southern Frontier Defense Force is located. It is a lively border town with many merchants selling their wares there.

name: Caleria Fortress
description: Caleria Fortress (カレリア砦, Kareria Toride) is a location in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2 and Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Caleria Fortress is the headquarters of the Harmonian Southern Frontier Defense Force, of which Nash Latkje was once a member. From this location, Harmonia dispatched spies and mercenaries from to various locations.

The fortress is relatively small but under Vandam had a number of skilled officers. The fortress plays a role in border security for the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia.

In the center of the fortress is a large plaza used for training. Inside the fort are barracks, an infirmary, a mess hall, and prison cells.

name: Cave of the Past
description: The Cave of the Past (過去の洞窟, Kako no Doukutsu), sometimes called the Cave of Runes (紋章の洞窟, Monshō no Doukutsu), is a location in Suikoden. The cave is located at the back of the Qlon Temple.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Qlon Temple was rumored to hide a secret and Fukien, the head monk at Qlon, revealed that a way to defeat Neclord was hidden in this cave.

Fukien allowed McDohl and his companions to enter the cave. The group would found the Celestial Sword who, upon being awoken, transported the party some 300 years into the past. Presumably, this time-traveling phenomenon is where the cave derives its name from.

The great wizard Crowley spent most of the Gate Rune War hidden here, isolating himself from the world. Hugo's Illustrated War Scroll and Window's Window Rune were also hidden in this winding system of caverns.

The cave was also speculated to have some connection to the Rune of Life and Death. Because of this, it is sometimes referred to as the Cave of Runes.

name: Cave of the Wind
description: The Cave of the Wind (風の洞窟 Kaze no Dōkutsu) is a location that appears in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This dark cave, located south of South Window City, gained its name from the sound of wind blowing that can be heard inside. Inside the cave, air vents are blowing gusts of wind constantly, making it difficult to navigate.

In the past, the cave served as something of a test of courage for the children of South Window but in recent years monsters have settled in the cavern, making it dangerous to enter blindly.

This was also the place where Viktor sealed the Celestial Sword after the end of the Gate Rune War, much to its chagrin. He was familiar with the caves, having visited them as a child. He would eventually return for the sword in order to fight the resurrected Neclord.

name: Ceras Lake Headquarters
description: The Ceras Lake Headquarters (セラス湖の本拠地, Serasu Mizūmi no Honkyochi) is a location seen in Suikoden V. It was part of the Ceras Lake Ruins and served as the headquarters of the Imperial City Recapture Army during the Sun Rune War.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This base was part of the Ceras Lake Ruins, which once laid at the bottom of Ceras Lake. The base was uncovered when the ruins sluice gate was activated during the battle at Hatred Fortress. The expelling of water revealed the construction and at the suggestion of Lyon it was adopted as the Imperial City Recapture Army.

At first, the water level of Ceras Lake was quite high, so the amount of usable space was limited but as the war continued the water level continued to drop and the base grew in turn. Its appearance would end up resembling the Dawn Rune by the war's end.

name: Ceras Lake Ruins
description: The Ceras Lake Ruins (セラス湖の遺跡, Serasu Mizūmi no Iseki) is a location seen in Suikoden V. It is an ancient Sindar ruin site located in the center of the Queendom of Falena.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These ruins were located on the banks of Lake Ceras and lay in the center of the Queendom of Falena. These ruins were a legacy of the Sindar civilization and served as a sluice gate for Lake Ceras, which had no natural waterways.

Deep within the ruins, there was a door that opened in response to the Dawn Rune and at the deepest part of the ruins, an engraving that responded to the Twilight Rune was engraved there. This was the controls for the ancient sluice gate lay.

Using the sluice gate to direct a huge wave of water at Hatred Fortress was part of Lucretia Merces' strategy to ensure the Imperial City Recapture Army brought down the fortification.

name: Ceremonial Site
description: The Ceremonial Site (儀式の地, Gishiki no Ji) is a location in Suikoden III. It is a Sindar ruin and the location of the final battle of the game.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Ceremonial Site is a large Sindar ruin, located to the far north of the Great Hollow. As a structure, it is a series of passages centerd around a square altar in the middle, where the Sindar conducted large-scale ceremonies.

During the War of the Champions, the Destroyers tried to destroy the True Wind Rune by placing the remaining elemental True Runes at the altar. The ceremony would then converge the runes power, destroying the True Wind Rune and causing immense destruction of the surrounding lands. These plans would be thwarted by the Fire Bringer in what would be known as the Ceremonial Site Offensive.

Following the war, the Zexen Archaeology Association, including Rhett, would uncover many historical and cultural relics in the ruins. Ernie would uncover the Secret Zexen Ritual Inscription there.

name: Chisha Village
description: The Chisha Village (チシャの村, Chisha no Mura) is a location that appears in Suikoden III. It is a settlement located in the north-east of the Grasslands.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Chisha Village is the home of the Chisha Clan, one of the Six Clans of the Grasslands. Each house serves as a resting place for villagers and many buildings are connected by underground tunnels. The village's inn and tavern are located underground.

In the fields that lay on the slopes surrounding the village, villagers work to grow grapes. The wines made when these grapes bear fruit is a specialty of the area and is exported for sale to the Zexen Federation.

name: Circle Palace
description: The Circle Palace (円の宮殿, En no Kyūden) is a location shown in Suikoden III. It is located in the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Circle Palace is where priests of the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia, including High Priest Hikusaak meet. Its interior shines brightly, lending the palace a solemn and mysterious quality.

The sanctity of the Circle Palace was violated during the War of the Champions when Luc forcibly removed the True Earth Rune from Sasarai there.

name: Coronet Town
description: Coronet Town (コロネの村 Korone no Mura) is a location that appears in Suikoden II. It is a port town which lies on the northern bank of Lake Dunan.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Coronet is a port town located to the south of Muse City and lies against the bank of Lake Dunan. Due to its location, many traders and sailors live in this town. Fishing is the primary industry in Coronet, which has a population of 2,200.

Kuskus Town lies on the other side of the lake and, together, the two provide a link between Muse and South Window City with regular departures from one town to the other. Because of this steady stream of travelers, Coronet has a big two-story inn, with the first story being a large tavern. The town also has a blacksmith.

After the fall of Muse during the Dunan Unification War, this town came under Highland martial law and ships were banned from leaving the port without permission. This stranded the hero's group in the town until they were able to obtain illict passage from the port from Tai Ho.

The restriction of sailing from the part lasted until its liberation by the New Alliance Army.

name: Council
description: The Council (評議会, Hyōgi-kai) is the name given to the assembly hall for the Zexen Council. It appears in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Council building is located in the heart of Vinay del Zexay. As the finest building in the Zexen capital, it impresses the idea of the Zexen Council as the nation's highest authority.

There are several stores and other facilities inside, available to Zexen Council members and those with business with the council.

Chris Lightfellow was called to the Council building to meet the Zexen Council several times over the course of the War of the Champions.

name: Crom Village
description: Crom Village (クロムの村, Kuromu no Mura) is a location in Suikoden II. It is a village in Tinto Principality, with a population of 2,500.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Crom is a small village located to the southwest of Tinto City, close to the Grasslands. Because much of the gold bullion excavated in Tinto City is sold wholesale to the village trading post, Gold Bars can be purchased in Crom for comparatively low prices. Beyond this, it had no real industry.

During the Dunan Unification War, Crom would host the New Alliance Army as Tinto City was overrun by the forces of Neclord.

name: Dana
description: Dana (ダナ Dana) is a region of the Toran Republic, and its predecessor, the Scarlet Moon Empire. It is more commonly referred to simply as "the north".

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The name Dana comes from the City-States of Jowston. To the people of the Toran Republic it, along with Senan, is usually referred to simply as "the north".

Dana borders Lake Toran to the south and the Banner Mountains separate it from the region of Arus to the east. Due to its northern location, it has often been on the front of wars between the Scarlet Moon Empire and the City-States of Jowston. 

The important trading town of Kirov is located in Dana, along with the ruined village of Kalekka and the Toran Liberation Army's Secret Factory. Even further north lies the Banner Mines as well as the hidden ninja compound of Rokkaku Hamlet.

Due to the winds pushed down from the Banner Mountains, Dana tends to have a harsh climate.

name: Deep Forest
description: The Deep Forest (深き森, Fukaki Mori), also referred to as the Nearby Forest (近くの森, Chiaku no Mori), is a location in Suikoden II and Genso Suikoden Card Stories. It is a dense forest located in northern Dunan.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This deep forest referred specifically to the vast forest area north of Greenhill City and which surrounded the Forest Village in Greenhill Principality.

This forest was also Ayda's home, where she served as a Guardian of the Forest.

name: Deep Twilight Forest
description: The Deep Twilight Forest (深き薄明の森, Fukaki Hakumei no Mori) is a location in Suikoden V. It is a dense forest located in southern Falena.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Deep Twilight Forest is a forest whose trees grow so thick and lush that they block out sunlight and form a natural maze, making it difficult to traverse.

Deep in the forest, there are ruins belonging to the Sindar. These ruins are emblazoned with a rendering of the Twilight Rune and seem to open in response to its counterpart, the Dawn Rune. Bringing the Twilight Rune to the ruins activates a projection of the seer, Leknaat.

name: Deep Twilight Forest Ruins
description: The Deep Twilight Forest (深き薄明の森の遺跡, Fukaki Hakumei no Mori no Iseki) is a location in Suikoden V. These Sindar ruins are located deep within the Deep Twilight Forest.
reading: Fukaki Hakumei no Mori no Iseki

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Located deep within the Deep Twilight Forest, there are ruins belonging to the Sindar. These ruins were emblazoned with a rendering of the Twilight Rune and seemed to open in response to its counterpart, the Dawn Rune. 

Bringing the Twilight Rune to the ruins activated a vision of the rune's past, as well as a meeting with seer, Leknaat.

name: Deserted Island
description: The Deserted Island (無人島, Mujintō), also known as Hero Island (主人公島, Shujinkō Shima), with 'Hero' being the name given to the protagonist, and Chiepoo Island (チープー島, Chīpū Shima) is a location that appears in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. It is a small island located in the central Island Nations.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This deserted island was located in the center of the Island Nations, far from any inhabited islands. As such, its was untouched and fully covered by nature. Its beautiful sandy beaches and dense jungles, as well as an underground lake, also meant the island was inhabitable. 

This would prove vital when the hero, his friends, and Chiepoo would end up shipwrecked on this island for a time, following their exile from Razril. The underground lake, found at the back of the cave extending from the beach, was where the hero would meet Lilin.

Following the Island Liberation War, the mermaid siblings would return to the Deserted Island. Chiepoo would also journey to the island, using it as the base of operations for Chiepoo & Co., his business concern. The island would become increasingly named after the hero, due to the events of the Island Liberation War, whereas Chiepoo would attempt to popularize the name "Chiepoo Island" to limited success.

name: Donut Island
description: Donut Island (ドーナッツ島, Dōnattsu Shima) is a location that appears in Suikoden IV. It is a small island located in the south-eastern Island Nations.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This deserted island is located within an archipelago of small islands located east of the Kingdom of Obel. It is called Donut Island because of its unique appearance, being formed as a ring of rocky terrain and coral reefs.

There were rumors of hidden treasure being buried on this island, resulting in many treasure hunters visiting in search of wealth. During the Island Liberation War, the treasure hunter Rene was found divining the location of such treasures on this island.

name: Doraat
description: Doraat Fortress (ドラート要塞, Dorāto Jōsai) is a location seen in Suikoden V. It is a huge fortress town located in the Queendom of Falena.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Doraat is a castle town surrounded by a truly imposing wall, gaining it a reputation as an impregnable fortress town. Located in western Falena on the banks of the Feitas River, Doraat is also the second largest Godwin stronghold after Stormfist itself.

Throughout the Sun Rune War, it would prove be a particularly difficult target for the Imperial City Recapture Army to take but the cruel treatment of its citizens by Childerich, despite Doraat's high levels of loyalty to the Godwin regime, would eventually fuel a rebellion that saw the city decisively switch sides during the conflict.

name: Dragon Horse Lair
description: The Dragon Horse Lair (竜馬の巣, Ryōma no Su) is a location seen in Suikoden V. It is a cave used for the hatching and raising of dragon horses for the Feitas Dragon Cavalry.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Dragon Horse Lair was a cave located near Sauronix Castle where dragon horses laid their eggs. Young dragon horses and their eggs were also raised there.

This cave was once occupied by the Godwin Faction during the Sun Rune War, forcing the Feitas Dragon Cavalry into neutrality to protect the eggs there. Georg Prime was able to help see off the Godwin forces, freeing the Dragon Cavalry and allowing them to enter the war on the site of the Imperial City Recapture Army.

name: Dragon Knight's Domain
description: The Dragon Knight's Domain (竜洞騎士団, Ryū Hora Kishi-dan) is a region in Suikoden. It was an autonomous area with a close relationship with the Scarlet Moon Empire.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Dragon Knight's Domain lies to the north of Lorimar and is the home of the formidable Knights of the Dragon's Den. Although granted autonomy, it was considered part of the lands of the Scarlet Moon Empire.

To the east lies the region of Kunan. The region of Dana is located north of the Dragon Knight's Domain.

Due to being located in a basin surrounded by the peaks of the Lorimar Mountains, the temperature is extreme and varies between heat and cold.

name: Dragon Knights' Fortress
description: The Dragon Knight's Fortress (竜騎士の砦, Ryūkishi no Toride), also known as Goya Fortress (ゴーヤ砦, Gōya Toride), is a location in Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The Dragon Knight's Fortress is the home of the Knights of the Dragon's Den and holds between 50 and 60 Dragon Knights, though the order as a whole claims over 3000 knights. It is governed by the leader of the Knights of the Dragon's Den, Joshua Levenheit, the immortal bearer of the Dragon Rune who has power over dragons.

name: Dragon's Den
description: The Dragon's Den (竜洞 Ryūdō), sometimes referred to as the Dragon's Cave, is a location that appears in Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Dragon's Den is a cave linking the region of Kunan and the lands of the Knights of the Dragon's Den. As well as being the entryway to the domain of the Dragon Knights, it is where many dragons themselves reside. It is revered as a sacred place as the only place in the world where dragons are born.

There is a secret passage allowing one entry to the den, hidden behind a rock by the entrance. It was this entrance that was used by the Toran Liberation Army during the Gate Rune War as the main entrance was fiercely guarded during the illness that had stricken the dragons there.

name: Drakemouth Village
description: Drakemouth Village (竜口の村, Ryūko no Mura) is a location in Suikoden II. It is a small village with a population of 1,200.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Drakemouth is a small village that lies in the eastern part of Tinto Principality, at the foot of Lampdragon Mountain. On the eastern side of the pass lies Tigermouth Village. Going west will take you to Tinto City.

Sierra Mikain stayed in Drakemouth Village while journeying to confront Neclord during the Dunan Unification War. A small village, it had no real industry to speak of but held a good relationship with the Lampdragon Bandits. One of its guards, Kokto, was a friend of Koyu for example.

name: Duck Village
description: The Duck Village (ダックの村, Dakku no Mura) is a location that appears in Suikoden III. It is a settlement located in the central Grasslands.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Duck Village is the home of the Duck Clan, one of the Six Clans of the Grasslands. The houses of the village are build on piers and jetties stretching out over a lake, forming the village.

It is a peaceful village which takes advantage of its location, exploiting the advantages of the lake by harboring many fishing boats and hosting a large water wheel.

name: Dunan River
description: The Dunan River (デュナン川, Deyunan Kawa) is a river in Suikoden and Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The Dunan River is a river which begins in the Toran region. From south of the Fortress of Garan, it flows into Lake Toran. The river then continues north of the lake, passing by Kirov. It eventually flows past Banner Village and Radat Town, emptying into Lake Dunan in the north.

name: Dwarf Camp
description: The Dwarf Camp (ドワーフキャンプ, Dowāfukyanpu) is a location seen in Suikoden V. It is a cave dwarf settlement underneath the Queendom of Falena.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Dwarf Camp is a cave dwarf settlement in the Queendom of Falena that can be accessed through the Baska Mine in the country's south. The underground passages that the dwarves have dug from this camp spread throughout the entire southern continent with one tunnel in particular opening out into Agate Prison.

Some of these underground passages boast of trucks, installed by Wabon, which greatly reduce the time spent wandering through these tunnels.

name: Dwarf Mines
description: The Dwarf Mines (ドワーフ鉱山, Dowāfu Kōzan) is a location mentioned in Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Sanchez mentioned that the Toran Liberation Army paid a lot of money in order to obtain the blueprints for the Fire Spear from the Dwarf Mines.

As the Chief of the Dwarves welcomes the Toran Liberation Army to the Dwarf Mines upon meeting in the Village of the Dwarves, it is possible that the two places are one and the same.

name: Dwarf Trail
description: The Dwarf Trail (ドワーフの山道, Dowāfu no Yamamichi, Dwarf Mountain Trail) is a mountain trail that connects the elven and dwarven communities of the Great Forest region.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: As the path from the Great Forest region to the Village of the Dwarves, the Dwarf Pass serves as an important pass for anyone who wishes to travel between the two regions. The side nearest to the Village of the Dwarves bears the mark of the dwarven citizens who live around it, as pieces of advanced technology can be glimpsed around the path.

name: Dwarves' Vault
description: The Dwarves' Vault (ドワーフのきんこ Dowafu no Kinko), also called the Great Vault (大金庫, Dai Kinko), is a giant safe located north of the Village of the Dwarves. It contains many dwarven treasures and blueprints.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Dwarves' vault contains many of the dwarves greatest treasures and technology including the Flowing Water Cane, Burning Mirror and Firewind Cannon. It is located to the north of the Village of the Dwarves because it was too large to build within the village.

The vault was infiltrated by Kage who stole the plans for the Burning Mirror, delivering them to Kwanda Rosman. Despite this, the Chief of the Dwarves did not believe the vault could be infiltrated by "sluggish" humans and challenged McDohl and his entourage to steal the Flowing Water Cane from within to verify their story.

When the party succeeded, the Chief of the Dwarves grudgingly built the Firewind Cannon to combat Kwanda's mirror. Following the Gate Rune War, Roc was hired to design a new safe for the dwarves, suggesting that the numerous infiltrations during the war brought doubt on the vault's security.

Along with the above treasures, it is also rumored that the vault holds a weapon to be used only at the end of the world, secure in a part of the vault called "the center of the center of the vault". It is unknown if this is true or a result of the tendency of dwarves to exaggerate, however.

name: East Palace
description: The East Palace (東の離宮, Azuma no Rikyū) is a location seen in Suikoden V. It is a royal residence in the Queendom of Falena and hosts the Dawn Rune, one of the nation's three runes.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The East Palace was a small palace located to the east of the Falenan capital of Sol-Falena. The Dawn Rune was enshrined here, juxtaposing itself with the West Palace where the Twilight Rune was located.

However, the Dawn Rune was lost during the SY 447 Lordlake Uprising, with the people of Lordlake being blamed and ostracized for the incident. In truth, Salum Barows took advantage of the riot to steal the Dawn Rune, storing it in his Rainwall mansion.

When Lymsleia Falenas stopped at the East Palace prior to the Sacred Games, Zerase would appear demanding to know of the Dawn Rune's location. The Dawn Rune was returned to the East Palace following the Sun Rune War.

name: El-Eal Fortress
description: El-Eal Fortress (エルイール要塞, Eruīru Yōsai) is a location that appears in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. It is a large stronghold located on the southernmost tip of the Kooluk Empire.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
El-Eal Fortress was an enormous fortification on the southern tip of the Kooluk Empire, comprised of five levels; four floors and a single basement. As the fortification was built into the cliff side, it was extremely hard to attack or invade by sea. In addition, the Magnus Rune Cannon was also constructed there, making it a true symbol of the military power of the Kooluk Empire.

It was the site of the final battle of the Island Liberation War. After being successfully infiltrated, the fortress collapsed following the defeat of the Giant Tree, leaving only the foundations left.

The fortress was apparently at the beginning of reconstruction by the Empire just prior to the final collapse of the Kooluk Empire.

name: Elven Settlement
description: The Elven Settlement (エルフの集落, Erufu no Shūraku) is a location that appears in Suikoden IV. It is an elven settlement located on Na-Nal Island.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This elven settlement was located on Na-Nal Island, to the north of the small town where the human inhabitants of the island lived. The Great Elven Tree there towered over the settlement and formed something of a residential area for its inhabitants.

As the elven and human populations on the island did not have a friendly relationship, exchanges between both races were few and far between.

name: Erud Forest
description: Erud Forest (エルドの森, Erudo no Mori) is a location in Suikoden II. It is a forest located in northern Greenhill Principality.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: Erud Forest is a stretch of woodland that lies between Rockaxe and Greenhill City. The Road to Matilda is located here and the entrance is guarded by the Matilda Knights.

name: Estrise
description: Estrise (エストライズ, Esutoraizu) is a location seen in Suikoden V. It is a large port town in the eastern reaches of the Queendom of Falena.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Estrise is a large port town located on the Straits of Scardi in Falena's east. It is well known for its windmills, built by the inventor Babbage, as well as for being the town from which Falena's few ocean-sailing ships, such as the Elmelark, would depart to Nirva Island.

During the Sun Rune War, it was administered by Lord Boz Wilde, who supported the Barows Faction. Later during the war, it would invaded by the Armes Western Marine Corps under Shula Valya but the town would not be affected greatly by this with Shula Valya treating the occupation as more of a vacation before withdrawing.

name: Feitas River
description: The Feitas River (フェイタス河 Feitasu Kawa) is a river in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Feitas is a freshwater river which flows throughout the Queendom of Falena. More accurately, the Feitas is a series of interconnecting rivers and tributaries, flowing south from their sources in the northern Ashtwal Mountains, weaving throughout the country as it does so, merging and diverging before either flowing into the ocean or continuing southwards out of Falenan lands.

Sol-Falena, the Imperial Capital of Falena, is built on a sandbank of the Feitas. The wide reach of the Feitas means that every part of the country is accessible by its waters, save for the mountainous region of Sable.

name: Flame Champion's Hideaway
description: The Flame Champion's Hideaway (炎の英雄の待つ地, Honō no Eiyū no Matsu Ji), also known as Serenia (炎の英雄の眠る場所, Honō no Eiyū no Nemuru Basho) is a location in Suikoden III. It is a cave located north of the Chisha Village.
reading: Honō no Eiyū no Matsu Ji

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A secluded cave located to the north of the Chisha Village, the Flame Champion's Hideaway is surrounded by steep rocks. In the back of the caves lie Sindar ruins, accessible through magical wards laid in place by the Flame Champion. Only those who the Flame Champion admits can use this magic to travel to the deepest areas of the ruins.

In the deepest reaches of the ruins, there is the place that Sana and the Flame Champion had lived together in, prior to his death. It is in this location that the Flame Champion had sealed his True Fire Rune.

name: Floating Fortress of Shasarazade
description: The Floating Fortress of Shasarazade (水上砦シャサラザード, Suijiyo Toride Shasarazādo, Water Fortress Shasarazade) is a fortress that appears in Suikoden. It was a vital defensive citadel for the Scarlet Moon Empire's capital city Gregminster.
originalName: 水上砦シャサラザード

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Floating Fortress of Shasarazade, despite its name, is not literally a floating fortress. The fortress itself lies on land on the western banks of the Tolna Canal with fortifications extending across the canal. As such, it is a key defensive structure for the capital city of Gregminster along with the Fortress of Kwaba.

Shasarazade also served as the base of the Imperial Navy and its commander Sonya Shulen. Although the fortress was considered extremely difficult to attack, Shasarazade has been approached by pirates several times; most notably when the pirate leader Anji pinned a wedding dress to its walls, taunting Sonya Shulen's unwed status.

In the latter stages of the Gate Rune War Shasarazade was chosen as the Toran Liberation Army's entrance to Gregminster precisely because it was believed to be unassailable by the liberation forces. Although the battle ended in victory for the rebel forces, Sanchez, a spy who had hidden in the army thus far without detection, stabbed Mathiu Silverberg, the head strategist of the Liberation Army, and set fire to the fortress prematurely, endangering many lives.

name: Floating Garden
description: The Floating Garden (空中庭園 Kūchūteien, Hanging Garden) is a private garden located atop Gregminster Palace. It is also the location of the final battle in Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Floating Garden at Gregminster was adorned with many beautiful flowers and other plants. Emperor Barbarossa himself sometimes strolled in his garden on sleepless nights. It was one of the favorite places of his late wife, Claudia, and Barbarossa would spend time here to ease his loneliness.

The Floating Garden is also the only known location for the rare Black Dragon Orchid. By the end of the Gate Rune War, Barbarossa considered his garden to be all that remained of his empire. Following his defeat, Barbarossa leaped from atop the garden with Windy in his arms.

While Gregminster Palace was restored following the Gate Rune War, the Floating Garden was not.

name: Fog Ship
description: The Fog Ship (霧の船, Kiri no Fune) is a location which appear in Suikoden IV. It is an ethereal vessel drifting through the Island Nations at the time of the game.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Fog Ship is an insubstantial, immaterial vessel that was created by the Fog Ship Guide. Its interior is abnormally large when compared to its outside form. Neither the navigation system nor its means of propulsion organization are known, but as the ship has no sails it is a common theory that the vessel is powered by some form of magic.

After Ted chose to leave the Fog Ship and its guide was banished, the ship quickly dissipated.

name: Forest Village
description: Forest Village (森の村, Mori no Mura) is a location in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Forest Village is a small rural settlement located in the far northwest of Greenhill Principality. Deep forests surround and envelope the village, which is said to be home to flying squirrels.

Legends about mythical beasts, such as griffins exist in the village. During the Dunan Unification War, the leader of the New Alliance Army encountered the griffin Feather here.

name: Forest of Illusion
description: The Forest of Illusion (迷いの森, Mayoi no Mori) is a location in Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Forest of Illusion is part of the Great Forest, located in the region of Gouran. When the Great Contract was signed many years ago, the Six Wise Men of the Forest erected barrier magic in order to isolate the area. Because of this barrier, non-human races such as kobolds, dwarves and elves were not well known to the public.

Located at the entrance to the Great Forest, the Forest of Illusion's barrier magic ensured that human wanderers would make no progress in the woods. It is a very thick forest where the sun wasn't visible, making it impossible to navigate by the sun or stars. The area flourished and animals thrived without the presence of human development.

The barrier magic could be circumvented with the aid of an elf and took roughly half a day to cross. However, Lukie Ende Towayo was the key to this magic and it was destroyed during the Gate Rune War.

Following the war, more and more humans have been entering the Great Forest, with the former Forest of Illusion now being no different to any ordinary forest.

name: Fortress of Garan
description: The Fortress of Garan (ガランの城塞, Garan no Jōsai) is a fortress that appears in Suikoden. It is a citadel located between the regions of Kunan and Gouran.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Fortress of Garan, although visually resembling a stone bridge, is an internal citadel dividing the regions of Kunan and Gouran. As the only connecting point between the two regions, its strategic value was said to have been very high.

The fortress had a small settlement attached to it. Apple lived in the Garan town for a while with distant relatives before being taken in by Mathiu Silverberg. The winds of Lake Toran meant that little plants could grow in the area so fish was the main form of sustenance.

During the early stages of the Gate Rune War the fortress was closed, with travelers forbidden from passing through. Following the defeat of Kwanda Rosman and the addition of Flik's forces to the Toran Liberation Army, Garan was attacked by the Liberation Army and quickly fell.

Following the disastrous First Battle at Scarleticia Castle, the Liberation Army regrouped at Garan before deciding on a reconnaissance mission in Kunan.

name: Fortress of Kwaba
description: The Fortress of Kwaba (クワバの城塞, Kuwaba no Jōsai) is a fortress that appears in Suikoden. It functions as a defensive citadel for the Imperial capital of Gregminster as well as a border checkpoint between the regions of Arus and Gouran.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Fortress of Kwaba adds to the defensive line surrounding the capital, Gregminster and was considered vital to that purpose. The fortress was also the site of decisive battle of the War of Succession and separates the regions of Arus and Gouran. It also function as a checkpoint for travelers between the two regions.

The fortress was administered by General Ain Gide during the Gate Rune War, who had let the hero pass through during his escape from Gregminster as a favour to his father, Teo McDohl. The fortress was abandoned after the fall of Shasarazade, with a path already cleared to Gregminster, their forces falling back to protect the capital.

name: Fortress of Lorimar
description: The Fortress of Lorimar (ロリマーの城塞, Rorimā no Jōsai) is a citadel which separates the regions of Lorimar and Kunan.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
As the fortress checkpoint between the regions of Kunan and Lorimar, the Fortress of Lorimar became the target of the Toran Liberation Army once it was noted as being poorly protected by Liberation Army intelligence. On arrival at the fortress, it was revealed that it was, in fact, entirely empty with only several dug-up graves suggesting anything unusual.

It would transpire that Neclord, who had been appointed magistrate of the Lorimar region had killed his men before transforming them into zombies and skeletons. The executioner Kirke could be found at the fortress, muttering about being put out of a job by Neclord, until his recruitment by the Liberation Army. 

Before its evisceration by Neclord, the fortress was known for its robustness, making it a strong checkpoint between the two regions.

name: Founder's Site
description: The Founder's Site (始祖の地, Shiso no Ji) is a location seen in Suikoden V. It is a glacial area within the Ashtwal Mountains of far-northern Falena.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Founder's Site is the name given to the large glacial area in the Ashtwal Mountains that spreads out once one exits the secret passage hidden behind the fountain's sliding door in Lunas. To the rear of this area are ruins belonging to the Sindar civilization.

The final battle between the Prince and Marscal Godwin, who had become enthralled by the Sun Rune, took place in these ruins.

name: Gordius
description: Gordius (ゴルディアス, Gorudiasu) is a location seen in Suikoden V. It is a small village in the southwestern reaches of the Queendom of Falena.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Gordius is a village located in the southwest of the Queendom of Falena that exists as a training ground for apprentices in the Feitas Dragon Cavalry. There is a dragon horse nest to the rear of the village, where Dragon Cavalry apprentices meet and bond with their future mounts.

As woman are forbidden from becoming members of the Dragon Cavalry, it is also forbidden for females to enter Gordius itself.

name: Gouran
description: Gouran (ゴウラン Gouran) is a region of the Toran Republic, and its predecessor, the Scarlet Moon Empire. It is located to the south of the region of Arus and comprised most of the nation's eastern lands.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Due to the winds blowing south from Lake Toran, Gouran has a slightly harsher climate than other areas in the Toran region. Being mostly bordered by the lake shore, the region has a great many fishing villages and small port towns.

As such, people of the region tended to value bravado and candidness. Gouran was generally considered a poor region and its people wore simple clothing made of pieces of cloth tied together by string.

Udon, a type of thick noodle made from wheat flour, was a specialty of the Gouran region not normally found elsewhere.

name: Great Elven Tree
description: The Great Elven Tree (エルフの大樹, Erufu no Taiju) is a location that appears in Suikoden IV. It is an elven settlement located on Na-Nal Island.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This great tree had grown to become the living area of the elves living in the Elven Settlement in the northern part of Na-Nal Island.

The dewdrops within the tree were collected and stores as the rare and valuable Elven Remedy. This remedy was stolen by the hero at the behest of the Na-Nal Island Chief but it turned out that the remedy had been replaced with a poison before hand. This would result in the poisoning of several Kooluk soldiers, leading to their furious retaliation and attack on the island's human inhabitants.

name: Great Forest
description: The Great Forest (大森林, Dai Shinrin) is a region in Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Great Forest covers much of the eastern portion of the Gouran region. Entering the forest brings you to the Forest of Illusion due to the barrier magic of the Six Wise Men of the Forest who lived there in ancient times. Because of this barrier, which has existed since around SY 80, non-human races, such as dwarves and elves, are not well known in the area.

Once the Scarlet Moon Empire rose to power in the Toran area, the Empire inherited the Great Contract. So as to avoid breaking its terms, the government never revealed the existence of the Great Forest to other regions of the Empire.

However, Lukie Ende Towayo was the key to this magic and it was destroyed during the Gate Rune War. Following the war, more and more humans have been entering the Great Forest.

name: Great Forest Village
description: Great Forest Village (大森林の村, Dai Shinrin no Mura) is a village that appears in Suikoden. The Great Forest Village is a small village located on the western fringes of the Great Forest.

facts.label: Nation
facts.value: Scarlet Moon Empire (until 457), Toran Republic (457 onwards)

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Great Forest Village sits almost right on the border between southern Gouran and the Great Forest. It lies roughly three days away from Kaku.

The population living here were relatively unaware of their non-human neighbors due to the Forest of Illusion, which was considered maze-like and confusing due to its magical protections and the supposed sighting of an elf or kobold were often treated with disbelief.

Following the Gate Rune War, more and more people from the village became aware of their previously hidden neighbors. The village also began to shed off its poor reputation, caused by its mystic forest, and started attracting more visitors.

name: Great Hollow
description: The Great Hollow (大空洞, Dai Kūdō) is a location that appears in Suikoden III. It is a settlement located in the central Grasslands.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Great Hollow is an enormous cavern that serves as the home of the Lizard Clan, one of the Grasslands's Six Clans. Glowing minerals are embedded in the rocks forming the cavern and this, along with the flickering blue flames present there, gives the location an air of mystery.

The Great Hollow is known for its massive size, housing the Karaya Clan following the destruction of their village. The Fire Bringer also temporarily take refuge there after being defeated by the Harmonian Army near the Duck Village.

Additional caves and tunnels spread out from the Great Hollow in a circular formation. Item shops and inns are also available here.

name: Great Lighthouse
description: The Great Lighthouse (大灯台, Dai Tōdai) is a location in Suikoden V. It is a towering lighthouse that helps guide ships around Nirva Island.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Great Lighthouse was Nirva Island's most famous landmark. It was built along with the town on Nirva to facilitate trade and travel between the Island Nations Federation and the Queendom of Falena.

Without the lighthouse, it was said many ships would run aground in the region.

This lighthouse would be taken over by pirates, fearful that the arrival of the Lino En Kuldes foreshadowed a crackdown on their activities in the area.

The pirates threatened to burn down the lighthouse with its own oil stores if the Island Nations Federation forces did not withdraw but were thwarted by the combined efforts of the Prince and Bernadette Egan.

name: Greenhill City
description: Greenhill City (グリンヒル市, Gurinhiru Shi) is a location in Suikoden II, Genso Suikogaiden Vol.1, and Genso Suikoden Card Stories. It is the lead city of Greenhill Principality.

facts.label: Nation
facts.value: Dunan Monarchy (until 185), City-States of Jowston (314-373, 378-460), Dunan Republic (460 onwards)

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Greenhill City is an academic city that has evolved around the New Leaf Academy, which allowed it to become the largest center of studying within the City-States of Jowston. The city is divided into a school district and residential areas. Many stores line the streets and students can be often found here, in their uniforms, for a change of scenery. Tempura and pizza are two of the specialties found at the inn here.

In the past, it was a part of Muse Principality but became an independent city in 373 with the blessing of Muse mayor Uriah. The Wisemail family has traditionally served as mayors of the city. Prior to the Dunan Unification War, poor harvests and lack of industry beyond its educational facilities led to Greenhill suffering a deficit of contributions, and thus influence, within the City-States of Jowston.

Following the Dunan Unification War, while Teresa Wisemail served as mayor of the city before becoming President of the Dunan Republic, it was Emilia who serves as headmaster of the academy there.

name: Greenhill Forest
description: Greenhill Forest (グリンヒルの森, Gurinhiru no Mori) is a location in Suikoden II. It is a forest that surrounds Greenhill City.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Greenhill Forest is a forested area that spreads out from behind the New Leaf Academy in Greenhill City. New Leaf Academy contains a secret passage on the ground floor that exits into this forest.

During the Highland occupation of Greenhill, Teresa Wisemail hid in a cabin built deep inside this forest. Later during the Dunan Unification War, the forest was used to sent a small group inside Greenhill City as part of its liberation.

name: Gregminster Palace
description: Gregminster Palace (グレッグミンスターの城 Guregguminsutā no Shiro) is a location in Suikoden. It is the home of the emperor of the Scarlet Moon Empire.
originalName: グレッグミンスターの城

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Gregminster Palace towers over the city of Gregminster. As the palace of the emperor of the Scarlet Moon Empire, it existed as a symbol of Imperial authority.

Outside the palace itself is a barn while inside is a selection of offices, including that of the Imperial Guard. The Imperial throne room lies on the first floor. Located on top of the palace is the Floating Garden which contains many rare plants, accessbile from a secret passage in the throne room.

There was where the final battle of the Gate Rune War took place. Following the defeat of Barbarossa Rugner the palace began to shake, forcing a rushed evacuation by the Toran Liberation Army officers within but the palace apparently remained intact.

By the time of the Dunan Unification War, it served as the residence for Lepant, President of the Toran Republic.

name: Hatred Fortress
description: Hatred Fortress (ヘイトリッド城塞, Heitoriddo Jōsai) is a location seen in Suikoden V. It is a large dam built in the Queendom of Falena.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Hatred Fortress is a citadel and dam constructed on the branch of the Feitas River that runs through Lordlake, designed to stop the flow of water to the town.

Its construction was ordered by Marscal Godwin in SY 447, following the Lordlake Uprising in order to further punish the town. The dam itself was built with forced labor by the beavers of Beaver Lodge. Hatred Fortress was what truly stopped Lordlake's recovery in its tracks and left the area parched and desolated.

In the early stages of the Sun Rune War, the dam would be destroyed thanks to the efforts of the Imperial City Recapture Army. The attack was conducted in three steps. First, the Rebel Army conducted an attack on the Fortress. Second, a small ship loaded with burning oil was sent crashing into the Fortress, setting it on fire. As the garrison consisted mostly of freshly recruited, unexperienced soldiers, the Fortress has been consequently evacuated.

These steps, proposed by Lucretia Merces, were needed to reduce the casualties in the process to a minimum. Last, a huge wave destroyed the fortress, ultimately restoring the flow of water to Lordlake and allowing it to at last begin its recovery. Following the destruction of the Hatred Fortress, Lordlake joined the Imperial City Recapture Army.

name: Haud Village
description: Haud Village (ハウド村, Haudo-mura) is a location seen in Suikoden V. It is a artistic community located in northeastern Falena.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: Haud Village is a settlement of would-be artists and other intellectuals located in northeastern Falena. The village is supported by Salum Barows and the resulting works and buildings that litter the streets, designed to cater to his eclectic tastes create a very ostentatious aesthetic for visitors.

name: Hermitage Island
description: Hermitage Island (庵の小島, An no Kojima) is a location that appears in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. It is a small island located in the southern Island Nations.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This small island is located to the north-west of the Nest of Pirates and is notable for being covered by forest. The only structure on the island is a small hermitage where Elenor Silverberg, former strategist of the Scarlet Moon Empire, once resided.

After the Island Liberation War, Maxine lived here for a short time until she joined Kyril's group as requested by Ramada.

name: Hershville Naval Base
description: Hershville Naval Base (ハーシュビル軍港, Hāshubiru Gunkō) is a location seen in Suikoden V. It is a busy port town located on the western coast of the Queendom of Falena.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Hershville is a thriving naval port located on the western coast of the Queendom of Falena and was administered by the Godwin Faction, and a century before that, the House of Aethelbald. It also served as the port where the royal navy, commanded by Bahram Luger was stationed.

As a heavily busy port, while under the administration of the Godwins, trading in the town was a hotbed of corruption and smuggling was also not uncommon, even prior to the Sun Rune War.

name: Highway Village
description: Highway Village (街道の村, Kaidō no Mura) is a location in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Highway Village is a small hamlet located to the southeast of Rockaxe Castle. As its name suggests, the village is a series of stores and private homes that line the main road that passes through the area. Though small, it contains most basic amenities.

This highway, which is a trade route with the commercial center of Zexen, also imports goods from Grasslands, providing the village with its main industry. Traders traveling the Muse-Grasslands trade route often stop at this village.

name: Iksay Village
description: Iksay Village (イクセの村, Ikuse no Mura) is a location that appears in Suikoden III. It is a small farming village located north of Vinay del Zexay.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This idyllic village lies to the north of Vinay del Zexay and is known for its rolling hills and large windmills. The stunning wheat fields that spread out from the village help keep the village granaries rich and full. 

The village attracts a lot of people for its annual fertility festival designed to give thanks for the harvest to the Goddess Sadie.

name: Iluya Island
description: Iluya Island (イルヤ島, Iruya Shima) is a location that appears in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. It is a island located in the northern Island Nations.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Iluya Island was one of the closest locations in the Island Nations to the Kooluk Empire and so was chosen as the first target in the Empire's Southern Expansion Policy. Using the Magnus Rune Cannon constructed at El-Eal Fortress, the beautiful town, which spread out from a round plaza in its center, was turned to ashes in an instant.

Most of the island's inhabitants were killed but some fortunate few drifted to other nearby islands like Nay Island. Following the island's destruction, the Kooluk would establish a garrison on the scorched land.

Following the Island Liberation War, the Island Nations Federation began reconstruction efforts and the island began to reclaim its former vigor piece by piece.

name: Jowston Hill
description: Jowston Hill (ジョウストンの丘, Jousuton no Oka) is a location in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Jowston Hill, located in the west of Muse City, was named after the famed general and former mayor of Muse, Jowston who had held off the invading Highland Army from the hill for two months.

The Jowston Alliance was signed under the large tree on the side of the hill, founding the City-States of Jowston, named ostensibly after the hill and not the Muse mayor. Jowston Hill was commonly regarded as the center of the alliance.

Members of the Alliance would continue to meet at Jowston Hill for the Hilltop Conference and other ceremonies until its dissolution in the Dunan Unification War.

name: Kaku
description: Kaku (カク, Kaku) is a village that appears in Suikoden. Kaku is a small village located on the east bank of Lake Toran.

facts.label: Nation
facts.value: Scarlet Moon Empire (until 457), Toran Republic (457 onwards)

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Kaku is known as the town where fishermen gather and has a noted reputation for its sordid characters. The fishermen who reside here are said to be very tanned due to their outdoors work. The town also produced a lot of flour.

Toran Castle is visible from the village and before becoming the base for the Toran Liberation Army there were a host of rumors about the castle being haunted. It was possible to take boat rides out to the castle. 

The small town itself bustles with several businesses, including inns and blacksmiths. Many of the town's lively fishermen, and other notable figures such as Tai Ho and Gaspar spen their time rolling dice in the basement of one of the town's bars.

The claiming of the castle by the Toran Liberation Army opened up more fishing opportunities, with the fog around the castle dispersing.

name: Kalekka
description: Kalekka (カレッカ Karekka) is a town that appears in Suikoden. It was destroyed long before the events of the Gate Rune War.

facts.label: Nation
facts.value: Scarlet Moon Empire (until 457), Toran Republic (457 onwards)

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Kalekka lies north of Kirov, in the region of Dana. The town itself was razed during the Border Conflict between the Scarlet Moon Empire and the City-States of Jowston in SY 448.

The official story is that the armies of the City-State razed the town and massacred the population. However, the truth is that the Kalekka Incident was orchestrated by the Empire itself in order to bolster flagging support for its war with Jowston.

In the past, it was a farming community whose specialty was the export of cotton. Since the massacre, the town has lain mostly abandoned and in ruins, although several people still lived there at the time of the Gate Rune War.

Following the war, the Toran Republic began to encourage people to move to Kalekka in the hopes of rejuvenating the town but uptake has been slow due to the memories of the town's past being hard to shift.

name: Karaya Village
description: The Karaya Village (カラヤの村, Karaya no Mura) is a location that appears in Suikoden III. It is a settlement located in the center of the Grasslands.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This village is the home of the Karaya Clan, one of the Grasslands's Six Clans. It is located on the southeastern fringes of the Amur Plain. It is a simple collection of settlements, made up of tented buildings with thatched roofs.

The village was razed by the Zexen Knights early in the War of the Champions, as part of a Destroyers ploy, but the village would be rebuilt following the war by Hugo and other surviving Karayans.

name: Kingdom of Obel
description: The Kingdom of Obel (オベル王国, Oberu Ōkoku) is a nation that appears in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. It is the most prominent member of the Island Nations Federation.

facts.label: Nation
facts.value: Independent state (until 307), Island Nations Federation (307 onwards)

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Obel is located in the south-eastern reaches of the Island Nations and is the largest of the nations there. It is also the home of Island Nations Federation founding leader, Lino En Kuldes.

During the events of the Island Liberation War, it was ruled by King Lino whose policies made him a popular monarch. This nation's ruins once contained the Rune of Punishment, one of the 27 True Runes. The rune would be taken by the Queen of Obel and would later cut a path of misery through the region.

The nation was attacked and occupied by the Kooluk Empire early into the Island Liberation War but would later be liberated by the Stronghold Ship-led alliance and would go on to be one of the founding members of the Island Nations Federation. Due to its size and influence, it is the primary member of the Island Nations Federation and commands much respect within the region.

name: Kirov
description: Kirov (キーロフ, Kīrofu) is a town that appears in Suikoden. Kirov is a port city which thrives on trade. It is the only major city in Dana.

facts.label: Nation
facts.value: Scarlet Moon Empire (until 457), Toran Republic (457 onwards)

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Kirov was a port town located on the north bank of Lake Toran and was a major trading city, known for its fishing. Due to its location in southern Dana, it was a center for trade with the City-States of Jowston.

Over time, the town had become more rustic and was quite sleepy by the time of the Gate Rune War. The lax nature of the Scarlet Moon Empire's control meant a lot of smuggling normally took place in the city although during the war it was mentioned that Imperial control had increased.

Kirov was not considered a particularly picturesque town, attracting few tourists. Still, following the war, the chef Lester opened a specialty stew restaurant called "Lake Surface Sunset", sparking a boom of specialty shops in the town and drawing fans of fine food from far and wide. The most recommended dish is the "Marie's Daily Routine".

The biggest house in Kirov belongs to Kun To, known as the 'Boss' of Kirov by some. Located north of Kirov is the village of Kalekka.

name: Kobold Village
description: Kobold Village (コボルトの村, Koboruto no Mura) is a village that appears in Suikoden II. It is the home of the kobold community of the Dunan region.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Kobold Village is located in Two River Principality, to the south of Two River City. It is known as one of the larger kobold settlements, though others exist through the land. The village is known for producing several fine warriors and its main industry is agriculture.

Kobold traditional cuisine such as Kobold Pie and other dishes are served in the village. The forest behind the village is said to be inhabited by a legendary unicorn.

name: Kobold Village
description: Kobold Village (コボルトの村, Koboruto no Mura, Kobold Village) is a village that appears in Suikoden. It is the home of the kobold community of the Toran region.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This Kobold Village was located directly at the end of the western path through the Forest of Moran; the town is small, but the kobolds have managed to earn a living there by working as humble farmers. However, during times of strife, the kobolds do not hesitate to pick up their arms and go to war.

During the Gate Rune War, most of the kobolds of the village fell ill to an effect of General Kwanda Rosman's Conqueror Rune, leaving the village abandoned as they had become feral and wild before being cured by his defeat.

Kuromimi would go on to serve in the Toran Liberation Army during the war, before returning back to the village, where he became the mayor of the community, helping to restore its previously cheerful atmosphere.

name: Kobold Village Forest
description: The Kobold Village Forest (コボルト村の森, Koboruto-mura no mori) is a location in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The forest spread out behind the Kobold Village in Two River Principality. It was said to be inhabited by a legendary unicorn, and this is where the hero actually met Siegfried, the unicorn of legend. Hix would prove himself a true warrior when he protected Tengaar from Siegfried here during the Dunan Unification War.

Due to its association with unicorns, the forest was also known as the Unicorn Woods.

name: Kouan
description: Kouan (コウアン, Kouan) is a town that appears in Suikoden. Kouan is a small town which is nevertheless one of the more prominent towns in the Gouran region.

facts.label: Nation
facts.value: Scarlet Moon Empire (until 457), Toran Republic (457 onwards)

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Kouan is located just south of Lake Toran, in the Gouran region. It had no exceptional industries or specialities of note. A lot of the success of Kouan and, indeed, its very survival has been credited to Lepant. 

When Kraze Miles was appointed military commander of the town, it did not meet with the popular support of most residents. In fact, when Kraze was run out of Kouan and Lepant joined the Toran Liberation Army, most rejoiced in the circumstances.

Lepant's mansion is in Kouan and has remained Lepant's home even after he became President of the Toran Republic.

name: Kunan
description: Kunan (クナン Kunan) is a region of the Toran Republic, and its predecessor, the Scarlet Moon Empire. It is located to the west of Lake Toran.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Kunan is a large region, bordered to the east by Lake Toran, to the north by Sadhna Stream, to the south by the region of Lorimar and to the west by the Lorimar Mountains and the Dragon Knight's Domain. Specialties of the region often flowed from here to Gouran and other regions.

During the Gate Rune War the region came under the control of Milich Oppenheimer who forced his own aesthetic tastes on the region, renaming many of the towns, while cracking down on rebel forces. He also encouraged the gathering of skilled workers, such as artists, doctors, and merchants. After the liberation of the region by the Toran Liberation Army, normalcy returned to the area.

name: Kuput Forest
description: Kuput Forest (クプトの森, Kuputo no Mori) is a location in Suikoden III. It is a sprawling forest where the hidden Alma Kinan Village is located.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Kuput Forest is a dense forest which lies between the Duck Village and the Chisha Village. It is a very densely wooded forest; only a small amount of light makes it through the vast number of trees there, making the forest perpetually gloomy and dim.

There is a trail in the forest that leads to the Alma Kinan Village. However, the village is protected by a magic barrier so those who are not invited will instead be lost in fog until they leave the area.

name: Kuskus Town
description: Kuskus Town (クスクスの街, Kusukusu no Machi) is a location in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Nation
facts.value: Dunan Monarchy (until 252), City-States of Jowston (314-460), Dunan Republic (460 onwards)

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Kuskus is a fishing town on the banks of Lake Dunan, located to the north of South Window City. The main industry of the town is fishing and its streets are usually teeming with fishermen. Its dance shows at the local inn, as well as its skillful blacksmith are also famous. The north side of the town focuses on fishing while the south side is where businesses are concentrated.

It was occupied for a time by the Highland Army during the Dunan Unification War but would later be liberated by the New Alliance Army.

name: Kyaro Town
description: Kyaro Town (キャロの街, Kyaro no Machi) is a location that appears in Suikoden II. It is a town located in southern Highland and home to the Unicorn Brigade.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Kyaro Town lies in the southern reaches of the Highland Kingdom and was a former territory of the City-States of Jowston. It can be reached from Jowston via the North Swallow Pass.

It is located at high altitude, so the weather is generally cool even in the summer. This made Kyaro a popular destination for tourists, as well as Highland nobility and royalty, to spend a refreshing summer at. As such, the town was occassionally visited by Jillia Blight in her youth.

In addition, the town serves of something of a symbol for the Dunan Unification War itself as both Jowy Atreides and the leader of the New Alliance Army came from there.

name: L'Renouille
description: L'Renouille (ルルノイエ, Rurunoie) is a location that appears in Suikoden II. It is the old capital city of the Highland Kingdom.

facts.label: Nation
facts.value: Holy Kingdom of Harmonia (until 237), Highland Kingdom (237-460), Dunan Republic (460 onwards)

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
L'Renouille was the imperial capital of the Highland Kingdom since the nation's foundation in 237. The city serves as a symbol of the Blight family's rule. With a population of 25,000, it was the largest city in the Kingdom. The King of Highland dwelled in L'Renouille Palace, which was renovated in the year 365. With its renovation, L'Renouille was considered a truly majestic capital city. The palace collapsed at the end of the Dunan Unification War and after its absorption by the new Dunan Republic, the city was renamed Higheast.

The temperature around L'Renouille is somewhat lower than its surroundings due to its proximity to the polar climate.

name: L'Renouille Palace
description: L'Renouille Palace (ルルノイエ城, Rurunoie-jō) is a location that appears in Suikoden II. It is located in L'Renouille, the capital city of the former Highland Kingdom.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
L'Renouille Palace was the royal palace of the Highland Kingdom. It was a castle that combined the practical defensive nature of a fortress with the grandeur of a palace. The palace was renovated in the year 365, giving it the majestic look and ornate interior it is known for. 

The palace collapsed and was destroyed at the end of the Dunan Unification War, along with the Highland Kingdom it represented. Some say it collapsed due to the fighting between the New Alliance Army and the Silver Wolf. Others say it was due to the palace losing the blessing of the Beast Rune. It has since become a ruin.

name: Lake Toran
description: Lake Toran (トラン湖, Toran Mizūmi) is a location in Suikoden. It is a large lake that dominates the Toran region.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Lake Toran is a large lake that lies almost exactly in the center of the Toran region as if to symbolize the region as a whole. It is particularly famous for its high quality fishing areas, many fishing villages dot the shores of the lake.

A small island on the eastern side of the lake is dominated by Toran Castle, the home base of the Toran Liberation Army who toppled the Scarlet Moon Empire in the Gate Rune War.

name: Lakewest
description: Lakewest (レイクウェスト, Reikūesuto) is a location in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Lakewest is a small village located to the east of Two River City that lies on the western banks of Lake Dunan. The strong winds blowing from the lake make the land unsuitable for farming.

As such, its main industry is fishing and it is known for the quality of its dried fish in particular.

name: Lampdragon Mountain
description: Lampdragon Mountain (灯竜山, Hiryūyama) is a mountain that appears in Suikoden II. It separates the regions of Two River and Tinto. The mountain itself is 320 meters tall.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Although the mountain itself has few features, many people live in the valleys this mountain creates. Villages such as Drakemouth and Tigermouth lie at the base of this mountain.

A group of bandits led by the infamous Lampdragon Trio run their operations from this mountain with a number of small fortifications. Tinto Principality had tried to drive the bandit out repeatedly over the years, but the bandits knowledge of the mountain and narrow valleys meant they did not succeed.

The mountain would become overrun by Neclord's horde during the Dunan Unification War but returned to normal with Neclord's death.

name: Lana River
description: The Lana River (ラナ川 Rana Kawa) is a river in Suikoden II.

name: Le Buque
