description: Le Buque (ルビーク, Rubīku) is a location that appears in Suikoden III. It is a village known for its Mantors.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Le Buque is a village which lies on the border between the Grasslands and the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia. The settlement was originally the village of the Carna Clan but when the town was annexed by Harmonia in its initial invasion of the region, it was renamed Le Buque.

The town is built into the side of a steep, vertical cliff and buildings are connected to each other by bridges made of rope. The village, and its people, are known for being Mantor trainers and so there is a Mantor nest located on the top of every house where these creatures are carefully raised.

name: Lelcar
description: Lelcar (レルカー, Rerukā) is a location seen in Suikoden V. It is a city in the southern part of the Queendom of Falena.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Located in southern Falena, Lelcar is a town built across three sandbanks, each connected by bridge. Although one city, the atmosphere of each islet varies greatly.

The town was formerly administered by Lord De Beers but following their retreat from the city, the three sandbanks had become virtually autonomous. Orok ran the Western Islet, Wasil ran the Central Islet, and Volga ran the Eastern Islet. Each leader supported different factions in the Sun Rune War, leading to much acrimony.

Following the razing of Lelcar by the Godwin Faction, the three islets would unite in response, backing the Prince of Falena in the Sun Rune War.

name: Lenankamp
description: Lenankamp (レナンカンプ, Renankanpu) is a town that appears in Suikoden and the upcoming Suikoden STAR LEAP. It is a medium sized town located to the south of the Imperial capital of Gregminster.

facts.label: Nation
facts.value: Scarlet Moon Empire (until 457), Toran Republic (457 onwards)

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Lenankamp is the second-largest town in the Arus region, smaller only that Gregminster. Located near the Tolna Canal and roughly seven days from Gregminster on foot, it was well served by aqueducts, making it a busy town.

The town has carefully placed stone pavements and stone buildings. It is well known for its excellent blacksmiths and armor makers and is especially famous for its circlets, which are frequently sold as souvenirs. 

In addition, Lenankamp is quite famous for its excellent alcohol and food. The main street of Lenankamp is always busy and crowded with people with many travelers stopping there on the way to Gregminster.

The Keyaki Inn in the town comes highly recommended. The inn also hid an entry to the original hideout of the Toran Liberation Army which caused a lot of commotion in the town when it was uncovered early in the Gate Rune War.

name: Lepant's Mansion
description: Lepant's Mansion (レパント邸, Repanto Tei) is a location in Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This mansion, located in Kouan, is where Lepant, president of the Toran Republic, once resided. It was known as one of the most luxurious mansions in Kouan. Within the mansion various tricks, installed by Juppo, who was a guest, are used to protect the mansion and its treasure, the great sword Kirinji.

The mansion was infiltrated by the Toran Liberation Army during the Gate Rune War.

name: Lime Shelf Island
description: Lime Shelf Island (石灰棚の島, Sekkaidana no Shima) is a location that appears in Suikoden IV. It is a small island located in the south-western Island Nations.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This deserted island is covered with limestone for the most part. It is located in the south-west of the Island Nations, isolated from other islands. The limestone that covers most of the island has been eroded by wind and rain over the years, creating a complex, maze-like terrain.

Stories tell that someone hid a lot of treasure on this island resulting in many treasure hunters visiting in search of wealth.

name: Liukan's Hermitage
description: Liukan's Hermitage (リュウカンの庵, Ryuukan no Iori) is a location that appears in Suikoden. Located in the region of Lorimar, the hermitage is simply a single hut sheltering the great doctor Liukan.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Liukan's Hermitage is located down river from the village of Rikon where Liukan was said to live in the past. Liukan moved to these mist-covered peaks after being consumed by his despair for the world following a certain incident.

During the Gate Rune War, the hero and his party managed to navigate the rapids using Kamandol's Engine reaching the hermitage and contacting Liukan. Liukan was abducted from his home soon after by Milich Oppenheimer and then joined the Toran Liberation Army. As Liukan has continued his medical practice it is possible the hermitage has been abandoned since that point.

name: Lordlake
description: Lordlake (ロードレイク, Rōdoreiku) is a location seen in Suikoden V. It is a city in the southern part of the Queendom of Falena.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Lordlake is located in the southern reaches of the Queendom of Falena. In times past, it was known as the "Jewel of the Queendom" as a beautiful city, rich in water. It was traditional governed by the Rovere family, its last governor being Lord Rodney Rondot Rovere.

However, following the events of the Lordlake Uprising, an enraged Queen Arshtat would raze the town with the power of the Sun Rune. After that, the city was completely devastated, and moves from both factions of the senate to further punished the town damaged its recovery prospects further. Following this, most of Lordlake's citizens had little choice but to leave the area, leaving only the infirm and stubborn in the town. 

During the Sun Rune War, the dam at Hatred Fortress would be destroyed with the help of the beavers and the the Prince of Falena, allowing water to flow to Lordlake again and beginning the town's recovery.

name: Lorimar
description: Lorimar (ロリマー Rorima) is a region within the Toran Republic and a sub-region of Toran as a whole. The region has historically enjoyed a large degree of autonomy throughout history.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Warriors' Village dominates the Lorimar region, which ensured the region's autonomy following the Final Battle against the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia. The Qlon Temple lies on the western frontier of the region and the castle of Neclord, one-time Imperial governor of Lorimar exists to the south.

The eastern frontier of the region is bordered by the Dunan River and Liukan's Hermitage, which is cut off from the rest of the region by mountains.

The region has a lot of grassland and open plains and tends to have a mild, temperate climate. The west contains the Lorimar Mountains which extend up through Kunan and the Dragon Knight's Domain.

As an autonomous and mostly self-sufficient region, Lorimar does not have strong trading ties or developed industry and so people from Lorimar rarely travel to other areas.

name: Lorimar Mountains
description: The Lorimar Mountains (ロリマー山脈 Rorima Sanmyaku) is a mountain range located in the west of the Toran region. It is mentioned by name several times in Genso Suikoden: Soul Eater 1.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Lorimar Mountains began in the far-west of the Lorimar region. From there, it extended up through Kunan and the Dragon Knight's Domain.

From Gregminster, the sun could be seen to set behind the Lorimar Mountains in the evening. Leknaat, meanwhile, could 'sense' the world around her from Magician's Island roughly as far as the Lorimar Mountains in the west.

name: Lude Woods
description: The Lude Woods (ルードの森, Rūdo no Mori) are a location mentioned in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Lude Woods were located on the outskirts of Kyaro Town in the Highland Kingdom. When they were young, the 8-year old hero and the 9-year old Jowy Atreides once visited these woods to collect mushrooms and got lost.

When his friend suffered a sprain on a rocky riverbank, Jowy went down the river to call for help. Genkaku eventually found the crying Jowy and they returned for the hero, Genkaku carrying him home on his back.

Years later, during the Dunan Unification War, Nanami would reminisce about this little adventure while awaiting for Jowy's return to Muse City from his mission infiltrating the Highland garrison.

name: Lunas
description: Lunas (ルナス, Runasu) is a location seen in Suikoden V. It is the Falenan holy land, located in the country's north.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Located in the northern reaches of Falena, Lunas is said to be the land where the first Queen of Falena descended. Since that time, the area has become a holy land, served and maintained by its oracles and protected against those who would exploit its generous natural gold resources.

During the Sun Rune War, Haswar, cousin of Sialeeds and Arshtat Falenas served as Oracle of Lunas and sheltered her nephew, the Prince when he fled the Godwin Faction's clutches. Many of Haswar's priests had been supporters of Haswar's mother, Shahrewar's, claim to the throne and so some bore resentment, and even betrayed, the Prince's party to the Godwins.

Male clerics at Lunas traditionally shave their heads as proof of their devotion to their duty.

name: Lunas North Woods
description: The Lunas North Woods (ルナス北の森, Runasu Kita no Mori) is a location in Suikoden V. It is a forested mountain path north of Lunas.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This mountainous forest path connected Lunas with the lands of the Barows Faction. It had many hidden passages that normally only elves could navigate as the woods also contained the elven settlement of Alseid.

Isato would guide the Prince's party through the woods to help them escape the Godwin Faction's pursuit following the Queen's Assassination. Gold deposits in the area led to an amount of gold dust in the river's water, catching the eye of greedy individuals.

Zegai would stay in the woods for a time, hunting for food. Takamu's attention to detail allowed him to navigate the hidden paths, much to Isato and Urda's disbelief.

name: Magician's Island
description: Magician's Island (魔術師の島, Majutsushi no Shima) is an island that appears in Suikoden. It is the home of the Seer of the Scarlet Moon Empire and, later, the Toran Republic.

facts.label: Nation
facts.value: Scarlet Moon Empire (until 457), Toran Republic (457 onwards)

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Magician's Island is an isolated island located in the bay of the Tolna Canal.

It served as the home of the Scarlet Moon Empire's former seer, Leknaat. Leknaat's home, a large tower called Magician's Tower (魔術師の塔, Majutsushi no Tō), is the only building on the island.

The tower itself was mode of huge stones. Some of the stones had collapsed, probably due to weathering over the years. The exterior walls were gray, but in places they were colored green by moss and vines that had entangled them. It was reminiscent of an ancient civilization and very tall.

The tower is protected by Leknaat's magic; Leknaat had isolated herself on the island, wary of her sister, Windy. The magical barrier around Magician's Island meant that only those Leknaat's permission, such as messengers coming for her astrological readings, the Astral Predications, could approach the island.

Following the Gate Rune War, Leknaat departed from the island along with her apprentice, Luc. However, the place did not stay vacant for long. Hellion, the newly appointed Seer, moved in soon after Leknaat's disappearance and Window was hired to work on the windows there.

name: Mercenary Fortress
description: The Mercenary Fortress (傭兵砦, Youhei Toride) is a location in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This mercenary fortress was built with the assistance of Muse City following the Gate Rune War. Viktor served as the leader of the soldiers stationed there with Flik serving as his second-in-command. Anabelle supported its establishment in an attempt to deter the Highland Kingdom from invading.

Located in eastern Muse Principality, south of Ryube Village, the fortress is surrounded by forest to the east and west. The flag which waved proudly over the fortress was designed by Viktor. It was also the place where the hero was first taken in after jumping from Tenzan Pass. Its soldiers were known for their skill and tenacity in battle.

Still, the fortress would fall early in the Dunan Unification War to the onslaught of the Highland Army under Luca Blight. Viktor was able to delay the fortress' occupation by setting it ablaze. It would be occupied by the New Alliance Army late in the war after troops were moved there in order to divide the attention of the Highland Army.

In a sense, the officers of the Mercenary Fortress could be considered the forbearers to the New Alliance Army.

name: Middleport
description: Middleport (ミドルポート, Midorupōto) is a location that appears in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. It is a island city located in the western Island Nations.

facts.label: Nation
facts.value: Dukedom of Gaien (until 299), Independent city-state (299-307), Island Nations Federation (307 onwards)

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Middleport began as a colony of the Dukedom of Gaien until it was occupied by the Kooluk Empire. Kooluk would agree to withdraw from the island under the condition that Middleport would become independent, breaking off all ties with Gaien. This was apparently done to help facilitate the gathering of Rune Cannon materials, which had recently been invented in the city. Following Kooluk's withdrawal, Middleport would be governed by Lord Schtolteheim Reinbach II.

Middleport's political neutrality helped it to become a thriving trade city and both overt regular trade and Reinbach II's underground trading in Rune Cannon materials, would cause the city to become very wealthy. The city would also become known as the birthplace of Rune Cannon technology.

The island would remain neutral throughout the Island Liberation War, although it agreed to let the Stronghold Ship visit and use the island for trade, recruitment and any other matters. Once the war ended, Middleport would become a founding member of the Island Nations Federation, with Reinbach III succeeding his father as Lord.

name: Moravia
description: Moravia (モレビア, Morebia) is a castle that appears in Suikoden. It is a key defensive bastion for the Scarlet Moon Empire and one of the few buildings in the region of Senan.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Moravia was built on the fringes of the Karakas Desert in 407 as the front-line defense against the City-States of Jowston, along with the Fortresses of Duha and Rakan. The castle was built in a very pragmatic fashion, with high stark walls and so lacked the frills and ornate work present in other castles. It also had monsters roaming the halls for additional security purposes.

Kasim Hazil was the lord of Moravia during the Gate Rune War, succeeding Teo McDohl as protector of the north following the latter's death. The castle was infiltrated by the Toran Liberation Army during the war to free Viktor and noted rebel sympathizer Warren from captivity.

Kasim Hazil surrendered the castle without a fight when the Jowston army was manipulated into invading the region while Moravia's forces were assisting the beleaguered Duha and Rakan fortresses, cutting the castle off from any possible rescue.

Following the war, Kasim Hazil returned to Moravia to continue protecting the northern border.

name: Mordo Island
description: Mordo Island (モルド島, Morudo-jima) is a location that appears in Suikoden IV. It is a small island located in the south-eastern Island Nations.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Located just to the north-west of the Kingdom of Obel, Mordo Island is known for its enjoyable hot springs on the island's beach, where visitors can relax while enjoying the ocean view. In the mountains that lie in the northern part of the island, treasure explorers hunt for buried valuables said to have been hidden there by pirates in the past.

Following the Island Liberation War, Lo Fong, Lo Hak and Lo Seng would help run the hot springs there along with Igor, making the island an even greater attraction.

name: Mountain Mass Island
description: Mountain Mass Island (山塊の島, Sankai no Shima) is a location that appears in Suikoden IV. It is a small island located in the north-western Island Nations.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This island is located in the far north-west of the Island Nations, isolated from other islands. The island has a sandy beach but following the narrow roads that stem from it will lead visitors on a winding trail through the steep mountains.

Due to its inhospitable terrain, it was used as a hiding place for treasure and so many valuable items could be found on this island.

name: Mountain Path
description: The Mountain Path (山道, Yamamichi) is a location in Suikoden III. It is a winding trail located near the Grasslands-Harmonian border.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Mountain Path is a three-way trail which connects the Grasslands with the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia. The trail has exit points to the south-west of the Karaya Village, the occupied town of Le Buque and the Harmonian frontier town of Caleria.

The trail between Caleria and Le Buque is busier and more maintained that the corresponding path to the Grasslands. During the War of the Champions, Viki, as well as her younger counterpart mysteriously appeared on this pass.

name: Mountain Path
description: The Mountain Path (山道, Yamamichi) is a location in Suikoden V. It is the path the player must take to reach Beaver Lodge.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This lengthy mountain path allowed travelers to journey to Beaver Lodge from Yashuna Village.

The DoReMi Elf SoLaTi fled to this path after fleeing from Cornelio's home in Haud Village.

name: Mt. Hei-Tou
description: Mt. Hei-Tou (平頭山, Heitō Yama, Mt. Flat-Head) is a location in Suikoden III. It is a mountain located in the northern Grasslands, near Budehuc Castle.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: Mt. Hei-Tou is a mountain located north of the Great Hollow, which a river runs alongside. Deviating from the riverside trail will bring travelers into a complex series of mountain paths almost immediately. Many wild animals, including tigers and wild boars live in the area, making it a dangerous place for some to visit.

name: Mt. Seifu
description: Mt. Seifu (清風山, Seifūzan) is a mountain that appears in Suikoden. It is the home of the Mt. Seifu bandits and is located to the east of Rockland.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
At 470 meters high, Mount Seifu is one of the tallest mountains in the Toran Republic. The temperature of the mountain is quite low, leading it to be an ideal growing place for plants that are not native to Toran. Its name derives from the cold winds which comes down from the mountains.

In addition, because this mountain is intricate has many valleys, many bandit hideouts have been built here in the past. Varkas's bandit group lurked in the mountains usually, attacking Imperial tax convoys.

The bandits had built a two-story wooden fortress, able to accommodate between 40 and 50 bandits, on the mountain two or three years before McDohl would investigate the peak. Following the Gate Rune War, the mountain has become much more quiet and peaceful.

name: Mt. Senai
description: Mt. Senai (セナイ山, Senai Yama) is a location in Suikoden III. It is a steep mountain located in the far-eastern Grasslands, within the western fringes of the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This steep mountain is located to the east of Le Buque. A series of caves run through the mountain, leading to the Altar of the Mantor Trainers in the rear of the cavern. This altar was built by Le Buque back when it was the Carna Clan during the initial war between the Grasslands and the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia as a shrine to the Flame Champion.

name: Mt. Tigerwolf
description: Mt. Tigerwolf (虎狼山, Korōzan) is a mountain that appears in Suikoden. It divides the village of Sarady from the rest of Arus and is home to several bandits.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
At 500 meters above sea level, Mount Tigerwolf is one of the tallest mountains in Toran, located just a short distance from the small village of Sarady and north of the Tolna Canal and the Floating Fortress of Shasarazade. The mountain is considered an entrance to the foreboding Banner Mountains. The path through the mountain was considered dangerous due to the monsters inhabiting the range.

The mountain's name came from rumors that many tigers and wolves lived in the mountain range. This was a deliberate lie spread by some to try and scare people away when gold dust and veins were discovered in the Banner Mountains. 

The mountain was also known to be home to several bandits, including Ledon and Kessler, who drugged and stole valuables from travellers through the mountains but an Imperial crackdown in the area during the Gate Rune War forced the majority of bandits to flee the area.

name: Muse City
description: Muse City (ミューズ市, Myūzu Shi) is a location in Suikoden II which also features in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.1 and Genso Suikoden Card Stories.

facts.label: Nation
facts.value: Dunan Monarchy (until 185), City-States of Jowston (314-460), Dunan Republic (460 onwards)

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Muse City, once a part of the Dunan Monarchy, gained its status as a city-state in SY 185 during the fall of the monarchy. Its inaugural mayor was Revier. Positioned strategically, the city remained at the forefront of conflicts with the Highland Kingdom. The city would later become one of the founding members of the City-States of Jowston.

As the thriving center of the City-States of Jowston, Muse City boasted a sizable population of 13,000, making it one of the largest cities within the alliance. Its prosperity was evident in the constant bustle of its stores and the vibrant activity of its people. Although lacking a formal trading post, the city's streets were lined with numerous businesses, attracting daily crowds of customers. Commerce and trade stood as the primary industries, reflecting the city's economic vitality.

West of the city lay the gates leading to Jowston Hill, a significant venue for essential City-State meetings. The gates were selectively opened during special occasions and ceremonies, such as these crucial gatherings.

Given its strategic importance and wealth, Muse City became the primary target of the Highland Army once the Dunan Unification War intensified. Tragically, the city suffered extensive damage, particularly after the murder of Mayor Anabelle and the subsequent occupation by Highland forces. The dire situation worsened when Luca Blight rounded up citizens of the city to sacrifice to the Beast Rune, leading to an infestation of Golden Wolves.

After the conflict, Muse City embarked on the challenging path to healing the scars of war. Implementing policies centered around its traditional strengths of trade and commerce, the city now strides confidently towards a smooth recovery.

name: Muse-Highland Border
description: The Muse-Highland Border is a fortified wall that appears in Suikoden II. It is located between Muse Principality and the Highland Kingdom.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
After invading Eastern Muse via North Swallow Pass and destroying the towns and forts in that area, the main force of the Highland army set up camp just north of the Muse-Highland Border wall. The hero and Jowy were recruited by Jess to don Highland Youth Brigade uniforms and infiltrate this camp to determine the amount of supplies and therefore the likely strategy of this army.

Overwhelmed by the Highland forces, this wall remained intact throughout the Dunan Unification war and was the last fortification to fall before the siege of L'Renouille.

name: Na-Nal Island
description: Na-Nal Island (ナ・ナル島, Na Naru-jima) is a location that appears in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. It is a island located in the eastern Island Nations.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Na-Nal Island was the eastern-most inhabited island in the Island Nations region. The custom for the humans there was to have a triangle painted on the forehead, used to represent the status of the individual villagers there.

The island was relatively built up, with rune, armor and item shops as well as a popular hot spring, which served as a tourist attraction, on the western side of the island. In the far north, across a gorge, lay the Great Elven Tree and the Elven Settlement. The elves who resided there harbored a disdain for the human settlers and this almost turned into full-blown conflict during the Island Liberation War. Since then, however, relations have slowly improved.

name: Nay Island
description: Nay Island (ネイ島, Nei-jima) is a location that appears in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. It is a island located in the eastern Island Nations.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Ney Island is located in the eastern reaches of the Island Nations, just to the west of Na-Nal Island. A gorge divides the island in two and there is a human and Nay-Kobold Settlement on each of the two segments.

The human town lies on the slopes near the island's harbor while the Nay-Kobold settlement lies to the east, across the Stonecutter's Field and Suspension Bridge. Unlike their Na-Nal neighbors, the two different communities on Nay have built up a friendly relationship.

Although Nay is a peaceful island it has its problems in that emigration has started to increase with many young people leaving to look for work or excitement in their lives. As such, Nay now faces the threat of serious depopulation.

name: Nay-Kobold Settlement
description: The Nay-Kobold Settlement (ネコボルトの集落, Nekoborudo no Shūraku) is a location that appears in Suikoden IV. It is an Nay-Kobold settlement located on Nay Island.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Nay-Kobold settlement on Nay Island lay across the Suspension Bridge, across from the Stonecutter's Field which separated the Nay-Kobold settlement from the human village. The buildings in this settlement were all uniquely shaped like cats and had many facilities such as hot springs and trading posts. The colorful buildings reflected the aesthetic tastes of the Nay-Kobold villagers.

Despite these attractions, it seems that humans rarely visited the settlement. Bartholomew chose to hide out there when the Kooluk Empire were hunting him, for example.

name: Neclord's Castle
description: Neclord's Castle (ネクロードの城, Nekurōdo no Shiro) is a castle that appears in Suikoden. It is where Neclord dwelt during his stint as a magistrate in the Scarlet Moon Empire.

name: Nest of Pirates
description: The Nest of Pirates (海賊島, Kaizoku Shima, Pirate Island) is a location that appears in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. It is a small island located in the southern Island Nations.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Nest of Pirates is a small island located in the far south, away from other islands in the Island Nations. Due to its distance, it had become a haunt for pirates included the famed Pirate King Edgar as well as Kika.

Since the island was located outside the reaches of the Kooluk Empire's subjugation fleets, it was used as a base by the resisting Island Nations forces during the Island Liberation War.

name: New Alliance Army Headquarters
description: The New Alliance Army Headquarters (新同盟軍本拠地, Shin Doumei Gun Honkyochi) is a location in Suikoden II. It serves as the player-named base for the protagonist's forces and allies.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Early in the Dunan Unification War, this headquarters was established by the leader of the New Alliance Army. Established at the ruins of North Window Castle, the base was where the New Alliance Army, aided by Shu, began their resistance against the Highland Army, repelling their forces in battle and bolstering the spirit of then-occupied South Window Principality.

The headquarters would continue to grow and expand during the war, as word of its security spread. By the end of the war, it was something of a large town in its own right.

name: New Leaf Academy
description: New Leaf Academy (ニューリーフ学院, Nyūrīfu Gakuin) is a location in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Nation
facts.value: City-States of Jowston (362-373, 378-460), Dunan Republic (460 onwards)

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
New Leaf Academy, Greenhill City's academic center, is a symbol of the city. The school was established in 362 by Alen Wisemail as Grand Leaf Academy, an educational institution for the children of influential people in the region.

The headmaster position was originally the hereditary right of the Wisemail family for generations. Academic reform in 392 would see this system replaced with an electoral process. This reform would also see the school's name changed to its current appelation, New Leaf Academy.

At the academy, rune work, history, smithery and appraising, among other subjects, can be studied. Each year, students who gain excellent results graduate from the academy and contribute to society.

name: Nirva Island
description: Nirva Island (ニルバ島, Niruba Tou) is a location in Suikoden V. It is an important island that forms part of the trade route between the Island Nations Federation and the Queendom of Falena.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Nirva Island is located on the sea route connecting the Island Nations Federation and the Queendom of Falena, far north of the Straits of Scardi. The island has developed as an independent trading city, specializing in serving as a relay point for supply ships as well as a tourist destination for its Great Lighthouse.

As the island lies along several trading routes, ships of many different countries visit the island. This bustling trade has led to the island's harbor being expanded significantly.

During the Sun Rune War, the Great Lighthouse on Nirva would be taken over by pirates trying to secure the release of their incarcerated captain. Their plot would be foiled by the Prince of Falena and Bernadette Egan, with the aid of Chisato.

The island lies far enough from Falena that the Elmelark at Estrise is one of the few ways to travel there. Even Viki's blinking magic does not reach that far.

name: North Amur Plain
description: The North Amur Plain (アムル平原・北, Amuru Heigan Kita) is a location in Suikoden III. It is a grass plain located in southern Grasslands.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: Located in the northern reaches of the Amur Plain, this wide open plain is a relatively safe area. Kathy would sometimes bring her horses grazing here.

name: North Cavern
description: The North Cavern (北の洞窟, Kita no Dōkutsu) is a location in Suikoden III. It is located north of Vinay del Zexay.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The North Cavern is located north of Vinay del Zexay, within the Zexen Federation. It was originally a mine and scattered mining tools could still be found there, even after it became a storage place for illicit goods.

It is a linear cave that, when followed, leads back outside close to a large lake. From that vantage point, it is possible to see the stranded ship that had collided with Budehuc Castle in the north.

name: North Swallow Pass
description: North Swallow Pass (燕北の峠, Enhoku no Tōge) is a location that appears in Suikoden II. It is a mountain pass located north of Ryube Village.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
North Swallow Pass, located in eastern Muse Principality, connects the City-States of Jowston with the southern territories of Highland. It is a dangerous area, blanketed with deep fog, allowing monsters and bandits to roam the area day and night.

In addition to this, there were rumors of a terrible Mist Shade living here during the early Dunan Unification War. However, it was defeated by a band of wandering entertainers and their companions.

name: North Window
description: North Window (ノースウィンドゥ, Nōsūindou), localized as Northwind in Suikoden, is a location in Suikoden II. It is the destroyed hometown of Viktor.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
North Window was once a large town, on a par with South Window City. Its castle had been built in the past as a defensive stronghold during South Window Principality's conflicts with the Scarlet Moon Empire.

In 449, Neclord laid waste to the town in a single night along with a host of zombies. The people were transformed into zombies and would turn on each other, leaving no survivors.

Viktor was one of the few people not to be present during the massacre but returned in time to see the transformed villagers and was forced to cut them all down. A South Window City force would be dispatched following this in order to mop up any remaining zombies.

Official knowledge of Neclord's appearance and the reason behind North Window's destruction would be suppressed by agreement of the member states of the City-States of Jowston, giving the mystery of North Window an enduring presence in people's minds. Following the destruction of the town, it was left as ruins for years with only its old castle remaining standing in some form.

The deserted ruins of the town became subject to rumors of disappearances during the Dunan Unification War, which were found to be caused by Neclord. After he was driven off, the remains of the town would form the basis for the headquarters of the New Alliance Army.

name: North Window Castle
description: North Window Castle (ノースウィンドゥの城, Nōsuuindou no Shiro) is a location in Suikoden II. It is an abandoned ruin in North Window.
originalName: ノースウィンドゥの城

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This castle once stood on the southern shore of Lake Dunan. It was once a symbol of North Window's prosperity, but after the village was destroyed by Neclord, it became a monster-infested ruin.

During the Dunan Unification War, the hero's group investigated reports of Neclord's return and fought his minion, the Abomination, in the castle. Later, with the formation of the New Alliance Army, the castle was chosen as the stronghold to oppose the Highland Army from, beginning its reconstruction as the New Alliance Army Headquarters.

name: Northern Checkpoint
description: The Northern Checkpoint (北の関所, Kita no Sekisho) is a fortress that appears in Suikoden. It is a citadel located between the regions of Kunan and Senan.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Northern Checkpoint was built over the Sadhna Stream separating Senan and Kunan and as such was the only way to gain entrance to the mostly desolate region of Senan.

During the Gate Rune War, the Battle at the Northern Checkpoint occurred here when what was assumed by Imperial spies to be Toran Liberation Army training exercises turned into a surprise all-out attack on the checkpoint. Griffith was in command of the Northern Checkpoint during the war.

The checkpoint quickly fell to Liberation Army in the resulting Battle at the Northern Checkpoint.

name: Obel Royal Palace
description: The Obel Royal Palace (オベル王宮, Oberu Ōkyū) is a location that appears in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The Obel Royal Palace serves as the seat of government for the Kingdom of Obel. It was a very modest building in comparison to other palaces. Though the palace did not showcase the grandeur of the King of Obel, that is due to Lino En Kuldes' ability to see things from the perspective of his subjects.

name: Pannu Yakuta Castle
description: Pannu Yakuta Castle (パンヌ・ヤクタ城 Pannu Yakuta Jō) is a location in Suikoden. It was the headquarters of the Imperial Army stationed in the Great Forest.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Pannu Yakuta Castle served as the administrative center of the Great Forest region and played significant roles during both the War of Succession and the Gate Rune War. It was located roughly half a day away by foot from the Village of the Elves.

During the War of Succession, the castle became the stronghold for Barbarossa Rugner's forces, who sought refuge there after facing early setbacks. Some historical accounts suggest that the castle's foundations were built on ancient Sindar ruins, adding an aura of mystique to its history.

In the subsequent Gate Rune War, Pannu Yakuta Castle was under the command of Great General Kwanda Rosman who had been assigned there some years prior. As the conflict escalated, the Toran Liberation Army assembled its forces and launched an assault on the castle, leading to the decisive Battle at Pannu Yakuta.

Following a hard-fought victory for the Liberation Army, the castle was infiltrated by the army's leader and his entourage. Within the castle, they encountered and defeated a formidable Dragon before the leader confronted and ultimately defeated General Rosman in an intense one-on-one duel.

name: Pirate's Fortress
description: The Pirate's Fortress (こぞくのとりで, Kozoku no Toride) is a fortress that appears in Suikoden. Less a fortress, it is more of a small base located on a small island on the western side of Lake Toran.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The Pirate's Fortress was home to the band of pirates led by Anji, Leonardo and Kanak. The "fortress" itself is nothing more than a large wooden hut built on a small island in south-east Senan on the northern waters of Lake Toran. After the pirates joined the Toran Liberation Army, Anji admitted that there was no comparison between his own fortress and Toran Castle.

name: Port Spinacks
description: Port Spinacks (スピナクス港, Supinakusu Minato) is a location seen in Suikoden V. It is a harbor located in southwestern Falena.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Port Spinacks is a prominent harbor that lies over a tributary of the Feitas River that flows from east to west. The port itself exits on both sides, allowing people to pass through the port or across the river with ease. The majority of traffic are travelers going from Stormfist to Sauronix Castle and vice-versa.

The port also stops any ships from approaching Sauronix Castle easily, helping to protect the castle town from attacks by sea.

name: Qlon Temple
description: Qlon Temple (クロン寺, Kuron Dera) is a temple that appears in Suikoden. Qlon Temple is a peaceful place which once held the resting place of the Celestial Sword.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Qlon Temple is an ancient temple situated west of Warriors' Village in the north-western reaches of the Lorimar region. The temple itself is considered historic. Residents of the Warriors' Village would sometimes visit Qlon Temple to pay their respects. The martial artist Morgan trained spiritually at this temple both during and after the Gate Rune War.

The temple was founded ages ago by a priest, named Qlon and was said to hold a secret. This secret, known to Fukien, head of the temple during the Gate Rune War was that deep in the Cave of the Past, located behind the temple, was the resting place of the Celestial Sword.

name: Radat Town
description: Radat Town (ラダトの街, Radato no Machi) is a location that appears in Suikoden II. It is a town which lies on the Dunan River.

facts.label: Nation
facts.value: Dunan Monarchy (until 252), City-States of Jowston (314-460), Dunan Republic (460 onwards)

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Radat Town in located on the eastern reaches of South Window Principality. Crossing the bridge in the east takes you into Muse Principality.

In addition to its inn, item store and appraisers, the town also boasts a large tavern. Korokke and nizakana are two great tasting delicacies from the town. As such, the town is regarded as a prosperous source of trade and is frequently busy with travelers and merchants.

It is also known for its large sluice gate, which regulates the amount of water which flows from the Dunan River, to Lake Dunan and also towards the Toran Republic. The sight of all its gates closing at once was said to be a spectacular sight.

The city was occupied several times during the Dunan Unification War by the Highland Army but was successfully liberated each time.

name: Raftfleet
description: Raftfleet (ラフトフリート, Rafutofurīto) is a location in Suikoden V. It is a fishing community comprised of boats located in the Queendom of Falena.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Raftfleet is a community of fishing boats and other vessels that, over time, came together and formed a community. Centered around the great battleship Dahak, Raftfleet is able to travel all over the Feitas River, giving it a unique character in the Queendom of Falena. It is has a tradition of being fiercely independent and citizens of Raftfleet are not subject to taxes.

During the Sun Rune War, Raftfleet was forced to change locations several times due to the threat of the Godwin Faction and the Falenan Royal Navy which wished to subjugate Raftfleet in the name of cultural hegemony.

name: Rainwall
description: Rainwall (レインウォール, Reinuōru) is a location seen in Suikoden V. It is a town in the eastern part of the Queendom of Falena.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Rainwall is located in the eastern Queendom of Falena and is a town governed by the Barows Faction, one of the two leading Falenan noble houses and opponent of the Godwin Faction.

It is a picturesque town with many water features and running water, befitting its name. In the center of town lies the Barows mansion. Despite its proximity to the New Armes Kingdom, the town is not fortified, relying on the eastern city of Estrise as a bulwark.

Early in the Sun Rune War, the town was the center of the Prince of Falena's counterattack against the Godwin army. Later in the war, Rainwall would be occupied by the Godwin forces, leading to much damage as well as the fall of the Barows family.

Following the town's liberation, several of its inhabitants would whisper of the atrocities committed by the Godwin forces during their occupation.

name: Rakutei Mountain
description: Rakutei Mountain (洛帝山, Rakutei Zan) is a mountain that appears in Suikoden II. It is the home of many monsters and is located to the north of Highway Village.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This 650 meter-high mountain lies in the northernmost reaches of the City-States of Jowston. The mountain was usually covered with fog. This and the dangerous beasts that inhabited it ensured that few people dared climb it.

During the Dunan Unification War, a large monster with wings appeared on this mountain. A boy, Kent, would climb the mountain thinking it was a dragon. It turned out not to be a dragon but a Harpy.

Dspite this Futch found a dragon egg after rescuing Kent from the Harpy's clutches, which hatched the dragon Bright. Bright would be taken from the mountain and raised as Futch's mount.

name: Ranro Mountain
description: Ranro Mountain (乱稜山, Ranryō Yama) is a location in Suikoden V. It is a mountain located in southeastern Falena.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Ranro Mountain is a steep mountain in the southeastern reaches of the Queendom of Falena, overlooking the town of Sable.

During the Sun Rune War, the bandit Roy operated out of Ranro Mountain as part of Euram Barows' plot to discredit the Prince of Falena and his Imperial City Recapture Army.

name: Razril
description: Razril (ラズリル, Razuriru) is a location that appears in Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics. It is a major port city and island on the western side of the Island Nations region. As part of the Dukedom of Gaien, Razril prospered through its vast harbor, shipbuilding, and trade, while serving as the headquarters of the Knights of Gaien.

facts.label: Nation
facts.value: Dukedom of Gaien (until 307), Island Nations Federation (307 onwards)

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
===Gaien Rule and Decline===
.]]
For centuries, Razril served as the Dukedom of Gaien's naval bastion, with the Gaien Knights Headquarters stationed on the island. Though nominally ruled by the Gaien Dukedom's monarchy, Razril fell under the authority of Lord Vingerhut, a vain and self-serving ruler more concerned with appearances than governance. His mismanagement weakened Razril’s position, and following internal strife within the Knights, Kooluk forces were able to seize the city with little resistance.

===Recapture of Razril===
Razril became a central battleground during the Island Liberation War. With the strategist Elenor Silverberg coordinating the Allied Forces, the fleet launched a counteroffensive to reclaim the city.

Initially outnumbered by the joint Kooluk-Gaien fleet, the Allies turned the tide when the Gaien Knights aboard the Gaien ships defected to their side, shifting the balance of power. The ensuing battle ended in a decisive Allied victory. Helmut, commander of the enemy fleet, was captured and later persuaded by the allied leader to join their cause.

===Independence===
Following the conclusion of the Island Liberation War, Razril achieved independence from the Dukedom of Gaien. The Gaien Knights stationed there were reorganized into the Knights of Razril, solidifying the city’s new role as both a political and military power within the Island Nations Federation.

name: Revolving Bridge
description: Revolving Bridge (回転橋, Kaiten-bashi) is a location that appears in Suikoden V. It is a large, gear-powered bridge that divides Barows Faction and Godwin Faction lands.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Revolving Bridge was a large bridge connecting the eastern Barows Faction and western Godwin Faction territories.

It was of an ingenious design with exposed gears courtesy of its designer, Babbage, who specialized in the field. It was originally built for the internal defense of Falena and as such could only be turned from the Godwin side.

When the Sun Rune War broke out, the Revolving Bridge's significance was recognized by the nascent Imperial City Recapture Army. With Babbage's help they were able to infiltrate the bridge and remove the gear vital to its operation so that the Godwins could not use the bridge.

name: Rikon
description: Rikon (リコン, Rikon) is a village that appears in Suikoden. Known as Premier L'Amour (プルミエ・ラムール, Purumie Ramūru) for a short period of time, Rikon is a small village in the region of Kunan.

facts.label: Nation
facts.value: Scarlet Moon Empire (until 457), Toran Republic (457 onwards)

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Rikon is located in the south-east of the Kunan region and south-west of the Fortress of Garan. The "Holy Doctor", Liukan, once lived in Rikon but eventually secluded himself down river from the village, past the Rapids of Dunan. Despite this, people desperate for medical aid would sometimes come to the village, hoping to meet him.

During the Toran Liberation War, the town was called "Premier L'Amour" as per the order of General Milich Oppenheimer. It was an unpopular name, due to it being difficult to say and the name reverted back to Rikon following the defeat of Milich.

Juppo's Workshop was located in Rikon, making it a home for all sorts of trickster inventions, and the noises and smoke that went along with them, for a time. The people of Rikon quickly because used to the commotion and some would frequent the workshop for mechanical devices, such as herb grinders.

name: Road to Matilda
description: The Road to Matilda (マチルダ騎士団への抜け道, Machiruda Kishi-dan e no Nukemichi), also referred to as the Forest Path (森の抜け道, Mori no Nukemichi), is a location in Suikoden II and Genso Suikoden Card Stories. It is a forest path connecting Greenhill and Matilda.
originalName: マチルダ騎士団への抜け道

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This pathway through the Greenhill Forest connecting Greenhill Principality and the Matilda Knightdom. A hut has been set up at the entrance of the forest path and was guarded by the gallant Matilda Knights.

name: Rockaxe
description: Rockaxe (ロックアックス Rokkuakkusu) is a location in Suikoden II. It is the major town of the Matilda Knightdom.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: Rockaxe is the castle town to the Matilda Knights. It is built on the side of a hill, with Rockaxe Castle at its peak. This geographical feature makes Rockaxe very difficult to attack.

name: Rockaxe Castle
description: Rockaxe Castle (ロックアックス城 Rokkuakkusu Shiro) is a location in Suikoden II. It is the headquarters of the Matilda Knights.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The magnificent Rockaxe Castle is the primary symbol of the Matilda Knights. The town of Rockaxe is built on a hillside and Rockaxe Castle lies on top of that hill.

This geographical feature gives Rockaxe Castle a defensive advantage should it come under attack. The castle also had many narrow passages and small rooms to stymie intruders.

During the Dunan Unification War, the castle was the residence of Gordeau, the leader of the White Knights.

name: Rockland
description: Rockland (ロックランド, Rokkurando) is a village that appears in Suikoden and the upcoming Suikoden STAR LEAP. It is a small rural village located to the east of the Imperial capital of Gregminster.

facts.label: Nation
facts.value: Scarlet Moon Empire (until 457), Toran Republic (457 onwards)

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Rockland's name comes from its harsh terrain, barren save for rocks and nearly completely devoid of plant life. It was located roughly three days east, by horseback, of Gregminster.

Rockland was a desolate, poor town with buildings made of stone. Sand and loose stones could be found everywhere.

Ordinarily, Rockland was considered a simple, but peaceful, village. During the Gate Rune War, the area was said to be more dangerous than in years past and dissatisfaction with the village's military commander, Grady, was high.

Grady would also institute frivolous taxes for his own benefit. Growing hair, wearing clothes, and married couples sharing a bed were all taxes Grady implemented on the people of Rockland.

An Imperial Guard party, led by Kanaan, investigated a lack of taxes coming from Rockland in the early stages of the Toran Liberation War, with Grady blaming the issue on the Mt. Seifu bandits. A young girl, Haruna, acted as a guide.

The gambler Marco spent time in Rockland before traveling to the Warriors' Village. Botan nabe (wild boar hot pot) was a Rockland specialty and served at its inn.

Grady abandoned the village towards the end of the war, with the people of Rockland happy with their improved circumstances and thoughtful about the future.

name: Rokkaku
description: Rokkaku Hamlet (ロッカクの里, Rokkaku no Mura) is a hamlet which is mentioned in Suikoden but first appears in Suikoden II. It is a secretive hamlet inhabited by ninja.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Located off the forest paths between Gregminster and Banner Village, the secluded Rokkaku traditionally avoided being ruled by other nations. 

Unmarked on maps, the village could only be reached by a secret passage through the woods unknown to most people. In addition, those who managed to somehow stumble across the settlement would be taken back to the forest path in an instant by ninjutsu.

Rokkaku had resisted attempts by the Scarlet Moon Empire to exert influence for many years. These attempts continued to grow in intensity during the Gate Rune War.

Chancellor Hanzo's final rejection of an order to surrender by a Scarlet Moon envoy would see the hamlet razed by Teo McDohl during the Gate Rune War. The ninja Kasumi was ordered to report the carnage to the Toran Liberation Army and joined the rebel forces for the remainder of the war.

Following the last battle, it was revealed by Chief Hanzo that there were survivors. The majority of non-combatants had hidden in the City-States of Jowston during this time.

Hanzo and Kasumi returned to Rokkaku following the Gate Rune War. Over the next three years, the people of Rokkaku would come together to rebuild their village.

During the Dunan Unification War, the New Alliance Army would visit the hamlet. Hanzo would have Mondo and Sasuke join the New Alliance Army as a token of alliance.

name: Ruins of Cyndar
description: The Ruins of Cyndar (シンダルの遺跡, Shindaru no Iseki) are a location in Suikoden III. They are an expensive set of ruins located at the underground Ancient Highway.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Huge ruins that exist at the end of the Ancient Highway in Grasslands. There was a lot of magical power contained within, and the structure was very intricate and elaborately laid out. The cultural importance of these ruins appeared to be very high due to the ceremonial halls located throughout.

These ruins were also known as the place where Wyatt Lightfellow had sealed his True Water Rune before the War of the Champions. When he returned to the site to protect the seal, he was ambushed by the Destroyers who proceeded to take the True Rune as their own.

name: Ryube Forest
description: Ryube Forest (リューべの村の山道, Ryūbe no Mura no Yamamichi, Ryube Village Mountain Path) is a location that appears in Suikoden II. It is a small forested, mountainous area located in eastern Muse Principality.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Ryube Forest mountain path was located just to the north of Ryube Village itself, located near the Highland border at the North Swallow Pass.

In the depths of the forest which spread to the north-east of the village was a hut which was previously the workplace of the famed Tsai of the Divine Spear. The hunters Kinnison and Shiro also resided in these forests.

name: Ryube Village
description: Ryube Village (リューべの村, Ryūbe no Mura) is a location that appears in Suikoden II. It is a small village located in eastern Muse Principality.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Ryube Village is a small mountainside village with a population of 1,300. It is located near the Highland border; crossing the North Swallow Pass there leads directly to Kyaro Town.

The village was located close to the Mercenary Fortress and so was frequented by members of the brigade looking to purchase supplies. In the depths of the Ryube Forest which spread to the north-east of the village is a hut which was once the workplace of the famed Tsai of the Divine Spear.

The town was razed to the ground by the Highland Army under Luca Blight during the early Dunan Unification War.

name: Sable
description: Sable (セーブル, Sēburu) is a location seen in Suikoden V. It is a border town lying on the eastern frontier between the Queendom of Falena and the New Armes Kingdom.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Sable is a town located on the southeastern frontier of the Queendom of Falena and serves to protect the border between Falena and the New Armes Kingdom. Its huge gates and strong city walls gives the town a distinctive look, resembling a defense base as much as anything else.

During the Sun Rune War, the city was ruled by Lord Solis Raulbel.

name: Sable, East Checkpoint
description: The Sable, East Checkpoint (セーブル面の問所, Sēburu Men no Toisho) is a location in Suikoden V. It is a border crossing between Lordlake and Sable.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This checkpoint was located on the road between Lordlake and Sable. Guarded by the Sable Patrol Guard, it was the only route to Sable from within Falena.

The guards there confronted the Prince, believing him to to be the bandit prince that had been plaguing Sable.

name: Sadhna Stream
description: The Sadhna Stream (サドナの流れ Sadona no Nagare) is a river in Suikoden.

name: Sajah Village
description: Sajah Village (サジャの村 Saja no Mura) is a location that appears in Suikoden II. It is a small village which lies near the border between the Highland Kingdom and the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Sajah Village is located in the far north-west of Highland, close to the border with the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia. It is located closer to the Matilda Knightdom than L'Renouille.

Its isolation meant it survived the Dunan Unification War without any consequences. It was a poor village with little to attract visitors save for a shop that traded in rare runes.

Sajah is the home town of Elza and it is here she met her end after her final duel with Clive. Killey had visited Sajah before journeying to South Window City, inquiring about the Sindar.

name: Sarady
description: Sarady (サラディ, Saradi) is a village that appears in Suikoden. It is a tiny, isolated village located beyond Mt. Tigerwolf, to the north-west of the Imperial capital of Gregminster.

facts.label: Nation
facts.value: Scarlet Moon Empire (until 457), Toran Republic (457 onwards)

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Sarady, located north, beyond Mt. Tigerwolf in the Banner Mountains, is far removed from the other bustling cities of the Arus region, but it nonetheless draws a small number of visitors due to several rare plants and Water Runes which come from the region. Due to being such an isolated village, news rarely reaches the area and much of what does find its way to Sarady is so distorted as to be considered hearsay.

During the War of Succession, Sarady received a large number of refugees from both Gregminster and Lenankamp as the village was too isolated to be harassed much by the forces of Geil Rugner. When Geil's forces kidnapped a young McDohl, he was taken to Sarady to be handed over the bandits of Mt. Tigerwolf.

Odessa Silverberg used the village as a meeting place during the Toran Liberation Army in order to pass on plans for Fire Spear to an intermediary who would bring them to their destination. McDohl's discussion with Odessa later that night was when he developed a real interest in the liberation movement.

name: Sauronix Castle Town
description: Sauronix Castle Town (サウロニクス門前町, Sauronikusu Monzenmachi) is a location seen in Suikoden V. It is a castle town in the southwestern reaches of the Queendom of Falena.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This castle town, located in the southwestern lands of the Queendom of Falena, is governed by Craig Laden and is the headquarters of the Feitas Dragon Cavalry. Sauronix Castle itself is built on a quay, with the castle town spreading out from there.

This town is also known as the hometown of Miakis.

name: Scarleticia Castle
description: Scarleticia Castle (スカーレティシアの城, Sukāretishia no Shiro) is a location that appears in Suikoden. It is a castle located in the middle of the Kunan region.
originalName: スカーレティシアの城

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Scarleticia Castle was built in SY 370 to be the front line of the Scarlet Moon Empire's defenses against the City-States of Jowston. That particular role vanished with the construction of Moravia Castle and since then, much like Gregminster Palace, it has played a more important role as a symbol of Imperial authority in the land than as a strictly strategic fortification.

Milich Oppenheimer was appointed to the castle during the Gate Rune War where he installed the poisonous flower Antoinette as part of its defense under the influence of Windy's Conqueror Rune. Scarleticia repelled the Toran Liberation Army during the First Battle at Scarleticia Castle but fell to the Liberation Army once Antoninette's poison was neutralised.

name: Secret Factory
description: The Secret Factory (秘密工場, Himitsu Kōjō) is a location that appears in Suikoden. It is a building located in the southern Dana region.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The secret factory was a building located to the north of the razed village of Kalekka. The factory served to manufacture weapons, in secret, for the Toran Liberation Army. The blueprints for the Fire Spear was delivered by Kage to the factory.

Following the fall of the Liberation Army's Lenankamp base and the crackdown on rebel activities, contact with the factory was lost and its status was unknown until it was felt it was necessary to journey there in the hope of retrieving fire spears to battle Teo McDohl with.

The factory had indeed survived and was staffed by Mose and Ronnie Bell, who supplied the weapons needed before joining the main Liberation Army force at Toran Castle.

name: Seek Valley
description: Seek Valley (シークの谷 Shīku no Tani) is a location that appears in Suikoden. It is a valley located to the southwest reaches of the Toran region.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Seek Valley is a relatively obscure and mostly unexplored area as it surrounded by steep mountains and is readily accessible only by dragon, as such the Knights of the Dragon's Den are one of the few groups with any knowledge of the valley at all.

Located to the southwest of the Dragon Knight's Domain, beautiful crystals grow there. However, it is also very dangerous and no person who has ever gone there in attempt to remove said crystals have ever returned. The rare plant Moonlight Herb also grows in clusters there, deep within the valley.

While searching for the Moonlight Herb, McDohl encountered Ted for the final time before his death here, under the control of Windy. The famed blacksmith, Mace had retired to Seek Valley to live out his days in isolation until persuaded by his disciples to join the Toran Liberation Army.

name: Seigal Gate
description: Seigal Gate (セイガレ門, Seigare Mon) is a location in Suikoden III. It is the entrance gate to Vinay del Zexay.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: Seigal Gate is the city gate for Vinay del Zexay. The people of the city take pride in its robust walls as well as the iron gate, both said to be comparable in strength to those of a fortress.

name: Seika
description: Seika (セイカ, Seika) is a village that appears in Suikoden. Seika is a small village located just to the south of the Fortress of Kwaba.

facts.label: Nation
facts.value: Scarlet Moon Empire (until 457), Toran Republic (457 onwards)

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Seika is a quiet hamlet in the northern Gouran region, located just south of the Fortress of Kwaba. Some Seika residents occasionally go to Kaku to fish but its main source of food was flour.

This rustic village is most known for being the location of the school of Mathiu Silverberg. Opinions on Mathiu's school in the village was mixed. A number of residents resented Mathiu taking in orphans and the like, regarding them as little more than criminals.

These opinions would change after Apple and Shu helped save the life of Aya, a local villager. The school would become more celebrated and residents gratefully helped the school.

Several early members of the Toran Liberation Army also lived in, or were visiting, Seika when they were recruited, including Antonio, Marie, Onil, and Sheena.

name: Senan
description: Senan (セナン Senan) is a region of the Toran Republic, and its predecessor, the Scarlet Moon Empire. It is the site of the nation's northern defenses.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Senan is a mostly empty region, lying to the north of the Sadhna Stream. It is defended internally by the Northern Checkpoint to the south. Moravia Castle provides the bulk of the region's defenses, with the Fortresses of Duha and Rakan also providing security to the region.

The region, along with Dana, is usually referred to simply as "the north" or the "Karakas Desert" in the Toran Republic and as "Senan" in the City-States of Jowston. The Karakas Desert is located in Senan and makes up about 70% of the region. Outside of the Karakas Desert, Senan tends to have a mild, temperate climate.

The walls that extend into the desert have existed before the founding of the Scarlet Moon Empire. It is theorized that they were built by the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia. The infamous Red Wind blows through a large portion of the region.

name: Ship Graveyard
description: The Ship Graveyard (船の墓場, Fune no Hakaba) is a location seen in Suikoden V. It is an area of sea near Nirva Island containing the wreckage of many vessels.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This area of the sea is so called because the amount of ship wreckage in the area was reminiscent of a graveyard.

During the Sun Rune War, the prince, as well as Yahr and Nelis from the Island Nations Federation discovered a functioning Rune Cannon aboard the wreckage Island Nations vessel Kerzalik. The group would end up destroying it to ensure no one could gain its destructive power.

name: Sindar Ruins
description: The Sindar Ruins (シンダル遺跡, Shindaru Iseki) are a location in Suikoden II. They are an expensive set of ruins located to the rear of the White Deer Inn.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Ruins of the legendary wandering Sindar could be found all over the world. It was often said that various treasures and advanced knowledge lay deep within them. In the Dunan region, the ruins by the White Deer Inn, near Muse City were especially acclaimed.

During the early stages of the Dunan Unification War, the hero accompanied Alex on an expedition into these ruins. There they discovered a fearsome Double Head as well as a healing herb that came in handy after their return when they discovered that Alex's wife Hilda had fallen ill.

name: Sol-Falena
description: Sol-Falena (ソルファレナ, Sorufarena) is a location seen in Suikoden V. It is the Imperial Capital of the Queendom of Falena.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Sol-Falena is the Imperial Capital of the Queendom of Falena and is located in the country's north. It is famous for the Sun Palace where the Falenan Royal Family traditionally lives. The city's east was home to many of the city's available facilities and businesses while the western side of the city held the residential areas. The city's center leads to a port dedicated to the needs of the royal family.

Sol-Falena was not always the nation's capital. Until SY 212 Stormfist was in fact the capital of Falena, but it was moved by Queen Khorshed to Sol Falena in that year and since then the city has grown from strength to strength.

name: Soniere Prison
description: Soniere Prison (ソニエールの監獄, Soniēru no Kangoku) is a location that appears in Suikoden. It is a prison located in the southern Kunan region.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Soniere Prison was a prison for criminals and political dissidents located in the Kunan region, around five days travel from the Fortress of Garan.

Built in SY 347, it was constructed by the prisoners of Gaya Prison, which had previously stood in the same location. It was considered very tough to break into, with Milich Oppenheimer describing it as impregnable.

Despite this, it has been destroyed twice in the past. It was once razed to the ground by the City-States of Jowston when, during an invasion, the Imperial Army attempted to use the prison as a fortification.

The prison was razed again when the thief Wilborn caused a riot to break out and the prison to be set ablaze. Over 300 prisoners and guards died in the ensuing violence and inferno. Wilborn was eventually recaptured by the Imperial Army and publicly executed.

Following this, a system of several layers of iron doors was implemented. As such, the prison was able to accommodate both minor and heavy criminals though more minor offenders were usually sent to the Banner Mines.

During the Gate Rune War, Liukan was taken to Soniere in order to ensure that an antitoxin to Antoinette could be brewed, but the Toran Liberation Army managed to break the doctor out, even with their ruse to sneak in being detected.

name: South Window City
description: South Window City (サウスウィンドゥ市 Sausuwindou Shi) is a location that appears in Suikoden II. It is a historical city which lies to the south of Lake Dunan.

facts.label: Nation
facts.value: Dunan Monarchy (until 252), City-States of Jowston (314-460), Dunan Republic (460 onwards)

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
South Window City is the principal city of South Window Principality. It is one of the oldest cities in Dunan and was once the capital of the Dunan Monarchy. As such, it was usually treated with a measure of respect within the City-States of Jowston. Its current mayoral system was established following the civil war that saw the Dunan Monarchy collapse in SY 252.

Its main industries are tourism and commerce, with the city crowded year-round by tourists and traders. The carefully cultivated bonsai have become something of a feature of the city and the plaza outside the city hall is open for the peace and relaxation of its citizens.

Six months after the destruction of North Window, South Window City would receive a zombie head from Two River City, claiming to be under the influence of Neclord and pushing the two city-states to the brink of war. However, it would be revealed that the message accompanying the head would be a forgery, commissioned by Kou Long and his compatriots to spark a war and make a profit in the arms trade. The plot uncovered; tensions would quickly resolve.

During the Dunan Unification War, the city suffered many attacks from the Highland Kingdom and was occupied for a time. Following the war, however, South Window has followed a path of steady recovery.

name: Southern Wharf
description: The Southern Wharf (南の船着場, Minami no Funatsuba) is a location in Suikoden V. It is a small wharf that can be accessed by the player to reach Lordlake in Chapter 3.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
One of several wharfs built on the Feitas River throughout Falena, this wharf was located north of the town of Lordlake.

When the Prince helped Shigure and Sagiri investigate the Lordlake Uprising, Barows Soldiers would attempt to stop them at this wharf, only to be soundly defeated.

name: Stonecutter's Field
description: The Stonecutter's Field (石切りが原, Ishikirigahara) is a location that appears in Suikoden IV. It is a large plain on Nay Island.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These plains lie on the eastern parts of Nay Island and seperate the human settle from the Nay-Kobold Settlement. Monsters roam the area but as the area holds abundant ore reserves, mining operations are in action around the clock regardless.

At the eastern end of the plains there is a suspension bridge that leads directly to the Nay-Kobold Settlement.

name: Stormfist
description: Stormfist (ストームフィスト, Sutōmufisuto) is a location seen in Suikoden V. It is a very large city in the western part of the Queendom of Falena.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Stormfist is the second-largest city in the entire Queendom of Falena and had been ruled for the last century by the Godwin Faction after they took over the city and surrounding lands from the House of Aethelbald. Even further in the past, Stormfist was in fact the capital of Falena, but it was moved by Queen Khorshed to Sol-Falena in SY 233.

The Stormfist Arena, famous for its gladiator battles and the hosting arena for the Sacred Games was a prominent symbol of Stormfist's importance to the nation. To the rear of the arena, the towering Godwin Castle surveys the entire city.

During the time of the Sun Rune War, the town was sharply divided into two districts by Marscal Godwin, forcing the poorer resident into a slum-style "New Town", while the nobles continued to enjoy the amenities of the main city itself.

name: Stormfist Arena
description: Stormfist Arena (ストームフィストの闘技場, Sutōmufisuto no Tōgi-ba) is a location seen in Suikoden V. It is a large gladiatorial arena located in the north of the city of Stormfist.
originalName: ストームフィストの闘技場

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: Stormfist Arena served as the symbol of Stormfist as the venue for the Sacred Games. As an arena for gladiatorial battles, it held an infirmary as well as a guest room exclusively for royalty and Queen's Knights. Betting matches between the nobility's prized gladiators were once common in this arena.

name: Sun Palace
description: The Sun Palace (太陽宮, Taiyō Miya) is a location seen in Suikoden V. Located in the nation's capital of Sol-Falena, it is the primary royal residence and diplomatic center of the Queendom of Falena.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The Sun Palace served as the residence of the Falenan royal family and its Queen's Knights. It was also the political center of the Queendom. Normally a residence, it also held official functions such as coronation ceremonies and other diplomatic events. The Sun Rune was enshrined in the seal chamber on the top floor.

name: Tablet of Stars
description: The Tablet of Stars (石板の地, Sekiban no Ji, Site of the Stone Tablet) is a location in Suikoden III. It contains the Stone Tablet of Promise for that game.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This place is located in the immediate vicinity of Budehuc Castle. It is lined with rock columns, with a tablet placed in the center of the formation.

This slate is the Stone Tablet of Promise. The names of the 108 Stars who were involved in the War of the Champions are engraved on this tablet.

name: Teien
description: Teien (テイエン, Teien) is a town that appears in Suikoden. Known as Lac Virginite (ラック・ビイルジニテ, Rakku Biirujinite) for a short period of time, Teien is a medium sized town in the region of Kunan.

facts.label: Nation
facts.value: Scarlet Moon Empire (until 457), Toran Republic (457 onwards)

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Teien is a port town which lies on the southwestern banks of Lake Toran in eastern Kunan, making it a natural connection for people crossing the lake from Kaku.

During the Gate Rune War, Teien was renamed "Lac Virginite" (Lake of the Maiden) as per General Milich Oppenheimer's orders.

However, the new name proved both unpopular and difficult to remember. When the Toran Liberation Army freed Lac Virginite, the name was quickly changed back to Teien.

name: Tenzan Pass
description: Tenzan Pass (天山の峠, Tenzan no Tōge) is a location in Suikoden II. It is a mountain pass that divides the northern Highland Kingdom and Kyaro Town in the south.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This mountain pass seprated the Highland Kingdom capital of L'Renouille from Kyaro Town. As such, it was a strategically important point within Highland. The Tenzan River began in these mountains and flowed southwest into Lake Dunan.

Tenzan Pass was also the location of the Dunan Unification War's catalyst. It is where the Unicorn Brigade soldiers were slaughtered by Luca Blight as a pretense to war against the City-States of Jowston.

The Unicorn Brigade camp was located in the middle of the pass and north of the camp was "the promised place" where Jowy and his best friend promised to meet again.

At the end of the war, the two friends would meet once more here. It is said that here they settled their fate by the power of the Rune of Beginning.

name: Tigermouth Village
description: Tigermouth Village (虎口の村 Kokō no Mura) is a location that appears in Suikoden II. It is a small village which lies on the western side of the Lampdragon Mountain. It has a population of 1,300.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Tigermouth is a small village located at the foot of Lampdragon Mountain, on the border between Two River and Tinto Principality. It is very similar to Drakemouth Village, which lies on the opposite side of the pass, although it is slightly more populated than its eastern equivalent.

Sierra Mikain spent some time in Tigermouth while searching for Neclord and was almost attacked by villagers who assumed she was in league with Neclord's undead forces.

name: Tinto City
description: Tinto City (ティント市, Tinto Shi) is a location in Suikoden II. It is the governing city of Tinto Principality. Its population is 11,000.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Tinto City is a renowned mining town that was built against the slope of a steep mountain. Its main industry is in extracting iron ore, water gems, and other precious metals from the Tinto Mines. The sale of weapons and armor produced from these resources generates a large profit for the city and allows it to fund a powerful military.

As long as the city has existed, the head of the mining guild has also served as mayor of the city. Membership of the guild required the recommendation of at least two guild members. Usually a set of parents would nominate their own children to become members of the guild. As a mining city, those possessed of physical strength were highly regarded.

Gustav Pendragon served as mayor during the Dunan Unification War and was Tinto's last before the foundation of the Tinto Republic.

name: Tinto Mines
description: The Tinto Mines (ティント坑道, Tinto Kōdō) are a location in Suikoden II.

name: Toran Lake Castle
description: Toran Lake Castle (トラン湖の城, Toran Mizūmi no Shiro) is a location in Suikoden. It is an abandoned castle located on Lake Toran.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This ancient castle floats in the middle of Lake Toran. It was formerly the headquarters of the Scarlet Moon Imperial Navy, but the castle was abandoned with the construction of the Floating Fortress of Shasarazade.

Since then, the lake castle had been occupied several times by different pirate and bandits groups but were repelled by the Imperial Navy each time. Eventually, the Zombie Dragon was placed here in order to ensure its abandonment.

However, when the Toran Liberation Army were able to defeat the Zombie Dragon, they would take the castle and use it as their main headquarters, in the form of Toran Castle.

name: Toto Village
description: Toto Village (トトの村, Toto no Mura) is a location that appears in Suikoden II. It is a small village located in eastern Muse Principality.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Toto Village lies on the north-eastern shores of Lake Dunan. Although the population is small, with only 800 residents, and the village has no major industry, its villagers are generally regarded as cheerful and have a very good reputation from passing travelers.

The rear of the village contains an important shrine, protected by the villagers. The door of the shrine is only opened once a year during the village's festival.

At the beginning of the Dunan Unification War, the village was razed by the invading Highland Army turning it into a barren landscape. There, Jowy Atreides and his companion entered the shrine and received the Black Sword and Bright Shield Rune.

name: Two River City
description: Two River City (トウーリバー市, Toūribā Shi) is a location in Suikoden II. It is the largest city in the City-States of Jowston with a population of 17,000.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Two River City is a major city, built on the banks of the Lana River. The city is comprised of three distinct races, humans, kobolds and wingers, each of whom make up a third of Two River's three houses of parliament. This arrangement was often inefficient due to the tension and strife between the three districts.

Six months after the destruction of North Window, Two River City would receive what appeared to be a sealed declaration of war from South Window City, believing Neclord to have taken Two River, pushing the two city-states to the brink of violence. However, it would be revealed that the seal would be a forgery, commissioned by Kou Long and his compatriots to spark a war and make a profit in the arms trade. The plot uncovered, tensions would quickly resolve.

In the past, the Winged Horde contingent were discriminated against due to their recent arrival in the city but following the actions of wingers such as Chaco and Sid in the Dunan Unification War, they seem to be receiving their due recognition.

name: Two River Sewers
