description: The Two River Sewers (トウーリバーの地下鉄, Toūribā no Chikatetsu or トウーリバーの地下水路, Toūribā no Chikasuiro) is a location in Suikoden II. It is a large sewer system running beneath Two River City.
originalName: トウーリバーの地下鉄

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This waterway was built under Two River City, accessible from the Winged Horde district of the city. It was said that the sewers had sanitation and pest issues and a giant rat monster, the Pest Rat, lurked in its depths. It was also the hiding place of Sid when he supposedly left Two River. Though what he actually did there was a mystery.

The leader of the New Alliance Army would use these sewers to sneak into the kobold section of Two River and confront Ridley Wizen for his refusal to cooperate with Makai. Since then, the sewer's issues have been reduced.

name: Underground Passage
description: The Underground Passage (地下通路, Chika Tsūro) is a location in Suikoden V. It is an old waterway underneath Stormfist.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This secret passage connected the basement of Stormfist Arena with the Western Woods as well as Stormfist's new town district. It appeared to be very old and its existence was generally unknown.

Shoon discovered the passage and used to it alert the Prince of possible danger during the Sacred Games. Egbert was also aware of the passage and resided there for quite some time.

Following the Sacred Games, the passage was blocked up by the Godwin Faction but would be reopened by Shoon and his fellow gladiators so as to allow the Prince's party to infiltrate Stormfist.

name: Underground Ruins
description: The Underground Ruins (地下遺跡, Chika Iseki) are a location in Suikoden V. These ruins become available when recruiting Jeane but the lower levels are only available when Eresh is in the player's party.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
At the bottom of the Big Hole lay the ruins of this underground city, dominated by a large temple in its center.

The city was seemingly that of the Ancient Armes Dynasty, who lived in these lands long before even the Sindar, although details are shrouded in mystery. 

The city was made up of two and three story buildings, none of which had entrances. All the walls facing south are blackened, as thought they were all burned simultaneously by a powerful force, though mysteriously no structural damage seems to have been inflicted.

Inside the temple lay a huge altar. Jeane once obtained something from this altar and, later, Eresh was able to open the altar, revealing a set of passages beneath the ruins.

name: Village of the Dwarves
description: The Village of the Dwarves (ドワーフの村, Dowaafu no Mura, Dwarf Village) is a village that appears in Suikoden. It is the home of the dwarf community of the Toran region.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Village of the Dwarves in the mountainous area of the Great Forest located across the Dwarf Trail and as such it was considered rare for non-dwarf races such as humans to make the journey successfully. It was the largest known dwarf settlement.

The village is dominated by a windmill, said to generate electricity, which is based on the dwarves' ancient teachings. The village sustains itself mostly by selling armor made from iron ore and other precious metals. It is said that the village is a perfect example of the dwarves' advanced architectural skills.

Despite a lack of knowledge about humans, some humans do come to the village, such as Meese, who came to refine his blacksmith techniques. Humans also sometimes acted as intermediaries for the dwarves in their dealing with other races as their exacting craftsmanship sometimes prevented them from brokering their own deal efficiently.

name: Village of the Elves
description: The Village of the Elves (エルフの村, Erufu no Mura) is a location that appears in Suikoden. It is a small village located in the Great Forest.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Village of the Elves is located in the southern reaches of the Toran Republic, to the rear of the Forest of Moran. The entire village was located on top of trees, linked together by buildings of pale green. The elves took great pride in its mystic beauty.

The village was destroyed by Kwanda Rosman during the Gate Rune War. Appointed to the area, he began suppressing the kobold and elven population before wiping out the village with the Burning Mirror. Only a small number survived the disaster.

Following the war, former Toran Liberation Army officer Kirkis has been leading reconstruction efforts. However, it seems unlikely that the village can ever recover its past beauty.

name: Village of the Hidden Rune
description: The Village of the Hidden Rune (隠された紋章の村, Kakusareta Monshō no Mura) is a village that appears in Suikoden. It was the hidden home of the Rune of Life and Death until its destruction.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Village of the Hidden Rune was a secluded mountain community which guarded the Rune of Life and Death. The village's purpose is hidden from outsiders, who are encouraged to leave as soon as possible. It was the hometown of Ted.

Some 300 years past, the village was attacked by Windy, Neclord and Yuber who were searching for the Soul Eater. The village was decimated during the conflict but the rune did not fall into Windy's hands. The Village Chief passed the rune to his grandson, Ted, and distracted Windy long enough for the child to escape.

The village would be razed by Yuber in the ensuing confrontation between Windy and the Village Chief, leaving the settlement decimated and seemingly abandoned. These events would be seen by members of the Toran Liberation Army who were temporarily transported to the past by the Celestial Sword during the Gate Rune War.

name: Vinay del Zexay
description: Vinay del Zexay (ビネ・デル・ゼクセ, Bine Deru Zekuse) is a location in Suikoden III. It is the capital city of the Zexen Federation.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The capital of the Zexen Federation, Vinay del Zexay boasts a modern city skyline as well as a beautiful harbor. The town was established by Gezel Hyatt who built a fortune in trade, developing a harbor and establishing a large trade route that would eventually to grow to stretch from the east to the Dunan Republic and west, across the ocean.

Situated by the sea, the city is built on the hills overlooking it. As such, the city boasts of a great few from a slope near the Seigal Gate. The gate, along with the magnificent and robust walls it forms part of, protects the city, including the bustling streets that spread across the vibrant port, teeming with businesses. These qualities have allowed Vinay del Zexay to flourish and it has grown into one of the preeminent cities on the northern continent.

name: Warriors' Village
description: Warriors' Village (戦士の村, Senshi no Mura) is a village that appears in Suikoden. It is a strongly independent village which, befitting its name, carries a strong martial tradition.

facts.label: Nation
facts.value: Scarlet Moon Empire (until 457), Toran Republic (457 onwards)

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Warriors' Village, located in the Lorimar region, was originally founded around SY 20 by Clift the Crusader following a conflict with the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia known retrospectively as the "Final Battle". Despite the name of that conflict, the village had been involved in several conflicts even before its participation in the Gate Rune War. While Zorak held pride in the history and traditions of the village, others held a different view, dismissing it as a backwater.

The village came under the supervision of the vampire Neclord during the Gate Rune War and the two soon came into conflict over Neclord's desire to take Tengaar of the Warriors' Village to be his wife. The intervention of the Toran Liberation Army and their defeat of Neclord led to the Warriors' Village allying with the Liberation Army for the remainder of the war.

name: Water Vein
description: The Water Vein (地下水脈, Chika Suimyaku) is a location in Suikoden V. It is a series of tunnels created by the cave dwarves. Although it is mandatory in Chapter 5, a small portion in Yashuna Village can be accessed during Chapter 4.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This underground water vein was created as a result of the cave dwarves mining. One such tunnel ran from the Dwarf Camp all the way to the Deep Twilight Forest and could be accessed by Gunde's minecart.

Another part of the water vein stretched to the hot springs water source for Yashuna Village in the east. It was damaged by the mining, threatening the survival of the inns who relied on the hot springs but the source would be repaired by the apologetic dwarves before the end of the Sun Rune War.

name: Waterfall Basin
description: The Waterfall Basin (滝つぼ, Takitsubo) is a location in Suikoden V. It is a large dam built in the Queendom of Falena.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This waterfall basin was located upstream of Beaver Lodge on a river that fed into Ceras Lake. The river could be crossed back and forth through a series of stepping stones.

It was known to the beaver folk as the purported home of the legendary 'Boss', the giant turtle named Genoh. Richard spent some time in the waterfall basin, contemplating its waters after being ordered to go train under a waterfall by Mueller.

name: West Palace
description: The West Palace (西の離宮, Nishi no Rikyū) is a location seen in Suikoden V. It is a royal residence in the Queendom of Falena and hosts the Twilight Rune, one of the nation's three runes.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The West Palace was located west of the capital city of Sol-Falena. The Twilight Rune was enshrined here, juxtaposing itself with the East Palace where the Dawn Rune was located.

The Twilight Rune was taken from the palace by the Godwin Faction during the Sun Rune War and given first to Alenia and later, Sialeeds.

name: Western Woods
description: The Western Woods (西の林, Nishi no Hayashi) is a location in Suikoden V. It is a small dungeon-type area located immediately outside Stormfist.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
These woods were located just outside Stormfist. A secret Underground Passage hidden in the brush connected these woods to the Stormfist Arena.

The Prince and Shoon would use this passage to pursue a cadre of suspicious men before the Sacred Games. The woods were also used a hideout by thugs who had kidnapped Marina in an attempt to thwart Belcoot's efforts to win the contest.

Towards the end of the Sun Rune War, the woods were used again, this time to infiltrate the arena.

name: White Deer Inn
description: The White Deer Inn (白鹿亭 Shiroshika Tei) is a location that appears in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The White Deer Inn is a small roadside inn located at the edge of a forest to the east of Muse City. Although small, a warm meal and a soft bed are assured to all guests here thanks to the red-carpet hospitality of Hilda, the inn's proprietress.

The Sindar Ruins that lie behind the inn has made it quite popular with tourists in recent years, especially those who want to go off the beaten track and have some stories to bring home.

Alex was technically also an innkeeper there, though he was often away on treasure hunts at the ruins, leaving Hilda to practically run the place by herself.

name: Withered Forest
description: The Withered Forest (枯れた森, Kareta Mori) is a location in Suikoden V. It is the first dungeon-type area that the player visits in the game.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This withered forest, which once surrounded Lordlake, was dried up the power of the Sun Rune following the Lordlake Uprising. There is no evidence of their former lush greenery and starving beasts now prowl the remains, making it a dangerous location.

Toma once braved this forest in an attempt to find water and was attacked by a pack of starving Ulse. He would be rescued by the Prince, Lyon, Sialeeds, and Georg.

name: Yashuna Village
description: Yashuna Village (ヤシュナ村, Yashuna-mura) is a location in Suikoden V. It is a tourist village in the eastern reaches of the Queendom of Falena.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Yashuna is a tourist village located in eastern Falena that has a calm, idyllic atmosphere to it.

The village is also famous for its hot springs and many travelers come from far and wide to recuperate or just relax at these hot springs. One inn, the Tricolor Inn, in particular is renowned for its hot spring baths.

name: Yaza Plain
description: The Yaza Plain (ヤザ平原, Yaza Heigan) is a location in Suikoden III. It is a grass plain located in northern Zexen.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The Yaza Plain lies in the northern territories of the Zexen Federation. The roads that run through the plain form an intersection, leading to Budehuc Castle to the north, Brass Castle to the south and, after crossing into the Grasslands, to the Great Hollow to the east.

name: Zexen Forest
description: Zexen Forest (ゼクセンの森, Zekusen no Mori) is a location in Suikoden III. It is a forest located in the Zexen Federation.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Zexen Forest is a forest which spreads from the Zexen capital of Vinay del Zexay to its eastern frontier at Brass Castle.

The forest is not very dense so beautiful, bright sunshine often passes through the trees, brightening up the route through the woods.

name: 100 Kobold Attack
description: The 100 Kobold Attack (コボルト１００人攻撃, Koboruto 100-ri Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This attack was used by Gengen and Gabocha. Gengen slashes with his sword and Gabocha attacks with his slingshot.

On rare occasions, an additional hundred kobolds appeared after the two attacked, charging through the enemy. This breathtaking sight showed the power of the kobolds' unity.

name: Adonis Attack
description: The Adonis Attack (美青年攻撃, Biseinen Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Adonis Attack is a flashy, high-speed assault by Fred Maximillian, Franz, and Futch. The trio streaks across the battlefield in a cross formation, striking with precision before striking a dramatic heroic pose. Though it enrages the enemy, it lands a devastating single-target blow.

A stylish yet risky move, charm and fury combined.

name: Adonis Attack (Inverse)
description: The Adonis Attack (Inverse) (美青年攻撃（裹）, Biseinen Kōgeki (Ura)) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Adonis Attack (Inverse) is a fierce, high-speed assault by Sgt. Joe, Bazba, and Ruby. The trio strikes with blinding speed before striking a triumphant pose. Usable only once per battle, it deals massive damage and has a high chance of silencing the enemy.

A dazzling yet brutal display of non-human flair.

name: Alma Kinan Attack
description: The Alma Kinan Attack (アルマキナン攻撃, Aruma Kinan Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This unite attack was used by the Alma Kinan archer duo of Yumi and Yuiri. This attack would involve the two firing a barrage of arrows at all enemies on the battlefield. The individual power of each attack was low, but as it hit every opponent, its accumulated damage could be quite high regardless.

name: Armes Greeting
description: The Armes Greeting (アーメス, Āmesu) is a Coop Attack in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This unite attack was used by Shula Valya's adjutants, Sharmista and Nifsara. With this combination attack, Nifsara would attack the enemy with a series of spear strikes while Sharmista lined up a devastating shot, throwing her chakrams the moment Nifsara moved aside. Though the two had very different personalities, their teamwork and coordination were unmatched.

name: Arrow Storm
description: The Arrow Storm (エルフ, Erufu, Elf) attack is a Coop Attack in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The elves Isato and Urda, skilled with bows, collaborate in this coop attack. They simultaneously draw their bows and shower the enemy with countless arrows. Finally, they channel all their energy into their arrows and unleash a powerful finishing blow.

name: Azure Charge
description: The Azure Charge (ライドオン・青, Raidōn・Ao, Ride On・Blue) attack is a Coop Attack in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A cooperative attack available only to partnered pairs within the Feitas Dragon Cavalry. Rahal rides his dragon horse Flail and launches a coordinated charge into enemy lines. While Flail is left off-balance after the attack, its power is said to be overwhelming.

This skill can only be performed by members of the Feitas Dragon Cavalry and their mounts, who are bound by a deep, unbreakable bond.

name: Bandit Attack
description: The Bandit Attack (山賊攻撃, Sanzoku Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This cooperative attack was used by the Lampdragon Bandits; Lo Wen, Koyu, and Gijimu.

Gijimu and Koyu would attack simultaneously, and then Lo Wen would follow up with her ball and chain. It is said that many enemies were unable to withstand the trio attack and were knocked down as a result.

name: Bandit Attack
description: The Bandit Attack (山賊攻撃, Sanzoku Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This technique was used by heads of the Mt. Seifu bandits, Varkas and Sydonia. Varkas unleashes a powerful attack, knocking their target into the air, allowing Sydonia the opportunity to teleport to their location and knock them back down to earth with a finishing blow.

name: Beast Tamer
description: The Beast Tamer (らいどおん, Raidōn, Ride On) attack is a Coop Attack in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A cooperative attack performed by childhood friends Norma and Ernst. Norma mounts Ernst in his leopard form and charges straight toward the enemy.

As this is an unfamiliar method of attack for the pair, using it leaves Ernst off-balance and unable to regain his posture.

name: Beastmaster Attack
description: The Beastmaster Attack (モンスター使い攻撃, Monsutā Dzukai Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Members
facts.value: Badeaux and any 2 of Abizboah, Feather, Rulodia, Siegfried

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This cooperative attack was used by Badeaux, leading two of his beast allies (Siegfried, Rulodia, Feather, and Abizboah).

In this attack, Badeaux would order his allies to charge the enemy, driving them into a frenzy in the process. This technique showcased Badeaux's bonds with his bestial comrades.

name: Beat' Em Up Attack
description: The Beat' Em Up Attack (ボコボコ攻撃, Bokoboko Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A cooperative attack between the martial arts duo of Pahn and Ronnie Bell. Pahn charges forward to deliver a flurry of blows to the opponent while Ronnie Bell charges and releases a shock wave.

It was an attack that boasted tremendous destructive power, but it was a dangerous technique that caused Pahn to lose his balance after receiving the recoil of Ronnie's blast.

name: Beauty Attack
description: The Beauty Attack (美女攻撃, Bijo Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Members
facts.value: Any 3 of Karen, Kasumi, Lorelai, Rina

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This cooperative attack was performed by a combination of three of four women known for their beauty: Rina, Kasumi, Karen, and Lorelai.

When the trio step forward, a pink mist appears and countless hearts flow forth from the group, discouraging the enemy from fighting. The hearts deal a small amount of damage to all enemies but the fragrance and beauty of the trio had a high chance of putting the enemies to sleep also.

The addition of a Sexy Wink made this attack even more potent.

name: Beauty Attack
description: The Beauty Attack (美女攻撃, Bijo Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden.

facts.label: Members
facts.value: Any 3 of Cleo, Eileen, Sonya, and Valeria

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A powerful attack used by the Toran Liberation Army's most acclaimed beauties; Valeria, Sonya Shulen, Eileen, and Cleo.

By striking a pose that exuded sex appeal, they were able to charm the entire enemy, discouraging them from fighting, causing damage and even lulling them to sleep.

name: Beaver Rush
description: The Beaver Rush (ビーバー５, Bībā Fuaibu) attack is a Coop Attack that appears in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: A cooperative attack by the five beaver siblings. Each sibling uses their proud teeth to gnaw away at a single enemy. Occasionally, one beaver bites with such ferocious intensity that the enemy perishes on the spot. However, due to exhaustion following the attack, the beavers find it difficult to maintain their balance.

name: Best Effort Attack
description: The Best Effort Attack (せいいっばい攻撃, Seībbai Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Best Effort Attack is a joint strike by Thomas and Cecile. They charge at the enemy and unleash a rapid series of blows, dealing greater-than-normal damage. With luck, they can hit even harder, though Thomas loses his balance in the process.

This attack showcases the courage of the two.

name: Bird Attack
description: The Bird Attack (バード攻撃, Bādo Kōgeki, Bard Attack) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This bardic attack was a harmonious assault by Nei, Toppo, and Shabon. The trio unleashes sound waves through their music, damaging all enemies while cleansing status ailments and magic effects from both allies and foes. 

A masterful blend of art and combat, honed through their street performance synergy. More than just an attack, it functioned as a battlefield reset.

name: Blacksmith Attack
description: The Blacksmith Attack (かじ屋攻撃, Kaji-ya Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden.

facts.label: Members
facts.value: Any 4 of Maas, Mace, Meese, Moose, and Mose

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This attack combines the power of four of the five blacksmiths: Maas, Meese, Moose, Mose and Mace. Each blacksmith takes it in turn to strike their target with their hammers.

name: Bow Attack
description: The Bow Attack (弓攻撃, Yumi Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This cooperative attack was performed by two of three of the New Alliance Army's most potent archers: Stallion, Kinnison, and Ayda.

The two chosen archers would move to the frontline and hit all enemies with a flurry of arrows.

name: Bow Wow Attack
description: The Bow Wow Attack (ワンワン攻撃, Wan Wan Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A cooperative attack used by Shiro, with the aid of either Gengen or Gabocha. The kobold would straddle Shiro, catch his attention with a juicy bone tied to a stick, causing Shiro to chase after it, rushing into the enemy.

Even with his high intelligence, it appears that Shiro could not fight his dog's instincts in this species-transcending attack.

name: Bow-Wow Attack
description: The Bow-Wow Attack (ワンワン攻撃, Wan Wan Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Bow-Wow Attack is whirling canine assault by Koroku and his four companions. The dogs form a spinning ring of fur and fangs before barreling into a single target with multiplied force—though the dizzying maneuver will leave the pups wobbly afterward. 

Proof that teamwork knows no species, this doggedly effective strike rivals any human-led combo.

name: Brotherly Love
description: The Brotherly Love (兄妹, Kyōdai, Siblings) attack is a Coop Attack that appears in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This attack was used by the former bandit siblings Faylon and Faylen.

As Faylen prepares to attack, Faylon suddenly grabs her leg and with a great spin, flings her into the enemy group. It's a dynamic attack unique to siblings. It hits enemies around the landing point, but the downside is that it throws both of them off balance.

name: Buddy Attack
description: The Buddy Attack (おさななじみ攻撃, Osananajimi Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This is an attack performed by the childhood friends Jowy and the hero, and is one of the most powerful unite attacks.

After misleading the enemy's eyes by moving around the enemy in a fast, circular motion, the two take advantage of the momentary lapse in focus to launch an attack that inflicts massive damage on the entire enemy group.

Gordeau was one person who felt this attack's potency when the hero and Jowy reunited temporarily at Rockaxe Castle.

name: Carpenter Attack
description: The Carpenter Attack (大工攻撃, Daiku Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This technique was used by the carpenters Gen and Sansuke. Together, the two jump forward, targeting a single enemy and cutting away at the target with their saws, the same way they would cut wood.

Due to their experience with saws and respect for each other as craftsmen, this attack was very effective.

name: Case Closed
description: The Case Closed (探偵助手, Tantei Joshu, Assistant Detectives) attack is a Coop Attack that appears in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This cooperative attack was unleashed by Shigure and Sagiri, assistants at the Oboro Detective Agency. Shigure slashes the enemy, then leaps back to create distance. As if precisely targeting the opening created, Sagiri's kunai pierces the foe. After the attack, Shigure's manner, muttering "What a pain..." while still competently completing the task, is notably characteristic.

name: Child Attack
description: The Child Attack (子供攻撃, Kodomo Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Child Attack is a coordinated move where Melville and Alanis swing their arms to punch the enemy. It deals heavy damage to a single target but causes Alanis to lose her balance.

Though it appears unimpressive and unreliable, its power is surprisingly strong.

name: Circus Attack
description: The Circus Attack (旅の一座攻撃, Tabi no Ichiza Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This cooperative attack was used by the traveling troupe of Rina, Eilie, and Bolgan. Bolgan would jump forward and use his fire breathing talent at maximum power, with Eilie and Rina supporting him. Afterwards, Bolgan would be left unbalanced due to a lack of oxygen.

It is thought that the trio mastered this technique while traveling around the world as traveling entertainers, and must have made great use of it when encountering monsters.

name: Clan Chief Attack
description: The Clan Chief Attack (族長攻撃, Zokuchō Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The Clan Chief Attack is a coordinated assault by Lucia and Dupa. At their command, a massive force of Lizard Clan and Karaya Clan warriors storms the battlefield, striking all enemies at once.

name: Conceit
description: The Conceit (高飛車, Takabisha, Domineering) attack is a Coop Attack that appears in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A cooperative attack executed by the proud and high-born trio of Isabel, Zerase, and Cathari. Isabel initiates with a single, dismissive glance that belittles the enemy, followed by Cathari and Zerase delivering icy, contemptuous stares. To such a scorned foe, they then administer a powerful, punishing blow.

This technique, while showcasing a terrifyingly elegant aspect of femininity, allows a glimpse of their inherent nobility.

name: Condemnation Attack
description: The Condemnation Attack (断罪攻撃, Danzai Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Condemnation Attack is Ayame and Landis's deadly combo. Ayame immobilizes the enemy from behind while Landis delivers a devastating scythe strike, dealing massive damage to a single target. However, the exertion puts Landis to sleep immediately after. 

A high-risk, high-reward technique that shows their lethal coordination.

name: Copycat Attack
description: The Copycat Attack (にせもの攻撃, Nisemono Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This cooperative attack was attempted by Hoi and the hero he impersonated as part of his grift.

The two leaped forward together and Hoi attempts an attack similar to the hero, but unfortunately fails at the first strike. Because of this, most of the damage inflicted on the enemy is done by the hero alone.

name: Couple Attack
description: The Couple Attack (夫婦攻撃, Fūfu Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A cooperative attack used by the husband and wife team of Lepant and Eileen.

Lepant, wrapped in flames by his wife's magic, rushes at the enemy and attacks with his blazing sword for great damage.

name: Cross Attack
description: The Cross Attack (クロス攻撃, Kurosu Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This attack was used by Viktor and Flik, veterans of the Gate Rune War who excelled in battle. 

Attacking simulanteously and intersecting with each other, this attack inflicted great damage on the enemy. It also had a chance of knocking down the target.

name: Crowd Favorites
description: The Crowd Favorites (闘技奴隸, Tōgi Dorei, Combat Slave) attack is a Coop Attack that appears in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: A cooperative attack performed by the active gladiator slaves, Shoon and Zegai, alongside the former slave, Belcoot. Zegai expertly uses his spear technique to launch the enemy into the air. Shoon then pursues, delivering a series of attacks on the airborne foe. As the enemy plummets back down, Belcoot finishes the assault with a devastatingly heavy slash of his own.

name: Dad & Daughter Attack
description: The Dad & Daughter Attack (親子攻撃, Oyako Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden II. It is also known as the Dad-Daughter Attack.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This unite attack was wielded by the familial pair of Tsai and Tomo. Together, the sharp thrusts of their expert spears inflicted damage to enemies in the front line. Though normally spear attacks were known for penetrating enemy lines, this attack was more focused.

Tsai's skill with a spear was well known by all, but her daughter Tomo's skill was also quite impressive in this attack. The coordination of the two made up for any weakness in the spear as a weapon.

name: Dad and Daughter
description: The Dad and Daughter (親子, Oyako) attack is a Coop Attack that appears in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This attack was used by the Raftfleet father and daughter combo of Logg and Lun. Logg swings his anchor to hit the enemy, followed by Lun stabbing the enemy with her spear. However, the strain of swinging his anchor so much does leave Logg Unbalanced.

name: DoReMi
description: The DoReMi (ドレミ, DoReMi) attack is a Coop Attack that appears in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: A cooperative attack executed by the five DoReMi Elves. The elves soar into the air and spin in unison, bathing the enemy in cascading waves of sound. As the five elves form a perfect circle, they weave together a sublime harmony, giving rise to a blizzard of musical notes.

name: Double Kraken Attack
description: The Double Kraken Attack (ダブル格闘家攻撃, Daburukurāken Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden II.
originalName: ダブルクラーケン攻撃

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This conjugal unite attack was performed by the kraken spouses Rulodia and Abizboah.

The two swung the tentacles from their massive bodies and deals powerful damage to all enemies in the front row.

name: Double Leader Attack
description: The Double Leader Attack (ダブルリーダー攻撃, Dabururīdā Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This is an attack performed by the heroes of two wars; the leader of the New Alliance Army and the leader of the Toran Liberation Army.

Splitting the enemies between them, the two would unleash a barrage of attacks.

name: Double Monster Attack
description: The Double Monster Attack (ダブルクラーケン攻撃, Daburumonsutā Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden II.
originalName: ダブルクラーケン攻撃

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This unite attack was performed by the beast due of Siegfried and Feather.

Feather would grab the enemy and drop them from a great height before Siegfried charged in for a crushing tackle. The two would be left unbalanced from the exertion, however, making it a potent but risky attack.

name: Dragon Cavalry
description: The Dragon Cavalry (竜馬騎兵団, Ryōma Kihei Dan, Dragon Horse Cavalry) attack is a Coop Attack in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: A cooperative attack performed by Roog, Rahal, and Nick, members of the Feitas Dragon Cavalry. Nick begins by firing a shockwave into the enemy ranks. Roog and Rahal then split to the left and right, respectively, shredding the foes with their sword strikes. However, after using this technique, all three combatants are left off-balance.

name: Dragon Knight A Attack
description: The Dragon Knight A Attack (竜騎士攻撃, Ryū Kishi Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A soaring aerial strike by Futch and Sharon. The Dragon Knight duo takes to the skies before plunging down in a devastating dive-slash, unleashing massive damage on a single target. The sheer force of the attack leaves Sharon unsteady upon landing.

A high-risk, high-reward maneuver where dragon and knight strike as one.

name: Dragon Knight Attack
description: The Dragon Knight Attack (竜騎士攻撃, Ryūkishi Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A cooperative attack between the Dragon Knights Milia and Futch.

The two of them jump high in the sky, raise their spears, and swoop down in one fell swoop. Futch only had the experience of an apprentice at the time, but he still kept up with Milia's movements and bravery.

name: Dragon Knight B Attack
description: The Dragon Knight B Attack (竜騎士Ｂ攻撃, Ryū Kishi B Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A breathtaking aerial assault by Futch, Bright, and Sharon. Mounted on Bright, the trio soars skyward before executing a rapid series of dive strikes, hammering all enemies below with relentless force.

A masterful display of airborne devastation where dragon and riders move as a single weapon.

name: Elf Attack
description: The Elf Attack (エルフ攻撃, Erufu Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This attack is led by the elven trio of Stallion, Sylvina and Kirkis. Focusing on a single target, the three pull out their bows and unleash a simultaneous flurry of arrows. With no room for even an inch of deviation, this attack has delivered many a fatal blow to their opponents.

name: Endless Blows
description: The Endless Blows (漢, Otoko, Manly) attack is a Coop Attack that appears in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: Goesch and Yahr team up in this cooperative (?) attack. Yahr can only watch in amazement as Goesch pounds the enemy with a fearful smile and laugh. After the attack, Goesch becomes fatigued and loses his balance from the exertion.

name: Falena's Finest
description: The Falena's Finest (ファレナ, Farena) attack is a Coop Attack that appears in Suikoden V.

facts.label: Members
facts.value: Prince, Galleon, Georg, Kyle, Lyon, Miakis

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A cooperative attack performed by key members of the Imperial City Recapture Army: the Prince and Lyon, alongside the Queen's Knights Georg, Kyle, Miakis, and Galleon.

Georg and Galleon first lead the charge and spearhead the assault on the enemy. This is followed by Kyle and Miakis executing a dazzling sword dance. Finally, after Lyon unleashes a rapid combo, the Prince delivers the decisive finishing blow.

Despite its low power, the amount of people participating in the attack make it strong regardless. The power of this attack increases as the average favorability towards the Prince rises.

name: Family
description: The Family (真・親子, Shin・Oyako, True Parent and Child) attack is a Coop Attack that appears in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A cooperative attack that unites the strength of the parent-child trio. Kisara begins by sweeping all enemies with her own net. Logg then follows by wildly swinging his heavy anchor to further batter the foes.

Finally, Lun delivers the finishing blow with a powerful, explosive harpoon thrust. Following the attack, Logg temporarily loses his posture and is left Unbalanced.

name: Family Attack
description: The Family Attack (兄弟攻撃, Kyōdai Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
With this attack, Nanami and the hero cooperated to unleash a combination of amazing techniques in a breathtakingly coordinated manner, taught by Genkaku . They have been training together since they were children, and as such their combination is perfect.

The attack inflicts severe damage to a single enemy, but Nanami becomes unbalanced after the attack. On rare occasions, Nanami can afford to relax with a cup of tea and some fruit, and her strength is slightly restored from the leisure time.

name: Fancy Lad
description: The Fancy Lad (美青年, Biseinen) attack is a Coop Attack that appears in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A cooperative attack performed by Dinn, Rahal, and Mathias, recognized as handsome young men among the 108 Stars. Dinn and Rahal combine their energy to create a blue sphere, which Mathias then launches at the enemy.

While this is a highly potent coop attack, any enemy that survives being struck by it will enter a state of rage.

name: Fancy Lad Attack
description: The Fancy Lad Attack (美青年攻撃, Yoshi Senin Kougeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This attack was led by Miklotov, Flik, and Camus, recognized as three of the most handsome fellows in the New Alliance Army.

After the three of them pose together, they attack the enemy in a quick, graceful manner. Their charm was so much that some enemies could be instantly defeated, especially by their Invincible Smiles.

name: Fancy Lad Attack
description: The Fancy Lad Attack (美青年攻撃, Yoshi Senin Kougeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden. It was localized as the Pretty Boy Attack in the 1996 localization of Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This attack was led Alen, Glenshiel, and Flik, recognized as three of the most handsome fellows in the Toran Liberation Army.

After the three of them pose together, they attack the enemy in a quick, graceful manner. It is said that those who see the pose are unintentionally mesmerized, regardless of gender.

name: Fate
description: The Fate (縁, En) attack is a Coop Attack in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
More than a decade before reuniting as members of the New Alliance Army in the Dunan Unification War, Georg Prime, Lorelai, Killey, and Viki attempted this coordinated (?) attack. Lorelai and Killey would attempt to attack the enemy, but an ill-timed sneeze from Viki teleports them away. Just as Georg is about to attack, the two reappear and fall from the sky, falling on him. The attack is interrupted, leaving the enemy unharmed.

This unfavorable move further strains the relationship between Killey and Lorelei.

name: Fighter Attack
description: The Fighter Attack (格闘家攻撃, Kakutōka Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This unite attack was used by the martial arts duo of Juan and Emily.

The attack utilized a continuous barrage typical of martial artists. The two would attack the enemy at unbelievable speed, striking them one after another until all were hit. Emily would become unbalanced after the attack.

name: Fisherman Attack
description: The Fisherman Attack (漁師攻撃, Ryōshi Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This attack was used by Yam Koo and Tai Ho, fishermen who had sworn an oath of brotherhood together.

The two would attack the enemy simultaneously in an cross-shaped trajectory. It was powerful, but had a weak point where the two would lose their balance after attacking.

name: Five Squirrel Attack
description: The Five Squirrel Attack (むささび5攻撃, Musasabi Go Kougeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This was the ultimate special technique of the Flying Squirrel Corps. 

After posing together like a team of superheroes, the five flying squirrels would lift the target and fly it far over the horizon. It has a high chance of instantly defeating most targets.

Even large or powerful enemies would normally be immune to such effects can fall to this attack, albeit with a low probability.

name: Flash Attack
description: The Flash Attack (ヒカリ攻撃, Hikari Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A cooperative attack by three bald-headed warriors, Long Chan-Chan, Gantetsu, and Bolgan. This fearsome attack had the trio suddenly emit a glow from their shining heads, dealing great damage to the enemy.

Though amusing it was not without its risk. Allies other than the three who performed the attack would also take damage, unless they came prepared with a pair of sunglasses.

name: Flash Attack
description: The Flash Attack (ヒカリ攻撃, Hikari Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A cooperative attack by three bald-headed warriors, Liukan, Kai, and Fukien. This unique attack had the trio emit flash from their shining heads, dealing great damage to the enemy.

For some reason, this technique seemed to be heavily taxing on the body with all three falling into an unbalanced state afterwards.

name: Flash of Steel
description: The Flash of Steel (女王騎士剣技, Joō Kishi Kenki, Queen's Knights Swordsmanship) attack is a Coop Attack that appears in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A cooperative attack executed by Georg and Kyle, both master swordsmen ranked among the best active Queen's Knights. Kyle initiates the assault with a flowing, rapid-series sword technique that slices the enemy apart.

Immediately following, Georg delivers a swift, decisive iaido strike to sever the enemy's life. It is said that due to the extreme speed of their combined blade work, the enemy perishes instantly without experiencing the agony of death throes.

name: Friends Forever
description: The Friends Forever (幼なじみ, Osananajimi, Childhood Friends) attack is a Coop Attack in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: A cooperative attack unleashed by the three childhood friends: Roog, Rahal, and Miakis. The trio exchange a glance before Rahal and Roog lead the charge, launching successive attacks, and Miakis follows up by striking with her two short swords. Though Miakis left her hometown to become a Queen's Knight, her powerful teamwork with Rahal and Roog remains as effective as ever.

name: Fugitive Hunt
description: The Fugitive Hunt (影, Kage, Shadow) attack is a Coop Attack that appears in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The self-proclaimed master thief Raven and the Oboro Detective Agency assistants, Shigure and Sagiri, team up to unleash this coordinated attack. First, Shigure and Raven launch a barrage of attacks at the enemy, and Sagiri finishes them off with a flurry of kunai.

name: Gadget Attack
description: The Gadget Attack (からくり攻撃, Karakuri Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden III.

facts.label: Damage
facts.value: Damage equal to Gadget Z's Max HP

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This unite attack was used by the trickster Belle and her companion Gadget Z.

At Belle's command, Gadget Z would merge into a giant robot and attacks the entire enemy formation with a powerful beam from the moon. The beam was powered by Gadget Z's life force, more HP he had making for a more powerful attack. Such a massive attack would reduce Gadget Z's HP to 1.

name: Girl Attack
description: The Girl Attack (娘攻擊, Musume Kōgeki, Daughter Attack) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This attack is a heroic legacy strike by Emily, Sanae Yamamoto, and Sharon. Emily launches the enemy skyward with a mighty blow, while Sanae Y. and Sharon follow up with a punishing sword-and-spear combo. Though Emily stumbles from the recoil, the attack deals devastating double damage to a single foe.

A fierce tribute to their warrior bloodlines, descendants of legends from the Gate Rune and Dunan Unification Wars.

name: Groupie Attack
description: The Groupie Attack (おっかけ攻撃, Okkake Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This unorthodox unite attack was used by Nina and Flik. Nina spins herself around by swinging her book belt and attacks an enemy with the centrifugal force of the book. Some speculate that the attack was so powerful due to her love for Flik.

After the attack, Nina cannot stop spinning by herself, forcing Flik to step in a stop her. Usually, Nina's eyes would roll back from dizziness and she would become unbalanced, but on rare occasions, she would become toasty instead.

name: Guardian Attack
description: The Guardian Attack (お守り攻撃, Omamori Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden. It was called the Talisman Attack in the 1996 English localization of Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This attack is used by Gremio and Pahn. In their eagerness to protect their Young Master, both of them jumped forward and attack their enemy in unison, inflicting great damage.

name: Han Attack
description: The Han Attack (漢攻撃, Kan Kōgeki, Man's Han Attack) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This unite attack could be used by any two of the manly group of Wan Fu, Mua, and Leo Gallen. It seems the attack could only be used by the most manly of manly men, a criteria each of the three clearly met.

The chosen two would attack the enemy in a crossing motion, and then deliver a finishing below with the character "漢" appearing in the background. Both of the attackers would become unbalanced after the attack, but would deal a devastating blow to all enemies within range in the process.

name: Heads Up!
description: Heads Up! (いっちゃえーーーー, Itchaeeeee) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This cooperative attack was used by Viki and either Rulodia or Abizboah. Viki teleports the kraken into the sky, causing it to come crashing down on the enemy.

Although clearly powerful and certainly impressive looking, the kraken involved would lose its balance due to the impact of the fall.

name: Husband and Wife Attack
description: The Husband and Wife Attack (美青年攻撃, Meoto Kougeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This unite attack was used by the married pair of Yoshino YamamotoYoshino and Freed Yamamoto. The two face each other, and after Freed salutes and Yoshino nods, they attack the enemy together.

The pair's close relationship is shown with this attack. Yoshino loses her balance afterwards, and Freed's concern is plain upon his face. Due to this, Freed did not like to use this attack often.

name: Island Storm
description: The Island Storm (群島諸国, Guntō Shokoku, Island Nations) attack is a Coop Attack that appears in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This cooperative attack was used by Yahr, Nelis, and Bernadette Egan, all members of the Island Nations Federation Navy. In this attack, Bernadette and Nelis use throwing knives to hold off the enemy, while Yahr approaches and slashes at the enemy. Finally, Yahr, supported by his comrades' throwing knives, delivers the final blow with a surprise aerial attack.

name: Kiss Goodbye
description: The Kiss Goodbye (美女, Bijo, Beauty) attack is a Coop Attack that appears in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A cooperative attack executed by Jeane, Sialeeds, and Nelis, renowned as the most beautiful women among the 108 Stars. Jeane and Nelis first enchant the enemy with their allure, creating an opening. For the finishing blow, Sialeeds then shreds the foe with a heart-wreathed chakram.

This feat is only possible for them, as masters of exuding mature charm and grace.

name: Kiss Goodnight
description: The Kiss Goodnight (新・美女, Shin・Bijo, New Beauty) attack is a Coop Attack that appears in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A cooperative attack woven together by Jeane, Sharmista, and Nelis, each exuding an aura of mature charm. Jeane and Nelis begin by beguiling and distracting the enemy. As the foe falters, Sharmista concludes the assault by unleashing a devastating, charm-infused finishing strike.

Notably, this charm-based power is said to have significant sleep-inducing properties. It is an undeniably bewitching cooperative attack, marked by a flurry of swirling heart motifs.

name: Knight A Attack
description: The Knight A Attack (騎士Ａ攻撃, Kishi A Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This unite attack was used by the Zexen Knights comrades Borus Redrum and Percival Fraulein.

The two attackers would cross each other while unleashing high-speed slashes, inflicting more damage to their opponent than their normal attacks could hope to achieve.

name: Knight Attack
description: The Knight Attack (騎士攻撃, Kishi Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This attack was used by the Matilda Knights Miklotov and Camus who fought side-by-side during the Dunan Unification War.

Together, the two would aim their swords at the enemy and slash at the same time, inflicting great damage. It is said that the two of them sometimes lost their balance due to the ferocity of the attack.

name: Knight B Attack
description: The Knight B Attack (騎士Ｂ攻撃, Kishi B Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This unite attack was used by the Zexen Knights, namely Chris Lightfellow and two of her comrades between Leo Gallen, Borus Redrum, and Percival Fraulein.

This was a horseback attack and so could only be used when the three knights were mounted. The attack dealt a great amount of damage to its target and even had a chance of defeating an enemy instantly.

name: Kobold Attack
description: The Kobold Attack (コボルト攻撃, Koboruto Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This attack was used by Gengen and Gabocha. Gengen slashes with his sword and Gabocha attacks with his slingshot.

On rare occasions, an additional hundred kobolds appeared after the two attacked, charging through the enemy. This breathtaking sight showed the power of the kobolds' unity.

name: Kobold Attack
description: The Kobold Attack (コボルト攻撃, Koboruto Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This technique was used by the kobolds Gon and Kuromimi. Closing in on the enemy together, they would synchronise their movements to strike simultaneously.

name: Kobolds & Pal Attack
description: The Kobolds & Pal Attack (コボルト+1攻撃, Koboruto +1 Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden. It was localized as the Kobold +1 Attack in the 1996 English localization of Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This technique was used by the kobolds Gon and Kuromimi and enhanced by Fu Su Lu. It starts off similar to the regular Kobold Attack but with Fu Su Lu accompanying the kobold duo and delivering a powerful final blow after their joint attack.

name: Leisure Attack
description: The Leisure Attack (くつろぎ攻撃, Kutsurogi Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: With their Family Attack, Nanami and the hero cooperated to unleash a combination of amazing techniques in a breathtakingly coordinated manner, taught by Genkaku. However, on rare occasions, Nanami can afford to slack off with a cup of tea and some fruit, and her strength is slightly restored from the leisure time.

name: Lepant Family Attack
description: The Lepant Family Attack (レパント一家攻撃, Repanto Ikka Kougeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
During the Gate Rune War, the fighting trio of Lepant, Eileen and Sheena used this cooperation attack. Lepant and Sheena would attack with the enemy and Eileen would complete the technique with her throwing darts.

This stunning combination is proof of the family's flair.

name: Lockdown
description: The Lockdown (看守, Kanshu, Warden) attack is a Coop Attack that appears in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This attack was used by Cius and Lelei, the former prison guards of Agate Prison. After a series of attacks from Cius, Lelei follows up with a follow-up attack, culminating in a simultaneous, continuous slash at the enemy.

name: Looks Can Kill
description: The Looks Can Kill (美少年（裹）, Bishōnen (Ura), Pretty Boy (Inverse)) attack is a Coop Attack that appears in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: A cooperative attack performed by Ax, Bergen, and Moroon, each of which are regarded as handsome youths among their races. Bergen leads the charge, followed by Moroon, with Ax delivering the final, powerful blow. However, this is a double-edged attack: if it fails to defeat the enemy, it kindles a raging fury within the foe's heart.

name: Lovely Woman Attack
description: The Lovely Woman Attack (美女攻撃, Bijo Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Lovely Woman Attack is a devastatingly charming assault by Lucia, Estella, and Yuiri. The trio blows synchronized kisses, filling the battlefield with a storm of hearts before crushing enemies under a colossal heart. Deals area damage and carries an even higher chance of putting foes to sleep than the Pretty Girl Attack. 

A mesmerizing, and dangerously effective, display of feminine allure.

name: Loyal Dog Attack
description: The Loyal Dog Attack (忠犬攻撃, Chūken Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A cooperative attack in which Kinnison and Shiro move together. Kinnison would draw his bow against one enemy while Shiro delivered a charge on Kinnison's target as well as any enemies in the same column.

This attack showed the depth of the relationship between man and dog, who communicate with each other through their feelings, not through treats.

name: Manly Attack
description: The Manly Attack (男気攻撃, Otokogi Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This unite attack was used by Rikimaru and Amada. The attack consisted of the two simultaneously launching an assault, slashing all enemies in a straight line, and showering them with spotlights. A blizzard of cherry blossoms would then blow over the heads of the slashed enemies.

The Cup of the Oath would further strengthen the attack by potentially knocking down the surviving enemies.

name: Martial Arts Attack
description: The Martial Arts Attack (格闘家攻撃, Kakutō-ka Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: During the Gate Rune War, the fighting trio of Eikei, Pahn, and Morgan devised this strong cooperation attack. Attacking in unison, the trio deliver so many powerful blows that it lifts their opponents clear off the ground before bringing them down to earth with a crash. This exciting and brilliant technique defeated a myriad of enemies.

name: Master & Pupil Attack
description: The Master & Pupil Attack (師弟攻撃, Shitei Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden. It was localized as the Master Pupil Attack in the 1996 English localization of Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This technique was used by the hero and his fighting master, Kai. This simultaneous attack involved the two rushing their targets faster than the eye could see before spreading out and attacking each enemy individually.

Such a coordinated attack could only come from a pairing of teacher and student and it seems likely that the hero had been drilled in this technique repeatedly in training.

name: Maximilian Attack
description: The Maximilian Attack マクシミリアン攻撃, Makushimirian Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Maximilian Attack is a chivalrous dual strike by Fred Maximillian and Rico, the Knights of Maximillian. Fred leads with an initial blow, followed by Rico's relentless barrage. The assault deals crushing damage, though Rico stumbles from the effort. 

A noble yet reckless display of knightly prowess.

name: Maze of Steel
description: The Maze of Steel (真・女王騎士, Shin・Joō Kishi, True Queen's Knights) attack is a Coop Attack that appears in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A cooperative attack by the Queen's Knights Galleon, Georg, Kyle, and Miakis. The eldest, Galleon, charges forward to lead the assault and spearhead the attack on the enemy. This is followed by a seamless, flowing barrage of sword strikes from Kyle and Miakis. Georg concludes the sequence with his signature iaido slash, delivering the final, fatal blow.

Although these knights were once set against each other during the Godwin Faction's rebellion, the unshaken trust between them remained.

name: Mercenary A Attack
description: The Mercenary A Attack (傭兵Ａ攻擊, Yōhei A Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This mercenary attack was a high-risk assault by Joker and Ace. Joker engulfs Ace in volatile magic energy before Ace plows through enemies in a reckless charge. Deals area damage, but leaves Ace stumbling from the backlash. 

A brutal, no-holds-barred tactic favored by these hardened sellswords.

name: Mercenary B Attack
description: The Mercenary B Attack (傭兵Ｂ攻擊, Yōhei B Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Southern Frontier Defense Force's 12th Unit unleashes their ultimate coordinated strike in this unite attack. Jacques stuns the target with an opening blow, followed by a relentless barrage from Geddoe and the rest of the unit. Though usable only once per battle, it multiplies damage against a single enemy. 

A precision assault showcasing the 12th Unit's legendary teamwork, where every soldier moves as one.

name: Mercenary C Attack
description: The Mercenary C Attack (傭兵Ｃ攻擊, Yōhei C Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This mercenary attack was a brutal pincer strike by Duke and Elaine. Elaine slices through enemies to lock them in place, while Duke follows up with a sweeping greatsword cleave. Though the force leaves Duke unsteady, it carves through all foes in range.

A devastating tandem technique where raw power meets surgical precision.

name: Mercenary D Attack
description: The Mercenary D Attack (傭兵Ｄ攻擊, Yōhei D Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The 14th Unit's ultimate coordinated assault. Elaine initiates with a crippling slash, followed by a rapid chain of strikes from each member, culminating in Duke's earth-shaking finishing blow. Though usable only once per battle, it devastates the primary target with multiplied damage while punishing surrounding foes.

A flawless execution of military discipline and brute force, the 14th Platoon's signature annihilation tactic.

name: Mounted Attack
description: The Mounted Attack (ライドオン攻撃, Raidōn Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden III.

facts.label: Damage
facts.value: 1.2x to target, 0.6x to others in area

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This mounted attack was a high-impact cavalry charge by Sgt. Joe and Shabon. With Shabon riding on his back, Sgt. Joe rams into the enemy at full speed, crushing the primary target and knocking back surrounding foes. The sheer momentum leaves Joe unsteady after impact.

A reckless but devastating maneuver where raw power and teamwork collide.

name: Narcissists Attack
description: The Narcissists Attack (ナルシー攻撃, Narushī Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden II. It was called the Narcissus Attack in the 1999 English localization of Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This unite attack was used by the dear friends, Simone Verdricci and Vincent De Boule. Roses, a testimony to their gentlemanly spirit, would fall from the sky and then the duo would attack all enemies simultaneously while they were bewildered by their elegance.

The Rose Bouquet would further strengthen the attack by potentially rendering the surviving enemies speechless.

name: Ninja Attack
description: The Ninja Attack (忍者攻撃, Ninja Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A cooperative attack used by the Rokkaku Hamlet ninja Mondo and Kasumi. Kasumi would apply a piledriver to the enemy, with Mondo attacking the falling foe.

The impact is said to have knocked many enemies flat. The Secret Writings made this attack even more potent.

name: Ninja Attack
description: The Ninja Attack (忍者攻撃, Ninja Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Ninja Attack is Ayame and Watari's lightning-fast dual strike. Moving in a blur, they slice through all enemies in range with deadly precision. A single-use technique reserved for critical moments. 

A fleeting but devastating display of ninja mastery.

name: Ninja Attack
description: The Ninja Attack (ニンジャ攻撃, Ninja Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A cooperative attack by the three ninjas, Kasumi, Kage, and Fuma. The moment the enemy shows a sign of weakness, Kasumi grabs the enemy, soars high into the air, and then slams them to the ground, followed by Kage and Fuma attacking with shuriken.

This expertly coordinated attack is a true showpiece of the ninja arts.

name: Oops!
description: The Oops! (いっちゃえーーー, Itsuchaeｰｰｰ, Here We Goooo) attack is a Coop Attack in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This cooperative attack was unleashed by Viki, bearer of the Blinking Rune, and the ancient giant turtle Genoh. Viki would use the power of her rune to teleport Genoh above the enemy, with his fall causing shockwaves.

This attack would not only hit the targeted enemy but also nearby enemies, but the shock was so great that Genoh would invariably lose his balance.

name: Opposites Attrack
description: Opposites Attrack (デコボコ攻撃, Dekoboko Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden. This was called the Bumpy Attack in the 1996 English localization of Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This attack is used by Humphrey Mintz and Krin. In this attack, Krin (the 'boko') flings a needle at the enemy before Humphrey (the 'deko') rushes forward, thumping Krin with his sword and sending the thief flying into the target.

name: Perfect Likeness
description: The Perfect Likeness (ニセモノ, Nisemono, Imitation) attack is a Coop Attack that appears in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A cooperative attack performed by the Prince and Roy, who serves as his body double. The two unleash a rapid, successive series of strikes on the same target. The flawlessly synchronized movements are only possible because Roy, having once operated as a look-alike for nefarious purposes, can mimic the Prince without error.

However, a weakness exists: after the attack, Roy occasionally falls into a state of jealousy towards the Prince. The attack's power scales with Roy's favorability toward the Prince.

name: Petty Rivals
description: The Petty Rivals (ライバル, Raibaru, Rival) attack is a Coop Attack in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: A cooperative attack performed by lifelong rivals and one-time childhood friends, Lun and Subala. The moment Lun attempts to initiate an attack, Subala interferes and charges at the enemy alone. After the attack, Subala's triumphant demeanor contrasted with Lun's exasperated expression makes this a memorable cooperative technique.

name: Pirate Attack
description: The Pirate Attack (湖賊攻撃, Kozoku Kōgeki, Lake Bandit Attack) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: An attack used by the close trio Leonardo, Anji and Kanak. It involved the three lake pirates diving at the enemy, attacking with a relentless barrage of blows, dealing a great deal of damage.

name: Pretty Boy
description: The Pretty Boy (美少年, Bishōnen) attack is a Coop Attack that appears in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A cooperative attack performed by Roy, Nick, and Richard, noted for their status as cute boys among the 108 Stars. All three frequently appear on Nifsara's enigmatic "ranking list," which is notorious for its focus on beautiful young men.

Nick delivers the initial strike, Roy follows up with his three-section staff, and Richard concludes with a graceful sword technique to finish the enemy.

name: Pretty Boy Attack
description: The Pretty Boy Attack (美少女攻撃, Bishōnen Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A cooperative attack used by the trio of Sasuke, Luc, and Futch.

Futch and Sasuke attack the enemy with their techniques but while doing so Luc launches a magic attack, engulfing everyone. As a result, all enemies are damaged, but so are Sasuke and Futch.

As a result, all three would become unfriendly towards each other for a time, making things a little awkward for the battle party.

name: Pretty Girl
description: The Pretty Girl (美少女, Bishōjo) attack is a Coop Attack that appears in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: A cooperative attack performed by Viki, Faylen, and Norma, celebrated as the cutest girls among the 108 Stars. Norma uses her street performance skills to conjure a heart-shaped magical orb. Viki then sneezes, propelling the heart to strike the enemy. Seizing the opening, Faylen finishes the foe off with a well-aimed strike from her massive boomerang.

name: Pretty Girl Attack
description: The Pretty Girl Attack (美少女攻撃, Bishōjo Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A cooperative attack used by the trio of Tengaar, Millie, and Meg. After dazzling the enemy with a blown kiss, the three would attack with their weapons, dealing damage to all their foes.

Although it shared the same name as an attack used during the Gate Rune War, it was clearly very different in its technique.

name: Pretty Girl Attack
description: The Pretty Girl Attack (美少女攻撃, Bishōjo Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Pretty Girl Attack is a dazzling assault by Viki, Mel, and Belle. The trio blows kisses in unison, showering the battlefield with fluttering hearts before crushing enemies under a giant heart. Deals damage to all in range and might just lull foes to sleep.

A charming yet dangerous display of their synchronized allure.

name: Pretty Girl Attack
description: The Pretty Girl Attack (美少女攻撃, Bishōjo Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A powerful attack made by Tengaar, Kasumi, and Camille, recognized as among the most beautiful women in the Toran Liberation Army. Using Kasumi's signature attack, the trio lift the enemy high in the air and slam them into the ground.

While it is a very powerful attack, Kasumi loses her balance for a while due to the impact.

name: Pride of Magic
description: The Pride of Magic (魔力自慢, Maryoku Jiman) attack is a Coop Attack in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A cooperative attack performed by the powerful mages Zerase and Levi. The two incant spells alternately, targeting all enemies before them. While they chant magic of lightning, fire, and wind attributes, the resultant attack attribute is star magic. In the presence of Zerase and Levi, any enemy is merely a subject upon which to flaunt the duo's immense magical prowess.

The attack does not use MP and so can be used even if Zerase and Levi have no MP remaining.

name: Rival Attack
description: The Rival Attack (ライバル攻撃, Raibaru Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A cooperative attack used by the Falcon Rune-wielding rivals of Valeria and Anita. The two attack a single enemy with a furious, uncontrolled flurry of thrusts. After the attack, Valeria is left unbalanced.

Although they were rivals, they were remarkably well coordinated when it came to fighting a common foe.

name: Royal Power
description: The Royal Power (王族, Ōzoku, Royal Family) attack is a Coop Attack in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A cooperative attack utilized by the Prince and Sialeeds, both members of the Falenan royal family. First, the Prince strikes all enemies with his articulated tri-nunchaku, then Sialeeds' chakrams deliver the final blow.

The strength of the attack is based on how fondly Sialeeds feels towards her nephew.

name: Royal Protector
description: The Royal Protector (お守り, Omamori, Guardian) attack is a Coop Attack that appears in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A cooperative attack between the Prince and his bodyguard, Lyon, or the Queen's Knight Miakis. Lyon or Miakis takes the initiative and launches a flurry of sword slashes, followed by the Prince, who delivers a series of consecutive attacks. This combination is only possible because of the trust its participants have built up over the years.

The attack's power depends on the affinity the Prince's attack partner has for him. The higher their affection, the more powerful the attack will become.

name: Sacred Greenhorn
description: The Sacred Greenhorn (闘神祭未体験, Tōshin Matsuri Mitaiken, Didn't Participate in the Sacred Games) attack is a Coop Attack that appears in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This cooperative attack was performed by Gavaya and Nikea, who were unable to participate in the Sacred Games. They initiate with a pincer assault, culminating in both warriors simultaneously launching the enemy high into the air for the finish. The technique strongly conveys the profound intensity of their thwarted aspirations for the festival.

name: Scarlet Charge
description: The Scarlet Charge (ライドオン・赤, Raidōn・Aka, Ride On・Red) attack is a Coop Attack in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A cooperative attack available only to partnered pairs within the Feitas Dragon Cavalry. Roog rides his dragon horse Lance and launches a coordinated charge into enemy lines. While Lance is left off-balance after the attack, its power is said to be overwhelming.

This skill can only be performed by members of the Feitas Dragon Cavalry and their mounts, who are bound by a deep, unbreakable bond.

name: Secret Crush
description: The Kiss Goodbye (美女（裹）, Bijo (Ura), Beauty (Inverse)) attack is a Coop Attack that appears in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A cooperative attack performed by Byakuren, Flail, and Miroon, acclaimed as beauties among their respective non-human races. The heart-shaped charm power emitted by Flail and Byakuren is repeatedly driven into the enemy by Miroon using her deck brush.

The assault is said to be so overwhelming that enemies are left covering their mouths in shock. Furthermore, frog-type enemies are rendered completely immobile.

name: Set! Attack
description: The Set! Attack (えいえいえい攻撃, Eieiei Kōgeki, What? What? What? Attack) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This unite attack was used by the teleporting pair of Viki and her younger counterpart.

After teleporting a chair or barrel onto an enemy, Viki would also teleport and fall onto the enemy. This attack could damage all enemies, but Viki's health would be halved as well. It could also only be used once during battle.

name: Shining Scalp
description: The Shining Scalp (ヒカリ, Hikari, Light) attack is a Coop Attack that appears in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: A dazzling cooperative attack performed by those with glowing foreheads. Alhazred begins by emitting an intense beam of light from his forehead, which Isato then amplifies. Wabon concludes by unleashing the collected radiance upon all enemies, dazing them. The light emitted from their foreheads is so overwhelming that affected foes suffer from a temporarily narrowed field of vision.

name: Shoot and Smash
description: The Shoot and Smash (からくり, Karakuri, Gadget) attack is a Coop Attack that appears in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The reckless girl Lu and Babbage's apprentice, Sorensen, teamed up for this collaborative attack. Sorensen smacks the enemy with his homemade contraption arm, and Lu takes aim at the stunned foe with Babbage's special contraption crossbow.

name: Showdown Attack
description: The Showdown Attack (決戦攻撃, Kessen Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden III.

facts.label: Damage
facts.value: 0.8x Fire-based dmg to all enemies, 0.25x Fire-based dmg to allies

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This unite attack was used by the unlikely duo of Gadget Z and Bright.

In this attack, Gadget Z shoots a beam from the sky while Bright shoots flames at the ground, creating a massive explosion that engulfed both allies and enemies without discrimination.

name: Sindar Surge
description: The Sindar Surge (謎, Nazo, Riddle) attack is a Coop Attack that appears in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: A cooperative attack involving the Sindar researchers Killey, Lorelai, and Zweig. Each combatant launches a wave-like assault on the enemy using their equipped weapons. However, the conclusion is marred when Zweig mutters the dismissive line, "Waste of time," causing Killey and Lorelai to become openly hostile toward him.

name: Speak with Fists
description: The Speak with Fists (拳で語ろう, Kobushi Dekatarō) attack is a Coop Attack that appears in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The martial artists Shoon and Nikea unleash this powerful cooperative attack. This technique can only be performed when both fighters' rage reaches its peak, allowing them to deliver a powerful combo strike to the enemy.

Despite acknowledging each other's strength, they deliberately choose to settle things with their fists, a move true to their fighting spirit.

name: Stampede
description: The Stampede (竜馬, Ryōma, dragon horse) attack is a Coop Attack in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A cooperative attack performed by the three dragon horses, Lance, Flail, and Ax. Flail initiates with a surprise attack using her prized sickle claws. Lance follows by sweeping through with his sharp, thorn-studded tail. Finally, Ax delivers the finishing blow with a devastating bite attack.

Due to the intricate maneuvers required, the dragon horses are left off-balance and unable to maintain their posture after the attack.

name: Stuck With You Attack
description: The Stuck With You Attack (腐れ縁攻撃, Kusareen Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden. It was localized as the Fatal Attack in the 1996 English localization of Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This attack was used by squabbling friendship of Gen and Kamandol. First, Kamandol instructs Gen to attack the enemy and once he does so, Kamandol follows up with a strike of his own. Although the two argue a lot, their cooperation in this regard is quite impressive.

It is said that this attack showed the underlying trust between Gen and Kamandol's argumentative relationship.

name: Sword Master
description: The Sword Master (剣豪, Kengō, Swordsman) attack is a Coop Attack that appears in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This attack was used by Belcott and Dinn, both skilled in the use of two-handed swords. The two simultaneously draw their swords and deliver a powerful, spirited slash into the enemy. The shockwave is so powerful, it damages all enemies in its way.

This unique attack serves to ask the question, why use any other weapon when you can simply overpower the enemy with broadswords?

name: Sword Sage
description: The Sword Sage (剣聖, Kensei) attack is a Coop Attack that appears in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The prodigious swordswoman, Hazuki and the famed Blademaster, Richard, both young warriors, shine with their talent. They combine their attacks to slash the enemy, finishing with a shockwave.

The sword strike appears slow, but only appears that way to the victim awaiting their demise, and its actual speed is impressive.

name: Swordsman Attack
description: The Swordsman Attack (剣豪攻撃, Kengō Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This attack was performed by the duo of Shin and Genshu, both excellent swordsmen in their own right. Though its not known exactly how the two became acquainted, they seemed to have hit it off over a discussion of great swords, and they showed their skill fighting together.

The second after they ready their swords, they would slash through the enemy in the blink of an eye. In rare cases, they were able to defeat their enemies with a single blow.

name: Tackle Attack
description: The Tackle Attack (タックル攻撃, Takkuru Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This united attack was used by Oulan and Hanna, two of the New Alliance Army's strongest women. Their tackle blows away the enemy, but was pretty dangerous as both of them lose their balance after the attack.

name: Teen Rebellion
description: The Teen Rebellion (１４歳, Jūyonsai, 14 years old) attack is a Coop Attack in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: As the name suggests, this was a cooperative attack by a group of teenagers, namely Shoon, Lu, and Faylen, each 14 years old. While Lu attacks with her beloved mechanical crossbow, Shoon dives in and delivers a flurry of punches. Finally, Faylon hurls a large boomerang at the enemy to finish the attack.

name: Tinto Attack
description: The Tinto Attack (ティント攻撃, Tinto Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Tinto Attack is a fierce coordinated strike by Lilly, Samus, and Reed. At Lilly's command, Samus and Reed slash the enemy before she delivers the final blow. The assault deals massive single-target damage and sends Lilly into a rage. 

A powerful team effort, with Lilly at its core.

name: Triangle Strike Attack
description: The Triangle Strike Attack (トライファイト攻撃, Toraifaito Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Triangle Strike Attack is a decisive strike by the Lizard Trio. Shiba and Bazba pin the enemy down with their grides, allowing Dupa to land a crushing finishing blow. Though usable only once per battle, its power makes it a fight-ending move. 

A perfectly synchronized display of the trio's strength.

name: Trick Attack
description: The Trick Attack (からくり攻撃, Karakuri Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden II.

facts.label: Damage
facts.value: 1x damage to all enemies (damage increases to 1.5x with Lubricating Oil)

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This attack was used by the trickster Meg and Gadget. Meg uses her control box to transform Gadget into a giant robot and has him attack all enemies.

When Meg had the Lubricating Oil on her person, this made the attack even more effective.

name: Trickster Attack
