description: The Trickster Attack (からくり攻撃, Karakuri Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden. It was called the Trick Attack in the 1996 English localization of Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This attack was used by the tricksters Juppo and Meg. Together, the two combine to unleash a horde of small mechanical dolls who charge their opponents, damaging all enemies.

name: True Beauty Attack
description: The True Beauty Attack (真・美女攻撃, Shin Bijo Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This cooperative attack was performed by four women known in the New Alliance Army for their beauty: Rina, Kasumi, Karen, and Lorelai.

When the four step forward, a pink mist appears and countless hearts flow forth from the group. The hearts dealt a small amount of damage to all enemies and had a high chance of putting the enemies to sleep also.

The addition of a Sexy Wink made this attack even more potent.

name: Tunnelin' Thru
description: The Tunnelin' Thru (ドワーフ, Dowāfu, Dwarf) attack is a Coop Attack that appears in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: A cooperative attack performed by the cave dwarves, Gunde, Wabon, and Bergen. Gunde and Wabon first pummel the enemy with their favored pickaxe and wrench, respectively. To finish, Bergen drives in with his shovel to deliver a punishing final blow. That Gunde and Wabon ceded the final strike to Bergen likely stems from their recognition of the promise in his youthful strength.

name: Twin Fighter Attack
description: The Twin Fighter Attack (ダブル格闘家攻撃, Daburu Kakutōka Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This unite attack was performed by the martial arts duo of Wakaba and Long Chan-Chan, who were disciple and mentor.

The two would leap forward and strike and kick at the enemy before delivering a final volley. This attack could only be used when both fighters were berserk, so its power was considerable.

name: Twister Attack
description: The Twister Attack (竜巻攻撃, Tatsumaki Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Twister Attack is a joint strike by Kenji, Juan, and Joker. The trio stacks vertically like a totem pole, spinning violently to create a tornado as they plow through enemies. The attack damages all foes but leaves the users disoriented. 

Despite its chaotic nature, this unconventional move packs surprising power.

name: Underling Attack
description: The Underling Attack (おとも攻撃, Otomo Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden II. It was previously localized as the Servant Attack.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This unite attack was performed by the trio of Sierra Mikain, Bolgan, and Bob.

Sierra would use her vampiric power to absorb half of Bob and Bolgan's vitality while empowering them to attack an enemy. The resulting damage was immense and was the most powerful of the unite attacks available to the New Alliance Army.

However, the damage done to Sierra's allies meant that it was perhaps wiser to avoid using this attack if at all possible.

name: Violet Charge
description: The Violet Charge (ライドオン・紫, Raidōn・Murasaki, Ride On・Purple) attack is a Coop Attack in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A cooperative attack available only to partnered pairs within the Feitas Dragon Cavalry. Nick rides his dragon horse Ax and launches a coordinated charge into enemy lines. While Ax is left off-balance after the attack, its power is said to be overwhelming.

This skill can only be performed by members of the Feitas Dragon Cavalry and their mounts, who are bound by a deep, unbreakable bond.

name: Warrior Attack
description: The Warrior Attack (戦士の村攻撃, Senshi no Mura Kōgeki, Warriors' Village Attack) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden. It was called the Warriors Attack in the 1996 English localization of Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This technique was used by two of the Warriors' Village members, Hix and Tengaar. Tengaar uses her fire magic to create a ring of fire, which Hix then sends towards their foe with a swing of his sword.

name: Warriors' Village Attack
description: The Warriors' Village Attack (戦士の村攻撃, Senshi no Mura Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden II. It was called the Warrior Attack in the 1999 English localization of Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This cooperative was attack has been a part of Tengaar and Hix's arsenal since the Gate Rune War. By the time of the Dunan Unification War, the attack had become more effective, showing the duo's growth in the preceeding years.

In it Hix would attack an enemy while Tengaar would simultaneously shoot fireballs at the foe. Although its power was high, it had the disadvantage that Hix was damaged by Tengaar's blasts.

name: Wild Arrow Attack
description: The Wild Arrow Attack (弓矢乱射攻撃, Yumiya Ransha Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden.

facts.label: Members
facts.value: Kirkis and any 1 of Rubi, Stallion, or Sylvina

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This cooperative attack could be performed by Kirkis and any other of the elven archers; Sylvina, Stallion, or Rubi.

Both archers would fire their bows and arrows at the same time, dealing damage to multiple enemies at once. However, the recoil and exertion would throw both of them off balance in the process, making this an attack to be used only in critical situations.

name: Wild Roar Attack
description: The Wild Roar Attack (野生のおたけび攻撃, Yasei no Otakebi Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Wild Roar Attack is a primal onslaught by Hallec and Fubar. The duo unleashes a thunderous roar, blasting all enemies with a concussive shockwave while sending themselves into a battle frenzy. 

A devastating, unrestrained technique of pure fury unleashed by warriors of wild instinct.

name: Winger Attack
description: The Winger Attack (ウイングホード攻撃, Uinguhōdo Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This cooperative attack was used by Chaco and Sid. The two soared into the sky and descended in a V-shaped trajectory, striking the enemy. It was surprisingly effective, considering Chaco did not enjoy fighting alongside Sid.

On rare occasions, Cid would attack and poison the enemy with the poison needle that he secretly carried on his person.

name: Wizard Attack
description: The Wizard Attack (魔法使い攻撃, Mahōtsukai Kōgeki) is a Unite Attack in Suikoden III.

facts.label: Damage
facts.value: Equal to the combined MGC of Estella and Rody

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Wizard Attack is a storm of arcane fury unleashed by Estella and Rody. The master-disciple duo bombards all enemies with chaotic bursts of fire and lightning magic. Though devastating, only Rody expends his magical energy in this display of spellcraft. 

A brilliant, if one-sided, showcase of magical mastery.

name: Battle of Muse
description: The Battle of Muse (ミューズ市攻防戦, Myūzu-shi Kōbō-sen) is a battle in Suikoden II. It is the third war battle of the game and the first where the player commands more than one unit.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Battle of Muse was an early campaign of the Dunan Unification War. When the Highland Army invaded Muse Principality with an aim to take Muse City, Mayor Anabelle of Muse, requested the help of Viktor's mercenaries to delay the advance until the City-States of Jowston could mobilize fully. Viktor agreed and his forces, which included the hero, led the defense.

The Highland was force was led by the 4th Army under the command of Solon Jhee. Although the Muse forces experienced a number of unexpected setbacks during the battle, such as the withdrawal of the Matilda Knights in the middle of the engagement, the defending force succeeded in repelling the Highland advance.

However, Muse City would still fall, some two days later, when the gates of the city were opened from within by Jowy Atreides following the assassination of Lady Anabelle, allowing a Highland night raid to swiftly take the city.

name: Conquest of Matilda
description: The Conquest of Matilda (マチルダ攻略戦, Machiruda Kōryaku-sen), also known as the Battle of Rockaxe (ロックアックス攻防戦, Rokkuakkusu Kōbō-sen) was a major battle and campaign of the Dunan Unification War. It was fought between the New Alliance Army and the Highland Royal Army and their Matilda Knights allies.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
During the defeat of the New Alliance Army in the Battle to Retake Muse City, the Highland Army exploited the situation by invading and annexing the Matilda Knightdom. This battle pitted the 25,000 soldiers of the New Alliance Army against the 55,000 strong Highland Army, its numbers swelled by the addition of the Matilda Knights now under its command.

With the Highland Army holding the advantage, Shu attempted to decrease the Highland advantage by ordering General Kiba to attack eastern Muse Principality while the majority of the New Alliance Army would march on Rockaxe. This would turn the battlefield numbers into roughly 20,000 versus 30,000 soldiers.

Though the Highland Army would correctly ascertain the gambit, they had no choice but to send Leon Silverberg to fight Kiba as east Muse's position so close to Highland meant that it could not be allowed to be retaken, however unlikely the threat was.

The initial battle in Matilda would see the New Alliance forces nearly overwhelmed but the timely intercession of reinforcements (actually two units of New Alliance logistics in disguise) would see the Highland Army withdraw. This would give the New Alliance Army the opportunity to slip former Matilda Knights into Rockaxe Castle.

The New Alliance would then reignite the battle, allowing its agents to infiltrate the castle, and would end with the burning of the Highland flag atop Rockaxe Castle. With the Matilda Knight's morale and willingness to fight plummeting, King Jowy would order a Highland withdrawal, allowing the New Alliance Army to declare victory in the battle.

name: Great Offensive
description: The Great Offensive (大攻勢, Dai Kōsei) was a campaign during the SY 428-432 war between the Highland Kingdom and the City-States of Jowston in the Dunan region. The campaign and its fallout form part of the background for Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The term Great Offensive refers specifically to the counterattack launched by the City-States of Jowston under the command of Genkaku in SY 432 against the 428 invasion by the Highland Kingdom, which had captures great swathes of Jowston territory.

Through Genkaku's leadership, Greenhill City and the Matilda Knightdom were recaptured by Alliance forces. The elite "Black Lion" unit of the South Window City forces, of which Zamji was a part, were also active at this time.

name: Higheast Rebellion
description: The Higheast Rebellion (ハイイースト動乱, Haiīsuto Dōran) was a war fought between the Dunan Republic and the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia over the territories of the former Highland Kingdom. The events of the war form part of the backstory for Suikoden III.

name: Island Liberation War
description: The Island Liberation War (群島解放戦争, Guntō Kaihō Sensō) was a major war fought between several powers, including the various states of the Island Nations region and the Kooluk Empire. The events of the war form the focus of Suikoden IV.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Island Liberation War began in SY 306 with an increase in Kooluk aggression towards the Island Nations as part of its Southern Expansion Policy. This war began with the destruction of Iluya Island by a Magnus Rune Cannon and the swift invasion of the Kingdom of Obel.

The Kooluk's established fleet and military superiority gave the Empire the initial advantage. However, the giant Stronghold Ship which had escaped the Obel occupation as well as its leader, was able to gather together disparate forces from across the region, forming an alliance that was able to fight back against the Kooluk advance.

This war would eventually come to an end with a final battle at the Kooluk southern base of El-Eal Fortress in SY 307. With Kooluk's naval forces broken and the fortress destroyed, a cease-fire was quickly negotiated, ending the war. In the aftermath, the Island Nations Federation would be established as a political body in the region. The Kooluk Empire would suffer fallout of its own, with the Imperial Faction weakening greatly in influence for their role in the disastrous war.

name: Second Battle of Muse
description: The Second Battle of Muse (第ニ次ミューズ市攻防戦, Daini-ji Myūzu-shi Kōbō-sen) is a battle in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
A battle of the latter half of the Dunan Unification War, the Second Battle of Muse City saw the New Alliance Army attempt to take advantage of the Highland Army's annexation of the Matilda Knightdom by marching on and securing the relatively undefended Muse City.

Although the majority of the Highland forces were in Matilda, units from several of Highland's armies, including the 1st, led by King Jowy Blight himself, fought in the battle. The battle was short with the Highland Army quickly withdrawing from the field, leaving their Karaya Clan allies behind to futilely attempt to hold the line.

Following the battle, the New Alliance Army would enter Muse and discover the reason for the Highland withdrawal. The city was infested with Golden Wolves, monsters created by the power of the Beast Rune. These beasts would reappear even when defeated and in the end, the New Alliance Army had little choice but to abandon Muse.

The Highland Army then took advantage of this retreat and deployed its main forces to the north and east, with ambush troops to the south and west. The New Alliance Army's strategist for the battle, Apple, anticipating the presence of the ambushers, and ordered a retreat through the relatively undefended north.

The New Alliance Army was thus was able to retreat with difficulty by thrusting through the Highland forces. Still, despite the narrow escape, the battle was considered a major setback for the New Alliance forces.

name: Sun Rune War
description: The Sun Rune War (太陽の紋章戦争, Taiyō no Monshō Sensō) was a civil war that took place within the Queendom of Falena. It forms the majority of the plot of Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
In SY 449, Marscal Godwin enacted his plot to assassinate Queen Arshtat Falenas, which would make his son, Gizel, the fiance (and later husband) of the ascending Princess Lymsleia, in charge, granting the Godwin Faction control over the Queendom of Falena. This coup would mark the beginning of the Sun Rune War, with the escape of the royal family's Prince leading to the formation of the Imperial City Recapture Army to resist the Godwin regime.

With the aid of prodigious strategist Lucretia Merces, the Recapture Army would fight against the Godwin army into SY 450 before finally liberating Sol-Falena and achieving victory. In one final gambit, Marscal Godwin would steal the Sun Rune and flee to the Ashtwal Mountains but was hunted down and defeated, bringing an end to the conflict.

Following the war, with both of Falena's largest noble houses decimated, the Falenan government transitioned from noble-backed senatorial politics to a more egalitarian parliamentary system under the rule of Queen Lymsleia.

name: War of Succession
description: The War of Succession (継承戦争 Keishō Sensō, Succession War) was a major civil war in the Scarlet Moon Empire in 446 of the solar calendar, sparked by Geil Rugner's attempt to usurp the throne from his nephew, the rightful heir Barbarossa Rugner.

sections.title: Profile

name: War of the Champions
description: The War of the Champions, or Hero's War, (英雄戦争, Eiyū Sensō) was a major war fought between several powers, including the Zexen Federation, the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia and the Six Clans of the Grasslands. The events of the war form the focus of Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The War of the Champions broke out in the year 475, with the sudden attack on the Zexen Federation by the Six Clans of the Grasslands at a treaty signing. However, this attack was in fact orchestrated by Luc, with the assassination of Lizard Clan leader Zepon made to look like a Zexen plot.

As the forces of Zexen and Grasslands warred, the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia started their own invasion of the Grasslands. Although the Zexen and Grasslands forces tried to resist the Harmonian Army, they would be quickly pushed further and further back.

At the same time, three people, the Zexen Knight Chris Lightfellow, Hugo of the Karaya Clan and Geddoe, leader of the 12th unit of the Harmonian Southern Frontier Defense Force pursued the trail of the Flame Champion, the savior of Grasslands in a similar invasion 50 years prior. Although the Flame Champion had passed away, Hugo was able to inherit the True Fire Rune.

Successor to the Flame Champion's legacy, Hugo was able to marshal the resisting forces under the banner of the Fire Bringer. This newly allied force was able to resist the Harmonian invasion until it was revealed that Luc's plan was to destroy his True Wind Rune with the other elemental runes that had shown up in the conflict. Thus, the invasion was called off and all parties united to stop Luc's apocalyptic plans.

Marching to the Ceremonial Site, the Fire Bringer were able to defeat the Destroyers and prevent Luc from carrying out his plan, ending the war. Afterwards, several overtures were made towards a more cooperative future between the powers involved but whether that will amount to anything has yet to be seen.

name: Dragon Plan
description: The Dragon Plan (竜命計画, Ryū Inochi Keikaku, Dragon Life Plan) was a scheme concocted by the Black Dragon Cooking Clan in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Dragon Plan was a conspiracy by the Black Dragon Cooking Clan to exploit the ultimate Moon Bird Recipe in order to satisfy all people, elevating the reputations of cooks everywhere. Shun Min stole the Moon Bird Recipe and passed it to Hai Yo just before her death.

When Hai Yo tore the recipe to pieces after defeating Jinkai in a cook-off, the plan was thwarted.

name: Great Contract
description: The Great Contract (大盟約, Dai Meiyaku) was a treaty signed many years before the events of Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
In ancient times, the Great Contract was established between the four races of the Great Forest; humans, dwarves, kobolds, and elves in order to cease the continuous conflict between them. It was intended to define the lands of each of the four races, each agreeing to the inviolability of the agreement.

The Forest of Illusion was created by the Six Wise Men of the Forest following the agreement. The contract was inherited by the Scarlet Moon Empire and is the reason why the government did not reveal the existence of the Great Forest to other regions of the Empire.

Once Lukie Ende Towayo was destroyed during the Gate Rune War, the magic sealing off the Great Forest vanished, effectively dissolving the Great Contract, though the Toran Republic reaffirmed its commitment to the agreement in principle.

name: Kalekka Incident
description: The Kalekka Incident (カレッカの虐殺事件, Karekka no Gyakusatsu Jiken, Massacre of Kalekka) was an event that happened several years before the events of Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
As far as public perception is concerned, the Kalekka Incident was the massacre of Kalekka's civilians by the City-States of Jowston as part of the Border Conflict, an attempt to take advantage of the internal turmoil caused by the recent War of Succession in the Scarlet Moon Empire. With Moravia Castle having fallen to the City-State, the Jowston forces were able to advance to Kalekka, slaughtering the population before the Imperial Army could arrive.

However, the truth is that it was an ingenious strategy concocted by Leon Silverberg, then the strategist of the Empire. Although Leon Silverberg is often blamed for the incident by those who knew the truth, it must be remembered that it was Emperor Barbarossa who approved the strategy proposed to him. Although the Emperor signed off on the plan, the Great Generals all expressed deep reservations and Kilauea Shulen in particular continued to object strongly, going as far as slapping Leon.


The massacre at Kalekka was caused by the Imperial Army. Most imperial soldiers were also unaware of their true role, having been told they were to surprise the enemy troops that had supposedly already taken the town. When news of the massacre broke out, it enraged the people of the Empire and renewed their morale that had been drained by war with Jowston and the previous civil war. The resulting surge allowed the Imperial Army to repel the Jowston invasion and in that regard at least the massacre must be regarded as a tactical success.

Several future members of the Toran Liberation Army were involved in the Kalekka Incident, including Mathiu Silverberg, who served under Leon, and Humphrey Mintz who was one of the troops sent to Kalekka to attack the town.

name: Kindling Ritual
description: The Kindling Ritual (火入れの儀式, Hiire no Gishiki) was a ceremony performed by the Knights of Gaien in Suikoden IV.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Kindling Ritual was an annual festival that formed part of the graduation ceremony of the Knights of Gaien, located on the island of Razril.

Its origin lies in a Razril legend. The legend stated that long ago, when Razril was in crisis, it was saved by the power of a legendary knight. The Kindling Ritual is said to be a recreation of when that knight was welcomed into Razril.

In the ceremony, a person is selected from among the graduating trainees to carry a torch, and the chosen torchbearer distributes the fire to the townspeople. The SY 306 ceremony saw Snowe Vingerhut chosen as the torchbearer at graduation.

name: Man-Hunting Incident
description: The Man-Hunting Incident (人間狩り事件, Ningen Kari Jiken) was an event that happened several years before the events of Suikoden IV.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Man-Hunting Incident was a tragic occurrence along the border between the Scarlet Moon and Kooluk Empires which resulted in a large loss of life in a single attack. It occurred several years prior to the outbreak of the Island Liberation War.

Leading up to the incident, Scarlet Moon aristocrats, irritated that a recent cease-fire between the two powers removed their chances to prove their valour in combat, would disguise themselves as Kooluk soldiers and lay siege to border villages.

Scarlet Moon strategist Elenor Silverberg soon recognised what has happening and tried to call an immediate halt to the man-hunting nobles. Still, the attacks went on regardless until one day, the village attacked was that of Graham Cray. Cray's son, bearer of the Rune of Punishment saw what was happening and use the power of the rune to try and stop it, killing the aristocrats, the villagers and taking his own life in the process.

It was Cray who would take on the blame for the incident and both he and Elenor, as his superior, would be banished from the Empire. Following the incident, the gruesome man-hunting raids would be no more. Although the incident would be covered up some affected, such as Frederica, would continue to investigate what had happened.

name: Queen's Assassination
description: The Queen's Assassination (女王暗殺, Joō Ansatsu) was an event that happened in the Queendom of Falena at the beginning of Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
In SY 449, the rebellion led by the Godwin Faction engulfed the Queendom of Falena, resulting in the death of Queen Arshtat Falenas.

Queen's Knight Georg Prime, who had indeed dealt the blow, was stigmatised and outlawed as the "Queen Killer". Of course, the true cause of the late queen's demise was the Godwins who had enacted their coup, bringing Arshtat to the brink of madness and forcing Georg to kill her in order to prevent the destruction of Falena via Arshtat's Sun Rune. Manipulating the facts, the Godwins painted Georg as the one true perpetrator of the act.

In fact, Georg Prime had held up an oath made to Ferid to stop Arshtat if her bouts of fury caused by the Sun Rune got out of hand. Georg was prepared to accept the burden of this sin in order to keep his promise to his close friend.

Following the Sun Rune War, the truth of what happened allowed the stigma of this killing to fade within Falena but overseas, where news spreads slower and the truth is even more distorted, some people may still believe Georg Prime to be the "Queen Killer".

name: Royal Family Succession Conflict
description: The Royal Family Succession Conflict (女王家の内紛, Joō-ka no Naifun) was an event that happened in the Queendom of Falena some years before Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The death of Queen Olhazeta in SY 438 saw open conflict break out between the two sisters even before the mourning period for the Queen was over. With no Queen yet on the throne, Nether Gate was commanded by both sides, committing assassinations for both factions, in order to maintain their own favor and grip on power.

However, the conflict's roots stretch back to the late 420s. Princess Falzrahm, second in line to the throne, began maneuvering to win over the majority of the Senate, wanting her sister, Princess Shahrewar, to give up her right of succession and take it for herself. Rumor has it that the Barows family was the real force behind all this scheming, as they had married one of their members to Princess Falzrahm and were obsessed with maintaining their power given that both Queen Olhazeta's and Princess Shahrewar's husbands were from the Godwin family. However, how true these rumors are is unclear.

With the elderly Queen Olhazeta no longer possessing the strength to resolve the situation, matters devolved into a cold war between the two factions. The height of these plans was the Sacred Games of Falzrahm's daughter, Princess Arshtat. Even though Princess Shahrewar's daughter, Haswar, was older than Arshtat and therefore had a higher priority of succession, holding the games for Arshtat first would lend credibility to the idea that Falzrahm herself was the predominant successor.

Ironically, the unexpected outcome of Ferid winning the Sacred Games would cool tensions as this allowed neither side to gain a decisive upper hand in the power struggles. But then, when Queen Olhazeta finally passed away, the reality of the situation meant that things quickly broke down into open conflict. The resulting conflict saw countless victims, as many from both the Godwin and Barows factions were slain. In the end, even the husbands of the two Princesses themselves were as

name: Southern Expansion Policy
description: The Southern Expansion Policy (南進政策, Nanshin Seisaku) was the name given by the Kooluk Empire to its campaign in the Island Nations region. Kooluk’s pursuit of this policy directly triggered the events of Suikoden IV.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Imperial Faction of the Kooluk Empire spearheaded the advance into the Island Nations. While the stated aim of the Southern Expansion Policy was to “modernize” the region, its true purpose was to subjugate the Island Nations and secure full dominance over the southern Kuknasi Sea. 

Although formally supported by the Imperial Faction and led by admirals dispatched at the emperor’s command, Graham Cray manipulated the policy to center on the search for the Rune of Punishment. His plan was to obtain the rune and supposedly present it to the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia in exchange for military assistance. 

The policy ultimately escalated into the Island Liberation War, ending in a disastrous defeat for the Kooluk Empire. The failure of the Southern Expansion Policy would undermine the Imperial Faction's influence in internal Kooluk politics.

name: Trial of Disinheritance
description: The Trial of Disinheritance (廃嫡裁判, Haichaku Saiban) was an event that took place prior to the first Suikoden game. It helped spark the War of Succession crisis.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Trial of Disinheritance was a legal proceeding to challenge the legitimacy of the newly crowned Emperor Barbarossa and abolish his right to the throne. The Geil Rugner faction, aided by judiciary Assam in the courts, initiated this trial in the absence of Barbarossa and his supporters.

With a stacked court, the ruling to depose Barbarossa was passed in only 30 minutes. The reaction by Barbarossa to this attempted coup would spark a civil war, the War of Succession.

name: Zexen Federation Day Ball
description: The Zexen Federation Day Ball (ゼクセン連邦の建国記念パーティ, Zekusen Renpō no Kenkoku Kinen Pāti) is a celebration mentioned in Suikoden III. It is where Chris Lightfellow and Lilly Pendragon first met.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
In SY 470, the Zexen Federation Day Ball was held to celebrate the 50th anniversary of the founding of the Zexen Federation.

The celebration was overshadowed by the confrontation between Lilly Pendragon, first daughter of the Tinto Republic, and Chris Lightfellow, a member of the Zexen Knights. Lilly would insist that Chris dance with her and when Chris, who was not good at dancing at the time, accidentally spilled a glass of wine on Lilly's dress, a brawl soon broke out. Lilly ended up throwing a cake at Chris, who retaliated with a slap.

The fight would finally end after the two finished hurling tables at one another. Despite, or rather because of, this incident, the two recognized the strong will in each other and the two, especially Lilly, considered themselves friends.

name: Zexen-Grasslands Armistice
description: The Zexen-Grasslands Armistice (休戦協定, Kyūsen Kyōtei) was an agreement scheduled in Suikoden III between the Zexen Federation and the Six Clans of the Grasslands.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The armistice agreement was a planned cease-fire that was to be signed between the Zexen Federation and the Six Clans of the Grasslands prior to the War of the Champions. The Zexen Council privately regarded the planned armistice as a temporary measure following the loss of Zexen Knights Captain Galahad.

The aim of the agreement was to formally recognize the border between Zexen and the Grasslands, ending years of conflict. The armistice was due to be negotiated at the Amur Plain with Chris Lightfellow representing Zexen and Lucia, Dupa, and others representing the Six Clans. Amur Plain was chosen for its flat, clear view, deterring ambush.

However, due to a plot by the Destroyers, fighting broke out at the site, which each party believing the other had betrayed the agreement. As a result, the ongoing conflict between the Zexen Federation and the Grasslands continued to escalate, eventually becoming the War of the Champions.

name: Armes Invasion
description: The Armes Invasion (アーメス大侵攻, Āmesu Dai Shinkō, Great Armes Invasion) was a war between the Queendom of Falena and the New Armes Kingdom.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
In SY 441, the New Armes Kingdom hoped to take advantage of the discord caused by Queen Falzrahm's bloody rise to power and unexpected death and launched an invasion of the Queendom of Falena, early in the reign of Queen Arshtat.

Although New Armes held the numerical advantage and dealt great damage to Falena's eastern lands, the efforts of Arshtat and Ferid, combined with the Queen's Knights and the skills of strategist Lucretia Merces, allow the Falenan forces to overcome these odds and push the New Armes forces out of the country.

In the aftermath, the King of New Armes who supported the invasion attempt would be deposed and the more moderate Jalat would ascend to the throne.

name: Battle at Floating Fortress Shasarazade
description: The Battle at Floating Fortress Shasarazade (水上砦シャサラザード突破, Suijiyo Toride Shasarazādo Toppa) was the penultimate battle of the Gate Rune War. It was fought between the Toran Liberation Army and the Scarlet Moon Imperial Army.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
With rebel forces around the Scarlet Moon Empire now united, all that remained was the Imperial capital of Gregminster. Two options presented themselves; to strike the Fortress of Kwaba or attack the Floating Fortress of Shasarazade, both gateways to Gregminster. Mathiu Silverberg made the decision to attack Shasarazade, precisely because the Liberation Army appeared to not have the ships to do so.

Mathiu used dragon's breath to freeze water, creating 500 ice ships, allowing the Liberation Army to sail to Shasarazade where the battle commenced. The Imperial troops, led by General Sonya Shulen were eventually defeated, despite Sonya's determination to win in order to avenge the death of Teo McDohl, and Shasarazade was razed. However, the battle was not a complete victory. Sanchez revealed himself to be an Imperial spy, stabbing Mathiu and setting the fortress aflame while officers were still inside.

name: Battle at Fortress of Garan
description: The Battle at Fortress of Garan (ガラン城塞攻略, Garan Jōsai Kōryaku, Garan Fortress Capture) was the second major battle of the Gate Rune War. It was fought between the Toran Liberation Army and the Scarlet Moon Imperial Army.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
After the destruction of the old Liberation Army base in Lenankamp, a crackdown on rebel activities occurred in Kunan, the domain of Milich Oppenheimer. After allying with the remnants of the old Liberation Army, led by Flik who first suggested the liberation of Kunan, Mathiu Silverberg judged that the Liberation Army now had enough manpower to break through the Fortress of Garan, gateway to the region, with the eventual goal being the freeing of all captured rebels.

Coming off an impressive victory over Kwanda Rosman at the Battle at Pannu Yakuta, the united troops manage to take the fortress, opening up the west. But the flush of victory caused the overeager Flik to strike at the heart of Kunan without first engaging in reconnaissance immediately after the battle, resulting in the disastrous First Battle at Scarleticia Castle.

name: Battle at Pannu Yakuta
description: The Battle at Pannu Yakuta was the first major battle of the Gate Rune War. It was fought between the Toran Liberation Army and the Scarlet Moon Imperial Army.

name: Battle at the Northern Checkpoint
description: The Battle at the Northern Checkpoint (北の関所突破, Kita no Sekisho Toppa) was a major battle of the Gate Rune War. It was fought between the Toran Liberation Army and the Scarlet Moon Imperial Army.

name: Battle of L'Renouille
description: The Battle of L'Renouille (ルルノイエ攻略戦, Rurunoie Kōryaku Sen) was a major battle of the Dunan Unification War. It was fought between the New Alliance Army and the Highland Royal Army.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Taking place during the late Dunan Unification War, the New Alliance Army buoyed from its victory at the Conquest of Matilda, planned to march on the Highland capital of L'Renouille before the Highland Army could regain its composure. The Highland strategist Leon Silverberg anticipated this move and engaged the New Alliance Army outside of Muse City.

During the battle, Shu and Viktor disengaged from the New Alliance Army and engaged Leon Silverberg directly. Leading the forces of Leon into a forest, Shu unleashed an unprecedented fire attack, wiping out a great amount of Leon's troops. Although Leon himself survived the battle, the fire strategy allowed the New Alliance Army to win and open the route to Highland.

name: Border Conflict
description: The Border Conflict (国境紛争 Kokkyō Funsō) was a series of conflicts between the Scarlet Moon Empire and the City-States of Jowston.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The "Border Conflict" in the history of the Scarlet Moon Empire refers to the invasion of the empire's northern lands by the armies of South Window and Tinto Principality. These two city-states were members of the City-States of Jowston, located to the empire's north, and aimed to expand their territory.

During the 446 War of Succession, the City-States took advantage of the civil war and advanced south into Senan and then Dana. Once Barbarossa Rugner had secured the throne, he would begin a campaign to expel the invaders.

This conflict would last until 448 solar calendar, when the Kalekka Incident took place. The outrage from the destruction of Kalekka would reignite flagging imperial morale, resulting in the total expulsion of the Jowston army from imperial lands soon after.

Though not part of the Border Conflict, the armies of South Window and Tinto would attempt a similar invasion during the Gate Rune War, temporarily occupying lands in Senan in 456. Again, the Jowston forces would be expeled following the conclusion of the war, this time by the new Toran Republic in 458.

name: Conquest of L'Renouille
description: The Conquest of L'Renouille (ルルノイエ攻略戦, Rurunoie Kōryaku-sen), also known as The Last Battle (最後の戦い Saigo no Tatakai), was the final battle of the Dunan Unification War. It was fought between the New Alliance Army and the Highland Royal Army.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Immediately following the beginning of the New Alliance Army advance at the Battle of L'Renouille, the follow-up push had the army march on the Highland Kingdom capital of L'Renouille leading to this final battle. Although they fought bravely, the Highland forces were completely overrun and the city was taken. Yuber withdrew before the battle while other units fell back once the New Alliance Army began to enter L'Renouille.

Resistance still held firm in L'Renouille Palace, with Lucia, Han Cunningham, Seed and Culgan all challenging the advancing New Alliance forces before being overcome. Once the New Alliance troops defeated the Silver Wolf summoned by Leon Silverberg, the war was over.

name: Final Battle
description: The Final Battle (最後の戦い Saigo no Tatakai, Last Battle) is the name given by the Warriors' Village to the battle in which the armies led by Clift defeated the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia thus securing the autonomy of Lorimar and directly leading to the foundation of the Warriors' Village.

name: First Battle at Scarleticia Castle
description: The First Battle at Scarleticia Castle (第一次スカーレティシア城攻略 Daiichiji Sukareteishia Shiro Kouryaku, First Scarleticia Castle Battle) was a battle in the Gate Rune War. It was fought between the Toran Liberation Army and the Scarlet Moon Imperial Army.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Immediately after the Toran Liberation Army victory at the Battle at Fortress of Garan, Flik, overconfident in victory, attempted to capaitilize on the momentum by having the army immediately march on Scarleticia Castle.

The intent of the battle, which took place immediately after the Liberation Army victory at the Fortress of Garan was to free and relieve pressure on the rebel groups being suppressed in the western Scarlet Moon Empire by the policies of Milich Oppenheimer.

There, the Liberation Army was decimated by the poisonous attributes of Milich Oppenheimer's Antoinette and were forced to retreat. The Imperial Army suffered zero casualties, making this battle an extremely damaging rout for the Liberation Army.

name: First Battle with Teo
description: The first Battle with Teo (テオとの戦い（1回目）, Teo to no Tatakai (1-kai-me)) was a significant military encounter that took place during the course of the Gate Rune War. The confrontation transpired between the Toran Liberation Army and the Scarlet Moon Imperial Army.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Following the triumphant outcome of the Second Battle at Scarleticia Castle, the Toran Liberation Army swiftly received intelligence regarding the approaching Imperial Army forces under the command of General Teo McDohl. The Imperial Army's advancement was observed on the eastern shores of Lake Toran, heading towards Toran Castle.

Upon receiving this information, the Liberation Army's strategist, Mathiu Silverberg, suggested engaging in a preliminary skirmish to gauge the strength of the Imperial Army and to assess General Teo's capabilities. Concurrently, Teo's son, resolute in his determination to confront his father, managed to suppress any doubts in his heart and prepared to participate in the battle.

However, during the actual engagement, the Liberation Army encountered unexpected and overwhelming resistance from Teo McDohl's Armored Cavalry. Despite their valorous efforts, the Liberation Army suffered heavy casualties and found themselves in a precarious situation, prompting them to issue a strategic order for a full retreat.

Exploiting the advantage gained from their successful engagement, the Imperial Army pursued the retreating rebel forces with unwavering determination. In the face of the impending threat of the Liberation Army's complete annihilation, the tide of battle shifted when Pahn, a member of the Liberation Army, confronted General Teo in a one-on-one duel.

This act of courage and skill demonstrated by Pahn temporarily halted the advance of Teo's army, sparing the Liberation Army from utter destruction despite the Imperial Army's overwhelming advantage.

name: Liberation of Greenhill
description: The Liberation of Greenhill (グリンヒル解放戦, Gurinhiru Kaihō-Sen) was a major battle of the Dunan Unification War. It was fought between the New Alliance Army and the Highland Royal Army.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
With the addition of the forces of Tinto City and the remaining remnants of the Muse City forces, the New Alliance Army gained an additional 7,000 soldiers. Now able to launch an offensive for the first time in the Dunan Unification War, the New Alliance Army decided to attempt to liberate Greenhill City, which had been an early victim of the Highland Army's invasion.

For this battle, the New Alliance Army deployed on two fronts. General Hauser's forces were deployed in Muse Principality to hold off reinforcements and splinter the Highland effort while General Kiba Windamier led the main army in a siege on Greenhill City itself.

With that, the fierce battle came to an end with the city of Greenhill released from Highland occupation.

name: Second Battle at Scarleticia Castle
description: The Second Battle at Scarleticia Castle (第二次スカーレティシア城攻略 Dainiji Sukāreteishia Shiro Kouryaku, Second Scarleticia Castle Battle) was a battle in the Gate Rune War. It was fought between the Toran Liberation Army and the Scarlet Moon Imperial Army.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Following the rescue of the great doctor Liukan from Soniere Prison, an antitoxin could be prepared for the poisonous effects of Antoinette, the giant flower under Milich Oppenheimer's control which led to the Toran Liberation Army's defeat in the First Battle at Scarleticia Castle.

Now able to battle as normal, the Liberation Army marched once more for Scarleticia Castle where they managed to defeat the Imperial Army, now without its great weapon, after a tough battle, freeing the beleaguered rebels in Kunan.

Following the battle, the poisonous flowers were destroyed but the celebration was cut short when news of Teo McDohl's approach quickly became known.

name: Second Battle with Teo
description: The second Battle with Teo (テオとの戦い（2回目）, Teo to no Tatakai (2-kai-me) ) was a battle in the Gate Rune War. It was fought between the Toran Liberation Army and the Scarlet Moon Imperial Army.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Following the disastrous First Battle with Teo, the Liberation Army discovered that the Secret Factory had survived the Imperial crackdown and procured Fire Spears to try and counter Teo McDohl's Armored Cavalry with. The Fire Spears performed even better than expected. The Armored Cavalry were decimated as the Liberation Army served Teo McDohl his first defeat in warfare.

Even with his army defeated, however, Teo, backed by his generals Alen and Glenshiel, refused to surrender to the Liberation Army and challenged his son to a one-on-one duel. Teo was defeated and the battle ended with his dying, giving his blessing to his son. This battle was a key turning point in the war as it saw the loss of the empire's most powerful unit and its greatest general.

name: The Last Battle
description: The Last Battle (最後の戦い Saigo no Tatakai) was the final battle in the Gate Rune War. It was fought between the Toran Liberation Army and the Scarlet Moon Imperial Army.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
With the Toran Liberation Army victory at the Battle at Floating Fortress Shasarazade, the only remaining Scarlet Moon Empire citadel was Gregminster itself. Although 20,000 troops remained to defend Gregminster, Liberation Army morale had been raised by an impassioned speech and the resurrection of Gremio.

However, on the day of battle, scouting from Kirkis revealed that the Imperial Army under General Yuber numbered over 100,000. It was revealed by Leknaat that most of that number were monsters summoned by Windy's Gate Rune. Using her own Gate Rune, combined with Joshua Levenheit's Dragon Rune, the monsters were sent away, creating a much more even battlefield.

The Imperial Army was led in the battle by Yuber and Kanaan. The Liberation Army emerged victorious in this final battle, clearing the path to Gregminster. After defeating Ain Gide who guarded the gates to Gregminster Palace, the stage for the final confrontation with Emperor Barbarossa was set.

name: Feast of Hooves and Reed Pipes
description: The Feast of Hooves and Reed Pipes (蹄と草笛の宴, Hidzume to Kusabue no Utage) is a festival that takes places in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Feast of Hooves and Reed Pipes was a festival used to celebrate the coming of the harvest season. It had been held in Milit Village, a small Grasslands settlement, since antiquity.

Even in a small village like Milit, stalls lined the streets and traveling entertainers would come to perform, making it a busy and popular festival for the area.

name: Lordlake Uprising
description: The Lordlake Uprising (ロードレイクの暴動, Rōdoreiku no Bōdō) was a series of riots in Queendom of Falena. The Lordlake Uprising led to the razing of the town and surrounding areas by the Sun Rune.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
SY 447 saw an attempt by the Barows Faction to construct a dam on the branch of the Feitas River that ran past Lordlake, gifting the town its water. This dam would have caused environmental and social strife in the region, leading to a protest march taking place.

When the march reached the still-under construction dam, the garrison there, under the orders of a young Euram Barows, attempted to forcefully repel the agitated, if still peaceful, protesters. This turned the protest into a riot and the small garrison stationed at the dam were quickly overwhelmed and the dam destroyed.

In the immediate aftermath, it was decided by the protesters to take a petition to Queen Arshtat herself in order to seek redress for their woes. It was at this time that Salum Barows saw the opportunity to take advantage of the situation. He would insert his own agents into the protesters who would then instigate another riot in the East Palace, stealing the Dawn Rune and bringing it to Rainwall where it would be hidden by Salum.

At the same time, the Godwin Faction would plan to take advantage of the spiraling situation by seizing the Sun Rune itself. In order to prevent this, Godwin strategist Lucretia Merces would betray Marscal Godwin and advise Queen Arshtat to bear the Sun Rune herself to thwart their ambitions.

This meant that when word reached the Sun Palace of the theft of the Dawn Rune, Arshtat, under the influence of the Sun Rune, would extract a terrible vengeance on the town. Using the rune's power, she would scorch the earth around the town, turning it into an arid wasteland.

name: New Liberation Army's Founding Day
description: The New Liberation Army's Founding Day (解放軍決起の日, Kaihou Gun Kekki no Hi, Liberation Army Uprising Day) is a historical day during the Gate Rune War.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: This day is the day in SY 455 when, after the death of Odessa, the castle at Lake Toran became the home of the Toran Liberation Army after the defeat of the Zombie Dragon. On that day, Leknaat visited the headquarters bearing the Stone Tablet of Promise.

name: Sacred Games
description: The Sacred Games (闘神祭, Tōshin-sai) is a regular custom held by the Queendom of Falena. The Sacred Games determine the husband of the future Queen of Falena.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Sacred Games were a fighting tournament used to decide the husband of the future Queen of Falena and as such held enormous significance towards the direction the nation would take under that administration. Adding to its significance, the winner of the Sacred Games would also become the Commander of the Queen's Knights following the new Queen's coronation.

The Sacred Games originated from writings on the first Queen of Falena in which it is stated that on the day of Falena's foundation, it was said that the Queen was to be like unto a goddess and that her companion must shield the land like a mighty god of war. The Sacred Games were established to choose a man most worthy of this duty. However, in practice, the participants in the Scared Games were mostly gladiators fighting for the noble that hired them. In fact, in the history of the Sacred Games only one non-noble was decreed to have won the competition, Ferid won the hand of Princess Arshtat Falenas in SY 433.

In SY 449, the Barows Faction pushed for the Sacred Games to take place to crown the husband of the then-10-year-old Princess Lymsleia in order to try and counteract greater Godwin influence in the Senate. The attempt backfired when the Godwin gladiator, Childerich, won the tournament under dubious circumstances. In previous years, the Barows Faction had pushed for a Sacred Games to take place for the young Arshtat to establish her mother, Princess Falzrahm as the legitimate heir to the throne, feeding into what would become the Royal Family Succession Conflict.

Following the events of the Sun Rune War, the Sacred Games were abolished with Queen Lymsleia declaring that she would choose her own husband at a future date and that the Commander of the Queen's Knights would now be an office she would appoint a worthy candidate to.

name: Caleron
description: Caleron (カレルロン, Kareruron) is a location vaguely implied in Suikoden Tactics.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Caleron is a town noted for its Caleron Laboratory, where Iskas researched the Evil Eye.

Beyond this, nothing is noted about the town and it appears that Kyril and company never visited the town proper when infiltrating its laboratory.

name: Caleron Laboratory
description: Caleron Laboratory (カレルロン研究所, Kareruron Kenkyūjo) is a location in Suikoden Tactics. It is a research facility within the Kooluk Empire.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Caleron Laboratory was a large research facility based in the town of Caleron. Its interior was highly complex, making it very easy for people unfamiliar with the layout to become lost.

Studies regarding the Evil Eye were conducted here by Iskas. This included human experimentation and the transforming of humans into fishmen.

name: Canal Town Merseto
description: The Canal Town Merseto (運河の町メルセト, Unga no Machi Meruseto), usually referred to as just Merseto (メルセト, Meruseto), is a location in Suikoden Tactics. It is a town within the Kooluk Empire.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Merseto was a port city located to the south-west of the Imperial City of Graska. As its moniker, the canal town, suggested, the town was built on a system of canals and riverways which helped to facilitate trade. This combined with allowances for foreign merchants to visit the city, made it a booming port town for the Kooluk Empire.

However, the stagnation that had gripped Kooluk did not exclude Merseto and it too was struggling with unemployment and economic strife, especially as goods from the Island Nations region were cut off due to the Island Liberation War, a war which most normal Merseto civilians were not even aware of.

name: Collanbal Ruins
description: The Collanbal Ruins (コランバルの廃墟, Koranbaru no Haikyo) is a location in Suikoden Tactics. It is a series of ruins within the Kooluk Empire.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Collanbal Ruins were abandoned ruins located to the southwest of Merseto. It was a dangerous place due to both bandits and fishmen, but it was also a spot that nearby children would explore.

The traveling martial artist Karl would find himself defending a group of children from bandits here, before being aided by Kyril and his companions.

In addition, rarities such as Gold Ore and Diamonds were buried here, so many adventurers visited these ruins hoping to find treasure.

name: Haruna
description: The Frontier Town Haruna (辺境の町ハルナ, Henkyō no Machi Haruna), usually referred to as just Haruna (ハルナ, Haruna), is a location in Suikoden Tactics. It is a town within the Kooluk Empire.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Haruna was a town located in northern Kooluk, spanning a large bridge. Shops and residential areas were all constructed on the sides of the bridge which crossed a large gorge.

According to legend, Haruna was once two villages on both sides of the bridge but as it became a hassle to cross the bridge every time to visit a person or business in the other village, the two villages would spread across the bridge, becoming the modern Haruna known today.

It was governed by Ornela just prior to the collapse of the Kooluk Empire. The sorcerer Simeon also lived in this town.

name: Graska
description: The Imperial City of Graska (皇都グラスカ, Sumeragi-to Gurasuka), usually referred to more simply as Graska (グラスカ, Gurasuka), is a location in Suikoden Tactics. It is the capital of the Kooluk Empire.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
At the time of Kooluk's fall, Emperor Julius reigned from Graska. The conservative nature of the elderly ruler had led to a steady maintaining of the internal power struggle. Instability reigned in the Kooluk Imperial City as the Imperial and Patriarchal Factions vied for political control. As such, the strength of the nation, emanating from Graska, has been steadily decaying for quite some time.

While internal conditions would prove to be the ruination of the empire, outside the palace walls, the city had many shops, selling some of the finest items in all Kooluk. The enormous imperial palace rose from the center of the city, which was protected by the famed Gate of Graska, said to be an impregnable defense for the city.

name: Klotzeo Church
description: Klotzeo Church (クロッツェオ教会, Kurottseo Kyōkai) is a location in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2. It is located in Crystal Valley, capital of the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: Klotzeo Church is located in the slums of the west district of Crystal Valley. It is abandoned and lies in the middle of similarly ruined buildings. This building was where Nash Latkje and Zaj Quiroz had their final battle.

name: Marid Village
description: Marid Village (マリードの村, Marīdo no Mura) is a location that appears in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2. It is a small village located in the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: Marid is a small village in the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia without outstanding characteristics. The village suffered an attack from a brutal black dragon, but the beast was repulsed thanks to the efforts of Nash Latkje and a number of other travelers who were passing through the hamlet on their way to Crystal Valley.

name: Mido Shallows
description: The Mido Shallows (ミドーの浅瀬, Midō no Asase) is a location in Suikoden Tactics. It is a shallow river located to the north of the Imperial City of Graska.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This river, located to the north of the Imperial City of Graska, was where Martin confronted Kyril and his friends over their alleged kidnapping of Princess Corselia. At this spot, Kyril and Martin would duel, with Kyril proving his sincerity.

These shoals were rich with precious metals, making it a good place for excavators to travel to.

name: Milit Village
description: Milit Village (ミリトの村, Mirito no Mura) is a location that appears in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2. It is a small village located in the Grasslands.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Located in the Grasslands, the small village of Milit is known for its annual Feast of Hooves and Reed Pipes to commemorate the harvest. In the past, the village has been attacked by soldiers claiming to be from the New Alliance Army, leaving the villagers ill-disposed towards their officers and the Dunan Republic. The village was attacked again in 460 during its festival by bandits claiming to be the Fire Bringer, led by Clant.

Although Mayor Clad attempted to appease the group, they would eventually be driven from the village by Nash Latkje, Gijimu and Camus, among others. Due to the efforts of those former New Alliance Army soldiers in saving the village, the people there began to trust the Dunan Republic once again.

name: Secret Patriarchal Facility
description: The Secret Patriarchal Facility (長老派秘密施設, Chōrō-ha Himitsu Shisetsu) is a location in Suikoden Tactics. It is a research facility that serves as the stage of the game's penultimate battle.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This huge facility was built in secret by the Patriarchal Faction south of Graska. The interior was long and narrow, and the Evil Eye was placed at the rear of the facility. Because it was built in secret, its existence was unknown even to the imperial princess, Corselia.

Iskas retreated here following his coup of the Kooluk Empire Imperial Faction but was confronted by Kyril and his allies, falling in battle. The facility collapsed upon itself following this climatic battle.

name: Small Border Village
description: The Small Border Village (国境の小さな村, Kokkyō no Chīsana Mura) is a location in Suikoden Tactics. It is a small settlement located near the Kooluk-Scarlet Moon border.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Located on the border between the Scarlet Moon Empire and the Kooluk Empire, this nameless village holds no great fondness for either power, with their border skirmishes having razed the village time and again.

A forest located near the village serves as a back route to crossing the border undetected. This access point is used by those who would import and sell contraband foods from the Scarlet Moon Empire in Kooluk. Those who are involved in the smuggling trade can lead a satisfactory life in the village, paying off Kooluk soldiers to look the other way.

During the last days of the Kooluk Empire, a number of Rune Cannons were set up at this settlement to form a deterrent against Scarlet Moon, but these cannons were destroyed by Kyril and his companions.

name: Terana Plains
description: The Terana Plains (テラナー平原, Teranā Heigen) is a location in Suikoden Tactics. It is a grassland located to the in the center of the Kooluk Empire.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Terana Plains were located in central Kooluk and stretched from Frontier Town Haruna in the north to Caleron in the south. Kyril and his companions would be attacked by bandits on this plain as they attempted to travel north.

Kyril also met Charlemagne for the first time here and dueled Mitsuba in this location.

name: Village
description: The village (里, sato) is a location in the upcoming Suikoden STAR LEAP. It may be named Taiju Village (タイジュの里, Taiju no Sato) according to Viki's teleportation options in the game.

name: Xasta Grassland
description: The Xasta Grassland (ザスタ草原, Zasuta Sōgen) is a location in Suikoden Tactics. It is a grassland located to the west of the Imperial City of Graska.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This range of grassland is located to the immediate west of the Imperial City of Graska.

Kyril would receive a quest from Chiepoo & Co. to retrieve a Blue Savage Sprout Flower from the Savage Sprouts that lived in the region. After retrieving the sprout, the group would be ambushed by the Mercenary Leader and her company.

name: Allied Forces
description: The Allied Forces (連合軍, Rengō-gun) were a group of soldiers and other volunteers who fought against the Kooluk Empire during the Island Liberation War in Suikoden IV.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Allied Forces came together following the occupation of the Kingdom of Obel by the Kooluk Empire. The giant Stronghold Ship which had escaped the Obel occupation as well as its leader, was able to gather together disparate forces from across the region, forming an alliance that was able to fight back against the Kooluk advance.

This war would eventually come to an end with a final battle at the Kooluk southern base of El-Eal Fortress in SY 307. With Kooluk's naval forces broken and the fortress destroyed, a cease-fire was quickly negotiated, ending the war. With the foundation of the Island Nations Federation, the Federation navy would become the authorized military force for the fledgling nation.

name: Alma Kinan
description: The Alma Kinan (アルマ・キナン, Aruma Kinan) are one of the Six Clans of the Grasslands.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Alma Kinan is one of the Six Clans of the Grasslands. The clan is located deep within the Kuput Forest and the Alma Kinan Village there has cut off all contact with the outside world, with the aid of a magical barrier. The clan is made up entirely of women, living in harmony with the spirits.

By living a life of self-sufficiency in an isolated environment, Alma Kinan culture is deeply rooted in its traditions and customs. Due to their rare sighting and mysterious nature, other clans view them with almost a feeling of awe.

name: Ancient Armes Dynasty
description: The Ancient Armes Dynasty (古代アーメス王朝, Kodai Āmesu Ōchō) is an ancient nation mentioned in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Ancient Armes Dynasty was a mighty, dynastic nation that had flourished years before the establishment of the Queendom of Falena.

In the distant past, it was a superpower whose prosperity extended far and wide thanks to the blessing of the Sun Rune. This era of prosperity came to an end when the Sun Rune was said to cause the sudden breakdown of the dynasty's king, leading to the overnight destruction of the nation.

The modern New Armes Kingdom proclaims itself to be the inheritor of the ancient Armes lineage but there is no lineal or cultural connection between the two.

name: Armes Southern Mountain Corps
description: The Armes Southern Mountain Corps (アーメス新王国南岳兵団, Āmesu Shin Ōkoku Minamidake Heidan) is a military unit in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Southern Mountain Corps was traditionally led by the more hardline factions within the New Armes Kingdom. In the past, the unit had been frequently involved in clashes against the Queendom of Falena.

During the Sun Rune War, Maha Sparna served as the commander of the unit, with Jidan Guisu serving as her assistant. When New Armes allied with the Godwin Faction, the Southern Mountain Corps managed to take both Sable and Lordlake. Thanks to the strategies of Lucretia Merces, however, the unit was pushed back and Guisu would fall protecting Maha Sparna's retreat through Sable, across the New Armes border.

Nakula once served in the Southern Mountain Corps but deserted the group in order to seek his revenge against Sagiri in the Imperial City Recapture Army.

name: Armes Western Marine Corps
description: The Armes Western Marine Corps (アーメス新王国西海兵団, Āmesu Shin Ōkoku Saikai Heidan) is a military unit in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Western Marine Corps was traditionally led by the Valya family, a member of the Madra Clan, one of the more moderate factions in the New Armes Kingdom. In the past, the Western Marine Corps had engaged in skirmishes and battles against the navies of the Island Nations Federation.

During the Sun Rune War, Shula Valya served as the commander of the unit, assisted by Sharmista and Nifsara. The unit would arrive at Estrise, under orders to occupy the town, but would remain docked and treated the invasion as something more akin to a vacation. Rumours suggested that the Western Marine Corps was assigned primarily to watch over their counterparts in the Armes Southern Mountain Corps in order to dissuade any acts of slaughter in captured Falenan cities.

The Western Marine Corps would withdraw from Estrise without incident following the collapse of the New Armes/Godwin alliance.

name: Armored Cavalry
description: The Armored Cavalry (鉄甲騎馬隊 Tetsu Kabuto Kiba Tai Iron Clad Cavalry) is a military unit in Suikoden. The unit, led by Teo McDohl was known for being virtually unbeatable in battle.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
leads his Armored Cavalry in pursuit of the routed Liberation Army. (PSP) ]]The armored cavalry was renowned through the Scarlet Moon Empire as a unit that had never known defeat during its countless battles against the City-States of Jowston. During the War of Succession, the armored cavalry was feared enough that the forces of Geil Rugner kidnapped Teo's son in an attempt to force Teo to fight without the famed unit.

The final battle of the war took place at the Fortress of Kwaba, with Teo McDohl's 500 armored cavalry and Ain Gide's 1,000 regular cavalry easily dispatching the 3,000 strong forces of Generals Schmidt and Bergen.

In the Gate Rune War, the armored cavalry decisively routed the Toran Liberation Army in the First Battle with Teo, coming close to ending the war there and then. Still, the Liberation Army was able to secure a supply of Fire Spears, something the heavily armored unit was susceptible to, and emerged victorious in the Second Battle with Teo, ending the legacy of the famed unit.

The cavalry was not made up of horses but gulhorses, an animal unique to the Toran region. Still, gulhorses were ignored as a combat animal for a long time due to their stubbornness and resistance to handling. It was Teo McDohl who asserted the gulhorses use in combat, making them the formidable unit they became under his charge.

name: Barows Faction
description: The Barows Faction (バロウズ派, Barōzu-ha) was one of two major political groups in the Queendom of Falena during Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Barows faction was one of two major political bodies that made up the political make-up of the Queendom of Falena. Centered around the Barows family of Rainwall, the body is made up of eastern nobles. In comparison to the Godwin Faction, the Barows faction is more moderate, seeking to live in harmony with Falena's neighbors. However, this can sometimes take the form of self-serving schemes that would only benefit immediate members of the faction.

During the Sun Rune War, Salum Barows attempted to convince the Prince of Falena to establish a kingdom in opposition to the Godwin regime in Sol-Falena, a plot that would have ended with the Barows' lands become an New Armes Kingdom vassal state. The Barows Faction was also responsible for the theft of the Dawn Rune from the East Palace during the Lordlake Uprising, cementing their reputation as duplicitous manipulators when their deeds were revealed.

Following the war, Luserina and Euram Barows gave up their noble titles and used the Barows wealth to fund the reconstruction of the Queendom of Falena, in order to atone for their father's misdeeds.

name: Black Dragon Cooking Clan
description: The Black Dragon Cooking Clan (黒竜料理会, Kokuryū Ryōri Kai) is an organization in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Black Dragon Cooking Clan was a mysterious cooking society based out of Black Dragon Island that united much of the culinary community behind the scenes. Hai Yo was once a member of the organization but the group gradually corrupted as Jinkai and Retso lusted after the ultimate Moon Bird Recipe in order to control the hearts of those they were supposed to serve. Retso's daughter Shun Min would steal the recipe and pass it on to Hai Yo, urging him to flee.

After Hai Yo established himself at the New Alliance Army Headquarters, the Black Dragon Cooking Clan sent several assassin cooks to duel him in an attempt to recapture the recipe. Following the defeat of Grand Wazoo Jinkai, however, the group regained their pride in cooking and the Dragon Plan was abandoned.

name: Black Lions
description: The Black Lions (黒き獅子, Kuroki Shishi) were an elite military unit in South Window City.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The Black Lions were South Window City's elite military unit that had fought many battles against the Scarlet Moon Empire in the past. The inn keeper Zamji was once a member of this organization but was forced to retire after suffering a leg injury.

name: Black Rose Society
description: The Black Rose Society (黒バラ団, Kuro Bara Dan) is an organization mentioned in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: Located in an obscure country, the Black Rose Society contracted Kidd to investigate the kidnapping of one of its ladies following the War of the Champions.

name: Border Guard
description: The Border Guard (国境警備隊, Kokkyou Keibitai), localized as Border Patrol in Suikoden II, is an organization founded immediately after the establishment of the Toran Republic following the Gate Rune War.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The former bandit Varkas was appointed as the commanding officer of this new order following the Gate Rune War and the establishment of the Toran Republic.

During the Dunan Unification War, that meant that Varkas was the commander of the border guards stationed at the border between the Toran Republic and City-States of Jowston.

When the leader of the New Alliance Army attempted to travel to Gregminster, Varkas would insist on escorting him in his capacity as commander.

name: Carna Clan
description: The Carna Clan (カーナー・クラン, Kānā Kuran) was a clan of the Grasslands.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The Carna Clan was the name of the people who reside in present-day Le Buque. When it was occupied by the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia during their invasion of the Grasslands some 50 years prior to the War of the Champions, it was renamed Le Buque as part of Harmonia's assimilation policies.

name: Centurion
description: Centurion (百卒長, Hyakusotsucho) was a rank within the Scarlet Moon Imperial Army. In the 1996 localization of Suikoden, it was localized as the Hundred Man Battalion, a military unit.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: Humphrey Mintz served as a centurion in the Imperial Army. The rank of centurion was the highest that civilian soldiers could achieve within the Imperial Army. In fact, Humphrey was noted as being one of the most famed centurions in the Imperial Army. Humphrey attempted to use his former status as a centurion to gain entrance to the Dragon's Den.

name: Chisha Clan
description: The Chisha Clan (チシャクラン, Chishakuran) are one of the Six Clans of the Grasslands.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The Chisha Clan serves as one of the Grasslands's Six Clans with its members residing in the Chisha Village. Sana, the long-time companion of the Flame Champion, led this clan, known for its gentle, kind-hearted ways. The people of the Chisha Clan live a mostly agrarian lifestyle and so do not have much means to fight or defend themselves.

name: City-States of Jowston
description: The City-States of Jowston (ジョウストン都市同盟, Jousuton Toshidōmei), also known as the Jowston Alliance or simply Jowston, is a decentralized alliance of five cities and one knightly order, formed in year 314 of the solar calendar to counter threats from the Highland Kingdom and the Scarlet Moon Empire.

facts.label: Rulers
facts.value: Alliance Leader, Unknown number of leaders, Uriah, Unknown number of leaders, Darell, Anabelle (?-460)

originalName: ジョウストン都市同盟

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
===Founding and Structure===
The alliance was established in response to the constant aggression from the Highland Kingdom, which had long sought to expand its territory into the Dunan region. The cities and knights united under a decentralized governance system, with policies decided by voting rights allocated according to each member's population.

Muse City, the alliance's capital, served as its political and cultural center, while the Matilda Knights provided military strength. Due its lack of a unified army, the alliance relied on the combined forces of its member cities and occasional mercenary contracts to defend its territories. This loose structure allowed for cultural diversity and economic prosperity, making the City-States a hub for trade and cultural exchange.

===Conflicts and Key Events===
The City-States' history was marked by frequent conflicts with both the Highland Kingdom and the Scarlet Moon Empire. In 428, King Robert Blight launched a major invasion of the City-States. The defense was spearheaded by Genkaku, a legendary commander from Muse, who later negotiated a ceasefire with Han Cunningham, a Highland general and his childhood friend. This peace, though short-lived, was highly regarded and demonstrated the alliance's resilience.

Members of the Jowston Alliance also fought wars of expansion. In 447, forces of Tinto and South Window attempted to take advantage of the War of Succession in the Scarlet Moon Empire by invading only to be eventually repelled the following year.

A similar situation would happen in 456 where those same City-States invaded again during the Gate Rune War, occupying the region of Senan. Following the war, the new Toran Republic would lead a force to reclaim the territory, succeeding by 458.

In 460, the Dunan Unification War erupted after Prince Luca Blight of Highland orchestrated a false flag attack to incite war. Th

name: Clan of the Gate
description: The Clan of the Gate (門の一族 Mon no Ichizoku) was a clan of powerful rune users. They were wiped out several hundred years before the events of Suikoden but their two surviving members still influenced the Gate Rune War.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Hundreds of years ago the Clan of the Gate were a prosperous people. But because of their power, they were massacred by the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia. Windy and Leknaat managed to escape, using the power of the Gate Rune. Windy would go on to look for vengeance against the entire world, searching for the Rune of Life and Death.

Traditionally, members of the clan would refer to each other as siblings, regardless of blood ties. Windy and Leknaat were only distantly related to each as clan members.

name: Cray Trading Company
description: Cray Trading Company (クレイ商会, Kurei Shōkai) was a trading concern that appears in Suikoden IV.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: Cray Trading Company was a business concern established and led by Graham Cray. It had a dark reputation and was known for the smuggling of Rune Cannon parts as well as the buying and selling of mermaids. As such, it attracted a lot of criminals and pirates and operated in underworld circles, although it also presented a respectable face through front companies such as Orark Maritime Trade.

name: Destroyers
description: The Destroyers (破壊者, Hakaimono) are a group in Suikoden III. Their goal is to destroy a True Rune, hence their name. Although loosely united in goal, their motivations differ.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Destroyers is a term used for the group led by Luc, consisting of himself, Yuber, Sarah and Albert Silverberg. Their ultimate goal was the destruction of a True Rune, an action which would have caused unprecedented destruction in the Grasslands and beyond.

It was Yun who first used the term to describe the group. Although they worked together, their motivations differed greatly. For Luc, his goal was to shatter the True Wind Rune and his own soul, which was intertwined with it. Seeing the Gray World through the rune, he was determined to stop this by destroying his rune, even if it means taking the lives of many people.

Sarah fought in order to support Luc. Still, she harbored intense sense of guilt over the loss of so many lives for their cause and questioned Luc about his decisions. Nevertheless, she continued to fight alongside Luc. Yuber, meanwhile, worked alongside Luc because of the death and chaos the destruction of a True Rune would cause and also because of the war that was necessary to forward their plans.

Albert's goal was to use the power of the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia to build a united nation. He cooperated with Luc as a stepping stone to that goal. Still, when Luc felt the need to seize the True Earth Rune from Sasarai and throw away his position as a Bishop, Albert detached himself from Luc, having established himself as a strategist within Harmonia.

name: Duck Clan
description: The Duck Clan (ダッククラン, Dakkukuran) are one of the Six Clans of the Grasslands.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This clan of Duck people are one of the Grasslands's Six Clans and reside in the Duck Village. Members of the Duck Clan are known for their cheerful temperament and are very familiar with Grasslands geography and so are often hired as escorts and guides for tourists and merchants from foreign lands.

When the Flame Champion rose up to fight against the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia in the past, it was the Duck Clan who were first to respond to his call for arms, a fact that the clan is very proud of. Similarly, during the War of the Champions, the Duck Clan would fight alongside the Fire Bringer, wielding their famed Duck Halberds in battle.

name: Dukedom of Gaien
description:
.]]
The Dukedom of Gaien (ガイエン公国, Gaien Kōkoku) is a nation that forms part of the plot and back story for Suikoden IV and is mentioned in Suikoden Tactics.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Dukedom of Gaien was a huge island country located in the south-west Island Nations. A great power in the region, it was bordered to the east by the Kuknasi Sea and to the north by the Rassina Sea.

Gaien was officially a monarchy but around the time of the Island Liberation War, the power of regional governing lords had increased. Governance tended to be left to the discretion of these lords and Gaien functioned almost as a republic while the monarch reigned in name only.

Gaien's primary industry was the production of carpets and clothing as well as ship manufacturing and repair. Farming was rarer and the country tended to rely on food imports.

The country had a long history and especially notable was its many repeated conflicts with the north-eastern Kooluk Empire. Middleport was originally a Gaien territory and there was much bitterness from the nation over its past occupation by Kooluk.

A series of defeats to the Kooluk Empire would mark the beginning of a decline for Gaien's eastern forces. Around the time of the Island Liberation War, the number of regional lords who were loyal only to their own profit and self-preservation rather than to their nation was not inconsiderable.

The Gaien colony of Razril was once home to the Knights of Gaien.
