Due to the changing times within Gaien itself, when Razril was captured during the Island Liberation War, Gaien chose not to send any relief to try and liberate the occupied island, apparently out of a desire not to further aggravate tensions with Kooluk.

That colony would become independent following the Island Liberation War, becoming a member of the new Island Nations Federation.

name: Dunan Monarchy
description: The Dunan Monarchy (デュナン君主国, Deyunan Kunshukuni) was a country that existed across much of the modern Dunan region before the establishment of the City-States of Jowston. It is considered the predecessor to modern South Window City and South Window Principality.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Dunan Monarchy was established in South Window City in the year 110 by Bernand. Although Bernand was said to have been a despotic ruler, it is said he created a firm foundation for the nation. The monarchy was said to have reached its greatest heights under King Beryl, three generations after its founding. The nation stretched from Two River Principality in the west to Muse Principality in the east.

Due to a lack of foreign threat (Holy Harmonia having no southern designs at this time), the military gradually weakened in stature. It had frequent border clashes with Muse Principality, following its independence in 185. Two River Principality also broke away in 212. Following this, the strength of the Dunan Monarchy was broken.

The nation limped on as a spent force until 250 when civil war broke out and the monarchy was overthrown by 252 with a mayoral position being established in South Window City. Sometimes the name of the Dunan Monarchy is used by armies since that time in order to invoke a sense of prestige.

name: Dunan Republic
description: The Dunan Republic (デュナン共和国, Deyunan Kyōwakoku) is a country located in the eastern region of the northern continent. It was established as the successor state of both the City-States of Jowston and the Highland Kingdom at the end of Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Dunan Republic was formed from the victorious New Alliance Army after the Dunan Unification War. It is a vast nation centerd around Lake Dunan, incorporating the territories of the old City-States of Jowston and the former Highland Kingdom.

It was expected that the leader of the New Alliance Army would be appointed President with the backing of the former City-State leaders but he left the nation around the time of its founding. Instead former Greenhill City Mayor Teresa Wisemail was appointed Acting President with Shu as her Chancellor. 

Two years later, Teresa would be elected President by representatives of each city. Shu would step down from his position and Klaus Windamier would be appointed Chancellor in his place. In other regions of the country, Fitcher would become Mayor of Muse City and Miklotov would be appointed leader of the Matilda Knights.

name: Falenan Royal Navy
description: The Falenan Royal Navy (ファレナ女王国水軍, Farena On'na Ōkoku Suigun) is the main maritime military force of the Queendom of Falena.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Royal Navy is the main maritime fleet of the Queendom of Falena. As befitting the nation's dependence on the Feitas River, the majority of its ships are, in fact, river going vessels. Due to the Feitas' size, however, this does not imply that the navy's vessels are diminutive or lacking in martial power compared to ocean-going vessels.

Ships of the Royal Navy are known for their excellent maneuvering capabilities as well as for being well drilled in the science of warfare. During the Armes Invasion, Raja served as Admiral of the Royal Navy. After her retirement when the war ended, she would be succeeded by her protégé, Bahram Luger, who would command the force against the Imperial City Recapture Army during the Sun Rune War.

name: Feitas Dragon Cavalry
description: The Feitas Dragon Cavalry (フェイタス竜馬騎兵団, Feitasu Ryōma Kiheidan) is a military corps in Suikoden V. It is a Falenan dragon horse unit committed to internal neutrality.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Based at Sauronix Castle in the south of the Queendom of Falena, this cavalry unit fought alongside and coexisted with dragon horses. A dragon horse rider was only allowed to go into battle after training and becoming a full-fledged rider together with their dragon horse partner.

The Feitas Dragon Cavalry was known for its strict rules, such as "no involvement in internal conflicts in Falena" and "no women in the cavalry". The cavalry's stubborn adherence to these principles was generally considered commendable, but at times was regarded as obstinate. At the time of the Sun Rune War, Craig Laden was the commander of the group but attempted to keep the cavalry neutral in order to abide by the rule that the Dragon Cavalry would not get involved in internal politics.

However, a Godwin Faction plot put the young dragon horses and their eggs in danger, and the Dragon Cavalry decided to cooperate with the Imperial City Recapture Army out of gratitude for their help during that crisis. After the war ended, Rahal took over as commander and there have since been signs of change, such as the acceptance of female dragon horse riders.

The first female Dragon Cavalry member was Lun from Raftfleet.

name: Fire Bringer
description: The Fire Bringer (炎の運び手, Honō no Hakobite), localized in Suikoden II as the Keepers of the Flame, were a bandit and resistance group which struggled against invasion by the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia on two separate occasions.

facts.label: Allies
facts.value: Free Knights of Camaro, Six Clans, Tinto Republic, Zexen Federation

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
===Original Fire Bringer===
 attempted to use the name of the Fire Bringer for intimidation purposes.]]The Fire Bringer were originally a small band of thieves, led by the Flame Champion that operated in the Grasslands during the 420s. When this group began to resist the Harmonian invasion of the Grasslands, other groups and rebels began to gather under its banner. The Duck Clan is noted in history for being the first of the Grasslands clans to join the Fire Bringer and other clans would soon follow suit increasing the groups size with each new clan added. This would eventually make the Fire Bringer the grand allied force that united the Grasslands against their common foe.

Following the final battle against the Harmonian army, in which the Flame Champion's True Fire Rune went out of control, decimating both armies and forcing a truce between the two powers, the Flame Champion disappeared, and the Fire Bringer was dissolved.

Since then, the Fire Bringer passed into legend, known for their fight against Harmonia and their stealing of a True Rune from the One Temple in Crystal Valley. Groups of bandits that plague the Grasslands since have occasionally taken up the name of the Fire Bringer, but these groups of thieves are generally considered as fakes, unworthy of the title.

Once such band of outlaws attacked Milit Village shortly after the end of the Dunan Unification War in 460 but were exposed and thwarted by Nash Latkje, Camus and Miklotov among others.

===Second Fire Bringer===
 invasion of Grasslands would spark a revival of the Fire Bringer.]]In 475, when the War of the Champions broke out between the Grasslands clans and the Zexen Federation, rumors of a resurgent Fire Bringer and the return of the Flame Champion would spread. Many people tried to trace the trail of the Flame Champion, but it was not until the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia began their renewed inv

name: Free Knights of Camaro
description: The Free Knights of Camaro (カマロ自由騎士連合, Kamaro Jiyū Kishi Rengō), rendered as the Kamaro Alliance of Free Knights in Suikoden III, are a nation and order first mentioned in Suikoden II that later appears in Suikoden III. They are a band of knights from the Grasslands.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Camaro Knights are a knightly order who hold territories in the Grasslands. Their green and gold garb distinguishes them, as does their philosophy which is that they should serve as a shield to protect those who cannot protect themselves.

In the past, the Free Knights of Camaro have allied with the Karaya Clan and the Chisha Clan, fighting together to repel invasions of the Grasslands by Tinto City. In SY 434, the knights would come under attack from a nation in the Nameless Lands.

In the resulting battle, the Camaro forces, led by Godefroy would repel the attackers, despite only fielding 7,000 troops to their opponent's 12,000.

In Camaro, only eldest sons may rise to knighthood, leading some younger sons to leave and make their own path. Camus, member of the Matilda Knights was one such native of Camaro who left to pursue a knightly career in Jowston. The Camaro notions of chivalry may have influenced Camus' passion for justice.

The knight Mua led a contingent of knights on the side of the Fire Bringer during the War of the Champions.

name: Godwin Faction
description: The Godwin Faction (ゴドウイン派, Godouin-ha) was one of two major political groups in the Queendom of Falena during Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The House of Godwin was one of two major political bodies that made up the political make-up of the Queendom of Falena. Centered around the Godwin family of Stormfist, the body is made up of western nobles. They are mainly hardliners who believe that the nation should use the power of the Sun Rune to establish political hegemony through complete control of Falena's neighbors. They were also characterized by their discrimination against non-human races, which they derided as inferior.

name: Gordon's Mercentile
description: Gordon's Mercentile (ゴードン商会, Gōdon Shoukai, Gordon Company) was the name of Gordon's trading concern.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Gordon's Mercentile was headed by Gordon and centered on his trading post in the city of Gregminster. It was one of the leading trading companies in the city.

Eager to expand his business concerns, Gordon left the running of his Gregminster business to his son, Coates, when he joined the New Alliance Army and opened a new trading post at the New Alliance Army Headquarters in Dunan.

name: Greenhill Principality
description: Greenhill Principality (グリンヒル市国, Gurinhiru Shikuni, Greenhill City-State) is a region in Suikoden II. It is a constituent city-state of the City-States of Jowston.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Greenhill Principality lies to the west of Muse Principality and in the past was the western part of the principality before gaining its independence. To the north-east lies the Matilda Knightdom and in the south lies Two River Principality. To the west lies the eastern frontiers of the Grasslands.

In the past, the lands of Greenhill formed the western territories of Muse Principality. The region would become an independent city-state in SY 373 with the blessing of Muse mayor Uriah after the city’s Grand Leaf Academy insisted on "academic independence".

It would become a member of the City-States of Jowston five years later, in 378, once the transfer of government facilities was complete. The Wisemail family has traditionally served as mayors of the city.

Prior to the 460 Dunan Unification War, poor harvests and lack of industry beyond its educational facilities led to Greenhill suffering a deficit of contributions, and thus influence, within the City-States of Jowston. During the conflict, Greenhill would fall to the Highland invasion.

Late in the war, the city was liberated by the New Alliance Army. Following the war, the principality would join the new Dunan Republic.

name: Highland Army
description: The Highland Army (ハイランド王国軍, Hairando Ōkokugun) are the armed forces of the Highland Kingdom. It serves as the antagonistic force for Suikoden II.

facts.label: Allies
facts.value: Holy Kingdom of Harmonia, Karaya Clan

facts.label: Opponents
facts.value: City-States of Jowston, New Alliance Army

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Because the Highland Kingdom has had antagonistic relations with other cities and nations since its establishment, great efforts had been put into the development of its military. Each general of a company was expected to rise to the position by merit, in order to ensure that only the strong and elite would battle the enemy.

In addition, Highland actively hired mercenaries and incorporated them into their forces. Also, organizations such as the Unicorn Brigade trained young boys as soldiers, developing those who would be making up the Highland Army in the future.

There was little tradition of dedicated military strategy in Highland Army culture. Highland martial culture emphasized military prowess and generals were expected to formulate tactics themselves.

Highland camps normally functioned under three 8-hour shifts, with soldiers been given a small ration of alcohol following their shift.

name: Highland Kingdom
description: The Highland Kingdom (ハイランド王国, Hairando Ōkoku) was a nation located on the Northern continent. It forms part of the setting for Suikoden II.

facts.label: Rulers
facts.value: King, Mauro Blight (237-?), 19 unknown Kings, Robert Blight (?-434), Agares Blight (434-460), Luca Blight (460), Jowy Blight (460)

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
In the past, the Highland region existed as part of the southern territories of the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia. The Highland Kingdom was founded in the year 237. In that year, Mauro Blight received the Beast Rune, the Platinum Spear, and the title of King from Harmonia for his work in quelling civil war in the nation. Although ostensibly a reward for faithful service, the lands were granted with an ulterior motive; to provide Harmonia with a buffer zone against rebellious states, such as the Scarlet Moon Empire.

Centered around its capital of L'Renouille and being a military-focused nation, Highland had been involved in repeated military conflicts, for many years, with the City-States of Jowston. The roots of this conflict extend back to the year 252 when Highland took advantage of civil war in the Dunan Monarchy, invading what was then eastern South Window (modern day eastern Muse).

As Highland had a smaller area and population compared to Jowston, the nation saw a necessity in keeping an advantage in terms of military power, leading to a strong military influence as well as a strong meritocracy.

428 saw the beginning of the Highland-Jowston war, with Highland under the reign of King Robert and the military leadership of Han Cunningham, invading Jowston and quickly seizing Muse City. Greenhill and Matilda would soon follow. However, 432 saw the Jowston Army's Great Offensive push back the invading force, liberating Greenhill and Matilda.

The moderate King Agares' ascension to the throne in 434 allowed for a cease-fire to be agreed on between the two nations, following the settlement of a border dispute centered on Kyaro Town and an uneasy peace settled in for a while although espionage and covert attacks, such as the one which struck the Blight royal caravan in 443, continued.

 was the first of three kings to fall in Highland's final days.]]Highland's more peacefu

name: Holy Kingdom of Harmonia
description: The Holy Kingdom of Harmonia (ハルモニア神聖国, Harumonia Shinseikuni), known more informally as Holy Harmonia or simply Harmonia, is a large superstate located in the northernmost region of the northern continent. It borders the Dunan Republic to the south and the Grasslands to the west.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
writing reading 'The Holy Harmonia Kingdom']]
The Holy Kingdom of Harmonia was founded in Solar Year -2 with the overthrow of the Kingdom of Aronia by the hero Hikusaak. Two years later, Hikusaak would take the title of High Priest, setting up the priest-based government that would rule the nation from the Circle Palace.

The nation would become a powerhouse in the region, home to a powerful, peerless military and strong, influential culture. The nation's national policy would become centered around the acquisition of the 27 True Runes. This aggressive foreign policy has led to a large number of invasions and annexations in Harmonia's history.

Harmonia's culture has made it an example of higher learning and many academics throughout history have studied there. Many promising students from other nations journey to Harmonia in order to study there. The One Temple in the nation's capital, Crystal Valley, is famed as a source of all the world's knowledge. A great number of guilds also exist in the nation, one of the most feared being the Howling Voice Guild.

The nation is set up that, officially, it is under the absolute rule of High Priest Hikusaak. However, Hikusaak sometimes does not appear in public for great lengths of time and so, in reality, Harmonia's political system is under the influence of the aristocracy.

The aristocracy is divided into two factions: the Temple Faction, which supports Hikusaak's absolute rule and the People's Faction with aims for a balance of power between the priesthood and the people. These absences have led to internal conflict and civil war in the past.

Harmonia also functions under a strict class system. First-class citizens are pure-blooded Harmonian citizens, distinguishable by their blonde hair and blue eyes. Second-class citizens are citizens from nations and territories that did not resist Harmonian annexation or otherwise prov

name: Howling Voice Guild
description: The Howling Voice Guild (吠え猛る声の組合, Hoe Takeru Koe no Kumiai), translated as the Roaring Brotherhood in Suikoden III, is an organization located in the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia. Their agents appear in several titles in the Suikoden series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Howling Voice Guild holds expertise in the alchemical field of gunpowder and firearms, engaging in escort missions and assassinations on behalf of influential individuals. They are based out of "The Tower", their enigmatic headquarters.

Its members, known as gunners, are proficient wielders of guns and are categorized into different classes, including pages, squires, knights, and the Guildmaster. Renowned for their formidable combat skills, the gunners bolster the Howling Voice Guild's considerable military power and influence within the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia.

Though the Howling Voice Guild operates within the realm of Harmonia, it retains an independent status, closely cooperating with the Holy Kingdom. Consequently, it has been referred to derogatorily as "Harmonia's dogs" on various occasions.

Noteworthy figures within the guild include Clive, Elza, and Zaj, who each held significant roles as gunners. Following Elza's passing, Clive assumed the position of Guildmaster.

name: Imperial City Recapture Army
description: The Imperial City Recapture Army (王都奪還軍, Ōto Dakkan-gun) is the protagonist's force in Suikoden V. It was formed to resist the Godwin Faction coup d'etat and formed one side of the Sun Rune War.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
In SY 449, the initial Imperial City Recapture Army was established by the Prince of Falena to resist the Godwin Faction, who had rebelled against the Queendom of Falena's royal family, beginning the Sun Rune War.

Initially, the force acted as a rebel army under the backing of Salum Barows, but after the Barows Faction's theft of the Dawn Rune came to light the two parted ways. At this point a truly independent forced led by the prince was born. With the aid of Lucretia Merces, the army established the Ceras Lake Headquarters and became a formidable force both militarily and diplomatically.

As the Sun Rune War continued not only did the Recapture Army achieve victories in battle against the Godwin Faction but also entered into agreements with the Island Nations Federation, Feitas Dragon Cavalry, and even the Godwin's former allies in the New Armes Kingdom.

Following the liberation of Sol-Falena and Queen Lymsleia Falenas, the army would stand down with its officers falling into a variety of roles in the nation post-war.

name: Imperial Faction
description: The Imperial Faction (皇王派, Sumeragiō-ha) was one of two major political groups in the Kooluk Empire during Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Along with the Patriarchal Faction, the Imperial Faction was one of the two major political blocs in Kooluk politics. Save for high-ranking officers, only immediate members of the Imperial family could belong to this faction. Their beliefs are simply that the Emperor and the Imperial family command the Empire and need brook no insubordination from other forces.

Compared to the Patriarchal Faction, which tended to drive internal affairs, the Imperial Faction tended to drive national policy for the Empire as a whole. As the tension between the two factions grew into outright political instability, the Imperial Faction's losses in the Island Liberation War, including the death of Troy, regarded by many as the last truly great Imperial officer, laid the seeds for a coup d'etat to be planned by Iskas.

Iskas' plan would destroy the weakened Imperials but his own defeat at the hands of Kyril would leave the Empire in ruins before being dissolved by Princess Corselia.

name: Island Nations Federation
description: The Island Nations Federation (群島諸国連合, Guntō Shokoku Rengō, Archipelago Nation Alliance) is an archipelago nation located to the south of the Northern Continent, in the Island Nations region of the Kuknasi Sea. It was founded at the end of Suikoden IV and also appears in Suikoden Tactics.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Island Nations Federation was established following the Island Liberation War, as a union of islands from the Island Nations region, centerd around the Kingdom of Obel.

The nation was engaged in a positive trading relationship with the Queendom of Falena, using Nirva Island as a neutral port.

name: Kanakan
description: Kanakan (カナカン, Kanakan) is a region located on the Northern continent. It is mentioned in several games of the series.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: Kanakan is a region that exists south of the Toran Republic. It is renowned for its famous liquor. Many people love Kanakan-produced liquor, including Viktor and Anabelle. Several Stars have come from Kanakan, including Hoi, Lotte and Templeton.

name: Karaya Clan
description: The Karaya Clan (カラヤクラン, Karayakuran or カラヤ族, Karaya Zoku) are one of the Six Clans of the Grasslands.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Karaya Clan, led by Chief Lucia, are one the numerous clans that reside in the Grasslands as well as one of its Six Clans. Members of the clan are known for their distinctive brown skin, colorful clothing and their use of curved swords in battle. Their home is the Karaya Village.

They are known to be a people with excellent fighting skills, and have repeatedly repelled forces from Tinto Principality, forcing their retreat from the Grasslands. They were also involved in disputes with Greenhill Principality and the Matilda Knightdom in the past.

During the Dunan Unification War, the Karaya Clan allied with the Highland Kingdom, out of resentment of their treatment by the City-States of Jowston and the promise of the restoration of historical Karayan lands. Following the war, they entered into reconciliation with the new Dunan Republic.

Their skills in combat make them very influential within the Six Clans, despite their relatively small numbers. Their chief, Lucia, who has led the clan since the Dunan Unification War has something of a leadership role within the Six Clans because of this.

Male warriors of the Karaya used jambiya, a type of dagger which had a distorted blade, were double-edged, and had a groove carved in the center of the blade. Young warriors transitioned to larger blades as they grew. It was a matter of personal choice whether to wield their weapons backward or frontward.

While jambiya were popular among male Karayan warriors, female warriors, with less upper-body strength, tended to use more diverse weapons suited to their talents. Aila, for example, wielded a bow as it allowed her to combine long-range attacks with her ability to located targets by communing with the earth spirits.

name: Katana
description: Katana (カタナ, Katana) is the nickname given to a nation whose actual name is unknown. It is the homeland of Genshu and Hazuki.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Sake is a traditional liquor in this land and has spread outside the country to some extent. The type of sake produced there has its own name, but to outsiders was frequently referred to simply as "katana sake."

The word "katana", although used in many countries, traces its origin to this land. Young people from this nation frequently went on journeys of training and self-discovery before returning home.

Swordplay in this country focuses on developing speed and nimbleness in combat.

name: Kingdom of Aronia
description: The Kingdom of Aronia (アロニア王国, Aronia Ōkoku) is a nation mentioned in several Suikoden series publications. It was a predecessor of sorts to the modern Holy Kingdom of Harmonia.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: Little is known about the Kingdom of Aronia save that it was defeated by the hero Hikusaak in Solar Year -2. Two years later, Hikusaak would take his place as ruler of the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia.

name: Kingdom of Zelant
description: The Kingdom of Zelant (ゼアラント王国, Zearanto Ōkoku) is a nation mentioned in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Kingdom of Zelant is a nation located to the west of the Queendom of Falena, across the sea. Zelantian armor is well known and, in fact, (false) stereotypes would have us believe that all Zelantians wear armor in everyday life. Shields are also imported to Falena from Zelant, via the Hershville Naval Base on Falena's western coast. 

The Lindwurm Mercenary Brigade is said to be stationed in Zelant. Norma and Ernst also both come from Zelant and following the Sun Rune War journeyed back to the country along with Levi in order to investigate the ruins where Ernst first came across his cursed Beast Rune.

name: Knights of Gaien
description: The Knights of Gaien (ガイエン海上騎士団, Gaien Kaijō Kishi-dan, Gaien Marine Knights) are a knightly order in Suikoden IV. They are the organization the hero and his companions belong to at the beginning of the game.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Knights of Gaien were a naval order based on the island of Razril, an island of the Dukedom of Gaien. The organization was responsible for the protection of waters surrounding Razril. This would most frequently mean the subduing of pirates in the area and the escort of ships traversing their waters.

In the past, when Gaien held a greater focus on its eastern waters, the Knights of Gaien had fought in several conflicts against the Kooluk Empire. The year 299 (solar calendar) saw the knights, as part of the greater Gaien navy, suffer a catastrophic defeat against the Kooluk navy at El-Eal Fortress. This defeat would effectively end Gaien ambitions on its eastern waters.

At the beginning of the Island Liberation War, the order was commanded by Glen Cott. However, as the group relied on its funding from Lord Vingerhut, the Lord of Razril, Commander Glen would often find he could not ignore Vingerhut's influences and wishes as regards the knight's activities or membership.

Following Glen's death, the Knights of Gaien would be led by Lord Vingerhut's son, Snowe. Under Snowe's direction the order would collaborate with their Kooluk occupiers until the liberation of the island later in the war. Following this, the remaining order would ally with their liberators.

With Razril declaring its independence following the war and the formation of the Island Nations Federation, the Knights of Gaien would be disbanded and reformed, this time as the Knights of Razril.

name: Knights of the Dragon's Den
description: The Knights of the Dragon's Den (竜洞騎士団, Ryū Hora Kishidan), more simply referred to as Dragon Knights, are a military order that existed in the Toran region. They are an order of knights who are able to bond with dragons so as to fight on them in battle.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Based mainly at the Dragon's Den and Goya Fortress, the Knights of the Dragon's Den had traditionally been granted autonomy by the Scarlet Moon Empire leading to a mutually beneficial relationship. On the one hand, the Empire was able the obtain the services of the Dragon Knights and in return, the Empire would protect the Dragon Knights during the dragon's spawning season where they would otherwise be completely vulnerable.

Dragon Knights had fought in the War of Succession on the side of Barbarossa Rugner. However, as the Empire began to corrupt, relations between the two cooled during the Gate Rune War, especially in light of the attempted poisoning of the dragons in the Dragon's Den. They would align themselves with the Toran Liberation Army for the remainder of the war.

The knights were led by Joshua Levenheit, bearer of the Dragon Rune, with Anos, and later Milia, operating as his Vice-Commander. Since the Gate Rune War, command of the knights and the Dragon Rune has fallen to the hands of Milia. Futch was a member of the Dragon Knights who lost his position with the death of his dragon, Black, but subsequently regained it and his position, eventually rising to Milia's Vice-Commander in turn.

The knights are divided between nine different ranks, ranked from 9th to 1st class. Knight and dragon numbers were always in strict equilbrium through their bonding ceremonies. 9th class Dragon Knights were considered apprentices and was comprised of those who had newly-formed bonds with their dragons.

8th class Dragon Knights were regarded as squires and knight groups usually consisted of three 7th class regular knights and two squires, under the command of a 6th class platoon commander. 5th class Dragon Knights served as company commanders, with each company being made up of three platoons. 4th class Dragon Knights were in turn battalion captains, with a battalion con

name: Kooluk Empire
description: The Kooluk Empire (クールーク皇国, Kūrūku Kōkoku) is the antagonistic nation of Suikoden IV that also forms part of the setting of Suikoden Tactics. The nation collapsed at the end of the latter game.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Kooluk Empire was a great power located to the north of the Island Nations, ruled in its final days by Emperor Julius. Possessing powerful military strength, it was often in conflict with the Dukedom of Gaien to the southwest and the Scarlet Moon Empire to its north. In the years prior to its collapse, it had entered into a cold war conflict with Scarlet Moon following a ceasefire agreement.

The nation's government had been traditionally divided between the Imperial Faction, made up of those related to the emperor, and the Patriarchal Faction, which consisted of the ordinary officers of the Empire. Sandwiched between the northern Scarlet Moon Empire and southern pirates, internal tensions began to rise along with the number of citizens living in poverty. The Empire had many towns and territories under its control, large and small, with administrators appointed in each location.

In 306, with forces freed up from the ceasefire with Scarlet Moon, Kooluk attempted to expand its territory with an invasion of the Island Nations, under the guise of modernising the region with a Southern Expansion Policy but was repelled in the Island Liberation War. Following the failure of the Southern Expansion Policy, pushed heavily by the Imperial Faction, internal pressures continue to build, resulting in an attempted coup d'état by the Patriarchal Faction. 

This coup would consist of Iskas, leader of the Patriarchal Faction, using the power of the Evil Eye to kill both Emperor Julius as well as Prince Martin, leading the path clear to complete control of the Empire. However, due to the efforts of Kyril as well as Corselia, who was next in line to the throne, Iskas' plans were thwarted. However, following this coup, Corselia would choose to dissolve the nation, closing the curtain on the history of this one great nation.

name: Lindwurm Mercenary Brigade
description: The Lindwurm Mercenary Brigade (リンドブルム傭兵旅団, Rindoburumu Yōhei Ryodan) are a military brigade that appear in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Lindwurm Mercenary Brigade was a huge mercenary organization based in the Kingdom of Zelant. With a total of about 5,000 mercenaries, members of the brigade are usually hired in squads of dozens or hundreds of mercenary soldiers.

Wilhelm, assisted by Mueller and Richard, led an attachment of the brigade during the Sun Rune War. Initially contracted by Salum Barows to fight the Godwin Faction, they would withdraw from battle, citing breach of contract, when the presence of New Armes forces was revealed.

The same brigade would later be hired by the Prince of Falena later in the war. There, they would fight loyally, with no contract breaches on either side.

name: Lizard Clan
description: The Lizard Clan (リザードクラン, Rizādo Kuran) are one of the Six Clans of the Grasslands.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Lizard Clan is a group of lizards who live in the Great Hollow in the Grasslands. They are one of the region's Six Clans. They are a very powerful clan and by using the tunnels of the Great Hollow to traverse the Grasslands stealthily, they are also known as elusive and able to strike quickly and without warning.

Members of the Lizard Clan traditionally fight with grides, spear-like weapons with a long three-pronged blade at the tip. Zepon was Chief of the Lizard Clan just prior to the War of the Champions. His assassination by the Destroyers would be the catalyst for the war and he would be succeeded by Dupa.

name: Matilda Knightdom
description: The Matilda Knightdom (マチルダ騎士団領, Machiruda Kishidan Ryō) is a region in Suikoden II. It is a constituent city-state of the City-States of Jowston.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Matilda Knightdom lies to the north of Muse Principality and is the home of the formidable Matilda Knights.

To the south-west lies Greenhill Principality and in the east lies Higheast Province, the former Highland Kingdom. The Holy Kingdom of Harmonia is located north of the Knightdom while heading west takes one to the Grasslands.

Initially a defense force to protect Muse City from invasion from the Highland Kingdom, the Matilda Knights would be granted land of its own in SY 294. This knightdom would become a founding member of the City-States of Jowston in 314.

It would boast the largest military force in the alliance and wielded considerable influence due to this. During the 460 Dunan Unification War, the head of the knights, Gordeau, refused several times to fight in battles against the Highland Army.

When Gordeau eventually allied with Highland, the knightdom would be occupied by its allies and eventually be invaded by the New Alliance Army. Rockaxe Castle would be taken and marked the end of the knightdom as a city-state.

Following the war, with the aid of Camus and Miklotov, the Matilda Knights were rebuilt and the knightdom would become part of the new Dunan Republic.

name: Matilda Knights
description: The Matilda Knights (マチルダ騎士団, Machiruda Kishidan) are a military order that appears in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Matilda Knights were founded in SY 280 with the financial support of Muse City. Initially a defense force to protect Muse City from invasion from the Highland Kingdom, it would become an independent body of its own in 294 and was a founding member of the City-States of Jowston in 314.

It would be the largest military force in the City-State and wielded considerable influence due to this. During the Dunan Unification War, the head of the knights, Gordeau, refused several times to fight in battles against the Highland Army, ultimately causing many knights to resign and fight with the New Alliance Army.

When Gordeau eventually allied with Highland, the order was fragmented further. Following the war, with the aid of Camus and Miklotov, the Matilda Knights were rebuilt.

name: Maximillian Knights
description: The Maximillian Knights (マクシミリアン騎士団, Makushimirian Kishi-Dan), also known as the Knights of Maximillian were a knightly order that existed in the old Scarlet Moon Empire.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Maximillian Knights were established by their namesake, Maximillian. They fought for the Scarlet Moon Empire though they were not an official recognized organization. As such, records of their activities are spotty though it is known they enjoyed popular support due to their championing of justice.

At some point in the past, they chose to aid the City-States of Jowston in one of their invasions by the Highland Kingdom. For acting without permission, the order was officially disbanded by imperial authorities, fearful of the political repercussions of a quasi-official order fighting in a conflict between two foreign powers.

During the Sun Rune War, Isabel and Mathias fought for the ideals of the Maximillian Knights as part of the Imperial City Recapture Army. Isabel's father had been a Maximillian Knight before the group's censure.

During the Gate Rune War, Maximillian and his squire, Sancho, joined forces with the Toran Liberation Army to overthrow the corrupt Scarlet Moon Empire. There were still many who admired Maximillian, and many former members of the Knights joined the Liberation Army alongside him. 1,200 soldiers in all joined the Liberation Army alongside Maximillian, an enormous number.

Three years later, Maximillian, now without his squire, aided the New Alliance Army during the Dunan Unification War, once again fighting against the Highland Kingdom. He then retired, his grandson Fred Maximillian taking over the leadership of the Maximilian Knights. Fred would later join the forces of the Fire Bringer.

name: Millian's Armor Emporium
description: Millian's Armor Emporium (ミリアン防具商会, Mirian Bōgu Shoukai) is the name of a business in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Millian's Armor Emporium was a popular armor store located in Muse City. It was run by Millian and Melia, who were reputed to be beautiful sisters, and the store and its goods and services were very well regarded.

Melia would be killed during Luca Blight's occupation of the city and Millian ran the store alone from then on.

name: Muse Principality
description: Muse Principality (ミューズ市国, Myūzu Shikuni, Muse City-State) is a region in Suikoden II. It is a constituent city-state of the City-States of Jowston.

facts.label: Rulers
facts.value: Mayor, Revier, Jowston, Uriah, Darell, Anabelle

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Muse Principality lies north of Lake Dunan. Due to its location in a temperate zone, Muse Principality usually has a mild climate.

To the north lies the Matilda Knightdom and in the west lies Greenhill Principality. To the north-east lies Higheast Province, the former Highland Kingdom.

Muse City, once a part of the Dunan Monarchy, gained its status as a city-state in SY 185 during the fall of the monarchy. Its inaugural mayor was Revier.

In 252, the collapse of the Dunan Monarchy and the subsequent Highland invasion would see Muse attack the occupied former eastern lands of South Window, bringing them under Muse's control. At its height, the city-state controlled roughly two-thirds of the Dunan region.

Positioned strategically, the city remained at the forefront of conflicts with the Highland Kingdom. The city-state would later become one of the founding members of the City-States of Jowston in 314 and served as the center of the alliance ever since.

The City-State of Muse once controlled the lands of the Matilda Knightdom and modern Greenhill Principality but later relinquished control of both. In 294, it would agree to grant the Matilda Knights a domain and in 373 it would grant Greenhill City independence after a petition from the Grand Leaf Academy there.

During the 460 Dunan Unification War, Muse would swiftly fall to the Highland onslaught despite an army of some 15,000 soldiers. Its population would be decimated by Luca Blight and its surviving forces were scattered, swiftly removing the pillar of the alliance as a factor in the conflict.

Eventually, the remnant of those forces would be organized by Hauser and Jess and align with the New Alliance Army. Following the war, the former principality would join the new Dunan Republic.

name: Nagarea
description: Nagarea (ナガール教主国, Nagāru Kyōshu-koku, Nagarea Hierarch Nation) is a nation mentioned in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Nagarea is a religious state located to the south of the Queendom of Falena. It was founded sometime around SY 330 through the uniting of a group of small, southern nations and acted as a violent theocracy.

The nation is known for its "armed missionary group", which in actuality serves as little more than an invading army. It was with this army that Nagarea invaded southern Falena around SY 360, the Nagarist Order claiming that dragon horses were evil and needed to be "cleansed" from the world. This Nagarist invasion would bind the Dragon-Horse People and the Queendom of Falena together and the threat would be repelled.

Since that time, the threat of Nagarist invasion loomed over southern Falena, emphasising the importance of the Feitas Dragon Cavalry but with the Great Earthquake destroying the land route that connected Falena and Nagarea in SY 376 the Nagarist threat has diminished greatly. Along with its wars against Falena, the New Armes Kingdom also considered Nagarea a potential enemy. The Lindwurm Mercenary Brigade also saw high demand from Nagarea's enemies.

Since the Great Earthquake, it is said that Nagarea has become an insular, isolationist nation. Following the dissolution of Nether Gate, two of its leaders; Kayanu, head of assassin training, and Takefutsu, head of assassination duties, would flee to Nagarea where they are said to have stayed ever since.

name: Nether Gate
description: Nether Gate (幽世の門, Kakuriyo no Mon) is an organization in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Nether Gate was a covert assassination group that reported directly to the Queen of Falena. The group recruited children by a variety of means, pushing them through a thorough education, molding them into killers and spies who would then be dispatched as assassins or intelligence personnel.

The group was made up of several departments, including drug development, led by Tsuranami, which produced many powerful drugs such as Raging Nostrum and Dark Arcanum.

During the royal family succession struggles between Falzrahm and Shahrewar, many lives on both sides were lost to Nether Gate and their assassinations. Once Arshtat ascended to the throne, Ferid would disband the organization. However, out of the group's executives, only Oboro, head of the intelligence division, agreed to the dissolution. Hinoyagi, head of the sabotage division, would resist the order to disband to the last, committing suicide rather than being captured.

Tsurunami disappeared while Kayanu, head of assassin training, and Takefutsu, head of assassination duties, would flee to Nagarea where they are said to have stayed ever since. With the dissolution of Nether Gate, Falena would then rely on its regular army for defense.

However, when the Sun Rune War began, it would be revealed that remnants of Nether Gate had survived, having been organized as a private army under the control of Marscal Godwin. With the fall of the Godwin Faction, the organization was disbanded again, seemingly for good.

name: New Alliance Army
description: The New Alliance Army (新同盟軍, Shin Dōmei-gun) was a group of soldiers and other volunteers who fought against the Highland Kingdom during the Dunan Unification War.

name: New Armes Kingdom
description: The New Armes Kingdom (アーメス新王国, Āmesu Shin Ōkoku) is a nation in Suikoden V. It is a long-standing political and military rival of the Queendom of Falena.

facts.label: Rulers
facts.value: King, Halad (140-?), Unknown number of leaders, Jalat (441-)

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The New Armes Kingdom is a cooperative nation made up of five clans located to the southeast of the Queendom of Falena. The nation was established around the year SY 140 by Halad, ruler of the Ishvaak Clan. The kingdom claims to be the successor of the Ancient Armes Dynasty but there is no evidence linking the two nations whatsoever, making this more of a propaganda piece than a legitimate claim.

During the Sun Rune War, King Jalat of the Ishvaak ruled the nation. Jalat had become king after the previous king was overthrown following his failure in the Armes Invasion of SY 441.

Among the clans, the Darja Clan, most notably the Sparna family, were know as hawkish, pro-war hardliners. Still, moderate groups such as the Ishavaak and Madra Clans, and the Valya family, also existed, balancing out their pro-war tendencies to a degree.

name: Orark Maritime Trade
description: managed Orark Maritime Trade as part of his duties working for Graham Cray.]]Orark Maritime Trade (オラーク海運, Orāku Kaiun) was a trading concern mentioned in Suikoden IV.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Orark Maritime Trade was a shipping company managed by Ramada. Ostensibly the company dealt with the trade of spices but was, in reality, a front for the more notorious Cray Trading Company. As such it dealt with more suspicious activities such as the transport of Rune Cannons.

As part of Cray Trading Company, it would become involved in an number of incidents, including the transport of Magnus Rune Cannon ammunition which would later be used to destroy Iluya Island, hiring pirates to attack Razril and the attempt to assassinate the bearer of the Rune of Punishment on Obel.

name: Patriarchal Faction
description: The Patriarchal Faction (長老派, Chōrō-ha) was one of two major political groups in the Kooluk Empire during Suikoden IV and Suikoden Tactics.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Along with the Imperial Faction, the Patriarchal Faction was one of the two major political blocs in Kooluk politics. It was run by the organization known as the Central Committee, comprised of all non-Imperial Faction officers, save for those of truly low status.

Compared to the Imperial Faction, which tended to drive national policy for the Empire as a whole, the Patriarchal Faction's influence was strongest in internal affairs. As the tension between the two factions grew into outright political instability, the Imperial Faction's losses in the Island Liberation War laid the seeds for a coup d'etat to be planned by Iskas.

Iskas' plan would destroy the weakened Imperials but his own defeat at the hands of Kyril would leave the Empire in ruins before being dissolved by Princess Corselia.

name: People's Faction
description: The People's Faction (民衆派, Minshū-ha) was one of two major political groups in the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia and is shown most prominently in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The People's Faction was one of the blocs within the aristocracy of the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia. It aimed to break away from the absolute rule of High Priest Hikusaak and advocated a form of power-sharing between the temple and representatives of the citizenry.

As such, it was at odds with the Temple Faction, which advocated for Hikusaak's absolute rule, regardless of his involvement. The Latkje family were once prominent members of this faction.

name: Queen's Knights
description: The Queen's Knights (女王騎士, Joō Kishi) are a military unit in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Queen's Knights history allegedly dates back to the founding of the Queendom of Falena, where they were originally a part of the Imperial Defense Knights, with hundreds of members.

When the capital of Falena was moved from Stormfist to Sol-Falena in SY 212, a separate Imperial Defense Corps was organized. Instead, the Queen's Knights were assigned as 'personal bodyguards' to protect the Queen and her closest relatives alone. In accordance with this new, focused duty, their numbers were also drastically reduced, with less than ten at any given time. Being chosen as a Queen's Knight, therefore, meant being recognized as one of the best warriors in Falena.


The Commander of the Queen's Knights was traditionally the husband of the Queen herself, an honor won by emerging victorious in the Sacred Games. Just prior to the Sun Rune War, Ferid served as Queen's Knight Commander, with Alenia, Galleon, Kyle, Zahhak, Miakis and Georg Prime serving under him. Lyon would serve as a Queen's Knight apprentice. Following the Queen's Assassination, the new Godwin backed Queen's Knights were led by Gizel Godwin, with Alenia and Zahhak serving faithfully while Miakis and Galleon would abandon the group as soon as possible. Childerich would be appointed to the elite group by Gizel also.

Following the Sun Rune War, the Queen's Knights would be reformed once more, this time under the leadership of the Prince of Falena, in a break from Falenan tradition. Lyon would become a formal Queen's Knight while Kyle, Galleon and Georg Prime each retired from their positions. Toma would begin training in order to become a Queen's Knight in the future.

name: Queendom of Falena
description: The Queendom of Falena (ファレナ女王国, Farena On'na Ōkoku) is a nation that is the setting for Suikoden V. It is also mentioned in Suikoden II and Suikoden IV.

facts.label: Rulers
facts.value: Queen, Elmelark, Olhazeta, Falzrahm, Arshtat Falenas, Lymsleia Falenas

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Located on the southern continent, to the south of the Island Nations Federation, the Queendom of Falena is a nation that practices a matriarchal form of primogeniture in its royal house, wherein the eldest daughter succeeds her mother to the throne. It is bordered to the south by both Nagarea and the New Armes Kingdom and Falena has held tense relationships with both these nations throughout its history.

Falena was said to have been founded in -250 SY when the first Queen descended to Lunas, bearing the Sun Rune following the destruction of the Ancient Armes Dynasty. Since then, the Sun Rune, and its partners, the Twilight and Dawn Runes have been enshrined in the country.

It appears to be the home of Lorelai, Killey and Morgan but the exact details are unclear. Bartholomew would journey to Falena following the Island Liberation War.

Also, there is a rumor that Georg Prime once visited Falena, murdered its queen and has been hunted as a criminal ever since.

name: Republican Army
description:
]]
The Republican Army (共和軍 Kyouwa Gun) was the national army of the Toran Republic, established following the fall of the Scarlet Moon Empire at the end of the Gate Rune War.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Kwanda Rosman served as the Republican Army's Commander-in-Chief in the aftermath of the Gate Rune Wars. Kasim Hazil was also offered the post of Commander-in-Chief but turned it down in order to concentrate on the defense of Moravia Castle.

Other former Toran Liberation Army officers who achieved positions of note in the Republican Army include Camille, who was appointed the instructor for spearmanship within the army and Kai who was given the position of martial arts instructor. Valeria was stated to have become a Commanding Officer within the army also.

name: Republican Congress
description: The Republican Congress (共和議会 Kyouwa Gikai) is a government body founded following the end of the Gate Rune War and the formation of the Toran Republic.

name: Safir Clan
description: The Safir Clan (セフィクラン, Sefikuran) was one of the Six Clans of the Grasslands. It is mentioned in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body: The Safir Clan was noted as being one of the Six Clans of the Grasslands, signifying its importance. After Luc was appointed a Bishop in the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia, he ordered the forces of Le Buque to attack the Safir Clan at the outset of the Harmonian invasion of Grasslands. The clan was completely wiped out.

name: Sanadia
description: Sanadia (サナディア, Sanadeia) is a former nation located on the northern continent. It is mentioned in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Sanadia was the homeland of Zaj Quiroz which lay to the west of the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia. It had repeatedly refused to submit to Harmonian rule and in the end was brutally invaded and conquered by Harmonia, about 30 years before the Dunan Unification War. Due to their resistance, the children were rounded up and sent to parts of Harmonia to be culturally integrated. It is now one of Harmonia's westward 3rd class regions.

Some speculate that Sanadia may be the full name of Sanady, the similarly annexed homeland of Queen, but this cannot be confirmed because so many different nations have borne the brunt of Harmonian aggression.

name: Sanady
description: Sanady (サナディ, Sanadi) is a former nation located on the northern continent. It is mentioned in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Sanady was the homeland of Queen and was invaded in her youth as someone there had apparently inherited one of the 27 True Runes there, prompting an immediate Harmonian invasion to obtain the rune. What type of True Rune Sanady supposedly had and whether it was obtained by Harmonia or not is shrouded in mystery but in the end, Sanady was conquered and annexed.

Some speculate that Sanady may be an abbreviation of Sanadia, the similarly annexed homeland of Zaj Quiroz, but this cannot be confirmed because so many different nations have borne the brunt of Harmonian aggression.

name: Scarlet Moon Empire
description: The Scarlet Moon Empire (赤月帝国, Akatsuki Teikoku) is a powerful and centralized nation that rules the lands around Lake Toran. Established by Kranach Rugner, a nobleman of the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia, the empire declared independence during Harmonia's civil war and became a dominant force in the region. Known for its absolute monarchy and highly structured Imperial Army, the empire has fallen into decline due to corruption and internal strife.

facts.label: Rulers
facts.value: Emperor, Kranach Rugner, 13 unknown Emperors, Kallnach Rugner, Michelan Rugner, Barbarossa Rugner

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
===Founding and Early History===
The Scarlet Moon Empire was born in year 230 of the solar calendar when Kranach Rugner, a Harmonian nobleman, seized the Holy City of Rupanda (later Gregminster) during Harmonia's civil war. Declaring himself emperor, Kranach established a centralized state that would dominate the region for over two centuries.

The empire's early years were marked by territorial expansion and frequent clashes with the Kooluk Empire to the south, as well as the consolidation of its military and administrative systems. The empire's governance relied heavily on the Imperial Army, which governed through a system of military governors.

This system allowed the empire to maintain tight control over its vast territories, with military governors wielding absolute power over towns and villages. The empire's military structure was highly autonomous, enabling rapid responses to threats and ensuring its dominance in the region.

Following the collapse of Kooluk, Scarlet Moon would see the young City-States of Jowston become its primary rival to the north. The late 300s and early 400s saw the empire build additional defenses to protect against northern incursion with the construction of Moravia Castle and Scarleticia Castle.

===Decline===
The empire reached its zenith under Barbarossa Rugner, the 17th Emperor, who earned the title of "Golden Emperor" after securing victory in the War of Succession (446 solar calendar, 217 imperial reckoning). This civil war erupted when Barbarossa's uncle, Geil Rugner, attempted to usurp the throne during Barbarossa's absence. With the support of the Six Great Generals and the loyalty of his forces, Barbarossa reclaimed the throne and repelled invasions from the City-States of Jowston, solidifying his rule.

However, Barbarossa's later reign was marked by neglect and corruption. After the court magician Windy entered his service,

name: Secret Alliance for the Protection of Pretty Hunks In Real Endangerment
description: The Secret Alliance for the Protection of Pretty Hunks In Real Endangerment (秘密結社・美少年保護観察協会, Himitsu Kessha Bishōnen Hogo Kansatsu Kyōkai), usually referred to as SAPPHIRE, is a secret society founded by Nifsara and Linfa in Suikoden V.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
SAPPHIRE was a secret club created by Linfa and Nifsara in order to discuss and share their appreciation for all the cute, young men serving in the Imperial City Recapture Army. Their meetings usually took place in the baths at Ceras Lake where they could discuss things privately. The duo also inducted Norma into the group, in order to find out more information about Ernst's human form.

Group members refer to each other as comrades. Their motto is "To observe! To protect! To observe some more!".

name: Six Clans
description:
]]
The Six Clans (シックスクラン, Shikkusu Kuran) is a term referring to the six dominant clans that reside in the Grasslands.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Six Clans, as a term, refers collectively to the six most powerful clans located in the Grasslands region. Historically, those clans are the Karaya Clan, Lizard Clan, Duck Clan, Alma Kinan, Safir Clan and Chisha Clan.

Although the members of the Six Clans have seen conflict between each other, they have also cooperated in the past, usually in order to repulse foreign invasions of the Grasslands.

name: South Window Principality
description: South Window Principality (サウスウィンドゥ市国, Sausu Windou Shikuni, South Window City-State) is a region in Suikoden II. It is a constituent city-state of the City-States of Jowston.
originalName: サウスウィンドゥ市国

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
South Window Principality lies south of Lake Dunan and in the past was the center of the Dunan Monarchy. As South Window lies in a temperate area, the climate is generally mild.

To the east lies eastern Muse Principality and in the west lies Two River Principality. To the south lies the Karakas Desert and the border with the Toran Republic.

Civil war would break out in SY 250. By 252, the revolt would succeed and South Window Principality would be established under a mayoral system.

The city-state would later become one of the founding members of the City-States of Jowston in 314. Due to its location, it bore the brunt of conflicts with the southern Scarlet Moon Empire and so was the city-state that was the most distrustful of the southern nation.

In 447, South Window would join Tinto Principality in an invasion of Scarlet Moon in the aftermath of their War of Succession but were eventually repelled. In 456, during the Gate Rune War, they and Tinto would occupy Senan for a time but were eventually pushed out by the new Toran Republic by 458.

The city-state would collapse early in the Dunan Unification War of 460 with the Highland Kingdom invasion. However, its lands would be liberated and become the center of the New Alliance Army, giving it a key role in the war.

Following the war, the former principality would join the new Dunan Republic.

name: Southern Frontier Defense Force
description: The Southern Frontier Defense Force (南部辺境警備隊, Nanbu Henkyō Keibitai) is an organization that appears in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2 and Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Southern Frontier Defense Force is a border guard of sorts, stationed at Harmonia's southern frontier with the Grasslands at Caleria. It is comprised of officers of many different skill types and background, including mercenaries and those trying to escape their past.

SFDF members usually protect the border or engage in espionage and other missions in the areas bordering Harmonia's south, such as the Grasslands or the Dunan Republic. Members of the SFDF are not generally considered part of the main Harmonian army, but while serving in the Defense Force are expected to be loyal.

name: Temple Faction
description: The Temple Faction (神殿派, Shinden-ha) was one of two major political groups in the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia and is shown most prominently in Genso Suikogaiden Vol.2.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
and Sasarai moved against Zaj]]
The Temple Faction was one of the blocs within the aristocracy of the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia. It supports the absolute rule of High Priest Hikusaak, and so is at odds with the People's Faction, which aims for both divine rule by the temple and rule by the citizenry. Behind the scenes there were many a power struggle between the two.

Zaj Quiroz, under the alias of Thanatos Crawford, would eventually become the head of the Temple Faction in his efforts to extract revenge against Harmonia.

name: Tinto Principality
description: Tinto Principality (ティント市国, Tinto Shikuni, Tinto City State) is a region in Suikoden II. It is a constituent city-state of the City-States of Jowston.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Tinto Principality lies in the far west of Dunan and is relatively isolated from the rest of the region. To the east lies Two River Principality and in the west lies the frontier of the Grasslands.

Tinto City was established by the Tinto’s Miner Guild, using its organizational structure in SY 366. It would become a member of the City-States of Jowston immediately afterwards. 

Tinto served as a mostly neutral member of the City-States of Jowston. Separated from the rest of Jowston by the Lampdragon Mountains, its political objectives and outlooks often differed from its eastern partners.

It was one of the more militarily powerful members of the alliance and repeatedly invaded the Grasslands unaided. It also engaged in warfare with the southern Scarlet Moon Empire in alliance with South Window Principality.

In 447, Tinto would lead an invasion of Scarlet Moon in the aftermath of their War of Succession but were eventually repelled. In 456, during the Gate Rune War, they and South Window would occupy Senan for a time but were eventually pushed out by the new Toran Republic by 458.

Following the establishment of the Dunan Republic in 460, Tinto Principality would choose not to join the new nation. Instead, the Tinto Republic was founded by Gustav Pendragon.

name: Tinto Republic
description: The Tinto Republic (ティント共和国, Tinto Kyōwakoku) is a nation located on the Northern continent. It was founded after the events of Suikoden II and is mentioned frequently in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Tinto Republic is located across the mountains, in the eastern reaches of the Grasslands. As Tinto Principality, it was a member of the City-States of Jowston but following the Dunan Unification War it declared its independence, becoming the Tinto Republic.

The former head of the Tinto's miner guild, Gustav Pendragon, was appointed the first president of the republic. Thanks to its mining revenue, it has a powerful army as well as a stable economy. The weapons and decorative items manufactured from Tinto's raw materials is the nation's primary source of income. As much of Tinto is mountainous, Tinto has repeatedly invaded the Grasslands in hopes of claiming fertile land.

name: Toran Liberation Army
description: The Toran Liberation Army (トラン解放軍, Toran Kaihō-gun), also known informally as the Liberation Army, the Freedom Fighters and the Rebel Army was a group of soldiers and other volunteers who fought against the Scarlet Moon Empire during the Gate Rune War.

facts.label: Allies
facts.value: Knights of the Dragon's Den, Warriors' Village

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Liberation Army was founded and led by Odessa Silverberg after years of discontent and resentment coalesced with the imprisonment and death of her lover, Achilles. The Liberation Army's stated goal was to fight and overthrow the corruption of the Imperial regime.

Even in the early stages of the war, the Liberation Army had to deal with insurrectionists. According to imperial propaganda, the Liberation Army were a group of rampant murderers who only cared about pillaging. In the past, Teo McDohl had led efforts to try and squash the rebel movement.

Although the Liberation Army under Odessa was tiny, it prepared plans and laid groundwork for future gains which would prove vital to the army's victory in the Gate Rune War. However, before the Odessa-led force could continue to grow, their hideout in Lenankamp was attacked and Odessa was killed.

With the fall of the Liberation Army after the sack of their Lenankamp hideout, officers fled before trying to pull together as many survivors as possible. One large contingent, led by former Liberation Army Vice-Commander Flik, fled to Kunan before a crackdown on rebel factions forced him to flee to the revived Liberation Army under McDohl, who had assumed the leadership of the army with the backing of the late Odessa and her brother, Mathiu Silverberg. Other rebel hideouts were destroyed in the counter-revolutionary attack, spreading the rebels far and wide.

Following the resurrection of the Liberation Army until McDohl, which could be considered in some ways to be a distinct organization from the original, the organization expanded from just a few hundred members to a much larger force. The Liberation Army under Odessa is sometimes referred to as the "Early Liberation Army" and contrasted with the hero's "New Liberation Army". This is due to Odessa's group being a small collection of warriors as compared to the later Lib

name: Toran Republic Patrol Corps
description: The Toran Republic Patrol Corps (トラン共和国のパトロール Toran Kyōwakoku no Patorōru Toran Republic Patrol) is an organization mentioned in Suikoden.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
This organization was founded immediately after the establishment of the Toran Republic following the Gate Rune War, with the disbandment of both the Toran Liberation Army and the Imperial Army.

The former bandit Kessler was appointed as the captain of this new order.

name: Two River Principality
description: Two River Principality (トウーリバー市国, Toūribā Shikuni, Two River City State) is a region in Suikoden II. It is a constituent city-state of the City-States of Jowston.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
Two River Principality lies to the west of Lake Dunan. To the north lies Greenhill Principality and in the south-east lies South Window Principality. To the distant south-west lies the isolated Tinto Principality.

Two River once formed the western lands of the Dunan Monarchy. However, the decline of that monarchy would see a bicameral parliament established in Two River City in SY 212.

In 314, Two River Principality would become one of the founding members of the City-States of Jowston. As a western territory, it tended to be less worried about invasion from the eastern Highland Kingdom.

During the 460 Dunan Unification War, Two River would be attacked by Highland but would manage to repel the attempt with the aid of the New Alliance Army. Two River would align itself with the force for the rest of the conflict.

Following the war, the principality would join the new Dunan Republic.

name: Unicorn Brigade
description: The Unicorn Brigade (ユニコーン少年兵部隊, Yunikōn Shounen Hyoubu Tai, Unicorn Child Soldier Unit) is a military unit in Suikoden II. It is the youth corps of the Highland Kingdom town of Kyaro.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Unicorn Brigade was a regional unit stationed in Kyaro Town. It was comprised of young males over a certain age. Unicorn Brigade training focused on paramilitary training and installment of good moral values.

Rowd was commander of the unit immediately prior to the beginning of the Dunan Unification War. The Unicorn Brigade at Tenzan Pass counted over 200 youth soliders.

The hero and Jowy Atreides were both well-regarded members of the Unicorn Brigade. Sulis was another member of the unit at the time.

The Unicorn Brigade was wiped out in 460 at Tenzan Pass by Luca Blight as part of his plot to bring the Highland Kingdom to war with the City-States of Jowston.

name: Zexen Council
description: The Zexen Council (ゼクセン評議会, Zekusen Hyōgi-kai) is the administrative body that governs the Zexen Federation. It appears in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Zexen Council is the government body of the Zexen Federation, which sits at the Council building in Vinay del Zexay. The council had its beginnings in the merchant's guild that controlled the city, eventually forming a governing panel to determine policy. Its members are chosen by the most powerful merchant guild.

Although the council appears outwardly to be an effective administrative body, intense power struggles and conflicts over social position rage in its halls. During the War of the Champions, several council members appeared to be conspiring with the Holy Kingdom of Harmonia, causing further problems, and limiting the council's participation in the conflict as the political intrigue played out.

name: Zexen Federation
description: The Zexen Federation (ゼクセン連邦, Zekusen Renpō) is a nation that forms part of the focus of Suikoden III. It is also mentioned in Suikoden II.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Zexen Federation is a center of commerce that lies to the west of the City-States of Jowston, even further west than the Grasslands. It is a bustling area, dealing in a wide range of industries and taking in products from all over the world.

Several members of the Dunan Unification War hailed from Zexen, including Hans, Lebrante, Bolgan, and Gilbert.

Gezel Hyatt was a trader who built a harbor in Vinay del Zexay, allowing it to become a center of trade. Following this, the town continued to develop and in SY 420, a parliamentary federation was established in the city, beginning the Zexen Federation.

The Zexen Council oversees political matters, while the merchant's guild controls the economy and the Zexen Knights were established to strengthen the Zexen military. Over the years, Vinay del Zexay would become one of the continent's leading cities.

As a major commercial center in the west, it is an extremely appealing location for traders and the merchant's guild holds a great deal of power in the enforcement of government there. 

However, the nation has had frequent border clashes with the Grasslands. Relations between Zexen and Grasland had been relatively calm since the exploits of the Fire Bringer, with occasional flare ups due to looting expeditions, until SY 465. In that year, Zexen would lay claim to an area of Grasslands territory. This territorial dispute triggered a resurgence of conflict.

In SY 471, a Lizard Clan force attacked a Zexen trading party on its way to Grasland. A conference was held, but the Lizard Clan claimed the attack was a fabrication by Zexen, looking to expand their territory. This set off a full-scale war between the two powers yet again.

Skirmishes and conflicts escalated, leading to the War of the Champions in 475. During this war, the Six Clans and the Zexen Federation went to war only to ally against the invasion of the Grass

name: Zexen Knights
description: The Zexen Knights are the knightly order of the Zexen Federation. They appear in Suikoden III.

sections.title: Profile
sections.body:
The Zexen Knights are based out of Brass Castle, near the border with Grasslands. The order was established for Zexen's self-defense alongside the nation's establishment. Although a military order, they are under civilian control as they are subject to the orders of the Zexen Council, with some knights under the direct command of council members.

Zexen Knights can fight both on foot and on horseback. They wear heavy, full-body armor and as such, they tend to have very high defensive ability in battle and are difficult to fight head-on. Zexen Knights are also known to be excellent sword wielders.

Immediately prior to the War of the Champions, Galahad, the Captain of the Zexen Knights, and his deputy, Pelize were killed in battle against the Six Clans. Following this, Chris Lightfellow was established as head of the order, following her bravery in battle, leading the Zexen forces to victory.
